Sights: Arundel Castle, Cathedral of Our Lady and St Philip Howard, Arundel Park, and St Leonard's Parish Church. Lunch in Houghton Bridge: the Bridge Inn. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 9 km.

**Arundel to Amberley via Arundel Castle & Hiorne Tower**: Historic town, parkland, and riverside bridleway to Houghton Bridge. Sights: Arundel Castle, Hiorne Tower, Cathedral of Our Lady and St Philip Howard, Arundel Park, and Swanbourne Lake. Lunch in Houghton Bridge: the Bridge Inn. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 7 km.

**Arundel to Worthing**: A longer variant of [Arundel to Goring-by-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/arundel-to-goring-by-sea/). River path, wooded estate tracks, open downland, chalk hills, coastal plain, seafront promenade, and town streets. Sights: [Arundel Castle](https://www.arundelcastle.org), [Angmering Park Estate](https://www.angmeringwalks.com/angmering-park-estate), [Highdown Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Highdown_Hill), [Highdown Gardens](https://highdowngardens.co.uk), Iron Age hill fort, St John the Divine, Miller's Tomb, Canadian War Memorial, Worthing Lido, Worthing Pier, Pavilion Theatre, and Christ Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Patching, or Highdown. Patching lunch stop: the [World's End](https://www.worldsendpatching.co.uk). Highdown lunch stop: the [Highdown](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/highdown). 22 km. 5 hours.

**Amberley to Arundel**: South Downs, wild flower meadows, gradual climbs, riverside path along River Arun, and historic market town. Sights: Amberley Museum, Arundel Castle, and Arundel Cathedral. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in Burpham: the George and Dragon. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 14 km. 4.5 hours.

**Amberley to Arundel via Arundel Park & St Nicholas, Arundel**: An alternative variant of [Amberley Circular via Arundel Park](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/amberley-circular-via-arundel-park/). Water meadows, riverside embankments, wooded downs, open chalk parkland, and historic town. Sights: [Arundel Castle](https://www.arundelcastle.org), [St Nicholas, Arundel](https://www.stnicholas-arundel.co.uk/visiting-st-nicholas), [Cathedral Church of Our Lady and St Philip Howard](https://arundelcathedral.uk), [Arundel Museum](https://arundelmuseum.org/your-visit), [Arundel Wetland Centre](https://www.wwt.org.uk/wetland-centres/arundel), Swanbourne Lake, Hiorne Tower, and Blackfriars. Flooding risk in meadows. Lunch in Arundel: the Waterside Café, the [Swan Hotel](https://www.swanarundel.co.uk), the [Norfolk Arms Hotel](https://www.norfolkarmsarundel.com), the [Red Lion](https://www.redlionarundel.com), or the [St Mary's Gate Inn](https://stmarysgate.co.uk). 14 km. 3.5 hours.
ARU.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Amberley to Arundel along the Arun**: An essential walk! Woods, flowers, fields, hedgegrowth, and castle. Lunch in Burpham: the George (very nice; reservations recommended). From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 11 km. 50 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2498061103)**

ASH.name: Ash
ASH.adminWalksAll:
**[Ash Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-circular-via-the-mod-ranges/)**: Lowland heathland, conifer and broad-leaved woodland, stony paths, boggy sections, canal towpath, gorse and bracken, and with many short sharp climbs and wide views. Sights: [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/conservation/projects/3), Basingstoke Canal, Guildford Cathedral, and Keogh Barracks. MOD range closures apply. Best in Apr, May and Aug. Lunch in Ash Vale: the Swan. 22 km.

**[Ash to Brookwood via American Military Cemetery](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/)**: Lowland heathland, firing range ridgeline, open grassland, conifer and broad-leaved woods, farmland fields, and historic cemetery grounds. Sights: [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/reserves/9), St Michael and All Angels Church, Henry Morton Stanley's Grave, [Brookwood Cemetery](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/), [Brookwood Military Cemetery](http://www.cwgc.org), [Orthodox Church and Monastery of St Edward](http://www.saintedwardbrotherhood.org), American Military Cemetery, Brookwood Memorial, [Cemetery Railway remains (North and South station platforms)](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/cemetery_railway.htm), and Colquhoun Mausoleum. Army range closures apply. Lunch in Pirbright: the White Hart, or the Cricketers on The Green. 15 km.

**Ash to Brookwood via Ash Ranges**: A shorter variant of [Ash to Brookwood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/). Lowland heathland, firing range ridgeline, woods, and farmland fields. Sights: [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/reserves/9). Army range closures apply. 11 km.

**Ash to Brookwood via Glades of Remembrance**: A variant of [Ash to Brookwood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/). Lowland heathland, firing range ridgeline, woods, farmland fields, and extensive cemetery grounds including neglected sections. Sights: [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/reserves/9), Colquhoun Mausoleum, Glades of Remembrance, Pelham-Clinton Memorial, Drake Family Mausoleum, [Brookwood Cemetery](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/), [Brookwood Military Cemetery](http://www.cwgc.org), [Orthodox Church and Monastery of St Edward](http://www.saintedwardbrotherhood.org), St Michael and All Angels Church, and Henry Morton Stanley's Grave. Uneven pauper burial ground. Lunch in Pirbright: the White Hart, or the Cricketers on The Green. 16 km.

**Ash to Brookwood via Basingstoke Canal**: A longer variant of [Ash to Brookwood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/). Canal towpath, heathland, woods, farmland fields, and historic cemetery grounds. Sights: Basingstoke Canal, [Brookwood Cemetery](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/), [Brookwood Military Cemetery](http://www.cwgc.org), St Michael and All Angels Church, and Henry Morton Stanley's Grave. Army range closures apply. Lunch in Ash Vale, or Pirbright. Ash Vale lunch stops: the Swan, or the Napiers. Pirbright lunch stops: the White Hart, or the Cricketers on The Green. 22 km.

**Ash to Ash Vale**: A shorter variant of [Ash Vale Circular via North Downs Way](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-circular-via-north-downs-way/). Flat canal towpath, disused railway line, heathland commons, North Downs Way, open fields, mixed woodland, and the Ash Ranges. Sights: Basingstoke Canal, Puttenham Common, St John the Baptist, Broadstreet Common, and Ash/Pirbright Ranges. MOD firing range closures. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in Puttenham: the Good Intent. 22 km.

**Ash Vale to Ash via Ash Ranges**: A shorter variant of [Ash Vale to Worplesdon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-to-worplesdon/). Basingstoke Canal towpath, MOD firing range heathland, open heath, and pine woodland. Sights: Basingstoke Canal and Ash Ranges. MOD closures apply. 9 km.

**Brookwood to Ash**: A shorter variant of [Ash to Brookwood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/). Cemetery grounds, farmland fields, lowland heathland, and firing range ridgeline. Sights: [Brookwood Cemetery](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/) and [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/reserves/9). Army range closures apply. 9 km.

**Ash Vale to Ash via North Downs Way**: A variant of [Ash Vale Circular via North Downs Way](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-circular-via-north-downs-way/). Flat canal towpath, disused railway line, heathland commons, North Downs Way, open fields, mixed woodland, and ridge traverse of Ash Ranges. Sights: Basingstoke Canal, Puttenham Common, St John the Baptist, and Ash/Pirbright Ranges. MOD firing range closures. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in Puttenham: the Good Intent. 24 km.
ASH.publicWalksS2S: **[Ash to Brookwood via American Military Cemetery](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/)**: Lowland heathland, firing range ridgeline, open grassland, conifer and broad-leaved woods, farmland fields, and historic cemetery grounds. Sights: [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/reserves/9), St Michael and All Angels Church, Henry Morton Stanley's Grave, [Brookwood Cemetery](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/), [Brookwood Military Cemetery](http://www.cwgc.org), [Orthodox Church and Monastery of St Edward](http://www.saintedwardbrotherhood.org), American Military Cemetery, Brookwood Memorial, [Cemetery Railway remains (North and South station platforms)](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/cemetery_railway.htm), and Colquhoun Mausoleum. Army range closures apply. Lunch in Pirbright: the White Hart, or the Cricketers on The Green. 15 km.
ASH.publicWalksCircular: **[Ash Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-circular-via-the-mod-ranges/)**: Lowland heathland, conifer and broad-leaved woodland, stony paths, boggy sections, canal towpath, gorse and bracken, and with many short sharp climbs and wide views. Sights: [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/conservation/projects/3), Basingstoke Canal, Guildford Cathedral, and Keogh Barracks. MOD range closures apply. Best in Apr, May and Aug. Lunch in Ash Vale: the Swan. 22 km.

ATB.name: Attenborough
ATB.adminWalksAll: **[Attenborough Circular via Nature Reserve](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Good paths, nature reserve, canal, and river. Sights: Attenborough Nature Reserve, Beeston Canal, and River Trent. Check for flooding. Lunch in Attenborough Nature Reserve: the cafe. 14 km. 20 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/402%20Attenborough.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925786815)**
ATB.publicWalksCircular: **[Attenborough Circular via Nature Reserve](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Good paths, nature reserve, canal, and river. Sights: Attenborough Nature Reserve, Beeston Canal, and River Trent. Check for flooding. Lunch in Attenborough Nature Reserve: the cafe. 14 km. 20 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/402%20Attenborough.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925786815)**

ATH.name: Atherstone
ATH.adminWalksAll:
**[Atherstone Circular via Sheepy Magna](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Canal and villages. Sights: Coventry Canal, Sheepy Magna, Ratcliffe Culey, and Witherley. 17 km. 70 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/212%20Atherstone%20Circular%2010.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925784899)**

**[Atherstone Circular via Bluebell Woodland](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Woodlands, country park, and valley views. Sights: Hartshill Country Park and Anker Valley. Best in Apr. Lunch in Ridge Lane: the Church End Brewery. 20 km. 290 m uphill. 5.5 hours. Hard. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/206%20Bluebells%20and%20Beer%2012.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925784524)**
ATH.publicWalksCircular:
**[Atherstone Circular via Sheepy Magna](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Canal and villages. Sights: Coventry Canal, Sheepy Magna, Ratcliffe Culey, and Witherley. 17 km. 70 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/212%20Atherstone%20Circular%2010.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925784899)**

**[Atherstone Circular via Bluebell Woodland](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Woodlands, country park, and valley views. Sights: Hartshill Country Park and Anker Valley. Best in Apr. Lunch in Ridge Lane: the Church End Brewery. 20 km. 290 m uphill. 5.5 hours. Hard. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/206%20Bluebells%20and%20Beer%2012.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925784524)**

AUD.name: Audley End
AUD.adminWalksAll:
**Newport to Audley End via Saffron Walden**: Spoiled by a long trek along a busy road near Audley End. *Last reviewed 11 February 2023.* 13 km. 150 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921497547)**

**[Audley End Circular via The Cottage (pargeted house, Wendens Ambo)](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wendens-ambo-circular/)**: Gently rolling chalky uplands, farmland, woods and copses, green lanes, field boundaries, and pretty villages with thatched cottages and pargeted houses. Sights: St. Mary the Virgin, Arkesden and The Cottage (pargeted house, Wendens Ambo). Lunch in Arkesden, or Chrishall. Arkesden lunch stop: the [Axe & Compasses](http://www.axeandcompasses.co.uk/). Chrishall lunch stop: the [Red Cow](http://www.theredcow.com/). 23 km. 5.5 hours.

**Audley End Circular via Audley End House**: A variant of [Great Chesterford to Newport](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/great-chesterford-to-newport/). Road sections, Audley End parkland, farmland, river valleys, historic market town, woods, and village with thatched cottages. Sights: [Audley End House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/audley-end-house-and-gardens), Audley End Miniature Railway, Bridge End Garden, [Fry Art Gallery](https://fryartgallery.org), [St Mary the Virgin, Saffron Walden](https://www.stmaryssaffronwalden.org/Groups/313211/History_and_Guides.aspx), Walden Castle, [Saffron Walden Museum](https://www.saffronwaldenmuseum.org), Turf Maze, Old Sun Inn, King Edward VI Almshouses, St Mary the Virgin, Newport, Monk's Barn, and St Mary the Virgin, Wendens Ambo. Can be muddy. Lunch in Saffron Walden: the [Eight Bells](https://www.theeightbellssaffronwalden.com), the Kings Arms, the [Cross Keys Hotel](https://www.theoldcrosskeys.co.uk), or the [Temeraire](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/the-temeraire-saffron-walden). 18 km. 4 hours.

**Audley End to Newport**: A shorter variant of [Great Chesterford to Newport](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/great-chesterford-to-newport/). Road sections, Audley End parkland, farmland, river valleys, historic market town, and woods. Sights: [Audley End House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/audley-end-house-and-gardens), Audley End Miniature Railway, Bridge End Garden, [Fry Art Gallery](https://fryartgallery.org), [St Mary the Virgin, Saffron Walden](https://www.stmaryssaffronwalden.org/Groups/313211/History_and_Guides.aspx), Walden Castle, [Saffron Walden Museum](https://www.saffronwaldenmuseum.org), Turf Maze, Old Sun Inn, King Edward VI Almshouses, St Mary the Virgin, Newport, and Monk's Barn. Can be muddy. Lunch in Saffron Walden: the [Eight Bells](https://www.theeightbellssaffronwalden.com), the Kings Arms, the [Cross Keys Hotel](https://www.theoldcrosskeys.co.uk), or the [Temeraire](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/the-temeraire-saffron-walden). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**Great Chesterford to Audley End**: A longer variant of [Great Chesterford to Newport](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/great-chesterford-to-newport/). Rolling Essex farmland, grassy field edges, small woods, river valleys, historic market town, parkland, and village with thatched cottages. Sights: All Saints Church, Great Chesterford, St Mary the Virgin, Little Chesterford, Bridge End Garden, [Fry Art Gallery](https://fryartgallery.org), [St Mary the Virgin, Saffron Walden](https://www.stmaryssaffronwalden.org/Groups/313211/History_and_Guides.aspx), Walden Castle, [Saffron Walden Museum](https://www.saffronwaldenmuseum.org), Turf Maze, Old Sun Inn, [Audley End House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/audley-end-house-and-gardens), Audley End Miniature Railway, King Edward VI Almshouses, St Mary the Virgin, Newport, Monk's Barn, and St Mary the Virgin, Wendens Ambo. Can be muddy. Lunch in Saffron Walden: the [Eight Bells](https://www.theeightbellssaffronwalden.com), the Kings Arms, the [Cross Keys Hotel](https://www.theoldcrosskeys.co.uk), or the [Temeraire](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/the-temeraire-saffron-walden). 20 km. 5 hours.
AUD.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Newport to Audley End via Saffron Walden**: Spoiled by a long trek along a busy road near Audley End. 13 km. 150 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921497547)**

AVP.name: Aylesbury Vale Parkway
AVP.adminWalksAll:
**[Aylesbury Vale Parkway to Aylesbury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/aylesbury-vale-parkway-to-aylesbury/)**: Rolling hills, pastures, hay meadows, river valleys, quiet hilltop villages, and a long canal towpath stretch into a historic town centre. Sights: Quarrendon deserted medieval village, Bolebec castle mound, St. Osyth's Well, Grand Union Canal, and [King's Head](http://www.kingsheadaylesbury.co.uk/). Can be muddy. Avoid midsummer. Lunch in Weedon, Hardwick, Whitchurch, or Aston Abbotts. Weedon lunch stop: the [Five Elms](http://fiveelmsweedon.com/). Hardwick lunch stop: the [Bell at Hardwick](http://www.thebellathardwick.co.uk/). Whitchurch lunch stop: the [White Swan](http://whiteswanwhitchurch.co.uk/). Aston Abbotts lunch stop: the Royal Oak Inn. 25 km. 5.5 hours.

**Haddenham and Thame Parkway to Aylesbury Vale Parkway**: An alternative variant of [Haddenham to Aylesbury via Waddesdon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haddenham-to-aylesbury-via-waddesdon/). Thame Valley floodplain, energetic climbs, open vistas, Rothschild estate parkland, and tree-lined avenue. Sights: [Waddesdon Manor](http://www.waddesdon.org.uk/), Eythrope Park, and Upper Winchendon. Lunch in Waddesdon: the [Waddesdon Manor](http://www.waddesdon.org.uk/), the [Five Arrows Hotel and Restaurant](https://fivearrowshotel.co.uk/), the [Bow](https://www.thebowwaddesdon.co.uk/), the 94 Coffee Shop, or the [Lion](http://www.thelionwaddesdon.co.uk/). 19 km.
AVP.publicWalksS2S: **[Aylesbury Vale Parkway to Aylesbury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/aylesbury-vale-parkway-to-aylesbury/)**: Rolling hills, pastures, hay meadows, river valleys, quiet hilltop villages, and a long canal towpath stretch into a historic town centre. Sights: Quarrendon deserted medieval village, Bolebec castle mound, St. Osyth's Well, Grand Union Canal, and [King's Head](http://www.kingsheadaylesbury.co.uk/). Can be muddy. Avoid midsummer. Lunch in Weedon, Hardwick, Whitchurch, or Aston Abbotts. Weedon lunch stop: the [Five Elms](http://fiveelmsweedon.com/). Hardwick lunch stop: the [Bell at Hardwick](http://www.thebellathardwick.co.uk/). Whitchurch lunch stop: the [White Swan](http://whiteswanwhitchurch.co.uk/). Aston Abbotts lunch stop: the Royal Oak Inn. 25 km. 5.5 hours.

AYL.name: Aylesford
AYL.adminWalksAll:
**Aylesford Circular**: A shorter variant of [Aylesford to Maidstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/aylesford-to-maidstone/). River meadows, chalk downland, wooded North Downs escarpment, open hilltop, farmland, and riverside path back to Aylesford. Sights: [The Friars](https://www.thefriars.org.uk), [Kit's Coty House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/kits-coty-house-and-little-kits-coty-house/history), [Cobtree Manor Park](https://www.cobtreecafe.co.uk/about-cobtree-manor-park), Aylesford Bridge, Ss Peter and Paul, Kit's Coty Vineyard, St Mary Burham, Little Kit's Coty House, White Horse Stone, Battle of Medway memorial, and Chequers Inn. Can be muddy. Lunch in Eccles, Burham, or Burham Common. Eccles lunch stop: the Red Bull. Burham lunch stops: the [Butcher's Block](https://thebutchersblockpub.co.uk), or the [Windmill](https://windmillburham.com). Burham Common lunch stop: the [Robin Hood](https://www.robinhood-pub.co.uk). 17 km. 4 hours.

**[Aylesford to Maidstone East](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/aylesford-to-maidstone/)**: River meadows, chalk downland, wooded North Downs escarpment, open hilltop, farmland, riverside towpath, and country park. Sights: [The Friars](https://www.thefriars.org.uk), [Kit's Coty House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/kits-coty-house-and-little-kits-coty-house/history), [Cobtree Manor Park](https://www.cobtreecafe.co.uk/about-cobtree-manor-park), [Whatman Park](https://www.maidstoneriverpark.co.uk/the-park/whatman-park), Aylesford Bridge, Ss Peter and Paul, Kit's Coty Vineyard, St Mary Burham, Little Kit's Coty House, White Horse Stone, Allington Castle, Kent Life, Allington Sluice Gate, Brenchley Gardens, Iguanodon, Battle of Maidstone memorial, Maidstone Museum, and Battle of Medway memorial. Can be muddy. Lunch in Eccles, Burham, or Burham Common. Eccles lunch stop: the Red Bull. Burham lunch stops: the [Butcher's Block](https://thebutchersblockpub.co.uk), or the [Windmill](https://windmillburham.com). Burham Common lunch stop: the [Robin Hood](https://www.robinhood-pub.co.uk). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

Sights: [The Friars](https://www.thefriars.org.uk), [Kit's Coty House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/kits-coty-house-and-little-kits-coty-house/history), [Cobtree Manor Park](https://www.cobtreecafe.co.uk/about-cobtree-manor-park), [Whatman Park](https://www.maidstoneriverpark.co.uk/the-park/whatman-park), Aylesford Bridge, Ss Peter and Paul, Kit's Coty Vineyard, St Mary Burham, Little Kit's Coty House, White Horse Stone, Allington Castle, Kent Life, Allington Sluice Gate, Brenchley Gardens, Iguanodon, Battle of Maidstone memorial, Maidstone Museum, and Battle of Medway memorial. Burham Common lunch stop: the [Robin Hood](https://www.robinhood-pub.co.uk). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

AYS.name: Aylesbury
AYS.adminWalksAll:
**[Haddenham and Thame Parkway to Aylesbury via Gibraltar and Ford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haddenham-to-aylesbury-via-gibraltar-and-ford/)**: Flat Thame Valley farmland, tranquil hamlets, pastures, community woodland, and stately mansion grounds. Sights: Notley Abbey, Dinton Hall, [Hartwell House](http://www.hartwell-house.com/spa-cafe-and-bar/), and [King's Head](http://www.kingsheadaylesbury.co.uk/). Lunch in Cuddington, Gibraltar, Dinton, or Ford. Cuddington lunch stop: the [Crown at Cuddington](https://www.thecrowncuddington.com/). Gibraltar lunch stop: the [Bottle & Glass](http://www.thebottleandglass.com/). Dinton lunch stop: the [Seven Stars](http://www.sevenstarsdinton.com/). Ford lunch stop: the [Dinton Hermit Inn](https://www.thedintonhermit.co.uk/). 23 km. 5 hours.

**[Haddenham and Thame Parkway to Aylesbury via Waddesdon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haddenham-to-aylesbury-via-waddesdon/)**: Thame Valley floodplain, energetic climbs, open vistas, Rothschild estate parkland, tree-lined avenue, and river and brook paths into a market town. Sights: [Waddesdon Manor](http://www.waddesdon.org.uk/), Eythrope Park, and Upper Winchendon. Lunch in Waddesdon: the [Waddesdon Manor](http://www.waddesdon.org.uk/), the [Five Arrows Hotel and Restaurant](https://fivearrowshotel.co.uk/), the [Bow](https://www.thebowwaddesdon.co.uk/), the 94 Coffee Shop, or the [Lion](http://www.thelionwaddesdon.co.uk/). 25 km. 6 hours.

**[Aylesbury Vale Parkway to Aylesbury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/aylesbury-vale-parkway-to-aylesbury/)**: Rolling hills, pastures, hay meadows, river valleys, quiet hilltop villages, and a long canal towpath stretch into a historic town centre. Sights: Quarrendon deserted medieval village, Bolebec castle mound, St. Osyth's Well, Grand Union Canal, and [King's Head](http://www.kingsheadaylesbury.co.uk/). Can be muddy. Avoid midsummer. Lunch in Weedon, Hardwick, Whitchurch, or Aston Abbotts. Weedon lunch stop: the [Five Elms](http://fiveelmsweedon.com/). Hardwick lunch stop: the [Bell at Hardwick](http://www.thebellathardwick.co.uk/). Whitchurch lunch stop: the [White Swan](http://whiteswanwhitchurch.co.uk/). Aston Abbotts lunch stop: the Royal Oak Inn. 25 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Aylesbury Vale Parkway to Aylesbury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/aylesbury-vale-parkway-to-aylesbury/)**: Rolling hills, pastures, hay meadows, river valleys, quiet hilltop villages, and a long canal towpath stretch into a historic town centre. Sights: Quarrendon deserted medieval village, Bolebec castle mound, St. Osyth's Well, Grand Union Canal, and [King's Head](http://www.kingsheadaylesbury.co.uk/). Can be muddy. Avoid midsummer. Lunch in Weedon, Hardwick, Whitchurch, or Aston Abbotts. Weedon lunch stop: the [Five Elms](http://fiveelmsweedon.com/). Hardwick lunch stop: the [Bell at Hardwick](http://www.thebellathardwick.co.uk/). Whitchurch lunch stop: the [White Swan](http://whiteswanwhitchurch.co.uk/). Aston Abbotts lunch stop: the Royal Oak Inn. 25 km. 5.5 hours.

BAB.name: Balcombe
BAB.adminWalksAll:
**Balcombe Circular via Nymans Gardens**: Rambler favourite! Fields, woods, stream valleys, grassy hillsides, estate parkland, a fine old village, and stately home. Sights: [Nymans House & Gardens](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/sussex/nymans) (quite small but worthwhile; can easily fit a visit into a day hike), Lord Snowdon's lake with Japanese pavilion, St Mary's Parish Church, Slaugham Place, Arch with Roman pillars, River Ouse, and Old Hall. Can be muddy. Lunch in Handcross, Nymans, Slaugham, or Staplefield. Handcross lunch stop: the Red Lion. Nymans lunch stop: the Riding House. Slaugham lunch stop: the Heritage. Staplefield lunch stops: the Victory Inn, or the Jolly Tanners. Other lunch stop: the [Nymans Gardens National Trust café](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/sussex/nymans). *Last reviewed 16 December 2025.* 15 km. 270 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2707110991)**

**Balcome Circular via Ardingly Reservoir**: Sights: [Ouse Valley Viaduct](https://hellosussex.com/ouse-valley-viaduct-west-sussex-visitor-guide/) (Victorian architectural marvel). *Last reviewed 3 April 2021.* 11 km. 140 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921150488)**

**[Balcombe Circular via Wakehurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/balcombe-circular-via-wakehurst/)**: Undulating High Weald countryside, wooded hillsides, farm fields, covered footbridge over reservoir valley, permissive parkland paths, and botanic gardens. Sights: [Wakehurst](https://www.kew.org/wakehurst), [South of England Event Centre](https://southofenglandeventcentre.co.uk/events), Loder Valley Nature Reserve, Ardingly Reservoir, Millennium Seed Bank, Wakehurst Mansion, and Balcombe Lake. Can be muddy. Lunch in Little London: the [Gardeners Arms](https://www.gardenersarmsardingly.co.uk). 14 km. 4 hours.

**[Balcombe Circular via Ardingly Reservoir](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/balcombe-circular-via-ardingly-reservoir/)**: Wealden hills, woods, pasture, hidden farms, quiet lanes, dry tracks, and reservoir edge. Sights: Ouse Valley Viaduct and Ardingly Reservoir. Can be muddy. Best in Dec–Feb. Lunch in Ardingly: the Ardingly Activity Centre, the Ardingly Inn, the Ardingly Cafe, or the Gardeners Arms. 16 km.

**Balcombe Circular via Wakehurst & St Peter, Ardingly**: A shorter variant of [Balcombe Circular via Wakehurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/balcombe-circular-via-wakehurst/). Reservoir perimeter path, scrubland, farm fields, church lane, showground perimeter, permissive parkland paths, and botanic gardens. Sights: [Wakehurst](https://www.kew.org/wakehurst), Ardingly Reservoir, St Peter, Ardingly, [South of England Event Centre](https://southofenglandeventcentre.co.uk/events), Ardingly Showground, Millennium Seed Bank, Wakehurst Mansion, Balcombe Lake, and Queen's Green Canopy. Can be muddy. Lunch in Little London: the [Gardeners Arms](https://www.gardenersarmsardingly.co.uk). 12 km. 3 hours.

**Balcombe Circular via Ardingly**: A variant of [Haywards Heath to Balcombe via Ardingly](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haywards-heath-to-balcombe-via-ardingly/). Undulating High Weald farmland, woodland, reservoir perimeter path, fields, and estate grounds. Sights: Ardingly Reservoir, [South of England Event Centre](https://southofenglandeventcentre.co.uk/events), Ardingly College, Ouse Valley Viaduct, Stone Hall, Balcombe Place, St Peter, Ardingly, and Balcombe Lake. Can be muddy. Lunch in Ardingly Reservoir, or Ardingly. Ardingly Reservoir lunch stop: the [Ardingly Activity Centre Café](https://www.ardinglyactivitycentre.co.uk/our-cafe). Ardingly lunch stops: the [Ardingly Inn](https://theardinglyinn.co.uk), the Fellows Bakery, or the Ardingly Café. 16 km. 4 hours.

**Balcombe Circular via Ardingly Reservoir & Millennium Seedbank**: A longer variant of [Balcombe Circular via Ardingly Reservoir](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/balcombe-circular-via-ardingly-reservoir/). Hilly woodland paths, farm tracks, bluebell woods, fields, reservoir views, and High Weald valleys. Sights: Ouse Valley Viaduct, Ardingly Reservoir, Wakehurst Place, and Millennium Seedbank. Steep climbs and descents. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Ardingly: the Ardingly Activity Centre, the Ardingly Inn, the Ardingly Cafe, or the Gardeners Arms. 19 km.

**Balcombe to East Grinstead**: Gentle wooded hills, muddy farm tracks, landscaped gardens, reservoir shoreline, open fields, and woodland paths. Sights: [Wakehurst Place](http://www.wakehurst.org/visitor/visitwp.html), [Priest House Museum](http://www.sussexpast.co.uk/property/site.php?site_id=17), [St Margaret's Church](http://www.westhoathly.org.uk/), Stone Farm Rocks, Weir Wood Reservoir, Standen House, and Balcombe House. Can be very muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in West Hoathly: the [Cat Inn](http://www.catinn.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 17 km. 5 hours.

**[Balcombe to Horsham via Chesworth Farm Riverside Fields](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/balcombe-to-horsham/)**: Wealden woods, farmland fields, streams, ponds, riverside nature reserve, and old market town. Sights: [High Weald](http://www.highweald.org/), [Nymans Estate](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/nymans), [St Leonard's Forest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Leonard%27s_Forest), [Chesworth Farm Riverside Fields](https://friendsofchesworthfarm.com/), [River Arun](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Arun), [Horsham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Horsham), and [Black Jug](http://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/blackjug/). Can be very overgrown. Lunch in Plummers Plain: the [Wheatsheaf](http://www.thewheatsheafplummersplain.co.uk/). 27 km.

**Balcombe to Horsham via High Weald**: A shorter variant of [Balcombe to Horsham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/balcombe-to-horsham/). Wealden woods, farmland, streams, and direct route to Horsham station. Sights: [High Weald](http://www.highweald.org/), [Nymans Estate](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/nymans), and [St Leonard's Forest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Leonard%27s_Forest). Can be very overgrown. Lunch in Plummers Plain: the [Wheatsheaf](http://www.thewheatsheafplummersplain.co.uk/). 21 km.

**[Haywards Heath to Balcombe via the Ouse valley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haywards-heath-to-balcombe/)**: Rolling High Weald hills, woodland, farmland, river valley, golf course, level crossing, and quiet lanes. Sights: [Ouse Valley Viaduct](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ouse_Valley_Viaduct), [Sussex Ouse Valley Way](http://www.sussexousevalleyway.co.uk/index.htm), and [High Weald Landscape Trail](http://www.highweald.org/home/news/1850-new-online-guide-for-landscape-trail-.html). Can be muddy. 10 km. 2.5 hours.

**[Haywards Heath to Balcombe via Ardingly](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haywards-heath-to-balcombe-via-ardingly/)**: Undulating High Weald farmland, woodland, golf course, reservoir dam wall, fields, and a private school grounds. Sights: Ardingly Reservoir, [South of England Event Centre](https://southofenglandeventcentre.co.uk/events), Ardingly College, Ouse Valley Viaduct, Stone Hall, Balcombe Place, St Peter, Ardingly, Balcombe Lake, Sussex Ouse Valley Way, and High Weald Landscape Trail. Can be muddy. Lunch in Ardingly Reservoir, or Ardingly. Ardingly Reservoir lunch stop: the [Ardingly Activity Centre Café](https://www.ardinglyactivitycentre.co.uk/our-cafe). Ardingly lunch stops: the [Ardingly Inn](https://theardinglyinn.co.uk), the Fellows Bakery, or the Ardingly Café. 16 km. 4 hours.

**Uckfield to Balcombe**: A shorter variant of [Uckfield to Pease Pottage](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-pease-pottage/). Linear walk through East Sussex countryside, villages, and farmland ending at Balcombe station. Lunch in Fletching, or Horsted Keynes. Fletching lunch stop: the [Griffin Inn](https://thegriffininn.co.uk/). Horsted Keynes lunch stops: the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrownhorstedkeynes.co.uk/), or the [Green Man](http://greenmanhorstedkeynes.co.uk/). 29 km.
BAB.publicWalksCircular:
**Balcombe Circular via Nymans Gardens**: Rambler favourite! Fields, woods, stream valleys, grassy hillsides, estate parkland, a fine old village, and stately home. Sights: [Nymans House & Gardens](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/sussex/nymans) (quite small but worthwhile; can easily fit a visit into a day hike), Lord Snowdon's lake with Japanese pavilion, St Mary's Parish Church, Slaugham Place, Arch with Roman pillars, River Ouse, and Old Hall. Can be muddy. Lunch in Handcross, Nymans, Slaugham, or Staplefield. Handcross lunch stop: the Red Lion. Nymans lunch stop: the Riding House. Slaugham lunch stop: the Heritage. Staplefield lunch stops: the Victory Inn, or the Jolly Tanners. Other lunch stop: the [Nymans Gardens National Trust café](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/sussex/nymans). 15 km. 270 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2707110991)**

**Balcome Circular via Ardingly Reservoir**: Sights: [Ouse Valley Viaduct](https://hellosussex.com/ouse-valley-viaduct-west-sussex-visitor-guide/) (Victorian architectural marvel). 11 km. 140 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921150488)**

BAM.name: Bamford
BAM.adminWalksAll:
**[Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bamford-to-edale-via-win-hill-and-great-ridge/)**: Steep ascent to heathery ridge, exposed moorland, gritstone escarpments, upland peat bog, and scenic ridgeline with panoramic valley views. Sights: Win Hill Pike, The Great Ridge, Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, Upper Derwent Valley reservoirs, Lose Hill, and [Old Nag's Head](http://www.the-old-nags-head.co.uk/). Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Hope: the [Cheshire Cheese Inn](http://www.thecheshirecheeseinn.co.uk/). 20 km. 6.5 hours.

**Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge & Hollins Cross**: A shorter variant of [Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bamford-to-edale-via-win-hill-and-great-ridge/). Steep ascent to heathery ridge, exposed moorland, gritstone escarpments, and shorter ridgeline descent to Edale. Sights: Win Hill Pike, The Great Ridge, Lose Hill, and Hollins Cross. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Hope: the [Cheshire Cheese Inn](http://www.thecheshirecheeseinn.co.uk/). 13 km.

**Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge & Brown Knoll**: A longer variant of [Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bamford-to-edale-via-win-hill-and-great-ridge/). Steep ascent to heathery ridge, exposed moorland, upland peat bog on Brown Knoll, gritstone escarpments, and descent via Jacob's Ladder. Sights: Win Hill Pike, The Great Ridge, Mam Tor, Brown Knoll, and Jacob's Ladder. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Hope: the [Cheshire Cheese Inn](http://www.thecheshirecheeseinn.co.uk/). 25 km.

**[Hathersage to Bamford via Derwent Edge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hathersage-to-bamford-via-bamford-and-hordron-edges/)**: Gritstone edges, open moorland, pastures with views, ancient oak woods, plantation woods, reservoir shore, and Hope Valley. Sights: Stanage Edge, Ladybower Reservoir, Derwent Edge, Hordron Edge Stone Circle, Bamford Edge, and Ashopton Viaduct. Steep descents. Lunch in Bamford: the [Ladybower Inn](https://www.ladybowerinn.com). 25 km. 6.5 hours.

**[Sheffield to Bamford via Stanage Edge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sheffield-to-bamford-via-stanage-edge/)**: Urban streets, landscaped Victorian cemetery, municipal parkland, narrow wooded valley, open moorland, rugged gritstone edges, and a scenic descent into lush valley. Sights: [Victorian General Cemetery](http://gencem.org/), [Endcliffe Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Endcliffe_Park), [Porter Valley](https://www.fopv.org.uk/), [Peak District](http://www.peakdistrict.gov.uk/), Burbage Rocks, and Stanage Edge. Can be muddy. Lunch in Fulwood, Sheffield, Ringinglow, or Longshaw. Fulwood, Sheffield lunch stop: the [Forge Dam Café](https://forgedamcafe.co.uk/). Ringinglow lunch stop: the [Norfolk Arms](http://norfolkarms.com/). Longshaw lunch stop: the [Fox House Inn](http://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/yorkshire/thefoxhouselongshaw). 25 km. 6.5 hours.

**Hathersage to Bamford via Stanage Edge**: A shorter variant of [Hathersage to Bamford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hathersage-to-bamford-via-bamford-and-hordron-edges/). Gritstone edges, open moorland, pastures with views, ancient oak woods, reservoir shore, and Hope Valley. Sights: Stanage Edge, Ladybower Reservoir, Bamford Edge, and Ashopton Viaduct. Steep descents. Lunch in Bamford: the [Ladybower Inn](https://www.ladybowerinn.com). 20 km.

**Hathersage to Bamford via Hordron Edge Stone Circle**: A variant of [Hathersage to Bamford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hathersage-to-bamford-via-bamford-and-hordron-edges/). Open moorland, prehistoric cairns, stone circles, standing stones, pathless sections, and Moscar and Bamford Moors. Sights: Hordron Edge Stone Circle and Ladybower Reservoir. Avoid Ground Nesting Season. Avoid Mar–Jul. Lunch in Bamford: the [Ladybower Inn](https://www.ladybowerinn.com).

**Hope to Bamford**: An alternative variant of [Hope to Hathersage or Bamford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hope-to-hathersage-or-bamford-via-castleton/). Limestone and gritstone uplands, pastures with panoramic views, landslip area, dry limestone gorge, collapsed cave dale, upland moor, and descent to Bamford. Sights: Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, Cave Dale, Peveril Castle, and Hope Cement Works. Can be muddy. Lunch in Castleton, or Bradwell. Castleton lunch stops: the Castleton Fish & Chips, the Dolly Tearooms, the Peak District National Park Visitor Centre Café, the Three Roofs Café, the Castle, or the Bull's Head. Bradwell lunch stops: the [Ye Olde Bowling Green Inn](https://www.yeoldebowlinggreenbradwell.co.uk/), or the [Shoulder of Mutton Inn](https://www.shoulderbradwell.pub/).

**Sheffield to Bamford via Stanage Edge & Carl Wark**: A variant of [Sheffield to Bamford via Stanage Edge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sheffield-to-bamford-via-stanage-edge/). Urban streets, wooded valley, open moorland, rocky tors, gritstone edges, and valley descent. Sights: Carl Wark, Higger Tor, and Stanage Edge. Can be muddy. Lunch in Longshaw: the [Fox House Inn](http://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/yorkshire/thefoxhouselongshaw).
BAM.publicWalksS2S: **[Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bamford-to-edale-via-win-hill-and-great-ridge/)**: Steep ascent to heathery ridge, exposed moorland, gritstone escarpments, upland peat bog, and scenic ridgeline with panoramic valley views. Sights: Win Hill Pike, The Great Ridge, Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, Upper Derwent Valley reservoirs, Lose Hill, and [Old Nag's Head](http://www.the-old-nags-head.co.uk/). Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Hope: the [Cheshire Cheese Inn](http://www.thecheshirecheeseinn.co.uk/). 20 km. 6.5 hours.

Sights: Stanage Edge, Ladybower Reservoir, Derwent Edge, Hordron Edge Stone Circle, Bamford Edge, and Ashopton Viaduct. Steep descents. Sights: [Victorian General Cemetery](http://gencem.org/), [Endcliffe Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Endcliffe_Park), [Porter Valley](https://www.fopv.org.uk/), [Peak District](http://www.peakdistrict.gov.uk/), Burbage Rocks, and Stanage Edge. Can be muddy. Lunch in Fulwood, Sheffield, Ringinglow, or Longshaw. Fulwood, Sheffield lunch stop: the [Forge Dam Café](https://forgedamcafe.co.uk/).
