BAN.name: Banbury
BAN.adminWalksAll:
**[Banbury Circular via Milton Church](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/banbury-circular/)**: Gentle farmland, fields, wooded boundaries, villages, quiet lanes, and canal. Sights: St Mary's Church Bloxham and Milton Church. Map reading required. Lunch in Bodicote, Milton, or Bloxham. Bodicote lunch stops: the Horse & Jockey, or the Plough. Milton lunch stop: the [Marlstone Tavern](https://piepub.co.uk). Bloxham lunch stops: the [Elephant & Castle](https://www.hooky.co.uk/our_pubs/elephant-and-castle-in-bloxham/), the [Joiners Arms](https://thejoinersarmsbloxham.co.uk/), or the [Red Lion](https://www.the-red-lion-at-bloxham.co.uk). 17 km.

**Banbury Circular via St Michael's Church Barford St. Michael**: A longer variant of [Banbury Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/banbury-circular/). Gentle farmland, fields, wooded boundaries, market town, villages, quiet lanes, and canal. Sights: St Michael's Church Barford St. Michael, St Mary's Church Bloxham, and Market Place Deddington. Map reading required. Lunch in Bodicote, Deddington, or Bloxham. Bodicote lunch stops: the Horse & Jockey, or the Plough. Deddington lunch stops: the [Deddington Arms Hotel](http://www.deddington-arms-hotel.co.uk/), the [Red Lion](https://redliondeddington.co.uk/), or the [Unicorn Inn](https://www.unicorndeddington.co.uk/). Bloxham lunch stops: the [Elephant & Castle](https://www.hooky.co.uk/our_pubs/elephant-and-castle-in-bloxham/), the [Joiners Arms](https://thejoinersarmsbloxham.co.uk/), or the [Red Lion](https://www.the-red-lion-at-bloxham.co.uk). 29 km.

**Banbury to King's Sutton**: A longer variant of [Kings Sutton Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kings-sutton-circular/). Open fields, Cotswold villages, long views, and canal towpath from Banbury. Sights: Aynho Park, Aynho Grammar School, Kings Sutton Church, Croughton Church, and Souldern House. Lunch in Aynho, Souldern, Croughton, or Charlton. Aynho lunch stop: the [Cartwright Hotel and Restaurant](http://www.cartwright-hotel.co.uk/). Souldern lunch stop: the [Fox Inn](http://www.thefoxatsouldern.co.uk/). Croughton lunch stop: the [Blackbird Inn](https://whatpub.com/pubs/NTH/153/blackbird-croughton). Charlton lunch stop: the [Rose & Crown](http://theroseandcrowncharlton.co.uk/). 25 km.

**King's Sutton to Banbury**: A longer variant of [Kings Sutton Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kings-sutton-circular/). Open fields, Cotswold villages, long views, and finishing into Banbury. Sights: Aynho Park, Aynho Grammar School, Kings Sutton Church, Croughton Church, and Souldern House. Lunch in Aynho, Souldern, Croughton, or Charlton. Aynho lunch stop: the [Cartwright Hotel and Restaurant](http://www.cartwright-hotel.co.uk/). Souldern lunch stop: the [Fox Inn](http://www.thefoxatsouldern.co.uk/). Croughton lunch stop: the [Blackbird Inn](https://whatpub.com/pubs/NTH/153/blackbird-croughton). Charlton lunch stop: the [Rose & Crown](http://theroseandcrowncharlton.co.uk/). 23 km.
BAN.publicWalksCircular: **[Banbury Circular via Milton Church](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/banbury-circular/)**: Gentle farmland, fields, wooded boundaries, villages, quiet lanes, and canal. Sights: St Mary's Church Bloxham and Milton Church. Map reading required. Lunch in Bodicote, Milton, or Bloxham. Bodicote lunch stops: the Horse & Jockey, or the Plough. Milton lunch stop: the [Marlstone Tavern](https://piepub.co.uk). Bloxham lunch stops: the [Elephant & Castle](https://www.hooky.co.uk/our_pubs/elephant-and-castle-in-bloxham/), the [Joiners Arms](https://thejoinersarmsbloxham.co.uk/), or the [Red Lion](https://www.the-red-lion-at-bloxham.co.uk). 17 km.

BAT.name: Battle
BAT.adminWalksAll:
**Pevensey to Battle**: An essential walk! Sights: [Battle Abbey](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/1066-battle-of-hastings-abbey-and-battlefield/) (the site of the Battle of Hastings, upon which William the Conqeuror built his mighty Battle Abbey in papally-imposed contrition for the blood he had shed). From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 1 May 2023.* 28 km. 350 m uphill. 7.5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920345301)**

**[Battle Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/battle-circular/)**: Rolling Wealden countryside, fields, woods, and the historic town of Battle. Sights: Battle Abbey. Lunch in Catsfield, or Netherfield. Catsfield lunch stop: the [White Hart](https://whitehartcatsfield.co.uk/). Netherfield lunch stop: the [White Hart](https://www.white-hart-netherfield.co.uk/). 19 km. 5.5 hours.

**Crowhurst to Battle**: Gentle farmland, woodlands, golf course, parkland, riverside paths alongside streams and the River Brede, and historic town centre. Sights: Parish Church of St George, Crowhurst Manor House, St John the Baptist Church, Pestalozzi International Village, Battle Abbey, Battle of Hastings site, Parish Church of St Mary the Virgin, and Bayeux Cottage. Can be muddy. Lunch in Westfield: the New Inn. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 20 km. 6 hours.

**Robertsbridge to Battle via Battle**: Linear southerly walk, rolling High Weald countryside, and historic town finish. Sights: Battle. Lunch in Staplecross, or Seddlescombe. Staplecross lunch stop: the [Cross Inn](http://www.thecrossinn.com/). Seddlescombe lunch stop: the [Queens Head](http://www.thequeensheadsedlescombe.com/). An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 17 km. 5.5 hours.
BAT.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Pevensey to Battle**: An essential walk! Sights: [Battle Abbey](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/1066-battle-of-hastings-abbey-and-battlefield/) (the site of the Battle of Hastings, upon which William the Conqeuror built his mighty Battle Abbey in papally-imposed contrition for the blood he had shed). From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 28 km. 350 m uphill. 7.5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920345301)**

BAW.name: Blackwater
BAW.adminWalksAll:
**[Blackwater to Wokingham via Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackwater-to-wokingham/)**: River meadows, gravel-pit lakes, nature reserves, country lanes, mixed and pine woodland, heathland, golf course, soft fruit farms, and historic market town. Sights: Shepherd Meadows Nature Reserve, [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), Ambarrow Court Local Nature Reserve, Wellington College, Heathlake Nature Reserve, [Wokingham](https://www.wokinghamsociety.org.uk/history.html), St James's Church, Wellingtonia Avenue, Devil's Highway, Wokingham Town Hall, Tudor House, Rose Street, Wokingham railway footbridge, and Ludgrove School. Flood risk in places. Lunch in Finchampstead: the [Queen's Oak](https://www.thequeensoak.com), or the [Greyhound](https://greyhoundfinchampstead.co.uk). 25 km. 6 hours.

**Blackwater to Crowthorne**: A shorter variant of [Blackwater to Wokingham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackwater-to-wokingham/). River meadows, gravel-pit lakes, nature reserves, country lanes, mixed woodland, heathland, and pine woods alongside the railway. Sights: Shepherd Meadows Nature Reserve, [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), Ambarrow Court Local Nature Reserve, Wellington College, St James's Church, and Wellingtonia Avenue. Flood risk in places. Lunch in Finchampstead: the [Queen's Oak](https://www.thequeensoak.com), or the [Greyhound](https://greyhoundfinchampstead.co.uk). 17 km. 4 hours.

**Blackwater to Wokingham via Shepherd Meadows Nature Reserve**: An alternative variant of [Blackwater to Wokingham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackwater-to-wokingham/). River meadows, Horseshoe Lake circuit, fields, pine woodland, heathland, golf course, soft fruit farms, and market town. Sights: Shepherd Meadows Nature Reserve, [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), Ambarrow Court Local Nature Reserve, Wellington College, Heathlake Nature Reserve, [Wokingham](https://www.wokinghamsociety.org.uk/history.html), Wokingham Town Hall, Tudor House, Rose Street, and Wokingham railway footbridge. Flood risk in places. Lunch in Horseshoe Lake: the [Lake View Café](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk/lakeviewcafe). 17 km. 4 hours.

**[Farnham to Blackwater](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farnham-to-blackwater/)**: Rambler favourite! Deer park, heathland, MOD training area, Iron Age hillfort, reservoirs, stream valleys, woodland, nature reserve, wet meadows, lakeside sandy shores, and common. Sights: Caesar's Camp, Fleet Pond Nature Reserve, Farnham Park, and Foxlease Meadows. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Church Crookham: the [Foresters](https://www.foresters-dining.co.uk/). 23 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Crowthorne to Blackwater](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/crowthorne-to-blackwater-via-eversley-cross/)**: Pine woods, heathland, lakes and flooded gravel pits, riverside meadows, and green belt nature reserves. Sights: [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), Shepherd Meadows Nature Reserve, Wellingtonia Avenue, Martineau Cottages, Devil's Highway, Three Castles Path, and Blackwater Valley Path. Flood risk in places. Lunch in Eversley Cross: the [Frog and Wicket](https://www.thefrogandwicket.co.uk), or the [Chequers](https://www.thechequerseversleycross.co.uk). 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**Aldershot to Blackwater**: An alternative variant of [Farnham to Blackwater](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farnham-to-blackwater/). Rambler favourite! Urban parks, country park, copse, MOD heathland, Iron Age hillfort, reservoirs, stream valleys, woodland, nature reserve, wet meadows, and lakeside. Sights: Caesar's Camp, Aldershot Manor, and Rowhill Copse. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Church Crookham: the [Foresters](https://www.foresters-dining.co.uk/).
BAW.publicWalksS2S: **[Blackwater to Wokingham via Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackwater-to-wokingham/)**: River meadows, gravel-pit lakes, nature reserves, country lanes, mixed and pine woodland, heathland, golf course, soft fruit farms, and historic market town. Sights: Shepherd Meadows Nature Reserve, [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), Ambarrow Court Local Nature Reserve, Wellington College, Heathlake Nature Reserve, [Wokingham](https://www.wokinghamsociety.org.uk/history.html), St James's Church, Wellingtonia Avenue, Devil's Highway, Wokingham Town Hall, Tudor House, Rose Street, Wokingham railway footbridge, and Ludgrove School. Flood risk in places. Lunch in Finchampstead: the [Queen's Oak](https://www.thequeensoak.com), or the [Greyhound](https://greyhoundfinchampstead.co.uk). 25 km. 6 hours.
BAW.publicWalksS2SEnding:
**[Farnham to Blackwater](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farnham-to-blackwater/)**: Rambler favourite! Deer park, heathland, MOD training area, Iron Age hillfort, reservoirs, stream valleys, woodland, nature reserve, wet meadows, lakeside sandy shores, and common. Sights: Caesar's Camp, Fleet Pond Nature Reserve, Farnham Park, and Foxlease Meadows. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Church Crookham: the [Foresters](https://www.foresters-dining.co.uk/). 23 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Crowthorne to Blackwater](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/crowthorne-to-blackwater-via-eversley-cross/)**: Pine woods, heathland, lakes and flooded gravel pits, riverside meadows, and green belt nature reserves. Sights: [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), Shepherd Meadows Nature Reserve, Wellingtonia Avenue, Martineau Cottages, Devil's Highway, Three Castles Path, and Blackwater Valley Path. Flood risk in places. Lunch in Eversley Cross: the [Frog and Wicket](https://www.thefrogandwicket.co.uk), or the [Chequers](https://www.thechequerseversleycross.co.uk). 16 km. 3.5 hours.

BAY.name: Bayford
BAY.adminWalksAll: **Broxbourne to Bayford**: An alternative variant of [Broxbourne Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/broxbourne-circular/). Canal towpath, parkland, Spital Brook valley, ancient diverse woodland, streams, boardwalks, and green lanes. Sights: New River, Broxbourne Woods National Nature Reserve, Broxbourne Pumping Station, Wormley Wood, and Cowheath Wood. Can be muddy. Lunch in Wormley West End: the [Woodman & Olive](http://www.thewoodmanandolive.com/). 18 km.
BAY.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Broxbourne to Bayford**: An alternative variant of [Broxbourne Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/broxbourne-circular/). Canal towpath, parkland, Spital Brook valley, ancient diverse woodland, streams, boardwalks, and green lanes. Sights: New River, Broxbourne Woods National Nature Reserve, Broxbourne Pumping Station, Wormley Wood, and Cowheath Wood. Can be muddy. Lunch in Wormley West End: the [Woodman & Olive](http://www.thewoodmanandolive.com/). 18 km.

BCF.name: Beaconsfield
BCF.adminWalksAll:
**Beaconsfield Circular via Chalfont St Giles**: Gently rolling wooded hills, open fields, Buckinghamshire countryside, parkland, and golf course. Sights: Bekonscot Model Village and Railway, Parish Church of St Michael and All Angels, Hodgemoor Woods, Milton's Cottage, Parish Church of Chalfont St Giles, Friends Meeting House, Quaker Burial Ground, and Mayflower Barn. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chalfont St Giles, or Three Households. Chalfont St Giles lunch stops: the Merlins Cave, the Deli on the Green, the Feathers pub, the Crown Coffee, or the Milton's Head. Three Households lunch stop: the White Hart Inn. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 19 km. 160 m uphill. 5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920801358)**

**Beaconsfield to Chorleywood via Chiltern Open Air Museum**: Wooded hills, open fields, Chiltern countryside, historic museum grounds, and residential lanes. Sights: [Chiltern Open Air Museum](http://www.coam.org.uk/), Milton's Cottage, and Friends Meeting House. Lunch in Chalfont St Giles: the Merlins Cave. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 15 km.

**Beaconsfield to Seer Green and Jordans**: Gently rolling wooded hills, open fields, and Buckinghamshire countryside. Sights: Bekonscot Model Village and Railway, Hodgemoor Woods, Milton's Cottage, Friends Meeting House, Quaker Burial Ground, and Mayflower Barn. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chalfont St Giles, or Three Households. Chalfont St Giles lunch stops: the Merlins Cave, the Deli on the Green, or the Feathers pub. Three Households lunch stop: the White Hart Inn. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).
BCF.publicWalksCircular: **Beaconsfield Circular via Chalfont St Giles**: Gently rolling wooded hills, open fields, Buckinghamshire countryside, parkland, and golf course. Sights: Bekonscot Model Village and Railway, Parish Church of St Michael and All Angels, Hodgemoor Woods, Milton's Cottage, Parish Church of Chalfont St Giles, Friends Meeting House, Quaker Burial Ground, and Mayflower Barn. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chalfont St Giles, or Three Households. Chalfont St Giles lunch stops: the Merlins Cave, the Deli on the Green, the Feathers pub, the Crown Coffee, or the Milton's Head. Three Households lunch stop: the White Hart Inn. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 19 km. 160 m uphill. 5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920801358)**

BCH.name: Birchington-on-Sea
BCH.adminWalksAll:
**[Birchington-on-Sea to Herne Bay](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/birchington-to-herne-bay/)**: Entirely flat coastal walk; seafront promenade, raised sea wall, remote shingle beach, grassy clifftop slopes, and a seaside resort. Sights: [Reculver Towers and Roman Fort](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reculver), Herne Bay Pier, Kentish Flats offshore windfarm, and Maunsell Forts. Lunch in Reculver: the [King Ethelbert Inn](https://www.kingethelbertinn.co.uk/), or the [HatHats Coffee Company](https://hathats.co.uk/pages/reculver). 14 km. 5 hours.

**Birchington-on-Sea to Whitstable**: A longer variant of [Birchington to Herne Bay](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/birchington-to-herne-bay/). Flat coastal promenade and sea wall; shingle beach, beach huts, caravan-park front, residential clifftops, and seaside towns. Sights: [Reculver Towers and Roman Fort](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reculver), Long Rock nature reserve, and Herne Bay Pier. Lunch in Reculver: the [King Ethelbert Inn](https://www.kingethelbertinn.co.uk/), or the [HatHats Coffee Company](https://hathats.co.uk/pages/reculver). 21 km.
BCH.publicWalksS2S: **[Birchington-on-Sea to Herne Bay](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/birchington-to-herne-bay/)**: Entirely flat coastal walk; seafront promenade, raised sea wall, remote shingle beach, grassy clifftop slopes, and a seaside resort. Sights: [Reculver Towers and Roman Fort](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reculver), Herne Bay Pier, Kentish Flats offshore windfarm, and Maunsell Forts. Lunch in Reculver: the [King Ethelbert Inn](https://www.kingethelbertinn.co.uk/), or the [HatHats Coffee Company](https://hathats.co.uk/pages/reculver). 14 km. 5 hours.

BCU.name: Brockenhurst
BCU.adminWalksAll:
**Lymington to Brockenhurst via Roydon Woods**: Can be EXTREME mud in Lymington Reeds Beds Nature Reserve. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 3 February 2024.* 12 km. 90 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921509210)**

**[Brockenhurst Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/brockenhurst-circular/)**: Level forest walking, dense woodland cycle-ways, open lawns, and inclosures. Can be muddy. Lunch in Bank: the Oak Inn. 16 km. 4 hours.

**[Brockenhurst to Lymington Town via Lymington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/brockenhurst-to-lymington/)**: Landscaped parkland, mixed woodland, open heathland, farm fields, coastal saltmarsh embankments, and sailing town. Sights: New Zealand Cemetery, St Nicholas Church, Brockenhurst Park, [Roydon Woods Nature Reserve](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/roydon-woods-nature-reserve), Roydon Manor, Shirley Holms, [Lymington–Keyhaven Marshes](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/lymingtonkeyhaven), [Lymington Sea Water Baths](https://www.lymingtonseawaterbaths.org.uk), [Lymington](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lymington), Salt Boiling Houses, Moses Dock, Lymington Yacht Haven, and Royal Lymington Yacht Club. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chequers Green: the [Chequers Inn](http://www.chequersinnlymington.com). 20 km. 4.5 hours.

**Brockenhurst to Lymington Town via Hurst Castle**: A longer variant of [Brockenhurst to Lymington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/brockenhurst-to-lymington/). Landscaped parkland, woodland, heathland, farm fields, coastal marshes, shingle spit, and saltmarsh embankments. Sights: New Zealand Cemetery, St Nicholas Church, Brockenhurst Park, [Roydon Woods Nature Reserve](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/roydon-woods-nature-reserve), Roydon Manor, Shirley Holms, [Hurst Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/hurst-castle), Hurst Point Lighthouse, Fort Albert, Keyhaven Bird Observatory, The Pans & Avon Water Wetlands, [Lymington–Keyhaven Marshes](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/lymingtonkeyhaven), Salt Boiling Houses, Moses Dock, [Lymington Sea Water Baths](https://www.lymingtonseawaterbaths.org.uk), and Lymington Yacht Haven. Can be muddy. Lunch in Keyhaven: the [Gun Inn](https://www.theguninn.co.uk). 25 km. 5.5 hours.
BCU.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Lymington to Brockenhurst via Roydon Woods**: Can be EXTREME mud in Lymington Reeds Beds Nature Reserve. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 12 km. 90 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921509210)**

BDK.name: Baldock
BDK.adminWalksAll: **[Baldock Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/baldock-circular/)**: Vast flat Hertfordshire arable farmland, open field edges, big skies, gentle hills, small historic villages, and patches of woodland. Sights: St Mary's Church, Ashwell, Arbury Banks, Ashwell Museum, St Mary Magdalene Church, Caldecote, St Vincent's Church, Newnham, and Ivel Springs Local Nature Reserve. Can be muddy. Lunch in Ashwell: the Rose and Crown pub, the Bushel & Strike pub, or the Three Tuns Hotel. 19 km. 5 hours.
BDK.publicWalksCircular: **[Baldock Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/baldock-circular/)**: Vast flat Hertfordshire arable farmland, open field edges, big skies, gentle hills, small historic villages, and patches of woodland. Sights: St Mary's Church, Ashwell, Arbury Banks, Ashwell Museum, St Mary Magdalene Church, Caldecote, St Vincent's Church, Newnham, and Ivel Springs Local Nature Reserve. Can be muddy. Lunch in Ashwell: the Rose and Crown pub, the Bushel & Strike pub, or the Three Tuns Hotel. 19 km. 5 hours.

BDM.name: Bedford
BDM.adminWalksAll:
**[Bedford Circular via Stevington Cross](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bedford-circular/)**: Largely flat riverside paths, fields, woods, parks, and pedestrianised town centre. Sights: Bromham Bridge, Bromham Mill, Stevington Cross, Stevington Windmill, St Mary the Virgin Church, St Peter's Church, Bedford Castle, St Paul's Church, John Bunyan Statue, Bedford Park, and Bedford School. Can be muddy. Lunch in Bromham, Stevington, Pavenham, Oakley, or Clapham. Bromham lunch stops: the [Bromham Mill coffee shop](https://themillcoffeeshop.co.uk/), or the [Swan](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/bedfordshire/swan). Stevington lunch stop: the [Royal George](https://theroyalgeorgestevington.co.uk/). Pavenham lunch stop: the [Cock Inn](https://www.thecockatpavenham.co.uk/). Oakley lunch stop: the [Bedford Arms](https://bedfordarmsoakley.co.uk/). Clapham lunch stop: the [Fox and Hounds](https://www.foxandhoundsclapham.co.uk/index). 26 km.

**Bedford Circular via Bromham Bridge**: A shorter variant of [Bedford Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bedford-circular/). Flat riverside paths, old railway track, fields, and road sections. Sights: Bromham Bridge, Bromham Mill, Stevington Windmill, and John Bunyan Statue. Largely on road. Lunch in Bromham, or Clapham. Bromham lunch stops: the [Bromham Mill coffee shop](https://themillcoffeeshop.co.uk/), or the [Swan](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/bedfordshire/swan). Clapham lunch stop: the [Fox and Hounds](https://www.foxandhoundsclapham.co.uk/index). 20 km.
BDM.publicWalksCircular: **[Bedford Circular via Stevington Cross](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bedford-circular/)**: Largely flat riverside paths, fields, woods, parks, and pedestrianised town centre. Sights: Bromham Bridge, Bromham Mill, Stevington Cross, Stevington Windmill, St Mary the Virgin Church, St Peter's Church, Bedford Castle, St Paul's Church, John Bunyan Statue, Bedford Park, and Bedford School. Can be muddy. Lunch in Bromham, Stevington, Pavenham, Oakley, or Clapham. Bromham lunch stops: the [Bromham Mill coffee shop](https://themillcoffeeshop.co.uk/), or the [Swan](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/bedfordshire/swan). Stevington lunch stop: the [Royal George](https://theroyalgeorgestevington.co.uk/). Pavenham lunch stop: the [Cock Inn](https://www.thecockatpavenham.co.uk/). Oakley lunch stop: the [Bedford Arms](https://bedfordarmsoakley.co.uk/). Clapham lunch stop: the [Fox and Hounds](https://www.foxandhoundsclapham.co.uk/index). 26 km.

BDW.name: Bedwyn
BDW.adminWalksAll:
**[Bedwyn Circular via Savernake Forest and Marlborough](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bedwyn-circular-via-savernake-forest-and-marlborough/)**: Ancient forest with veteran oaks and beeches, chalk plateau, rolling downland, dry and wet valleys, undulating pastures, landscaped parkland, river meadows, dismantled railway line, and historic market town. Sights: Savernake Forest, Tottenham Park, Chisbury Camp, St. Martin's Chapel, Ailesbury Column, King of Limbs, Cathedral Oak, Big Belly Oak, Original Queen Oak, and Marlborough High Street. Can be muddy. Lunch in Marlborough: the Numerous options along Marlborough's High Street. 31 km. 7 hours.

**Kintbury to Bedwyn via Bedwyn Brail Roman Villa**: Quiet woods and pastures, long open ridge with panoramic views, forest walk, and canalside village. Sights: Inkpen Hill, St Mary's Great Bedwyn, Kennet and Avon Canal, and Bedwyn Brail Roman Villa. Can be muddy. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 21 km. 6.5 hours.

**Kintbury to Bedwyn via Inkpen Hill**: Quiet woods and pastures and ridge walk shortened by descending to Shalbourne valley earlier. Sights: Inkpen Hill, St Mary's Great Bedwyn, and Kennet and Avon Canal. Can be muddy. Lunch in Shalbourne: the [Plough Inn](http://www.ploughshalbourne.co.uk/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km.
BDW.publicWalksCircular: **[Bedwyn Circular via Savernake Forest and Marlborough](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bedwyn-circular-via-savernake-forest-and-marlborough/)**: Ancient forest with veteran oaks and beeches, chalk plateau, rolling downland, dry and wet valleys, undulating pastures, landscaped parkland, river meadows, dismantled railway line, and historic market town. Sights: Savernake Forest, Tottenham Park, Chisbury Camp, St. Martin's Chapel, Ailesbury Column, King of Limbs, Cathedral Oak, Big Belly Oak, Original Queen Oak, and Marlborough High Street. Can be muddy. Lunch in Marlborough: the Numerous options along Marlborough's High Street. 31 km. 7 hours.
BDW.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Kintbury to Bedwyn via Bedwyn Brail Roman Villa**: Quiet woods and pastures, long open ridge with panoramic views, forest walk, and canalside village. Sights: Inkpen Hill, St Mary's Great Bedwyn, Kennet and Avon Canal, and Bedwyn Brail Roman Villa. Can be muddy. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 21 km. 6.5 hours.

BEF.name: Benfleet
BEF.adminWalksAll:
**[Benfleet Circular via Leigh on Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/benfleet-circular-via-leigh-on-sea/)**: Hilly woodland, narrow grassy paths, open estuary waterscape, wide flat creek-side track, and gentrifying seaside town. Sights: Hadleigh Castle, Two Tree Island, and Benfleet Creek. Can be muddy. Best in May–Oct. Lunch in Leigh-on-Sea: the Crooked Billet, or the Osborne Bros. 15 km.

**[Benfleet Circular via Canvey Island](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/benfleet-circular-via-canvey-island/)**: Flat seawall path, tidal mudflats, salt marshes, grassy marshes, sandy beaches, industrial waterfront, tidal creeks, and oil terminals. Sights: Hadleigh Castle, Labworth Beach Café, [Lobster Smack](https://www.lobstersmackcanveyisland.co.uk/), Concord Beach Paddling Pool, Dr. Feelgood seawall murals, and Canvey Point. Check tide timings. Lunch in Canvey Island: the Labworth Beach Café, the Street & Co Coffee Shop, or the [Lobster Smack](https://www.lobstersmackcanveyisland.co.uk/). 23 km. 5 hours.

**Benfleet to Leigh-on-Sea via Leigh on Sea & Hadleigh Castle**: A shorter variant of [Benfleet Circular via Leigh on Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/benfleet-circular-via-leigh-on-sea/). Hilly woodland, narrow grassy paths, Hadleigh Country Park, and estuary views. Sights: Hadleigh Castle. Can be muddy. Best in May–Oct. Lunch in Leigh-on-Sea: the Crooked Billet, or the Osborne Bros. 8 km.

**Benfleet to Leigh-on-Sea via Leigh on Sea & Benfleet Creek**: An alternative variant of [Benfleet Circular via Leigh on Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/benfleet-circular-via-leigh-on-sea/). Completely flat estuary path along Benfleet Creek shoreline. Sights: Benfleet Creek. Lunch in Leigh-on-Sea: the Crooked Billet. 6 km.
BEF.publicWalksCircular:
**[Benfleet Circular via Leigh on Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/benfleet-circular-via-leigh-on-sea/)**: Hilly woodland, narrow grassy paths, open estuary waterscape, wide flat creek-side track, and gentrifying seaside town. Sights: Hadleigh Castle, Two Tree Island, and Benfleet Creek. Can be muddy. Best in May–Oct. Lunch in Leigh-on-Sea: the Crooked Billet, or the Osborne Bros. 15 km.

**[Benfleet Circular via Canvey Island](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/benfleet-circular-via-canvey-island/)**: Flat seawall path, tidal mudflats, salt marshes, grassy marshes, sandy beaches, industrial waterfront, tidal creeks, and oil terminals. Sights: Hadleigh Castle, Labworth Beach Café, [Lobster Smack](https://www.lobstersmackcanveyisland.co.uk/), Concord Beach Paddling Pool, Dr. Feelgood seawall murals, and Canvey Point. Check tide timings. Lunch in Canvey Island: the Labworth Beach Café, the Street & Co Coffee Shop, or the [Lobster Smack](https://www.lobstersmackcanveyisland.co.uk/). 23 km. 5 hours.

BEG.name: Beltring
BEG.adminWalksAll: **Yalding to Beltring**: A longer variant of [Yalding Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-circular/). Flat riverside paths, farmland, woods, and a pretty stretch of river Medway. Sights: [Teston Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teston_Bridge), [Nettlestead Church](http://nettlesteadchurch.co.uk/), [Teston Bridge Country Park](http://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/teston-bridge-country-park), and [Tea Pot Island](http://www.teapotisland.co.uk/). Busy A-road crossing. Lunch in West Farleigh, or East Peckham. West Farleigh lunch stop: the [Tickled Trout](http://www.thetickledtrout.com/). East Peckham lunch stop: the [Man of Kent](http://themanofkentpub.com/). 20 km.
BEG.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Yalding to Beltring**: A longer variant of [Yalding Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-circular/). Flat riverside paths, farmland, woods, and a pretty stretch of river Medway. Sights: [Teston Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teston_Bridge), [Nettlestead Church](http://nettlesteadchurch.co.uk/), [Teston Bridge Country Park](http://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/teston-bridge-country-park), and [Tea Pot Island](http://www.teapotisland.co.uk/). Busy A-road crossing. Lunch in West Farleigh, or East Peckham. West Farleigh lunch stop: the [Tickled Trout](http://www.thetickledtrout.com/). East Peckham lunch stop: the [Man of Kent](http://themanofkentpub.com/). 20 km.

BEH.name: Bedworth
BEH.adminWalksAll: **[Bedworth Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-831)**: Market town, field paths, Coventry Canal towpath, Ashby Canal towpath, village streets, suburban roads, and woodland edge. Sights: [Coventry Canal](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Marston Junction](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [St James Church](https://www.stjameschurchbulkington.org.uk), [Miners Welfare Park](https://www.facebook.com/bedworthcommunitygardens/), and Weston Hall Hotel. Can be muddy. Lunch in Bedworth, or Bulkington. Bedworth lunch stops: the Harry's Bakery, or the Latte Artist Coffee Shop. Bulkington lunch stops: the [Fishnet](https://www.thefishnetbulkington.com), the Village Café, or the [Weavers Arms](https://www.facebook.com/theweaversbulko/). 10 km. 3 hours.
BEH.publicWalksCircular: **[Bedworth Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-831)**: Market town, field paths, Coventry Canal towpath, Ashby Canal towpath, village streets, suburban roads, and woodland edge. Sights: [Coventry Canal](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Marston Junction](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [St James Church](https://www.stjameschurchbulkington.org.uk), [Miners Welfare Park](https://www.facebook.com/bedworthcommunitygardens/), and Weston Hall Hotel. Can be muddy. Lunch in Bedworth, or Bulkington. Bedworth lunch stops: the Harry's Bakery, or the Latte Artist Coffee Shop. Bulkington lunch stops: the [Fishnet](https://www.thefishnetbulkington.com), the Village Café, or the [Weavers Arms](https://www.facebook.com/theweaversbulko/). 10 km. 3 hours.

BEN.name: Bentham
BEN.adminWalksAll: **Ribblehead to Bentham**: An alternative variant of [Ingleborough via Ingleton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ingleborough-via-ingleton/). High moorland, steep mountain ascent, exposed plateau, and largely flat finish from Ingleton to Bentham. Sights: Ingleborough, Ribblehead Viaduct, Park Fell, and Simon Fell. Very exposed summit. Lunch in Ingleton: the [Wheatsheaf Inn](https://www.thewheatsheaf-ingleton.co.uk/), the [Peaks & Troughs Bar & Kitchen](https://www.peakstroughs.co.uk/), the Village Kitchen, the Ingleton Chippy, the Ingleton Pizza, the G&T Café and Kitchen, or the Three Horse Shoes. 17 km.
BEN.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Ribblehead to Bentham**: An alternative variant of [Ingleborough via Ingleton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ingleborough-via-ingleton/). High moorland, steep mountain ascent, exposed plateau, and largely flat finish from Ingleton to Bentham. Sights: Ingleborough, Ribblehead Viaduct, Park Fell, and Simon Fell. Very exposed summit. Lunch in Ingleton: the [Wheatsheaf Inn](https://www.thewheatsheaf-ingleton.co.uk/), the [Peaks & Troughs Bar & Kitchen](https://www.peakstroughs.co.uk/), the Village Kitchen, the Ingleton Chippy, the Ingleton Pizza, the G&T Café and Kitchen, or the Three Horse Shoes. 17 km.

BEP.name: Bermuda Park
BEP.adminWalksAll: **[Bermuda Park Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-814)**: Canal towpath, field paths, and suburban town. Sights: [Coventry Canal](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Marston Junction](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), Chilvers Coton Craft Centre, and [Bedworth Heritage Centre](https://www.bedworth-society.co.uk/heritage-centre). Can be muddy. Lunch in Chilvers Coton, Bermuda Park, or Nuneaton. Chilvers Coton lunch stop: the [Rhubarb & Mustard Cafe](https://www.rhubarbandmustard.co.uk). Bermuda Park lunch stops: the [Middlemarch Farmhouse Inn](https://www.farmhouseinns.co.uk), or the Griff House Beefeater. Nuneaton lunch stop: the [Cornerhouse](https://www.cornerhousepubnuneaton.co.uk). 5 km. 2 hours.
BEP.publicWalksCircular: **[Bermuda Park Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-814)**: Canal towpath, field paths, and suburban town. Sights: [Coventry Canal](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Marston Junction](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), Chilvers Coton Craft Centre, and [Bedworth Heritage Centre](https://www.bedworth-society.co.uk/heritage-centre). Can be muddy. Lunch in Chilvers Coton, Bermuda Park, or Nuneaton. Chilvers Coton lunch stop: the [Rhubarb & Mustard Cafe](https://www.rhubarbandmustard.co.uk). Bermuda Park lunch stops: the [Middlemarch Farmhouse Inn](https://www.farmhouseinns.co.uk), or the Griff House Beefeater. Nuneaton lunch stop: the [Cornerhouse](https://www.cornerhousepubnuneaton.co.uk). 5 km. 2 hours.

BER.name: Bearley
BER.adminWalksAll: **[Bearley Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-813)**: Ancient villages, leafy woodland, open fields, canal aqueduct, nature reserve, and country lanes. Sights: [St Mary's Church](https://www.bearley.org/st-marys-church/), [Edstone Aqueduct](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [St Michael's Church](https://www.churchesofarden.org.uk/claverdon), [Snitterfield Bushes Nature Reserve](https://www.warwickshirewildlifetrust.org.uk), and [Warwickshire Flight Experience](https://www.warwickshireflightexperience.co.uk). Electric fence near stables. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Claverdon: the [Red Lion Claverdon](https://www.redlionclaverdon.co.uk), or the Claverdon Community Shop. 7 km. 2.5 hours.
BER.publicWalksCircular: **[Bearley Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-813)**: Ancient villages, leafy woodland, open fields, canal aqueduct, nature reserve, and country lanes. Sights: [St Mary's Church](https://www.bearley.org/st-marys-church/), [Edstone Aqueduct](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [St Michael's Church](https://www.churchesofarden.org.uk/claverdon), [Snitterfield Bushes Nature Reserve](https://www.warwickshirewildlifetrust.org.uk), and [Warwickshire Flight Experience](https://www.warwickshireflightexperience.co.uk). Electric fence near stables. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Claverdon: the [Red Lion Claverdon](https://www.redlionclaverdon.co.uk), or the Claverdon Community Shop. 7 km. 2.5 hours.

BFD.name: Brentford
BFD.adminWalksAll: **Ealing Broadway to Brentford**: A shorter variant of [Ealing Broadway Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ealing-broadway-circular/). Canal towpath, urban parks, and suburban streets ending at Brentford Station. Sights: Pitshanger Manor, Grand Union Canal, Boston Manor, and Syon House. Lunch in Syon Park, or Isleworth. Syon Park lunch stop: the Syon Park Refectory. Isleworth lunch stop: the London Apprentice.
BFD.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Ealing Broadway to Brentford**: A shorter variant of [Ealing Broadway Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ealing-broadway-circular/). Canal towpath, urban parks, and suburban streets ending at Brentford Station. Sights: Pitshanger Manor, Grand Union Canal, Boston Manor, and Syon House. Lunch in Syon Park, or Isleworth. Syon Park lunch stop: the Syon Park Refectory. Isleworth lunch stop: the London Apprentice.

BHI.name: Birmingham International
BHI.adminWalksAll: **[Birmingham International Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-675)**: Suburban paths, brook-side walking, oak woodland, wildflower meadow, parkland, and sports fields. Sights: [Alcott Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk), [Marston Green Park](https://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/Marston-Green-Park), [Aircraft Spotting (Birmingham Airport)](https://www.airportspotting.com/where-to-spot-birmingham-airport/), [National Motorcycle Museum](https://www.nationalmotorcyclemuseum.co.uk), [Sheldon Country Park](https://www.birmingham.gov.uk/sheldonpark), and National Exhibition Centre (NEC). Lunch in Marston Green: the [Marston Green Tavern](https://www.sizzlingpubs.co.uk), or the Simla Restaurant and Takeaway. 6 km. 1.5 hours.
BHI.publicWalksCircular: **[Birmingham International Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-675)**: Suburban paths, brook-side walking, oak woodland, wildflower meadow, parkland, and sports fields. Sights: [Alcott Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk), [Marston Green Park](https://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/Marston-Green-Park), [Aircraft Spotting (Birmingham Airport)](https://www.airportspotting.com/where-to-spot-birmingham-airport/), [National Motorcycle Museum](https://www.nationalmotorcyclemuseum.co.uk), [Sheldon Country Park](https://www.birmingham.gov.uk/sheldonpark), and National Exhibition Centre (NEC). Lunch in Marston Green: the [Marston Green Tavern](https://www.sizzlingpubs.co.uk), or the Simla Restaurant and Takeaway. 6 km. 1.5 hours.

BIG.name: Billingshurst
BIG.adminWalksAll:
**[Billingshurst to Amberley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/billingshurst-to-amberley/)**: Gentle countryside, steep hills, hidden valleys, bluebell woods, and South Downs crest with panoramic views. Stiff hill climb. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Nutbourne: the Rising Sun. 22 km.

**[Christ's Hospital to Billingshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/christs-hospital-to-billingshurst/)**: Very flat farmland, re-wilding estate, woods, streams, fields, and quiet lanes. Sights: [Knepp Wildlands](http://www.knepp.co.uk/), Knepp Castle, Old Knepp Castle, Shipley Windmill, St Mary the Virgin, Barnhouse Farm, and Blue Idol Quaker Meeting House. Can be muddy. Lunch in Dial Post, Dragons Green, or Whitehall near Shipley. Dial Post lunch stop: the Crown Inn. Dragons Green lunch stop: the George & Dragon. Whitehall near Shipley lunch stop: the Countryman. 27 km. 6.5 hours.
BIG.publicWalksS2S: **[Billingshurst to Amberley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/billingshurst-to-amberley/)**: Gentle countryside, steep hills, hidden valleys, bluebell woods, and South Downs crest with panoramic views. Stiff hill climb. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Nutbourne: the Rising Sun. 22 km.
BIG.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Christ's Hospital to Billingshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/christs-hospital-to-billingshurst/)**: Very flat farmland, re-wilding estate, woods, streams, fields, and quiet lanes. Sights: [Knepp Wildlands](http://www.knepp.co.uk/), Knepp Castle, Old Knepp Castle, Shipley Windmill, St Mary the Virgin, Barnhouse Farm, and Blue Idol Quaker Meeting House. Can be muddy. Lunch in Dial Post, Dragons Green, or Whitehall near Shipley. Dial Post lunch stop: the Crown Inn. Dragons Green lunch stop: the George & Dragon. Whitehall near Shipley lunch stop: the Countryman. 27 km. 6.5 hours.

BIN.name: Bingham
BIN.adminWalksAll: **[Bingham Circular via Screveton](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Village greens and countryside. Sights: Scarrington Horseshoe Pile and Car Colston village green. Lunch in Car Colston: the Cafe Velo. 12 km. 30 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/317%20Bingham.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925786627)**
BIN.publicWalksCircular: **[Bingham Circular via Screveton](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Village greens and countryside. Sights: Scarrington Horseshoe Pile and Car Colston village green. Lunch in Car Colston: the Cafe Velo. 12 km. 30 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/317%20Bingham.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925786627)**

BIP.name: Bishopstone
BIP.adminWalksAll:
**[Bishopstone Circular via Saxon Church of St Andrew](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/)**: Steep repeated climbs and descents, chalk downland, grassy ridges, vineyard tracks, valley paths, quiet village lanes, and open hilltop views to sea and Downs. Sights: Saxon Church of St Andrew, Long Burgh Barrow, St Michael and All Angels church, [Cuckmere Pilgrim Path](http://cuckmerepilgrimpath.org.uk), Bishopstone station pillbox, and Alciston Court Farm dovecote. Can be overgrown. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in Berwick: the [Cricketers' Arms](https://www.cricketersberwick.co.uk/). 21 km.

**Bishopstone Circular via Long Burgh Barrow**: A variant of [Bishopstone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/). Downland, South Downs Way, and alternative circular return via Walk 2/26. Sights: Long Burgh Barrow. Can be overgrown. 21 km.

**Bishopstone to Berwick**: A shorter variant of [Bishopstone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/). Downland climbs, South Downs Way, and descent to Berwick via Vanguard Way. Sights: Long Burgh Barrow and St Michael and All Angels church. Can be overgrown. Lunch in Berwick: the [Cricketers' Arms](https://www.cricketersberwick.co.uk/). 13 km.

**Bishopstone to Southease**: A shorter variant of [Bishopstone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/). Downland climbs, South Downs Way ridge walking, and descent to Southease. Sights: Long Burgh Barrow. 16 km.

**Bishopstone to Glynde**: A shorter variant of [Bishopstone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/). Downland climbs, South Downs Way ridge walking westward, and descent to Glynde. Sights: Long Burgh Barrow. 16 km.

**Bishopstone to Seaford via Alfriston and Bishopstone**: An alternative variant of [Seaford Circular via Alfriston and Bishopstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/seaford-circular/). Seafront, chalk downland, vineyard tracks, open ridgelines, and coastal cliff finish over Seaford Head. Sights: Seaford Head, Rathfinny Vineyard, Alfriston Church, George Inn, and Bishopstone Church. Steep coastal climbs. Lunch in Alfriston: the George Inn. 22 km.

**Bishopstone to Seaford via Saxon Church of St Andrew**: A longer variant of [Bishopstone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/). All main walk terrain plus seafront promenade into Seaford. Sights: Saxon Church of St Andrew, Long Burgh Barrow, and St Michael and All Angels church. Can be overgrown. Lunch in Berwick: the [Cricketers' Arms](https://www.cricketersberwick.co.uk/). 23 km.

**Southease to Bishopstone**: River valley, ridge-top South Downs, open downland, and pretty village. Sights: Monk's House and St Andrew's Church. Lunch in Rodmell: the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km.
BIP.publicWalksCircular: **[Bishopstone Circular via Saxon Church of St Andrew](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/)**: Steep repeated climbs and descents, chalk downland, grassy ridges, vineyard tracks, valley paths, quiet village lanes, and open hilltop views to sea and Downs. Sights: Saxon Church of St Andrew, Long Burgh Barrow, St Michael and All Angels church, [Cuckmere Pilgrim Path](http://cuckmerepilgrimpath.org.uk), Bishopstone station pillbox, and Alciston Court Farm dovecote. Can be overgrown. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in Berwick: the [Cricketers' Arms](https://www.cricketersberwick.co.uk/). 21 km.

BIS.name: Bishop's Stortford
BIS.adminWalksAll:
**Bishop’s Stortford to Sawbridgworth**: From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 22 November 2020.* 7 km. 10 m uphill. 2 hours. Easy. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921491885)**

**[Sawbridgeworth to Bishop's Stortford via St. Andrew's Church](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sawbridgeworth-to-bishops-stortford/)**: Gently rolling arable countryside, green lanes, quiet country roads, bluebell woods, river navigation towpath, and marshland nature reserves. Sights: Henry Moore Foundation, St. Andrew's Church, and Thorley Hall and Church. Lunch in Perry Green, Much Hadham, or Green Tye. Perry Green lunch stop: the [Rosey Lea Café](https://henry-moore.org/studios-and-gardens/plan-your-visit-studios-gardens/rosey-lea-cafe/). Much Hadham lunch stop: the Bull Inn. Green Tye lunch stop: the [Prince of Wales](http://www.thepow.co.uk/). 23 km. 5 hours.
BIS.publicWalksS2S: **Bishop’s Stortford to Sawbridgworth**: From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 7 km. 10 m uphill. 2 hours. Easy. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921491885)**

BIW.name: Biggleswade
BIW.adminWalksAll:
**Biggleswade Circular via Old Warden Aerodrome**: An alternative variant of [Biggleswade to Sandy](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/biggleswade-to-sandy/). Flat farmland, woodland, greensand ridge, riverside path, Iron Age hillfort site, and RSPB nature reserve. Sights: [Old Warden Aerodrome](https://oldwardenaerodrome.co.uk), [The Lodge](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/the-lodge), St Mary the Virgin, The Riddy, Captain Sir William Peel, Ivel Agricultural Motor, and Meccano Bridge. Lunch in Northill: the [Crown at Northill](https://crownnorthill.co.uk). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

**Biggleswade Circular via Shuttleworth Collection**: A longer variant of [Biggleswade to Sandy](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/biggleswade-to-sandy/). Flat farmland, grassy tracks, woodland, greensand ridge, RSPB nature reserve, riverside path, and thatched villages. Sights: [Old Warden Aerodrome](https://oldwardenaerodrome.co.uk), [Shuttleworth Collection](https://www.shuttleworth.org), [Landmark Trust properties](https://www.landmarktrust.org.uk/search-and-book/location/england/bedfordshire/#Search), Keeper's Cottage, Queen Anne's Summerhouse, Shuttleworth House, Abbey Church of St Leonard, Airship Hangars, St Mary the Virgin, [The Lodge](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/the-lodge), The Riddy, Captain Sir William Peel, Ivel Agricultural Motor, and Meccano Bridge. Airshow closures apply. Lunch in Old Warden: the [Hare & Hounds](https://www.hareandhoundsoldwarden.com). 26 km. 6 hours.

**[Biggleswade to Sandy via Shuttleworth Collection](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/biggleswade-to-sandy/)**: Flat arable farmland, grassy tracks, woodland trails, thatched villages, and low greensand ridge. Sights: [Old Warden Aerodrome](https://oldwardenaerodrome.co.uk), [Shuttleworth Collection](https://www.shuttleworth.org), [Landmark Trust properties](https://www.landmarktrust.org.uk/search-and-book/location/england/bedfordshire/#Search), [Greensand Ridge Walk](https://www.centralbedfordshire.gov.uk/info/82/countryside/434/walking/2), Keeper's Cottage, Queen Anne's Summerhouse, Shuttleworth House, Abbey Church of St Leonard, Airship Hangars, St Mary the Virgin, The Riddy, and Captain Sir William Peel. Airshow closures apply. Lunch in Old Warden: the [Hare & Hounds](https://www.hareandhoundsoldwarden.com). 18 km. 4 hours.

**Biggleswade to Sandy via Old Warden Aerodrome**: A shorter variant of [Biggleswade to Sandy](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/biggleswade-to-sandy/). Flat arable farmland, grassy bridleways, village greens, and thatched cottages. Sights: [Old Warden Aerodrome](https://oldwardenaerodrome.co.uk), St Mary the Virgin, The Riddy, and Captain Sir William Peel. Lunch in Northill: the [Crown at Northill](https://crownnorthill.co.uk). 11 km. 2.5 hours.

**Biggleswade to Arlesey**: A shorter variant of [Sandy to Arlesey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandy-to-arlesey/). Flat river meadows, conservation site, riverside paths, and gentle farmland. Sights: [Broom East Wildlife Conservation Site](https://www.birdingplaces.eu/en/birdingplaces/united-kingdom/broom-lakes), [Jordans Mill](https://jordansmill.com), [Henlow Grange](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Henlow_Grange), [Kingfisher Way](https://enjoybiggleswade.co.uk/local-walking-routes), [River Ivel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Ivel), Holme Mills, and River Hiz. Can be boggy. Lunch in Jordans Mill: the [Jordans Mill Riverside Café](https://jordansmill.com/plan-your-visit/whats-on#riverside-cafe). 11 km. 2.5 hours.

**Sandy to Biggleswade**: A shorter variant of [Sandy to Arlesey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandy-to-arlesey/). Flat river meadows, commons, heathland, woods, riverside paths, and conservation site perimeter. Sights: [The Lodge](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/the-lodge), [Broom East Wildlife Conservation Site](https://www.birdingplaces.eu/en/birdingplaces/united-kingdom/broom-lakes), [Jordans Mill](https://jordansmill.com), [Kingfisher Way](https://enjoybiggleswade.co.uk/local-walking-routes), [River Ivel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Ivel), Ivel Mill, Biggleswade Common, and Galley Hill hill fort. Can be boggy. Lunch in Jordans Mill: the [Jordans Mill Riverside Café](https://jordansmill.com/plan-your-visit/whats-on#riverside-cafe). 14 km. 3 hours.

Sights: [Old Warden Aerodrome](https://oldwardenaerodrome.co.uk), [Shuttleworth Collection](https://www.shuttleworth.org), [Landmark Trust properties](https://www.landmarktrust.org.uk/search-and-book/location/england/bedfordshire/#Search), [Greensand Ridge Walk](https://www.centralbedfordshire.gov.uk/info/82/countryside/434/walking/2), Keeper's Cottage, Queen Anne's Summerhouse, Shuttleworth House, Abbey Church of St Leonard, Airship Hangars, St Mary the Virgin, The Riddy, and Captain Sir William Peel. BKH.name: Blackheath
BKH.adminWalksAll:
**[Blackheath to Canary Wharf via Blackheath](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackheath-to-canary-wharf/)**: Leafy Georgian avenues, open heath, parkland, Royal Observatory hill, riverside Thames Path, Docklands waterfront, and financial district. Sights: [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), [Old Royal Naval College](https://www.ornc.org), [Royal Museums Greenwich](https://www.rmg.co.uk), National Maritime Museum, Queen's House, Royal Observatory, Cutty Sark, [Ranger's House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/rangers-house-the-wernher-collection), Blackheath, Painted Hall, Flamsteed House, Greenwich Meridian, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, [Canary Wharf outdoor public art](https://canarywharf.com/the-estate/art-culture/art-on-the-estate), Crossrail Place Roof Garden, One Tree Hill, SS Great Eastern site, Discover Greenwich Visitor Centre, Millennium Sundial, Vanbrugh Castle, Morden College, The Paragon, Anglo-Saxon tumuli, and Blackheath Halls. Lunch in Greenwich, or Ballast Quay, Greenwich. Greenwich lunch stops: the [Plume of Feathers](https://plumegreenwich.com), or the [Trafalgar Tavern](https://www.trafalgartavern.co.uk). Ballast Quay, Greenwich lunch stop: the [Cutty Sark](https://www.cuttysarkse10.co.uk). 12 km. 2.5 hours.

**[Blackheath to Deptford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackheath-to-deptford/)**: Urban London, genteel residential streets, Georgian and Victorian estates, large open heathland, well-kept parks, river valleys, Victorian cemeteries, and green hilltops with panoramic city views. Sights: Blackheath Cator Estate, Morden College, The Paragon, All Saints' Blackheath, Greenwich Park, St. Margaret of Antioch Church, Boone's Almshouses, Lee Manor House, Manor House Gardens, Mountsfield Park, One Tree Hill, St. Augustine's Church, Camberwell New Cemetery, Honor Oak Crematorium, Rivoli Ballroom, Brockley and Ladywell Cemetery, Hilly Fields Park, Millennium Stone Circle, Blythe Hill Fields, Telegraph Hill Upper Park, Telegraph Hill Lower Park, Deptford Market Yard, Albany Theatre, and Ladywell Fields. Lunch in Catford, Hilly Fields, or Honor Oak Park. Catford lunch stop: the Mountsfield Park Café. Hilly Fields lunch stop: the Pistachios in the Park. Honor Oak Park lunch stop: the Blythe Hill Tavern. 22 km. 5 hours.

**Blackheath to Canary Wharf via Whale on the Wharf sculpture**: An alternative variant of [Blackheath to Canary Wharf](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackheath-to-canary-wharf/). Leafy Georgian avenues, open heath, parkland, Royal Observatory hill, eastern Isle of Dogs riverside path, and Docklands waterfront. Sights: [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), [Old Royal Naval College](https://www.ornc.org), [Royal Museums Greenwich](https://www.rmg.co.uk), National Maritime Museum, Queen's House, Royal Observatory, Cutty Sark, [Ranger's House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/rangers-house-the-wernher-collection), Painted Hall, Flamsteed House, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, Crossrail Place Roof Garden, [Canary Wharf outdoor public art](https://canarywharf.com/the-estate/art-culture/art-on-the-estate), and Whale on the Wharf sculpture. Lunch in Greenwich, or Ballast Quay, Greenwich. Greenwich lunch stops: the [Plume of Feathers](https://plumegreenwich.com), or the [Trafalgar Tavern](https://www.trafalgartavern.co.uk). Ballast Quay, Greenwich lunch stop: the [Cutty Sark](https://www.cuttysarkse10.co.uk). 11 km.

**Blackheath to Canary Wharf via Mudchute Park & Farm**: A longer variant of [Blackheath to Canary Wharf](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackheath-to-canary-wharf/). Leafy Georgian avenues, open heath, parkland, Royal Observatory hill, riverside Thames Path, Docklands waterfront, Mudchute Park & Farm, and Isle of Dogs circuit. Sights: [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), [Old Royal Naval College](https://www.ornc.org), [Royal Museums Greenwich](https://www.rmg.co.uk), National Maritime Museum, Queen's House, Royal Observatory, Cutty Sark, [Ranger's House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/rangers-house-the-wernher-collection), Painted Hall, Flamsteed House, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, [Mudchute Park & Farm](https://www.mudchute.org), SS Great Eastern site, Crossrail Place Roof Garden, [Canary Wharf outdoor public art](https://canarywharf.com/the-estate/art-culture/art-on-the-estate), Millwall Railway Viaduct, and Isle of Dogs Memorial Garden. Lunch in Greenwich, or Ballast Quay, Greenwich. Greenwich lunch stops: the [Plume of Feathers](https://plumegreenwich.com), or the [Trafalgar Tavern](https://www.trafalgartavern.co.uk). Ballast Quay, Greenwich lunch stop: the [Cutty Sark](https://www.cuttysarkse10.co.uk). 18 km. 4 hours.

BKM.name: Berkhamsted
BKM.adminWalksAll:
**[Berkhamsted Circular via Hockeridge Woods](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berkhamsted-circular/)**: Canal towpath, wide bridleways, fields, woodland paths, school playing fields, and town centre. Sights: Berkhamsted Castle, Grand Union Canal, Hockeridge Woods, Pancake Wood, and River Bulbourne. Can be muddy. Lunch in Ashley Green: the [Golden Eagle](http://thegoldeneaglephchesham.com/). 15 km.

**Berkhamsted Circular via Berkhamsted Castle**: A shorter variant of [Berkhamsted Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berkhamsted-circular/). Canal towpath, wide bridleways, fields, and town centre. Sights: Berkhamsted Castle, Grand Union Canal, and River Bulbourne. Can be muddy. Lunch in Berkhamsted: the [Crystal Palace](https://www.crystalpalace-pub.co.uk/), the [Boat](https://www.boatberkhamsted.co.uk/), or the Rising Sun. 9 km.

**[Tring to Berkhamsted via Tom's Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tring-to-berkhamsted/)**: Canal towpath, ancient woodland, chalk commons, open grassy hillsides, beech and oak estate woodland, and ridge paths with town views. Sights: Grand Union Canal, Ashridge Estate, Bridgewater Monument, Ashridge Hult College, Berkhamsted WW1 Troop Training Trenches, Berkhamsted Castle, Little Gaddesden Parish Church (St Peter and St Paul), Little Gaddesden Village Green War Memorial, Frithsden Beeches, Thunderdell Lodge, and Old Park Lodge. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Little Gaddesden, Ashridge House, or Frithsden. Little Gaddesden lunch stop: the Bridgewater Arms. Ashridge House lunch stop: the Bakehouse. Frithsden lunch stop: the Alford Arms. 15 km.

**Tring to Berkhamsted via Tom's Hill & Tom's Hill House**: An alternative variant of [Tring to Berkhamsted via Tom's Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tring-to-berkhamsted/). Hertfordshire Way field paths, hedged lanes, light woodland, and ridge paths; avoids canal towpath. Sights: Ashridge Estate, Bridgewater Monument, Ashridge Hult College, Berkhamsted WW1 Troop Training Trenches, Berkhamsted Castle, Little Gaddesden Parish Church (St Peter and St Paul), Little Gaddesden Village Green War Memorial, Frithsden Beeches, and Tom's Hill House. Lunch in Little Gaddesden, or Ashridge House. Little Gaddesden lunch stop: the Bridgewater Arms. Ashridge House lunch stop: the Bakehouse. 13 km.

**Tring to Berkhamsted via Aldbury village stocks**: Direct route through Ashridge beechwoods, passing Aldbury village and Bridgewater Monument, and then open hills to Berkhamsted. Sights: Aldbury village stocks, Bridgewater Monument, Ashridge Estate, and Berkhamsted Castle. Lunch in Aldbury, or Ashridge Estate. Aldbury lunch stops: the Greyhound Inn, or the Trooper. Ashridge Estate lunch stop: the Monument Cafe. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 9 km.

**Tring to Berkhamsted via Tom's Hill & Ivinghoe Beacon**: A longer variant of [Tring to Berkhamsted via Tom's Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tring-to-berkhamsted/). Open downland, Ivinghoe Beacon ridge, farmland, ancient woodland, and chalk commons. Sights: Ivinghoe Beacon, Ashridge Estate, Bridgewater Monument, Berkhamsted WW1 Troop Training Trenches, and Berkhamsted Castle. Lunch in Little Gaddesden: the Bridgewater Arms. 18 km.

**Tring to Berkhamsted via Ivinghoe Beacon**: Ridgeway escarpment, open downland, ancient beechwoods, Ashridge Estate, open hills, and canal-side town. Sights: Ivinghoe Beacon, Ashridge Estate, Bridgewater Monument, and Berkhamsted Castle. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Little Gaddesden, or Ashridge Estate. Little Gaddesden lunch stop: the Bridgewater Arms. Ashridge Estate lunch stop: the Monument Cafe. A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 20 km.

**Tring to Berkhamsted via Hockeridge Woods**: A longer variant of [Berkhamsted Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berkhamsted-circular/). Canal towpath, wide bridleways, fields, woodland paths, and town centre. Sights: Berkhamsted Castle, Grand Union Canal, Hockeridge Woods, and River Bulbourne. Can be muddy. Lunch in Berkhamsted: the [Crystal Palace](https://www.crystalpalace-pub.co.uk/), the [Boat](https://www.boatberkhamsted.co.uk/), or the Rising Sun. 24 km.
BKM.publicWalksCircular: **[Berkhamsted Circular via Hockeridge Woods](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berkhamsted-circular/)**: Canal towpath, wide bridleways, fields, woodland paths, school playing fields, and town centre. Sights: Berkhamsted Castle, Grand Union Canal, Hockeridge Woods, Pancake Wood, and River Bulbourne. Can be muddy. Lunch in Ashley Green: the [Golden Eagle](http://thegoldeneaglephchesham.com/). 15 km.

Sights: Grand Union Canal, Ashridge Estate, Bridgewater Monument, Ashridge Hult College, Berkhamsted WW1 Troop Training Trenches, Berkhamsted Castle, Little Gaddesden Parish Church (St Peter and St Paul), Little Gaddesden Village Green War Memorial, Frithsden Beeches, Thunderdell Lodge, and Old Park Lodge. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Little Gaddesden, Ashridge House, or Frithsden. Little Gaddesden lunch stop: the Bridgewater Arms. Ashridge House lunch stop: the Bakehouse. Frithsden lunch stop: the Alford Arms. 15 km.

BKO.name: Brookwood
BKO.adminWalksAll:
**Brookwood Circular Walk**: A variant of [Ash to Brookwood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/). Cemetery grounds, farmland fields, and village lanes. Sights: [Brookwood Cemetery](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/), [Brookwood Military Cemetery](http://www.cwgc.org), American Military Cemetery, [Orthodox Church and Monastery of St Edward](http://www.saintedwardbrotherhood.org), St Michael and All Angels Church, and Henry Morton Stanley's Grave. Lunch in Pirbright: the White Hart, or the Cricketers on The Green. 8 km.

**Brookwood to Ash**: A shorter variant of [Ash to Brookwood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/). Cemetery grounds, farmland fields, lowland heathland, and firing range ridgeline. Sights: [Brookwood Cemetery](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/) and [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/reserves/9). Army range closures apply. 9 km.

**Brookwood to Ash Vale**: A shorter variant of [Ash to Brookwood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/). Cemetery grounds, heathland, and firing range ridgeline. Sights: [Brookwood Cemetery](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/) and [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/reserves/9). Army range closures apply. 8 km.

**[Ash to Brookwood via American Military Cemetery](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/)**: Lowland heathland, firing range ridgeline, open grassland, conifer and broad-leaved woods, farmland fields, and historic cemetery grounds. Sights: [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/reserves/9), St Michael and All Angels Church, Henry Morton Stanley's Grave, [Brookwood Cemetery](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/), [Brookwood Military Cemetery](http://www.cwgc.org), [Orthodox Church and Monastery of St Edward](http://www.saintedwardbrotherhood.org), American Military Cemetery, Brookwood Memorial, [Cemetery Railway remains (North and South station platforms)](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/cemetery_railway.htm), and Colquhoun Mausoleum. Army range closures apply. Lunch in Pirbright: the White Hart, or the Cricketers on The Green. 15 km.

**Ash to Brookwood via Ash Ranges**: A shorter variant of [Ash to Brookwood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/). Lowland heathland, firing range ridgeline, woods, and farmland fields. Sights: [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/reserves/9). Army range closures apply. 11 km.

**Ash Vale to Brookwood**: An alternative variant of [Ash Vale to Worplesdon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-to-worplesdon/). Basingstoke Canal towpath, MOD firing range heathland, Pirbright village, Brookwood Cemetery, and pine woodland. Sights: Basingstoke Canal, Ash Ranges, and Brookwood Cemetery. MOD closures apply. 16 km.

**Ash to Brookwood via Glades of Remembrance**: A variant of [Ash to Brookwood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/). Lowland heathland, firing range ridgeline, woods, farmland fields, and extensive cemetery grounds including neglected sections. Sights: [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/reserves/9), Colquhoun Mausoleum, Glades of Remembrance, Pelham-Clinton Memorial, Drake Family Mausoleum, [Brookwood Cemetery](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/), [Brookwood Military Cemetery](http://www.cwgc.org), [Orthodox Church and Monastery of St Edward](http://www.saintedwardbrotherhood.org), St Michael and All Angels Church, and Henry Morton Stanley's Grave. Uneven pauper burial ground. Lunch in Pirbright: the White Hart, or the Cricketers on The Green. 16 km.

**Ash to Brookwood via Basingstoke Canal**: A longer variant of [Ash to Brookwood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/). Canal towpath, heathland, woods, farmland fields, and historic cemetery grounds. Sights: Basingstoke Canal, [Brookwood Cemetery](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/), [Brookwood Military Cemetery](http://www.cwgc.org), St Michael and All Angels Church, and Henry Morton Stanley's Grave. Army range closures apply. Lunch in Ash Vale, or Pirbright. Ash Vale lunch stops: the Swan, or the Napiers. Pirbright lunch stops: the White Hart, or the Cricketers on The Green. 22 km.
BKO.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Ash to Brookwood via American Military Cemetery](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/)**: Lowland heathland, firing range ridgeline, open grassland, conifer and broad-leaved woods, farmland fields, and historic cemetery grounds. Sights: [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/reserves/9), St Michael and All Angels Church, Henry Morton Stanley's Grave, [Brookwood Cemetery](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/), [Brookwood Military Cemetery](http://www.cwgc.org), [Orthodox Church and Monastery of St Edward](http://www.saintedwardbrotherhood.org), American Military Cemetery, Brookwood Memorial, [Cemetery Railway remains (North and South station platforms)](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/cemetery_railway.htm), and Colquhoun Mausoleum. Army range closures apply. Lunch in Pirbright: the White Hart, or the Cricketers on The Green. 15 km.

Ickham lunch stop: the Duke William. Stodmarsh lunch stop: the Red Lion. 17 km.

BKW.name: Berkswell
BKW.adminWalksAll: **[Berkswell Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-853)**: Rural countryside, farmland, fields, farm driveways, quiet lanes, and village green. Sights: [St. John the Baptist](https://www.berkswellchurch.org.uk), Bercul's Well, and Rams Hall Farm. Can be muddy. Lunch in Berkswell, or Berryfields. Berkswell lunch stops: the [Store & Stove](https://www.facebook.com/Store-Stove-110291947813048/), the [Bear Inn](https://www.chefandbrewer.com), the [Brickmakers Arms](https://www.thebrickmakersberkswell.com), or the [Oakes Farm Shop](https://www.oakesfarmshop.co.uk). Berryfields lunch stop: the [Barn at Berryfields](https://www.thebarnatberryfields.co.uk). 4 km. 1.5 hours.
BKW.publicWalksCircular: **[Berkswell Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-853)**: Rural countryside, farmland, fields, farm driveways, quiet lanes, and village green. Sights: [St. John the Baptist](https://www.berkswellchurch.org.uk), Bercul's Well, and Rams Hall Farm. Can be muddy. Lunch in Berkswell, or Berryfields. Berkswell lunch stops: the [Store & Stove](https://www.facebook.com/Store-Stove-110291947813048/), the [Bear Inn](https://www.chefandbrewer.com), the [Brickmakers Arms](https://www.thebrickmakersberkswell.com), or the [Oakes Farm Shop](https://www.oakesfarmshop.co.uk). Berryfields lunch stop: the [Barn at Berryfields](https://www.thebarnatberryfields.co.uk). 4 km. 1.5 hours.

BLB.name: Battlesbridge
BLB.adminWalksAll:
**[Wickford to Battlesbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wickford-to-battlesbridge/)**: Rolling grassy fields, quiet lanes, scattered woods, reservoir nature reserve, river valley, and tidal mudflats. Sights: Hanningfield Reservoir Nature Reserve and Battlesbridge Antiques Centre. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hanningfield Reservoir, or South Hanningfield. Hanningfield Reservoir lunch stop: the [Café on the Water (& The Tackle Shop)](https://www.watersideparksuk.com/park/hanningfield/cafe-on-the-water/). South Hanningfield lunch stop: the [Old Windmill](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/oldwindmill/). 21 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Ingatestone to Battlesbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ingatestone-to-battlesbridge/)**: Wid Valley fields, riverside paths, cattle farm estate, bluebell woods, tree-lined tracks, open fields with far views, and Crouch Valley finish. Sights: Ingatestone Hall, St. Mary's Church Buttsbury, Stock Tower Mill, and Hanningfield Reservoir Nature Discovery Centre. Can be muddy. Lunch in Stock, or South Hanningfield. Stock lunch stops: the [Bakers Arms](http://www.bakersarms.co.uk/), the [Hoop](http://www.thehoop.co.uk/), the [Dandelion & Burdock Kitchen & Lounge](https://www.dandelion-burdock.co.uk/), the [Bear Inn](http://www.thebearstock.com/), or the [Harvard Inn](https://theharvardinn.co.uk/). South Hanningfield lunch stop: the [Old Windmill](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/oldwindmill/). 24 km. 5.5 hours.

South Hanningfield lunch stop: the [Old Windmill](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/oldwindmill/). 24 km. 5.5 hours.

BLL.name: Bardon Mill
BLL.adminWalksAll:
**Bardon Mill to Hexham**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). Common, upland plateau, crags, scenic Wall, gentle descent into North Tyne Valley, riverside, and country lanes. Sights: Housesteads Fort, Sycamore Gap, Carrowburgh Fort, Mithraeum, Black Carts Turret, Chesters Fort, Brunton Turret, and Vindolanda Roman Fort. Lunch in Housesteads, Chesters, or Chollerford. Housesteads lunch stop: the Housesteads Fort Café. Chesters lunch stop: the Chesters Fort Tearoom. Chollerford lunch stops: the Riverside Kitchen Café, or the George Hotel. 28 km.

**Haltwhistle to Bardon Mill**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). Burn gorge, upland plateau, Roman military zone, crags, Sycamore Gap, lough, and open moorland. Sights: Haltwhistle Burn Roman Fortlet, Great Chesters Fort, Sycamore Gap, Crag Lough, and Housesteads Fort. Lunch in Walltown Quarry, near Haltwhistle, near Steel Rigg, or Housesteads. Walltown Quarry lunch stop: the Roman Army Museum Tea Room. near Haltwhistle lunch stop: the Milecastle Inn. near Steel Rigg lunch stops: the Twice Brewed Inn, or the Once Brewed Coffee and Bakehouse at The Sill. Housesteads lunch stop: the Housesteads Fort Café. 18 km.

Sights: Housesteads Fort, Sycamore Gap, Carrowburgh Fort, Mithraeum, Black Carts Turret, Chesters Fort, Brunton Turret, and Vindolanda Roman Fort. Lunch in Housesteads, Chesters, or Chollerford. Housesteads lunch stop: the Housesteads Fort Café. Chesters lunch stop: the Chesters Fort Tearoom. Chollerford lunch stops: the Riverside Kitchen Café, or the George Hotel. 28 km.

Housesteads lunch stop: the Housesteads Fort Café. 18 km.

BLP.adminWalksAll:

**[Belper Circular via Blackbrook](https://www.visitambervalley.com/media/1102/belper-blackbrook-walking.pdf)**: Hilly countryside with steep climbs. 11 km.

BMH.name: Bournemouth
BMH.adminWalksAll: **Christchurch to Bournemouth**: A variant of [Bournemouth to Barton-on-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bournemouth-to-barton-on-sea/). Christchurch Harbour, wild coastal scenery, sand dunes, headland, promenade, and cliff-top paths. Sights: [Hengistbury Head](http://www.hengistbury-head.co.uk/), Mudeford Quay, and Boscombe Pier. Lunch in Mudeford Quay: the Haven House Inn. 12 km.
BMH.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Christchurch to Bournemouth**: A variant of [Bournemouth to Barton-on-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bournemouth-to-barton-on-sea/). Christchurch Harbour, wild coastal scenery, sand dunes, headland, promenade, and cliff-top paths. Sights: [Hengistbury Head](http://www.hengistbury-head.co.uk/), Mudeford Quay, and Boscombe Pier. Lunch in Mudeford Quay: the Haven House Inn. 12 km.

BMP.name: Brampton
BMP.adminWalksAll: **Brampton to Haltwhistle**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). Ridge, wooded gorge, river, Roman remains, quarry crags, upland moorland, and market town. Sights: Lanercost Priory, Birdoswald Fort, Willowford Bridge, Milecastle 48, Thirlwall Castle, Roman Army Museum, and Great Chesters Fort. Lunch in Haytongate, Birdoswald, Gilsland, Greenhead, or Walltown Quarry. Haytongate lunch stop: the Haytongate Farm Snack Hut. Birdoswald lunch stop: the Birdoswald Fort Café. Gilsland lunch stops: the House of Meg Café, the Samson Inn, or the Bridge Inn. Greenhead lunch stops: the Greenhead Hotel, or the Greenhead Tea Room. Walltown Quarry lunch stop: the Roman Army Museum Tea Room. 28 km.
BMP.publicWalksS2S: **Brampton to Haltwhistle**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). Ridge, wooded gorge, river, Roman remains, quarry crags, upland moorland, and market town. Sights: Lanercost Priory, Birdoswald Fort, Willowford Bridge, Milecastle 48, Thirlwall Castle, Roman Army Museum, and Great Chesters Fort. Lunch in Haytongate, Birdoswald, Gilsland, Greenhead, or Walltown Quarry. Haytongate lunch stop: the Haytongate Farm Snack Hut. Birdoswald lunch stop: the Birdoswald Fort Café. Gilsland lunch stops: the House of Meg Café, the Samson Inn, or the Bridge Inn. Greenhead lunch stops: the Greenhead Hotel, or the Greenhead Tea Room. Walltown Quarry lunch stop: the Roman Army Museum Tea Room. 28 km.

BMY.name: Bramley
BMY.privateNote: LON Trainline says 51m — Paddington via Reading
BMY.adminWalksAll: **Mortimer to Bramley via Roman Silchester**: An essential walk! Perhaps an idiosyncratic love of mine, as this isn't a popular hiking spot, but I find it exceptionally charming. This route takes you through what little remains of the once-thriving Roman city of Calleva Atrebatum, including the original walls, and a large amphitheatre where gladiatorial combats entertained up to 7,000 people at a time. Lunch in Silchester: the Cavella Arms (very nice; limited menu on Sundays). From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 17 May 2025.* 12 km. 70 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2902544251)**
BMY.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Mortimer to Bramley via Roman Silchester**: An essential walk! Perhaps an idiosyncratic love of mine, as this isn't a popular hiking spot, but I find it exceptionally charming. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 12 km. 70 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2902544251)**

BNE.name: Bourne End
BNE.adminWalksAll:
**Maidenhead to Bourne End via Maidenhead Rail Bridge**: A shorter variant of [Maidenhead to Marlow](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/maidenhead-to-marlow/). Thames riverside path all the way, flat, and passing Boulters Lock and Cookham. Sights: Maidenhead Rail Bridge, Boulters Lock, and Cliveden. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Cookham: the Bel and the Dragon, the Kings Arms, the Crown, or the Teapot Tea Shop. 9 km.

**Maidenhead to Bourne End via Winter Hill**: A longer variant of [Maidenhead to Marlow](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/maidenhead-to-marlow/). Thames riverside path, hill, town, and then riverside return to Bourne End. Sights: Maidenhead Rail Bridge, Boulters Lock, Cliveden, Winter Hill, and Marlow Bridge. Riverside can flood. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Cookham: the Bel and the Dragon, the Kings Arms, the Crown, or the Teapot Tea Shop. 19 km.
BNE.publicWalksS2SEnding:
