Lunch in Cookham: the Bel and the Dragon, the Kings Arms, the Crown, or the Teapot Tea Shop. 9 km.

**Maidenhead to Bourne End via Winter Hill**: A longer variant of [Maidenhead to Marlow](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/maidenhead-to-marlow/). Thames riverside path, hill, town, and then riverside return to Bourne End. Sights: Maidenhead Rail Bridge, Boulters Lock, Cliveden, Winter Hill, and Marlow Bridge. Riverside can flood. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Cookham: the Bel and the Dragon, the Kings Arms, the Crown, or the Teapot Tea Shop. 19 km.

BNI.name: Barnes Bridge
BNI.adminWalksAll:
**Wimbledon to Barnes Bridge**: A shorter variant of [Wimbledon to Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-putney/). Suburban streets, open common, landscaped park, woodland riverside path, Royal Park grassland, suburban roads, and Thames riverbank. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](https://cannizaropark.com/about-cannizaro-park), [Wimbledon Common](https://www.wpcc.org.uk), [Richmond Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/visit/parks/richmond-park), Millennium Fountain, Caesar's Camp Iron Age hill fort, Barnes Railway Bridge, and Ye White Hart. Can be muddy. Lunch in Richmond Park, or Barnes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Roehampton Gate café. Barnes lunch stops: the [Crossing](https://www.thecrossing-barnes.co.uk), the [Ye White Hart](https://www.whitehartbarnes.co.uk), the [Watermans Arms](https://www.watermansarms.co.uk), the [Bull's Head](https://www.thebullsheadbarnes.com), the [Coach & Horses](https://www.coachandhorsesbarnes.co.uk), or the [Sun Inn](https://www.thesuninnbarnes.co.uk). 11 km. 2.5 hours.

**Norbiton to Barnes Bridge**: A variant of [Norbiton to Hammersmith or Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/norbiton-to-hammersmith-or-putney/). Tree-lined residential streets, large deer park, woodland garden, open grassland, and riverside common. Sights: [Richmond Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond-park), Isabella Plantation, Pen Ponds, Thatched House Lodge, and White Lodge. Best in Mar–May. 10 km.

BNM.name: Burnham
BNM.adminWalksAll: **Langley to Burnham station**: A shorter variant of [West Drayton to Cookham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/west-drayton-to-cookham/). Country parks, lowland heaths, mature beech woodland, and farmland lanes. Sights: [Langley Park County Park](http://www.buckscc.gov.uk/leisure-and-culture/country-parks/langley-park/), Black Park County Park, and [Burnham Beeches](http://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/burnham-beeches-and-stoke-common/Pages/default.aspx). Lunch in Fulmer, Farnham Common, or Burnham Beeches. Fulmer lunch stop: the [Black Horse](http://www.theblackhorsefulmer.co.uk/). Farnham Common lunch stop: the [Forresters](http://www.theforesterspub.com/). Burnham Beeches lunch stop: the Beeches Cafe. 18 km.
BNM.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Langley to Burnham station**: A shorter variant of [West Drayton to Cookham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/west-drayton-to-cookham/). Farnham Common lunch stop: the [Forresters](http://www.theforesterspub.com/). Burnham Beeches lunch stop: the Beeches Cafe. 18 km.

BNS.name: Barnes
BNS.adminWalksAll:
**Barnes to Putney Bridge via Chiswick House & Gardens**: A variant of [Norbiton to Hammersmith or Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/norbiton-to-hammersmith-or-putney/). Village green, Thames riverfront, historic riverside houses, landscape garden, modern riverside developments, riverside park, and palace grounds. Sights: [Chiswick House & Gardens](https://chiswickhouseandgardens.org.uk), Hogarth Tomb-Monument, Craven Cottage, [Fulham Palace](https://www.fulhampalace.org), and [London Wetland Centre](https://www.wwt.org.uk/wetland-centres/london). Lunch in Barnes: the [Sun Inn](https://www.thesuninnbarnes.co.uk), the [Coach & Horses](https://www.coachandhorsesbarnes.co.uk), the [Bulls Head](https://www.thebullsheadbarnes.com), or the [Watermans Arms](https://www.watermansarms.co.uk). 10 km.

**Barnes to Putney Bridge via Barnes Common**: A shorter variant of [Beverley Brook](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/beverley-brook/). Commons, brookside paths, and Thames-side esplanade. Sights: Barnes Common and Barn Elms. Lunch in Barnes: the Café Loris, or the [Stag's Head](https://www.stagsheadbarnes.co.uk/). 4 km.

**New Malden to Barnes**: A shorter variant of [Beverley Brook](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/beverley-brook/). Parkland, commons, brookside paths, and wet woodland. Sights: Wimbledon Common, Richmond Park, and Barnes Common. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Wimbledon, London SW15, East Sheen, or Barnes. West Wimbledon lunch stop: the [Da Mimmo](https://www.damimmowimbledon.com/). London SW15 lunch stop: the [Roehampton Gate Café](https://www.colicci.co.uk/roehampton-gate-cafe). East Sheen lunch stop: the [Palewell Café on the Common](https://www.colicci.co.uk/roehampton-gate-cafe). Barnes lunch stops: the Café Loris, or the [Stag's Head](https://www.stagsheadbarnes.co.uk/). 9 km.

BOA.name: Bradford-on-Avon
BOA.adminWalksAll:
**[Bradford-on-Avon to Bath Spa](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bradford-on-avon-to-bath/)**: Flat canal towpath, steep-sided valley, river aqueducts, canalside pubs and tea rooms, and Georgian city finish. Sights: [Kennet and Avon Canal](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kennet_and_Avon_Canal), Avoncliff Aqueduct, Dundas Aqueduct, and Bradford-upon-Avon historic centre. Can be muddy. Lunch in Avoncliff, Dundas Aqueduct, or Bathampton. Avoncliff lunch stop: the [Cross Guns Avoncliffe](https://crossgunsavoncliff.com/). Dundas Aqueduct lunch stop: the [Angelfish Cafe](https://www.facebook.com/TheAngelfishRestaurant/). Bathampton lunch stop: the [George Inn](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/somerset/george-inn/). 15 km. 4.5 hours.

**Bath Spa to Bradford-on-Avon**: An alternative variant of [Bath Circular via 2 Tunnels and a Canal](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bath-circular-via-2-tunnels-and-a-canal/). Disused railway path, tunnels, viaducts, and canal towpath including Dundas Aqueduct crossing. Sights: [Dundas Aqueduct](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dundas_Aqueduct) and [Kennet and Avon Canal](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kennet_and_Avon_Canal). Lunch in Midford, or Monkton Combe. Midford lunch stop: the [Hope and Anchor](http://www.hopeandanchormidford.co.uk/). Monkton Combe lunch stop: the [Wheelwrights Arms Country Inn](http://www.wheelwrightsarms.co.uk/).
BOA.publicWalksS2S: **[Bradford-on-Avon to Bath Spa](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bradford-on-avon-to-bath/)**: Flat canal towpath, steep-sided valley, river aqueducts, canalside pubs and tea rooms, and Georgian city finish. Sights: [Kennet and Avon Canal](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kennet_and_Avon_Canal), Avoncliff Aqueduct, Dundas Aqueduct, and Bradford-upon-Avon historic centre. Can be muddy. Lunch in Avoncliff, Dundas Aqueduct, or Bathampton. Avoncliff lunch stop: the [Cross Guns Avoncliffe](https://crossgunsavoncliff.com/). Dundas Aqueduct lunch stop: the [Angelfish Cafe](https://www.facebook.com/TheAngelfishRestaurant/). Bathampton lunch stop: the [George Inn](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/somerset/george-inn/). 15 km. 4.5 hours.

BOE.name: Botley
BOE.adminWalksAll:
**Netley to Botley via Hamble Ferry**: Coastal country park, stony beach, light woodland, mudflats, riverside causeway with marinas, open farmland, and village lanes. Sights: Royal Victoria Country Park, Netley Chapel, Hamble Common, Warsash Ferry, Universal Marina and Boatyard, Manor Farm and Country Park, and St Bartholomew's Church. Check tide timings. Lunch in Lower Swanwick: the Harper's Steakhouse (The Navigator), the Boat House Cafe, the Banana Wharf, or the Mermaids at Universal. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 17 km. 70 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920240122)**

**Botley to Netley (reverse walk)**: Farmland, woodland, riverside causeway with marinas and mudflats, stony beach, and coastal country park. Sights: Manor Farm and Country Park, St Bartholomew's Church, Strawberry Trail, Warsash Ferry, Universal Marina and Boatyard, Banana Wharf, Mermaids at Universal, Hamble Common, and Netley Chapel. Check tide timings. Lunch in Lower Swanwick: the Harper's Steakhouse (formerly The Navigator), the Boat House Cafe and Restaurant, the Banana Wharf, or the Mermaids at Universal. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 15 km.

**Botley to Sholing (reverse walk with extension)**: Farmland, woodland, riverside causeway, stony beach, coastal country park, shoreline tarmac path, and wooded valley following the Itchen Way. Sights: Manor Farm and Country Park, St Bartholomew's Church, Warsash Ferry, Hamble Common, Netley Chapel, Netley Castle, Westwood Woodland Park, Cruise Liner Terminal, and Itchen Way. Check tide timings. Lunch in Lower Swanwick: the Harper's Steakhouse (formerly The Navigator), the Boat House Cafe and Restaurant, the Banana Wharf, or the Mermaids at Universal. A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 20 km.
BOE.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Netley to Botley via Hamble Ferry**: Coastal country park, stony beach, light woodland, mudflats, riverside causeway with marinas, open farmland, and village lanes. Sights: Royal Victoria Country Park, Netley Chapel, Hamble Common, Warsash Ferry, Universal Marina and Boatyard, Manor Farm and Country Park, and St Bartholomew's Church. Check tide timings. Lunch in Lower Swanwick: the Harper's Steakhouse (The Navigator), the Boat House Cafe, the Banana Wharf, or the Mermaids at Universal. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 17 km. 70 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920240122)**

BOG.name: Bognor Regis
BOG.adminWalksAll: **[Littlehampton to Bognor Regis](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/littlehampton-to-bognor-regis/)**: Coastal seafront promenades, shingle beaches, sand dunes, sea wall paths, quiet seafront homes, and south coast seaside resorts. Sights: [West Beach Cafe](https://www.instagram.com/west.beachcafe/), Climping Beach, Pagham Harbour, and Bognor Regis Pier. Check tide timings. Lunch in Littlehampton, Littlehampton West Beach, Felpham, or Bognor Regis. Littlehampton lunch stops: the Steam Packet, or the Arun View Inn. Littlehampton West Beach lunch stop: the [West Beach Cafe](https://www.instagram.com/west.beachcafe/). Felpham lunch stops: the Boat House Cafe, or the [Lobster Pot](http://www.lobsterpotfelpham.com/). Bognor Regis lunch stop: the Regis (Brewers Fayre). 12 km.
BOG.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Littlehampton to Bognor Regis](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/littlehampton-to-bognor-regis/)**: Coastal seafront promenades, shingle beaches, sand dunes, sea wall paths, quiet seafront homes, and south coast seaside resorts. Sights: [West Beach Cafe](https://www.instagram.com/west.beachcafe/), Climping Beach, Pagham Harbour, and Bognor Regis Pier. Check tide timings. Lunch in Littlehampton, Littlehampton West Beach, Felpham, or Bognor Regis. Littlehampton lunch stops: the Steam Packet, or the Arun View Inn. Littlehampton West Beach lunch stop: the [West Beach Cafe](https://www.instagram.com/west.beachcafe/). Felpham lunch stops: the Boat House Cafe, or the [Lobster Pot](http://www.lobsterpotfelpham.com/). Bognor Regis lunch stop: the Regis (Brewers Fayre). 12 km.

BOH.name: Bosham
BOH.adminWalksAll: **Southbourne to Bosham**: Flat tidal harbour shoreline, raised sea walls, mudflats, fields, and quaint harbourside village. Sights: Parish Church of St Mary, Chidham and Holy Trinity Church. Can be muddy. Best at high tide. Lunch in Chidham, or Bosham. Chidham lunch stop: the Old House at Home. Bosham lunch stops: the Anchor Bleu, or the Shoreside Cafe. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).
BOH.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Southbourne to Bosham**: Flat tidal harbour shoreline, raised sea walls, mudflats, fields, and quaint harbourside village. Sights: Parish Church of St Mary, Chidham and Holy Trinity Church. Can be muddy. Best at high tide. Lunch in Chidham, or Bosham. Chidham lunch stop: the Old House at Home. Bosham lunch stops: the Anchor Bleu, or the Shoreside Cafe. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).

BRG.name: Borough Green & Wrotham
BRG.adminWalksAll:
**Borough Green to Sevenoaks via Ightham Mote**: An essential walk! Greensand hills, apple orchards, woodland paths, streams, deer park, open parkland, and historic manor grounds. Sights: [Ightham Mote](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-ighthammote) (smallish but charming 14th C. manor; you can easily fit a full visit into your walk), [Knole House](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-knole) (sprawling ancient aristocratic seat; well worth a visit but requires an entire day in itself), Knole Park, Plaxtol Parish Church, and Fairlawne House. Can be muddy. Lunch in Ightham Mote, or Shipbourne. Ightham Mote lunch stop: the [National Trust café](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-ighthammote). Shipbourne lunch stop: the Chaser Inn. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 25 May 2025.* 15 km. 250 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2902569123)**

**Yalding to Borough Green & Wrotham via Church of St Lawrence**: River Medway start, open fields, deer-fenced woodland, Kentish Weald countryside, vast managed woods, bluebell woodland valleys, orchard edges, and small villages. Sights: Roydon Hall, St Michaels Church, Church of St Lawrence, and Mereworth Wines. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Mereworth: the Mereworth Wines. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 5 hours.

**Yalding to Borough Green & Wrotham via Roydon Hall**: Open fields, country lanes, managed Mereworth Woods, bluebell woodland valleys, and small villages. Sights: Roydon Hall, St Michaels Church, and Mereworth Wines. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Mereworth: the Moody Mare Country Pub. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 5 hours.

**Yalding to Borough Green & Wrotham via St Dunstan's Church**: Sunken bridleways, open fields, Greensand Way, Gower Hill woodland, Mereworth Woods, bluebell valley, and small villages. Sights: Roydon Hall, St Michaels Church, and St Dunstan's Church. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in West Peckham: the Swan on the Green. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).
BRG.publicWalksS2S: **Borough Green to Sevenoaks via Ightham Mote**: An essential walk! Greensand hills, apple orchards, woodland paths, streams, deer park, open parkland, and historic manor grounds. Sights: [Ightham Mote](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-ighthammote) (smallish but charming 14th C. manor; you can easily fit a full visit into your walk), [Knole House](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-knole) (sprawling ancient aristocratic seat; well worth a visit but requires an entire day in itself), Knole Park, Plaxtol Parish Church, and Fairlawne House. Can be muddy. Lunch in Ightham Mote, or Shipbourne. Ightham Mote lunch stop: the [National Trust café](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-ighthammote). Shipbourne lunch stop: the Chaser Inn. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 15 km. 250 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2902569123)**

BRK.name: Berwick
BRK.adminWalksAll:
**[Berwick Circular via Upper Dicker Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-via-upper-dicker-circular/)**: Gently undulating farmland, open fields, bluebell woods, reservoir shorepath, and quiet country lanes with fine downs and weald views. Sights: [Michelham Priory](https://sussexpast.co.uk/attraction/michelham-priory/), Abbot's Wood, Arlington Reservoir, Long Man of Wilmington, and Holy Trinity Church. Can be slippery. Lunch in Arlington, Michelham Priory, or Upper Dicker. Arlington lunch stop: the [Old Oak Inn](https://www.oldoakinnarlington.co.uk/). Michelham Priory lunch stop: the [Blue Door Cafe](https://bluedoorcafe.co.uk/). Upper Dicker lunch stop: the [Plough](https://www.plough-upperdicker.co.uk/). 17 km. 5.5 hours.

**Berwick Circular via Windover Hill**: A shorter variant of [Berwick to Seaford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-seaford/). South Downs chalk hills, open downland, river valley, village lanes, and fields. Sights: Long Man of Wilmington, Windover Hill, High and Over Hill, and Berwick church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Wilmington, Alfriston, or Berwick. Wilmington lunch stop: the Long Man Inn. Alfriston lunch stop: the George Inn. Berwick lunch stops: the Cricketer's Arms, or the Berwick Arms. 13 km.

**Berwick to Eastbourne via Filching Manor Motor Museum**: Rambler favourite! Farmland, reservoir, chalk downland, ridge grassland, chalk figure, smugglers villages, and seaside resort. Sights: [Long Man of Wilmington](http://www.villagenet.co.uk/sevensisters/villages/wilmington.php), Arlington Reservoir, and Filching Manor Motor Museum. Lunch in Wilmington, or Jevington. Wilmington lunch stop: the [Long Man Inn](https://longmaninn.co.uk/). Jevington lunch stop: the [Eight Bells](https://eightbellsjevington.com/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 20 km. 6.5 hours.

**[Berwick to Seaford via Long Man of Wilmington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-seaford/)**: South Downs chalk hills, open downland, river valley, coastal cliffs, shingle beach, and picturesque village. Sights: Long Man of Wilmington, Windover Hill, High and Over Hill, Berwick church, Cuckmere Haven, Seven Sisters, Seaford Head, and Martello Tower. Can be muddy. Lunch in Wilmington, Alfriston, Exceat, or Seaford. Wilmington lunch stop: the Long Man Inn. Alfriston lunch stop: the George Inn. Exceat lunch stop: the Cuckmere Inn. Seaford lunch stops: the Trawlers, the Plough Inn, or the Steamworks. 20 km.

**Berwick to Polegate**: Rambler favourite! Farmland, chalk downland, Folkington village, and suburban roads at finish. Sights: [Long Man of Wilmington](http://www.villagenet.co.uk/sevensisters/villages/wilmington.php). Suburban roads at end. Lunch in Wilmington: the [Long Man Inn](https://longmaninn.co.uk/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 11 km.

**Berwick to Eastbourne via Beachy Head**: Rambler favourite! Farmland, chalk downland, ridge grassland, South Downs Way, clifftop, and highest chalk sea cliff in Britain. Sights: [Long Man of Wilmington](http://www.villagenet.co.uk/sevensisters/villages/wilmington.php), Beachy Head, and Arlington Reservoir. Steep cliffs. Lunch in Wilmington, or Jevington. Wilmington lunch stop: the [Long Man Inn](https://longmaninn.co.uk/). Jevington lunch stop: the [Eight Bells](https://eightbellsjevington.com/). A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 24 km.

**Berwick to Seaford via Berwick church**: An alternative variant of [Berwick to Seaford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-seaford/). Fields, downland, village lanes, river valley, coastal cliffs, and shingle beach. Sights: Berwick church, High and Over Hill, Cuckmere Haven, Seven Sisters, Seaford Head, and Martello Tower. Can be muddy. Lunch in Berwick, Alfriston, Exceat, or Seaford. Berwick lunch stop: the Cricketer's Arms. Alfriston lunch stop: the George Inn. Exceat lunch stop: the Cuckmere Inn. Seaford lunch stops: the Trawlers, the Plough Inn, or the Steamworks. 18 km.

**Bishopstone to Berwick**: A shorter variant of [Bishopstone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/). Downland climbs, South Downs Way, and descent to Berwick via Vanguard Way. Sights: Long Burgh Barrow and St Michael and All Angels church. Can be overgrown. Lunch in Berwick: the [Cricketers' Arms](https://www.cricketersberwick.co.uk/). 13 km.

**Seaford to Berwick**: An alternative variant of [Berwick to Seaford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-seaford/). Coastal chalk cliffs, downland, river valley, open fields, and village lanes. Sights: Seaford Head, Seven Sisters, High and Over Hill, Cuckmere Haven, Berwick church, and Martello Tower. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch in Alfriston, or Berwick. Alfriston lunch stop: the George Inn. Berwick lunch stops: the Cricketer's Arms, or the Berwick Arms. 16 km.
BRK.publicWalksS2S:
**Berwick to Eastbourne via Filching Manor Motor Museum**: Rambler favourite! Farmland, reservoir, chalk downland, ridge grassland, chalk figure, smugglers villages, and seaside resort. Sights: [Long Man of Wilmington](http://www.villagenet.co.uk/sevensisters/villages/wilmington.php), Arlington Reservoir, and Filching Manor Motor Museum. Lunch in Wilmington, or Jevington. Wilmington lunch stop: the [Long Man Inn](https://longmaninn.co.uk/). Jevington lunch stop: the [Eight Bells](https://eightbellsjevington.com/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 20 km. 6.5 hours.

**[Berwick to Seaford via Long Man of Wilmington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-seaford/)**: South Downs chalk hills, open downland, river valley, coastal cliffs, shingle beach, and picturesque village. Sights: Long Man of Wilmington, Windover Hill, High and Over Hill, Berwick church, Cuckmere Haven, Seven Sisters, Seaford Head, and Martello Tower. Can be muddy. Lunch in Wilmington, Alfriston, Exceat, or Seaford. Wilmington lunch stop: the Long Man Inn. Alfriston lunch stop: the George Inn. Exceat lunch stop: the Cuckmere Inn. Seaford lunch stops: the Trawlers, the Plough Inn, or the Steamworks. 20 km.
BRK.publicWalksCircular: **[Berwick Circular via Upper Dicker Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-via-upper-dicker-circular/)**: Gently undulating farmland, open fields, bluebell woods, reservoir shorepath, and quiet country lanes with fine downs and weald views. Sights: [Michelham Priory](https://sussexpast.co.uk/attraction/michelham-priory/), Abbot's Wood, Arlington Reservoir, Long Man of Wilmington, and Holy Trinity Church. Can be slippery. Lunch in Arlington, Michelham Priory, or Upper Dicker. Arlington lunch stop: the [Old Oak Inn](https://www.oldoakinnarlington.co.uk/). Michelham Priory lunch stop: the [Blue Door Cafe](https://bluedoorcafe.co.uk/). Upper Dicker lunch stop: the [Plough](https://www.plough-upperdicker.co.uk/). 17 km. 5.5 hours.

BRU.name: Bruton
BRU.adminWalksAll:
**[Bruton Circular via Stourhead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bruton-circular-via-stourhead/)**: South Somerset pastures, wooded ridge forming the Wiltshire/Somerset boundary, hanging woods, landscaped estate gardens with lakeside walks, grassy U-shaped valley, and honey-coloured stone-built town. Sights: Alfred's Tower, Stourhead Estate, Palladian Bridge, Pantheon, Bristol Cross, Stourhead House, Bruton Dovecote, and Hauser & Wirth Somerset. Lunch in North Brewham, Kingsettle Hill, or Stourton. North Brewham lunch stop: the [Old Red Lion](http://www.theoldredlion.org.uk/). Kingsettle Hill lunch stop: the Alpine Coffee Co. Stourton lunch stops: the [Spread Eagle Inn](https://www.spreadeagleinn.com/), or the [Stourhead Estate Restaurant](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/wiltshire/stourhead/eating-and-shopping-at-stourhead). 22 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Bruton Circular via Alfred's Tower](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bruton-circular-via-hauser-wirth-somerset/)**: Narrow steep-sided valleys and coombes, fast-flowing streams, rolling hilltops, honey-coloured stone villages, and far views to Wiltshire and Dorset boundary. Sights: Alfred's Tower, [Hauser & Wirth Somerset](https://www.hauserwirth.com/locations/10068-hauser-wirth-somerset), and Bruton Dovecote. Can be muddy. Lunch in Batcombe, or Bruton. Batcombe lunch stop: the [Three Horseshoes Inn](https://www.thethreehorseshoesbatcombe.co.uk/). Bruton lunch stops: the [Matt's Kitchen](http://www.mattskitchen.co.uk/), the [Prickly Pear](https://www.prickly-pear.com/), the [Sun Inn](https://thesuninnbruton.co.uk/), the [At the Chapel](http://www.atthechapel.co.uk/), the [Old Pharmacy](https://www.oldpharmacybruton.com/), the [Briar](https://numberonebruton.com/briar/), the [Blue Ball Bruton](https://www.blueballbruton.com/), the [Da Costa](https://da-costa.co.uk/), or the [Roth Bar](https://rothbar.co.uk/). 25 km. 6.5 hours.

**Bruton Circular via Hauser & Wirth Somerset**: A shorter variant of [Bruton Circular or from Castle Cary](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bruton-circular-via-hauser-wirth-somerset/). Valleys, coombes, rolling hilltops, honey-coloured stone village, and extended time at art gallery. Sights: [Hauser & Wirth Somerset](https://www.hauserwirth.com/locations/10068-hauser-wirth-somerset) and Bruton Dovecote. Lunch in Bruton: the [Matt's Kitchen](http://www.mattskitchen.co.uk/), the [Prickly Pear](https://www.prickly-pear.com/), the [Sun Inn](https://thesuninnbruton.co.uk/), the [At the Chapel](http://www.atthechapel.co.uk/), the [Old Pharmacy](https://www.oldpharmacybruton.com/), the [Briar](https://numberonebruton.com/briar/), the [Blue Ball Bruton](https://www.blueballbruton.com/), the [Da Costa](https://da-costa.co.uk/), or the [Roth Bar](https://rothbar.co.uk/). 16 km.

**Bruton Circular via Stourhead & White Sheet Hill Iron Age Hillfort**: A longer variant of [Bruton Circular via Stourhead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bruton-circular-via-stourhead/). South Somerset pastures, wooded ridge, hanging woods, landscaped estate gardens, and Iron Age hillfort and Neolithic camp on exposed downland hill. Sights: Alfred's Tower, Stourhead Estate, White Sheet Hill Iron Age Hillfort, White Sheet Hill Neolithic Camp, Palladian Bridge, Pantheon, Stourhead House, Bruton Dovecote, and Hauser & Wirth Somerset. Lunch in North Brewham, Kingsettle Hill, or Stourton. North Brewham lunch stop: the [Old Red Lion](http://www.theoldredlion.org.uk/). Kingsettle Hill lunch stop: the Alpine Coffee Co. Stourton lunch stops: the [Spread Eagle Inn](https://www.spreadeagleinn.com/), or the [Stourhead Estate Restaurant](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/wiltshire/stourhead/eating-and-shopping-at-stourhead). 29 km.

**Castle Cary to Bruton via Stourhead**: An alternative variant of [Bruton Circular via Stourhead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bruton-circular-via-stourhead/). South Somerset pastures, wooded ridge, hanging woods, landscaped estate gardens with lakeside walks, and grottoes and classical temples. Sights: Alfred's Tower, Stourhead Estate, Palladian Bridge, Pantheon, Stourhead House, Bruton Dovecote, and Hauser & Wirth Somerset. Lunch in North Brewham, or Stourton. North Brewham lunch stop: the [Old Red Lion](http://www.theoldredlion.org.uk/). Stourton lunch stops: the [Spread Eagle Inn](https://www.spreadeagleinn.com/), or the [Stourhead Estate Restaurant](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/wiltshire/stourhead/eating-and-shopping-at-stourhead). 27 km.

**Castle Cary to Bruton via Alfred's Tower**: An alternative variant of [Bruton Circular or from Castle Cary](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bruton-circular-via-hauser-wirth-somerset/). Narrow steep-sided valleys and coombes, fast-flowing streams, rolling hilltops, honey-coloured stone villages, and far views to Wiltshire and Dorset boundary. Sights: Alfred's Tower, [Hauser & Wirth Somerset](https://www.hauserwirth.com/locations/10068-hauser-wirth-somerset), and Bruton Dovecote. Can be muddy. Lunch in Batcombe, or Bruton. Batcombe lunch stop: the [Three Horseshoes Inn](https://www.thethreehorseshoesbatcombe.co.uk/). Bruton lunch stops: the [Matt's Kitchen](http://www.mattskitchen.co.uk/), the [Prickly Pear](https://www.prickly-pear.com/), the [Sun Inn](https://thesuninnbruton.co.uk/), the [At the Chapel](http://www.atthechapel.co.uk/), the [Old Pharmacy](https://www.oldpharmacybruton.com/), the [Briar](https://numberonebruton.com/briar/), the [Blue Ball Bruton](https://www.blueballbruton.com/), the [Da Costa](https://da-costa.co.uk/), or the [Roth Bar](https://rothbar.co.uk/). 29 km.

BRY.name: Barry
BRY.adminWalksAll:
**[Barry to Llantwit Major](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/barry-to-llantwit-major/)**: Coastal headlands, pebble and sandy beaches, flat rock-shelf beaches, low cliffs, striated limestone cliffs, parkland, quiet cliff paths, wetland, and historic town centre. Sights: [Barry Docks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Docks), [Barry Island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Island), [Porthkerry Country Park](http://www.valeofglamorgan.gov.uk/en/enjoying/Coast-and-Countryside/Porthkerry-Country-Park/Porthkerry-Country-Park.aspx), [Porthkerry Viaduct](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Porthkerry_Viaduct), [The Bulwarks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Bulwarks,_Porthkerry), [St Illtyd's Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Illtyd's_Church,_Llantwit_Major), Cold Knap Point, Rhoose Point, and Summerhouse Point. Check tide timings. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in East Aberthaw: the [Blue Anchor](http://www.blueanchoraberthaw.com). 21 km.

**Barry to Rhoose Cardiff International Airport**: A shorter variant of [Barry to Llantwit Major](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/barry-to-llantwit-major/). Coastal headlands, pebble beach, low cliffs, parkland, railway viaduct, and cliff tops above former quarries. Sights: [Barry Docks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Docks), [Barry Island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Island), Cold Knap Point, [Porthkerry Country Park](http://www.valeofglamorgan.gov.uk/en/enjoying/Coast-and-Countryside/Porthkerry-Country-Park/Porthkerry-Country-Park.aspx), [Porthkerry Viaduct](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Porthkerry_Viaduct), [The Bulwarks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Bulwarks,_Porthkerry), and Rhoose Point. Check tide timings.
BRY.publicWalksS2S: **[Barry to Llantwit Major](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/barry-to-llantwit-major/)**: Coastal headlands, pebble and sandy beaches, flat rock-shelf beaches, low cliffs, striated limestone cliffs, parkland, quiet cliff paths, wetland, and historic town centre. Sights: [Barry Docks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Docks), [Barry Island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Island), [Porthkerry Country Park](http://www.valeofglamorgan.gov.uk/en/enjoying/Coast-and-Countryside/Porthkerry-Country-Park/Porthkerry-Country-Park.aspx), [Porthkerry Viaduct](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Porthkerry_Viaduct), [The Bulwarks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Bulwarks,_Porthkerry), [St Illtyd's Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Illtyd's_Church,_Llantwit_Major), Cold Knap Point, Rhoose Point, and Summerhouse Point. Check tide timings. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in East Aberthaw: the [Blue Anchor](http://www.blueanchoraberthaw.com). 21 km.

BSR.name: Broadstairs
BSR.adminWalksAll: **Broadstairs Circular**: A variant of [Ramsgate to Margate](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ramsgate-to-margate/). Low chalk cliffs, sandy bays and coves, cliff-top grassland, and seaside promenades. Sights: Dickens House Museum, Turner Contemporary Gallery, and North Foreland Lighthouse. Check tide timings. Lunch in Broadstairs, Kingsgate Bay, or Botany Bay. Broadstairs lunch stop: the Charles Dickens. Kingsgate Bay lunch stop: the [Captain Digby](http://www.captaindigby.co.uk/). Botany Bay lunch stop: the [Botany Bay](https://www.botanybayhotel.co.uk/).
BSR.publicWalksCircular: **Broadstairs Circular**: A variant of [Ramsgate to Margate](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ramsgate-to-margate/). Low chalk cliffs, sandy bays and coves, cliff-top grassland, and seaside promenades. Sights: Dickens House Museum, Turner Contemporary Gallery, and North Foreland Lighthouse. Check tide timings. Lunch in Broadstairs, Kingsgate Bay, or Botany Bay. Broadstairs lunch stop: the Charles Dickens. Kingsgate Bay lunch stop: the [Captain Digby](http://www.captaindigby.co.uk/). Botany Bay lunch stop: the [Botany Bay](https://www.botanybayhotel.co.uk/).

BTG.name: Barnt Green
BTG.adminWalksAll: **[Hagley to Barnt Green](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hagley-to-barnt-green-via-the-clent-hills/)**: Ridge walk over North Worcestershire Hills, open hilltops, scrubland, gorse, woods, pastures, motorway bridge crossing, country parks, and heathland with bilberry and heather. Sights: [Clent Hills](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clent_Hills), [Wychbury Hill](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wychbury_Hill), [Hagley Park](https://www.hagleypark.co.uk/), The Four Stones, Walton Hill, Waseley Hills Country Park, Beacon Hill Castle, Lickey Hills Country Park, Bilberry Hill, and Cofton Hill. Steep descents. 19 km. 5 hours.
BTG.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Hagley to Barnt Green](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hagley-to-barnt-green-via-the-clent-hills/)**: Ridge walk over North Worcestershire Hills, open hilltops, scrubland, gorse, woods, pastures, motorway bridge crossing, country parks, and heathland with bilberry and heather. Sights: [Clent Hills](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clent_Hills), [Wychbury Hill](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wychbury_Hill), [Hagley Park](https://www.hagleypark.co.uk/), The Four Stones, Walton Hill, Waseley Hills Country Park, Beacon Hill Castle, Lickey Hills Country Park, Bilberry Hill, and Cofton Hill. Steep descents. 19 km. 5 hours.

BTH.name: Bath Spa
BTH.adminWalksAll:
**[Bath Spa Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bath-circular-via-2-tunnels-and-a-canal/)**: Disused railway path, tunnels, viaducts, canal towpath, river, and historic city centre. Sights: [Bath](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bath,_Somerset), [Somerset and Dorset Joint Railway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Somerset_and_Dorset_Joint_Railway), [Combe Down Tunnel](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Combe_Down_Tunnel), [2 Tunnels Greenway](http://www.twotunnels.org.uk/), [Kennet and Avon Canal](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kennet_and_Avon_Canal), [Dundas Aqueduct](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dundas_Aqueduct), Prior Park, Devonshire Tunnel, and Tucking Mill Viaduct. Lunch in Midford, or Monkton Combe. Midford lunch stop: the [Hope and Anchor](http://www.hopeandanchormidford.co.uk/). Monkton Combe lunch stop: the [Wheelwrights Arms Country Inn](http://www.wheelwrightsarms.co.uk/). 19 km.

**Bath Spa to Bradford-on-Avon**: An alternative variant of [Bath Circular via 2 Tunnels and a Canal](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bath-circular-via-2-tunnels-and-a-canal/). Disused railway path, tunnels, viaducts, and canal towpath including Dundas Aqueduct crossing. Sights: [Dundas Aqueduct](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dundas_Aqueduct) and [Kennet and Avon Canal](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kennet_and_Avon_Canal). Lunch in Midford, or Monkton Combe. Midford lunch stop: the [Hope and Anchor](http://www.hopeandanchormidford.co.uk/). Monkton Combe lunch stop: the [Wheelwrights Arms Country Inn](http://www.wheelwrightsarms.co.uk/).

**[Bradford-on-Avon to Bath Spa](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bradford-on-avon-to-bath/)**: Flat canal towpath, steep-sided valley, river aqueducts, canalside pubs and tea rooms, and Georgian city finish. Sights: [Kennet and Avon Canal](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kennet_and_Avon_Canal), Avoncliff Aqueduct, Dundas Aqueduct, and Bradford-upon-Avon historic centre. Can be muddy. Lunch in Avoncliff, Dundas Aqueduct, or Bathampton. Avoncliff lunch stop: the [Cross Guns Avoncliffe](https://crossgunsavoncliff.com/). Dundas Aqueduct lunch stop: the [Angelfish Cafe](https://www.facebook.com/TheAngelfishRestaurant/). Bathampton lunch stop: the [George Inn](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/somerset/george-inn/). 15 km. 4.5 hours.
BTH.publicWalksCircular: **[Bath Spa Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bath-circular-via-2-tunnels-and-a-canal/)**: Disused railway path, tunnels, viaducts, canal towpath, river, and historic city centre. Sights: [Bath](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bath,_Somerset), [Somerset and Dorset Joint Railway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Somerset_and_Dorset_Joint_Railway), [Combe Down Tunnel](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Combe_Down_Tunnel), [2 Tunnels Greenway](http://www.twotunnels.org.uk/), [Kennet and Avon Canal](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kennet_and_Avon_Canal), [Dundas Aqueduct](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dundas_Aqueduct), Prior Park, Devonshire Tunnel, and Tucking Mill Viaduct. Lunch in Midford, or Monkton Combe. Midford lunch stop: the [Hope and Anchor](http://www.hopeandanchormidford.co.uk/). Monkton Combe lunch stop: the [Wheelwrights Arms Country Inn](http://www.wheelwrightsarms.co.uk/). 19 km.
BTH.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Bradford-on-Avon to Bath Spa](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bradford-on-avon-to-bath/)**: Flat canal towpath, steep-sided valley, river aqueducts, canalside pubs and tea rooms, and Georgian city finish. Sights: [Kennet and Avon Canal](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kennet_and_Avon_Canal), Avoncliff Aqueduct, Dundas Aqueduct, and Bradford-upon-Avon historic centre. Can be muddy. Dundas Aqueduct lunch stop: the [Angelfish Cafe](https://www.facebook.com/TheAngelfishRestaurant/). Bathampton lunch stop: the [George Inn](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/somerset/george-inn/). 15 km. 4.5 hours.

BTN.name: Brighton
BTN.adminWalksAll:
**[Newhaven Town to Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/newhaven-to-brighton-and-portslade/)**: Harbourside path, white chalk cliffs, clifftop and undercliff concrete paths, shingle beach, and busy seaside promenade and boardwalk. Sights: [Newhaven Fort](http://www.newhavenfort.org.uk), Brighton Pier, Brighton Marina, Brighton Pavilion, The Lanes, and Saltdean Lido. Exposed in high wind. Lunch in Newhaven, Peacehaven, Telescombe, Saltdean, Rottingdean, or Brighton Marina. Newhaven lunch stop: the Hope Inn. Peacehaven lunch stops: the [Peacehaven](https://www.stonehouserestaurants.co.uk/nationalsearch/southeast/thepeacehavenpeacehaven), or the Smuggler's Rest. Telescombe lunch stop: the [Telescombe Tavern](http://telscombetavern.wix.com/thetelscombetavern). Saltdean lunch stop: the [Whitecliffs](https://www.facebook.com/whitecliffssaltdean/). Rottingdean lunch stops: the Molly's at the Beach, or the White Horse. Brighton Marina lunch stop: the West Quay. 18 km.

**[Hassocks to Brighton via St Margaret's Church](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hassocks-to-brighton/)**: South Downs valleys, open chalk downland, ancient woods, parkland, university campus, suburban streets, and seafront. Sights: St Margaret's Church, Ditchling Beacon, Ditchling Beacon Nature Reserve, Stanmer House, Stanmer Church, University of Sussex, Swan Inn, Amex Stadium, Kemp Town Racecourse, Brighton Pier, Royal Pavilion, The Lanes, and Brighton College. Can be muddy. Lunch in Stanmer, Falmer, or Falmer South. Stanmer lunch stops: the Stanmer House, or the Stanmer Tea Rooms. Falmer lunch stop: the Swan Inn. Falmer South lunch stops: the Falmer Cafe, or the Park Farm Shop. 22 km. 6 hours.

**Lewes to Brighton**: A variant of [Southease to Rottingdean or Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-rottingdean-or-brighton/). Chalk downs, open escarpment, quaint village, seafront promenade, and urban Brighton. Sights: Lewes Castle, Kipling Gardens, Brighton Pier, and [Brighton Royal Pavilion](http://www.royalpavilion.org.uk/). Lunch in Kingston: the Juggs. 19 km.

**Southease to Brighton**: A longer variant of [Southease to Rottingdean or Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-rottingdean-or-brighton/). Flat Ouse valley, riverbank, chalk downs, open escarpment, quaint village, seafront promenade, and urban Brighton. Sights: Monk's House, Lewes Castle, Kipling Gardens, Brighton Pier, and [Brighton Royal Pavilion](http://www.royalpavilion.org.uk/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Rodmell, or Kingston. Rodmell lunch stop: the Abergavenny Arms. Kingston lunch stop: the Juggs. 23 km.

**Hassocks to Brighton via Clayton Windmills**: An alternative variant of [Hassocks to Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hassocks-to-brighton/). Residential paths, woodland, open downland ridge, chalk track along South Downs Way, and windmills. Sights: Clayton Windmills, Jack Windmill, Jill Windmill, Church of St John the Baptist, Butcher's Wood, Keymer Post, and Ditchling Beacon Nature Reserve. Can be muddy. Lunch in Stanmer, or Falmer. Stanmer lunch stops: the Stanmer House, or the Stanmer Tea Rooms. Falmer lunch stop: the Swan Inn.
BTN.publicWalksS2SEnding:
**[Newhaven Town to Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/newhaven-to-brighton-and-portslade/)**: Harbourside path, white chalk cliffs, clifftop and undercliff concrete paths, shingle beach, and busy seaside promenade and boardwalk. Sights: [Newhaven Fort](http://www.newhavenfort.org.uk), Brighton Pier, Brighton Marina, Brighton Pavilion, The Lanes, and Saltdean Lido. Exposed in high wind. Telescombe lunch stop: the [Telescombe Tavern](http://telscombetavern.wix.com/thetelscombetavern). Saltdean lunch stop: the [Whitecliffs](https://www.facebook.com/whitecliffssaltdean/). Rottingdean lunch stops: the Molly's at the Beach, or the White Horse. Brighton Marina lunch stop: the West Quay. 18 km.

**[Hassocks to Brighton via St Margaret's Church](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hassocks-to-brighton/)**: South Downs valleys, open chalk downland, ancient woods, parkland, university campus, suburban streets, and seafront. Sights: St Margaret's Church, Ditchling Beacon, Ditchling Beacon Nature Reserve, Stanmer House, Stanmer Church, University of Sussex, Swan Inn, Amex Stadium, Kemp Town Racecourse, Brighton Pier, Royal Pavilion, The Lanes, and Brighton College. Can be muddy. Lunch in Stanmer, Falmer, or Falmer South. Stanmer lunch stops: the Stanmer House, or the Stanmer Tea Rooms. Falmer lunch stop: the Swan Inn. Falmer South lunch stops: the Falmer Cafe, or the Park Farm Shop. 22 km. 6 hours.

BTY.name: Bentley
BTY.adminWalksAll:
**[Bentley to Farnham via Devil's Jumps](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bentley-to-farnham/)**: Rambler favourite! Ancient forests, heathland ridges, Scots pine, heather, large ponds with swimming, remote commons, and quiet woodland paths and lanes. Sights: Alice Holt Forest, Frensham Great Pond, Devil's Jumps, Hankley Common D-Day training site, and Waverley Abbey. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Churt, or Tilford. Churt lunch stop: the [Bel and the Dragon](https://belandthedragon-churt.co.uk/). Tilford lunch stop: the [Barley Mow](http://www.thebarleymowtilford.com/). 26 km. 6 hours.

**Bentley to Farnham via Frensham Little Pond**: A shorter variant of [Bentley to Farnham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bentley-to-farnham/). Rambler favourite! Ancient forests, heathland, Scots pine, large ponds with swimming, woodland paths and lanes, and skipping Devil's Jumps and Hankley Common. Sights: Alice Holt Forest, Frensham Great Pond, and Frensham Little Pond. Lunch in Churt, or Tilford. Churt lunch stop: the [Bel and the Dragon](https://belandthedragon-churt.co.uk/). Tilford lunch stop: the [Barley Mow](http://www.thebarleymowtilford.com/). 19 km.

**Bentley to Farnham via Waverley Abbey**: An alternative variant of [Bentley to Farnham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bentley-to-farnham/). Rambler favourite! Greensand Way, River Wey, optional out-and-back to Waverley Abbey, and cuts steep ascents near Farnham. Sights: Waverley Abbey. Lunch in Tilford: the [Barley Mow](http://www.thebarleymowtilford.com/).
BTY.publicWalksS2S: **[Bentley to Farnham via Devil's Jumps](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bentley-to-farnham/)**: Rambler favourite! Ancient forests, heathland ridges, Scots pine, heather, large ponds with swimming, remote commons, and quiet woodland paths and lanes. Sights: Alice Holt Forest, Frensham Great Pond, Devil's Jumps, Hankley Common D-Day training site, and Waverley Abbey. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Churt, or Tilford. Churt lunch stop: the [Bel and the Dragon](https://belandthedragon-churt.co.uk/). Tilford lunch stop: the [Barley Mow](http://www.thebarleymowtilford.com/). 26 km. 6 hours.

BUE.name: Bures
BUE.adminWalksAll:
**Bures to Sudbury via Lamarsh**: Gently rolling farmland, river meadows, woodland, open fields, historic market town, and pastoral common lands. Sights: Holy Innocents Church Lamarsh, St Mary's Church Great Henny, Gainsborough's House, St Gregory's Church Sudbury, Sudbury Common Lands, and Loshes Woodland. Can be muddy. Lunch in Bulmer Tye, Lamarsh, or Henny Street. Bulmer Tye lunch stop: the Fox. Lamarsh lunch stop: the Lamarsh Lion. Henny Street lunch stop: the Henny Swan. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 140 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920318605)**

**Bures to Sudbury via Holy Innocents Church Lamarsh**: Gently rolling farmland, river meadows, open countryside, and historic market town. Sights: Holy Innocents Church Lamarsh, St Mary's Church Great Henny, Gainsborough's House, St Gregory's Church Sudbury, and Sudbury Common Lands. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lamarsh, or Henny Street. Lamarsh lunch stop: the Lamarsh Lion. Henny Street lunch stop: the Henny Swan. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 12 km.

**Chappel and Wakes Colne to Bures**: Undulating hills, brooks, farms, mixed woodland, fishing lakes, thatched cottages and barns, river valley, and golf course diversion. Sights: East Anglian Railway Museum, Chappel Viaduct, Chalkney Wood, Colne Priory, and St Andrew's Church. Can be very muddy. Lunch in Colne Engaine: the Five Bells. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 17 km. 5.5 hours.
BUE.publicWalksS2S: **Bures to Sudbury via Lamarsh**: Gently rolling farmland, river meadows, woodland, open fields, historic market town, and pastoral common lands. Sights: Holy Innocents Church Lamarsh, St Mary's Church Great Henny, Gainsborough's House, St Gregory's Church Sudbury, Sudbury Common Lands, and Loshes Woodland. Can be muddy. Lunch in Bulmer Tye, Lamarsh, or Henny Street. Bulmer Tye lunch stop: the Fox. Lamarsh lunch stop: the Lamarsh Lion. Henny Street lunch stop: the Henny Swan. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 140 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920318605)**

BUG.name: Burgess Hill
BUG.adminWalksAll: **[Burgess Hill to Hassocks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/burgess-hill-to-hassocks/)**: Rolling farmland, chalky downland, wooded copses, open hilltops, golf courses, picturesque villages, and panoramic South Downs ridge. Sights: Ditchling Beacon, Wolstonbury Hill, Jack and Jill Windmills, Hurstpierpoint College, Sussex Border Path, Indian War Memorial, and Holy Trinity Church. Can be very muddy. Lunch in Ditchling, Pyecombe, Hurstpierpoint, or Clayton. Ditchling lunch stop: the [Bull](https://www.thebullditchling.com/). Pyecombe lunch stop: the [Plough](http://www.theploughpyecombe.co.uk/). Hurstpierpoint lunch stops: the [New Inn](https://www.thenewinnhurst.com/), the [Horse Inn Hurst](https://whatpub.com/pubs/BRI/418/horse-inn-hurst-hurstpierpoint), the [Poacher](https://whatpub.com/pubs/BRI/20/poacher-hurstpierpoint), or the [Morleys Bistro](https://morleys-bistro.com/). Clayton lunch stop: the [Jack and Jill](http://www.thejackandjillinn.co.uk/). 26 km. 7 hours.
BUG.publicWalksS2S: **[Burgess Hill to Hassocks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/burgess-hill-to-hassocks/)**: Rolling farmland, chalky downland, wooded copses, open hilltops, golf courses, picturesque villages, and panoramic South Downs ridge. Sights: Ditchling Beacon, Wolstonbury Hill, Jack and Jill Windmills, Hurstpierpoint College, Sussex Border Path, Indian War Memorial, and Holy Trinity Church. Can be very muddy. Lunch in Ditchling, Pyecombe, Hurstpierpoint, or Clayton. Ditchling lunch stop: the [Bull](https://www.thebullditchling.com/). Pyecombe lunch stop: the [Plough](http://www.theploughpyecombe.co.uk/). Hurstpierpoint lunch stops: the [New Inn](https://www.thenewinnhurst.com/), the [Horse Inn Hurst](https://whatpub.com/pubs/BRI/418/horse-inn-hurst-hurstpierpoint), the [Poacher](https://whatpub.com/pubs/BRI/20/poacher-hurstpierpoint), or the [Morleys Bistro](https://morleys-bistro.com/). Clayton lunch stop: the [Jack and Jill](http://www.thejackandjillinn.co.uk/). 26 km. 7 hours.

BUJ.name: Burton Joyce
BUJ.adminWalksAll:
**[Burton Joyce to Fiskerton](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Riverside, good paths, and open views. Sights: Gunthorpe Lock, Gunthorpe village, and River Trent. Irregular train times. Lunch in Gunthorpe, or Fiskerton. Gunthorpe lunch stops: the pubs, or the cafe at the lock. Fiskerton lunch stop: the pub. 16 km. 10 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/404%20Burton%20Joyce%20to%20Fiskerton.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925786946)**

**[Lowdham to Burton Joyce](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Countryside and river. Sights: River Trent, Gonalston, Caythorpe, and Gunthorpe. Lunch in Burton Joyce: the pub. 14 km. 30 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/417%20Lowdham%20to%20Burton%20Joyce.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925788637)**

Fiskerton lunch stop: the pub. 16 km. 10 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. BUU.name: Burnham-on-Crouch
BUU.adminWalksAll: **[North Fambridge to Burnham-on-Crouch](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/north-fambridge-to-burnham-on-crouch/)**: Flat sea wall path, tidal river, salt marshes, mudflats, reed ponds, creeks, marinas, and riverside promenade. Lunch in Althorne: the Three Horseshoes. 20 km. 4.5 hours.
BUU.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[North Fambridge to Burnham-on-Crouch](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/north-fambridge-to-burnham-on-crouch/)**: Flat sea wall path, tidal river, salt marshes, mudflats, reed ponds, creeks, marinas, and riverside promenade. Lunch in Althorne: the Three Horseshoes. 20 km. 4.5 hours.

BUX.name: Buxton
BUX.adminWalksAll:
**[Buxton Circular via Combs Moss](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-via-combs-moss/)**: Quiet moorland plateau, gritstone scarps, green valleys, reservoirs, and hillfort promontory. Sights: Castle Naze, Black Edge, Combs Edge, Combs Reservoir, and Corbar Hill. Can be muddy. Lunch in Combs: the [Beehive Inn & Farm Shop](http://thebeehiveinn.co.uk/). 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Buxton Circular via Chee Dale](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-or-to-monsal-dale-via-the-wye-valley/)**: Limestone gorges, stepping stones, overhanging rockfaces, dismantled railway line, limekilns, high pastures, dry valleys, and quiet villages. Sights: Chee Dale, Miller's Dale Viaducts, Five Wells Chambered Tomb, Deep Dale, Wye Valley, and Monsal Trail. Paths flood after rain. Lunch in Miller's Dale, or Millers Dale. Miller's Dale lunch stop: the Angler's Rest. Millers Dale lunch stop: the [Refreshment Room](https://www.blueberryfood.co.uk/the-refreshment-room). 26 km. 7.5 hours.

**[Buxton Circular via the Dragon's Back](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-via-the-dragons-back/)**: Limestone uplands, remote valleys, dramatic reef knoll ridges, dry valleys, disused quarries, and open moorland. Sights: Chrome Hill, Parkhouse Hill, Hitter Hill, High Wheeldon, High Edge, Dowel Dale, Harpur Hill Quarry, and Blue Lagoon. Steep scrambling sections. Lunch in Crowdecote, or Longnor. Crowdecote lunch stop: the [Packhorse Inn](https://www.thepackhorseinn.net). Longnor lunch stops: the [Ye Olde Cheshire Cheese](https://www.robinsonsbrewery.com/pubs/ye-olde-cheshire-cheese-inn-longnor), the Longnor Fish and Chip Shop and Tea Room, or the Cobbles Coffee Shop. 26 km. 7 hours.

**[Buxton Circular via Axe Edge Moor and Three Shires Head](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-via-axe-edge-moor-and-three-shires-head/)**: Open upland moor, broad grassy and gravel paths, boggy moorland sections with groughs and peat hags, scenic river valleys, disused quarries, pastures, and long far views. Sights: Solomon's Temple, Three Shires Head, Panniers Bridge, and [Cat & Fiddle](https://theforestdistillery.com/cat-fiddle). Can be boggy. Lunch in Flash Bar, or Flash. Flash Bar lunch stop: the [Flash Bar Café and Shop](http://www.flashbarstores.co.uk/). Flash lunch stop: the New Inn. 27 km. 7 hours.

**[Buxton to Macclesfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-to-macclesfield/)**: Moorland, exposed ridges, gritstone uplands, wooded valleys, rolling pastures, ancient packhorse routes, and quarried country park. Sights: Shining Tor, Buxton Crescent Hotel, [Cat & Fiddle](https://theforestdistillery.com/the-bar-at-cat-and-fiddle/), Forest Chapel, Macclesfield Forest, and Tegg's Nose Country Park. Very exposed moorland. Lunch in Buxton New Road, or Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest. Buxton New Road lunch stop: the [Peak View Restaurant & Tea Room](http://www.peakviewtearooms.co.uk). Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest lunch stops: the [Forest Distillery](https://theforestdistillery.com), or the Stanley Arms Hotel. 24 km. 6.5 hours.

**Buxton to Chapel-en-le-Frith**: A shorter variant of [Buxton Circular via Combs Moss](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-via-combs-moss/). Moorland plateau, gritstone scarps, and descent via Castle Naze causeway to valley station. Sights: Castle Naze, Combs Edge, and Combs Reservoir. Can be muddy. Lunch in Combs: the [Beehive Inn & Farm Shop](http://thebeehiveinn.co.uk/).

**[Macclesfield to Buxton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/macclesfield-to-buxton/)**: Residential roads, pastures, rolling hills, quarried hilltop, forest tracks, exposed moorland, lonely upland valleys, and riverside approach into a spa town. Sights: Tegg's Nose Country Park, Macclesfield Forest, Forest Chapel, Shining Tor, Goytsclough Quarry, Pavilion Gardens, [Cat & Fiddle](https://theforestdistillery.com/cat-fiddle/), and [Peak View Restaurant & Tea Room](http://www.peakviewtearooms.co.uk/). Exposed in bad weather. Lunch in Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest: the [Forest Distillery](https://theforestdistillery.com/), or the [Stanley Arms Hotel](https://www.stanleyarms.com/). 25 km. 7 hours.

**Chapel-en-le-Frith to Buxton**: An alternative variant of [Buxton Circular via Combs Moss](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-via-combs-moss/). Moorland plateau ascent from valley station, gritstone scarps, green valleys, and hillfort promontory. Sights: Castle Naze, Combs Edge, Combs Reservoir, and Corbar Hill. Can be muddy. Lunch in Combs: the [Beehive Inn & Farm Shop](http://thebeehiveinn.co.uk/).
BUX.publicWalksS2S: **[Buxton to Macclesfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-to-macclesfield/)**: Moorland, exposed ridges, gritstone uplands, wooded valleys, rolling pastures, ancient packhorse routes, and quarried country park. Sights: Shining Tor, Buxton Crescent Hotel, [Cat & Fiddle](https://theforestdistillery.com/the-bar-at-cat-and-fiddle/), Forest Chapel, Macclesfield Forest, and Tegg's Nose Country Park. Very exposed moorland. Lunch in Buxton New Road, or Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest. Buxton New Road lunch stop: the [Peak View Restaurant & Tea Room](http://www.peakviewtearooms.co.uk). Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest lunch stops: the [Forest Distillery](https://theforestdistillery.com), or the Stanley Arms Hotel. 24 km. 6.5 hours.
BUX.publicWalksCircular:
**[Buxton Circular via Combs Moss](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-via-combs-moss/)**: Quiet moorland plateau, gritstone scarps, green valleys, reservoirs, and hillfort promontory. Sights: Castle Naze, Black Edge, Combs Edge, Combs Reservoir, and Corbar Hill. Can be muddy. Lunch in Combs: the [Beehive Inn & Farm Shop](http://thebeehiveinn.co.uk/). 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Buxton Circular via Chee Dale](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-or-to-monsal-dale-via-the-wye-valley/)**: Limestone gorges, stepping stones, overhanging rockfaces, dismantled railway line, limekilns, high pastures, dry valleys, and quiet villages. Sights: Chee Dale, Miller's Dale Viaducts, Five Wells Chambered Tomb, Deep Dale, Wye Valley, and Monsal Trail. Paths flood after rain. Lunch in Miller's Dale, or Millers Dale. Miller's Dale lunch stop: the Angler's Rest. Millers Dale lunch stop: the [Refreshment Room](https://www.blueberryfood.co.uk/the-refreshment-room). 26 km. 7.5 hours.

**[Buxton Circular via the Dragon's Back](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-via-the-dragons-back/)**: Limestone uplands, remote valleys, dramatic reef knoll ridges, dry valleys, disused quarries, and open moorland. Sights: Chrome Hill, Parkhouse Hill, Hitter Hill, High Wheeldon, High Edge, Dowel Dale, Harpur Hill Quarry, and Blue Lagoon. Steep scrambling sections. Lunch in Crowdecote, or Longnor. Crowdecote lunch stop: the [Packhorse Inn](https://www.thepackhorseinn.net). Longnor lunch stops: the [Ye Olde Cheshire Cheese](https://www.robinsonsbrewery.com/pubs/ye-olde-cheshire-cheese-inn-longnor), the Longnor Fish and Chip Shop and Tea Room, or the Cobbles Coffee Shop. 26 km. 7 hours.

**[Buxton Circular via Axe Edge Moor and Three Shires Head](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-via-axe-edge-moor-and-three-shires-head/)**: Open upland moor, broad grassy and gravel paths, boggy moorland sections with groughs and peat hags, scenic river valleys, disused quarries, pastures, and long far views. Sights: Solomon's Temple, Three Shires Head, Panniers Bridge, and [Cat & Fiddle](https://theforestdistillery.com/cat-fiddle). Can be boggy. Lunch in Flash Bar, or Flash. Flash Bar lunch stop: the [Flash Bar Café and Shop](http://www.flashbarstores.co.uk/). Flash lunch stop: the New Inn. 27 km. 7 hours.
BUX.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Macclesfield to Buxton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/macclesfield-to-buxton/)**: Residential roads, pastures, rolling hills, quarried hilltop, forest tracks, exposed moorland, lonely upland valleys, and riverside approach into a spa town. Sights: Tegg's Nose Country Park, Macclesfield Forest, Forest Chapel, Shining Tor, Goytsclough Quarry, Pavilion Gardens, [Cat & Fiddle](https://theforestdistillery.com/cat-fiddle/), and [Peak View Restaurant & Tea Room](http://www.peakviewtearooms.co.uk/). Exposed in bad weather. Lunch in Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest: the [Forest Distillery](https://theforestdistillery.com/), or the [Stanley Arms Hotel](https://www.stanleyarms.com/). 25 km. 7 hours.

BWB.name: Bow Brickhill
BWB.adminWalksAll:
**Bow Brickhill to Woburn Sands**: Woods, golf course, gentle hills, landscaped deer park, village streets, playing fields, and golf links. Sights: Woburn Abbey, Woburn Park, [Woburn Safari Park](https://www.woburnsafari.co.uk/), St Mary Old Church, St Mary parish church, Parish Church of St Botolph, and Sierra redwood (Wellingtonia) trees. Can be muddy. Lunch in Woburn: the [Bell Inn](https://www.greeneking-pubs.co.uk/pubs/bedfordshire/bell-hotel-inn/), the [Longs Inn](https://www.longsinn.co.uk/), the [Woburn Brasserie](http://www.woburnbrasserie.co.uk/), or the [Black Horse](http://www.blackhorsewoburn.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 19 km. 230 m uphill. 5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921098649)**

**Bow Brickhill to Leighton Buzzard**: Woodland paths, pine and beech woods, greensand ridge with valley views, riverside meadows, and canal towpath. Sights: Back Wood, Duncombe Wood, Rushmere Country Park, Greensand Ridge Walk, River Ouzel, Grand Union Canal, and Leighton Lock. Can be muddy. Best avoided in winter. Lunch in Great Brickhill, or Rushmere Country Park. Great Brickhill lunch stop: the Old Red Lion. Rushmere Country Park lunch stop: the Tree Tops Cafe. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 4 hours.

**Bow Brickhill to Aspley Guise**: Woods, golf course, gentle hills, landscaped deer park, and village streets. Sights: Woburn Abbey, Woburn Park, [Woburn Safari Park](https://www.woburnsafari.co.uk/), St Mary Old Church, St Mary parish church, Parish Church of St Botolph, and Sierra redwood (Wellingtonia) trees. Can be muddy. Lunch in Woburn: the [Bell Inn](https://www.greeneking-pubs.co.uk/pubs/bedfordshire/bell-hotel-inn/), the [Longs Inn](https://www.longsinn.co.uk/), the [Woburn Brasserie](http://www.woburnbrasserie.co.uk/), or the [Black Horse](http://www.blackhorsewoburn.co.uk/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).
BWB.publicWalksS2S: **Bow Brickhill to Woburn Sands**: Woods, golf course, gentle hills, landscaped deer park, village streets, playing fields, and golf links. Sights: Woburn Abbey, Woburn Park, [Woburn Safari Park](https://www.woburnsafari.co.uk/), St Mary Old Church, St Mary parish church, Parish Church of St Botolph, and Sierra redwood (Wellingtonia) trees. Can be muddy. Lunch in Woburn: the [Bell Inn](https://www.greeneking-pubs.co.uk/pubs/bedfordshire/bell-hotel-inn/), the [Longs Inn](https://www.longsinn.co.uk/), the [Woburn Brasserie](http://www.woburnbrasserie.co.uk/), or the [Black Horse](http://www.blackhorsewoburn.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 19 km. 230 m uphill. 5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921098649)**

BWS.name: Barrow-upon-Soar
BWS.adminWalksAll:
**[Barrow upon Soar to Leicester](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Flat and on decent paths. Sights: Cossington Lock, Watermead Country Park, National Space Centre, and Abbey Park. 19 km. 40 m uphill. 5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/103%20Barrow%20on%20Soar%20to%20Leicester.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925759790)**

**[East Midlands Parkway to Barrow upon Soar](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Flat, mostly on decent paths, and along the River Soar and Grand Union Canal. Sights: Redhill Boatyard, Kegworth, Zouch, River Soar, and Grand Union Canal. Lunch in Zouch, Loughborough, and Barrow: the pubs. 22 km. 40 m uphill. 6 hours. Hard. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/102%20Parkway%20Station%20to%20Barrow%20on%20Soar.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925755432)**
