BXB.name: Broxbourne
BXB.adminWalksAll:
**[Broxbourne Circular via Waltham Abbey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/broxbourne-circular-via-waltham-abbey/)**: Flat river floodplain, towpaths, water meadows, lakeside paths, woodland strips, farmland fields, and open parkland. Sights: [River Lee Country Park](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/river-lee-country-park), [Waltham Abbey](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waltham_Abbey_(town)), [Waltham Abbey Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waltham_Abbey_Church), [Church of the Holy Cross](http://www.walthamabbeychurch.co.uk), [Royal Gunpowder Mills](https://www.royalgunpowdermills.com), [Lee Valley White Water Centre](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/lee-valley-white-water-centre), [Broxbourne Waterside](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Broxbourne), [Harold Godwinson](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Harold_Godwinson), Wildlife Discovery Centre, Cornmill Meadows Dragonfly Sanctuary, Waltham Abbey Gardens, Epping Forest District Museum, St Augustine's Church, Waltham Abbey Town Hall, 2012 Olympic Shield, Ancestor sculpture, and Broxbourne Mill. Can be muddy. Lunch in Waltham Abbey: the [Angel](https://www.mcmullens.co.uk/local-pub/angel), the Sun Inn, the Crown, the Kalik Coffee House, or the Bake You Happy. 19 km. 4 hours.

**[Broxbourne Circular via New River](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/broxbourne-circular/)**: Canal towpath, parkland, Spital Brook valley, ancient diverse woodland, streams, boardwalks, and green lanes. Sights: New River, Broxbourne Woods National Nature Reserve, Broxbourne Pumping Station, Wormley Wood, and Cowheath Wood. Can be muddy. Lunch in Wormley West End: the [Woodman & Olive](http://www.thewoodmanandolive.com/). 24 km. 5.5 hours.

**Broxbourne to Cheshunt**: A shorter variant of [Broxbourne Circular via Waltham Abbey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/broxbourne-circular-via-waltham-abbey/). Flat river floodplain, towpaths, water meadows, lakeside paths, woodland strips, and open parkland. Sights: [River Lee Country Park](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/river-lee-country-park), [Waltham Abbey Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waltham_Abbey_Church), [Church of the Holy Cross](http://www.walthamabbeychurch.co.uk), [Royal Gunpowder Mills](https://www.royalgunpowdermills.com), Wildlife Discovery Centre, Cornmill Meadows Dragonfly Sanctuary, Waltham Abbey Gardens, and Epping Forest District Museum. Can be muddy. Lunch in Waltham Abbey: the [Angel](https://www.mcmullens.co.uk/local-pub/angel), the Sun Inn, the Crown, the Kalik Coffee House, or the Bake You Happy. 15 km. 3 hours.

**Broxbourne to Bayford**: An alternative variant of [Broxbourne Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/broxbourne-circular/). Canal towpath, parkland, Spital Brook valley, ancient diverse woodland, streams, boardwalks, and green lanes. Sights: New River, Broxbourne Woods National Nature Reserve, Broxbourne Pumping Station, Wormley Wood, and Cowheath Wood. Can be muddy. Lunch in Wormley West End: the [Woodman & Olive](http://www.thewoodmanandolive.com/). 18 km.

**Cheshunt to Broxbourne**: An alternative variant of [Broxbourne Circular via Waltham Abbey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/broxbourne-circular-via-waltham-abbey/). Flat river floodplain, towpaths, water meadows, lakeside paths, woodland strips, orchid area boardwalk, and open parkland. Sights: [River Lee Country Park](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/river-lee-country-park), [Waltham Abbey Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waltham_Abbey_Church), [Church of the Holy Cross](http://www.walthamabbeychurch.co.uk), [Royal Gunpowder Mills](https://www.royalgunpowdermills.com), [Lee Valley White Water Centre](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/lee-valley-white-water-centre), [Broxbourne Waterside](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Broxbourne), Wildlife Discovery Centre, Cornmill Meadows Dragonfly Sanctuary, North Met Pit Orchid Area, Waltham Abbey Gardens, and Epping Forest District Museum. Can be muddy. Lunch in Waltham Abbey: the [Angel](https://www.mcmullens.co.uk/local-pub/angel), the Sun Inn, the Crown, the Kalik Coffee House, or the Bake You Happy. 16 km. 3.5 hours.
BXB.publicWalksCircular:
**[Broxbourne Circular via Waltham Abbey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/broxbourne-circular-via-waltham-abbey/)**: Flat river floodplain, towpaths, water meadows, lakeside paths, woodland strips, farmland fields, and open parkland. Sights: [River Lee Country Park](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/river-lee-country-park), [Waltham Abbey](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waltham_Abbey_(town)), [Waltham Abbey Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waltham_Abbey_Church), [Church of the Holy Cross](http://www.walthamabbeychurch.co.uk), [Royal Gunpowder Mills](https://www.royalgunpowdermills.com), [Lee Valley White Water Centre](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/lee-valley-white-water-centre), [Broxbourne Waterside](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Broxbourne), [Harold Godwinson](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Harold_Godwinson), Wildlife Discovery Centre, Cornmill Meadows Dragonfly Sanctuary, Waltham Abbey Gardens, Epping Forest District Museum, St Augustine's Church, Waltham Abbey Town Hall, 2012 Olympic Shield, Ancestor sculpture, and Broxbourne Mill. Can be muddy. Lunch in Waltham Abbey: the [Angel](https://www.mcmullens.co.uk/local-pub/angel), the Sun Inn, the Crown, the Kalik Coffee House, or the Bake You Happy. 19 km. 4 hours.

**[Broxbourne Circular via New River](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/broxbourne-circular/)**: Canal towpath, parkland, Spital Brook valley, ancient diverse woodland, streams, boardwalks, and green lanes. Sights: New River, Broxbourne Woods National Nature Reserve, Broxbourne Pumping Station, Wormley Wood, and Cowheath Wood. Can be muddy. Lunch in Wormley West End: the [Woodman & Olive](http://www.thewoodmanandolive.com/). 24 km. 5.5 hours.

BXD.name: Buxted
BXD.adminWalksAll:
**[Buxted Circular Walk](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxted-circular/)**: Gentle mix of fields and woods, Weald countryside, and views of the South Downs ridge. Can be muddy. Lunch in Poundgate: the Crow and Gate. 16 km.

**Buxted to Ashurst**: An alternative variant of [Ashurst Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-circular/). River valleys, woodland, elevated Ashdown Forest heathland, Scots pine clumps, Wealden farmland, and villages. Sights: [Ashdown Forest](https://ashdownforest.org/walking), Gills Lap Clump, King's Standing Clump, Heffalump Trap, Pooh Sticks Bridge, [Pooh Corner](https://www.poohcorner.co.uk), [Piglet's Tearoom](https://www.poohcorner.co.uk/tearoom), St Mary the Virgin, Lych Gate Cottage, Linkhorns Farmhouse, Holy Trinity Church, and St Mary the Virgin. Can be muddy. Lunch in Poundgate: the [Crow & Gate Inn](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thecrowandgatecrowborough). 24 km. 6 hours.

**[Uckfield to Buxted via East Sussex National Golf Course](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-buxted/)**: Undulating Low Weald countryside, Uck Valley, golf course, stream valleys, old deer park, parkland, and bluebell woods. Sights: East Sussex National Golf Course, High Cross Estate, Tickerage Mill, Buxted Park, and Buxted House. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Blackboys: the [Blackboys Inn](http://www.theblackboys.co.uk/). 21 km. 5 hours.

**Uckfield to Buxted via Tickerage Mill**: A shorter variant of [Uckfield to Buxted](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-buxted/). Undulating Low Weald countryside, stream valleys, parkland, bluebell woods, and routed through Framfield. Sights: Tickerage Mill, Buxted Park, and Buxted House. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Framfield: the [Hare & Hounds](https://hareandhounds.net/). 18 km.
BXD.publicWalksCircular: **[Buxted Circular Walk](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxted-circular/)**: Gentle mix of fields and woods, Weald countryside, and views of the South Downs ridge. Can be muddy. Lunch in Poundgate: the Crow and Gate. 16 km.
BXD.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Uckfield to Buxted via East Sussex National Golf Course](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-buxted/)**: Undulating Low Weald countryside, Uck Valley, golf course, stream valleys, old deer park, parkland, and bluebell woods. Sights: East Sussex National Golf Course, High Cross Estate, Tickerage Mill, Buxted Park, and Buxted House. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Blackboys: the [Blackboys Inn](http://www.theblackboys.co.uk/). 21 km. 5 hours.

BXW.name: Box Hill & Westhumble
BXW.adminWalksAll:
**Box Hill via Mickleham**: Hills and woodland. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 1 November 2020.* 8 km. 220 m uphill. 2.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/921270625)**

**[Box Hill & Westhumble Circular via Betchworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-betchworth/)**: Sustained chalk downland climb, escarpment, disused quarries, Mole Valley riverside meadows, bluebell woods, parkland, and village greens. Sights: [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Salomons Memorial, Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, Betchworth Quarry and Lime Works, [St Michael, Betchworth](https://www.nationalchurchestrust.org/church/betchworth-st-michael), [Betchworth Castle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betchworth_Castle), Stepping Stones, Swiss Cottage, and Christ Church, Brockham. Can be muddy. Lunch in Betchworth, or Brockham. Betchworth lunch stops: the [Red Lion](https://www.theredlionbetchworth.com), or the [Dolphin](https://www.dolphinbetchworth.com). Brockham lunch stops: the [Inn on the Green](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/brockham), or the Royal Oak. 16 km. 4 hours.

**[Box Hill & Westhumble Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-headley-heath-and-mickleham/)**: North Downs hills, open downland, wooded heathland, grassy spurs, chalk paths, beech woodland, and valley views. Sights: [Headley Heath](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/headley-heath), [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Salomons Memorial, Box Hill Fort, St Michael and All Angels, The Gallops, Stane Street, Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, and North Downs Way. Steep descents. Lunch in Mickleham: the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk), or the [King William IV](https://www.thekingwilliamiv.com). 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**Box Hill & Westhumble Circular via Norbury Park and Mickleham**: A shorter variant of [Dorking to Box Hill via Norbury Park and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-to-box-hill-via-norbury-park-and-mickleham/). Rambler favourite! Wooded hills, chalk downland, open grassland, Mole valley views, and North Downs. Sights: [Norbury Park Nature Reserve](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/norbury-park), [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Westhumble Chapel, Broadwood's Tower, and Box Hill Fort. Can be muddy. Lunch in Mickleham: the [King William IV](https://www.thekingwilliamiv.com), or the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk). 11 km. 3 hours.

**Box Hill & Westhumble Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham & Norbury Park**: A shorter variant of [Box Hill Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-headley-heath-and-mickleham/). North Downs hills, open downland, heathland, Norbury Park woodland edge, and valley paths. Sights: [Headley Heath](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/headley-heath), [Norbury Park](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/norbury-park), St Michael and All Angels, The Gallops, and Stane Street. Steep descents. Lunch in Mickleham: the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk). 13 km. 3.5 hours.

**Box Hill & Westhumble Circular via Polesden Lacey**: Quiet valley, woodland, North Downs escarpment, vineyard, and circular route from Westhumble. Sights: Polesden Lacey, Denbies Wine Estate, and Ranmore Common. Best in Apr, May, Sep, Oct and Nov. Lunch in Polesden Lacey: the Granary Cafe, Polesden Lacey. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 13 km.

**Box Hill & Westhumble Circular via Stepping Stones pub**: Steep chalk hills, box and yew woodland, grassy ridges, rural woods, riverside fields, and a valley return through Norbury Park. Sights: Box Hill, Stepping Stones, Juniper Top, White Hill, St Michael & All Angels Church, Norbury Park, and Stepping Stones pub. Steep descents, can be slippery. Lunch in Mickleham: the Running Horses, or the King William IV. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).

**Box Hill & Westhumble Circular via Norbury Park House**: Steep chalk hills, open downland ridge with valley views, Norbury Park woods, vineyard, and bluebell woods. Sights: Box Hill, Stepping Stones, Juniper Top, White Hill, St Michael & All Angels Church, Norbury Park, Norbury Park House, and Denbies Wine Estate. Steep descents, can be slippery. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Mickleham: the Running Horses, or the King William IV. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).

**Box Hill & Westhumble to Leatherhead**: Steep chalk hills, open downland, yew and box woodland, grassy ridges with panoramic views, gentle river valley fields, and historic parkland. Sights: Box Hill, Stepping Stones, Juniper Top, White Hill, St Michael & All Angels Church, Thorncroft Manor, Leopold Salomons Memorial, Norbury Park, Denbies Wine Estate, Box Hill & Westhumble Railway Station, Town Bridge, and King George V Memorial Gardens. Steep descents, can be slippery. Lunch in Mickleham: the Running Horses, or the King William IV. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 11 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Box Hill & Westhumble to Merstham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-to-merstham/)**: North Downs ridge, wooded escarpment, open hilltops, undulating ridge paths, rural farmland, parkland, and golf course. Sights: Box Hill, Reigate Hill, Colley Hill, Gatton Park, and Reigate Fort. Hilly, multiple ascents. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Reigate Hill, or Merstham. Reigate Hill lunch stop: the [Chalk Hare](http://www.chalkhare.co.uk/). Merstham lunch stop: the Feathers. 16 km.

**Box Hill & Westhumble to Dorking via Headley Heath and Mickleham**: An alternative variant of [Box Hill Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-headley-heath-and-mickleham/). North Downs hills, open downland, heathland, woodland edge of Norbury Park, vineyard, and valley paths. Sights: [Headley Heath](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/headley-heath), [Norbury Park](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/norbury-park), [Denbies Wine Estate](https://www.denbies.co.uk), St Michael and All Angels, The Gallops, Stane Street, Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, and Mole Gap Trail. Steep descents. Lunch in Mickleham: the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk). 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**Box Hill & Westhumble to Dorking via Betchworth & Deepdene Trail**: A longer variant of [Box Hill Circular via Betchworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-betchworth/). Chalk downland, escarpment, disused quarries, parkland, Deepdene estate woodland, terraced gardens, and town. Sights: [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Salomons Memorial, Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, Betchworth Quarry and Lime Works, [St Michael, Betchworth](https://www.nationalchurchestrust.org/church/betchworth-st-michael), [Betchworth Castle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betchworth_Castle), Christ Church, Brockham, [Deepdene Trail](https://visitmolevalley.co.uk/the-deepdene-trail), Deepdene Terrace, Deepdene Gardens, Hope Mausoleum, St Martin, Dorking, and Mullins Coffee Shop. Can be muddy. Lunch in Betchworth, or Brockham. Betchworth lunch stops: the [Red Lion](https://www.theredlionbetchworth.com), or the [Dolphin](https://www.dolphinbetchworth.com). Brockham lunch stops: the [Inn on the Green](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/brockham), or the Royal Oak. 18 km. 5 hours.

**Box Hill & Westhumble to Caterham**: A longer variant of [Box Hill to Merstham and Caterham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-to-merstham/). North Downs ridge, wooded escarpment, open hilltops, heathland, suburban approaches, and motorway crossings. Sights: Box Hill, Reigate Hill, Colley Hill, Gatton Park, Reigate Fort, and Happy Valley. Hilly, multiple ascents. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Reigate Hill: the [Chalk Hare](http://www.chalkhare.co.uk/).

**[Dorking to Box Hill & Westhumble via Norbury Park and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-to-box-hill-via-norbury-park-and-mickleham/)**: Rambler favourite! Vineyard, wooded hills, chalk downland, open grassland, Mole valley views, and North Downs. Sights: [Denbies Wine Estate](https://www.denbies.co.uk), [Norbury Park Nature Reserve](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/norbury-park), [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Westhumble Chapel, Broadwood's Tower, and Box Hill Fort. Can be muddy. Lunch in Mickleham: the [King William IV](https://www.thekingwilliamiv.com), or the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk). 14 km. 4 hours.

**Effingham Junction to Box Hill & Westhumble via Polesden Lacey**: Hidden valleys, pretty woodland, gentle pasture, North Downs escarpment with extensive views, and vineyard. Sights: Polesden Lacey, Denbies Wine Estate, Great Ridings Wood, and Ranmore Common. Best in Apr, May, Sep, Oct and Nov. Lunch in Polesden Lacey, or Effingham. Polesden Lacey lunch stop: the Granary Cafe, Polesden Lacey. Effingham lunch stop: the Queen Stage. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 15 km.

**Gomshall to Box Hill & Westhumble**: North Downs Way, open and wooded downland, and extended ridge walking. Sights: [Denbies Wine Estate](http://www.denbies.co.uk). A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 13 km.

**Leatherhead to Box Hill & Westhumble**: Gentle river valley start, steep chalk climbs after lunch, open downland ridges, yew and box woodland, and stepping stones. Sights: Town Bridge, Thorncroft Manor, Norbury Park, St Michael & All Angels Church, White Hill, Juniper Top, Box Hill, Stepping Stones, Denbies Wine Estate, and Leopold Salomons Memorial. Steep descents, can be slippery. Lunch in Mickleham: the Running Horses, or the King William IV. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 11 km. 3.5 hours.

**Dorking to Box Hill & Westhumble via Betchworth**: An alternative variant of [Box Hill Circular via Betchworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-betchworth/). Mole Valley riverside meadows, chalk escarpment, disused quarries, parkland, and village greens. Sights: [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, Betchworth Quarry and Lime Works, [St Michael, Betchworth](https://www.nationalchurchestrust.org/church/betchworth-st-michael), [Betchworth Castle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betchworth_Castle), Stepping Stones, and Christ Church, Brockham. Can be muddy. Lunch in Betchworth, or Brockham. Betchworth lunch stops: the [Red Lion](https://www.theredlionbetchworth.com), or the [Dolphin](https://www.dolphinbetchworth.com). Brockham lunch stops: the [Inn on the Green](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/brockham), or the Royal Oak. 16 km.

**Dorking to Box Hill & Westhumble via Headley Heath and Mickleham**: A variant of [Box Hill Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-headley-heath-and-mickleham/). North Downs hills, open downland, wooded heathland, grassy spurs, chalk paths, beech woodland, and valley views. Sights: [Headley Heath](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/headley-heath), [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Salomons Memorial, Box Hill Fort, St Michael and All Angels, The Gallops, Stane Street, Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, and North Downs Way. Steep descents. Lunch in Mickleham: the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk), or the [King William IV](https://www.thekingwilliamiv.com). 16 km.

**Guildford to Box Hill & Westhumble**: Sandy ridges, downland escarpments, extended North Downs Way, open downland, and wooded sections. Sights: Guildford Castle, Pewley Down, St Martha-on-the-Hill Church, Shere, and [Denbies Wine Estate](http://www.denbies.co.uk). Lunch in Shere, or Gomshall. Shere lunch stops: the White Horse, the William Bray, the Dabbling Duck, or the Hilly's Tea Shop. Gomshall lunch stops: the Compasses Inn, or the Gomshall Mill. A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 23 km.
BXW.publicWalksCircular: **Box Hill via Mickleham**: Hills and woodland. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 8 km. 220 m uphill. 2.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/921270625)**

CAA.name: Coventry Arena
CAA.adminWalksAll: **[Coventry Arena Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-1236)**: Canal towpath, former colliery park, formal gardens, wildflower meadows, and market town streets. Sights: [Coventry Canal](www.canalrivertrust.org.uk/coventry-canal), [Miners Welfare Park](www.facebook.com/bedworthcommunitygardens/), Nicholas Chamberlain Almshouses, [Bedworth Heritage Centre](www.bedworth-society.co.uk/heritage-centre), and Hawkesbury Junction. Point-to-point trail. Lunch in Coventry Arena, or Bedworth. Coventry Arena lunch stop: the [Arena Shopping Park](www.arenashopping.com). Bedworth lunch stops: the [Harry's Bakery](www.facebook.com/HarrysBakery/), or the [JoJo's Bistro](https://www.facebook.com/joannecharley1971/). 5 km. 2 hours.
CAA.publicWalksCircular: **[Coventry Arena Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-1236)**: Canal towpath, former colliery park, formal gardens, wildflower meadows, and market town streets. Sights: [Coventry Canal](www.canalrivertrust.org.uk/coventry-canal), [Miners Welfare Park](www.facebook.com/bedworthcommunitygardens/), Nicholas Chamberlain Almshouses, [Bedworth Heritage Centre](www.bedworth-society.co.uk/heritage-centre), and Hawkesbury Junction. Point-to-point trail. Lunch in Coventry Arena, or Bedworth. Coventry Arena lunch stop: the [Arena Shopping Park](www.arenashopping.com). Bedworth lunch stops: the [Harry's Bakery](www.facebook.com/HarrysBakery/), or the [JoJo's Bistro](https://www.facebook.com/joannecharley1971/). 5 km. 2 hours.

CASH.name: Ash
CASH.adminWalksAll:
**[Ash Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-circular-via-the-mod-ranges/)**: Lowland heathland, conifer and broad-leaved woodland, stony paths, boggy sections, canal towpath, gorse and bracken, and with many short sharp climbs and wide views. Sights: [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/conservation/projects/3), Basingstoke Canal, Guildford Cathedral, and Keogh Barracks. MOD range closures apply. Best in Apr, May and Aug. Lunch in Ash Vale: the Swan. 22 km.

**[Ash Vale Circular via North Downs Way](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-circular-via-north-downs-way/)**: Flat canal towpath, disused railway line, heathland commons, North Downs Way, open fields, mixed woodland, and the barren Ash Ranges. Sights: Basingstoke Canal, Ash Wharf, Ash Green to Tongham railway, Puttenham Common, St John the Baptist, Broadstreet Common, Ash/Pirbright Ranges, and Hillbury Hill Fort. MOD firing range closures. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in Puttenham: the Good Intent. 25 km. 6.5 hours.

**[Ash to Brookwood via American Military Cemetery](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/)**: Lowland heathland, firing range ridgeline, open grassland, conifer and broad-leaved woods, farmland fields, and historic cemetery grounds. Sights: [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/reserves/9), St Michael and All Angels Church, Henry Morton Stanley's Grave, [Brookwood Cemetery](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/), [Brookwood Military Cemetery](http://www.cwgc.org), [Orthodox Church and Monastery of St Edward](http://www.saintedwardbrotherhood.org), American Military Cemetery, Brookwood Memorial, [Cemetery Railway remains (North and South station platforms)](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/cemetery_railway.htm), and Colquhoun Mausoleum. Army range closures apply. Lunch in Pirbright: the White Hart, or the Cricketers on The Green. 15 km.

**[Ash Vale to Worplesdon via Basingstoke Canal Visitors Centre](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-to-worplesdon/)**: Basingstoke Canal towpath, hilly MOD heathland (Ash Ranges), pine-covered heath, farmland tracks, mixed woodland, open common, and mature woodland on Whitmoor Common. Sights: Basingstoke Canal, [Basingstoke Canal Visitors Centre](http://www3.hants.gov.uk/basingstoke-canal/canal-centre.htm), Ash Ranges, Merrist Wood College, [St Mary the Virgin Church](http://www.exploringsurreyspast.org.uk/GetRecord/SHHER_8732), Whitmoor Common, Fox Way, Cobbett Hill Earth Station, and Brookwood Cemetery. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Stanford Common: the [Royal Oak](http://www.royaloak-pub-pirbright.co.uk/). 19 km.

**Ash Vale to Wanborough via Ash Ranges**: An alternative variant of [Ash Vale to Worplesdon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-to-worplesdon/). Basingstoke Canal towpath, MOD firing range heathland, pine woodland, and farmland. Sights: Basingstoke Canal and Ash Ranges. MOD closures apply. 11 km.

**Ash to Brookwood via Ash Ranges**: A shorter variant of [Ash to Brookwood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/). Lowland heathland, firing range ridgeline, woods, and farmland fields. Sights: [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/reserves/9). Army range closures apply. 11 km.

**Ash Vale to Ash via Ash Ranges**: A shorter variant of [Ash Vale to Worplesdon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-to-worplesdon/). Basingstoke Canal towpath, MOD firing range heathland, open heath, and pine woodland. Sights: Basingstoke Canal and Ash Ranges. MOD closures apply. 9 km.

**Ash Vale to Brookwood**: An alternative variant of [Ash Vale to Worplesdon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-to-worplesdon/). Basingstoke Canal towpath, MOD firing range heathland, Pirbright village, Brookwood Cemetery, and pine woodland. Sights: Basingstoke Canal, Ash Ranges, and Brookwood Cemetery. MOD closures apply. 16 km.

**Ash to Brookwood via Glades of Remembrance**: A variant of [Ash to Brookwood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/). Lowland heathland, firing range ridgeline, woods, farmland fields, and extensive cemetery grounds including neglected sections. Sights: [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/reserves/9), Colquhoun Mausoleum, Glades of Remembrance, Pelham-Clinton Memorial, Drake Family Mausoleum, [Brookwood Cemetery](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/), [Brookwood Military Cemetery](http://www.cwgc.org), [Orthodox Church and Monastery of St Edward](http://www.saintedwardbrotherhood.org), St Michael and All Angels Church, and Henry Morton Stanley's Grave. Uneven pauper burial ground. Lunch in Pirbright: the White Hart, or the Cricketers on The Green. 16 km.

**Ash Vale to Worplesdon via Basingstoke Canal**: A shorter variant of [Ash Vale to Worplesdon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-to-worplesdon/). Basingstoke Canal towpath, restricted MOD firing range, pine-covered heathland, farmland tracks, mixed woodland, and Whitmoor Common. Sights: Basingstoke Canal, Ash Ranges, [St Mary the Virgin Church](http://www.exploringsurreyspast.org.uk/GetRecord/SHHER_8732), and Whitmoor Common. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Stanford Common: the [Royal Oak](http://www.royaloak-pub-pirbright.co.uk/). 17 km.

**Ash Vale to Wanborough via North Downs Way & Broadstreet Common**: A shorter variant of [Ash Vale Circular via North Downs Way](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-circular-via-north-downs-way/). Canal towpath, disused railway line, heathland, North Downs Way, open fields, and mixed woodland. Sights: Basingstoke Canal, Puttenham Common, St John the Baptist, and Broadstreet Common. Can be muddy. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in Puttenham: the Good Intent. 20 km.

**Ash to Brookwood via Basingstoke Canal**: A longer variant of [Ash to Brookwood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/). Canal towpath, heathland, woods, farmland fields, and historic cemetery grounds. Sights: Basingstoke Canal, [Brookwood Cemetery](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/), [Brookwood Military Cemetery](http://www.cwgc.org), St Michael and All Angels Church, and Henry Morton Stanley's Grave. Army range closures apply. Lunch in Ash Vale, or Pirbright. Ash Vale lunch stops: the Swan, or the Napiers. Pirbright lunch stops: the White Hart, or the Cricketers on The Green. 22 km.

**Ash to Ash Vale**: A shorter variant of [Ash Vale Circular via North Downs Way](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-circular-via-north-downs-way/). Flat canal towpath, disused railway line, heathland commons, North Downs Way, open fields, mixed woodland, and the Ash Ranges. Sights: Basingstoke Canal, Puttenham Common, St John the Baptist, Broadstreet Common, and Ash/Pirbright Ranges. MOD firing range closures. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in Puttenham: the Good Intent. 22 km.

**Ash Vale to Ash via North Downs Way**: A variant of [Ash Vale Circular via North Downs Way](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-circular-via-north-downs-way/). Flat canal towpath, disused railway line, heathland commons, North Downs Way, open fields, mixed woodland, and ridge traverse of Ash Ranges. Sights: Basingstoke Canal, Puttenham Common, St John the Baptist, and Ash/Pirbright Ranges. MOD firing range closures. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in Puttenham: the Good Intent. 24 km.

**Wanborough to Ash Vale**: A shorter variant of [Ash Vale Circular via North Downs Way](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-circular-via-north-downs-way/). North Downs Way, heathland commons, open fields, mixed woodland, and the Ash Ranges. Sights: Puttenham Common, St John the Baptist, Broadstreet Common, and Ash/Pirbright Ranges. MOD firing range closures. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in Puttenham: the Good Intent. 16 km.

**Brookwood to Ash**: A shorter variant of [Ash to Brookwood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/). Cemetery grounds, farmland fields, lowland heathland, and firing range ridgeline. Sights: [Brookwood Cemetery](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/) and [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/reserves/9). Army range closures apply. 9 km.

**Brookwood to Ash Vale**: A shorter variant of [Ash to Brookwood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-to-brookwood/). Cemetery grounds, heathland, and firing range ridgeline. Sights: [Brookwood Cemetery](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/) and [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/reserves/9). Army range closures apply. 8 km.

Sights: [Ash Ranges](http://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/reserves/9), St Michael and All Angels Church, Henry Morton Stanley's Grave, [Brookwood Cemetery](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/), [Brookwood Military Cemetery](http://www.cwgc.org), [Orthodox Church and Monastery of St Edward](http://www.saintedwardbrotherhood.org), American Military Cemetery, Brookwood Memorial, [Cemetery Railway remains (North and South station platforms)](http://www.brookwoodcemetery.com/cemetery_railway.htm), and Colquhoun Mausoleum. **[Ash Vale to Worplesdon via Basingstoke Canal Visitors Centre](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-to-worplesdon/)**: Basingstoke Canal towpath, hilly MOD heathland (Ash Ranges), pine-covered heath, farmland tracks, mixed woodland, open common, and mature woodland on Whitmoor Common. Sights: Basingstoke Canal, [Basingstoke Canal Visitors Centre](http://www3.hants.gov.uk/basingstoke-canal/canal-centre.htm), Ash Ranges, Merrist Wood College, [St Mary the Virgin Church](http://www.exploringsurreyspast.org.uk/GetRecord/SHHER_8732), Whitmoor Common, Fox Way, Cobbett Hill Earth Station, and Brookwood Cemetery. CAT.name: Caterham
CAT.adminWalksAll:
**[Caterham to Knockholt](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/caterham-to-knockholt/)**: North Downs escarpment, woods carpeted with wild garlic and bluebells, open pasture, arable fields, fine southward views, and some M25 road noise early on. Sights: [Titsey Place](http://www.titsey.org/), [North Downs Way](http://www.nationaltrail.co.uk/northdowns/), [Botley Hill](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Botley_Hill), Oxted Downs, Chevening House, and Titsey Brewing Taproom. Can be slippery. Lunch in Caterham area, Botley Hill, or Titsey. Caterham area lunch stop: the [Godstone Vineyards](http://www.godstonevineyards.uk/). Botley Hill lunch stop: the [Botley Hill Farmhouse](http://www.botleyhill-farmhouse.co.uk/). Titsey lunch stop: the [Titsey Brewing Taproom](https://titseybrewingco.com/taproom/). 26 km.

**[Kenley to Caterham via Woldingham Garden Village](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kenley-to-caterham/)**: Chalk downland, meadows, bluebell woods, hillside paths, undulating valleys, residential alleyways, and hilltop village. Sights: [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), Sanderstead to Whyteleafe Countryside Area, King's Wood, All Saints Warlingham, Blanchman's Farm Nature Reserve, Woldingham Garden Village, Peace Centre, Madeira Walk, St Paul Woldingham, Marden Park, and Woldingham School. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Warlingham: the [Horseshoe](https://www.baronspubs.com/horseshoe), or the [White Lion](https://www.emberinns.co.uk/nationalsearch/southeast/the-white-lion-warlingham#). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**Kenley to Caterham via Riddlesdown**: A shorter variant of [Kenley to Caterham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kenley-to-caterham/). Chalk downland, meadows, bluebell woods, direct valley route through Marden Park, and hillside paths. Sights: [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), Sanderstead to Whyteleafe Countryside Area, King's Wood, All Saints Warlingham, Blanchman's Farm Nature Reserve, Marden Park, and Woldingham School. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Warlingham: the [Horseshoe](https://www.baronspubs.com/horseshoe), or the [White Lion](https://www.emberinns.co.uk/nationalsearch/southeast/the-white-lion-warlingham#). 12 km. 3 hours.

**Box Hill & Westhumble to Caterham**: A longer variant of [Box Hill to Merstham and Caterham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-to-merstham/). North Downs ridge, wooded escarpment, open hilltops, heathland, suburban approaches, and motorway crossings. Sights: Box Hill, Reigate Hill, Colley Hill, Gatton Park, Reigate Fort, and Happy Valley. Hilly, multiple ascents. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Reigate Hill: the [Chalk Hare](http://www.chalkhare.co.uk/).
CAT.publicWalksS2S: **[Caterham to Knockholt](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/caterham-to-knockholt/)**: North Downs escarpment, woods carpeted with wild garlic and bluebells, open pasture, arable fields, fine southward views, and some M25 road noise early on. Sights: [Titsey Place](http://www.titsey.org/), [North Downs Way](http://www.nationaltrail.co.uk/northdowns/), [Botley Hill](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Botley_Hill), Oxted Downs, Chevening House, and Titsey Brewing Taproom. Can be slippery. Lunch in Caterham area, Botley Hill, or Titsey. Caterham area lunch stop: the [Godstone Vineyards](http://www.godstonevineyards.uk/). Botley Hill lunch stop: the [Botley Hill Farmhouse](http://www.botleyhill-farmhouse.co.uk/). Titsey lunch stop: the [Titsey Brewing Taproom](https://titseybrewingco.com/taproom/). 26 km.
CAT.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Kenley to Caterham via Woldingham Garden Village](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kenley-to-caterham/)**: Chalk downland, meadows, bluebell woods, hillside paths, undulating valleys, residential alleyways, and hilltop village. Sights: [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), Sanderstead to Whyteleafe Countryside Area, King's Wood, All Saints Warlingham, Blanchman's Farm Nature Reserve, Woldingham Garden Village, Peace Centre, Madeira Walk, St Paul Woldingham, Marden Park, and Woldingham School. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Warlingham: the [Horseshoe](https://www.baronspubs.com/horseshoe), or the [White Lion](https://www.emberinns.co.uk/nationalsearch/southeast/the-white-lion-warlingham#). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

CBAR.name: Barnes
CBAR.adminWalksAll:
**Barnes to Putney Bridge via Chiswick House & Gardens**: A variant of [Norbiton to Hammersmith or Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/norbiton-to-hammersmith-or-putney/). Village green, Thames riverfront, historic riverside houses, landscape garden, modern riverside developments, riverside park, and palace grounds. Sights: [Chiswick House & Gardens](https://chiswickhouseandgardens.org.uk), Hogarth Tomb-Monument, Craven Cottage, [Fulham Palace](https://www.fulhampalace.org), and [London Wetland Centre](https://www.wwt.org.uk/wetland-centres/london). Lunch in Barnes: the [Sun Inn](https://www.thesuninnbarnes.co.uk), the [Coach & Horses](https://www.coachandhorsesbarnes.co.uk), the [Bulls Head](https://www.thebullsheadbarnes.com), or the [Watermans Arms](https://www.watermansarms.co.uk). 10 km.

**Barnes to Putney Bridge via Barnes Common**: A shorter variant of [Beverley Brook](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/beverley-brook/). Commons, brookside paths, and Thames-side esplanade. Sights: Barnes Common and Barn Elms. Lunch in Barnes: the Café Loris, or the [Stag's Head](https://www.stagsheadbarnes.co.uk/). 4 km.

**Wimbledon to Barnes Bridge**: A shorter variant of [Wimbledon to Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-putney/). Suburban streets, open common, landscaped park, woodland riverside path, Royal Park grassland, suburban roads, and Thames riverbank. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](https://cannizaropark.com/about-cannizaro-park), [Wimbledon Common](https://www.wpcc.org.uk), [Richmond Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/visit/parks/richmond-park), Millennium Fountain, Caesar's Camp Iron Age hill fort, Barnes Railway Bridge, and Ye White Hart. Can be muddy. Lunch in Richmond Park, or Barnes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Roehampton Gate café. Barnes lunch stops: the [Crossing](https://www.thecrossing-barnes.co.uk), the [Ye White Hart](https://www.whitehartbarnes.co.uk), the [Watermans Arms](https://www.watermansarms.co.uk), the [Bull's Head](https://www.thebullsheadbarnes.com), the [Coach & Horses](https://www.coachandhorsesbarnes.co.uk), or the [Sun Inn](https://www.thesuninnbarnes.co.uk). 11 km. 2.5 hours.

**Norbiton to Barnes Bridge**: A variant of [Norbiton to Hammersmith or Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/norbiton-to-hammersmith-or-putney/). Tree-lined residential streets, large deer park, woodland garden, open grassland, and riverside common. Sights: [Richmond Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond-park), Isabella Plantation, Pen Ponds, Thatched House Lodge, and White Lodge. Best in Mar–May. 10 km.

**New Malden to Barnes**: A shorter variant of [Beverley Brook](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/beverley-brook/). Parkland, commons, brookside paths, and wet woodland. Sights: Wimbledon Common, Richmond Park, and Barnes Common. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Wimbledon, London SW15, East Sheen, or Barnes. West Wimbledon lunch stop: the [Da Mimmo](https://www.damimmowimbledon.com/). London SW15 lunch stop: the [Roehampton Gate Café](https://www.colicci.co.uk/roehampton-gate-cafe). East Sheen lunch stop: the [Palewell Café on the Common](https://www.colicci.co.uk/roehampton-gate-cafe). Barnes lunch stops: the Café Loris, or the [Stag's Head](https://www.stagsheadbarnes.co.uk/). 9 km.

CBE.name: Canterbury East
CBE.adminWalksAll:
**Chilham to Canterbury via the Pilgrim's Way**: Rambler favourite! From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 23 October 2022.* 14 km. 180 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/849788927)**

**[Canterbury East Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/canterbury-circular/)**: Flat Kent countryside, arable fields, woods, ancient villages, oast houses, polytunnel farms, and riverside. Sights: Canterbury Cathedral, Wickhambreaux church, Littlebourne church tithe barn, Stodmarsh church, and Fordwich. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Wickhambreaux, Ickham, or Stodmarsh. Wickhambreaux lunch stop: the Rose Inn. Ickham lunch stop: the Duke William. Stodmarsh lunch stop: the Red Lion. 22 km.

**Canterbury East to Sturry**: A shorter variant of [Canterbury Circular via Bekesbourne and Sturry](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/canterbury-circular/). Flat Kent countryside, arable fields, woods, ancient villages, and riverside near Fordwich. Sights: Wickhambreaux church, Littlebourne church tithe barn, Stodmarsh church, and Fordwich. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Wickhambreaux, Ickham, or Stodmarsh. Wickhambreaux lunch stop: the Rose Inn. Ickham lunch stop: the Duke William. Stodmarsh lunch stop: the Red Lion. 18 km.

**Chilham to Canterbury East**: River valleys, lakes, hop fields, apple orchards, mixed woodland, heathland, nature reserve, university parkland, and medieval city streets. Sights: Canterbury Cathedral, Westgate, Old Weavers' House, Blackfriars Gallery, Old Synagogue, Kings School, Dane John Mound, Blean Woods Nature Reserve, University of Kent, Chartham Vineyard, St Mary's Church, and St Peter the Apostle Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chartham, Canterbury Chartham Garden Centre, or Blean. Chartham lunch stops: the Artichoke pub, or the Local. Canterbury Chartham Garden Centre lunch stop: the Origins Restaurant & Patisserie Cafe. Blean lunch stop: the Hare at Blean. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 20 km. 5 hours.

**Bekesbourne to Canterbury East**: A variant of [Canterbury Circular via Bekesbourne and Sturry](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/canterbury-circular/). Flat Kent countryside, arable fields, woods, ancient villages, and riverside. Sights: Wickhambreaux church, Littlebourne church tithe barn, Stodmarsh church, and Fordwich. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Wickhambreaux, Ickham, or Stodmarsh. Wickhambreaux lunch stop: the Rose Inn. Ickham lunch stop: the Duke William. Stodmarsh lunch stop: the Red Lion. 16 km.

**Sturry to Canterbury East**: A variant of [Canterbury Circular via Bekesbourne and Sturry](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/canterbury-circular/). Flat Kent countryside, woods, arable fields, ancient villages, and riverside. Sights: Wickhambreaux church, Stodmarsh church, and Fordwich. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Wickhambreaux, Ickham, or Stodmarsh. Wickhambreaux lunch stop: the Rose Inn. Ickham lunch stop: the Duke William. Stodmarsh lunch stop: the Red Lion. 18 km.

From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 14 km. 180 m uphill. 3 hours. CBED.name: Bedford
CBED.adminWalksAll:
**[Bedford Circular via Stevington Cross](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bedford-circular/)**: Largely flat riverside paths, fields, woods, parks, and pedestrianised town centre. Sights: Bromham Bridge, Bromham Mill, Stevington Cross, Stevington Windmill, St Mary the Virgin Church, St Peter's Church, Bedford Castle, St Paul's Church, John Bunyan Statue, Bedford Park, and Bedford School. Can be muddy. Lunch in Bromham, Stevington, Pavenham, Oakley, or Clapham. Bromham lunch stops: the [Bromham Mill coffee shop](https://themillcoffeeshop.co.uk/), or the [Swan](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/bedfordshire/swan). Stevington lunch stop: the [Royal George](https://theroyalgeorgestevington.co.uk/). Pavenham lunch stop: the [Cock Inn](https://www.thecockatpavenham.co.uk/). Oakley lunch stop: the [Bedford Arms](https://bedfordarmsoakley.co.uk/). Clapham lunch stop: the [Fox and Hounds](https://www.foxandhoundsclapham.co.uk/index). 26 km.

**Bedford Circular via Bromham Bridge**: A shorter variant of [Bedford Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bedford-circular/). Flat riverside paths, old railway track, fields, and road sections. Sights: Bromham Bridge, Bromham Mill, Stevington Windmill, and John Bunyan Statue. Largely on road. Lunch in Bromham, or Clapham. Bromham lunch stops: the [Bromham Mill coffee shop](https://themillcoffeeshop.co.uk/), or the [Swan](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/bedfordshire/swan). Clapham lunch stop: the [Fox and Hounds](https://www.foxandhoundsclapham.co.uk/index). 20 km.
CBED.publicWalksCircular: **[Bedford Circular via Stevington Cross](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bedford-circular/)**: Largely flat riverside paths, fields, woods, parks, and pedestrianised town centre. Sights: Bromham Bridge, Bromham Mill, Stevington Cross, Stevington Windmill, St Mary the Virgin Church, St Peter's Church, Bedford Castle, St Paul's Church, John Bunyan Statue, Bedford Park, and Bedford School. Can be muddy. Lunch in Bromham, Stevington, Pavenham, Oakley, or Clapham. Bromham lunch stops: the [Bromham Mill coffee shop](https://themillcoffeeshop.co.uk/), or the [Swan](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/bedfordshire/swan). Stevington lunch stop: the [Royal George](https://theroyalgeorgestevington.co.uk/). Pavenham lunch stop: the [Cock Inn](https://www.thecockatpavenham.co.uk/). Oakley lunch stop: the [Bedford Arms](https://bedfordarmsoakley.co.uk/). Clapham lunch stop: the [Fox and Hounds](https://www.foxandhoundsclapham.co.uk/index). 26 km.

CBG.name: Cambridge
CBG.adminWalksAll:
**Cambridge Circular via Grantchester**: Pleasant but nothing spectacular except for Cambridge's architecture. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 15 May 2021.* 12 km. 40 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921492459)**

**[Cambridge Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cambridge-via-grantchester-circular/)**: Flat city streets, college courts, riverside meadow paths, surfaced cycle paths, and quiet village lanes. Sights: King's College Chapel, King's College, Clare College, Trinity College, Trinity Hall, St John's College, Gonville and Caius College, Sidney Sussex College, Pembroke College, Corpus Christi College, Peterhouse, Wren Library, Clare Bridge, Gate of Honour, St Bene't's Church, Holy Trinity Church, Great St Mary's Church, Church of St Edward, Little St Mary's Church, Church of St Andrew and St Mary, Old Vicarage, Rupert Brooke Museum, Orchard Tea Rooms, Cambridge University Botanic Gardens, Byron's Pool, Eagle pub, and Old Cavendish Lab. 15 km. 5 hours.

**Shelford to Cambridge via All Saints Church Little Shelford**: Flat farmland, permissive river paths, open fields, light woodland, and historic city centre streets. Sights: Church of St Mary the Virgin, All Saints Church Little Shelford, Church of St Edmunds Hauxton, All Saints Church Haslingfield, Travelling Telescope, Parish Church of St Andrew and St Mary Grantchester, Orchard Tea Rooms, Old Vicarage Grantchester, Rupert Brooke statue, Queens College, King's College, Clare College, Wren Library, Clare Bridge, Trinity Hall, Gate of Honour, Gonville and Caius College, Trinity College, St John's College, Sidney Sussex College, Great St Mary's Church, Church of St Edward, Saint Bene't's Church, Eagle pub, Corpus Christi College, Old Cavendish Lab, Pembroke College, Peterhouse, Little St Mary's Church, Fitzwilliam Museum, and Cambridge University Botanic Gardens. High tarmac count. Lunch in Haslingfield, or Grantchester. Haslingfield lunch stop: the Little Rose. Grantchester lunch stops: the Rupert Brooke, the Red Lion, or the Green Man. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 23 km. 6 hours.

**Shelford to Cambridge via Church of St Mary the Virgin**: Flat road and field paths and skipping college tour and heading direct to Cambridge station. Sights: Church of St Mary the Virgin, Church of St Edmunds Hauxton, and Orchard Tea Rooms. High tarmac count. Lunch in Haslingfield: the Little Rose. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 19 km.
CBG.publicWalksCircular: **Cambridge Circular via Grantchester**: Pleasant but nothing spectacular except for Cambridge's architecture. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 12 km. 40 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921492459)**

CBR.name: Cooksbridge
CBR.adminWalksAll: **Lewes to Cooksbridge via Swanborough and Black Cap & Old Hamsey Church**: A variant of [Lewes Circular via Swanborough and Black Cap](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-swanborough-and-black-cap/). Open chalk downland, South Downs ridgeline, River Ouse bank, farmland, and country lanes. Sights: Priory of St Pancras, Cockshut Stream Restoration Project, Black Cap, Old Hamsey Church, and Ashcombe Windmill. Track slippery when wet. Lunch in Offham: the Blacksmith's Arms. 24 km.
CBR.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Lewes to Cooksbridge via Swanborough and Black Cap & Old Hamsey Church**: A variant of [Lewes Circular via Swanborough and Black Cap](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-swanborough-and-black-cap/). Open chalk downland, South Downs ridgeline, River Ouse bank, farmland, and country lanes. Sights: Priory of St Pancras, Cockshut Stream Restoration Project, Black Cap, Old Hamsey Church, and Ashcombe Windmill. Track slippery when wet. Lunch in Offham: the Blacksmith's Arms. 24 km.

CBY.name: Charlbury
CBY.adminWalksAll:
**Charlbury to Hanborough**: Rolling Oxfordshire farmland, landscaped parkland, lakeside paths, open fields, ancient hedgerows, Roman road, Cotswold stone villages, and deer park. Sights: Blenheim Palace, Column of Victory, Great Bridge, Cornbury Park, and St Mary the Virgin Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Finstock, or Stonesfield. Finstock lunch stop: the Plough Inn. Stonesfield lunch stop: the White Horse. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 21 km.

**[Kingham to Charlbury via Bruern Abbey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kingham-to-charlbury/)**: Gentle rolling hills, riverside meadows, woods, fields, Cotswold stone villages, and bridleways shared with horse riders. Sights: Bruern Abbey, Shipton under Wychwood, and Ascott under Wychwood. Can be very muddy. Best in Apr, Jun, Jul and Aug. 17 km.

**Hanborough to Charlbury**: Rolling Oxfordshire farmland, landscaped parkland, lakeside paths, open fields, ancient hedgerows, Roman road, Cotswold stone villages, and deer park. Sights: Blenheim Palace, Column of Victory, Great Bridge, Cornbury Park, St Mary the Virgin Church, and Charlbury Railway Station. Can be muddy. Best in Sep. Lunch in Stonesfield, or Finstock. Stonesfield lunch stop: the White Horse. Finstock lunch stop: the Plough Inn. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 22 km. 6 hours.

**[Kingham to Charlbury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kingham-to-charlbury-long/)**: Peaceful linear walk through Cotswold countryside, fields, lanes, and villages. Lunch in Burford, or Swinbrook. Burford lunch stop: the Various. Swinbrook lunch stop: the [Swan Inn](https://www.theswanswinbrook.co.uk/). 34 km.
CBY.publicWalksS2SEnding:
**[Kingham to Charlbury via Bruern Abbey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kingham-to-charlbury/)**: Gentle rolling hills, riverside meadows, woods, fields, Cotswold stone villages, and bridleways shared with horse riders. Sights: Bruern Abbey, Shipton under Wychwood, and Ascott under Wychwood. Can be very muddy. Best in Apr, Jun, Jul and Aug. 17 km.

Stonesfield lunch stop: the White Horse. Finstock lunch stop: the Plough Inn. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 22 km. 6 hours.

**[Kingham to Charlbury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kingham-to-charlbury-long/)**: Peaceful linear walk through Cotswold countryside, fields, lanes, and villages. Lunch in Burford, or Swinbrook. Burford lunch stop: the Various. Swinbrook lunch stop: the [Swan Inn](https://www.theswanswinbrook.co.uk/). 34 km.
