CCH.name: Chichester
CCH.adminWalksAll: **Southbourne to Chichester**: Flat tidal harbour shoreline, raised sea walls, mudflats, fields, light woodland, duck pond, and pedestrianised historic city centre. Sights: Parish Church of St Mary, Chidham, Holy Trinity Church, Fishbourne Roman Palace, Bishop's Palace Gardens, Chichester Cathedral, Pallant House Gallery, Market Cross, and Festival Theatre. Can be muddy. Best at high tide. Lunch in Chidham, or Bosham. Chidham lunch stop: the Old House at Home. Bosham lunch stops: the Anchor Bleu, the Shoreside Cafe, the Bosham Walk Arts & Crafts Centre café, or the Cumberland Gallery coffee shop. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 18 km. 5 hours.
CCH.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Southbourne to Chichester**: Flat tidal harbour shoreline, raised sea walls, mudflats, fields, light woodland, duck pond, and pedestrianised historic city centre. Sights: Parish Church of St Mary, Chidham, Holy Trinity Church, Fishbourne Roman Palace, Bishop's Palace Gardens, Chichester Cathedral, Pallant House Gallery, Market Cross, and Festival Theatre. Can be muddy. Best at high tide. Lunch in Chidham, or Bosham. Chidham lunch stop: the Old House at Home. Bosham lunch stops: the Anchor Bleu, the Shoreside Cafe, the Bosham Walk Arts & Crafts Centre café, or the Cumberland Gallery coffee shop. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 18 km. 5 hours.

CCHE.name: Chessington
CCHE.adminWalksAll: **[Chessington South to Cheam](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chessington-to-cheam/)**: Flat riverside paths, open meadows, farmland, ancient parkland, and wooded country park. Sights: [Hogsmill Local Nature Reserve](https://www.epsom-ewell.gov.uk/sites/default/files/documents/residents/venues-open-space-hire/parks-playgrounds/local-nature-reserves/Hogsmill%20Local%20Nature%20Reserve%20information%20June%202016_0.pdf), [Warren Farm](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/warren-farm/), [Nonsuch Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nonsuch_Park), [St. Mary The Virgin](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1277324), [Nonsuch Palace Gallery](http://www.friendsofnonsuch.co.uk/museum-and-gallery.html), [Service Wing Museum](http://www.friendsofnonsuch.co.uk/museum-and-gallery.html), [Whitehall](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/), [St Dunstan's neo-Gothic lychgate](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1065676), [Lumley Chapel](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/index.php/136-2/), Nonsuch Mansion House, Packhorse Bridge, and Ewell Spring. Lunch in Chessington, or Ewell. Chessington lunch stop: the [William Bourne](https://https://www.greatukpubs.co.uk/william-bourne-chessington). Ewell lunch stops: the [Spring Tavern](https://www.springtavern.co.uk/), the [Famous Green Man](https://www.famousgreenman.com/), the [Ewell Tap & Bottle Shop](https://www.ewelltap.co.uk), the [Coffee Shop](http://www.bournehall.org/visit-us/coffee-shop), the [All Things Nice](https://www.allthingsnice-ewell.co.uk/index.html), the [Sonder Bakery](https://www.sonderbakery.com/), or the [Mandalina Patisserie](http://https://www.mandalinapatisserie.com/). 14 km.
CCHE.publicWalksS2S: **[Chessington South to Cheam](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chessington-to-cheam/)**: Flat riverside paths, open meadows, farmland, ancient parkland, and wooded country park. Sights: [Hogsmill Local Nature Reserve](https://www.epsom-ewell.gov.uk/sites/default/files/documents/residents/venues-open-space-hire/parks-playgrounds/local-nature-reserves/Hogsmill%20Local%20Nature%20Reserve%20information%20June%202016_0.pdf), [Warren Farm](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/warren-farm/), [Nonsuch Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nonsuch_Park), [St. Mary The Virgin](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1277324), [Nonsuch Palace Gallery](http://www.friendsofnonsuch.co.uk/museum-and-gallery.html), [Service Wing Museum](http://www.friendsofnonsuch.co.uk/museum-and-gallery.html), [Whitehall](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/), [St Dunstan's neo-Gothic lychgate](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1065676), [Lumley Chapel](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/index.php/136-2/), Nonsuch Mansion House, Packhorse Bridge, and Ewell Spring. Lunch in Chessington, or Ewell. Chessington lunch stop: the [William Bourne](https://https://www.greatukpubs.co.uk/william-bourne-chessington). Ewell lunch stops: the [Spring Tavern](https://www.springtavern.co.uk/), the [Famous Green Man](https://www.famousgreenman.com/), the [Ewell Tap & Bottle Shop](https://www.ewelltap.co.uk), the [Coffee Shop](http://www.bournehall.org/visit-us/coffee-shop), the [All Things Nice](https://www.allthingsnice-ewell.co.uk/index.html), the [Sonder Bakery](https://www.sonderbakery.com/), or the [Mandalina Patisserie](http://https://www.mandalinapatisserie.com/). 14 km.

CCOU.name: Coulsdon
CCOU.adminWalksAll:
**Coulsdon to Redhill via the Chaldon Way**: It's too apparent that you haven't left London. *Last reviewed 18 December 2021.* 15 km. 180 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921483662)**

**Coulsdon South Circular**: Ancient commons, open grazing land, bluebell woods, chalk downland, and quiet suburban fringe. Sights: Chaldon Church, Farthing Down, Kenley Common, Coulsdon Common, and Happy Valley. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in Coulsdon Common: the Fox. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 14 km.

**Coulsdon South to Whyteleafe**: An alternative variant of [Whyteleafe Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whyteleafe-circular/). Chalk downland, commons, wooded valleys, Happy Valley, airfield perimeter, and restored grassland. Sights: [Farthing Downs](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/farthing-downs), [Kenley Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/kenley-common), [Kenley Airfield](https://www.kenleyrevival.org/content/category/visiting), [Coulsdon Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/coulsdon-common), [Ss Peter and Paul, Chaldon](https://www.exploringsurreyspast.org.uk/themes/places/surrey/tandridge/chaldon/chaldon_church_mural), [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), Happy Valley, Burial Ground, and Riddlesdown Quarry. Can be muddy. Lunch in Coulsdon Common, or Caterham Barracks. Coulsdon Common lunch stop: the [Fox](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thefoxcoulsdoncommon#). Caterham Barracks lunch stop: the [Boundary on the Green](https://www.boundaryonthegreenpub.co.uk). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**Riddlesdown to Coulsdon South**: Ancient commons, open grazing land, bluebell woods, chalk downland, and quiet suburban fringe. Sights: Chaldon Church, Farthing Down, Kenley Common, Coulsdon Common, Riddlesdown, and Happy Valley. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in Coulsdon Common, or Kenley Common. Coulsdon Common lunch stop: the Fox. Kenley Common lunch stop: the [Wattenden Arms](http://www.thewattendenarmskenley.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km. 5 hours.
CCOU.publicWalksS2S: **Coulsdon to Redhill via the Chaldon Way**: It's too apparent that you haven't left London. 15 km. 180 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921483662)**

CCSH.name: Carshalton
CCSH.adminWalksAll:
**Carshalton to Colliers Wood**: A shorter variant of [Mitcham Junction to Colliers Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mitcham-junction-to-colliers-wood/). Flat urban riverside parks, nature reserves, chalk stream, and south London green corridors. Sights: [Wandle Trail](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk/projects/wandle-trail), [Sutton Ecology Centre](https://www.sutton.gov.uk/w/biodiversity-and-wildlife-sutton-ecology-centre), [Honeywood Museum](https://friendsofhoneywood.co.uk/index.html), [Carshalton Water Tower](https://carshaltonwatertower.co.uk/index.html), [Morden Hall Park](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/morden-hall-park), [Deen City Farm](https://www.deencityfarm.co.uk), [Merton Abbey Mills](https://www.mertonabbeymills.org.uk), Carshalton Ponds, Watermeads Nature Reserve, Ravensbury Park, Wilderness Island, Morden Hall, and Merton Priory. Lunch in Carshalton: the [Greyhound Hotel](https://www.thegreyhoundhotel.com), the [Hope](https://hopecarshalton.co.uk), or the [Sun](https://www.thesuncarshalton.com/food). 9 km. 2 hours.

**Carshalton to Morden**: A shorter variant of [Mitcham Junction to Colliers Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mitcham-junction-to-colliers-wood/). Flat urban riverside parks, nature reserves, chalk stream, and south London green corridors. Sights: [Wandle Trail](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk/projects/wandle-trail), [Sutton Ecology Centre](https://www.sutton.gov.uk/w/biodiversity-and-wildlife-sutton-ecology-centre), [Honeywood Museum](https://friendsofhoneywood.co.uk/index.html), [Carshalton Water Tower](https://carshaltonwatertower.co.uk/index.html), [Morden Hall Park](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/morden-hall-park), Carshalton Ponds, Watermeads Nature Reserve, Ravensbury Park, and Wilderness Island. Lunch in Carshalton: the [Greyhound Hotel](https://www.thegreyhoundhotel.com), the [Hope](https://hopecarshalton.co.uk), or the [Sun](https://www.thesuncarshalton.com/food). 7 km.
CCSH.publicWalksS2S:
**Carshalton to Colliers Wood**: A shorter variant of [Mitcham Junction to Colliers Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mitcham-junction-to-colliers-wood/). Flat urban riverside parks, nature reserves, chalk stream, and south London green corridors. Sights: [Wandle Trail](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk/projects/wandle-trail), [Sutton Ecology Centre](https://www.sutton.gov.uk/w/biodiversity-and-wildlife-sutton-ecology-centre), [Honeywood Museum](https://friendsofhoneywood.co.uk/index.html), [Carshalton Water Tower](https://carshaltonwatertower.co.uk/index.html), [Morden Hall Park](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/morden-hall-park), [Deen City Farm](https://www.deencityfarm.co.uk), [Merton Abbey Mills](https://www.mertonabbeymills.org.uk), Carshalton Ponds, Watermeads Nature Reserve, Ravensbury Park, Wilderness Island, Morden Hall, and Merton Priory. Lunch in Carshalton: the [Greyhound Hotel](https://www.thegreyhoundhotel.com), the [Hope](https://hopecarshalton.co.uk), or the [Sun](https://www.thesuncarshalton.com/food). 9 km. 2 hours.

**Carshalton to Morden**: A shorter variant of [Mitcham Junction to Colliers Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mitcham-junction-to-colliers-wood/). Flat urban riverside parks, nature reserves, chalk stream, and south London green corridors. Sights: [Wandle Trail](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk/projects/wandle-trail), [Sutton Ecology Centre](https://www.sutton.gov.uk/w/biodiversity-and-wildlife-sutton-ecology-centre), [Honeywood Museum](https://friendsofhoneywood.co.uk/index.html), [Carshalton Water Tower](https://carshaltonwatertower.co.uk/index.html), [Morden Hall Park](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/morden-hall-park), Carshalton Ponds, Watermeads Nature Reserve, Ravensbury Park, and Wilderness Island. Lunch in Carshalton: the [Greyhound Hotel](https://www.thegreyhoundhotel.com), the [Hope](https://hopecarshalton.co.uk), or the [Sun](https://www.thesuncarshalton.com/food). 7 km.

CDOC.name: Dorchester
CDOC.adminWalksAll:
**[Dorchester South Circular via Bronkham Hill barrows](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorchester-circular-or-to-portesham/)**: Prehistoric downland, South Winterbourne Valley, heathery hilltops, barrow-studded ridgeway, and sweeping views to Jurassic Coast. Sights: [Maumbury Rings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maumbury_Rings), [Maiden Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/maiden-castle/), [Hardy Monument](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/hardy-monument), and Bronkham Hill barrows. Lunch in Martinstown: the [Brewer's Arms](http://www.thebrewersarms.com/). 24 km. 6 hours.

**Dorchester South Circular via Valley of (Sarsen) Stones**: A longer variant of [Dorchester Circular or to Portesham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorchester-circular-or-to-portesham/). Prehistoric downland, Valley of Stones boulder train, stone circles, barrows, Bride Valley, and heathery hilltops. Sights: [Maumbury Rings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maumbury_Rings), [Maiden Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/maiden-castle/), [Hardy Monument](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/hardy-monument), Valley of (Sarsen) Stones, Hell Stone Burial Chamber, and Bridehead Lake. Lunch in Martinstown: the [Brewer's Arms](http://www.thebrewersarms.com/). 37 km. 9 hours.

**Maiden Newton to Dorchester South**: An alternative variant of [Maiden Newton Circular or to Dorchester](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/maiden-newton-circular-or-to-dorchester/). Chalk downland, Cerne Valley, historic Dorchester city centre, and two modest climbs with fine views. Sights: The Cerne Giant, Cerne Abbey, Hog Cliff National Nature Reserve, and Giant Hill. Hilly ascents. Lunch in Sydling St. Nicholas, or Cerne Abbas. Sydling St. Nicholas lunch stop: the [Greyhound Inn](https://dorsetgreyhound.co.uk). Cerne Abbas lunch stops: the [Royal Oak](https://theroyaloakcerneabbas.co.uk), the [Giant Inn (aka The Red Lion)](https://www.thegiantcerneabbas.co.uk), the [New Inn](https://www.thenewinncerneabbas.co.uk/index), or the [Abbots Tearoom](https://www.abbotsbedandbreakfast.co.uk).
CDOC.publicWalksCircular: **[Dorchester South Circular via Bronkham Hill barrows](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorchester-circular-or-to-portesham/)**: Prehistoric downland, South Winterbourne Valley, heathery hilltops, barrow-studded ridgeway, and sweeping views to Jurassic Coast. Sights: [Maumbury Rings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maumbury_Rings), [Maiden Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/maiden-castle/), [Hardy Monument](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/hardy-monument), and Bronkham Hill barrows. Lunch in Martinstown: the [Brewer's Arms](http://www.thebrewersarms.com/). 24 km. 6 hours.

CDOR.name: Dorking
CDOR.adminWalksAll:
**[Dorking Circular via Brockham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/deepdene-trail/)**: Restored landscaped gardens, rhododendron woods, surfaced parkland paths, open fields with Downs views, wooded valley trails, village green, and town centre. Sights: [Deepdene Gardens](https://deepdenetrail.co.uk/), Hope Mausoleum, Betchworth Castle, Cotmandene Common, Chat Park, Glory Woods, and Brockham village green. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Brockham: the Inn on the Green, the Reading Room Coffee and Cake House, or the Taphouse. 16 km.

**[Dorking Circular via Denbies Hillside](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-circular/)**: North Downs chalk escarpment, Greensand Hills, wooded ridges, open downland, river valleys, semi-natural woodland reserve, and ponds. Sights: [Denbies Hillside](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/denbies-hillside), St John the Evangelist, Hammer Pond, Friday Street, Bury Hill Fisheries, The Nower, Greensand Way, Rose Hill Arch, St Martin's Church, and Mullins Coffee Shop. Can be muddy. Lunch in Wotton: the [Wotton Hatch](https://www.wottonhatch.co.uk). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Dorking Circular via Norbury Park and Mickleham**: An alternative variant of [Dorking to Box Hill via Norbury Park and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-to-box-hill-via-norbury-park-and-mickleham/). Rambler favourite! Vineyard, wooded hills, chalk downland, open grassland, riverside path along the Mole, and North Downs. Sights: [Denbies Wine Estate](https://www.denbies.co.uk), [Norbury Park Nature Reserve](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/norbury-park), [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Broadwood's Tower, Box Hill Fort, and Swiss Cottage. Can be muddy. Lunch in Mickleham: the [King William IV](https://www.thekingwilliamiv.com), or the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk). 15 km. 4 hours.

**Dorking Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham**: An alternative variant of [Box Hill Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-headley-heath-and-mickleham/). North Downs hills, open downland, heathland, Norbury Park woodland edge, vineyard, and valley paths. Sights: [Headley Heath](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/headley-heath), [Norbury Park](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/norbury-park), [Denbies Wine Estate](https://www.denbies.co.uk), St Michael and All Angels, The Gallops, and Stane Street. Steep descents. Lunch in Mickleham: the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk). 16 km.

**Dorking Circular via Betchworth & Deepdene Trail**: A longer variant of [Box Hill Circular via Betchworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-betchworth/). Mole Valley riverside meadows, chalk escarpment, disused quarries, parkland, Deepdene estate woodland, terraced gardens, and town. Sights: Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, Betchworth Quarry and Lime Works, [St Michael, Betchworth](https://www.nationalchurchestrust.org/church/betchworth-st-michael), [Betchworth Castle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betchworth_Castle), Christ Church, Brockham, [Deepdene Trail](https://visitmolevalley.co.uk/the-deepdene-trail), Deepdene Terrace, Deepdene Gardens, Hope Mausoleum, St Martin, Dorking, and Mullins Coffee Shop. Can be muddy. Lunch in Betchworth, or Brockham. Betchworth lunch stops: the [Red Lion](https://www.theredlionbetchworth.com), or the [Dolphin](https://www.dolphinbetchworth.com). Brockham lunch stops: the [Inn on the Green](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/brockham), or the Royal Oak. 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Dorking Circular via Abinger Roughs**: A longer variant of [Dorking Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-circular/). North Downs chalk escarpment, Greensand Hills, Abinger Roughs mixed woodland and grassland, open hillside, river valleys, and wooded ridges. Sights: [Denbies Hillside](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/denbies-hillside), [Abinger Roughs](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/abinger-roughs-and-netley-park), Wilberforce Memorial, Witch's Broom Tree, Abinger Manor, St James, Abinger, Friday Street, Hammer Pond, Bury Hill Fisheries, The Nower, Greensand Way, Rose Hill Arch, and St Martin's Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Abinger Common: the [Abinger Hatch](https://theabingerhatch.co.uk). 22 km. 6 hours.

**[Dorking to Box Hill & Westhumble via Norbury Park and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-to-box-hill-via-norbury-park-and-mickleham/)**: Rambler favourite! Vineyard, wooded hills, chalk downland, open grassland, Mole valley views, and North Downs. Sights: [Denbies Wine Estate](https://www.denbies.co.uk), [Norbury Park Nature Reserve](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/norbury-park), [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Westhumble Chapel, Broadwood's Tower, and Box Hill Fort. Can be muddy. Lunch in Mickleham: the [King William IV](https://www.thekingwilliamiv.com), or the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk). 14 km. 4 hours.

**[Dorking to Reigate](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-to-reigate/)**: Mainly level riverside path, sandstone Greensand Way, picturesque Surrey villages, and parkland grounds. Sights: Betchworth Castle, St Michael's Church, and Reigate Priory. Lunch in Betchworth, Brockham, or Reigate Heath. Betchworth lunch stop: the [Dolphin Inn](http://www.youngs.co.uk/pub-detail.asp?PubID=387). Brockham lunch stop: the [Royal Oak](http://www.royaloak-brockham.co.uk/). Reigate Heath lunch stop: the [Skimmington Castle](http://www.skimmingtoncastle.co.uk/). 12 km. 3.5 hours.

**Dorking to Box Hill & Westhumble via Betchworth**: An alternative variant of [Box Hill Circular via Betchworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-betchworth/). Mole Valley riverside meadows, chalk escarpment, disused quarries, parkland, and village greens. Sights: [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, Betchworth Quarry and Lime Works, [St Michael, Betchworth](https://www.nationalchurchestrust.org/church/betchworth-st-michael), [Betchworth Castle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betchworth_Castle), Stepping Stones, and Christ Church, Brockham. Can be muddy. Lunch in Betchworth, or Brockham. Betchworth lunch stops: the [Red Lion](https://www.theredlionbetchworth.com), or the [Dolphin](https://www.dolphinbetchworth.com). Brockham lunch stops: the [Inn on the Green](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/brockham), or the Royal Oak. 16 km.

**Dorking to Box Hill & Westhumble via Headley Heath and Mickleham**: A variant of [Box Hill Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-headley-heath-and-mickleham/). North Downs hills, open downland, wooded heathland, grassy spurs, chalk paths, beech woodland, and valley views. Sights: [Headley Heath](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/headley-heath), [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Salomons Memorial, Box Hill Fort, St Michael and All Angels, The Gallops, Stane Street, Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, and North Downs Way. Steep descents. Lunch in Mickleham: the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk), or the [King William IV](https://www.thekingwilliamiv.com). 16 km.

**Dorking West to Merstham**: A variant of [Box Hill to Merstham and Caterham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-to-merstham/). Vineyard, North Downs ridge, wooded escarpment, open hilltops, and parkland. Sights: Denbies Vineyard, Box Hill, Reigate Hill, Gatton Park, and Reigate Fort. Hilly, multiple ascents. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Reigate Hill, or Merstham. Reigate Hill lunch stop: the [Chalk Hare](http://www.chalkhare.co.uk/). Merstham lunch stop: the Feathers.

**[Gomshall to Dorking via Holmbury Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/gomshall-to-dorking/)**: Greensand Ridge, heathland, hilltop moorland, woodland trails, suburban streets, and a high street. Sights: Holmbury Hill, Leith Hill Tower, The Nower, and Hurt Wood. Can be muddy. Lunch in Holmbury St. Mary: the [Kings Head](https://thekingsheadhsm.com/), the [Royal Oak](https://royaloakholmbury.co.uk/), or the [Heartwork Coffee Bar](https://www.heartworkcoffee.co.uk/). 23 km.

**Gomshall to Dorking via Piney Copse**: An alternative variant of [Dorking Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-circular/). Piney Copse woodland, Abinger Roughs mixed woodland and grassland, open hillside, river valleys, Greensand Hills, and wooded ridges. Sights: Piney Copse, [Abinger Roughs](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/abinger-roughs-and-netley-park), Wilberforce Memorial, Abinger Manor, St James, Abinger, Friday Street, Hammer Pond, Bury Hill Fisheries, The Nower, Greensand Way, Rose Hill Arch, and St Martin's Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Abinger Common: the [Abinger Hatch](https://theabingerhatch.co.uk). 15 km. 4 hours.

**Box Hill & Westhumble to Dorking via Headley Heath and Mickleham**: An alternative variant of [Box Hill Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-headley-heath-and-mickleham/). North Downs hills, open downland, heathland, woodland edge of Norbury Park, vineyard, and valley paths. Sights: [Headley Heath](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/headley-heath), [Norbury Park](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/norbury-park), [Denbies Wine Estate](https://www.denbies.co.uk), St Michael and All Angels, The Gallops, Stane Street, Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, and Mole Gap Trail. Steep descents. Lunch in Mickleham: the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk). 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**Effingham Junction to Dorking**: Hidden valleys, pretty woodland, gentle pasture, North Downs escarpment with extensive views, vineyard, and ending at Dorking. Sights: Polesden Lacey, Denbies Wine Estate, Great Ridings Wood, and Ranmore Common. Best in Apr, May, Sep, Oct and Nov. Lunch in Polesden Lacey: the Granary Cafe, Polesden Lacey. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km.

**Box Hill & Westhumble to Dorking via Betchworth & Deepdene Trail**: A longer variant of [Box Hill Circular via Betchworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-betchworth/). Chalk downland, escarpment, disused quarries, parkland, Deepdene estate woodland, terraced gardens, and town. Sights: [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Salomons Memorial, Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, Betchworth Quarry and Lime Works, [St Michael, Betchworth](https://www.nationalchurchestrust.org/church/betchworth-st-michael), [Betchworth Castle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betchworth_Castle), Christ Church, Brockham, [Deepdene Trail](https://visitmolevalley.co.uk/the-deepdene-trail), Deepdene Terrace, Deepdene Gardens, Hope Mausoleum, St Martin, Dorking, and Mullins Coffee Shop. Can be muddy. Lunch in Betchworth, or Brockham. Betchworth lunch stops: the [Red Lion](https://www.theredlionbetchworth.com), or the [Dolphin](https://www.dolphinbetchworth.com). Brockham lunch stops: the [Inn on the Green](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/brockham), or the Royal Oak. 18 km. 5 hours.
CDOR.publicWalksS2S:
**[Dorking to Box Hill & Westhumble via Norbury Park and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-to-box-hill-via-norbury-park-and-mickleham/)**: Rambler favourite! Vineyard, wooded hills, chalk downland, open grassland, Mole valley views, and North Downs. Sights: [Denbies Wine Estate](https://www.denbies.co.uk), [Norbury Park Nature Reserve](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/norbury-park), [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Westhumble Chapel, Broadwood's Tower, and Box Hill Fort. Can be muddy. Lunch in Mickleham: the [King William IV](https://www.thekingwilliamiv.com), or the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk). 14 km. 4 hours.

**[Dorking to Reigate](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-to-reigate/)**: Mainly level riverside path, sandstone Greensand Way, picturesque Surrey villages, and parkland grounds. Sights: Betchworth Castle, St Michael's Church, and Reigate Priory. Lunch in Betchworth, Brockham, or Reigate Heath. Betchworth lunch stop: the [Dolphin Inn](http://www.youngs.co.uk/pub-detail.asp?PubID=387). Brockham lunch stop: the [Royal Oak](http://www.royaloak-brockham.co.uk/). Reigate Heath lunch stop: the [Skimmington Castle](http://www.skimmingtoncastle.co.uk/). 12 km. 3.5 hours.
CDOR.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Gomshall to Dorking via Holmbury Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/gomshall-to-dorking/)**: Greensand Ridge, heathland, hilltop moorland, woodland trails, suburban streets, and a high street. Sights: Holmbury Hill, Leith Hill Tower, The Nower, and Hurt Wood. Can be muddy. Lunch in Holmbury St. Mary: the [Kings Head](https://thekingsheadhsm.com/), the [Royal Oak](https://royaloakholmbury.co.uk/), or the [Heartwork Coffee Bar](https://www.heartworkcoffee.co.uk/). 23 km.
CDOR.publicWalksCircular:
**[Dorking Circular via Brockham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/deepdene-trail/)**: Restored landscaped gardens, rhododendron woods, surfaced parkland paths, open fields with Downs views, wooded valley trails, village green, and town centre. Sights: [Deepdene Gardens](https://deepdenetrail.co.uk/), Hope Mausoleum, Betchworth Castle, Cotmandene Common, Chat Park, Glory Woods, and Brockham village green. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Brockham: the Inn on the Green, the Reading Room Coffee and Cake House, or the Taphouse. 16 km.

**[Dorking Circular via Denbies Hillside](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-circular/)**: North Downs chalk escarpment, Greensand Hills, wooded ridges, open downland, river valleys, semi-natural woodland reserve, and ponds. Sights: [Denbies Hillside](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/denbies-hillside), St John the Evangelist, Hammer Pond, Friday Street, Bury Hill Fisheries, The Nower, Greensand Way, Rose Hill Arch, St Martin's Church, and Mullins Coffee Shop. Can be muddy. Lunch in Wotton: the [Wotton Hatch](https://www.wottonhatch.co.uk). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

CDS.name: Coulsdon South
CDS.adminWalksAll:
**Coulsdon to Redhill via the Chaldon Way**: It's too apparent that you haven't left London. *Last reviewed 18 December 2021.* 15 km. 180 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921483662)**

**Coulsdon South Circular**: Ancient commons, open grazing land, bluebell woods, chalk downland, and quiet suburban fringe. Sights: Chaldon Church, Farthing Down, Kenley Common, Coulsdon Common, and Happy Valley. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in Coulsdon Common: the Fox. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 14 km.

**Coulsdon South to Whyteleafe**: An alternative variant of [Whyteleafe Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whyteleafe-circular/). Chalk downland, commons, wooded valleys, Happy Valley, airfield perimeter, and restored grassland. Sights: [Farthing Downs](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/farthing-downs), [Kenley Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/kenley-common), [Kenley Airfield](https://www.kenleyrevival.org/content/category/visiting), [Coulsdon Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/coulsdon-common), [Ss Peter and Paul, Chaldon](https://www.exploringsurreyspast.org.uk/themes/places/surrey/tandridge/chaldon/chaldon_church_mural), [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), Happy Valley, Burial Ground, and Riddlesdown Quarry. Can be muddy. Lunch in Coulsdon Common, or Caterham Barracks. Coulsdon Common lunch stop: the [Fox](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thefoxcoulsdoncommon#). Caterham Barracks lunch stop: the [Boundary on the Green](https://www.boundaryonthegreenpub.co.uk). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**Riddlesdown to Coulsdon South**: Ancient commons, open grazing land, bluebell woods, chalk downland, and quiet suburban fringe. Sights: Chaldon Church, Farthing Down, Kenley Common, Coulsdon Common, Riddlesdown, and Happy Valley. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in Coulsdon Common, or Kenley Common. Coulsdon Common lunch stop: the Fox. Kenley Common lunch stop: the [Wattenden Arms](http://www.thewattendenarmskenley.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km. 5 hours.
CDS.publicWalksS2S: **Coulsdon to Redhill via the Chaldon Way**: It's too apparent that you haven't left London. 15 km. 180 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921483662)**

CED.name: Cheddington
CED.adminWalksAll: **[Cheddington to Leighton Buzzard](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cheddington-to-leighton-buzzard/)**: Rolling fields, wooded areas, hilltop villages, panoramic views of the Chilterns and Aylesbury Vale, and level descent into Linslade. Sights: All Saints Church, Wing. Arable field crossings. Lunch in Wingrave: the [Rose & Crown](https://roseandcrownwingrave.net/). 24 km. 5.5 hours.
CED.publicWalksS2S: **[Cheddington to Leighton Buzzard](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cheddington-to-leighton-buzzard/)**: Rolling fields, wooded areas, hilltop villages, panoramic views of the Chilterns and Aylesbury Vale, and level descent into Linslade. Sights: All Saints Church, Wing. Arable field crossings. Lunch in Wingrave: the [Rose & Crown](https://roseandcrownwingrave.net/). 24 km. 5.5 hours.

CEDE.name: Edenbridge
CEDE.adminWalksAll:
**[Edenbridge Town Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edenbridge-figure-of-8/)**: Gentle farmland, water meadows, riverside paths, farm airstrip, and flat Eden Valley. Sights: [Edenbridge](https://www.visitedenbridge.com), [River Eden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Eden,_Kent), [Eden Valley Museum](https://evmt.org.uk), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), Stone Bridge, Great Stone Bridge Trust, Edenbridge Water Meadows, Gabriel's Fishery, and Devil's Den. Can be waterlogged. Lunch in Edenbridge: the [Old Eden](https://www.theoldeden.com), or the [Ye Olde Crown](https://www.yeoldecrown.co.uk). 14 km. 3 hours.

**Edenbridge Town to Hever via Edenbridge**: An alternative variant of [Edenbridge Figure of 8](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edenbridge-figure-of-8/). Water meadows, riverside paths, farm airstrip, and flat Eden Valley fields to Hever. Sights: [Edenbridge](https://www.visitedenbridge.com), [River Eden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Eden,_Kent), [Eden Valley Museum](https://evmt.org.uk), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), Stone Bridge, Gabriel's Fishery, and Devil's Den. Can be waterlogged. Lunch in Edenbridge: the [Old Eden](https://www.theoldeden.com), or the [Ye Olde Crown](https://www.yeoldecrown.co.uk). 12 km. 2.5 hours.

**Edenbridge to Sevenoaks**: Open fields, NE ascent to Toy's Hill, and easterly woodland walking after Ide Hill. Sights: Toy's Hill and Ide Hill. Lunch in Ide Hill: the [Cock Inn](https://cockinnidehill.co.uk/). An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 17 km.

**Edenbridge Town to Hever via Great Stone Bridge Trust**: A longer variant of [Edenbridge Figure of 8](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edenbridge-figure-of-8/). Gentle farmland, water meadows, riverside paths, farm airstrip, flat Eden Valley, and fields to Hever. Sights: [Edenbridge](https://www.visitedenbridge.com), [River Eden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Eden,_Kent), [Eden Valley Museum](https://evmt.org.uk), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), Stone Bridge, Great Stone Bridge Trust, Edenbridge Water Meadows, Gabriel's Fishery, and Devil's Den. Can be waterlogged. Lunch in Edenbridge: the [Old Eden](https://www.theoldeden.com), or the [Ye Olde Crown](https://www.yeoldecrown.co.uk). 18 km. 4 hours.
CEDE.publicWalksCircular: **[Edenbridge Town Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edenbridge-figure-of-8/)**: Gentle farmland, water meadows, riverside paths, farm airstrip, and flat Eden Valley. Sights: [Edenbridge](https://www.visitedenbridge.com), [River Eden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Eden,_Kent), [Eden Valley Museum](https://evmt.org.uk), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), Stone Bridge, Great Stone Bridge Trust, Edenbridge Water Meadows, Gabriel's Fishery, and Devil's Den. Can be waterlogged. Lunch in Edenbridge: the [Old Eden](https://www.theoldeden.com), or the [Ye Olde Crown](https://www.yeoldecrown.co.uk). 14 km. 3 hours.

CEF.name: Chapel-en-le-Frith
CEF.adminWalksAll:
**Chapel-en-le-Frith to Buxton**: An alternative variant of [Buxton Circular via Combs Moss](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-via-combs-moss/). Moorland plateau ascent from valley station, gritstone scarps, green valleys, and hillfort promontory. Sights: Castle Naze, Combs Edge, Combs Reservoir, and Corbar Hill. Can be muddy. Lunch in Combs: the [Beehive Inn & Farm Shop](http://thebeehiveinn.co.uk/).

**Buxton to Chapel-en-le-Frith**: A shorter variant of [Buxton Circular via Combs Moss](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-via-combs-moss/). Moorland plateau, gritstone scarps, and descent via Castle Naze causeway to valley station. Sights: Castle Naze, Combs Edge, and Combs Reservoir. Can be muddy. Lunch in Combs: the [Beehive Inn & Farm Shop](http://thebeehiveinn.co.uk/).
CEF.publicWalksS2S: **Chapel-en-le-Frith to Buxton**: An alternative variant of [Buxton Circular via Combs Moss](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-via-combs-moss/). Lunch in Combs: the [Beehive Inn & Farm Shop](http://thebeehiveinn.co.uk/).

Lunch in Combs: the [Beehive Inn & Farm Shop](http://thebeehiveinn.co.uk/).

CEPS.name: Epsom
CEPS.adminWalksAll:
**Epsom to Ewell West**: A shorter variant of [Chessington to Cheam](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chessington-to-cheam/). Ashtead Common, open heathland, woodland, and riverside meadows. Sights: Ashtead Common, [Hogsmill Local Nature Reserve](https://www.epsom-ewell.gov.uk/sites/default/files/documents/residents/venues-open-space-hire/parks-playgrounds/local-nature-reserves/Hogsmill%20Local%20Nature%20Reserve%20information%20June%202016_0.pdf), [St. Mary The Virgin](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1277324), and Ewell Spring. Lunch in Ewell: the [Spring Tavern](https://www.springtavern.co.uk/), the [Famous Green Man](https://www.famousgreenman.com/), the [Ewell Tap & Bottle Shop](https://www.ewelltap.co.uk), or the [Coffee Shop](http://www.bournehall.org/visit-us/coffee-shop). 13 km.

**Epsom to Cheam**: A longer variant of [Chessington to Cheam](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chessington-to-cheam/). Ashtead Common, open heathland, woodland, riverside meadows, farmland, and ancient parkland. Sights: Ashtead Common, [Hogsmill Local Nature Reserve](https://www.epsom-ewell.gov.uk/sites/default/files/documents/residents/venues-open-space-hire/parks-playgrounds/local-nature-reserves/Hogsmill%20Local%20Nature%20Reserve%20information%20June%202016_0.pdf), [Warren Farm](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/warren-farm/), [Nonsuch Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nonsuch_Park), [St. Mary The Virgin](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1277324), [Nonsuch Palace Gallery](http://www.friendsofnonsuch.co.uk/museum-and-gallery.html), [Whitehall](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/), [Lumley Chapel](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/index.php/136-2/), Nonsuch Mansion House, and Ewell Spring. Lunch in Ewell: the [Spring Tavern](https://www.springtavern.co.uk/), the [Famous Green Man](https://www.famousgreenman.com/), the [Ewell Tap & Bottle Shop](https://www.ewelltap.co.uk), the [Coffee Shop](http://www.bournehall.org/visit-us/coffee-shop), the [All Things Nice](https://www.allthingsnice-ewell.co.uk/index.html), the [Sonder Bakery](https://www.sonderbakery.com/), or the [Mandalina Patisserie](http://https://www.mandalinapatisserie.com/). 20 km.

**Merstham to Epsom Downs**: A variant of [Merstham to Tattenham Corner](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/merstham-to-tattenham-corner/). North Downs ridge, parkland, open heathland, racecourse downland, and quiet residential street to Epsom Downs station. Sights: [Gatton Park](https://gattonpark.co.uk), [Reigate Fort](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/reigate-hill-and-gatton-park/features/reigate-fort-at-reigate-hill-and-gatton-park), and [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets). Race days restrict access. Lunch in Mogador: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). 17 km.

**Merstham to Epsom**: A variant of [Merstham to Tattenham Corner](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/merstham-to-tattenham-corner/). North Downs ridge, parkland, open heathland, racecourse downland, and residential streets into Epsom town centre. Sights: [Gatton Park](https://gattonpark.co.uk), [Reigate Fort](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/reigate-hill-and-gatton-park/features/reigate-fort-at-reigate-hill-and-gatton-park), [Banstead Commons](https://bansteadcommons.org.uk), [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets), The Durdans, and Rosebery Park. Race days restrict access. Lunch in Mogador: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Tattenham Corner to Epsom Downs**: A variant of [Tattenham Corner Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tattenham-corner-circular/). Downland, racecourse gallops, WWI memorial woodland and wildflower meadows, heathland, golf course, and residential streets. Sights: [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets), [Langley Vale Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/langley-vale-wood), Regiment of Trees, Witness, Jutland Wood, Tadworth Windmill, and Banstead Heath. Can be muddy. Lunch in Walton on the Hill: the [Blue Ball](https://theblueballtadworth.co.uk/food-drink), the Village Café, or the [Bell](http://www.thebell-therat.co.uk). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

**Oxshott to Epsom**: An alternative variant of [Oxshott to Ashtead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxshott-to-ashtead/). Heathland, commons, mixed woodland, grassy rides, and open woodland. Sights: Oxshott Heath, [Prince's Coverts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prince%27s_Coverts), [Ashtead Common](http://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/city-commons/ashtead-common/Pages/default.aspx), and [Epsom Common](http://www.epsomcommon.org.uk/Epsom_Common/epsom_common.html). Can be muddy. Lunch in Kingston Road, Leatherhead: the [Star](http://www.baronspubs.com/star/).

Ashtead Common, open heathland, woodland, and riverside meadows. Sights: Ashtead Common, [Hogsmill Local Nature Reserve](https://www.epsom-ewell.gov.uk/sites/default/files/documents/residents/venues-open-space-hire/parks-playgrounds/local-nature-reserves/Hogsmill%20Local%20Nature%20Reserve%20information%20June%202016_0.pdf), [St. Mary The Virgin](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1277324), and Ewell Spring. Lunch in Ewell: the [Spring Tavern](https://www.springtavern.co.uk/), the [Famous Green Man](https://www.famousgreenman.com/), the [Ewell Tap & Bottle Shop](https://www.ewelltap.co.uk), or the [Coffee Shop](http://www.bournehall.org/visit-us/coffee-shop). 13 km.

CFO.name: Chalfont & Latimer
CFO.adminWalksAll: **Chorleywood to Chalfont & Latimer**: A shorter variant of [Chorleywood to Chesham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chorleywood-to-chesham/). Track with fine views, beech woods, bluebells and rare coralroot flower, and suburban descent to station. Sights: Chenies Manor, Church of St Michaels, and Latimer House. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Chenies: the Red Lion, or the Bedford Arms. 9 km.
CFO.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Chorleywood to Chalfont & Latimer**: A shorter variant of [Chorleywood to Chesham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chorleywood-to-chesham/). Track with fine views, beech woods, bluebells and rare coralroot flower, and suburban descent to station. Sights: Chenies Manor, Church of St Michaels, and Latimer House. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Chenies: the Red Lion, or the Bedford Arms. 9 km.

CFOL.name: Folkestone
CFOL.adminWalksAll:
**Folkestone Circular via Castle Hill**: Rambler favourite! Verdant coastal cliffs, rolling hills, and suburban streets. Begins, necessarily, with a lengthy suburban segment, but then becomes very beautiful and surprisingly quiet. Particularly lovely on the walk along the cliffs east of Folkestone, where there are also a number of lunch spot choices. The narrow overgrown paths are best. But note that there is a LOT of steep ascents and descents on this walk. *Last reviewed 27 April 2025.* 16 km. 370 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2913988093)**

**Folkestone Central Circular**: A shorter variant of [Folkestone to Dover](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/folkestone-to-dover/). Rambler favourite! Coastal seafront, Folkestone Warren sea defences, steep cliff climb, and cliff-top return with Channel views. Sights: Battle of Britain Memorial, Cliff Top Cafe, and Martello Towers. Cracked sea defences. Lunch in Folkestone Warren, or Folkestone. Folkestone Warren lunch stop: the Royal Oak. Folkestone lunch stop: the Cliff Top Cafe. 13 km.

**[Folkestone Central to Dover Priory via Martello Towers](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/folkestone-to-dover/)**: Rambler favourite! Coastal cliffs, chalk downland, sea defences, wild landslip area, wartime installations, ridge between valley and sea, and town crossing. Sights: Martello Towers, Folkestone Warren, World War Two sound mirror, and Western Heights. Cracked sea defences. Lunch in Folkestone Warren: the Royal Oak. 14 km.

**Folkestone Central to Dover Priory via Battle of Britain Memorial**: An alternative variant of [Folkestone to Dover](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/folkestone-to-dover/). Rambler favourite! Cliff-top path, panoramic Channel views, wartime installations, and chalk downland. Sights: Battle of Britain Memorial, Cliff Top Cafe, and World War Two sound mirror. Lunch in Folkestone, or Folkestone Warren. Folkestone lunch stop: the Cliff Top Cafe. Folkestone Warren lunch stop: the Royal Oak. 15 km.

**[Sandling to Folkestone Central via Folkestone Warren Halt railway station](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-or-dover/)**: Rambler favourite! Dismantled railway, chalky escarpment ridge, steep climbs, open downland with Channel views, cliff top promenade, shingle beach, and historic harbour. Sights: [Folkestone](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folkestone), [Sandgate Branch](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandgate_Branch), [Elham Valley Railway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elham_Valley_Railway), [Folkestone Warren Halt railway station](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folkestone_Warren_Halt_railway_station), Folkestone Harbour Arm, and Battle of Britain Memorial. Steep climbs near start. Lunch in Etchinghill: the [Gatekeeper Inn](http://www.gatekeeperinn.co.uk/). 17 km.

**[Sandling to Folkestone Central via the Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-via-the-sea/)**: Disused railway trackbed, orchards, fields, open downland, ridge with sea views, shingle beach, traffic-free seafront promenade, and cliff gardens. Sights: Saltwood Castle, Sandgate Castle, Folkestone Harbour, Mermaid Beach, and Sunny Sands. Two steep hill climbs. Best in Jul–Sep. Lunch in Sandgate, or Folkestone. Sandgate lunch stops: the Earl of Clarendon, the Famous Ship Inn, or the Boat House. Folkestone lunch stops: the Mermaids Cafe Bar, or the HatHats Coffee. 11 km.

**Sandling to Folkestone West**: A shorter variant of [Sandling to Folkestone or Dover](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-or-dover/). Rambler favourite! Dismantled railway, chalky escarpment ridge, steep climbs, open downland, and long pavement finish. Sights: [Sandgate Branch](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandgate_Branch) and [Elham Valley Railway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elham_Valley_Railway). Long pavement to station. Lunch in Etchinghill: the [Gatekeeper Inn](http://www.gatekeeperinn.co.uk/). 11 km.

**Sandling to Folkestone Central via White Cliffs of Dover**: A variant of [Sandling to Folkestone or Dover](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-or-dover/). Rambler favourite! Escarpment ridge, steep climbs, open downland, White Cliffs cliff-top path, Channel views, and beach and harbour. Sights: [Folkestone](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folkestone), [Sandgate Branch](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandgate_Branch), [Elham Valley Railway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elham_Valley_Railway), Battle of Britain Memorial, White Cliffs of Dover, and Folkestone Harbour Arm. Steep climbs near start. Lunch in Etchinghill: the [Gatekeeper Inn](http://www.gatekeeperinn.co.uk/). 20 km.

**Sandling to Folkestone Central via the Sea & Harbour Arm**: A longer variant of [Sandling to Folkestone via the Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-via-the-sea/). Disused railway trackbed, orchards, fields, open downland, shingle beach, seafront promenade, refurbished harbour area, historic old streets, and clifftop esplanade. Sights: Saltwood Castle, Sandgate Castle, Folkestone Harbour, Harbour Arm, Mermaid Beach, and Sunny Sands. Two steep hill climbs. Best in Jul–Sep. Lunch in Sandgate, or Folkestone. Sandgate lunch stops: the Earl of Clarendon, the Famous Ship Inn, or the Boat House. Folkestone lunch stops: the Mermaids Cafe Bar, the HatHats Coffee, the Harbour Arm, or the Steep Street Coffee House. 13 km.

**Wye to Folkestone West via NDW**: A longer variant of [Sandling to Wye](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-wye/). North Downs ridge, valley, disused railway, spectacular Channel views, and road walking at end. Sights: Channel Tunnel Terminal. Road walking at end.
CFOL.publicWalksCircular: **Folkestone Circular via Castle Hill**: Rambler favourite! Verdant coastal cliffs, rolling hills, and suburban streets. Begins, necessarily, with a lengthy suburban segment, but then becomes very beautiful and surprisingly quiet. Particularly lovely on the walk along the cliffs east of Folkestone, where there are also a number of lunch spot choices. The narrow overgrown paths are best. But note that there is a LOT of steep ascents and descents on this walk. 16 km. 370 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2913988093)**

CHAR.name: Harlow
CHAR.adminWalksAll: **[Harlow Town Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/harlow-circular/)**: Flat, easy farmland, open fields, riverside, and quiet villages. Sights: Gilston Park House and Hunsdon Airfield. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hunsdon: the Crown, or the [Fox & Hounds](http://www.foxandhounds-hunsdon.co.uk/). 14 km.
CHAR.publicWalksCircular: **[Harlow Town Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/harlow-circular/)**: Flat, easy farmland, open fields, riverside, and quiet villages. Sights: Gilston Park House and Hunsdon Airfield. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hunsdon: the Crown, or the [Fox & Hounds](http://www.foxandhounds-hunsdon.co.uk/). 14 km.

CHE.name: Cheam
CHE.adminWalksAll:
**[Chessington South to Cheam](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chessington-to-cheam/)**: Flat riverside paths, open meadows, farmland, ancient parkland, and wooded country park. Sights: [Hogsmill Local Nature Reserve](https://www.epsom-ewell.gov.uk/sites/default/files/documents/residents/venues-open-space-hire/parks-playgrounds/local-nature-reserves/Hogsmill%20Local%20Nature%20Reserve%20information%20June%202016_0.pdf), [Warren Farm](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/warren-farm/), [Nonsuch Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nonsuch_Park), [St. Mary The Virgin](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1277324), [Nonsuch Palace Gallery](http://www.friendsofnonsuch.co.uk/museum-and-gallery.html), [Service Wing Museum](http://www.friendsofnonsuch.co.uk/museum-and-gallery.html), [Whitehall](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/), [St Dunstan's neo-Gothic lychgate](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1065676), [Lumley Chapel](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/index.php/136-2/), Nonsuch Mansion House, Packhorse Bridge, and Ewell Spring. Lunch in Chessington, or Ewell. Chessington lunch stop: the [William Bourne](https://https://www.greatukpubs.co.uk/william-bourne-chessington). Ewell lunch stops: the [Spring Tavern](https://www.springtavern.co.uk/), the [Famous Green Man](https://www.famousgreenman.com/), the [Ewell Tap & Bottle Shop](https://www.ewelltap.co.uk), the [Coffee Shop](http://www.bournehall.org/visit-us/coffee-shop), the [All Things Nice](https://www.allthingsnice-ewell.co.uk/index.html), the [Sonder Bakery](https://www.sonderbakery.com/), or the [Mandalina Patisserie](http://https://www.mandalinapatisserie.com/). 14 km.

**Epsom to Cheam**: A longer variant of [Chessington to Cheam](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chessington-to-cheam/). Ashtead Common, open heathland, woodland, riverside meadows, farmland, and ancient parkland. Sights: Ashtead Common, [Hogsmill Local Nature Reserve](https://www.epsom-ewell.gov.uk/sites/default/files/documents/residents/venues-open-space-hire/parks-playgrounds/local-nature-reserves/Hogsmill%20Local%20Nature%20Reserve%20information%20June%202016_0.pdf), [Warren Farm](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/warren-farm/), [Nonsuch Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nonsuch_Park), [St. Mary The Virgin](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1277324), [Nonsuch Palace Gallery](http://www.friendsofnonsuch.co.uk/museum-and-gallery.html), [Whitehall](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/), [Lumley Chapel](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/index.php/136-2/), Nonsuch Mansion House, and Ewell Spring. Lunch in Ewell: the [Spring Tavern](https://www.springtavern.co.uk/), the [Famous Green Man](https://www.famousgreenman.com/), the [Ewell Tap & Bottle Shop](https://www.ewelltap.co.uk), the [Coffee Shop](http://www.bournehall.org/visit-us/coffee-shop), the [All Things Nice](https://www.allthingsnice-ewell.co.uk/index.html), the [Sonder Bakery](https://www.sonderbakery.com/), or the [Mandalina Patisserie](http://https://www.mandalinapatisserie.com/). 20 km.
CHE.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Chessington South to Cheam](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chessington-to-cheam/)**: Flat riverside paths, open meadows, farmland, ancient parkland, and wooded country park. Sights: [Hogsmill Local Nature Reserve](https://www.epsom-ewell.gov.uk/sites/default/files/documents/residents/venues-open-space-hire/parks-playgrounds/local-nature-reserves/Hogsmill%20Local%20Nature%20Reserve%20information%20June%202016_0.pdf), [Warren Farm](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/warren-farm/), [Nonsuch Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nonsuch_Park), [St. Mary The Virgin](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1277324), [Nonsuch Palace Gallery](http://www.friendsofnonsuch.co.uk/museum-and-gallery.html), [Service Wing Museum](http://www.friendsofnonsuch.co.uk/museum-and-gallery.html), [Whitehall](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/), [St Dunstan's neo-Gothic lychgate](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1065676), [Lumley Chapel](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/index.php/136-2/), Nonsuch Mansion House, Packhorse Bridge, and Ewell Spring. Lunch in Chessington, or Ewell. Chessington lunch stop: the [William Bourne](https://https://www.greatukpubs.co.uk/william-bourne-chessington). Ewell lunch stops: the [Spring Tavern](https://www.springtavern.co.uk/), the [Famous Green Man](https://www.famousgreenman.com/), the [Ewell Tap & Bottle Shop](https://www.ewelltap.co.uk), the [Coffee Shop](http://www.bournehall.org/visit-us/coffee-shop), the [All Things Nice](https://www.allthingsnice-ewell.co.uk/index.html), the [Sonder Bakery](https://www.sonderbakery.com/), or the [Mandalina Patisserie](http://https://www.mandalinapatisserie.com/). 14 km.

CHG.name: Charing
CHG.adminWalksAll:
**Charing to Chilham via the Pilgrim's Way**: Rambler favourite! Penultimate section of the Pilgrim's Way to Canterbury. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 22 October 2022.* 20 km. 210 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/849781899)**

**[Lenham to Charing via Stalisfield Green](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lenham-to-charing/)**: North Downs footpaths, little-used field paths, rape seed fields, and woods. Sights: Lenham Cross, Stalisfield Green, and Charing village. Can be overgrown. Lunch in Warren Street, Lenham, or Stalisfield Green. Warren Street, Lenham lunch stop: the [Harrow Hill Hotel & Inn](http://www.harrowhillhotel.com/). Stalisfield Green lunch stop: the [Plough](http://www.theploughinnstalisfield.co.uk/). 13 km. 4.5 hours.

**Lenham to Charing via Lenham Cross**: A shorter variant of [Lenham to Charing](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lenham-to-charing/). North Downs footpaths, little-used field paths, and woods. Sights: Lenham Cross. Can be overgrown. Lunch in Warren Street, Lenham, or Stalisfield Green. Warren Street, Lenham lunch stop: the [Harrow Hill Hotel & Inn](http://www.harrowhillhotel.com/). Stalisfield Green lunch stop: the [Plough](http://www.theploughinnstalisfield.co.uk/). 9 km. 3 hours.
CHG.publicWalksS2S: **Charing to Chilham via the Pilgrim's Way**: Rambler favourite! Penultimate section of the Pilgrim's Way to Canterbury. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 20 km. 210 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/849781899)**

CHH.name: Christ's Hospital
CHH.adminWalksAll: **[Christ's Hospital to Billingshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/christs-hospital-to-billingshurst/)**: Very flat farmland, re-wilding estate, woods, streams, fields, and quiet lanes. Sights: [Knepp Wildlands](http://www.knepp.co.uk/), Knepp Castle, Old Knepp Castle, Shipley Windmill, St Mary the Virgin, Barnhouse Farm, and Blue Idol Quaker Meeting House. Can be muddy. Lunch in Dial Post, Dragons Green, or Whitehall near Shipley. Dial Post lunch stop: the Crown Inn. Dragons Green lunch stop: the George & Dragon. Whitehall near Shipley lunch stop: the Countryman. 27 km. 6.5 hours.
CHH.publicWalksS2S: **[Christ's Hospital to Billingshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/christs-hospital-to-billingshurst/)**: Very flat farmland, re-wilding estate, woods, streams, fields, and quiet lanes. Sights: [Knepp Wildlands](http://www.knepp.co.uk/), Knepp Castle, Old Knepp Castle, Shipley Windmill, St Mary the Virgin, Barnhouse Farm, and Blue Idol Quaker Meeting House. Can be muddy. Lunch in Dial Post, Dragons Green, or Whitehall near Shipley. Dial Post lunch stop: the Crown Inn. Dragons Green lunch stop: the George & Dragon. Whitehall near Shipley lunch stop: the Countryman. 27 km. 6.5 hours.

CHI.name: Chingford
CHI.adminWalksAll:
**Chingford to Epping**: A shorter variant of [Manor Park to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/manor-park-to-epping/). Ancient woodland, forest rides, gravel tracks, and meadows. Sights: [Queen Elizabeth's Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre), [The View Epping Forest Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre), Connaught Water, and Ambresbury Banks Iron Age Hillfort. Can be muddy. Lunch in Rangers Road, High Beach, Ivy Chimneys, or Epping. Rangers Road lunch stop: the [Larder at Butler's Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/). High Beach lunch stops: the Original Tea Hut, or the [King's Oak Hotel](https://www.kingsoakhotel.com/home). Ivy Chimneys lunch stop: the [Forest Gate Inn](http://www.forestgateinnepping.co.uk/). Epping lunch stops: the [Duke of Wellington](https://dowepping.co.uk//), or the [George & Dragon](http://www.georgeanddragonepping.co.uk/). 13 km.

**Manor Park to Chingford**: A shorter variant of [Manor Park to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/manor-park-to-epping/). Open flats, commons, woodland, forest rides, and gravel tracks. Sights: [Epping Forest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Epping_Forest), Wanstead Flats, Hollow Pond, Boating Lake in Highams Park, [Queen Elizabeth's Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre), and [The View Epping Forest Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre). Can be muddy. Lunch in Manor Park, Whipps Cross, Highams Park, or Rangers Road. Manor Park lunch stop: the Blakesley Arms. Whipps Cross lunch stop: the Lakeside Diner. Highams Park lunch stop: the [Humphry's Café](https://highamspark.london/cafes/humphrys-cafe/). Rangers Road lunch stop: the [Larder at Butler's Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/). 12 km.

**Leytonstone to Chingford**: A shorter variant of [Leytonstone to Loughton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leytonstone-to-loughton/). Open woodland, earth tracks, grassy paths, ponds, and gentle slopes. Sights: [Queen Elizabeth Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/queen-elizabeths-hunting-lodge) and Epping Forest. Can be muddy. Best in Oct, Nov, Apr and May. Lunch in Chingford: the [Butlers Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/). 9 km.

CHK.name: Chiswick
CHK.adminWalksAll: **Norbiton to Chiswick**: A shorter variant of [Norbiton to Hammersmith or Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/norbiton-to-hammersmith-or-putney/). Deer park, woodland garden, open grassland, riverside common, village green, Thames riverfront, and suburban streets. Sights: [Richmond Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond-park), Isabella Plantation, Pen Ponds, [Chiswick House & Gardens](https://chiswickhouseandgardens.org.uk), and Inigo Jones Gateway. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Barnes: the [Sun Inn](https://www.thesuninnbarnes.co.uk), the [Coach & Horses](https://www.coachandhorsesbarnes.co.uk), the [Bulls Head](https://www.thebullsheadbarnes.com), or the [Watermans Arms](https://www.watermansarms.co.uk). 14 km. 3 hours.
CHK.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Norbiton to Chiswick**: A shorter variant of [Norbiton to Hammersmith or Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/norbiton-to-hammersmith-or-putney/). Deer park, woodland garden, open grassland, riverside common, village green, Thames riverfront, and suburban streets. Sights: [Richmond Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond-park), Isabella Plantation, Pen Ponds, [Chiswick House & Gardens](https://chiswickhouseandgardens.org.uk), and Inigo Jones Gateway. CHL.name: Chilworth
CHL.adminWalksAll:
**Chilworth Circular**: A shorter variant of [Guildford Circular via Albury Park](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/guildford-circular-via-albury-park/). Greensand hills, mixed woodland, heathland, parkland, river valley, and industrial ruins. Sights: [Chilworth Gunpowder Mills](https://www.chilworthgunpowdermills.co.uk), Chilworth Manor, Albury Park, [Ss Peter and Paul (Saxon Church)](https://www.alburyoldsaxonchurch.org), [Catholic Apostolic Church](https://www.alburychurches.org/catholic-apostolic-church-apostles-henry-drummond), Silent Pool, Newlands Corner, and [St Martha-on-the-Hill](https://www.parishofchilworth.org.uk/history/st-marthas/full-history). Can be muddy. Lunch in Little London: the [William IV](https://www.thewilliamivpub.com). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Chilworth to Guildford via Chantries Hill Circular**: A shorter variant of [Guildford via Chantries Hill Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/guildford-via-chantries-hill-circular/). Rambler favourite! Shorter start, skips Chantries Hill and St Martha's Hill, heathland and woods, and River Wey into Guildford. Lunch in Albury: the Drummond at Albury. 14 km.

**Chilworth to Shalford via Chantries Hill Circular**: A shorter variant of [Guildford via Chantries Hill Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/guildford-via-chantries-hill-circular/). Rambler favourite! Short walk through Albury, heathland and woods, and Shalford watermeadows. Lunch in Albury: the Drummond at Albury. 11 km.

**Chilworth to Shalford via Chilworth Gunpowder Mills**: A shorter variant of [Shalford Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shalford-circular/). Heathland, sandy woods, river valley, historic mill ruins, open downland, and water meadows. Sights: [Chilworth Gunpowder Mills](https://www.chilworthgunpowdermills.co.uk), Incorporating Mills, Blackheath Common War Memorial, and St Catherine's Chapel. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chilworth: the [Percy Arms](https://www.thepercyarms.net). 13 km. 3.5 hours.

**Chilworth to Horsley**: A shorter variant of [Guildford to Horsley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/guildford-to-horsley/). North Downs ridge, downland escarpment, wooded stretches, upland country, and estate grounds. Sights: Chilworth Gunpowder Mills, Newlands Corner, and West Horsley Place. Lunch in Albury, or Chilworth. Albury lunch stop: the Drummond at Albury. Chilworth lunch stop: the Percy Arms. 14 km.

**Chilworth to Guildford via Albury Park**: A shorter variant of [Guildford Circular via Albury Park](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/guildford-circular-via-albury-park/). Greensand hills, mixed woodland, heathland, parkland, river valley, industrial ruins, and water meadows. Sights: [Chilworth Gunpowder Mills](https://www.chilworthgunpowdermills.co.uk), Chilworth Manor, Albury Park, [Ss Peter and Paul (Saxon Church)](https://www.alburyoldsaxonchurch.org), [Catholic Apostolic Church](https://www.alburychurches.org/catholic-apostolic-church-apostles-henry-drummond), Silent Pool, Albury Organic Vineyard, Optohedron, Newlands Corner, [St Martha-on-the-Hill](https://www.parishofchilworth.org.uk/history/st-marthas/full-history), St Catherine's Chapel, Shalford Water Meadows, River Wey Navigation, and Alice sculptures. Can be muddy. Lunch in Little London: the [William IV](https://www.thewilliamivpub.com). 19 km. 5 hours.

**Shalford to Chilworth**: A shorter variant of [Shalford Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shalford-circular/). Sandy hills, heathland, woods, river valley, historic mill ruins, and open downland. Sights: [Shalford Mill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/shalford-mill), [St Martha-on-the-Hill](https://www.parishofchilworth.org.uk/history/st-marthas/full-history), [Chilworth Gunpowder Mills](https://www.chilworthgunpowdermills.co.uk), Incorporating Mills, Chilworth Manor, and Blackheath Common War Memorial. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chilworth: the [Percy Arms](https://www.thepercyarms.net). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**Guildford to Chilworth via Pewley Down**: Short climb, downland, hilltop church, and descent to station village. Sights: Pewley Down and St Martha-on-the-Hill Church. Lunch in Chilworth: the Percy Arms. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 6 km.

CHN.name: Cheshunt
CHN.adminWalksAll:
**[Cheshunt Circular via Royal Gunpowder Mills](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cheshunt-circular/)**: Flat river floodplain, gravel-pit lakes, water meadows, country park sculptures, farmland, and a market town. Sights: [River Lee Country Park](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/river-lee-country-park), [Waltham Abbey Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waltham_Abbey_Church), [Church of the Holy Cross](http://www.walthamabbeychurch.co.uk), [Royal Gunpowder Mills](https://www.royalgunpowdermills.com), [Lee Valley White Water Centre](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/lee-valley-white-water-centre), [Lea Valley](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lea_Valley), [Waltham Abbey (town)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waltham_Abbey_(town)), Wildlife Discovery Centre, Cornmill Meadows Dragonfly Sanctuary, Epping Forest District Museum, Abbey Gateway, Waltham Abbey Gardens, Greenwich Meridian Trail, Waltham Abbey Town Hall, and Gunpowder Railway. Can be muddy. Lunch in Waltham Abbey: the Crown, the [Angel](https://www.mcmullens.co.uk/local-pub/angel), the Sun Inn, the Kalik Coffee House, or the Bake You Happy. 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**Cheshunt Circular via River Lee Country Park**: A shorter variant of [Cheshunt Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cheshunt-circular/). Flat river floodplain, gravel-pit lakes, water meadows, country park, and a market town — omitting the afternoon lake loops. Sights: [River Lee Country Park](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/river-lee-country-park), [Waltham Abbey Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waltham_Abbey_Church), [Church of the Holy Cross](http://www.walthamabbeychurch.co.uk), Wildlife Discovery Centre, Cornmill Meadows Dragonfly Sanctuary, Epping Forest District Museum, Abbey Gateway, Waltham Abbey Gardens, and [Lee Valley White Water Centre](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/lee-valley-white-water-centre). Can be muddy. Lunch in Waltham Abbey: the Crown, the [Angel](https://www.mcmullens.co.uk/local-pub/angel), the Sun Inn, the Kalik Coffee House, or the Bake You Happy. 11 km. 2.5 hours.

**Cheshunt Circular via Waltham Abbey**: An alternative variant of [Broxbourne Circular via Waltham Abbey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/broxbourne-circular-via-waltham-abbey/). Flat river floodplain, towpaths, water meadows, lakeside paths, and open parkland. Sights: [River Lee Country Park](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/river-lee-country-park), [Waltham Abbey Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waltham_Abbey_Church), [Church of the Holy Cross](http://www.walthamabbeychurch.co.uk), Wildlife Discovery Centre, Cornmill Meadows Dragonfly Sanctuary, and Waltham Abbey Gardens. Can be muddy. Lunch in Waltham Abbey: the [Angel](https://www.mcmullens.co.uk/local-pub/angel), the Sun Inn, the Crown, the Kalik Coffee House, or the Bake You Happy. 10 km. 2 hours.

Flat river floodplain, towpaths, water meadows, lakeside paths, woodland strips, orchid area boardwalk, and open parkland. Sights: [River Lee Country Park](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/river-lee-country-park), [Waltham Abbey Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waltham_Abbey_Church), [Church of the Holy Cross](http://www.walthamabbeychurch.co.uk), [Royal Gunpowder Mills](https://www.royalgunpowdermills.com), [Lee Valley White Water Centre](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/lee-valley-white-water-centre), [Broxbourne Waterside](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Broxbourne), Wildlife Discovery Centre, Cornmill Meadows Dragonfly Sanctuary, North Met Pit Orchid Area, Waltham Abbey Gardens, and Epping Forest District Museum. Can be muddy. Lunch in Waltham Abbey: the [Angel](https://www.mcmullens.co.uk/local-pub/angel), the Sun Inn, the Crown, the Kalik Coffee House, or the Bake You Happy. 16 km. 3.5 hours.

CHO.name: Cholsey
CHO.adminWalksAll:
**[Cholsey to Goring & Streatley via Wallingford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cholsey-to-goring-via-wallingford/)**: Thames riverside path, gently rolling Oxfordshire countryside, open fields, and attractive villages. Sights: Pierreponts café. Long, exposed walk. Best in Apr, May, Jun, Sep, Oct and Nov. Lunch in Wallingford, or South Stoke. Wallingford lunch stop: the Boat House. South Stoke lunch stop: the Perch and Pike. 20 km.

**[Cholsey to Goring & Streatley via St Mary's Church, Cholsey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cholsey-to-goring/)**: Gentle Oxfordshire countryside, wide grassy horse training gallops, Ridgeway stretch on Berkshire Downs, and riverside walk along the Thames. Sights: St Mary's Church, Cholsey and [Beetle and Wedge](http://www.beetleandwedge.co.uk/). Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in Blewbury: the [Red Lion](http://www.theredlionblewbury.co.uk/). 23 km.

CHP.name: Chipstead
CHP.adminWalksAll:
**Chipstead Circular via Well House Inn**: A variant of [Chipstead to Kingswood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chipstead-to-kingswood/). Ancient woodland, open chalk downs, open farmland, golf course fairways, quiet country lanes, and semi-open downland. Sights: [Banstead Woods Nature Trail](https://www.reigatesociety.org.uk/Walks/BansteadWood_NatureTrail_Online.pdf), Narnia sculptures, Chipstead Downs SSSI, Shabden Park Estate, Well House Inn, St Margaret's Well, and Ramblers Rest. Can be muddy. Lunch in Mugswell: the [Well House Inn](https://thewellhouseinn.co.uk). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**Chipstead Circular via Banstead Woods Nature Trail**: A shorter variant of [Chipstead to Kingswood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chipstead-to-kingswood/). Sights: [Banstead Woods Nature Trail](https://www.reigatesociety.org.uk/Walks/BansteadWood_NatureTrail_Online.pdf), Narnia sculptures, Chipstead Downs SSSI, and Shabden Park Estate. Can be muddy. Christchurch Harbour, wild coastal scenery, sand dunes, headland, and spit with beach huts. Sights: [Hengistbury Head](http://www.hengistbury-head.co.uk/), Mudeford Spit, and Christchurch Harbour. 7 km.

**Christchurch to Bournemouth**: A variant of [Bournemouth to Barton-on-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bournemouth-to-barton-on-sea/). Christchurch Harbour, wild coastal scenery, sand dunes, headland, promenade, and cliff-top paths. Sights: [Hengistbury Head](http://www.hengistbury-head.co.uk/), Mudeford Quay, and Boscombe Pier. Lunch in Mudeford Quay: the Haven House Inn. 12 km.

CHX.name: Charing Cross
CHX.adminWalksAll:
**Charing Cross to Liverpool Street**: A shorter variant of [The London Stations](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-stations/). Flat riverside Thames path, South Bank, City of London streets, and historic financial district. Sights: Charing Cross station, Hungerford Bridge, Golden Jubilee Bridges, London Eye, Royal Festival Hall, National Theatre, Oxo Tower, Waterloo station, Tate Modern, Millennium Bridge, Shakespeare's Globe, Blackfriars station, Founders Arms, Southwark Bridge, Cannon Street station, Borough Market, Shard, London Bridge station, Hays Galleria, HMS Belfast, Tower Bridge, Tower of London, Fenchurch Street station, Leadenhall Market, Lloyd's Building, Cheesegrater, Gherkin, Walkie-Talkie, Skygarden, St Helen's Bishopsgate, St Ethelburga's, and Liverpool Street station. Lunch in Blackfriars, Cannon Street, or London Bridge. Blackfriars lunch stop: the Founders Arms. Cannon Street lunch stop: the Bankers. London Bridge lunch stop: the Borough Market. 8 km.

**Paddington to Charing Cross**: A shorter variant of [The London Stations](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-stations/). Flat urban streets, Hyde Park, Kensington Gardens, Belgravia, and St James's Park. Sights: Paddington station, Elizabeth Line station, Kensington Gardens, Italian Gardens, Serpentine, Serpentine Lido, Princess Diana Memorial, Hyde Park, Kensington Palace, Buckingham Palace, Queen's Gallery, St James's Park, Horseguard's Parade, Banqueting House, Victoria station, Trafalgar Square, Nelson's Column, and Charing Cross station. Lunch in Hyde Park, or St James's Park. Hyde Park lunch stop: the Lido Cafe. St James's Park lunch stop: the St James's Cafe. 7 km.
