CIL.name: Chilham
CIL.privateNote: Trainline says: 1h6m — St Pancras via Ashford International
CIL.adminWalksAll:
**Chilham to Canterbury via the Pilgrim's Way**: Rambler favourite! From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 23 October 2022.* 14 km. 180 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/849788927)**

**Charing to Chilham via the Pilgrim's Way**: Rambler favourite! Penultimate section of the Pilgrim's Way to Canterbury. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 22 October 2022.* 20 km. 210 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/849781899)**

**[Chilham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chilham-circular/)**: Downland escarpment, upland fields, woods, bluebell woods, country estate, and a hilltop village. Sights: Godmersham Park and [Chilham Castle](http://www.chilham-castle.co.uk). Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Sole Street: the Timber Batts @ The Compasses. 15 km.

**Chilham to Canterbury East**: River valleys, lakes, hop fields, apple orchards, mixed woodland, heathland, nature reserve, university parkland, and medieval city streets. Sights: Canterbury Cathedral, Westgate, Old Weavers' House, Blackfriars Gallery, Old Synagogue, Kings School, Dane John Mound, Blean Woods Nature Reserve, University of Kent, Chartham Vineyard, St Mary's Church, and St Peter the Apostle Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chartham, Canterbury Chartham Garden Centre, or Blean. Chartham lunch stops: the Artichoke pub, or the Local. Canterbury Chartham Garden Centre lunch stop: the Origins Restaurant & Patisserie Cafe. Blean lunch stop: the Hare at Blean. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 20 km. 5 hours.

**Chilham to Wye**: A variant of [Chilham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chilham-circular/). Downland escarpment, upland fields, woods, country estate, and a hilltop village. Sights: Godmersham Park and [Chilham Castle](http://www.chilham-castle.co.uk). Lunch in Sole Street: the Timber Batts @ The Compasses. 16 km.

**Wye to Chilham**: A variant of [Chilham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chilham-circular/). Downland escarpment, upland fields, woods, country estate, and a hilltop village. Sights: Godmersham Park and [Chilham Castle](http://www.chilham-castle.co.uk). Lunch in Sole Street: the Timber Batts @ The Compasses. 17 km.
CIL.publicWalksS2S: **Chilham to Canterbury via the Pilgrim's Way**: Rambler favourite! From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 14 km. 180 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/849788927)**
CIL.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Charing to Chilham via the Pilgrim's Way**: Rambler favourite! Penultimate section of the Pilgrim's Way to Canterbury. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 20 km. 210 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/849781899)**

CLC.name: Castle Cary
CLC.adminWalksAll:
**[Castle Cary Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/castle-cary-circular-via-camelot/)**: Somerset Levels fringes, hillock, quiet residential streets, charming market town, dry green valley, ancient holloway, pastures, shallow river valley, hillfort plateau, steep ascents, large WWII airfield remains, and broad plateau with views. Sights: Market House, Cadbury Castle, Lodge Hill, Glastonbury Tor, Mendip Hills, Parrock Hill, The Beacon, and Sigwells Plateau. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in North Cadbury, or South Cadbury. North Cadbury lunch stop: the Catash Inn. South Cadbury lunch stops: the Chapel Cross Coffee Rooms, or the Camelot Inn. 30 km. 7 hours.

**Castle Cary to Bruton via Stourhead**: An alternative variant of [Bruton Circular via Stourhead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bruton-circular-via-stourhead/). South Somerset pastures, wooded ridge, hanging woods, landscaped estate gardens with lakeside walks, and grottoes and classical temples. Sights: Alfred's Tower, Stourhead Estate, Palladian Bridge, Pantheon, Stourhead House, Bruton Dovecote, and Hauser & Wirth Somerset. Lunch in North Brewham, or Stourton. North Brewham lunch stop: the [Old Red Lion](http://www.theoldredlion.org.uk/). Stourton lunch stops: the [Spread Eagle Inn](https://www.spreadeagleinn.com/), or the [Stourhead Estate Restaurant](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/wiltshire/stourhead/eating-and-shopping-at-stourhead). 27 km.

**Castle Cary to Bruton via Alfred's Tower**: An alternative variant of [Bruton Circular or from Castle Cary](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bruton-circular-via-hauser-wirth-somerset/). Narrow steep-sided valleys and coombes, fast-flowing streams, rolling hilltops, honey-coloured stone villages, and far views to Wiltshire and Dorset boundary. Sights: Alfred's Tower, [Hauser & Wirth Somerset](https://www.hauserwirth.com/locations/10068-hauser-wirth-somerset), and Bruton Dovecote. Can be muddy. Lunch in Batcombe, or Bruton. Batcombe lunch stop: the [Three Horseshoes Inn](https://www.thethreehorseshoesbatcombe.co.uk/). Bruton lunch stops: the [Matt's Kitchen](http://www.mattskitchen.co.uk/), the [Prickly Pear](https://www.prickly-pear.com/), the [Sun Inn](https://thesuninnbruton.co.uk/), the [At the Chapel](http://www.atthechapel.co.uk/), the [Old Pharmacy](https://www.oldpharmacybruton.com/), the [Briar](https://numberonebruton.com/briar/), the [Blue Ball Bruton](https://www.blueballbruton.com/), the [Da Costa](https://da-costa.co.uk/), or the [Roth Bar](https://rothbar.co.uk/). 29 km.
CLC.publicWalksCircular: **[Castle Cary Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/castle-cary-circular-via-camelot/)**: Somerset Levels fringes, hillock, quiet residential streets, charming market town, dry green valley, ancient holloway, pastures, shallow river valley, hillfort plateau, steep ascents, large WWII airfield remains, and broad plateau with views. Sights: Market House, Cadbury Castle, Lodge Hill, Glastonbury Tor, Mendip Hills, Parrock Hill, The Beacon, and Sigwells Plateau. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in North Cadbury, or South Cadbury. North Cadbury lunch stop: the Catash Inn. South Cadbury lunch stops: the Chapel Cross Coffee Rooms, or the Camelot Inn. 30 km. 7 hours.

CLD.name: Chelsfield
CLD.adminWalksAll: **Chelsfield Circular via Down House**: From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 20 December 2020.* 15 km. 270 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921492991)**
CLD.publicWalksCircular: **Chelsfield Circular via Down House**: From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 15 km. 270 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921492991)**

CLH.name: Clitheroe
CLH.adminWalksAll: **[Whalley to Clitheroe](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pendle-hill/)**: Abbey precinct, town centre, riverside path, golf course, open pasture, old drove road, moorland ridge, plateau summit, steep scarp, lush valley, meandering river, and nature reserve. Sights: Whalley Abbey Precinct, Pendle Hill, Nick O' Pendle, Ogden Clough, Downham village, River Ribble, and St. Leonard's Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Sabden, or Downham. Sabden lunch stop: the [Wellsprings](https://thewellsprings.co.uk/). Downham lunch stops: the Downham Ice Cream Shop, or the [Assheton Arms](https://www.jamesplaces.com/assheton-arms/). 23 km. 5.5 hours.
CLH.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Whalley to Clitheroe](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pendle-hill/)**: Abbey precinct, town centre, riverside path, golf course, open pasture, old drove road, moorland ridge, plateau summit, steep scarp, lush valley, meandering river, and nature reserve. Sights: Whalley Abbey Precinct, Pendle Hill, Nick O' Pendle, Ogden Clough, Downham village, River Ribble, and St. Leonard's Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Sabden, or Downham. Sabden lunch stop: the [Wellsprings](https://thewellsprings.co.uk/). Downham lunch stops: the Downham Ice Cream Shop, or the [Assheton Arms](https://www.jamesplaces.com/assheton-arms/). 23 km. 5.5 hours.

CLIC.name: Lichfield
CLIC.adminWalksAll: **[Lichfield Circular via Whittington Heath](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Golf club, heath, woodland, and Heart of England Way. Sights: Hopwas Hayes Wood and Whittington Heath. MOD firing range — check access. 15 km. 120 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/305%20Lichfield.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925785196)**
CLIC.publicWalksCircular: **[Lichfield Circular via Whittington Heath](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Golf club, heath, woodland, and Heart of England Way. Sights: Hopwas Hayes Wood and Whittington Heath. MOD firing range — check access. 15 km. 120 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/305%20Lichfield.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925785196)**

CLON.name: London termini
CLON.privateNote: Obviously your real journey depends on where you live! Choose your closest London National Rail station for a more tailored experience.
CLON.adminWalksAll:
**[Kings Cross to Liverpool Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-stations/)**: Entirely flat, urban city-centre streets, royal parks, riverside Thames path, canal towpath, and historic financial district. Sights: Kings Cross station, St Pancras station, Kings Cross Central, Granary Square, Crick Institute, Euston station, Regent's Park, Queen Mary's Gardens, Regent's Park Open Air Theatre, Marylebone station, Great Central Hotel, Paddington Basin, Paddington station, Elizabeth Line station, Kensington Gardens, Italian Gardens, Serpentine, Serpentine Lido, Princess Diana Memorial, Hyde Park, Kensington Palace, Buckingham Palace, Queen's Gallery, St James's Park, Horseguard's Parade, Banqueting House, Trafalgar Square, Nelson's Column, Charing Cross station, Hungerford Bridge, Golden Jubilee Bridges, London Eye, Royal Festival Hall, National Theatre, Oxo Tower, Waterloo station, Tate Modern, Millennium Bridge, Shakespeare's Globe, Blackfriars station, Founders Arms, Southwark Bridge, Cannon Street station, Borough Market, Shard, London Bridge station, Hays Galleria, HMS Belfast, Tower Bridge, Tower of London, Fenchurch Street station, Leadenhall Market, Lloyd's Building, Cheesegrater, Gherkin, Walkie-Talkie, Skygarden, St Helen's Bishopsgate, St Ethelburga's, and Liverpool Street station. Lunch in Regent's Park, Marylebone, Hyde Park, St James's Park, Blackfriars, Cannon Street, or London Bridge. Regent's Park lunch stop: the Garden Cafe. Marylebone lunch stop: the Gino's Cafe Bar. Hyde Park lunch stop: the Lido Cafe. St James's Park lunch stop: the St James's Cafe. Blackfriars lunch stop: the Founders Arms. Cannon Street lunch stop: the Bankers. London Bridge lunch stop: the Borough Market. 21 km.

**Charing Cross to Liverpool Street**: A shorter variant of [The London Stations](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-stations/). Flat riverside Thames path, South Bank, City of London streets, and historic financial district. Sights: Charing Cross station, Hungerford Bridge, Golden Jubilee Bridges, London Eye, Royal Festival Hall, National Theatre, Oxo Tower, Waterloo station, Tate Modern, Millennium Bridge, Shakespeare's Globe, Blackfriars station, Founders Arms, Southwark Bridge, Cannon Street station, Borough Market, Shard, London Bridge station, Hays Galleria, HMS Belfast, Tower Bridge, Tower of London, Fenchurch Street station, Leadenhall Market, Lloyd's Building, Cheesegrater, Gherkin, Walkie-Talkie, Skygarden, St Helen's Bishopsgate, St Ethelburga's, and Liverpool Street station. Lunch in Blackfriars, Cannon Street, or London Bridge. Blackfriars lunch stop: the Founders Arms. Cannon Street lunch stop: the Bankers. London Bridge lunch stop: the Borough Market. 8 km.

**Paddington to Charing Cross**: A shorter variant of [The London Stations](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-stations/). Flat urban streets, Hyde Park, Kensington Gardens, Belgravia, and St James's Park. Sights: Paddington station, Elizabeth Line station, Kensington Gardens, Italian Gardens, Serpentine, Serpentine Lido, Princess Diana Memorial, Hyde Park, Kensington Palace, Buckingham Palace, Queen's Gallery, St James's Park, Horseguard's Parade, Banqueting House, Victoria station, Trafalgar Square, Nelson's Column, and Charing Cross station. Lunch in Hyde Park, or St James's Park. Hyde Park lunch stop: the Lido Cafe. St James's Park lunch stop: the St James's Cafe. 7 km.

**Kings Cross to Paddington**: A shorter variant of [The London Stations](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-stations/). Flat urban streets, canal towpath, and Regent's Park. Sights: Kings Cross station, St Pancras station, Granary Square, Crick Institute, Euston station, Regent's Park, Queen Mary's Gardens, Regent's Park Open Air Theatre, Marylebone station, Paddington Basin, and Paddington station. Lunch in Regent's Park, or Marylebone. Regent's Park lunch stop: the Garden Cafe. Marylebone lunch stop: the Gino's Cafe Bar. 5 km.

**Liverpool Street to Moorgate**: A longer variant of [The London Stations](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-stations/). Flat urban streets, shopping arcade, and City plazas. Sights: Broadgate Shopping Centre, Broadgate Circle, and Moorgate station. 0 km.

**London Bridge to Cutty Sark for Maritime Greenwich**: A shorter variant of [Westminster to Greenwich via the south bank](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-bridge-to-greenwich-via-the-south-bank/). Flat riverside pavement, converted Victorian wharves, residential riverside developments, industrial heritage, and pocket parks. Sights: [HMS Belfast](http://www.iwm.org.uk/visits/hms-belfast), City Hall, [Tower Bridge](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tower_Bridge), Design Museum, [Brunel Museum](http://www.brunel-museum.org.uk/), Surrey Docks Farm, Statue of Peter the Great, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, [Cutty Sark](http://www.rmg.co.uk/cuttysark), [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), and Royal Observatory. Lunch in Bermondsey, or Rotherhithe. Bermondsey lunch stop: the Angel Pub. Rotherhithe lunch stops: the [Mayflower pub](http://www.mayflowerpub.co.uk/), or the Salt Quay Pub.
CLON.publicWalksS2S: **[Kings Cross to Liverpool Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-stations/)**: Entirely flat, urban city-centre streets, royal parks, riverside Thames path, canal towpath, and historic financial district. Sights: Kings Cross station, St Pancras station, Kings Cross Central, Granary Square, Crick Institute, Euston station, Regent's Park, Queen Mary's Gardens, Regent's Park Open Air Theatre, Marylebone station, Great Central Hotel, Paddington Basin, Paddington station, Elizabeth Line station, Kensington Gardens, Italian Gardens, Serpentine, Serpentine Lido, Princess Diana Memorial, Hyde Park, Kensington Palace, Buckingham Palace, Queen's Gallery, St James's Park, Horseguard's Parade, Banqueting House, Trafalgar Square, Nelson's Column, Charing Cross station, Hungerford Bridge, Golden Jubilee Bridges, London Eye, Royal Festival Hall, National Theatre, Oxo Tower, Waterloo station, Tate Modern, Millennium Bridge, Shakespeare's Globe, Blackfriars station, Founders Arms, Southwark Bridge, Cannon Street station, Borough Market, Shard, London Bridge station, Hays Galleria, HMS Belfast, Tower Bridge, Tower of London, Fenchurch Street station, Leadenhall Market, Lloyd's Building, Cheesegrater, Gherkin, Walkie-Talkie, Skygarden, St Helen's Bishopsgate, St Ethelburga's, and Liverpool Street station. Lunch in Regent's Park, Marylebone, Hyde Park, St James's Park, Blackfriars, Cannon Street, or London Bridge. Regent's Park lunch stop: the Garden Cafe. Marylebone lunch stop: the Gino's Cafe Bar. Hyde Park lunch stop: the Lido Cafe. St James's Park lunch stop: the St James's Cafe. Blackfriars lunch stop: the Founders Arms. Cannon Street lunch stop: the Bankers. London Bridge lunch stop: the Borough Market. 21 km.

CLT.name: Clacton-on-Sea
CLT.privateNote: Midge recommends walk to St Othas via jaywick
CLT.adminWalksAll:
**[Clacton-on-Sea to Walton-on-the-Naze](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/clacton-on-sea-to-walton-on-the-naze/)**: Flat North Sea coastal path, sea wall, beaches, and seaside resort promenades. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in Holland-on-Sea: the Oakwood Inn. 11 km.

**Wivenhoe to Clacton-on-Sea (via ferry)**: A longer variant of [Wivenhoe to West Mersea or Clacton-on-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wivenhoe-to-west-mersea-or-clacton-on-sea/). Tidal river estuary, saltings, mud flats, disused railway trackbed, harbourfront, ferry crossing, coastal sea wall, caravan park frontages, sandy beach, and seaside town promenade. Sights: Jaywick Martello Tower and [Colne Point National Nature Reserve](http://www.essexwt.org.uk/reserves/colne-point). Check ferry times. Lunch in Brightlingsea, or Point Clear. Brightlingsea lunch stops: the No. 1 Harbour Square, or the [Yachtsman's Arms](https://www.facebook.com/theyachtsmansarms). Point Clear lunch stop: the Ferry Boat Inn. 24 km.
CLT.publicWalksS2S: **[Clacton-on-Sea to Walton-on-the-Naze](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/clacton-on-sea-to-walton-on-the-naze/)**: Flat North Sea coastal path, sea wall, beaches, and seaside resort promenades. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in Holland-on-Sea: the Oakwood Inn. 11 km.

CLV.name: Claverdon
CLV.adminWalksAll: **[Claverdon Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-816)**: Gently rolling farmland, small pockets of woodland, Grand Union Canal towpath, and motorway bridge crossing. Sights: [St Michaels Church](https://www.churchesofarden.org.uk/claverdon), Pinley Abbey, [Grand Union Canal](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Hatton Locks](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), and [Hatton Estate](https://www.hattonworld.com). Nettles and brambles. Lunch in Claverdon, or Shrewley. Claverdon lunch stop: the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrownclaverdon.co.uk). Shrewley lunch stop: the [Durham Ox](https://www.durhamoxshrewley.co.uk). 4 km. 1.5 hours.
CLV.publicWalksCircular: **[Claverdon Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-816)**: Gently rolling farmland, small pockets of woodland, Grand Union Canal towpath, and motorway bridge crossing. Sights: [St Michaels Church](https://www.churchesofarden.org.uk/claverdon), Pinley Abbey, [Grand Union Canal](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Hatton Locks](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), and [Hatton Estate](https://www.hattonworld.com). Nettles and brambles. Lunch in Claverdon, or Shrewley. Claverdon lunch stop: the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrownclaverdon.co.uk). Shrewley lunch stop: the [Durham Ox](https://www.durhamoxshrewley.co.uk). 4 km. 1.5 hours.

CLW.name: Chorleywood
CLW.adminWalksAll:
**[Chorleywood to Chesham via Bedford Chapel](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chorleywood-to-chesham/)**: Chess Valley, Chiltern Hills, hill climbs, panoramic views, woods, celandine and bluebell woods, valley rim permissive path, and suburban finish. Sights: Chenies Manor, Church of St Michaels, Bedford Chapel, and Latimer House. Can flood after rain. Best in Mar–Apr. Lunch in Chorleywood, Sarratt, or Chenies. Chorleywood lunch stop: the Cafe on the Common. Sarratt lunch stop: the Cock Inn. Chenies lunch stops: the Red Lion, or the Bedford Arms. 15 km.

**Chorleywood to Chalfont & Latimer**: A shorter variant of [Chorleywood to Chesham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chorleywood-to-chesham/). Track with fine views, beech woods, bluebells and rare coralroot flower, and suburban descent to station. Sights: Chenies Manor, Church of St Michaels, and Latimer House. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Chenies: the Red Lion, or the Bedford Arms. 9 km.

**Beaconsfield to Chorleywood via Chiltern Open Air Museum**: Wooded hills, open fields, Chiltern countryside, historic museum grounds, and residential lanes. Sights: [Chiltern Open Air Museum](http://www.coam.org.uk/), Milton's Cottage, and Friends Meeting House. Lunch in Chalfont St Giles: the Merlins Cave. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 15 km.
CLW.publicWalksS2S: **[Chorleywood to Chesham via Bedford Chapel](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chorleywood-to-chesham/)**: Chess Valley, Chiltern Hills, hill climbs, panoramic views, woods, celandine and bluebell woods, valley rim permissive path, and suburban finish. Sights: Chenies Manor, Church of St Michaels, Bedford Chapel, and Latimer House. Can flood after rain. Best in Mar–Apr. Lunch in Chorleywood, Sarratt, or Chenies. Chorleywood lunch stop: the Cafe on the Common. Sarratt lunch stop: the Cock Inn. Chenies lunch stops: the Red Lion, or the Bedford Arms. 15 km.

CLYM.name: Lymington
CLYM.adminWalksAll:
**Lymington to Brockenhurst via Roydon Woods**: Can be EXTREME mud in Lymington Reeds Beds Nature Reserve. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 3 February 2024.* 12 km. 90 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921509210)**

**Lymington Pier to Lymington Town via Shirley Holms**: A shorter variant of [Brockenhurst to Lymington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/brockenhurst-to-lymington/). River estuary path, wet woodland, Iron Age hill fort, farm fields, heathland, and coastal saltmarsh embankments. Sights: [Walhampton Monument](https://burrardneale.wordpress.com/history-of-the-walhampton-monument), [Lymington Reedbeds](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/lymington-reedbeds-nature-reserve), [Buckland Rings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Buckland_Rings), Shirley Holms, [Lymington–Keyhaven Marshes](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/lymingtonkeyhaven), Salt Boiling Houses, Moses Dock, [Lymington Sea Water Baths](https://www.lymingtonseawaterbaths.org.uk), and Lymington Yacht Haven. Wet woodland sections. Lunch in Chequers Green: the [Chequers Inn](http://www.chequersinnlymington.com). 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**Lymington Town to New Milton**: A variant of [Lymington to Barton-on-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lymington-to-barton-on-sea/). Coastal path, salt marshes, nature reserve, esplanade, low eroding cliffs, and then inland road walking to station. Sights: Hurst Castle, [Hordle Cliff](https://wessexcoastgeology.soton.ac.uk/hordle.htm), and [Beachcomber Café](http://www.beachcomber-cafe.co.uk/). Eroding cliff edges. Lunch in Keyhaven, or Milford-on-Sea. Keyhaven lunch stop: the [Gun Inn](http://www.theguninn.com/). Milford-on-Sea lunch stops: the [Marine](http://www.themarinemos.co.uk/), or the Needles Eye Café. 18 km.

**Lymington Pier to Lymington Town via Hurst Castle**: An alternative variant of [Brockenhurst to Lymington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/brockenhurst-to-lymington/). River estuary path, wet woodland, Iron Age hill fort, farm fields, coastal marshes, shingle spit, and saltmarsh embankments. Sights: [Walhampton Monument](https://burrardneale.wordpress.com/history-of-the-walhampton-monument), [Lymington Reedbeds](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/lymington-reedbeds-nature-reserve), [Buckland Rings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Buckland_Rings), [Hurst Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/hurst-castle), Hurst Point Lighthouse, Fort Albert, Keyhaven Bird Observatory, The Pans & Avon Water Wetlands, [Lymington–Keyhaven Marshes](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/lymingtonkeyhaven), Salt Boiling Houses, Moses Dock, [Lymington Sea Water Baths](https://www.lymingtonseawaterbaths.org.uk), and Lymington Yacht Haven. Wet woodland sections. Lunch in Keyhaven: the [Gun Inn](https://www.theguninn.co.uk). 21 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Brockenhurst to Lymington Town via Lymington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/brockenhurst-to-lymington/)**: Landscaped parkland, mixed woodland, open heathland, farm fields, coastal saltmarsh embankments, and sailing town. Sights: New Zealand Cemetery, St Nicholas Church, Brockenhurst Park, [Roydon Woods Nature Reserve](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/roydon-woods-nature-reserve), Roydon Manor, Shirley Holms, [Lymington–Keyhaven Marshes](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/lymingtonkeyhaven), [Lymington Sea Water Baths](https://www.lymingtonseawaterbaths.org.uk), [Lymington](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lymington), Salt Boiling Houses, Moses Dock, Lymington Yacht Haven, and Royal Lymington Yacht Club. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chequers Green: the [Chequers Inn](http://www.chequersinnlymington.com). 20 km. 4.5 hours.

**Brockenhurst to Lymington Town via Hurst Castle**: A longer variant of [Brockenhurst to Lymington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/brockenhurst-to-lymington/). Landscaped parkland, woodland, heathland, farm fields, coastal marshes, shingle spit, and saltmarsh embankments. Sights: New Zealand Cemetery, St Nicholas Church, Brockenhurst Park, [Roydon Woods Nature Reserve](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/roydon-woods-nature-reserve), Roydon Manor, Shirley Holms, [Hurst Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/hurst-castle), Hurst Point Lighthouse, Fort Albert, Keyhaven Bird Observatory, The Pans & Avon Water Wetlands, [Lymington–Keyhaven Marshes](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/lymingtonkeyhaven), Salt Boiling Houses, Moses Dock, [Lymington Sea Water Baths](https://www.lymingtonseawaterbaths.org.uk), and Lymington Yacht Haven. Can be muddy. Lunch in Keyhaven: the [Gun Inn](https://www.theguninn.co.uk). 25 km. 5.5 hours.
CLYM.publicWalksS2S: **Lymington to Brockenhurst via Roydon Woods**: Can be EXTREME mud in Lymington Reeds Beds Nature Reserve. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 12 km. 90 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921509210)**

Sights: Archbishop's Palace, All Saints, Maidstone, The Stag, Lockmeadow Millennium Bridge, Tovil Railway Bridge, East Farleigh Lock, East Farleigh Bridge, Barming Bridge, Teston Lock, Teston Bridge, and Maidstone Millennium River Park. 9 km. 2 hours.

Burham lunch stops: the [Butcher's Block](https://thebutchersblockpub.co.uk), or the [Windmill](https://windmillburham.com). Burham Common lunch stop: the [Robin Hood](https://www.robinhood-pub.co.uk). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

Lunch in Carshalton: the [Greyhound Hotel](https://www.thegreyhoundhotel.com), the [Hope](https://hopecarshalton.co.uk), or the [Sun](https://www.thesuncarshalton.com/food). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

Sights: [Newhaven Fort](http://www.newhavenfort.org.uk), Brighton Pier, Brighton Marina, Brighton Pavilion, The Lanes, and Saltdean Lido. Exposed in high wind. Lunch in Newhaven, Peacehaven, Telescombe, Saltdean, Rottingdean, or Brighton Marina. Newhaven lunch stop: the Hope Inn. Peacehaven lunch stops: the [Peacehaven](https://www.stonehouserestaurants.co.uk/nationalsearch/southeast/thepeacehavenpeacehaven), or the Smuggler's Rest. Telescombe lunch stop: the [Telescombe Tavern](http://telscombetavern.wix.com/thetelscombetavern). Saltdean lunch stop: the [Whitecliffs](https://www.facebook.com/whitecliffssaltdean/). Rottingdean lunch stops: the Molly's at the Beach, or the White Horse. Brighton Marina lunch stop: the West Quay. 18 km.

**[Newhaven Town to Newhaven Harbour via Newhaven and Seaford Sailing Club](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/newhaven-circular/)**: Clifftop coast path, woods and fields, river bank, South Downs foothills, hills, shingle beach, and deserted village ruins. Sights: Newhaven Fort, St John the Evangelist Church, Tide Mills, [Newhaven and Seaford Sailing Club](http://sailinginsussex.org/galley), Bishopstone Beach Halt station platform, and Chaley Heritage Marine Hospital excavations. Denton, Newhaven lunch stop: the [Flying Fish](https://www.flyingfishdenton.co.uk/). South Heighton, Newhaven lunch stop: the Hampden Arms. 24 km.

COO.name: Cookham
COO.adminWalksAll:
**Cookham Circular via Marlow Bridge**: A variant of [Maidenhead to Marlow](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/maidenhead-to-marlow/). Hill over Winter Hill to Marlow and then riverside return to Cookham. Sights: Winter Hill, Marlow Bridge, and Cliveden. Riverside can be slithery. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Marlow: the Marlow Donkey. 14 km.

**Cookham Circular via Stanley Spencer Gallery**: Hills, escarpment with valley views, beech woods, open fields, and riverside Thames Path. Sights: Stanley Spencer Gallery, Holy Trinity Church, Winter Hill, Bisham Woods, and Cock Marsh. Lunch in Cookham Dean, Cock Marsh, or Cookham. Cookham Dean lunch stops: the Jolly Farmer, or the Mole & Badger. Cock Marsh lunch stop: the Bounty. Cookham lunch stops: the Kings Arms, the Ferry, or the Bel and the Dragon. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 11 km.

**Cookham to Maidenhead**: Hills, escarpment with valley views, beech woods, open fields, riverside Thames Path, wooded estate banks, and town outskirts. Sights: Stanley Spencer Gallery, Holy Trinity Church, Cliveden Estate, Winter Hill, Boulter's Lock, Bisham Woods, Cock Marsh, and Brunel's Maidenhead Railway Bridge. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Cookham Dean, Cock Marsh, or Cookham. Cookham Dean lunch stops: the Jolly Farmer, or the Mole & Badger. Cock Marsh lunch stop: the Bounty. Cookham lunch stops: the Kings Arms, the Ferry, or the Bel and the Dragon. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 5 hours.

**Cookham to Marlow**: A shorter variant of [Maidenhead to Marlow](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/maidenhead-to-marlow/). Short steep hill over Winter Hill, escarpment views of Thames Valley, and descent into Marlow. Sights: Winter Hill and Marlow Bridge. Best in Sep–Nov. 8 km.

**Cookham to Taplow**: Hills, escarpment, beech woods, open fields, Thames riverside, country park, and residential roads. Sights: Stanley Spencer Gallery, Holy Trinity Church, Cliveden Estate, Boulter's Lock, Ray Mill Island, and Taplow Riverside. Lunch in Cookham: the Kings Arms, the Ferry, or the Bel and the Dragon. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).

**Gerrards Cross to Cookham**: Parkland, lakes, woods, beech forest, open fields, Thames riverside, village lanes, and National Trust common. Sights: Bulstrode Park, Bulstrode Manor, Hedgerley Green Nature Reserve, St Mary the Virgin, Egypt Wood, Burnham Beeches, Church of St Nicholas, Holy Trinity Church, Stanley Spencer Gallery, Stanley Spencer's memorial stone, and Cookham Moor. Can be muddy. Best in Nov. Lunch in Hedgerley, or Littleworth Common. Hedgerley lunch stop: the White Horse. Littleworth Common lunch stop: the Blackwood Arms. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 15 km. 4.5 hours.

**[West Drayton to Cookham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/west-drayton-to-cookham/)**: Lowland heaths, mature woodland, canal towpath, farmland, and historic riverside village. Sights: [Langley Park County Park](http://www.buckscc.gov.uk/leisure-and-culture/country-parks/langley-park/), Black Park County Park, [Burnham Beeches](http://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/burnham-beeches-and-stoke-common/Pages/default.aspx), Lord Boston's Victorian Folly, Grand Union Canal, and River Thames. Can be muddy. Lunch in Fulmer, Farnham Common, Burnham Beeches, Littleworth Common, or Cookham. Fulmer lunch stop: the [Black Horse](http://www.theblackhorsefulmer.co.uk/). Farnham Common lunch stop: the [Forresters](http://www.theforesterspub.com/). Burnham Beeches lunch stop: the Beeches Cafe. Littleworth Common lunch stop: the Blackwood Arms. Cookham lunch stops: the Infusions, the Kings Arms, or the Bel and the Dragon. 27 km.

**Maidenhead to Cookham via Maidenhead Rail Bridge**: A shorter variant of [Maidenhead to Marlow](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/maidenhead-to-marlow/). Thames riverside path, wooded escarpment views, and Boulters Lock. Sights: Maidenhead Rail Bridge, Boulters Lock, and Cliveden. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Cookham: the Bel and the Dragon, the Kings Arms, the Crown, or the Teapot Tea Shop. 8 km.

**Marlow to Cookham via Thames Path**: Flat riverside Thames Path, meadows, riverside gardens, and railway bridge crossing. Sights: Holy Trinity Church and Stanley Spencer Gallery. Lunch in Bourne End, or Cookham. Bourne End lunch stop: the Bounty Inn. Cookham lunch stops: the Kings Arms, the Ferry, or the Bel and the Dragon. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 7 km.

**Maidenhead to Cookham via Winter Hill**: A longer variant of [Maidenhead to Marlow](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/maidenhead-to-marlow/). Thames riverside path, hill, town, and then riverside return to Cookham. Sights: Maidenhead Rail Bridge, Boulters Lock, Cliveden, Winter Hill, and Marlow Bridge. Riverside can be slithery. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Cookham: the Bel and the Dragon, the Kings Arms, the Crown, or the Teapot Tea Shop. 21 km.

**Langley to Cookham**: A shorter variant of [West Drayton to Cookham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/west-drayton-to-cookham/). Lowland heaths, mature beech woodland, country parks, commons, and historic riverside village. Sights: [Langley Park County Park](http://www.buckscc.gov.uk/leisure-and-culture/country-parks/langley-park/), Black Park County Park, [Burnham Beeches](http://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/burnham-beeches-and-stoke-common/Pages/default.aspx), Lord Boston's Victorian Folly, and River Thames. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in Fulmer, Farnham Common, Burnham Beeches, Littleworth Common, or Cookham. Fulmer lunch stop: the [Black Horse](http://www.theblackhorsefulmer.co.uk/). Farnham Common lunch stop: the [Forresters](http://www.theforesterspub.com/). Burnham Beeches lunch stop: the Beeches Cafe. Littleworth Common lunch stop: the Blackwood Arms. Cookham lunch stops: the Infusions, the Kings Arms, or the Bel and the Dragon. 22 km.

Sights: Stanley Spencer Gallery, Holy Trinity Church, Cliveden Estate, Winter Hill, Boulter's Lock, Bisham Woods, Cock Marsh, and Brunel's Maidenhead Railway Bridge. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Cookham Dean, Cock Marsh, or Cookham. Cookham Dean lunch stops: the Jolly Farmer, or the Mole & Badger. Cock Marsh lunch stop: the Bounty. Cookham lunch stops: the Kings Arms, the Ferry, or the Bel and the Dragon. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 5 hours.

Cookham lunch stops: the Infusions, the Kings Arms, or the Bel and the Dragon. 27 km.

COV.name: Coventry
COV.adminWalksAll: **[Coventry Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-701)**: Historic city centre, pedestrian boulevards, cobbled streets, canal basin, city walls, and ring road underpasses. Sights: [Herbert Art Gallery & Museum](https://www.theherbert.org), [Holy Trinity Church](https://www.holytrinitycoventry.org.uk), [Coventry Cathedral](https://www.coventrycathedral.org.uk), [Coventry Transport Museum](https://www.transport-museum.com), [FarGo Village](https://www.fargovillage.co.uk), Canal Basin, Coventry Watch Museum, St John's Church, Bonds Hospital, Cook Street Gatehouse, Whittle Arches, Frank Whittle Statue, Priory Undercroft, St Michael and Devil Statue, St Mary's Guildhall, Priory Gardens, Lady Godiva Statue, Ford's Hospital, Christchurch Spire, Cheylesmore Manor, and Starley Memorial. Lunch in Spon Street, Coventry: the [Old Windmill](https://www.facebook.com/TheOldWindmill.Coventry). 4 km. 1.5 hours.
COV.publicWalksCircular: **[Coventry Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-701)**: Historic city centre, pedestrian boulevards, cobbled streets, canal basin, city walls, and ring road underpasses. Sights: [Herbert Art Gallery & Museum](https://www.theherbert.org), [Holy Trinity Church](https://www.holytrinitycoventry.org.uk), [Coventry Cathedral](https://www.coventrycathedral.org.uk), [Coventry Transport Museum](https://www.transport-museum.com), [FarGo Village](https://www.fargovillage.co.uk), Canal Basin, Coventry Watch Museum, St John's Church, Bonds Hospital, Cook Street Gatehouse, Whittle Arches, Frank Whittle Statue, Priory Undercroft, St Michael and Devil Statue, St Mary's Guildhall, Priory Gardens, Lady Godiva Statue, Ford's Hospital, Christchurch Spire, Cheylesmore Manor, and Starley Memorial. Lunch in Spon Street, Coventry: the [Old Windmill](https://www.facebook.com/TheOldWindmill.Coventry). 4 km. 1.5 hours.

CPEV.name: Pevensey
CPEV.adminWalksAll:
**Pevensey to Battle**: An essential walk! Sights: [Battle Abbey](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/1066-battle-of-hastings-abbey-and-battlefield/) (the site of the Battle of Hastings, upon which William the Conqeuror built his mighty Battle Abbey in papally-imposed contrition for the blood he had shed). From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 1 May 2023.* 28 km. 350 m uphill. 7.5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920345301)**

**Pevensey and Westham to Eastbourne**: A shorter variant of [Eastbourne to Hastings via Bexhill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eastbourne-to-hastings-via-bexhill/). Coastal path south-west from Pevensey, seafront promenade, sandy beach, and Eastbourne pier. Sights: [Pevensey Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/daysout/properties/pevensey-castle/) and [Eastbourne Pier](http://www.eastbournepier.com/). Lunch in Pevensey Bay: the Aqua Bar, or the [Castle Inn](http://castleinnpevenseybay.co.uk/). 9 km.
CPEV.publicWalksS2S: **Pevensey to Battle**: An essential walk! Sights: [Battle Abbey](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/1066-battle-of-hastings-abbey-and-battlefield/) (the site of the Battle of Hastings, upon which William the Conqeuror built his mighty Battle Abbey in papally-imposed contrition for the blood he had shed). From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 28 km. 350 m uphill. 7.5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920345301)**

CPM.name: Chippenham
CPM.adminWalksAll: **[Chippenham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chippenham-circular/)**: Flat fields, river meadows, canal path, farmland, small woods, and residential streets. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lacock, or Derry Hill. Lacock lunch stops: the [George Inn](http://www.georgeinnlacock.co.uk/), the [Red Lion](https://www.redlionlacock.co.uk/), or the [Sign of the Angel](https://www.signoftheangel.co.uk/). Derry Hill lunch stop: the [Lansdowne Arms](https://www.lansdownearms.co.uk/). 17 km.
CPM.publicWalksCircular: **[Chippenham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chippenham-circular/)**: Flat fields, river meadows, canal path, farmland, small woods, and residential streets. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lacock, or Derry Hill. Lacock lunch stops: the [George Inn](http://www.georgeinnlacock.co.uk/), the [Red Lion](https://www.redlionlacock.co.uk/), or the [Sign of the Angel](https://www.signoftheangel.co.uk/). Derry Hill lunch stop: the [Lansdowne Arms](https://www.lansdownearms.co.uk/). 17 km.

CPOR.name: Portsmouth
CPOR.adminWalksAll: **[Portsmouth Harbour to Havant](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/portsmouth-to-havant-via-hayling-island/)**: Flat coastal walk, seafront promenade, sandy beaches, short ferry crossing, disused railway path, and harbourside shore path. Sights: Spinnaker Tower, Southsea Castle, South Parade Pier, Lumps Fort, Eastney Batteries, Fort Cumberland, [Hayling Island Branch Line](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hayling_Island_Branch_Line), and St Nicholas Chapel. Lunch in Old Portsmouth, Hayling Island, or Langstone. Old Portsmouth lunch stop: the [Spice Island](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/hampshire/spice-island). Hayling Island lunch stops: the [Ferry Boat Inn](http://www.theferryboatinnhaylingisland.co.uk), or the [Inn on the Beach](http://www.innonthebeach.co.uk). Langstone lunch stops: the [Ship Inn](http://www.shiplangstone.co.uk), or the Royal Oak. 20 km.
CPOR.publicWalksS2S: **[Portsmouth Harbour to Havant](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/portsmouth-to-havant-via-hayling-island/)**: Flat coastal walk, seafront promenade, sandy beaches, short ferry crossing, disused railway path, and harbourside shore path. Sights: Spinnaker Tower, Southsea Castle, South Parade Pier, Lumps Fort, Eastney Batteries, Fort Cumberland, [Hayling Island Branch Line](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hayling_Island_Branch_Line), and St Nicholas Chapel. Lunch in Old Portsmouth, Hayling Island, or Langstone. Old Portsmouth lunch stop: the [Spice Island](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/hampshire/spice-island). Hayling Island lunch stops: the [Ferry Boat Inn](http://www.theferryboatinnhaylingisland.co.uk), or the [Inn on the Beach](http://www.innonthebeach.co.uk). Langstone lunch stops: the [Ship Inn](http://www.shiplangstone.co.uk), or the Royal Oak. 20 km.

CRB.name: Corbridge
CRB.adminWalksAll: **Hexham to Corbridge**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). River Tyne, country lanes, North Tyne Valley views, Wall stretches, ridge, and historic town of Corbridge. Sights: Chesters Fort, Brunton Turret, St. Oswald's Church, Halton Chesters Fort, Aydon Castle, Halton Castle, and Halton Church. Lunch in Wall, or near Halton Chesters. Wall lunch stop: the Hadrian Hotel. near Halton Chesters lunch stop: the Errington Coffee House. 21 km.
CRB.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Hexham to Corbridge**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). River Tyne, country lanes, North Tyne Valley views, Wall stretches, ridge, and historic town of Corbridge. Sights: Chesters Fort, Brunton Turret, St. Oswald's Church, Halton Chesters Fort, Aydon Castle, Halton Castle, and Halton Church. Lunch in Wall, or near Halton Chesters. Wall lunch stop: the Hadrian Hotel. near Halton Chesters lunch stop: the Errington Coffee House. 21 km.

CREA.name: Reading
CREA.adminWalksAll: **[Reading to Henley-on-Thames via Sonning Lock](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/reading-to-henley/)**: Flat Thames towpath, riverside parks, wooded sections, pasture fields, meadows, and riverside gardens. Sights: Sonning Lock, Sonning Bridge, Shiplake Lock, Marsh Lock, and [Thames Path](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thames_Path). Flood risk sections. Lunch in Sonning: the Bull, or the Coppa Club. 16 km.
CREA.publicWalksS2S: **[Reading to Henley-on-Thames via Sonning Lock](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/reading-to-henley/)**: Flat Thames towpath, riverside parks, wooded sections, pasture fields, meadows, and riverside gardens. Sights: Sonning Lock, Sonning Bridge, Shiplake Lock, Marsh Lock, and [Thames Path](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thames_Path). Flood risk sections. Lunch in Sonning: the Bull, or the Coppa Club. 16 km.

CRN.name: Crowthorne
CRN.adminWalksAll:
**Crowthorne Circular**: An alternative variant of [Crowthorne to Blackwater via Eversley Cross](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/crowthorne-to-blackwater-via-eversley-cross/). Pine woods, heathland, lakes and flooded gravel pits, riverside meadows, and small nature reserves. Sights: [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), Ambarrow Court Local Nature Reserve, Wellington College, Wellingtonia Avenue, and Martineau Cottages. Flood risk in places. Lunch in Eversley Cross: the [Frog and Wicket](https://www.thefrogandwicket.co.uk), or the [Chequers](https://www.thechequerseversleycross.co.uk). 14 km. 3 hours.

**[Crowthorne to Blackwater](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/crowthorne-to-blackwater-via-eversley-cross/)**: Pine woods, heathland, lakes and flooded gravel pits, riverside meadows, and green belt nature reserves. Sights: [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), Shepherd Meadows Nature Reserve, Wellingtonia Avenue, Martineau Cottages, Devil's Highway, Three Castles Path, and Blackwater Valley Path. Flood risk in places. Lunch in Eversley Cross: the [Frog and Wicket](https://www.thefrogandwicket.co.uk), or the [Chequers](https://www.thechequerseversleycross.co.uk). 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**Crowthorne to Sandhurst**: A shorter variant of [Crowthorne to Blackwater via Eversley Cross](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/crowthorne-to-blackwater-via-eversley-cross/). Pine woods, heathland, lakes and flooded gravel pits, riverside meadows, and town roads. Sights: [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), Wellingtonia Avenue, and Martineau Cottages. Flood risk in places. Lunch in Eversley Cross: the [Frog and Wicket](https://www.thefrogandwicket.co.uk), or the [Chequers](https://www.thechequerseversleycross.co.uk). 13 km.

**Sandhurst to Crowthorne**: A variant of [Blackwater to Wokingham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackwater-to-wokingham/). Roads from station, gravel-pit lakes, nature reserves, country lanes, mixed woodland, heathland, and pine woods. Sights: [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), Ambarrow Court Local Nature Reserve, Wellington College, and St James's Church. Lunch in Finchampstead: the [Queen's Oak](https://www.thequeensoak.com), or the [Greyhound](https://greyhoundfinchampstead.co.uk). 14 km.

**Blackwater to Crowthorne**: A shorter variant of [Blackwater to Wokingham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackwater-to-wokingham/). River meadows, gravel-pit lakes, nature reserves, country lanes, mixed woodland, heathland, and pine woods alongside the railway. Sights: Shepherd Meadows Nature Reserve, [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), Ambarrow Court Local Nature Reserve, Wellington College, St James's Church, and Wellingtonia Avenue. Flood risk in places. Lunch in Finchampstead: the [Queen's Oak](https://www.thequeensoak.com), or the [Greyhound](https://greyhoundfinchampstead.co.uk). 17 km. 4 hours.
CRN.publicWalksS2S: **[Crowthorne to Blackwater](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/crowthorne-to-blackwater-via-eversley-cross/)**: Pine woods, heathland, lakes and flooded gravel pits, riverside meadows, and green belt nature reserves. Sights: [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), Shepherd Meadows Nature Reserve, Wellingtonia Avenue, Martineau Cottages, Devil's Highway, Three Castles Path, and Blackwater Valley Path. Flood risk in places. Lunch in Eversley Cross: the [Frog and Wicket](https://www.thefrogandwicket.co.uk), or the [Chequers](https://www.thechequerseversleycross.co.uk). 16 km. 3.5 hours.

CSAL.name: St Albans
CSAL.adminWalksAll:
**St Albans Circular via Gorhambury House**: Historic city. Much of the walk is through St Albans, but it's a beautiful city so this is no bad thing. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 22 August 2020.* 13 km. 120 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921498612)**

**Harpenden to St Albans via Sanbridge**: Mostly fields and country lanes. Some beautiful new bluebell woods, stunning historic city streeets, commons, golf courses, and parkland. Sights: [Bluebell Woods in Heartwood Forest](https://heartwood.woodlandtrust.org.uk/), [Childwickbury Estate](https://www.google.com/maps/place/?q=place_id:ChIJxz4q38U4dkgRO4Z_rruVnV8), and [St Albans Cathedral and Abbey Church of St Alban](https://www.stalbanscathedral.org/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Sandridge: the [Queens Head](https://linktr.ee/TheQueensHeadSandridge), the [Heartwood Tea Rooms](http://heartwoodtearooms.com/), the [Green Man](http://www.greenmansandridge.co.uk/), or the [Rose & Crown](http://www.theroseandcrownsandridge.co.uk/). End-of-walk rests: [Mad Squirrel Tap](https://www.madsquirrelstalbans.co.uk/), [Abigail's Cafe](https://www.google.com/maps/place/?q=place_id:ChIJfy5BmiM_dkgRUNtX7o50Yx4), [The Peahen](https://www.mcmullens.co.uk/local-pub/peahen/), [The Snug](https://www.google.com/maps/place/?q=place_id:ChIJmT8rc8JHdkgR6s4QOCS4Wkw), or [The Boot](http://www.bootcantina.com/). *Last reviewed 4 May 2026.* 17 km. 130 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2930623590)**

**Garston to St Albans via Moor Mill**: Common woodland, farmland tracks, river meadows, water meadows, quiet residential roads, and historic city streets. Sights: Bricket Wood Common, Munden House, Moor Mill, Netherwylde Farm, [Verulamium Museum](http://www.stalbansmuseums.org.uk/Sites/Verulamium-Museum), [Verulamium](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Verulamium), [Roman Theatre](http://www.romantheatre.co.uk/), St Albans Cathedral, Great Gateway, Ye Olde Fighting Cocks, Clock Tower, Roman Wall, Hypocaust, and Sopwell Nunnery. Can be very muddy. Lunch in Bricket Wood, or Park Street. Bricket Wood lunch stop: the Moor Mill. Park Street lunch stops: the Overdraught, or the Falcon. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 12 km. 60 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920312228)**

**St Albans Abbey to Park Street**: An alternative variant of [Abbey Trail](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/). 4 km.

**[Watford Junction to St Albans Abbey](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/)**: Way-marked trail through the Ver and Colne Valleys and linking all seven Abbey Line stations — can be done end-to-end or as individual sections with a train hop back. Sights: Sopwell Nunnery and St Albans Abbey. Sections can become waterlogged after heavy rain, sometimes without obvious alternative routes. 14 km.

**Garston to St Albans Abbey**: Common woodland, farmland tracks, river meadows, water meadows, quiet residential roads, and historic city streets. Sights: Bricket Wood Common, Munden House, Moor Mill, Netherwylde Farm, [Verulamium Museum](http://www.stalbansmuseums.org.uk/Sites/Verulamium-Museum), [Verulamium](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Verulamium), [Roman Theatre](http://www.romantheatre.co.uk/), St Albans Cathedral, Great Gateway, Ye Olde Fighting Cocks, Clock Tower, Roman Wall, Hypocaust, and Sopwell Nunnery. Can be very muddy. Lunch in Bricket Wood, or Park Street. Bricket Wood lunch stop: the Moor Mill. Park Street lunch stops: the Overdraught, or the Falcon. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).
CSAL.publicWalksS2SEnding:
**Harpenden to St Albans via Sanbridge**: Mostly fields and country lanes. Some beautiful new bluebell woods, stunning historic city streeets, commons, golf courses, and parkland. Sights: [Bluebell Woods in Heartwood Forest](https://heartwood.woodlandtrust.org.uk/), [Childwickbury Estate](https://www.google.com/maps/place/?q=place_id:ChIJxz4q38U4dkgRO4Z_rruVnV8), and [St Albans Cathedral and Abbey Church of St Alban](https://www.stalbanscathedral.org/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Sandridge: the [Queens Head](https://linktr.ee/TheQueensHeadSandridge), the [Heartwood Tea Rooms](http://heartwoodtearooms.com/), the [Green Man](http://www.greenmansandridge.co.uk/), or the [Rose & Crown](http://www.theroseandcrownsandridge.co.uk/). End-of-walk rests: [Mad Squirrel Tap](https://www.madsquirrelstalbans.co.uk/), [Abigail's Cafe](https://www.google.com/maps/place/?q=place_id:ChIJfy5BmiM_dkgRUNtX7o50Yx4), [The Peahen](https://www.mcmullens.co.uk/local-pub/peahen/), [The Snug](https://www.google.com/maps/place/?q=place_id:ChIJmT8rc8JHdkgR6s4QOCS4Wkw), or [The Boot](http://www.bootcantina.com/). 17 km. 130 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2930623590)**

**Garston to St Albans via Moor Mill**: Common woodland, farmland tracks, river meadows, water meadows, quiet residential roads, and historic city streets. Sights: Bricket Wood Common, Munden House, Moor Mill, Netherwylde Farm, [Verulamium Museum](http://www.stalbansmuseums.org.uk/Sites/Verulamium-Museum), [Verulamium](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Verulamium), [Roman Theatre](http://www.romantheatre.co.uk/), St Albans Cathedral, Great Gateway, Ye Olde Fighting Cocks, Clock Tower, Roman Wall, Hypocaust, and Sopwell Nunnery. Can be very muddy. Lunch in Bricket Wood, or Park Street. Bricket Wood lunch stop: the Moor Mill. Park Street lunch stops: the Overdraught, or the Falcon. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 12 km. 60 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920312228)**
CSAL.publicWalksCircular: **St Albans Circular via Gorhambury House**: Historic city. Much of the walk is through St Albans, but it's a beautiful city so this is no bad thing. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 13 km. 120 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921498612)**

CSAV.name: Stratford-upon-Avon
CSAV.adminWalksAll:
**[Stratford-upon-Avon Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-1237)**: Medieval market town, canal basin, riverside path, parks and gardens, and historic streets. Sights: [Shakespeare's Birthplace](https://www.shakespeare.org.uk), New Place, [Shakespeare's Schoolroom & Guildhall](https://www.shakespearesschoolroom.org), Royal Shakespeare Theatre, Swan Theatre, [Holy Trinity Church](https://www.stratford-upon-avon.org), [Bancroft Gardens](https://www.visitstratforduponavon.co.uk), Gower Memorial, Shakespeare Memorial Fountain, Hall's Croft, Guild Chapel, and Nash's House. Lunch in Stratford-upon-Avon: the [Dirty Duck](https://www.greeneking-pubs.co.uk/pubs/warwickshire/dirty-duck/), or the [Shakespaw Cat Cafe](https://www.shakespawcatcafe.com). 4 km. 1.5 hours.

**[Stratford-upon-Avon Parkway Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-823)**: Canal towpath, village streets, kissing gate, and lock-side path. Sights: [Stratford-upon-Avon Canal](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [St Andrew's Church](https://www.britishlistedbuildings.co.uk), [Mary Arden's Farm](https://www.shakespeare.org.uk), [Stratford Armouries](https://www.stratfordarmouries.co.uk), and [Shakespeare Distillery](https://www.shakespearedistillery.com). Limited refreshment options. Lunch in Wilmcote: the [Mary Arden Inn](https://www.mary-arden.co.uk), or the Wilmcote stores. 3 km. 2.5 hours.

**[Stratford-upon-Avon Parkway Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-823)**: Canal towpath, village streets, kissing gate, and lock-side path. Limited refreshment options. Lunch in Wilmcote: the [Mary Arden Inn](https://www.mary-arden.co.uk), or the Wilmcote stores. 3 km. 2.5 hours.

CSD.name: Cobham & Stoke d'Abernon
CSD.adminWalksAll:
**Cobham & Stoke d'Abernon Circular via Painshill & Black Pond**: A longer variant of [Oxshott to Cobham via Painshill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxshott-to-cobham-via-painshill/). Surrey heaths, wooded commons via Littleheath, heath ponds, residential alleyways, water meadows, country lanes, and bridleways alongside the River Mole. Sights: Oxshott Heath, War Memorial, Littleheath Common, Black Pond, [Cobham Mill](https://www.cobhammill.org.uk), [Painshill](https://www.painshill.co.uk), St Andrew's Church, Church Stile House, Cobham Park Estate, and St Michael's Chapel. Water meadows flood easily. Lunch in Cobham: the [Bear](https://www.bearcobham.co.uk). 18 km. 4 hours.

**[Oxshott to Cobham & Stoke d'Abernon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxshott-to-cobham-via-painshill/)**: Surrey heaths, wooded commons, heath ponds, residential alleyways, water meadows, country lanes, and bridleways alongside the River Mole. Sights: Oxshott Heath, War Memorial, Black Pond, [Cobham Mill](https://www.cobhammill.org.uk), [Painshill](https://www.painshill.co.uk), St Andrew's Church, Church Stile House, Cobham Park Estate, and St Michael's Chapel. Water meadows flood easily. Lunch in Cobham: the [Bear](https://www.bearcobham.co.uk). 15 km. 3.5 hours.
CSD.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Oxshott to Cobham & Stoke d'Abernon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxshott-to-cobham-via-painshill/)**: Surrey heaths, wooded commons, heath ponds, residential alleyways, water meadows, country lanes, and bridleways alongside the River Mole. Sights: Oxshott Heath, War Memorial, Black Pond, [Cobham Mill](https://www.cobhammill.org.uk), [Painshill](https://www.painshill.co.uk), St Andrew's Church, Church Stile House, Cobham Park Estate, and St Michael's Chapel. Water meadows flood easily. Lunch in Cobham: the [Bear](https://www.bearcobham.co.uk). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

CSH.name: Carshalton
CSH.adminWalksAll:
**Carshalton to Colliers Wood**: A shorter variant of [Mitcham Junction to Colliers Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mitcham-junction-to-colliers-wood/). Flat urban riverside parks, nature reserves, chalk stream, and south London green corridors. Sights: [Wandle Trail](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk/projects/wandle-trail), [Sutton Ecology Centre](https://www.sutton.gov.uk/w/biodiversity-and-wildlife-sutton-ecology-centre), [Honeywood Museum](https://friendsofhoneywood.co.uk/index.html), [Carshalton Water Tower](https://carshaltonwatertower.co.uk/index.html), [Morden Hall Park](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/morden-hall-park), [Deen City Farm](https://www.deencityfarm.co.uk), [Merton Abbey Mills](https://www.mertonabbeymills.org.uk), Carshalton Ponds, Watermeads Nature Reserve, Ravensbury Park, Wilderness Island, Morden Hall, and Merton Priory. Lunch in Carshalton: the [Greyhound Hotel](https://www.thegreyhoundhotel.com), the [Hope](https://hopecarshalton.co.uk), or the [Sun](https://www.thesuncarshalton.com/food). 9 km. 2 hours.

**Carshalton to Morden**: A shorter variant of [Mitcham Junction to Colliers Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mitcham-junction-to-colliers-wood/). Flat urban riverside parks, nature reserves, chalk stream, and south London green corridors. Sights: [Wandle Trail](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk/projects/wandle-trail), [Sutton Ecology Centre](https://www.sutton.gov.uk/w/biodiversity-and-wildlife-sutton-ecology-centre), [Honeywood Museum](https://friendsofhoneywood.co.uk/index.html), [Carshalton Water Tower](https://carshaltonwatertower.co.uk/index.html), [Morden Hall Park](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/morden-hall-park), Carshalton Ponds, Watermeads Nature Reserve, Ravensbury Park, and Wilderness Island. Lunch in Carshalton: the [Greyhound Hotel](https://www.thegreyhoundhotel.com), the [Hope](https://hopecarshalton.co.uk), or the [Sun](https://www.thesuncarshalton.com/food). 7 km.

CSS.name: Chessington South
CSS.adminWalksAll: **[Chessington South to Cheam](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chessington-to-cheam/)**: Flat riverside paths, open meadows, farmland, ancient parkland, and wooded country park. Sights: [Hogsmill Local Nature Reserve](https://www.epsom-ewell.gov.uk/sites/default/files/documents/residents/venues-open-space-hire/parks-playgrounds/local-nature-reserves/Hogsmill%20Local%20Nature%20Reserve%20information%20June%202016_0.pdf), [Warren Farm](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/warren-farm/), [Nonsuch Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nonsuch_Park), [St. Mary The Virgin](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1277324), [Nonsuch Palace Gallery](http://www.friendsofnonsuch.co.uk/museum-and-gallery.html), [Service Wing Museum](http://www.friendsofnonsuch.co.uk/museum-and-gallery.html), [Whitehall](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/), [St Dunstan's neo-Gothic lychgate](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1065676), [Lumley Chapel](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/index.php/136-2/), Nonsuch Mansion House, Packhorse Bridge, and Ewell Spring. Lunch in Chessington, or Ewell. Chessington lunch stop: the [William Bourne](https://https://www.greatukpubs.co.uk/william-bourne-chessington). Ewell lunch stops: the [Spring Tavern](https://www.springtavern.co.uk/), the [Famous Green Man](https://www.famousgreenman.com/), the [Ewell Tap & Bottle Shop](https://www.ewelltap.co.uk), the [Coffee Shop](http://www.bournehall.org/visit-us/coffee-shop), the [All Things Nice](https://www.allthingsnice-ewell.co.uk/index.html), the [Sonder Bakery](https://www.sonderbakery.com/), or the [Mandalina Patisserie](http://https://www.mandalinapatisserie.com/). 14 km.
CSS.publicWalksS2S: **[Chessington South to Cheam](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chessington-to-cheam/)**: Flat riverside paths, open meadows, farmland, ancient parkland, and wooded country park. Sights: [Hogsmill Local Nature Reserve](https://www.epsom-ewell.gov.uk/sites/default/files/documents/residents/venues-open-space-hire/parks-playgrounds/local-nature-reserves/Hogsmill%20Local%20Nature%20Reserve%20information%20June%202016_0.pdf), [Warren Farm](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/warren-farm/), [Nonsuch Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nonsuch_Park), [St. Mary The Virgin](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1277324), [Nonsuch Palace Gallery](http://www.friendsofnonsuch.co.uk/museum-and-gallery.html), [Service Wing Museum](http://www.friendsofnonsuch.co.uk/museum-and-gallery.html), [Whitehall](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/), [St Dunstan's neo-Gothic lychgate](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1065676), [Lumley Chapel](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/index.php/136-2/), Nonsuch Mansion House, Packhorse Bridge, and Ewell Spring. CUX.name: Cuxton
CUX.adminWalksAll:
**[Cuxton to Sole Street via Cobham Church](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cuxton-to-sole-street/)**: Open downland views, wooded ridge, bluebell woods, organic vineyard, scenic ridges, and village churchyards. Sights: Cobham Church and Leather Bottle pub. Some strenuous climbs. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Luddesdown, or Cobham. Luddesdown lunch stop: the Golden Lion. Cobham lunch stop: the Leather Bottle. 17 km.

**Cuxton to Sole Street**: A shorter variant of [Cuxton to Sole Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cuxton-to-sole-street/). Open downland views, wooded ridge, bluebell woods, and organic vineyard. Some strenuous climbs. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Luddesdown, or Sole Street. Luddesdown lunch stop: the Golden Lion. Sole Street lunch stop: the Railway Inn. 11 km.
CUX.publicWalksS2S: **[Cuxton to Sole Street via Cobham Church](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cuxton-to-sole-street/)**: Open downland views, wooded ridge, bluebell woods, organic vineyard, scenic ridges, and village churchyards. Sights: Cobham Church and Leather Bottle pub. Some strenuous climbs. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Luddesdown, or Cobham. Luddesdown lunch stop: the Golden Lion. Cobham lunch stop: the Leather Bottle. 17 km.

CWAR.name: Warwick
CWAR.adminWalksAll:
**[Warwick Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-683)**: Historic market town, River Avon, Grand Union Canal towpath, open fields, racecourse, golf course, and woodland. Sights: [Warwick Castle](https://www.warwick-castle.com), [Collegiate Church of St Mary](https://www.stmaryswarwick.org.uk), Lord Leycester Hospital, [Market Hall Museum](https://www.visitwarwick.co.uk/market-hall-museum/), [Guy's Cliffe Estate](https://www.guyscliffewalledgarden.org.uk), and [Mill Garden](https://www.visitwarwick.co.uk/the-mill-garden/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Warwick, Guy's Cliffe, The Cape, or Smith Street, Warwick. Warwick lunch stop: the [Pasteleria Portuguesa](https://www.pastelariaportugesa.co.uk). Guy's Cliffe lunch stop: the [Saxon Mill](https://www.saxonmill.co.uk). The Cape lunch stop: the Cape of Good Hope. Smith Street, Warwick lunch stops: the Smithery, or the Golden Tea Monkey. 12 km. 4 hours.

**[Warwick Parkway Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-826)**: Rolling farmland, Grand Union Canal towpath, woodland, and farm tracks. Sights: [Hatton Locks](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Hatton Country World](https://www.hattonworld.com), [St Michael's, Budbrooke](https://www.stmichaels-budbrooke.org.uk), and [Church Farm Brewery](https://www.churchfarmbrewery.co.uk). Busy A road crossing. Lunch in Hatton: the [Hatton Arms](https://www.hattonarms.com), or the [Hatton Locks Cafe](https://www.facebook.com/Hatton-Locks-287533011322479). 8 km. 2.5 hours.

CWAT.name: Watford
CWAT.adminWalksAll:
**[Watford Junction to St Albans Abbey](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/)**: Way-marked trail through the Ver and Colne Valleys and linking all seven Abbey Line stations — can be done end-to-end or as individual sections with a train hop back. Sights: Sopwell Nunnery and St Albans Abbey. Sections can become waterlogged after heavy rain, sometimes without obvious alternative routes. 14 km.

**Watford North to Watford Junction**: An alternative variant of [Abbey Trail](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/). 2 km.

**Garston to Watford North**: An alternative variant of [Abbey Trail](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/). 4 km.
CWAT.publicWalksS2S: **[Watford Junction to St Albans Abbey](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/)**: Way-marked trail through the Ver and Colne Valleys and linking all seven Abbey Line stations — can be done end-to-end or as individual sections with a train hop back. Sights: Sopwell Nunnery and St Albans Abbey. Sections can become waterlogged after heavy rain, sometimes without obvious alternative routes. 14 km.

CWC.name: Chappel and Wakes Colne
CWC.adminWalksAll: **Chappel and Wakes Colne to Bures**: Undulating hills, brooks, farms, mixed woodland, fishing lakes, thatched cottages and barns, river valley, and golf course diversion. Sights: East Anglian Railway Museum, Chappel Viaduct, Chalkney Wood, Colne Priory, and St Andrew's Church. Can be very muddy. Lunch in Colne Engaine: the Five Bells. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 17 km. 5.5 hours.
CWC.publicWalksS2S: **Chappel and Wakes Colne to Bures**: Undulating hills, brooks, farms, mixed woodland, fishing lakes, thatched cottages and barns, river valley, and golf course diversion. Sights: East Anglian Railway Museum, Chappel Viaduct, Chalkney Wood, Colne Priory, and St Andrew's Church. Can be very muddy. Lunch in Colne Engaine: the Five Bells. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 17 km. 5.5 hours.

CWEL.name: Welwyn
CWEL.adminWalksAll:
**[Welwyn Garden City Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/welwyn-circular/)**: Rolling Hertfordshire countryside, landscaped parkland, arable fields, picturesque village, and Gothic church ruin. Sights: [Shaw's Corner](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shaw%27s_Corner), Old St. Lawrence's Church, Brocket Hall, and Lamer House. Lunch in Ayot St. Lawrence, or Wheathampstead. Ayot St. Lawrence lunch stop: the [Brocket Arms](http://www.brocketarms.com/). Wheathampstead lunch stops: the Bull, or the [Swan](http://theswanwheathampstead.co.uk/). 18 km.

**[Knebworth to Welwyn Garden City via Brocket Hall](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knebworth-to-welwyn-garden-city/)**: Gently undulating woodland, open fields, deer park, golf course, nature reserve, and formal garden city streets. Sights: Knebworth House, St Mary and Thomas of Canterbury Church, Ayot St Lawrence Palladian Church, Old St Lawrence Church, Brocket Hall, Shaw's Corner, and Sherrardspark Wood. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Ayot St Lawrence: the Brocket Arms. 21 km.

**Knebworth to Welwyn Garden City via Ayot Greenway**: A shorter variant of [Knebworth to Welwyn Garden City](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knebworth-to-welwyn-garden-city/). Gently undulating woodland, open fields, deer park, and disused railway trackbed through tree-lined cutting. Sights: Knebworth House, St Mary and Thomas of Canterbury Church, Ayot St Lawrence Palladian Church, Old St Lawrence Church, Shaw's Corner, and Ayot Greenway. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Ayot St Lawrence: the Brocket Arms. 19 km.
CWEL.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Knebworth to Welwyn Garden City via Brocket Hall](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knebworth-to-welwyn-garden-city/)**: Gently undulating woodland, open fields, deer park, golf course, nature reserve, and formal garden city streets. Sights: Knebworth House, St Mary and Thomas of Canterbury Church, Ayot St Lawrence Palladian Church, Old St Lawrence Church, Brocket Hall, Shaw's Corner, and Sherrardspark Wood. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Ayot St Lawrence: the Brocket Arms. 21 km.

CWHY.name: Whyteleafe
CWHY.adminWalksAll:
**[Whyteleafe Circular via Ss Peter and Paul, Chaldon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whyteleafe-circular/)**: Chalk downland, commons, airfield perimeter, wooded valleys, meadows, and restored grassland on the London/Surrey border. Sights: [Kenley Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/kenley-common), [Kenley Airfield](https://www.kenleyrevival.org/content/category/visiting), [Coulsdon Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/coulsdon-common), [Ss Peter and Paul, Chaldon](https://www.exploringsurreyspast.org.uk/themes/places/surrey/tandridge/chaldon/chaldon_church_mural), [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), RAF Kenley Tribute, Happy Valley, Burial Ground, and Riddlesdown Quarry. Can be muddy. Lunch in Coulsdon Common, or Caterham Barracks. Coulsdon Common lunch stop: the [Fox](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thefoxcoulsdoncommon#). Caterham Barracks lunch stop: the [Boundary on the Green](https://www.boundaryonthegreenpub.co.uk). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Whyteleafe Circular via Kenley Common**: A shorter variant of [Whyteleafe Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whyteleafe-circular/). Chalk downland, commons, airfield perimeter, wooded valleys, and restored grassland; omits Happy Valley loop. Sights: [Kenley Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/kenley-common), [Kenley Airfield](https://www.kenleyrevival.org/content/category/visiting), [Coulsdon Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/coulsdon-common), [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), RAF Kenley Tribute, and Riddlesdown Quarry. Can be muddy. Lunch in Coulsdon Common, or Caterham Barracks. Coulsdon Common lunch stop: the [Fox](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thefoxcoulsdoncommon#). Caterham Barracks lunch stop: the [Boundary on the Green](https://www.boundaryonthegreenpub.co.uk). 12 km. 3 hours.

**[Whyteleafe to Hayes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whyteleafe-to-hayes/)**: Gently rolling countryside, shady footpaths and bridleways, open valleys, woods, chalk downland, and quiet rural hamlets near the London/Surrey border. Sights: [Biggin Hill Airport](https://www.military-airshows.co.uk/ven10.htm), Hutchinson's Bank Local Nature Reserve, Greatpark Clock Tower, Coal Tax posts, Hayes Common, London Outer Orbital Path, Leaves Green Circular Walk, and Nash Circular Walk. Can be muddy. Lunch in Fickleshole, or Great Farleigh Green. Fickleshole lunch stop: the [White Bear](https://www.whitebearwarlingham.co.uk). Great Farleigh Green lunch stop: the [Harrow](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/theharrowwarlingham). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Coulsdon South to Whyteleafe**: An alternative variant of [Whyteleafe Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whyteleafe-circular/). Chalk downland, commons, wooded valleys, Happy Valley, airfield perimeter, and restored grassland. Sights: [Farthing Downs](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/farthing-downs), [Kenley Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/kenley-common), [Kenley Airfield](https://www.kenleyrevival.org/content/category/visiting), [Coulsdon Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/coulsdon-common), [Ss Peter and Paul, Chaldon](https://www.exploringsurreyspast.org.uk/themes/places/surrey/tandridge/chaldon/chaldon_church_mural), [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), Happy Valley, Burial Ground, and Riddlesdown Quarry. Can be muddy. Lunch in Coulsdon Common, or Caterham Barracks. Coulsdon Common lunch stop: the [Fox](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thefoxcoulsdoncommon#). Caterham Barracks lunch stop: the [Boundary on the Green](https://www.boundaryonthegreenpub.co.uk). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

Sights: [Biggin Hill Airport](https://www.military-airshows.co.uk/ven10.htm), Hutchinson's Bank Local Nature Reserve, Greatpark Clock Tower, Coal Tax posts, Hayes Common, London Outer Orbital Path, Leaves Green Circular Walk, and Nash Circular Walk.
