Caterham Barracks lunch stop: the [Boundary on the Green](https://www.boundaryonthegreenpub.co.uk). 16 km. 4 hours.

CWIM.name: Wimbledon
CWIM.adminWalksAll:
**[Wimbledon Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-circular/)**: Urban streets, open common, wooded paths, golf fairways, parkland, and quiet roads. Sights: Wimbledon Common, Cannizaro Park, Wimbledon Park, All England Lawn Tennis Club, and Queen's Mere. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Wimbledon Common: the Windmill Cafe. 10 km. 2.5 hours.

**[Wimbledon to Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-putney/)**: Suburban streets, open common, landscaped park, woodland riverside path, Royal Park grassland, suburban roads, and riverside Thames path. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](https://cannizaropark.com/about-cannizaro-park), [Wimbledon Common](https://www.wpcc.org.uk), [Richmond Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/visit/parks/richmond-park), [Barnes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barnes,_London), [Barnes Common](https://barnescommon.org.uk), [London Wetland Centre](https://www.wwt.org.uk/wetland-centres/london), [Oxford and Cambridge Boat Race](https://www.theboatrace.org/the-course), [Beverley Brook Walk](https://www.merton.gov.uk/system/files?file=beverley_brook_walk_web.pdf), Millennium Fountain, Cannizaro Park Aviary, Sunken Garden, Mediterranean Garden, Italian Garden, Rose Garden, Diana and the Fawn statue, Caesar's Camp Iron Age hill fort, Barnes Common Old Cemetery, Craven Cottage, St Mary's Church Putney, All Saints Church Putney, and Gustav Holst blue plaque. Can be muddy. Lunch in Richmond Park, or Barnes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Roehampton Gate café. Barnes lunch stops: the [Crossing](https://www.thecrossing-barnes.co.uk), the [Ye White Hart](https://www.whitehartbarnes.co.uk), the [Watermans Arms](https://www.watermansarms.co.uk), the [Bull's Head](https://www.thebullsheadbarnes.com), the [Coach & Horses](https://www.coachandhorsesbarnes.co.uk), or the [Sun Inn](https://www.thesuninnbarnes.co.uk). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Wimbledon to Kingston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-kingston/)**: Common heathland, woodland, royal deer park, formal gardens, Thames riverside path, and open grassland. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](http://www.cannizaropark.org.uk/index.htm), [Cannizaro House](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cannizaro_Park), [Richmond Park](http://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond_park/), [Southside House](http://www.southsidehouse.com/), [Caesar's Camp](http://www.themodernantiquarian.com/site/2393/caesars_camp_wimbledon.html), Isabella Plantation, Pembroke Lodge, King Henry VIII Mound, Ham House, Teddington Lock, and Hampton Court Palace. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Wimbledon Common, Richmond Park, or Richmond Hill. Wimbledon Common lunch stop: the Fox and Grapes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Pembroke Lodge. Richmond Hill lunch stop: the Roebuck. 18 km.

**Wimbledon to Barnes Bridge**: A shorter variant of [Wimbledon to Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-putney/). Suburban streets, open common, landscaped park, woodland riverside path, Royal Park grassland, suburban roads, and Thames riverbank. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](https://cannizaropark.com/about-cannizaro-park), [Wimbledon Common](https://www.wpcc.org.uk), [Richmond Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/visit/parks/richmond-park), Millennium Fountain, Caesar's Camp Iron Age hill fort, Barnes Railway Bridge, and Ye White Hart. Can be muddy. Lunch in Richmond Park, or Barnes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Roehampton Gate café. Barnes lunch stops: the [Crossing](https://www.thecrossing-barnes.co.uk), the [Ye White Hart](https://www.whitehartbarnes.co.uk), the [Watermans Arms](https://www.watermansarms.co.uk), the [Bull's Head](https://www.thebullsheadbarnes.com), the [Coach & Horses](https://www.coachandhorsesbarnes.co.uk), or the [Sun Inn](https://www.thesuninnbarnes.co.uk). 11 km. 2.5 hours.

**Wimbledon to Twickenham**: An alternative variant of [Wimbledon to Kingston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-kingston/). Common heathland, woodland, royal deer park, Thames riverside path, Palladian riverside park, and Italianate gardens. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](http://www.cannizaropark.org.uk/index.htm), [Richmond Park](http://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond_park/), Isabella Plantation, Pembroke Lodge, [Marble Hill House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/marble-hill-house/), [Orleans House Art Gallery](https://www.orleanshousegallery.org/), [York House Riverside Garden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Naked_Ladies), and [Eel Pie Island](http://www.eelpieislandartists.co.uk). Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Wimbledon Common, Richmond Park, or Twickenham riverside. Wimbledon Common lunch stop: the Fox and Grapes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Pembroke Lodge. Twickenham riverside lunch stop: the White Swan.

**Wimbledon to Kew Bridge**: An alternative variant of [Wimbledon to Kingston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-kingston/). Common heathland, woodland, royal deer park, and Thames riverside path northward to Kew. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](http://www.cannizaropark.org.uk/index.htm), [Richmond Park](http://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond_park/), Isabella Plantation, and Pembroke Lodge. Lunch in Wimbledon Common, or Richmond Park. Wimbledon Common lunch stop: the Fox and Grapes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Pembroke Lodge.

**[Wimbledon to Kingston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-kingston/)**: Common heathland, woodland, royal deer park, formal gardens, Thames riverside path, and open grassland. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](http://www.cannizaropark.org.uk/index.htm), [Cannizaro House](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cannizaro_Park), [Richmond Park](http://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond_park/), [Southside House](http://www.southsidehouse.com/), [Caesar's Camp](http://www.themodernantiquarian.com/site/2393/caesars_camp_wimbledon.html), Isabella Plantation, Pembroke Lodge, King Henry VIII Mound, Ham House, Teddington Lock, and Hampton Court Palace. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Wimbledon Common, Richmond Park, or Richmond Hill. Wimbledon Common lunch stop: the Fox and Grapes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Pembroke Lodge. Richmond Hill lunch stop: the Roebuck. 18 km.

CWIN.name: Windsor
CWIN.adminWalksAll:
**Windsor Circular via Windsor Great Park**: *Last reviewed 2 January 2023.* 18 km. 130 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921499158)**

**Sunningdale to Windsor**: Parkland, lake shores, ornamental gardens, light woodland, deer park, tree-lined avenue, and historic town streets. Sights: Holy Trinity Sunningdale Church, Coworth Park, Virginia Water Lake, Valley Gardens, Totem Pole, Obelisk, [Savill Gardens](http://www.windsorgreatpark.co.uk/), Cow Pond, Royal Lodge, Copper Horse, Long Walk, Windsor Castle, and Guildhall. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Savill Gardens: the Savill Garden Restaurant. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 90 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921106856)**

**Windsor and Eton Riverside to Egham**: An alternative variant of [Slough to Egham via Windsor Great Park](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/slough-to-egham-via-windsor-great-park/). Flat parkland, deer park, historic royal landscape, ornamental gardens, and lake. Sights: Windsor Castle, Savill Gardens, Valley Gardens, Virginia Water lake, and [The Royal Landscape](http://www.theroyallandscape.co.uk/visiting/).

**Sunningdale to Windsor and Eton Central**: Parkland, lake shores, ornamental gardens, light woodland, deer park, tree-lined avenue, and historic town streets. Sights: Holy Trinity Sunningdale Church, Coworth Park, Virginia Water Lake, Valley Gardens, Totem Pole, Obelisk, [Savill Gardens](http://www.windsorgreatpark.co.uk/), Cow Pond, Royal Lodge, Copper Horse, Long Walk, Windsor Castle, and Guildhall. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Savill Gardens: the Savill Garden Restaurant. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 4 hours.
CWIN.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Sunningdale to Windsor**: Parkland, lake shores, ornamental gardens, light woodland, deer park, tree-lined avenue, and historic town streets. Sights: Holy Trinity Sunningdale Church, Coworth Park, Virginia Water Lake, Valley Gardens, Totem Pole, Obelisk, [Savill Gardens](http://www.windsorgreatpark.co.uk/), Cow Pond, Royal Lodge, Copper Horse, Long Walk, Windsor Castle, and Guildhall. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Savill Gardens: the Savill Garden Restaurant. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 90 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921106856)**
CWIN.publicWalksCircular: **Windsor Circular via Windsor Great Park**: 18 km. 130 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921499158)**

CWL.name: Colwall
CWL.adminWalksAll: **Colwall to Great Malvern**: An alternative variant of [Malvern Hills from Great Malvern or Colwall](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/malvern-hills-from-great-malvern-or-colwall/). Fields, oak woods at westerly base of hills, ridge tops, open commons, and scrub and bracken slopes. Sights: Herefordshire Beacon, British Camp, Worcestershire Beacon, [St. Ann's Well](http://stannswell.co.uk/), and Great Malvern Priory. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Malvern: the [Malvern Hills Hotel & Restaurants](http://malvernhillshotel.co.uk/), the Sally's Place, the [Wyche Inn](http://www.thewycheinn.co.uk/), or the [Café H2O at the Malvern Hills GeoCentre](https://www.wyche-innovation.com/cafe-h2o). 18 km. 5.5 hours.
CWL.publicWalksS2S: **Colwall to Great Malvern**: An alternative variant of [Malvern Hills from Great Malvern or Colwall](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/malvern-hills-from-great-malvern-or-colwall/). Fields, oak woods at westerly base of hills, ridge tops, open commons, and scrub and bracken slopes. Sights: Herefordshire Beacon, British Camp, Worcestershire Beacon, [St. Ann's Well](http://stannswell.co.uk/), and Great Malvern Priory. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Malvern: the [Malvern Hills Hotel & Restaurants](http://malvernhillshotel.co.uk/), the Sally's Place, the [Wyche Inn](http://www.thewycheinn.co.uk/), or the [Café H2O at the Malvern Hills GeoCentre](https://www.wyche-innovation.com/cafe-h2o). 18 km. 5.5 hours.

CWN.name: Cowden
CWN.adminWalksAll:
**Cowden Circular**: A longer variant of [Cowden to Hever](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cowden-to-hever/). Remote wooded valleys, sandstone outcrops, undulating High Weald farmland, vineyard, Eden Valley meadows, quaint village streets, historic castle grounds, and views from Markbeech. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), [Hever Castle](https://www.hevercastle.co.uk), St John the Baptist, Penshurst, St Mary, Chiddingstone, St Peter, Hever, Chiding Stone, Leicester Square, Harden Vineyard, Holy Trinity Church, and Hole Cottage. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst, or Hoath Corner. Penshurst lunch stops: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). Hoath Corner lunch stop: the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com). 19 km. 5 hours.

**[Cowden to Hever](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cowden-to-hever/)**: Remote wooded valleys, sandstone outcrops, undulating High Weald farmland, vineyard, Eden Valley meadows, quaint village streets, and historic castle grounds. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), [Hever Castle](https://www.hevercastle.co.uk), St John the Baptist, Penshurst, St Mary, Chiddingstone, St Peter, Hever, Chiding Stone, Leicester Square, and Harden Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst, or Hoath Corner. Penshurst lunch stops: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). Hoath Corner lunch stop: the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com). 16 km. 4 hours.

**[Cowden to Eridge via Pooh Corner](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cowden-to-eridge/)**: Weald pasture, woods, hills, undulating fields, and valley paths. Sights: Pooh Corner, Old Buckhurst, and Church of St Michael and All Angels. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hartfield, or Withyham. Hartfield lunch stop: the [Bear Inn](https://www.thebearhartfield.com/). Withyham lunch stop: the [Dorset Arms](https://www.dorset-arms.co.uk/). 21 km. 6 hours.

**Cowden to Dormans**: A shorter variant of [Hever to Dormans](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-dormans/). Undulating countryside, woods, farmland, fields, streams, historic manor houses, iron-age hillfort, remote border area of Kent, and Surrey and Sussex. Sights: Cowden, St Mary Magdalene Church, Sussex Border Path, Waystrode Manor, Crippenden Manor, Vanguard Way, Dry Hill Fort, and Greathed Manor. Can be muddy. Lunch in Cowden: the [Fountain](https://www.thefountaininncowden.com/). 13 km.

**Cowden to Ashurst**: A shorter variant of [Hever to Ashurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-ashurst/). Low hills, wooded valleys, farm fields, bluebell woods, manor house grounds, golf course, orchards, and remote High Weald countryside. Sights: Waystrode Manor, Sussex Border Path, Sussex House Farm, Heathersome's Wood, Bolebroke Castle, [Orchard Tea Room](https://www.perryhillorchards.co.uk/orchard-tea-room), St Mary Magdalene, and [Cowden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cowden). Can be muddy. Lunch in Cowden: the [Fountain](https://www.thefountaincowden.co.uk). 12 km. 3 hours.

**Hever to Cowden**: A shorter variant of [Hever to Ashurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-ashurst/). Low hills, wooded valleys, farm fields, manor house grounds, and remote High Weald countryside. Sights: Crippenden Manor, Leighton Manor, Waystrode Manor, Sussex Border Path, St Mary Magdalene, and [Cowden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cowden). Can be muddy. Lunch in Cowden: the [Fountain](https://www.thefountaincowden.co.uk). 11 km. 2.5 hours.
CWN.publicWalksS2S:
**[Cowden to Hever](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cowden-to-hever/)**: Remote wooded valleys, sandstone outcrops, undulating High Weald farmland, vineyard, Eden Valley meadows, quaint village streets, and historic castle grounds. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), [Hever Castle](https://www.hevercastle.co.uk), St John the Baptist, Penshurst, St Mary, Chiddingstone, St Peter, Hever, Chiding Stone, Leicester Square, and Harden Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst, or Hoath Corner. Hoath Corner lunch stop: the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com). 16 km. 4 hours.

**[Cowden to Eridge via Pooh Corner](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cowden-to-eridge/)**: Weald pasture, woods, hills, undulating fields, and valley paths. Sights: Pooh Corner, Old Buckhurst, and Church of St Michael and All Angels. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hartfield, or Withyham. Hartfield lunch stop: the [Bear Inn](https://www.thebearhartfield.com/). Withyham lunch stop: the [Dorset Arms](https://www.dorset-arms.co.uk/). 21 km. 6 hours.

CWU.name: Crowhurst
CWU.adminWalksAll: **Crowhurst to Battle**: Gentle farmland, woodlands, golf course, parkland, riverside paths alongside streams and the River Brede, and historic town centre. Sights: Parish Church of St George, Crowhurst Manor House, St John the Baptist Church, Pestalozzi International Village, Battle Abbey, Battle of Hastings site, Parish Church of St Mary the Virgin, and Bayeux Cottage. Can be muddy. Lunch in Westfield: the New Inn. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 20 km. 6 hours.
CWU.publicWalksS2S: **Crowhurst to Battle**: Gentle farmland, woodlands, golf course, parkland, riverside paths alongside streams and the River Brede, and historic town centre. Sights: Parish Church of St George, Crowhurst Manor House, St John the Baptist Church, Pestalozzi International Village, Battle Abbey, Battle of Hastings site, Parish Church of St Mary the Virgin, and Bayeux Cottage. Can be muddy. Lunch in Westfield: the New Inn. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 20 km. 6 hours.

CWX.name: Canary Wharf
CWX.adminWalksAll:
**[Blackheath to Canary Wharf via Blackheath](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackheath-to-canary-wharf/)**: Leafy Georgian avenues, open heath, parkland, Royal Observatory hill, riverside Thames Path, Docklands waterfront, and financial district. Sights: [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), [Old Royal Naval College](https://www.ornc.org), [Royal Museums Greenwich](https://www.rmg.co.uk), National Maritime Museum, Queen's House, Royal Observatory, Cutty Sark, [Ranger's House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/rangers-house-the-wernher-collection), Blackheath, Painted Hall, Flamsteed House, Greenwich Meridian, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, [Canary Wharf outdoor public art](https://canarywharf.com/the-estate/art-culture/art-on-the-estate), Crossrail Place Roof Garden, One Tree Hill, SS Great Eastern site, Discover Greenwich Visitor Centre, Millennium Sundial, Vanbrugh Castle, Morden College, The Paragon, Anglo-Saxon tumuli, and Blackheath Halls. Lunch in Greenwich, or Ballast Quay, Greenwich. Greenwich lunch stops: the [Plume of Feathers](https://plumegreenwich.com), or the [Trafalgar Tavern](https://www.trafalgartavern.co.uk). Ballast Quay, Greenwich lunch stop: the [Cutty Sark](https://www.cuttysarkse10.co.uk). 12 km. 2.5 hours.

**Maze Hill to Canary Wharf**: An alternative variant of [Blackheath to Canary Wharf](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackheath-to-canary-wharf/). Greenwich Park, Royal Observatory hill, riverside Thames Path, eastern Isle of Dogs riverside path, and Docklands waterfront. Sights: [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), [Old Royal Naval College](https://www.ornc.org), [Royal Museums Greenwich](https://www.rmg.co.uk), National Maritime Museum, Queen's House, Royal Observatory, Cutty Sark, Painted Hall, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, Crossrail Place Roof Garden, [Canary Wharf outdoor public art](https://canarywharf.com/the-estate/art-culture/art-on-the-estate), Whale on the Wharf sculpture, and [Mudchute Park & Farm](https://www.mudchute.org). Lunch in Greenwich: the [Plume of Feathers](https://plumegreenwich.com). 12 km. 2.5 hours.

**Blackheath to Canary Wharf via Whale on the Wharf sculpture**: An alternative variant of [Blackheath to Canary Wharf](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackheath-to-canary-wharf/). Leafy Georgian avenues, open heath, parkland, Royal Observatory hill, eastern Isle of Dogs riverside path, and Docklands waterfront. Sights: [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), [Old Royal Naval College](https://www.ornc.org), [Royal Museums Greenwich](https://www.rmg.co.uk), National Maritime Museum, Queen's House, Royal Observatory, Cutty Sark, [Ranger's House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/rangers-house-the-wernher-collection), Painted Hall, Flamsteed House, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, Crossrail Place Roof Garden, [Canary Wharf outdoor public art](https://canarywharf.com/the-estate/art-culture/art-on-the-estate), and Whale on the Wharf sculpture. Lunch in Greenwich, or Ballast Quay, Greenwich. Greenwich lunch stops: the [Plume of Feathers](https://plumegreenwich.com), or the [Trafalgar Tavern](https://www.trafalgartavern.co.uk). Ballast Quay, Greenwich lunch stop: the [Cutty Sark](https://www.cuttysarkse10.co.uk). 11 km.

**Blackheath to Canary Wharf via Mudchute Park & Farm**: A longer variant of [Blackheath to Canary Wharf](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackheath-to-canary-wharf/). Leafy Georgian avenues, open heath, parkland, Royal Observatory hill, riverside Thames Path, Docklands waterfront, Mudchute Park & Farm, and Isle of Dogs circuit. Sights: [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), [Old Royal Naval College](https://www.ornc.org), [Royal Museums Greenwich](https://www.rmg.co.uk), National Maritime Museum, Queen's House, Royal Observatory, Cutty Sark, [Ranger's House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/rangers-house-the-wernher-collection), Painted Hall, Flamsteed House, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, [Mudchute Park & Farm](https://www.mudchute.org), SS Great Eastern site, Crossrail Place Roof Garden, [Canary Wharf outdoor public art](https://canarywharf.com/the-estate/art-culture/art-on-the-estate), Millwall Railway Viaduct, and Isle of Dogs Memorial Garden. Lunch in Greenwich, or Ballast Quay, Greenwich. Greenwich lunch stops: the [Plume of Feathers](https://plumegreenwich.com), or the [Trafalgar Tavern](https://www.trafalgartavern.co.uk). Ballast Quay, Greenwich lunch stop: the [Cutty Sark](https://www.cuttysarkse10.co.uk). 18 km. 4 hours.

Lunch in Greenwich, or Ballast Quay, Greenwich. Greenwich lunch stops: the [Plume of Feathers](https://plumegreenwich.com), or the [Trafalgar Tavern](https://www.trafalgartavern.co.uk). Ballast Quay, Greenwich lunch stop: the [Cutty Sark](https://www.cuttysarkse10.co.uk). 12 km. 2.5 hours.

CYP.name: Crystal Palace
CYP.adminWalksAll:
**[Crystal Palace to Forest Hill via Dulwich Picture Gallery](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/crystal-palace-to-forest-hill/)**: Suburban parks, ancient woodland, leafy avenues, Victorian gardens, and urban pavements. Sights: [Crystal Palace Museum](https://www.crystalpalacemuseum.org.uk), [Crystal Palace Dinosaurs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crystal_Palace_Dinosaurs), [Dulwich Picture Gallery](https://www.dulwichpicturegallery.org.uk), [Dulwich College](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dulwich_College), [Horniman Museum](https://www.horniman.ac.uk), Crystal Palace Park, Sydenham Hill Wood, Cox's Walk Footbridge, Dulwich Park, Old College, Belair House, Horniman Gardens, and Dulwich toll-gate. Lunch in Dulwich Village: the [Crown and Greyhound](https://www.thecrownandgreyhound.co.uk). 13 km. 3.5 hours.

**Crystal Palace to Forest Hill via Crystal Palace Museum**: A shorter variant of [Crystal Palace to Forest Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/crystal-palace-to-forest-hill/). Suburban parks, ancient woodland, Victorian gardens, and urban pavements. Sights: [Crystal Palace Museum](https://www.crystalpalacemuseum.org.uk), [Crystal Palace Dinosaurs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crystal_Palace_Dinosaurs), [Horniman Museum](https://www.horniman.ac.uk), Crystal Palace Park, Sydenham Hill Wood, Cox's Walk Footbridge, and Horniman Gardens. Lunch in Sydenham Hill: the [Wood House](https://www.thewoodhousedulwich.co.uk). 7 km. 2 hours.
CYP.publicWalksS2S: **[Crystal Palace to Forest Hill via Dulwich Picture Gallery](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/crystal-palace-to-forest-hill/)**: Suburban parks, ancient woodland, leafy avenues, Victorian gardens, and urban pavements. Sights: [Crystal Palace Museum](https://www.crystalpalacemuseum.org.uk), [Crystal Palace Dinosaurs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crystal_Palace_Dinosaurs), [Dulwich Picture Gallery](https://www.dulwichpicturegallery.org.uk), [Dulwich College](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dulwich_College), [Horniman Museum](https://www.horniman.ac.uk), Crystal Palace Park, Sydenham Hill Wood, Cox's Walk Footbridge, Dulwich Park, Old College, Belair House, Horniman Gardens, and Dulwich toll-gate. Lunch in Dulwich Village: the [Crown and Greyhound](https://www.thecrownandgreyhound.co.uk). 13 km. 3.5 hours.

DBG.name: Mottisfont & Dunbridge
DBG.adminWalksAll:
**Mottisfont & Dunbridge Circular**: A variant of [Dean to Mottisfont](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dean-to-mottisfont/). Chalk downlands, rolling hills, varied woods, field boundaries, farm tracks, river meadows, and Test Valley chalk stream. Sights: [Mottisfont Abbey & Gardens](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont) and St. Andrews Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in King's Somborne, or Houghton. King's Somborne lunch stop: the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrownks.co.uk/). Houghton lunch stop: the [Boot Inn](http://thebootinn-houghton.co.uk/). 25 km.

**[Mottisfont & Dunbridge to Romsey via War Memorial Park](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mottisfont-to-romsey/)**: Rolling chalk hills, ancient and bluebell woodlands, river meadows, managed farmland, and a picturesque market town. Sights: [Mottisfont Abbey](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont), St. Andrew's Church, Romsey Abbey, King John's House, Tudor Cottage, War Memorial Park, and Sadler's Mill. Can be muddy. Lunch in Mottisfont, Dunbridge, or Kimbridge. Mottisfont lunch stops: the [Old Kitchen Café](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont), or the [Coach House Café](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont). Dunbridge lunch stop: the [Mill Arms](http://www.millarmsdunbridge.co.uk/). Kimbridge lunch stops: the [Kimbridge Tea Rooms and Café](https://www.idealcollection.co.uk/venues/kimbridge-barn/), or the [Bear and Ragged Staff](http://www.bear-and-ragged-staff.co.uk/). 22 km. 5 hours.

**[Dean to Mottisfont & Dunbridge via Mottisfont Abbey & Gardens](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dean-to-mottisfont/)**: Chalk downlands, rolling hills, varied woods, field boundaries, farm tracks, steep-sided chalk downs, river meadows, and Test Valley chalk stream. Sights: [Mottisfont Abbey & Gardens](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont), St. Andrews Church, and Broughton Down Nature Reserve. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Tytherley, Broughton, Houghton, or King's Somborne. West Tytherley lunch stop: the [Black Horse](http://theblackhorsewesttytherley.com/). Broughton lunch stops: the [Greyhound Inn](https://greyhoundbroughton.co.uk/), or the Tally Ho! Inn. Houghton lunch stop: the [Boot Inn](http://thebootinn-houghton.co.uk/). King's Somborne lunch stop: the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrownks.co.uk/). 27 km. 6.5 hours.

**Salisbury to Mottisfont & Dunbridge**: An alternative variant of [Dean to Mottisfont](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dean-to-mottisfont/). Chalk downlands, rolling hills, varied woods, field boundaries, farm tracks, steep-sided chalk downs, river meadows, Test Valley chalk stream, and sculpture park. Sights: Clarendon Palace, [New Art Centre at Roche Court](http://sculpture.uk.com/), [Mottisfont Abbey & Gardens](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont), and St. Andrews Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Pitton, or Winterslow. Pitton lunch stop: the [Silver Plough](http://silverplough-pitton.co.uk/). Winterslow lunch stop: the [Lord Nelson](http://www.the-lord-nelson.co.uk/). 37 km.

Sights: [Mottisfont Abbey](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont), St. Andrew's Church, Romsey Abbey, King John's House, Tudor Cottage, War Memorial Park, and Sadler's Mill. Can be muddy. Lunch in Mottisfont, Dunbridge, or Kimbridge. Mottisfont lunch stops: the [Old Kitchen Café](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont), or the [Coach House Café](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont). Dunbridge lunch stop: the [Mill Arms](http://www.millarmsdunbridge.co.uk/). Kimbridge lunch stops: the [Kimbridge Tea Rooms and Café](https://www.idealcollection.co.uk/venues/kimbridge-barn/), or the [Bear and Ragged Staff](http://www.bear-and-ragged-staff.co.uk/). 22 km. 5 hours.

Broughton lunch stops: the [Greyhound Inn](https://greyhoundbroughton.co.uk/), or the Tally Ho! Inn. Houghton lunch stop: the [Boot Inn](http://thebootinn-houghton.co.uk/). DBY.name: Derby
DBY.adminWalksAll:
**[Derby Circular via Darley Abbey](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Parks, river, and heritage trail. Sights: Derby Cathedral, Markeaton Park, Allestree Park, Darley Abbey, Bonnie Prince Charlie Walk, Big Wood, and River Derwent Heritage Trail. 18 km. 140 m uphill. 5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/315%20Derby%20City%20Circular.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925786424)**

**[Derby to Long Eaton along waterways](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Waterways — River Derwent, Derby & Sandiacre Canal, and Erewash Canal. Sights: River Derwent, Derby & Sandiacre Canal, and Erewash Canal. 18 km. 10 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/210%20Derby%20to%20Long%20Eaton.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925784648)**

**[Duffield to Derby via Drum Hill](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Repurposed railway track along the Great Northern Greenway. Sights: Great Northern Greenway, Horsley Carr, and Morley. 16 km. 160 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/426%20Duffield%20to%20Derby.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925790185)**

DCH.name: Dorchester South
DCH.publicNote: **[Dorchester to Abbotsbury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/osmington-or-dorchester-to-abbotsbury/)**: a linear walk from Dorchester South to Abbotsbury (bus return). Part of a longer Osmington-to-Abbotsbury walk.
DCH.adminWalksAll:
**[Dorchester South Circular via Bronkham Hill barrows](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorchester-circular-or-to-portesham/)**: Prehistoric downland, South Winterbourne Valley, heathery hilltops, barrow-studded ridgeway, and sweeping views to Jurassic Coast. Sights: [Maumbury Rings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maumbury_Rings), [Maiden Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/maiden-castle/), [Hardy Monument](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/hardy-monument), and Bronkham Hill barrows. Lunch in Martinstown: the [Brewer's Arms](http://www.thebrewersarms.com/). 24 km. 6 hours.

**Dorchester South Circular via Valley of (Sarsen) Stones**: A longer variant of [Dorchester Circular or to Portesham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorchester-circular-or-to-portesham/). Prehistoric downland, Valley of Stones boulder train, stone circles, barrows, Bride Valley, and heathery hilltops. Sights: [Maumbury Rings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maumbury_Rings), [Maiden Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/maiden-castle/), [Hardy Monument](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/hardy-monument), Valley of (Sarsen) Stones, Hell Stone Burial Chamber, and Bridehead Lake. Lunch in Martinstown: the [Brewer's Arms](http://www.thebrewersarms.com/). 37 km. 9 hours.

**Maiden Newton to Dorchester South**: An alternative variant of [Maiden Newton Circular or to Dorchester](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/maiden-newton-circular-or-to-dorchester/). Chalk downland, Cerne Valley, historic Dorchester city centre, and two modest climbs with fine views. Sights: The Cerne Giant, Cerne Abbey, Hog Cliff National Nature Reserve, and Giant Hill. Hilly ascents. Lunch in Sydling St. Nicholas, or Cerne Abbas. Sydling St. Nicholas lunch stop: the [Greyhound Inn](https://dorsetgreyhound.co.uk). Cerne Abbas lunch stops: the [Royal Oak](https://theroyaloakcerneabbas.co.uk), the [Giant Inn (aka The Red Lion)](https://www.thegiantcerneabbas.co.uk), the [New Inn](https://www.thenewinncerneabbas.co.uk/index), or the [Abbots Tearoom](https://www.abbotsbedandbreakfast.co.uk).
DCH.publicWalksCircular: **[Dorchester South Circular via Bronkham Hill barrows](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorchester-circular-or-to-portesham/)**: Prehistoric downland, South Winterbourne Valley, heathery hilltops, barrow-studded ridgeway, and sweeping views to Jurassic Coast. Sights: [Maumbury Rings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maumbury_Rings), [Maiden Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/maiden-castle/), [Hardy Monument](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/hardy-monument), and Bronkham Hill barrows. Lunch in Martinstown: the [Brewer's Arms](http://www.thebrewersarms.com/). 24 km. 6 hours.

DCSM.name: Cutty Sark for Maritime Greenwich
DCSM.adminWalksAll:
**[Westminster to Cutty Sark for Maritime Greenwich](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-bridge-to-greenwich-via-the-south-bank/)**: Flat riverside pavement, historic South Bank, converted Victorian wharves, residential riverside developments, industrial heritage, and pocket parks. Sights: London Eye, Southbank Centre, National Theatre, Tate Modern, Millennium Bridge, Globe Theatre, Southwark Cathedral, Golden Hinde, Hays Galleria, [HMS Belfast](http://www.iwm.org.uk/visits/hms-belfast), City Hall, [Tower Bridge](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tower_Bridge), Design Museum, [Brunel Museum](http://www.brunel-museum.org.uk/), Surrey Docks Farm, Statue of Peter the Great, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, [Cutty Sark](http://www.rmg.co.uk/cuttysark), [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), Royal Observatory, Winchester Palace, and Tower of London. Lunch in Bermondsey, or Rotherhithe. Bermondsey lunch stop: the Angel Pub. Rotherhithe lunch stops: the [Mayflower pub](http://www.mayflowerpub.co.uk/), or the Salt Quay Pub.

**London Bridge to Cutty Sark for Maritime Greenwich**: A shorter variant of [Westminster to Greenwich via the south bank](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-bridge-to-greenwich-via-the-south-bank/). Flat riverside pavement, converted Victorian wharves, residential riverside developments, industrial heritage, and pocket parks. Sights: [HMS Belfast](http://www.iwm.org.uk/visits/hms-belfast), City Hall, [Tower Bridge](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tower_Bridge), Design Museum, [Brunel Museum](http://www.brunel-museum.org.uk/), Surrey Docks Farm, Statue of Peter the Great, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, [Cutty Sark](http://www.rmg.co.uk/cuttysark), [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), and Royal Observatory. Lunch in Bermondsey, or Rotherhithe. Bermondsey lunch stop: the Angel Pub. Rotherhithe lunch stops: the [Mayflower pub](http://www.mayflowerpub.co.uk/), or the Salt Quay Pub.
DCSM.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Westminster to Cutty Sark for Maritime Greenwich](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-bridge-to-greenwich-via-the-south-bank/)**: Flat riverside pavement, historic South Bank, converted Victorian wharves, residential riverside developments, industrial heritage, and pocket parks. Sights: London Eye, Southbank Centre, National Theatre, Tate Modern, Millennium Bridge, Globe Theatre, Southwark Cathedral, Golden Hinde, Hays Galleria, [HMS Belfast](http://www.iwm.org.uk/visits/hms-belfast), City Hall, [Tower Bridge](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tower_Bridge), Design Museum, [Brunel Museum](http://www.brunel-museum.org.uk/), Surrey Docks Farm, Statue of Peter the Great, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, [Cutty Sark](http://www.rmg.co.uk/cuttysark), [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), Royal Observatory, Winchester Palace, and Tower of London. Lunch in Bermondsey, or Rotherhithe. Bermondsey lunch stop: the Angel Pub. Rotherhithe lunch stops: the [Mayflower pub](http://www.mayflowerpub.co.uk/), or the Salt Quay Pub.

DDG.name: Dorridge
DDG.adminWalksAll: **[Dorridge Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-885)**: Wealthy suburbia, country lanes, Grand Union Canal towpath, and open fields. Sights: [Brook Meadow](https://www.warwickshirewildlifetrust.org.uk), [Grand Union Canal](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Baddesley Clinton](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/baddesley-clinton), [Dorridge Wood](https://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/), Packwood House, and Kingswood Junction. Can be muddy. Lunch in Dorridge, Knowle, or Lapworth. Dorridge lunch stops: the Espresso, or the Skogen. Knowle lunch stop: the [Rigby's Black Boy](https://www.theblackboyknowle.co.uk). Lapworth lunch stop: the [Navigation Inn](https://www.navigationlapworth.co.uk). 7 km. 2.5 hours.
DDG.publicWalksCircular: **[Dorridge Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-885)**: Wealthy suburbia, country lanes, Grand Union Canal towpath, and open fields. Sights: [Brook Meadow](https://www.warwickshirewildlifetrust.org.uk), [Grand Union Canal](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Baddesley Clinton](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/baddesley-clinton), [Dorridge Wood](https://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/), Packwood House, and Kingswood Junction. Can be muddy. Lunch in Dorridge, Knowle, or Lapworth. Dorridge lunch stops: the Espresso, or the Skogen. Knowle lunch stop: the [Rigby's Black Boy](https://www.theblackboyknowle.co.uk). Lapworth lunch stop: the [Navigation Inn](https://www.navigationlapworth.co.uk). 7 km. 2.5 hours.

DEA.name: Deal
DEA.adminWalksAll:
**Deal to Dover Priory**: Rambler favourite! Pebble beach coastpath, clifftop chalk paths, dramatic cliffs near Dover, and seaside town finish. Sights: [Deal Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/), [Walmer Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/), South Foreland Lighthouse, and Dover Castle. Mobile may switch networks. Best in May–Jun. Lunch in Kingsdown, or St Margaret's Bay. Kingsdown lunch stop: the Zetland Arms. St Margaret's Bay lunch stop: the Coastguard. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 17 km.

**Dover Priory to Deal**: Rambler favourite! Clifftop chalk paths, two significant climbs, level pebble beach coastpath, open sea views, and historic town. Sights: Dover Castle, South Foreland Lighthouse, [Walmer Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/), and [Deal Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/). Mobile may switch networks. Best in May–Jun. Lunch in Dover, South Foreland Lighthouse, St Margaret's Bay, Kingsdown, or Deal. Dover lunch stop: the White Cliffs Visitor Centre. South Foreland Lighthouse lunch stop: the Mrs Knott's Tea Room. St Margaret's Bay lunch stops: the Pines Garden Tea Room and Museum, or the Coastguard. Kingsdown lunch stop: the Zetland Arms. Deal lunch stops: the Deal Pier Kitchen, the Port Arms, the Kings Arm, or the Ship Inn. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 17 km.
DEA.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Dover Priory to Deal**: Rambler favourite! Clifftop chalk paths, two significant climbs, level pebble beach coastpath, open sea views, and historic town. Sights: Dover Castle, South Foreland Lighthouse, [Walmer Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/), and [Deal Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/). Mobile may switch networks. Best in May–Jun. Lunch in Dover, South Foreland Lighthouse, St Margaret's Bay, Kingsdown, or Deal. Dover lunch stop: the White Cliffs Visitor Centre. South Foreland Lighthouse lunch stop: the Mrs Knott's Tea Room. St Margaret's Bay lunch stops: the Pines Garden Tea Room and Museum, or the Coastguard. Kingsdown lunch stop: the Zetland Arms. Deal lunch stops: the Deal Pier Kitchen, the Port Arms, the Kings Arm, or the Ship Inn. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 17 km.

DEN.name: Dean
DEN.adminWalksAll:
**[Dean to Mottisfont & Dunbridge via Mottisfont Abbey & Gardens](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dean-to-mottisfont/)**: Chalk downlands, rolling hills, varied woods, field boundaries, farm tracks, steep-sided chalk downs, river meadows, and Test Valley chalk stream. Sights: [Mottisfont Abbey & Gardens](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont), St. Andrews Church, and Broughton Down Nature Reserve. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Tytherley, Broughton, Houghton, or King's Somborne. West Tytherley lunch stop: the [Black Horse](http://theblackhorsewesttytherley.com/). Broughton lunch stops: the [Greyhound Inn](https://greyhoundbroughton.co.uk/), or the Tally Ho! Inn. Houghton lunch stop: the [Boot Inn](http://thebootinn-houghton.co.uk/). King's Somborne lunch stop: the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrownks.co.uk/). 27 km. 6.5 hours.

**Salisbury to Dean**: An alternative variant of [Dean to Mottisfont](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dean-to-mottisfont/). Chalk downlands, rolling hills, varied woods, field boundaries, farm tracks, and following the Clarendon Way. Sights: Clarendon Palace and [New Art Centre at Roche Court](http://sculpture.uk.com/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Pitton, or Winterslow. Pitton lunch stop: the [Silver Plough](http://silverplough-pitton.co.uk/). Winterslow lunch stop: the [Lord Nelson](http://www.the-lord-nelson.co.uk/). 24 km.
DEN.publicWalksS2S: **[Dean to Mottisfont & Dunbridge via Mottisfont Abbey & Gardens](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dean-to-mottisfont/)**: Chalk downlands, rolling hills, varied woods, field boundaries, farm tracks, steep-sided chalk downs, river meadows, and Test Valley chalk stream. Sights: [Mottisfont Abbey & Gardens](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont), St. Andrews Church, and Broughton Down Nature Reserve. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Tytherley, Broughton, Houghton, or King's Somborne. West Tytherley lunch stop: the [Black Horse](http://theblackhorsewesttytherley.com/). Broughton lunch stops: the [Greyhound Inn](https://greyhoundbroughton.co.uk/), or the Tally Ho! Inn. Houghton lunch stop: the [Boot Inn](http://thebootinn-houghton.co.uk/). King's Somborne lunch stop: the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrownks.co.uk/). 27 km. 6.5 hours.

DEP.name: Deptford
DEP.adminWalksAll: **[Blackheath to Deptford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackheath-to-deptford/)**: Urban London, genteel residential streets, Georgian and Victorian estates, large open heathland, well-kept parks, river valleys, Victorian cemeteries, and green hilltops with panoramic city views. Sights: Blackheath Cator Estate, Morden College, The Paragon, All Saints' Blackheath, Greenwich Park, St. Margaret of Antioch Church, Boone's Almshouses, Lee Manor House, Manor House Gardens, Mountsfield Park, One Tree Hill, St. Augustine's Church, Camberwell New Cemetery, Honor Oak Crematorium, Rivoli Ballroom, Brockley and Ladywell Cemetery, Hilly Fields Park, Millennium Stone Circle, Blythe Hill Fields, Telegraph Hill Upper Park, Telegraph Hill Lower Park, Deptford Market Yard, Albany Theatre, and Ladywell Fields. Lunch in Catford, Hilly Fields, or Honor Oak Park. Catford lunch stop: the Mountsfield Park Café. Hilly Fields lunch stop: the Pistachios in the Park. Honor Oak Park lunch stop: the Blythe Hill Tavern. 22 km. 5 hours.
DEP.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Blackheath to Deptford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackheath-to-deptford/)**: Urban London, genteel residential streets, Georgian and Victorian estates, large open heathland, well-kept parks, river valleys, Victorian cemeteries, and green hilltops with panoramic city views. Sights: Blackheath Cator Estate, Morden College, The Paragon, All Saints' Blackheath, Greenwich Park, St. Margaret of Antioch Church, Boone's Almshouses, Lee Manor House, Manor House Gardens, Mountsfield Park, One Tree Hill, St. Augustine's Church, Camberwell New Cemetery, Honor Oak Crematorium, Rivoli Ballroom, Brockley and Ladywell Cemetery, Hilly Fields Park, Millennium Stone Circle, Blythe Hill Fields, Telegraph Hill Upper Park, Telegraph Hill Lower Park, Deptford Market Yard, Albany Theatre, and Ladywell Fields. Lunch in Catford, Hilly Fields, or Honor Oak Park. Catford lunch stop: the Mountsfield Park Café. Hilly Fields lunch stop: the Pistachios in the Park. Honor Oak Park lunch stop: the Blythe Hill Tavern. 22 km. 5 hours.

DFD.name: Dartford
DFD.adminWalksAll: **[Dartford to Greenhithe](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dartford-to-greenhithe/)**: Flat riverside paths, flood defences, marshland, industrial edge-land, and wide Thames estuary views. Sights: Queen Elizabeth Bridge, Dartford Marshes, Littlebrook Power Station, and [Thames Path](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thames_Path). Flood risk — check warnings. Lunch in Greenhithe: the Asda café. 11 km.
DFD.publicWalksS2S: **[Dartford to Greenhithe](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dartford-to-greenhithe/)**: Flat riverside paths, flood defences, marshland, industrial edge-land, and wide Thames estuary views. Sights: Queen Elizabeth Bridge, Dartford Marshes, Littlebrook Power Station, and [Thames Path](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thames_Path). Flood risk — check warnings. Lunch in Greenhithe: the Asda café. 11 km.

DFI.name: Duffield
DFI.adminWalksAll:
**[Duffield to Derby via Drum Hill](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Repurposed railway track along the Great Northern Greenway. Sights: Great Northern Greenway, Horsley Carr, and Morley. 16 km. 160 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/426%20Duffield%20to%20Derby.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925790185)**

**[Duffield Circular](https://www.visitambervalley.com/media/1106/10-duffield.pdf)**: Undulating countryside with several steep climbs. 12 km.
DFI.publicWalksS2S: **[Duffield to Derby via Drum Hill](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Repurposed railway track along the Great Northern Greenway. Sights: Great Northern Greenway, Horsley Carr, and Morley. 16 km. 160 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/426%20Duffield%20to%20Derby.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925790185)**

DID.name: Didcot Parkway
DID.adminWalksAll: **[Didcot Parkway Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/didcot-circular/)**: Thames Valley farmland, riverside paths, attractive villages, Iron Age hill forts, and a nature reserve. Sights: Dorchester Abbey and Wittenham Clumps. Lunch in Dorchester-on-Thames, Long Wittenham, or Didcot. Dorchester-on-Thames lunch stops: the [Fleur De Lys](http://www.fleurdelys-dorchester.co.uk/), the George Hotel, or the [White Hart Hotel](http://www.white-hart-hotel-dorchester.co.uk/). Long Wittenham lunch stop: the [Plough](http://www.theploughinnlw.co.uk/). Didcot lunch stop: the [Prince of Wales](http://www.princeofwales-didcot.co.uk/). 22 km.
DID.publicWalksCircular: **[Didcot Parkway Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/didcot-circular/)**: Thames Valley farmland, riverside paths, attractive villages, Iron Age hill forts, and a nature reserve. Sights: Dorchester Abbey and Wittenham Clumps. Lunch in Dorchester-on-Thames, Long Wittenham, or Didcot. Dorchester-on-Thames lunch stops: the [Fleur De Lys](http://www.fleurdelys-dorchester.co.uk/), the George Hotel, or the [White Hart Hotel](http://www.white-hart-hotel-dorchester.co.uk/). Long Wittenham lunch stop: the [Plough](http://www.theploughinnlw.co.uk/). Didcot lunch stop: the [Prince of Wales](http://www.princeofwales-didcot.co.uk/). 22 km.

DKG.name: Dorking
DKG.adminWalksAll:
**[Dorking Circular via Brockham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/deepdene-trail/)**: Restored landscaped gardens, rhododendron woods, surfaced parkland paths, open fields with Downs views, wooded valley trails, village green, and town centre. Sights: [Deepdene Gardens](https://deepdenetrail.co.uk/), Hope Mausoleum, Betchworth Castle, Cotmandene Common, Chat Park, Glory Woods, and Brockham village green. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Brockham: the Inn on the Green, the Reading Room Coffee and Cake House, or the Taphouse. 16 km.

**[Dorking Circular via Denbies Hillside](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-circular/)**: North Downs chalk escarpment, Greensand Hills, wooded ridges, open downland, river valleys, semi-natural woodland reserve, and ponds. Sights: [Denbies Hillside](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/denbies-hillside), St John the Evangelist, Hammer Pond, Friday Street, Bury Hill Fisheries, The Nower, Greensand Way, Rose Hill Arch, St Martin's Church, and Mullins Coffee Shop. Can be muddy. Lunch in Wotton: the [Wotton Hatch](https://www.wottonhatch.co.uk). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Dorking Circular via Norbury Park and Mickleham**: An alternative variant of [Dorking to Box Hill via Norbury Park and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-to-box-hill-via-norbury-park-and-mickleham/). Rambler favourite! Vineyard, wooded hills, chalk downland, open grassland, riverside path along the Mole, and North Downs. Sights: [Denbies Wine Estate](https://www.denbies.co.uk), [Norbury Park Nature Reserve](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/norbury-park), [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Broadwood's Tower, Box Hill Fort, and Swiss Cottage. Can be muddy. Lunch in Mickleham: the [King William IV](https://www.thekingwilliamiv.com), or the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk). 15 km. 4 hours.

**Dorking Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham**: An alternative variant of [Box Hill Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-headley-heath-and-mickleham/). North Downs hills, open downland, heathland, Norbury Park woodland edge, vineyard, and valley paths. Sights: [Headley Heath](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/headley-heath), [Norbury Park](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/norbury-park), [Denbies Wine Estate](https://www.denbies.co.uk), St Michael and All Angels, The Gallops, and Stane Street. Steep descents. Lunch in Mickleham: the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk). 16 km.

**Dorking Circular via Betchworth & Deepdene Trail**: A longer variant of [Box Hill Circular via Betchworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-betchworth/). Mole Valley riverside meadows, chalk escarpment, disused quarries, parkland, Deepdene estate woodland, terraced gardens, and town. Sights: Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, Betchworth Quarry and Lime Works, [St Michael, Betchworth](https://www.nationalchurchestrust.org/church/betchworth-st-michael), [Betchworth Castle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betchworth_Castle), Christ Church, Brockham, [Deepdene Trail](https://visitmolevalley.co.uk/the-deepdene-trail), Deepdene Terrace, Deepdene Gardens, Hope Mausoleum, St Martin, Dorking, and Mullins Coffee Shop. Can be muddy. Lunch in Betchworth, or Brockham. Betchworth lunch stops: the [Red Lion](https://www.theredlionbetchworth.com), or the [Dolphin](https://www.dolphinbetchworth.com). Brockham lunch stops: the [Inn on the Green](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/brockham), or the Royal Oak. 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Dorking Circular via Abinger Roughs**: A longer variant of [Dorking Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-circular/). North Downs chalk escarpment, Greensand Hills, Abinger Roughs mixed woodland and grassland, open hillside, river valleys, and wooded ridges. Sights: [Denbies Hillside](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/denbies-hillside), [Abinger Roughs](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/abinger-roughs-and-netley-park), Wilberforce Memorial, Witch's Broom Tree, Abinger Manor, St James, Abinger, Friday Street, Hammer Pond, Bury Hill Fisheries, The Nower, Greensand Way, Rose Hill Arch, and St Martin's Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Abinger Common: the [Abinger Hatch](https://theabingerhatch.co.uk). 22 km. 6 hours.

**[Dorking to Box Hill & Westhumble via Norbury Park and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-to-box-hill-via-norbury-park-and-mickleham/)**: Rambler favourite! Vineyard, wooded hills, chalk downland, open grassland, Mole valley views, and North Downs. Sights: [Denbies Wine Estate](https://www.denbies.co.uk), [Norbury Park Nature Reserve](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/norbury-park), [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Westhumble Chapel, Broadwood's Tower, and Box Hill Fort. Can be muddy. Lunch in Mickleham: the [King William IV](https://www.thekingwilliamiv.com), or the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk). 14 km. 4 hours.

**[Dorking to Reigate](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-to-reigate/)**: Mainly level riverside path, sandstone Greensand Way, picturesque Surrey villages, and parkland grounds. Sights: Betchworth Castle, St Michael's Church, and Reigate Priory. Lunch in Betchworth, Brockham, or Reigate Heath. Betchworth lunch stop: the [Dolphin Inn](http://www.youngs.co.uk/pub-detail.asp?PubID=387). Brockham lunch stop: the [Royal Oak](http://www.royaloak-brockham.co.uk/). Reigate Heath lunch stop: the [Skimmington Castle](http://www.skimmingtoncastle.co.uk/). 12 km. 3.5 hours.

**Dorking to Box Hill & Westhumble via Betchworth**: An alternative variant of [Box Hill Circular via Betchworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-betchworth/). Mole Valley riverside meadows, chalk escarpment, disused quarries, parkland, and village greens. Sights: [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, Betchworth Quarry and Lime Works, [St Michael, Betchworth](https://www.nationalchurchestrust.org/church/betchworth-st-michael), [Betchworth Castle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betchworth_Castle), Stepping Stones, and Christ Church, Brockham. Can be muddy. Lunch in Betchworth, or Brockham. Betchworth lunch stops: the [Red Lion](https://www.theredlionbetchworth.com), or the [Dolphin](https://www.dolphinbetchworth.com). Brockham lunch stops: the [Inn on the Green](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/brockham), or the Royal Oak. 16 km.

**Dorking to Box Hill & Westhumble via Headley Heath and Mickleham**: A variant of [Box Hill Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-headley-heath-and-mickleham/). North Downs hills, open downland, wooded heathland, grassy spurs, chalk paths, beech woodland, and valley views. Sights: [Headley Heath](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/headley-heath), [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Salomons Memorial, Box Hill Fort, St Michael and All Angels, The Gallops, Stane Street, Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, and North Downs Way. Steep descents. Lunch in Mickleham: the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk), or the [King William IV](https://www.thekingwilliamiv.com). 16 km.

**[Gomshall to Dorking via Holmbury Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/gomshall-to-dorking/)**: Greensand Ridge, heathland, hilltop moorland, woodland trails, suburban streets, and a high street. Sights: Holmbury Hill, Leith Hill Tower, The Nower, and Hurt Wood. Can be muddy. Lunch in Holmbury St. Mary: the [Kings Head](https://thekingsheadhsm.com/), the [Royal Oak](https://royaloakholmbury.co.uk/), or the [Heartwork Coffee Bar](https://www.heartworkcoffee.co.uk/). 23 km.

**Gomshall to Dorking via Piney Copse**: An alternative variant of [Dorking Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-circular/). Piney Copse woodland, Abinger Roughs mixed woodland and grassland, open hillside, river valleys, Greensand Hills, and wooded ridges. Sights: Piney Copse, [Abinger Roughs](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/abinger-roughs-and-netley-park), Wilberforce Memorial, Abinger Manor, St James, Abinger, Friday Street, Hammer Pond, Bury Hill Fisheries, The Nower, Greensand Way, Rose Hill Arch, and St Martin's Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Abinger Common: the [Abinger Hatch](https://theabingerhatch.co.uk). 15 km. 4 hours.

**Box Hill & Westhumble to Dorking via Headley Heath and Mickleham**: An alternative variant of [Box Hill Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-headley-heath-and-mickleham/). North Downs hills, open downland, heathland, woodland edge of Norbury Park, vineyard, and valley paths. Sights: [Headley Heath](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/headley-heath), [Norbury Park](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/norbury-park), [Denbies Wine Estate](https://www.denbies.co.uk), St Michael and All Angels, The Gallops, Stane Street, Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, and Mole Gap Trail. Steep descents. Lunch in Mickleham: the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk). 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**Effingham Junction to Dorking**: Hidden valleys, pretty woodland, gentle pasture, North Downs escarpment with extensive views, vineyard, and ending at Dorking. Sights: Polesden Lacey, Denbies Wine Estate, Great Ridings Wood, and Ranmore Common. Best in Apr, May, Sep, Oct and Nov. Lunch in Polesden Lacey: the Granary Cafe, Polesden Lacey. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km.

**Box Hill & Westhumble to Dorking via Betchworth & Deepdene Trail**: A longer variant of [Box Hill Circular via Betchworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-betchworth/). Chalk downland, escarpment, disused quarries, parkland, Deepdene estate woodland, terraced gardens, and town. Sights: [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Salomons Memorial, Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, Betchworth Quarry and Lime Works, [St Michael, Betchworth](https://www.nationalchurchestrust.org/church/betchworth-st-michael), [Betchworth Castle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betchworth_Castle), Christ Church, Brockham, [Deepdene Trail](https://visitmolevalley.co.uk/the-deepdene-trail), Deepdene Terrace, Deepdene Gardens, Hope Mausoleum, St Martin, Dorking, and Mullins Coffee Shop. Can be muddy. Lunch in Betchworth, or Brockham. Betchworth lunch stops: the [Red Lion](https://www.theredlionbetchworth.com), or the [Dolphin](https://www.dolphinbetchworth.com). Brockham lunch stops: the [Inn on the Green](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/brockham), or the Royal Oak. 18 km. 5 hours.

Can be muddy. Lunch in Wotton: the [Wotton Hatch](https://www.wottonhatch.co.uk). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

DKT.name: Dorking West
DKT.adminWalksAll: **Dorking West to Merstham**: A variant of [Box Hill to Merstham and Caterham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-to-merstham/). Vineyard, North Downs ridge, wooded escarpment, open hilltops, and parkland. Sights: Denbies Vineyard, Box Hill, Reigate Hill, Gatton Park, and Reigate Fort. Hilly, multiple ascents. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Reigate Hill, or Merstham. Reigate Hill lunch stop: the [Chalk Hare](http://www.chalkhare.co.uk/). Merstham lunch stop: the Feathers.
DKT.publicWalksS2S: **Dorking West to Merstham**: A variant of [Box Hill to Merstham and Caterham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-to-merstham/). Vineyard, North Downs ridge, wooded escarpment, open hilltops, and parkland. Sights: Denbies Vineyard, Box Hill, Reigate Hill, Gatton Park, and Reigate Fort. Hilly, multiple ascents. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Reigate Hill, or Merstham. Reigate Hill lunch stop: the [Chalk Hare](http://www.chalkhare.co.uk/). Merstham lunch stop: the Feathers.

DMS.name: Dormans
DMS.adminWalksAll:
**Dormans Circular via Cook's Pond Viaduct**: A shorter variant of [Dormans to Lingfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dormans-to-lingfield/). Undulating woodland, railway cuttings and viaducts, golf course, racecourse straight, nature reserve, farmland, and village conservation area. Sights: Cook's Pond Viaduct, [Lingfield Park Racecourse](https://www.lingfieldpark.co.uk), [Lingfield Nature Reserve](https://www.lingfieldreserves.org.uk), Ss Peter and Paul, New Place, Old Cage, Lingfield Oak, Community Orchard, and [Old House at Home](https://www.oldhouseathome.co.uk). Can be muddy. Lunch in Lingfield: the [Star](https://www.thestarlingfield.co.uk). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**Dormans Circular via Young Epilepsy**: A longer variant of [Dormans to Lingfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dormans-to-lingfield/). Undulating woodland, railway cuttings and viaducts, golf course, racecourse straight, farmland, nature reserve, village conservation area, and fields and copses. Sights: Cook's Pond Viaduct, [Lingfield Park Racecourse](https://www.lingfieldpark.co.uk), [Lingfield Nature Reserve](https://www.lingfieldreserves.org.uk), Ss Peter and Paul, New Place, Old Cage, Lingfield Oak, Community Orchard, [Old House at Home](https://www.oldhouseathome.co.uk), and Young Epilepsy. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lingfield: the [Star](https://www.thestarlingfield.co.uk). 20 km. 5 hours.

**[Dormans to Lingfield via Lingfield Old Town conservation area](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dormans-to-lingfield/)**: Undulating woodland, railway cuttings and viaducts, golf course, racecourse straight, farmland, nature reserve, and village conservation area. Sights: Cook's Pond Viaduct, [Lingfield Park Racecourse](https://www.lingfieldpark.co.uk), [Lingfield Nature Reserve](https://www.lingfieldreserves.org.uk), Ss Peter and Paul, New Place, Lingfield Old Town conservation area, Old Cage, Lingfield Oak, Community Orchard, and Lingfield College. Can be muddy. Avoid August Bank Holiday. Lunch in Lingfield: the [Star](https://www.thestarlingfield.co.uk). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Dormans to Lingfield via Cook's Pond Viaduct**: A shorter variant of [Dormans to Lingfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dormans-to-lingfield/). Undulating woodland, railway cuttings and viaducts, golf course, racecourse straight, nature reserve, and village conservation area. Sights: Cook's Pond Viaduct, [Lingfield Park Racecourse](https://www.lingfieldpark.co.uk), [Lingfield Nature Reserve](https://www.lingfieldreserves.org.uk), Ss Peter and Paul, New Place, Old Cage, Lingfield Oak, and Community Orchard. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lingfield: the [Star](https://www.thestarlingfield.co.uk). 11 km. 2.5 hours.

**[Hever to Dormans](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-dormans/)**: Undulating countryside, woods, farmland, fields, streams, historic manor houses, iron-age hillfort, remote border area of Kent, and Surrey and Sussex. Sights: Cowden, St Mary Magdalene Church, Sussex Border Path, Waystrode Manor, Crippenden Manor, Vanguard Way, Dry Hill Fort, and Greathed Manor. Can be muddy. Lunch in Cowden: the [Fountain](https://www.thefountaininncowden.com/). 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**Cowden to Dormans**: A shorter variant of [Hever to Dormans](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-dormans/). Undulating countryside, woods, farmland, fields, streams, historic manor houses, iron-age hillfort, remote border area of Kent, and Surrey and Sussex. Sights: Cowden, St Mary Magdalene Church, Sussex Border Path, Waystrode Manor, Crippenden Manor, Vanguard Way, Dry Hill Fort, and Greathed Manor. Can be muddy. Lunch in Cowden: the [Fountain](https://www.thefountaininncowden.com/). 13 km.

**Lingfield to Dormans**: A longer variant of [Dormans to Lingfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dormans-to-lingfield/). Undulating woodland, railway cuttings and viaducts, golf course, racecourse straight, farmland, nature reserve, village conservation area, water meadow, and fields and copses. Sights: Cook's Pond Viaduct, [Lingfield Park Racecourse](https://www.lingfieldpark.co.uk), [Lingfield Nature Reserve](https://www.lingfieldreserves.org.uk), Ss Peter and Paul, New Place, Old Cage, Lingfield Oak, Community Orchard, [Old House at Home](https://www.oldhouseathome.co.uk), Young Epilepsy, and Lingfield College. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lingfield: the [Star](https://www.thestarlingfield.co.uk). 24 km. 5.5 hours.
DMS.publicWalksS2S: **[Dormans to Lingfield via Lingfield Old Town conservation area](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dormans-to-lingfield/)**: Undulating woodland, railway cuttings and viaducts, golf course, racecourse straight, farmland, nature reserve, and village conservation area. Sights: Cook's Pond Viaduct, [Lingfield Park Racecourse](https://www.lingfieldpark.co.uk), [Lingfield Nature Reserve](https://www.lingfieldreserves.org.uk), Ss Peter and Paul, New Place, Lingfield Old Town conservation area, Old Cage, Lingfield Oak, Community Orchard, and Lingfield College. Can be muddy. Avoid August Bank Holiday. Lunch in Lingfield: the [Star](https://www.thestarlingfield.co.uk). 16 km. 4 hours.
