Sights: Cowden, St Mary Magdalene Church, Sussex Border Path, Waystrode Manor, Crippenden Manor, Vanguard Way, Dry Hill Fort, and Greathed Manor. Can be muddy. Lunch in Cowden: the [Fountain](https://www.thefountaininncowden.com/). 16 km. 4.5 hours.

DNT.name: Dent
DNT.adminWalksAll: **[Dent to Ribblehead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/great-knoutberry-hill/)**: Grassy moorland, high fells, quiet roads, good tracks, open views, railway viaducts, becks, and pastures. Sights: Dent Head Viaduct, Ribblehead Viaduct, Great Knoutberry Hill, Blea Moor Railway Tunnel, [Station Inn](https://www.thestationinnribblehead.com/), and Ribblehead Station Visitor Centre and Tea Room. Can be boggy. 18 km. 5 hours.
DNT.publicWalksS2S: **[Dent to Ribblehead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/great-knoutberry-hill/)**: Grassy moorland, high fells, quiet roads, good tracks, open views, railway viaducts, becks, and pastures. Sights: Dent Head Viaduct, Ribblehead Viaduct, Great Knoutberry Hill, Blea Moor Railway Tunnel, [Station Inn](https://www.thestationinnribblehead.com/), and Ribblehead Station Visitor Centre and Tea Room. Can be boggy. 18 km. 5 hours.

DOR.name: Dore and Totley
DOR.adminWalksAll: **Sheffield to Dore and Totley**: A shorter variant of [Sheffield Circular via Porter and Gleadless](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sheffield-circular-via-porter-and-gleadless/). Ancient escarpment woodlands, wooded valleys, moorland fringe, panoramic views, and urban parks. Sights: [Sheffield General Cemetery](http://gencem.org/), Endcliffe Park, [Porter Valley](https://www.fopv.org.uk/), [Peak District](http://www.peakdistrict.gov.uk/), [Ecclesall Woods](http://www.friendsofecclesallwoods.org.uk/about/ecclesall), and Whirlow Brook Park. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Sheffield, Fulwood, Sheffield, Ringinglow, Sheffield, Whirlow Brook Park, Sheffield, or Dore, Sheffield. Sheffield lunch stop: the Endcliffe Park Café. Fulwood, Sheffield lunch stop: the [Forge Dam Café](https://forgedamcafe.co.uk/). Ringinglow, Sheffield lunch stop: the [Norfolk Arms](http://norfolkarms.com/). Whirlow Brook Park, Sheffield lunch stop: the Shelter Café. Dore, Sheffield lunch stop: the [Summer House](http://www.thesummerhousesheffield.com/).
DOR.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Sheffield to Dore and Totley**: A shorter variant of [Sheffield Circular via Porter and Gleadless](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sheffield-circular-via-porter-and-gleadless/). Ancient escarpment woodlands, wooded valleys, moorland fringe, panoramic views, and urban parks. Sights: [Sheffield General Cemetery](http://gencem.org/), Endcliffe Park, [Porter Valley](https://www.fopv.org.uk/), [Peak District](http://www.peakdistrict.gov.uk/), [Ecclesall Woods](http://www.friendsofecclesallwoods.org.uk/about/ecclesall), and Whirlow Brook Park. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Sheffield, Fulwood, Sheffield, Ringinglow, Sheffield, Whirlow Brook Park, Sheffield, or Dore, Sheffield. Sheffield lunch stop: the Endcliffe Park Café. Fulwood, Sheffield lunch stop: the [Forge Dam Café](https://forgedamcafe.co.uk/). Ringinglow, Sheffield lunch stop: the [Norfolk Arms](http://norfolkarms.com/). Whirlow Brook Park, Sheffield lunch stop: the Shelter Café. Dore, Sheffield lunch stop: the [Summer House](http://www.thesummerhousesheffield.com/).

DVP.name: Dover Priory
DVP.adminWalksAll:
**Dover Priory to Deal**: Rambler favourite! Clifftop chalk paths, two significant climbs, level pebble beach coastpath, open sea views, and historic town. Sights: Dover Castle, South Foreland Lighthouse, [Walmer Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/), and [Deal Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/). Mobile may switch networks. Best in May–Jun. Lunch in Dover, South Foreland Lighthouse, St Margaret's Bay, Kingsdown, or Deal. Dover lunch stop: the White Cliffs Visitor Centre. South Foreland Lighthouse lunch stop: the Mrs Knott's Tea Room. St Margaret's Bay lunch stops: the Pines Garden Tea Room and Museum, or the Coastguard. Kingsdown lunch stop: the Zetland Arms. Deal lunch stops: the Deal Pier Kitchen, the Port Arms, the Kings Arm, or the Ship Inn. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 17 km.

**[Folkestone Central to Dover Priory via Martello Towers](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/folkestone-to-dover/)**: Rambler favourite! Coastal cliffs, chalk downland, sea defences, wild landslip area, wartime installations, ridge between valley and sea, and town crossing. Sights: Martello Towers, Folkestone Warren, World War Two sound mirror, and Western Heights. Cracked sea defences. Lunch in Folkestone Warren: the Royal Oak. 14 km.

**Folkestone Central to Dover Priory via Battle of Britain Memorial**: An alternative variant of [Folkestone to Dover](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/folkestone-to-dover/). Rambler favourite! Cliff-top path, panoramic Channel views, wartime installations, and chalk downland. Sights: Battle of Britain Memorial, Cliff Top Cafe, and World War Two sound mirror. Lunch in Folkestone, or Folkestone Warren. Folkestone lunch stop: the Cliff Top Cafe. Folkestone Warren lunch stop: the Royal Oak. 15 km.

**Deal to Dover Priory**: Rambler favourite! Pebble beach coastpath, clifftop chalk paths, dramatic cliffs near Dover, and seaside town finish. Sights: [Deal Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/), [Walmer Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/), South Foreland Lighthouse, and Dover Castle. Mobile may switch networks. Best in May–Jun. Lunch in Kingsdown, or St Margaret's Bay. Kingsdown lunch stop: the Zetland Arms. St Margaret's Bay lunch stop: the Coastguard. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 17 km.

**Sandling to Dover Priory**: A longer variant of [Sandling to Folkestone or Dover](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-or-dover/). Rambler favourite! Escarpment ridge, steep climbs, White Cliffs cliff-top path, remote chalk downland, housing estate outskirts, and Western Heights fortifications. Sights: [Folkestone](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folkestone), [Sandgate Branch](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandgate_Branch), [Elham Valley Railway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elham_Valley_Railway), Battle of Britain Memorial, White Cliffs of Dover, and Western Heights. Steep climbs near start. Lunch in Etchinghill, or White Cliffs. Etchinghill lunch stop: the [Gatekeeper Inn](http://www.gatekeeperinn.co.uk/). White Cliffs lunch stop: the Clifftop Cafe. 21 km.
DVP.publicWalksS2S: **Dover Priory to Deal**: Rambler favourite! Clifftop chalk paths, two significant climbs, level pebble beach coastpath, open sea views, and historic town. Sights: Dover Castle, South Foreland Lighthouse, [Walmer Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/), and [Deal Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/). Mobile may switch networks. Best in May–Jun. Lunch in Dover, South Foreland Lighthouse, St Margaret's Bay, Kingsdown, or Deal. Dover lunch stop: the White Cliffs Visitor Centre. South Foreland Lighthouse lunch stop: the Mrs Knott's Tea Room. St Margaret's Bay lunch stops: the Pines Garden Tea Room and Museum, or the Coastguard. Kingsdown lunch stop: the Zetland Arms. Deal lunch stops: the Deal Pier Kitchen, the Port Arms, the Kings Arm, or the Ship Inn. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 17 km.
DVP.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Folkestone Central to Dover Priory via Martello Towers](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/folkestone-to-dover/)**: Rambler favourite! Coastal cliffs, chalk downland, sea defences, wild landslip area, wartime installations, ridge between valley and sea, and town crossing. Sights: Martello Towers, Folkestone Warren, World War Two sound mirror, and Western Heights. Cracked sea defences. Lunch in Folkestone Warren: the Royal Oak. 14 km.

DZY.name: Danzey
DZY.adminWalksAll: **[Danzey Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-851)**: Farmland, woodland edge, country lanes, stream crossings, and open fields. Sights: Mockley Wood, [St. Mary Magdalene](http://www.tanworthchurch.co.uk), [Umberslade Estate](http://www.umberslade-estate.com), [The Vintage Barn](http://www.facebook.com/thevintagebarnhenley), [Middle England Farm](http://www.middleenglandfarm.co.uk), and Avoncroft Museum. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Tanworth-in-Arden, or Wood End. Tanworth-in-Arden lunch stop: the [Bell Inn](http://www.thebellattanworthinarden.co.uk). Wood End lunch stop: the [Warwickshire Lad](http://www.thewarwickshirelad.co.uk). 4 km. 1.5 hours.
DZY.publicWalksCircular: **[Danzey Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-851)**: Farmland, woodland edge, country lanes, stream crossings, and open fields. Sights: Mockley Wood, [St. Mary Magdalene](http://www.tanworthchurch.co.uk), [Umberslade Estate](http://www.umberslade-estate.com), [The Vintage Barn](http://www.facebook.com/thevintagebarnhenley), [Middle England Farm](http://www.middleenglandfarm.co.uk), and Avoncroft Museum. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Tanworth-in-Arden, or Wood End. Tanworth-in-Arden lunch stop: the [Bell Inn](http://www.thebellattanworthinarden.co.uk). Wood End lunch stop: the [Warwickshire Lad](http://www.thewarwickshirelad.co.uk). 4 km. 1.5 hours.

EAL.name: Ealing Broadway
EAL.adminWalksAll:
**[Ealing Broadway Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ealing-broadway-circular/)**: Urban green spaces, canal towpath, river Thames towpath, parks, gardens, and suburban streets. Sights: Pitshanger Manor, Walpole Park, Grand Union Canal, Boston Manor, Syon House, Richmond Old Deer Park, Kew Gardens, Kew Bridge Steam Museum, Gunnersbury Park, and Lammas Park. Lunch in Syon Park, or Isleworth. Syon Park lunch stop: the Syon Park Refectory. Isleworth lunch stop: the London Apprentice. 17 km.

**Ealing Broadway to Brentford**: A shorter variant of [Ealing Broadway Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ealing-broadway-circular/). Canal towpath, urban parks, and suburban streets ending at Brentford Station. Sights: Pitshanger Manor, Grand Union Canal, Boston Manor, and Syon House. Lunch in Syon Park, or Isleworth. Syon Park lunch stop: the Syon Park Refectory. Isleworth lunch stop: the London Apprentice.

**Ealing Broadway to Richmond**: A shorter variant of [Ealing Broadway Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ealing-broadway-circular/). Canal towpath, river Thames towpath, urban parks, and suburban streets ending at Richmond via Old Deer Park. Sights: Pitshanger Manor, Grand Union Canal, Boston Manor, Syon House, and Richmond Old Deer Park. Lunch in Syon Park, or Isleworth. Syon Park lunch stop: the Syon Park Refectory. Isleworth lunch stop: the London Apprentice.

**Ealing Broadway to Kew Bridge**: A shorter variant of [Ealing Broadway Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ealing-broadway-circular/). Canal towpath, river Thames towpath, urban parks, and suburban streets ending at Kew Bridge Station. Sights: Pitshanger Manor, Grand Union Canal, Boston Manor, Syon House, Richmond Old Deer Park, Kew Gardens, and Kew Bridge Steam Museum. Lunch in Syon Park, or Isleworth. Syon Park lunch stop: the Syon Park Refectory. Isleworth lunch stop: the London Apprentice.

**Ealing Broadway to Kew Gardens**: A shorter variant of [Ealing Broadway Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ealing-broadway-circular/). Canal towpath, river Thames towpath, urban parks, and suburban streets ending at Kew Gardens station. Sights: Pitshanger Manor, Grand Union Canal, Boston Manor, Syon House, Richmond Old Deer Park, and Kew Gardens. Lunch in Syon Park, or Isleworth. Syon Park lunch stop: the Syon Park Refectory. Isleworth lunch stop: the London Apprentice.
EAL.publicWalksCircular: **[Ealing Broadway Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ealing-broadway-circular/)**: Urban green spaces, canal towpath, river Thames towpath, parks, gardens, and suburban streets. Sights: Pitshanger Manor, Walpole Park, Grand Union Canal, Boston Manor, Syon House, Richmond Old Deer Park, Kew Gardens, Kew Bridge Steam Museum, Gunnersbury Park, and Lammas Park. Lunch in Syon Park, or Isleworth. Syon Park lunch stop: the Syon Park Refectory. Isleworth lunch stop: the London Apprentice. 17 km.

EBN.name: Eastbourne
EBN.adminWalksAll:
**Eastbourne to Seaford via Beachy Head**: An essential walk! Spectacular white cliffs in a well-protected national landscape. Immense white chalk cliffs, sea, marshes, meadows, and beachfront. Best on sunny high summer days when the chalk is gleaming. Seven Sisters extremely busy on Summer weekends/bank holidays. Best in May–Jul. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 18 March 2024.* 22 km. 290 m uphill. 6 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/1472764500)**

**Eastbourne Circular**: An alternative variant of [Eastbourne to East Dean](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eastbourne-to-east-dean/). Seafront promenade, chalk cliffs, open downs, quiet downland valley, ridge descent, and town streets. Sights: Beachy Head, Belle Tout, Birling Gap, and Seven Sisters. Mile on Eastbourne streets. Lunch in Beachy Head, Birling Gap, or East Dean. Beachy Head lunch stop: the Beachy Head pub. Birling Gap lunch stop: the Birling Gap National Trust Tea Room. East Dean lunch stops: the Tiger Inn, or the Hiker's Rest. 17 km.

**[Eastbourne to Hastings](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eastbourne-to-hastings-via-bexhill/)**: Flat coastal seafront promenades, shingle beach, sandy beach at low tide, sea wall paths, quiet coast roads, and low cliffs. Sights: [Eastbourne Pier](http://www.eastbournepier.com/), [Eastbourne Harbour](http://www.eastbourneharbour.com/walks.php), [Martello Towers](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Martello_tower), [Pevensey Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/daysout/properties/pevensey-castle/), [De La Warr Pavilion](http://www.dlwp.com/), and Hastings Pier. Shingle heavy going. Lunch in Pevensey Bay, Norman's Bay, Cooden Beach, Bexhill, or St Leonard's. Pevensey Bay lunch stops: the Aqua Bar, the Moorings, or the [Castle Inn](http://castleinnpevenseybay.co.uk/). Norman's Bay lunch stop: the [Star Inn](https://www.thestarinnnormansbay.co.uk/). Cooden Beach lunch stop: the [Relais Cooden Beach](https://www.therelaisretreats.com/coodenbeach/). Bexhill lunch stop: the [De La Warr Pavilion Cafe, Bar & Kitchen](http://www.dlwp.com/). St Leonard's lunch stops: the [Farmyard](https://farmyardwine.com/), or the [Royal](http://theroyalstleonards.co.uk/). 26 km.

**Berwick to Eastbourne via Filching Manor Motor Museum**: Rambler favourite! Farmland, reservoir, chalk downland, ridge grassland, chalk figure, smugglers villages, and seaside resort. Sights: [Long Man of Wilmington](http://www.villagenet.co.uk/sevensisters/villages/wilmington.php), Arlington Reservoir, and Filching Manor Motor Museum. Lunch in Wilmington, or Jevington. Wilmington lunch stop: the [Long Man Inn](https://longmaninn.co.uk/). Jevington lunch stop: the [Eight Bells](https://eightbellsjevington.com/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 20 km. 6.5 hours.

**Seaford to Eastbourne**: Rambler favourite! Dramatic clifftop chalk downs, undulating Seven Sisters cliffs, shingle beaches, river haven, and elegant seafront promenade. Sights: [Birling Gap](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/birling-gap-and-the-seven-sisters), Seven Sisters, Belle Tout, and Martello Tower. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch in Exceat: the Cuckmere Inn, the Saltmarsh Kitchen, or the Grab and Go. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 21 km.

**[Hampden Park to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hampden-park-to-eastbourne/)**: Parks, woods, chalk downland ridges, open grassland, chalk heath, dense beech forest, coastal farmland, golf course, and Victorian seaside town. Sights: Hampden Park, Lullington Heath National Nature Reserve, Friston Forest, St Andrew's Church, Jevington, Friston Place, Belle Tout Lighthouse, Birling Gap, Eastbourne cricket ground, Eastbourne Town Hall, Gildredge Park, and Little Chelsea. Steep descents. Lunch in Jevington, or East Dean. Jevington lunch stop: the [Eight Bells](https://eightbellsjevington.com/). East Dean lunch stops: the [Tiger Inn](https://www.beachyhead.org.uk/the-tiger-inn/), the Hikers' Rest Coffee Shop, the Thai Terre, or the Beehive on the Green. 26 km.

**[Southease to Eastbourne via Long Man of Wilmington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-eastbourne/)**: Open treeless downland ridges, steep ascents, scenic Cuckmere Valley, coastal promenade, beach, and pier. Sights: Firle Beacon, Long Burgh Long Barrow, Long Man of Wilmington, Windover Hill, Beachy Head, Wish Tower, Eastbourne Pier, and Italian Gardens. Steep ascents. Lunch in Alfriston: the Pubs, tea rooms and village shop. 27 km.

**Berwick to Eastbourne via Beachy Head**: Rambler favourite! Farmland, chalk downland, ridge grassland, South Downs Way, clifftop, and highest chalk sea cliff in Britain. Sights: [Long Man of Wilmington](http://www.villagenet.co.uk/sevensisters/villages/wilmington.php), Beachy Head, and Arlington Reservoir. Steep cliffs. Lunch in Wilmington, or Jevington. Wilmington lunch stop: the [Long Man Inn](https://longmaninn.co.uk/). Jevington lunch stop: the [Eight Bells](https://eightbellsjevington.com/). A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 24 km.

**Pevensey and Westham to Eastbourne**: A shorter variant of [Eastbourne to Hastings via Bexhill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eastbourne-to-hastings-via-bexhill/). Coastal path south-west from Pevensey, seafront promenade, sandy beach, and Eastbourne pier. Sights: [Pevensey Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/daysout/properties/pevensey-castle/) and [Eastbourne Pier](http://www.eastbournepier.com/). Lunch in Pevensey Bay: the Aqua Bar, or the [Castle Inn](http://castleinnpevenseybay.co.uk/). 9 km.

**Glynde to Eastbourne**: An alternative variant of [Southease to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-eastbourne/). Open treeless downland ridges, steep ascents, scenic Cuckmere Valley, coastal promenade, beach, and pier. Sights: Firle Beacon, Long Burgh Long Barrow, Long Man of Wilmington, Windover Hill, Beachy Head, Wish Tower, Eastbourne Pier, and Italian Gardens. Steep ascents. Lunch in Alfriston: the Pubs, tea rooms and village shop. 27 km.

**Southease to Eastbourne via Cuckmere Haven**: A longer variant of [Southease to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-eastbourne/). Open downland ridges, steep ascents, Cuckmere Haven, Seven Sisters chalk cliffs, and coastal path. Sights: Firle Beacon, Long Burgh Long Barrow, Cuckmere Haven, Seven Sisters, Beachy Head, and Eastbourne Pier. Very hilly afternoon. Lunch in Alfriston: the Pubs, tea rooms and village shop. 33 km.

**Polegate to Eastbourne**: A longer variant of [Polegate to Birling Gap](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/polegate-to-birling-gap/). South Downs downland and coastal clifftop path from Birling Gap eastward to Eastbourne. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch in East Dean: the [Tiger Inn](https://www.beachyhead.org.uk/the-tiger-inn/), or the [Deli on the Green](https://www.deli-onthegreen.com/).
EBN.publicWalksS2S: **Eastbourne to Seaford via Beachy Head**: An essential walk! Spectacular white cliffs in a well-protected national landscape. Immense white chalk cliffs, sea, marshes, meadows, and beachfront. Best on sunny high summer days when the chalk is gleaming. Seven Sisters extremely busy on Summer weekends/bank holidays. Best in May–Jul. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 22 km. 290 m uphill. 6 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/1472764500)**

EBR.name: Edenbridge
EBR.adminWalksAll: **Edenbridge to Sevenoaks**: Open fields, NE ascent to Toy's Hill, and easterly woodland walking after Ide Hill. Sights: Toy's Hill and Ide Hill. Lunch in Ide Hill: the [Cock Inn](https://cockinnidehill.co.uk/). An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 17 km.
EBR.publicWalksS2S: **Edenbridge to Sevenoaks**: Open fields, NE ascent to Toy's Hill, and easterly woodland walking after Ide Hill. Sights: Toy's Hill and Ide Hill. Lunch in Ide Hill: the [Cock Inn](https://cockinnidehill.co.uk/). An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 17 km.

EBT.name: Edenbridge Town
EBT.adminWalksAll:
**[Edenbridge Town Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edenbridge-figure-of-8/)**: Gentle farmland, water meadows, riverside paths, farm airstrip, and flat Eden Valley. Sights: [Edenbridge](https://www.visitedenbridge.com), [River Eden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Eden,_Kent), [Eden Valley Museum](https://evmt.org.uk), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), Stone Bridge, Great Stone Bridge Trust, Edenbridge Water Meadows, Gabriel's Fishery, and Devil's Den. Can be waterlogged. Lunch in Edenbridge: the [Old Eden](https://www.theoldeden.com), or the [Ye Olde Crown](https://www.yeoldecrown.co.uk). 14 km. 3 hours.

**Edenbridge Town to Hever via Edenbridge**: An alternative variant of [Edenbridge Figure of 8](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edenbridge-figure-of-8/). Water meadows, riverside paths, farm airstrip, and flat Eden Valley fields to Hever. Sights: [Edenbridge](https://www.visitedenbridge.com), [River Eden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Eden,_Kent), [Eden Valley Museum](https://evmt.org.uk), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), Stone Bridge, Gabriel's Fishery, and Devil's Den. Can be waterlogged. Lunch in Edenbridge: the [Old Eden](https://www.theoldeden.com), or the [Ye Olde Crown](https://www.yeoldecrown.co.uk). 12 km. 2.5 hours.

**Edenbridge Town to Hever via Great Stone Bridge Trust**: A longer variant of [Edenbridge Figure of 8](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edenbridge-figure-of-8/). Gentle farmland, water meadows, riverside paths, farm airstrip, flat Eden Valley, and fields to Hever. Sights: [Edenbridge](https://www.visitedenbridge.com), [River Eden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Eden,_Kent), [Eden Valley Museum](https://evmt.org.uk), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), Stone Bridge, Great Stone Bridge Trust, Edenbridge Water Meadows, Gabriel's Fishery, and Devil's Den. Can be waterlogged. Lunch in Edenbridge: the [Old Eden](https://www.theoldeden.com), or the [Ye Olde Crown](https://www.yeoldecrown.co.uk). 18 km. 4 hours.
EBT.publicWalksCircular: **[Edenbridge Town Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edenbridge-figure-of-8/)**: Gentle farmland, water meadows, riverside paths, farm airstrip, and flat Eden Valley. Sights: [Edenbridge](https://www.visitedenbridge.com), [River Eden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Eden,_Kent), [Eden Valley Museum](https://evmt.org.uk), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), Stone Bridge, Great Stone Bridge Trust, Edenbridge Water Meadows, Gabriel's Fishery, and Devil's Den. Can be waterlogged. Lunch in Edenbridge: the [Old Eden](https://www.theoldeden.com), or the [Ye Olde Crown](https://www.yeoldecrown.co.uk). 14 km. 3 hours.

EDL.name: Edale
EDL.adminWalksAll:
**[Edale Circular via Kinder Scout and Mam Tor](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edale-circular-via-kinder-scout-and-mam-tor/)**: Rugged moorland plateau, peat bog, gritstone outcrops and boulders, fertile valley floor, steep ridgeline, and Dark Peak and White Peak landscapes. Sights: Kinder Scout, Kinder Downfall, Jacob's Ladder, Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, Great Ridge, and Pennine Way. Can be very boggy. Lunch in Edale: the [Old Nags Head](http://www.the-old-nags-head.co.uk/), or the [Rambler Inn](https://www.dorbiere.co.uk/the-rambler-inn/#). 20 km. 6 hours.

**[Edale Circular via Kinder Low](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kinder-scout-circuit-from-edale/)**: Moorland plateau, boggy ground, gritstone boulder fields, exposed edges, steep cloughs, heather slopes, and high-level ridge paths. Sights: Kinder Scout, Kinder Downfall, Grindslow Knoll, Kinder Low, The Woolpacks, Crowden Tower, Fairbrook Naze, and Ringing Roger. Serious navigation hazard. 27 km. 8 hours.

**Edale Circular via Kinder Scout and Mam Tor & Grindslow Knoll**: A shorter variant of [Edale Circular via Kinder Scout and Mam Tor](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edale-circular-via-kinder-scout-and-mam-tor/). Moorland plateau, gritstone outcrops, valley floor, ridge, and with shortcut over Grindslow Knoll. Sights: Kinder Scout, Grindslow Knoll, and Mam Tor. Lunch in Edale: the [Old Nags Head](http://www.the-old-nags-head.co.uk/), or the [Rambler Inn](https://www.dorbiere.co.uk/the-rambler-inn/#). 18 km.

**Edale Circular via Kinder Scout and Mam Tor & Grindsbrook Clough**: A shorter variant of [Edale Circular via Kinder Scout and Mam Tor](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edale-circular-via-kinder-scout-and-mam-tor/). Moorland plateau, gritstone outcrops, scrambling descent through clough, and valley floor. Sights: Kinder Scout and Grindsbrook Clough. Light scrambling required. Lunch in Edale: the [Old Nags Head](http://www.the-old-nags-head.co.uk/), or the [Rambler Inn](https://www.dorbiere.co.uk/the-rambler-inn/#). 18 km.

**[Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bamford-to-edale-via-win-hill-and-great-ridge/)**: Steep ascent to heathery ridge, exposed moorland, gritstone escarpments, upland peat bog, and scenic ridgeline with panoramic valley views. Sights: Win Hill Pike, The Great Ridge, Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, Upper Derwent Valley reservoirs, Lose Hill, and [Old Nag's Head](http://www.the-old-nags-head.co.uk/). Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Hope: the [Cheshire Cheese Inn](http://www.thecheshirecheeseinn.co.uk/). 20 km. 6.5 hours.

**Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge & Hollins Cross**: A shorter variant of [Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bamford-to-edale-via-win-hill-and-great-ridge/). Steep ascent to heathery ridge, exposed moorland, gritstone escarpments, and shorter ridgeline descent to Edale. Sights: Win Hill Pike, The Great Ridge, Lose Hill, and Hollins Cross. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Hope: the [Cheshire Cheese Inn](http://www.thecheshirecheeseinn.co.uk/). 13 km.

**Hope to Edale**: An alternative variant of [Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bamford-to-edale-via-win-hill-and-great-ridge/). Steep ascent to heathery ridge, exposed moorland, gritstone escarpments, and ridgeline with panoramic valley views. Sights: Win Hill Pike, The Great Ridge, Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, and Lose Hill. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Hope: the [Cheshire Cheese Inn](http://www.thecheshirecheeseinn.co.uk/). 15 km.

**Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge & Brown Knoll**: A longer variant of [Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bamford-to-edale-via-win-hill-and-great-ridge/). Steep ascent to heathery ridge, exposed moorland, upland peat bog on Brown Knoll, gritstone escarpments, and descent via Jacob's Ladder. Sights: Win Hill Pike, The Great Ridge, Mam Tor, Brown Knoll, and Jacob's Ladder. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Hope: the [Cheshire Cheese Inn](http://www.thecheshirecheeseinn.co.uk/). 25 km.
EDL.publicWalksCircular:
**[Edale Circular via Kinder Scout and Mam Tor](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edale-circular-via-kinder-scout-and-mam-tor/)**: Rugged moorland plateau, peat bog, gritstone outcrops and boulders, fertile valley floor, steep ridgeline, and Dark Peak and White Peak landscapes. Sights: Kinder Scout, Kinder Downfall, Jacob's Ladder, Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, Great Ridge, and Pennine Way. Can be very boggy. Lunch in Edale: the [Old Nags Head](http://www.the-old-nags-head.co.uk/), or the [Rambler Inn](https://www.dorbiere.co.uk/the-rambler-inn/#). 20 km. 6 hours.

**[Edale Circular via Kinder Low](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kinder-scout-circuit-from-edale/)**: Moorland plateau, boggy ground, gritstone boulder fields, exposed edges, steep cloughs, heather slopes, and high-level ridge paths. Sights: Kinder Scout, Kinder Downfall, Grindslow Knoll, Kinder Low, The Woolpacks, Crowden Tower, Fairbrook Naze, and Ringing Roger. Serious navigation hazard. 27 km. 8 hours.
EDL.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bamford-to-edale-via-win-hill-and-great-ridge/)**: Steep ascent to heathery ridge, exposed moorland, gritstone escarpments, upland peat bog, and scenic ridgeline with panoramic valley views. Sights: Win Hill Pike, The Great Ridge, Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, Upper Derwent Valley reservoirs, Lose Hill, and [Old Nag's Head](http://www.the-old-nags-head.co.uk/). Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Hope: the [Cheshire Cheese Inn](http://www.thecheshirecheeseinn.co.uk/). 20 km. 6.5 hours.

EFF.name: Effingham Junction
EFF.adminWalksAll:
**Effingham Junction to Box Hill & Westhumble via Polesden Lacey**: Hidden valleys, pretty woodland, gentle pasture, North Downs escarpment with extensive views, and vineyard. Sights: Polesden Lacey, Denbies Wine Estate, Great Ridings Wood, and Ranmore Common. Best in Apr, May, Sep, Oct and Nov. Lunch in Polesden Lacey, or Effingham. Polesden Lacey lunch stop: the Granary Cafe, Polesden Lacey. Effingham lunch stop: the Queen Stage. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 15 km.

**Effingham Junction to Dorking**: Hidden valleys, pretty woodland, gentle pasture, North Downs escarpment with extensive views, vineyard, and ending at Dorking. Sights: Polesden Lacey, Denbies Wine Estate, Great Ridings Wood, and Ranmore Common. Best in Apr, May, Sep, Oct and Nov. Lunch in Polesden Lacey: the Granary Cafe, Polesden Lacey. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km.
EFF.publicWalksS2S: **Effingham Junction to Box Hill & Westhumble via Polesden Lacey**: Hidden valleys, pretty woodland, gentle pasture, North Downs escarpment with extensive views, and vineyard. Sights: Polesden Lacey, Denbies Wine Estate, Great Ridings Wood, and Ranmore Common. Best in Apr, May, Sep, Oct and Nov. Lunch in Polesden Lacey, or Effingham. Polesden Lacey lunch stop: the Granary Cafe, Polesden Lacey. Effingham lunch stop: the Queen Stage. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 15 km.

EFL.name: East Farleigh
EFL.adminWalksAll:
**East Farleigh to Wateringbury**: An alternative variant of [Wateringbury Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wateringbury-circular/). River Medway valley, riverside paths, low hills, orchard country, woodland, and country lanes. Sights: East Farleigh Bridge, [North Pole](https://northpolepub.co.uk), Teston Bridge, Teston Bridge Country Park, Tutsham Hall, Ss Peter and Paul, Teston, Alfred Reader factory, and Wateringbury Place. Can be muddy. Lunch in Red Hill: the [North Pole](https://northpolepub.co.uk). 13 km. 3 hours.

**East Malling to East Farleigh**: A shorter variant of [East Malling to Maidstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-malling-to-maidstone/). Orchards, coppiced woodland, water meadows, riverside paths, and low hills. Sights: [East Malling Research Station (Horticultural Research Institute)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Malling_Research_Station), [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), Warren Woods Nature Park, St Mary the Virgin, Nettlestead Place, Medway Valley Walk, Tutsham Hall, Smiths Hall, Barham Court, Teston Bridge, Maidstone Millennium River Park, East Farleigh Bridge, and East Farleigh Lock. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh, or Wateringbury. West Farleigh lunch stop: the [Tickled Trout](https://thetickledtrout.com). Wateringbury lunch stops: the [River Kitchen](https://www.theriverkitchen.co.uk), or the Ramblers Rest Café. 14 km.

**West Malling to East Farleigh**: A variant of [East Malling to Maidstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-malling-to-maidstone/). Orchards, country park, coppiced woodland, water meadows, riverside paths, and low hills. Sights: St Mary's Abbey, Manor Park Country Park, St Leonard's Tower, Douce's Manor, Warren Woods Nature Park, St Mary the Virgin, Nettlestead Place, Medway Valley Walk, Tutsham Hall, Smiths Hall, Teston Bridge, East Farleigh Bridge, and East Farleigh Lock. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh, or Wateringbury. West Farleigh lunch stop: the [Tickled Trout](https://thetickledtrout.com). Wateringbury lunch stops: the [River Kitchen](https://www.theriverkitchen.co.uk), or the Ramblers Rest Café. 17 km.
EFL.publicWalksS2S: **East Farleigh to Wateringbury**: An alternative variant of [Wateringbury Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wateringbury-circular/). River Medway valley, riverside paths, low hills, orchard country, woodland, and country lanes. Sights: East Farleigh Bridge, [North Pole](https://northpolepub.co.uk), Teston Bridge, Teston Bridge Country Park, Tutsham Hall, Ss Peter and Paul, Teston, Alfred Reader factory, and Wateringbury Place. Can be muddy. Lunch in Red Hill: the [North Pole](https://northpolepub.co.uk). 13 km. 3 hours.
EFL.publicWalksS2SEnding:
**East Malling to East Farleigh**: A shorter variant of [East Malling to Maidstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-malling-to-maidstone/). Orchards, coppiced woodland, water meadows, riverside paths, and low hills. Sights: [East Malling Research Station (Horticultural Research Institute)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Malling_Research_Station), [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), Warren Woods Nature Park, St Mary the Virgin, Nettlestead Place, Medway Valley Walk, Tutsham Hall, Smiths Hall, Barham Court, Teston Bridge, Maidstone Millennium River Park, East Farleigh Bridge, and East Farleigh Lock. Wateringbury lunch stops: the [River Kitchen](https://www.theriverkitchen.co.uk), or the Ramblers Rest Café. 14 km.

**West Malling to East Farleigh**: A variant of [East Malling to Maidstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-malling-to-maidstone/). Orchards, country park, coppiced woodland, water meadows, riverside paths, and low hills. Sights: St Mary's Abbey, Manor Park Country Park, St Leonard's Tower, Douce's Manor, Warren Woods Nature Park, St Mary the Virgin, Nettlestead Place, Medway Valley Walk, Tutsham Hall, Smiths Hall, Teston Bridge, East Farleigh Bridge, and East Farleigh Lock. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh, or Wateringbury. West Farleigh lunch stop: the [Tickled Trout](https://thetickledtrout.com). Wateringbury lunch stops: the [River Kitchen](https://www.theriverkitchen.co.uk), or the Ramblers Rest Café. 17 km.

EGH.name: Egham
EGH.adminWalksAll:
**[Slough to Egham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/slough-to-egham-via-windsor-great-park/)**: Flat urban start, parkland, deer park, historic royal landscape, ornamental gardens, and lake. Sights: Windsor Castle, Savill Gardens, Valley Gardens, Virginia Water lake, and [The Royal Landscape](http://www.theroyallandscape.co.uk/visiting/). 19 km. 4 hours.

**Windsor and Eton Riverside to Egham**: An alternative variant of [Slough to Egham via Windsor Great Park](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/slough-to-egham-via-windsor-great-park/). Flat parkland, deer park, historic royal landscape, ornamental gardens, and lake. Sights: Windsor Castle, Savill Gardens, Valley Gardens, Virginia Water lake, and [The Royal Landscape](http://www.theroyallandscape.co.uk/visiting/).
EGH.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Slough to Egham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/slough-to-egham-via-windsor-great-park/)**: Flat urban start, parkland, deer park, historic royal landscape, ornamental gardens, and lake. Sights: Windsor Castle, Savill Gardens, Valley Gardens, Virginia Water lake, and [The Royal Landscape](http://www.theroyallandscape.co.uk/visiting/). 19 km. 4 hours.

EGR.name: East Grinstead
EGR.adminWalksAll:
**East Grinstead Circular via Standen House**: An essential walk! Stately home and woods. Sights: [Standen House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/sussex/standen-house-and-garden) (late 19th Century; large and beautiful; filled with Arts and Crafts designs). Lunch at the Standen House café (very nice). *Last reviewed 1 January 2026.* 8 km. 130 m uphill. 2.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920186661)**

**[East Grinstead Circular via Tablehurst Farm](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-grinstead-circular/)**: Hilly Wealden countryside, pasture, woods, ancient houses, and quaint hilltop old town. Sights: Standen, Brambletye House, and Tablehurst Farm. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–Nov. Lunch in Forest Row: the Chequers Inn Hotel, the Java and Jazz Cafe and Pizzeria, the Brambletye Hotel, the Swan, the Hop Yard Brewery, or the Tablehurst Farm Shop. 17 km.

**East Grinstead Circular via Brambletye House & Stone Farm Rocks**: A variant of [East Grinstead Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-grinstead-circular/). Combines shorter morning through historic town and fields with longer afternoon along reservoir and past Standen. Sights: Brambletye House, Weir Wood Reservoir, Stone Farm Rocks, and Standen. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–Nov. Lunch in Forest Row: the Chequers Inn Hotel, the Java and Jazz Cafe and Pizzeria, the Hop Yard Brewery, or the Tablehurst Farm Shop. 18 km.

**East Grinstead Circular via Standen**: A variant of [East Grinstead Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-grinstead-circular/). Short circular through Wealden countryside to National Trust house and garden. Sights: Standen. Can be muddy. Lunch in Standen: the National Trust tea room. 7 km.

**East Grinstead Circular via Weir Wood Reservoir**: A longer variant of [East Grinstead Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-grinstead-circular/). Weir Wood Reservoir edge, road section, viewpoints, woods, and Wealden countryside. Sights: Weir Wood Reservoir, Stone Farm Rocks, and Standen. Road section with traffic. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Forest Row: the Chequers Inn Hotel, the Java and Jazz Cafe and Pizzeria, the Brambletye Hotel, the Swan, the Hop Yard Brewery, or the Tablehurst Farm Shop. 20 km.

**[East Grinstead to Wivelsfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-grinstead-to-wivelsfield/)**: Hills, streams, ponds, heaths, mixed woodland rich in bluebells and wood anemones, pastures, and heathery commons with South Downs views. Sights: Weir Wood Reservoir, Broadhurst Manor, Chailey Common, Chailey Windmill, and Bluebell Railway. Mud and flood prone. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Horsted Keynes, or Scaynes Hill. Horsted Keynes lunch stops: the [Green Man](http://greenmanhorstedkeynes.co.uk), or the [Crown Inn](http://www.thecrownhorstedkeynes.co.uk). Scaynes Hill lunch stop: the [Sloop Inn](https://thesloopinn.com/). 31 km. 7.5 hours.

**Balcombe to East Grinstead**: Gentle wooded hills, muddy farm tracks, landscaped gardens, reservoir shoreline, open fields, and woodland paths. Sights: [Wakehurst Place](http://www.wakehurst.org/visitor/visitwp.html), [Priest House Museum](http://www.sussexpast.co.uk/property/site.php?site_id=17), [St Margaret's Church](http://www.westhoathly.org.uk/), Stone Farm Rocks, Weir Wood Reservoir, Standen House, and Balcombe House. Can be very muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in West Hoathly: the [Cat Inn](http://www.catinn.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 17 km. 5 hours.

**Ashurst to East Grinstead**: A longer variant of [Ashurst to Forest Row and East Grinstead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-to-forest-row-and-east-grinstead/). Low hills, Wealden farmland, wooded bridleways, open Ashdown Forest heathland, golf course, disused railway trackbed (Forest Way), and historic town streets. Sights: [Ashdown Forest](https://ashdownforest.org/walking), Ashdown Forest Centre, [Forest Way](https://www.eastsussex.gov.uk/leisureandtourism/countryside/walks/forestway), [East Grinstead](http://www.westsussex.info/east-grinstead.shtml), Newbridge Mill, St Mary the Virgin, Lych Gate Cottage, Pooh Corner, Gallipot Inn, Brambletye House, Sackville College, St Swithun, Monument to Sir Archibald McIndoe, Freshfield Hall, and Brambletye Hotel. Can be muddy. Best in Oct–Nov. Lunch in Gallipot Street, Hartfield, or Colemans Hatch. Gallipot Street lunch stop: the [Gallipot Inn](https://www.the-gallipot-inn.co.uk). Hartfield lunch stop: the [Bear Inn](https://www.thebearhartfield.com). Colemans Hatch lunch stop: the [Hatch Inn](https://www.hatchinn.co.uk). 22 km. 5.5 hours.

**Eridge to East Grinstead**: A longer variant of [Eridge to Forest Row and East Grinstead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eridge-to-forest-row-and-east-grinstead/). Undulating High Weald countryside, clay paths, woods, fields, estate grounds, river valley, disused railway cycleway, and hilltop town streets. Sights: [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [High Weald Landscape Trail](https://highweald.org/things-to-do/high-weald-landscape-trail), Buckhurst Park, Penns in the Rocks, [St Michael and All Angels](https://www.withyhamchurch.org/history), Old Buckhurst, St Mary the Virgin, Lych Gate Cottage, [Pooh Corner](https://www.poohcorner.co.uk), [Tablehurst Farm](https://www.tablehurstfarm.org.uk), [Forest Way](https://www.eastsussex.gov.uk/leisureandtourism/countryside/walks/forestway), Brambletye House, Sackville College, St Swithun's Church, and [East Grinstead](http://www.westsussex.info/east-grinstead.shtml). Can be muddy. Lunch in Withyham, or Hartfield. Withyham lunch stop: the [Dorset Arms](https://www.dorset-arms.co.uk). Hartfield lunch stop: the [Bear Inn](https://www.thebearhartfield.com). 23 km. 5.5 hours.
EGR.publicWalksCircular: **East Grinstead Circular via Standen House**: An essential walk! Stately home and woods. Sights: [Standen House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/sussex/standen-house-and-garden) (late 19th Century; large and beautiful; filled with Arts and Crafts designs). Lunch at the Standen House café (very nice). 8 km. 130 m uphill. 2.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920186661)**

ELD.name: Earlswood
ELD.adminWalksAll: **[Earlswood to Oxted](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/earlswood-to-oxted/)**: Gentle greensand ridge, pasture, arable fields, woodland, bluebell woods, escarpment views, and low-lying meadows. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Bletchingley, or Tandridge. Bletchingley lunch stops: the Red Lion, the Whyte Hart Hotel, or the Lamingtons Tea Room. Tandridge lunch stop: the Barley Mow. 16 km.
ELD.publicWalksS2S: **[Earlswood to Oxted](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/earlswood-to-oxted/)**: Gentle greensand ridge, pasture, arable fields, woodland, bluebell woods, escarpment views, and low-lying meadows. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Bletchingley, or Tandridge. Bletchingley lunch stops: the Red Lion, the Whyte Hart Hotel, or the Lamingtons Tea Room. Tandridge lunch stop: the Barley Mow. 16 km.

ELY.name: Ely
ELY.adminWalksAll:
**Waterbeach to Ely**: Rambler favourite! Quaint village. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 3 August 2024.* 19 km. 20 m uphill. 5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/251949858)**

**[Ely Circular via Ely Cathedral](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ely-circular/)**: Open fenland, flat drained farmland, long straight tree-lined farm tracks, hedge-lined meadows, river banks, dykes and catchwater drains, quayside, and historic city. Sights: [Ely Cathedral](http://www.elycathedral.org/) and [Cromwell's House](http://www.olivercromwellshouse.co.uk/). Exposed river banks. Lunch in Stretham: the [Red Lion](https://www.theredlionstretham.co.uk/). 18 km. 4 hours.
ELY.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Waterbeach to Ely**: Rambler favourite! Quaint village. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 19 km. 20 m uphill. 5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/251949858)**

EMD.name: East Midlands Parkway
EMD.adminWalksAll:
**[East Midlands Parkway Circular via Gotham](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Rolling farmland. Sights: Cuckoo Tree, Ratcliffe-on-Soar, West Leake, and Gotham. Lunch in Gotham: the pubs and seating by the church. 18 km. 150 m uphill. 5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/109%20Parkway%20Circular%20via%20Gotham%2011.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925773451)**

**[East Midlands Parkway to Barrow upon Soar](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Flat, mostly on decent paths, and along the River Soar and Grand Union Canal. Sights: Redhill Boatyard, Kegworth, Zouch, River Soar, and Grand Union Canal. Lunch in Zouch, Loughborough, and Barrow: the pubs. 22 km. 40 m uphill. 6 hours. Hard. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/102%20Parkway%20Station%20to%20Barrow%20on%20Soar.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925755432)**

**[Notting to East Midlands Parkway along the Trent](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Canal, river, wooded track, and roadside footpath. Sights: Trent Bridge, West Bridgford, Clifton Hall, Ratcliffe Power Station, and River Trent. Short stretch on busy A453. Lunch at the Barton in Fabis farm shop and cafe. 17 km. 80 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/204%20Nottingham%20to%20Parkway%20South%20Bank.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925782195)**

**[Nottingham to East Midlands Parkway](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Flat and mostly on decent paths. Sights: Nottingham Canal, Beeston Canal, River Trent, Trentlock, and Sawley Cut. Lunch in Trentlock and Sawley Cut: the refreshments. 23 km. 30 m uphill. 6 hours. Hard. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/101%20Nottingham%20to%20Parkway%20Station.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925748565)**

EML.name: East Malling
EML.adminWalksAll:
**East Malling Circular**: An alternative variant of [West Malling Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/west-malling-circular/). Orchards, coppiced woodland, water meadows, low hills, river valley, and woodland nature park. Sights: [Horticultural Research Institute](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Malling_Research_Station), [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), Teston Bridge, Tutsham Hall, Smiths Hall, Barham Court, Teston Bridge Country Park, and Warren Woods Nature Park. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh: the [Tickled Trout](https://thetickledtrout.com). 16 km. 4 hours.

**[East Malling to Maidstone East](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-malling-to-maidstone/)**: Orchards, coppiced woodland, water meadows, riverside paths, low hills, and pedestrianised town centre. Sights: [East Malling Research Station (Horticultural Research Institute)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Malling_Research_Station), [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), [Maidstone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maidstone), St Mary's Abbey, Manor Park Country Park, St Leonard's Tower, Warren Woods Nature Park, Douce's Manor, St Mary the Virgin, Nettlestead Place, Medway Valley Walk, Tutsham Hall, Smiths Hall, Barham Court, Teston Bridge, Maidstone Millennium River Park, Barming Bridge, East Farleigh Bridge, East Farleigh Lock, Lockmeadow Millennium Bridge, The Stag, All Saints Church, Archbishop's Palace, Russian Cannon, Maidstone Town Hall, Maidstone Museum, Battle of Maidstone memorial, Brenchley Gardens, and Iguanodon. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh, or Wateringbury. West Farleigh lunch stop: the [Tickled Trout](https://thetickledtrout.com). Wateringbury lunch stops: the [River Kitchen](https://www.theriverkitchen.co.uk), or the Ramblers Rest Café. 19 km. 4.5 hours.

**East Malling to East Farleigh**: A shorter variant of [East Malling to Maidstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-malling-to-maidstone/). Orchards, coppiced woodland, water meadows, riverside paths, and low hills. Sights: [East Malling Research Station (Horticultural Research Institute)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Malling_Research_Station), [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), Warren Woods Nature Park, St Mary the Virgin, Nettlestead Place, Medway Valley Walk, Tutsham Hall, Smiths Hall, Barham Court, Teston Bridge, Maidstone Millennium River Park, East Farleigh Bridge, and East Farleigh Lock. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh, or Wateringbury. West Farleigh lunch stop: the [Tickled Trout](https://thetickledtrout.com). Wateringbury lunch stops: the [River Kitchen](https://www.theriverkitchen.co.uk), or the Ramblers Rest Café. 14 km.

**East Malling to West Malling**: A shorter variant of [West Malling Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/west-malling-circular/). Orchards, coppiced woodland, water meadows, low hills, river valley, parkland, and market town. Sights: [Horticultural Research Institute](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Malling_Research_Station), [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), Teston Bridge, Tutsham Hall, Smiths Hall, Barham Court, Teston Bridge Country Park, Warren Woods Nature Park, [Manor Park Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/find-a-kent-country-park/manor-park-country-park), [St Leonard's Tower](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/st-leonards-tower), Douce's Manor, St Mary's Abbey, and St Mary the Virgin. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh: the [Tickled Trout](https://thetickledtrout.com). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**East Malling to Maidstone West**: An alternative variant of [East Malling to Maidstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-malling-to-maidstone/). Orchards, coppiced woodland, water meadows, and riverside paths into Maidstone. Sights: Teston Bridge, East Farleigh Bridge, Lockmeadow Millennium Bridge, and Archbishop's Palace. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh: the [Tickled Trout](https://thetickledtrout.com).
EML.publicWalksS2S: **[East Malling to Maidstone East](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-malling-to-maidstone/)**: Orchards, coppiced woodland, water meadows, riverside paths, low hills, and pedestrianised town centre. Sights: [East Malling Research Station (Horticultural Research Institute)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Malling_Research_Station), [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), [Maidstone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maidstone), St Mary's Abbey, Manor Park Country Park, St Leonard's Tower, Warren Woods Nature Park, Douce's Manor, St Mary the Virgin, Nettlestead Place, Medway Valley Walk, Tutsham Hall, Smiths Hall, Barham Court, Teston Bridge, Maidstone Millennium River Park, Barming Bridge, East Farleigh Bridge, East Farleigh Lock, Lockmeadow Millennium Bridge, The Stag, All Saints Church, Archbishop's Palace, Russian Cannon, Maidstone Town Hall, Maidstone Museum, Battle of Maidstone memorial, Brenchley Gardens, and Iguanodon. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh, or Wateringbury. West Farleigh lunch stop: the [Tickled Trout](https://thetickledtrout.com). Wateringbury lunch stops: the [River Kitchen](https://www.theriverkitchen.co.uk), or the Ramblers Rest Café. 19 km. 4.5 hours.

EMS.name: Emsworth
EMS.adminWalksAll: **[Southbourne to Emsworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southbourne-to-emsworth-via-thorney-island/)**: Flat coastal sea wall, tidal harbour, salt marsh, sand dunes, beach, mudflats, tidal lake, and historic village high street. Sights: St Nicholas Church, Pilsey Island RSPB Reserve, Brook Meadow Local Nature Reserve, and [Chichester Harbour](http://www.conservancy.co.uk). Check tide timings. Lunch in Southbourne, or Emsworth. Southbourne lunch stop: the [Traveller's Joy](http://www.travellersjoyemsworth.co.uk/). Emsworth lunch stops: the [Lord Raglan](http://www.lord-raglan-emsworth.co.uk/), the [Ship Inn](http://www.shipinnemsworth.co.uk/), the [Driftwood Cafe](http://www.driftwood-cafe.co.uk/), the [Greenhouse Cafe](http://greenhousecafe.co.uk/), the [Coal Exchange](http://www.thecoalexchange.co.uk/), the [Blue Bell](http://www.bluebellinnemsworth.co.uk/), the [Crown Inn](http://www.thecrownemsworth.com/), or the [Railway Inn](http://www.therailwayemsworth.co.uk/). 15 km.
EMS.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Southbourne to Emsworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southbourne-to-emsworth-via-thorney-island/)**: Flat coastal sea wall, tidal harbour, salt marsh, sand dunes, beach, mudflats, tidal lake, and historic village high street. Sights: St Nicholas Church, Pilsey Island RSPB Reserve, Brook Meadow Local Nature Reserve, and [Chichester Harbour](http://www.conservancy.co.uk). Check tide timings. Lunch in Southbourne, or Emsworth. Southbourne lunch stop: the [Traveller's Joy](http://www.travellersjoyemsworth.co.uk/). Emsworth lunch stops: the [Lord Raglan](http://www.lord-raglan-emsworth.co.uk/), the [Ship Inn](http://www.shipinnemsworth.co.uk/), the [Driftwood Cafe](http://www.driftwood-cafe.co.uk/), the [Greenhouse Cafe](http://greenhousecafe.co.uk/), the [Coal Exchange](http://www.thecoalexchange.co.uk/), the [Blue Bell](http://www.bluebellinnemsworth.co.uk/), the [Crown Inn](http://www.thecrownemsworth.com/), or the [Railway Inn](http://www.therailwayemsworth.co.uk/). 15 km.

EPD.name: Epsom Downs
EPD.adminWalksAll:
**Merstham to Epsom Downs**: A variant of [Merstham to Tattenham Corner](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/merstham-to-tattenham-corner/). North Downs ridge, parkland, open heathland, racecourse downland, and quiet residential street to Epsom Downs station. Sights: [Gatton Park](https://gattonpark.co.uk), [Reigate Fort](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/reigate-hill-and-gatton-park/features/reigate-fort-at-reigate-hill-and-gatton-park), and [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets). Race days restrict access. Lunch in Mogador: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). 17 km.

**Tattenham Corner to Epsom Downs**: A variant of [Tattenham Corner Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tattenham-corner-circular/). Downland, racecourse gallops, WWI memorial woodland and wildflower meadows, heathland, golf course, and residential streets. Sights: [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets), [Langley Vale Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/langley-vale-wood), Regiment of Trees, Witness, Jutland Wood, Tadworth Windmill, and Banstead Heath. Can be muddy. Lunch in Walton on the Hill: the [Blue Ball](https://theblueballtadworth.co.uk/food-drink), the Village Café, or the [Bell](http://www.thebell-therat.co.uk). 19 km. 4.5 hours.
EPD.publicWalksS2SEnding:
**Merstham to Epsom Downs**: A variant of [Merstham to Tattenham Corner](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/merstham-to-tattenham-corner/). North Downs ridge, parkland, open heathland, racecourse downland, and quiet residential street to Epsom Downs station. Sights: [Gatton Park](https://gattonpark.co.uk), [Reigate Fort](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/reigate-hill-and-gatton-park/features/reigate-fort-at-reigate-hill-and-gatton-park), and [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets). Race days restrict access. Lunch in Mogador: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). 17 km.

**Tattenham Corner to Epsom Downs**: A variant of [Tattenham Corner Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tattenham-corner-circular/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Walton on the Hill: the [Blue Ball](https://theblueballtadworth.co.uk/food-drink), the Village Café, or the [Bell](http://www.thebell-therat.co.uk). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

EPS.name: Epsom
EPS.adminWalksAll:
**Epsom to Ewell West**: A shorter variant of [Chessington to Cheam](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chessington-to-cheam/). Ashtead Common, open heathland, woodland, and riverside meadows. Sights: Ashtead Common, [Hogsmill Local Nature Reserve](https://www.epsom-ewell.gov.uk/sites/default/files/documents/residents/venues-open-space-hire/parks-playgrounds/local-nature-reserves/Hogsmill%20Local%20Nature%20Reserve%20information%20June%202016_0.pdf), [St. Mary The Virgin](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1277324), and Ewell Spring. Lunch in Ewell: the [Spring Tavern](https://www.springtavern.co.uk/), the [Famous Green Man](https://www.famousgreenman.com/), the [Ewell Tap & Bottle Shop](https://www.ewelltap.co.uk), or the [Coffee Shop](http://www.bournehall.org/visit-us/coffee-shop). 13 km.

**Epsom to Cheam**: A longer variant of [Chessington to Cheam](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chessington-to-cheam/). Ashtead Common, open heathland, woodland, riverside meadows, farmland, and ancient parkland. Sights: Ashtead Common, [Hogsmill Local Nature Reserve](https://www.epsom-ewell.gov.uk/sites/default/files/documents/residents/venues-open-space-hire/parks-playgrounds/local-nature-reserves/Hogsmill%20Local%20Nature%20Reserve%20information%20June%202016_0.pdf), [Warren Farm](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/warren-farm/), [Nonsuch Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nonsuch_Park), [St. Mary The Virgin](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1277324), [Nonsuch Palace Gallery](http://www.friendsofnonsuch.co.uk/museum-and-gallery.html), [Whitehall](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/), [Lumley Chapel](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/index.php/136-2/), Nonsuch Mansion House, and Ewell Spring. Lunch in Ewell: the [Spring Tavern](https://www.springtavern.co.uk/), the [Famous Green Man](https://www.famousgreenman.com/), the [Ewell Tap & Bottle Shop](https://www.ewelltap.co.uk), the [Coffee Shop](http://www.bournehall.org/visit-us/coffee-shop), the [All Things Nice](https://www.allthingsnice-ewell.co.uk/index.html), the [Sonder Bakery](https://www.sonderbakery.com/), or the [Mandalina Patisserie](http://https://www.mandalinapatisserie.com/). 20 km.

**Merstham to Epsom**: A variant of [Merstham to Tattenham Corner](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/merstham-to-tattenham-corner/). North Downs ridge, parkland, open heathland, racecourse downland, and residential streets into Epsom town centre. Sights: [Gatton Park](https://gattonpark.co.uk), [Reigate Fort](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/reigate-hill-and-gatton-park/features/reigate-fort-at-reigate-hill-and-gatton-park), [Banstead Commons](https://bansteadcommons.org.uk), [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets), The Durdans, and Rosebery Park. Race days restrict access. Lunch in Mogador: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Oxshott to Epsom**: An alternative variant of [Oxshott to Ashtead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxshott-to-ashtead/). Heathland, commons, mixed woodland, grassy rides, and open woodland. Sights: Oxshott Heath, [Prince's Coverts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prince%27s_Coverts), [Ashtead Common](http://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/city-commons/ashtead-common/Pages/default.aspx), and [Epsom Common](http://www.epsomcommon.org.uk/Epsom_Common/epsom_common.html). Can be muddy. Lunch in Kingston Road, Leatherhead: the [Star](http://www.baronspubs.com/star/).
