ERI.name: Eridge
ERI.adminWalksAll:
**[Eridge Circular via Harrison's Rocks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eridge-circular/)**: Undulating High Weald, streams, meadows, sandstone outcrops, heathland, conifer-to-heathland nature reserve, and woods. Sights: [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Groombridge Place](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groombridge_Place), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), St Thomas the Apostle, St John the Evangelist, Groombridge station, Birchden Forge, and Wildwood Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch in Groombridge: the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrowngroombridge.com), or the Junction Inn. 16 km. 4 hours.

**Eridge Circular via Spa Valley Railway**: A shorter variant of [Eridge Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eridge-circular/). Undulating High Weald, streams, meadows, sandstone outcrops, heathland, and woods; omits Harrison's Rocks loop. Sights: [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Groombridge Place](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groombridge_Place), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), St Thomas the Apostle, St John the Evangelist, and Groombridge station. Can be muddy. Lunch in Groombridge: the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrowngroombridge.com), or the Junction Inn. 14 km.

**Eridge to East Grinstead**: A longer variant of [Eridge to Forest Row and East Grinstead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eridge-to-forest-row-and-east-grinstead/). Undulating High Weald countryside, clay paths, woods, fields, estate grounds, river valley, disused railway cycleway, and hilltop town streets. Sights: [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [High Weald Landscape Trail](https://highweald.org/things-to-do/high-weald-landscape-trail), Buckhurst Park, Penns in the Rocks, [St Michael and All Angels](https://www.withyhamchurch.org/history), Old Buckhurst, St Mary the Virgin, Lych Gate Cottage, [Pooh Corner](https://www.poohcorner.co.uk), [Tablehurst Farm](https://www.tablehurstfarm.org.uk), [Forest Way](https://www.eastsussex.gov.uk/leisureandtourism/countryside/walks/forestway), Brambletye House, Sackville College, St Swithun's Church, and [East Grinstead](http://www.westsussex.info/east-grinstead.shtml). Can be muddy. Lunch in Withyham, or Hartfield. Withyham lunch stop: the [Dorset Arms](https://www.dorset-arms.co.uk). Hartfield lunch stop: the [Bear Inn](https://www.thebearhartfield.com). 23 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Ashurst to Eridge via Speldhurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-to-eridge/)**: Low hills, steep-sided valleys, field edges, country lanes, wooded valleys, sandstone outcrops, heath, and meadows. Sights: [Speldhurst](https://www.speldhurst.org/history), [St Mary the Virgin](https://speldhurstchurch.org/about/building/history), [Groombridge Place](https://www.groombridgeplace.com), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), All Saints Church Langton Green, St John the Evangelist Groombridge, St Thomas the Apostle Groombridge, Ashurst Place, and Birchden Forge. Can be muddy. Lunch in Speldhurst: the [George & Dragon](https://ganddspeldhurst.com). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Cowden to Eridge via Pooh Corner](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cowden-to-eridge/)**: Weald pasture, woods, hills, undulating fields, and valley paths. Sights: Pooh Corner, Old Buckhurst, and Church of St Michael and All Angels. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hartfield, or Withyham. Hartfield lunch stop: the [Bear Inn](https://www.thebearhartfield.com/). Withyham lunch stop: the [Dorset Arms](https://www.dorset-arms.co.uk/). 21 km. 6 hours.

**Ashurst to Eridge via Spa Valley Railway & Harrison's Rocks**: An alternative variant of [Eridge Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eridge-circular/). High Weald valleys, bluebell woods, Burrswood estate, meadows, sandstone outcrops, heathland, and nature reserves. Sights: [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Groombridge Place](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groombridge_Place), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), Burrswood, St John the Evangelist, Groombridge station, and Birchden Forge. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Groombridge: the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrowngroombridge.com), or the Junction Inn. 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**Ashurst to Eridge via Groombridge Place & Harrison's Rocks**: An alternative variant of [Ashurst to Eridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-to-eridge/). Low hills, wooded valleys, field edges, country lanes, sandstone outcrops, and village greens. Sights: [Groombridge Place](https://www.groombridgeplace.com), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), All Saints Church Langton Green, St John the Evangelist Groombridge, St Thomas the Apostle Groombridge, and Birchden Forge. Can be muddy. Lunch in Langton Green: the [Hare](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/hare). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Ashurst to Eridge via Groombridge Place & Broadwater Warren**: A longer variant of [Ashurst to Eridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-to-eridge/). Low hills, wooded valleys, field edges, RSPB heathland reserve, sandstone outcrops, and nature reserves. Sights: [Groombridge Place](https://www.groombridgeplace.com), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), All Saints Church Langton Green, St John the Evangelist Groombridge, St Thomas the Apostle Groombridge, and Birchden Vineyards. Can be muddy. Lunch in Langton Green: the [Hare](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/hare). 17 km.

**Ashurst to Eridge via Speldhurst & Broadwater Warren**: A longer variant of [Ashurst to Eridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-to-eridge/). Low hills, steep-sided valleys, field edges, wooded valleys, RSPB heathland reserve, sandstone outcrops, and nature reserves. Sights: [Speldhurst](https://www.speldhurst.org/history), [St Mary the Virgin](https://speldhurstchurch.org/about/building/history), [Groombridge Place](https://www.groombridgeplace.com), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), All Saints Church Langton Green, St John the Evangelist Groombridge, St Thomas the Apostle Groombridge, Birchden Vineyards, Ashurst Place, and Birchden Forge. Can be muddy. Lunch in Speldhurst: the [George & Dragon](https://ganddspeldhurst.com). 20 km.
ERI.publicWalksCircular: **[Eridge Circular via Harrison's Rocks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eridge-circular/)**: Undulating High Weald, streams, meadows, sandstone outcrops, heathland, conifer-to-heathland nature reserve, and woods. Sights: [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Groombridge Place](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groombridge_Place), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), St Thomas the Apostle, St John the Evangelist, Groombridge station, Birchden Forge, and Wildwood Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch in Groombridge: the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrowngroombridge.com), or the Junction Inn. 16 km. 4 hours.
ERI.publicWalksS2SEnding:
**[Ashurst to Eridge via Speldhurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-to-eridge/)**: Low hills, steep-sided valleys, field edges, country lanes, wooded valleys, sandstone outcrops, heath, and meadows. Sights: [Speldhurst](https://www.speldhurst.org/history), [St Mary the Virgin](https://speldhurstchurch.org/about/building/history), [Groombridge Place](https://www.groombridgeplace.com), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), All Saints Church Langton Green, St John the Evangelist Groombridge, St Thomas the Apostle Groombridge, Ashurst Place, and Birchden Forge. Can be muddy. Lunch in Speldhurst: the [George & Dragon](https://ganddspeldhurst.com). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Cowden to Eridge via Pooh Corner](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cowden-to-eridge/)**: Weald pasture, woods, hills, undulating fields, and valley paths. Sights: Pooh Corner, Old Buckhurst, and Church of St Michael and All Angels. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hartfield, or Withyham. Hartfield lunch stop: the [Bear Inn](https://www.thebearhartfield.com/). Withyham lunch stop: the [Dorset Arms](https://www.dorset-arms.co.uk/). 21 km. 6 hours.

ETC.name: Etchingham
ETC.adminWalksAll:
**Etchingham Circular**: High Weald, rolling hills, woods, Sussex Border Path, and quiet farmland. Sights: [Bewl Water](http://www.bewl.co.uk). Can be muddy. Lunch in Three Leg Cross, or Robertsbridge. Three Leg Cross lunch stop: the Bull Inn. Robertsbridge lunch stop: the [Bistro at the station](http://www.bistroatthestation.co.uk/). A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 22 km.

**[Etchingham to Wadhurst via Ticehurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/etchingham-to-wadhurst-via-ticehurst/)**: Hilly Wealden pasture and woodland, panoramic valley views, converted oast houses, cobnut orchard, back lanes with wildflower verges, and reservoir shoreline. Sights: Bewl Water, Ticehurst village, and Wadhurst village. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Ticehurst, or Three Leg Cross. Ticehurst lunch stop: the Bell. Three Leg Cross lunch stop: the Bull Inn. 17 km.
ETC.publicWalksS2S: **[Etchingham to Wadhurst via Ticehurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/etchingham-to-wadhurst-via-ticehurst/)**: Hilly Wealden pasture and woodland, panoramic valley views, converted oast houses, cobnut orchard, back lanes with wildflower verges, and reservoir shoreline. Sights: Bewl Water, Ticehurst village, and Wadhurst village. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Ticehurst, or Three Leg Cross. Ticehurst lunch stop: the Bell. Three Leg Cross lunch stop: the Bull Inn. 17 km.

ETL.name: East Tilbury
ETL.adminWalksAll: **[Tilbury Town to East Tilbury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tilbury-to-east-tilbury-via-2-forts-way/)**: Flat Thames Estuary coast path, riverside sea wall, wild flower meadows, salt marsh views, former landfill landscape, and brief inland section. Sights: [Tilbury Fort](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tilbury_Fort), [Coalhouse Fort](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coalhouse_Fort), [Thurrock Thameside Nature Park](https://www.essexwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/thurrock-thameside), St Catherine's Church, and East Tilbury Radar Tower. Check tide timings. Lunch in Tilbury Fort, or Coalhouse Fort. Tilbury Fort lunch stop: the World's End. Coalhouse Fort lunch stop: the [Ship](https://theshipeasttilburyvillage.co.uk). 13 km.
ETL.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Tilbury Town to East Tilbury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tilbury-to-east-tilbury-via-2-forts-way/)**: Flat Thames Estuary coast path, riverside sea wall, wild flower meadows, salt marsh views, former landfill landscape, and brief inland section. Sights: [Tilbury Fort](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tilbury_Fort), [Coalhouse Fort](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coalhouse_Fort), [Thurrock Thameside Nature Park](https://www.essexwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/thurrock-thameside), St Catherine's Church, and East Tilbury Radar Tower. Check tide timings. Lunch in Tilbury Fort, or Coalhouse Fort. Tilbury Fort lunch stop: the World's End. Coalhouse Fort lunch stop: the [Ship](https://theshipeasttilburyvillage.co.uk). 13 km.

EVE.name: Evesham
EVE.adminWalksAll: **[Evesham to Pershore via Dumbleton and Bredon Hills](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/evesham-to-pershore-via-dumbleton-and-bredon-hills/)**: Vale of Evesham market gardens, river meadows, pastures, minor hills, country estate, woods, and Iron Age hillfort summit with panoramic views. Sights: Evesham Abbey Precinct, Dumbleton Hall, Bredon Hill Iron Age Hillfort, and Pershore Abbey. Can be muddy. Lunch in Ashton-Under-Hill: the [Star Inn](http://thestar-ashtonunderhill.co.uk/). 34 km. 8 hours.
EVE.publicWalksS2S: **[Evesham to Pershore via Dumbleton and Bredon Hills](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/evesham-to-pershore-via-dumbleton-and-bredon-hills/)**: Vale of Evesham market gardens, river meadows, pastures, minor hills, country estate, woods, and Iron Age hillfort summit with panoramic views. Sights: Evesham Abbey Precinct, Dumbleton Hall, Bredon Hill Iron Age Hillfort, and Pershore Abbey. Can be muddy. Lunch in Ashton-Under-Hill: the [Star Inn](http://thestar-ashtonunderhill.co.uk/). 34 km. 8 hours.

EWD.name: Earlswood
EWD.adminWalksAll: **[Earlswood Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-818)**: Farm, heathland, ancient woodland, river crossing, golf course, and suburban road. Sights: [River Cole](http://www.birminghamweare.com), [Clowes Wood & New Fallings Coppice](http://www.warwickshirewildlifetrust.org.uk), [Fulford Heath Golf Club](http://www.fulfordheathgolfclub.co.uk), and [Transport Museum Wythall](http://www.wythall.org.uk). Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Wythall: the [Wythall Fish Bar](http://www.facebook.com/WythallFB), the Select & Save, or the [Becketts Farm Shop and Restaurant](http://www.wythall-park.org.uk). 2 km. 1 hours.
EWD.publicWalksCircular: **[Earlswood Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-818)**: Farm, heathland, ancient woodland, river crossing, golf course, and suburban road. Sights: [River Cole](http://www.birminghamweare.com), [Clowes Wood & New Fallings Coppice](http://www.warwickshirewildlifetrust.org.uk), [Fulford Heath Golf Club](http://www.fulfordheathgolfclub.co.uk), and [Transport Museum Wythall](http://www.wythall.org.uk). Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Wythall: the [Wythall Fish Bar](http://www.facebook.com/WythallFB), the Select & Save, or the [Becketts Farm Shop and Restaurant](http://www.wythall-park.org.uk). 2 km. 1 hours.

EWW.name: Ewell West
EWW.adminWalksAll: **Epsom to Ewell West**: A shorter variant of [Chessington to Cheam](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chessington-to-cheam/). Ashtead Common, open heathland, woodland, and riverside meadows. Sights: Ashtead Common, [Hogsmill Local Nature Reserve](https://www.epsom-ewell.gov.uk/sites/default/files/documents/residents/venues-open-space-hire/parks-playgrounds/local-nature-reserves/Hogsmill%20Local%20Nature%20Reserve%20information%20June%202016_0.pdf), [St. Mary The Virgin](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1277324), and Ewell Spring. Lunch in Ewell: the [Spring Tavern](https://www.springtavern.co.uk/), the [Famous Green Man](https://www.famousgreenman.com/), the [Ewell Tap & Bottle Shop](https://www.ewelltap.co.uk), or the [Coffee Shop](http://www.bournehall.org/visit-us/coffee-shop). 13 km.
EWW.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Epsom to Ewell West**: A shorter variant of [Chessington to Cheam](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chessington-to-cheam/). Ashtead Common, open heathland, woodland, and riverside meadows. Sights: Ashtead Common, [Hogsmill Local Nature Reserve](https://www.epsom-ewell.gov.uk/sites/default/files/documents/residents/venues-open-space-hire/parks-playgrounds/local-nature-reserves/Hogsmill%20Local%20Nature%20Reserve%20information%20June%202016_0.pdf), [St. Mary The Virgin](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1277324), and Ewell Spring. Lunch in Ewell: the [Spring Tavern](https://www.springtavern.co.uk/), the [Famous Green Man](https://www.famousgreenman.com/), the [Ewell Tap & Bottle Shop](https://www.ewelltap.co.uk), or the [Coffee Shop](http://www.bournehall.org/visit-us/coffee-shop). 13 km.

EYN.name: Eynsford
EYN.privateNote: LON Trainline says 33m — London Bridge via Swanley (25 July)
EYN.adminWalksAll:
**[Eynsford Circular via Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eynsford-circular-via-otford/)**: Rambler favourite! Secluded valleys, chalk downland ridges, ancient woodland, golf course, chalk grassland nature reserves, riverside paths, and farmland. Sights: [Otford Solar System](https://www.otford.org/solarsystem), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/find-a-kent-country-park/lullingstone-country-park), [Eagle Heights](https://www.eagleheights.co.uk), [Lullingstone Castle](https://www.lullingstonecastle.co.uk), [Lullingstone Roman Villa](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/lullingstone-roman-villa), [Eynsford Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/eynsford-castle), and Otford Palace. Can be muddy. Lunch in Otford: the [Bull](https://thebull-otford.co.uk), or the [Olive Tree](https://hospicesofhope.co.uk/our-shops/#our-bistros). 21 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Eynsford Circular via Farningham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eynsford-circular-via-farningham/)**: Ancient woodlands, low hills, chalk grassland, riverside paths, open hillside, farm fields, and quiet villages in the Darent valley. Sights: [Lullingstone Roman Villa](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/lullingstone-roman-villa), [Lullingstone Castle](https://www.lullingstonecastle.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/lullingstone-country-park), [Eagle Heights](https://www.eagleheights.co.uk), [Eynsford Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/eynsford-castle), [Farningham Woods Nature Reserve](https://explorekent.org/activities/farningham-woods), Eynsford Viaduct, Tudor Gatehouse, St Botolph Lullingstone, St Martin of Tours, Franks Hall, Farningham Cattle Screen, Farningham Mill, and Ss Peter and Paul Farningham. Can be muddy. Lunch in Eynsford, Farningham, or Horton Kirby. Eynsford lunch stops: the [Plough Inn](https://www.theploughinneynsford.co.uk), the [Malt Shovel](https://themaltshoveleynsford.co.uk), the Five Bells, or the [Castle Hotel](https://www.castlehotelkent.com). Farningham lunch stops: the [Pied Bull](https://piedbullfarningham.co.uk), or the [Lion Hotel](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thelionfarningham). Horton Kirby lunch stop: the [Fighting Cocks](https://fightingcockshortonkirby.co.uk). 17 km. 4 hours.

**Eynsford to Shoreham**: A shorter variant of [Eynsford Circular via Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eynsford-circular-via-otford/). Rambler favourite! Secluded valleys, chalk downland ridges, woodland, chalk grassland nature reserves, and riverside village paths. Sights: [Otford Solar System](https://www.otford.org/solarsystem), Otford Palace, [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm), and [Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk). Can be muddy. Lunch in Otford: the [Bull](https://thebull-otford.co.uk), or the [Olive Tree](https://hospicesofhope.co.uk/our-shops/#our-bistros). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Otford to Eynsford**: River valley, open fields, orchards, beech woods, golf course, ridge path, and historic villages. Sights: St Bartholomew's Church, Otford Palace, Otford Solar System, Church of St Peter and St Paul, Shoreham Memorial Cross, Lullingstone Park Visitor Centre, [Lullingstone Castle](http://www.lullingstonecastle.co.uk/), [Lullingstone Roman Villa](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/lullingstone-roman-villa/), Eagle Heights Wildlife Foundation, [Eynsford Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/eynsford-castle/), St Martin's Church, and Eynsford Viaduct. Can be muddy. Lunch in Shoreham: the [King's Arms](https://www.facebook.com/thekingsarmsshoreham/), the [Crown](https://www.thecrownshoreham.co.uk/), or the [Samuel Palmer](https://thesamuelpalmer.com/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 4 hours.

From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 4 hours.

FAV.name: Faversham
FAV.adminWalksAll:
**[Faversham Circular via Uplees Marshes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/faversham-circular/)**: Flat marshland, creek embankments, farm fields, quiet country lanes, woodland, and tidal waterways. Sights: Faversham Guildhall, Shepherd Neame Brewery, St Mary of Charity, England Coast Path, Saxon Shore Way, TS Hazard, [Oare Gunpowder Works Country Park](https://oaregunpowderworks.co.uk), [St Mary Luddenham](https://www.visitchurches.org.uk/visit/church-listing/st-mary-luddenham.html), [Oare Marshes Nature Reserve](https://www.kentwildlifetrust.org.uk/nature-reserves/oare-marshes), Uplees Marshes, Harty Ferry, Oare Mill, St Mary Magdalene and St Lawrence, and Stonebridge Pond. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hollowshore, or Oare. Hollowshore lunch stop: the [Shipwright's Arms](http://www.theshipwrightsathollowshore.co.uk). Oare lunch stops: the [Castle](https://www.castleoarefaversham.co.uk), the [Three Mariners](https://www.threemarinersoare.co.uk), or the [Cafe by the Creek](https://www.thecafebythecreek.co.uk/cafe). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

**Faversham Circular via Faversham Guildhall & Oare Gunpowder Works Country Park**: A shorter variant of [Faversham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/faversham-circular/). Flat marshland, creek embankments, farm fields, quiet lanes, and omitting the Oare Marshes loop. Sights: Faversham Guildhall, Shepherd Neame Brewery, St Mary of Charity, England Coast Path, Saxon Shore Way, TS Hazard, [Oare Gunpowder Works Country Park](https://oaregunpowderworks.co.uk), [St Mary Luddenham](https://www.visitchurches.org.uk/visit/church-listing/st-mary-luddenham.html), Oare Mill, St Mary Magdalene and St Lawrence, and Stonebridge Pond. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hollowshore, or Oare. Hollowshore lunch stop: the [Shipwright's Arms](http://www.theshipwrightsathollowshore.co.uk). Oare lunch stops: the [Castle](https://www.castleoarefaversham.co.uk), the [Three Mariners](https://www.threemarinersoare.co.uk), or the [Cafe by the Creek](https://www.thecafebythecreek.co.uk/cafe). 14 km. 3 hours.

**Faversham Circular via Faversham Guildhall & Oare Marshes Nature Reserve**: A variant of [Faversham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/faversham-circular/). Flat marshland, creek embankments, farm fields, quiet country lanes, and bypassing Gunpowder Works woodland. Sights: Faversham Guildhall, Shepherd Neame Brewery, St Mary of Charity, England Coast Path, Saxon Shore Way, TS Hazard, [St Mary Luddenham](https://www.visitchurches.org.uk/visit/church-listing/st-mary-luddenham.html), [Oare Marshes Nature Reserve](https://www.kentwildlifetrust.org.uk/nature-reserves/oare-marshes), Harty Ferry, Oare Mill, St Mary Magdalene and St Lawrence, and Stonebridge Pond. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hollowshore, or Oare. Hollowshore lunch stop: the [Shipwright's Arms](http://www.theshipwrightsathollowshore.co.uk). Oare lunch stops: the [Castle](https://www.castleoarefaversham.co.uk), the [Three Mariners](https://www.threemarinersoare.co.uk), or the [Cafe by the Creek](https://www.thecafebythecreek.co.uk/cafe). 17 km.

**[Faversham to Whitstable](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/faversham-to-whitstable/)**: Easy coastal walk, flat terrain, seaside villages, and open shoreline. Lunch in Seasalter: the [Sportsman](http://www.thesportsmanseasalter.co.uk/), or the [Poppet's Café & Tea Room](http://www.poppetscafeseasalter.com/). 15 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Teynham to Faversham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/teynham-to-faversham/)**: Flat orchards and fruit farms, tidal river seawall, coastal creeks, mudflats, salt marshes, drainage ditches, dykes, windswept grazing marshes, and historic market town streets. Sights: Oare Marshes Nature Reserve and Faversham Market Place. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lynsted, Lewson Street, Conyer, or Oare. Lynsted lunch stop: the [Black Lion](https://blacklionlynsted.co.uk/). Lewson Street lunch stop: the [Plough Inn](http://www.ploughinnlewsonst.co.uk/). Conyer lunch stop: the [Ship Inn at Conyer](https://www.theshipinnconyerquay.co.uk/). Oare lunch stops: the [Three Mariners at Oare](https://www.threemarinersoare.co.uk/), or the [Castle Inn](https://www.castleoarefaversham.co.uk/). 24 km. 5.5 hours.
FAV.publicWalksS2S: **[Faversham to Whitstable](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/faversham-to-whitstable/)**: Easy coastal walk, flat terrain, seaside villages, and open shoreline. Lunch in Seasalter: the [Sportsman](http://www.thesportsmanseasalter.co.uk/), or the [Poppet's Café & Tea Room](http://www.poppetscafeseasalter.com/). 15 km. 4.5 hours.
FAV.publicWalksCircular: **[Faversham Circular via Uplees Marshes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/faversham-circular/)**: Flat marshland, creek embankments, farm fields, quiet country lanes, woodland, and tidal waterways. Sights: Faversham Guildhall, Shepherd Neame Brewery, St Mary of Charity, England Coast Path, Saxon Shore Way, TS Hazard, [Oare Gunpowder Works Country Park](https://oaregunpowderworks.co.uk), [St Mary Luddenham](https://www.visitchurches.org.uk/visit/church-listing/st-mary-luddenham.html), [Oare Marshes Nature Reserve](https://www.kentwildlifetrust.org.uk/nature-reserves/oare-marshes), Uplees Marshes, Harty Ferry, Oare Mill, St Mary Magdalene and St Lawrence, and Stonebridge Pond. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hollowshore, or Oare. Hollowshore lunch stop: the [Shipwright's Arms](http://www.theshipwrightsathollowshore.co.uk). Oare lunch stops: the [Castle](https://www.castleoarefaversham.co.uk), the [Three Mariners](https://www.threemarinersoare.co.uk), or the [Cafe by the Creek](https://www.thecafebythecreek.co.uk/cafe). 19 km. 4.5 hours.
FAV.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Teynham to Faversham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/teynham-to-faversham/)**: Flat orchards and fruit farms, tidal river seawall, coastal creeks, mudflats, salt marshes, drainage ditches, dykes, windswept grazing marshes, and historic market town streets. Sights: Oare Marshes Nature Reserve and Faversham Market Place. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lynsted, Lewson Street, Conyer, or Oare. Lynsted lunch stop: the [Black Lion](https://blacklionlynsted.co.uk/). Lewson Street lunch stop: the [Plough Inn](http://www.ploughinnlewsonst.co.uk/). Conyer lunch stop: the [Ship Inn at Conyer](https://www.theshipinnconyerquay.co.uk/). Oare lunch stops: the [Three Mariners at Oare](https://www.threemarinersoare.co.uk/), or the [Castle Inn](https://www.castleoarefaversham.co.uk/). 24 km. 5.5 hours.

FKC.name: Folkestone Central
FKC.adminWalksAll:
**Folkestone Circular via Castle Hill**: Rambler favourite! Verdant coastal cliffs, rolling hills, and suburban streets. Begins, necessarily, with a lengthy suburban segment, but then becomes very beautiful and surprisingly quiet. Particularly lovely on the walk along the cliffs east of Folkestone, where there are also a number of lunch spot choices. The narrow overgrown paths are best. But note that there is a LOT of steep ascents and descents on this walk. *Last reviewed 27 April 2025.* 16 km. 370 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2913988093)**

**Folkestone Central Circular**: A shorter variant of [Folkestone to Dover](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/folkestone-to-dover/). Rambler favourite! Coastal seafront, Folkestone Warren sea defences, steep cliff climb, and cliff-top return with Channel views. Sights: Battle of Britain Memorial, Cliff Top Cafe, and Martello Towers. Cracked sea defences. Lunch in Folkestone Warren, or Folkestone. Folkestone Warren lunch stop: the Royal Oak. Folkestone lunch stop: the Cliff Top Cafe. 13 km.

**[Folkestone Central to Dover Priory via Martello Towers](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/folkestone-to-dover/)**: Rambler favourite! Coastal cliffs, chalk downland, sea defences, wild landslip area, wartime installations, ridge between valley and sea, and town crossing. Sights: Martello Towers, Folkestone Warren, World War Two sound mirror, and Western Heights. Cracked sea defences. Lunch in Folkestone Warren: the Royal Oak. 14 km.

**Folkestone Central to Dover Priory via Battle of Britain Memorial**: An alternative variant of [Folkestone to Dover](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/folkestone-to-dover/). Rambler favourite! Cliff-top path, panoramic Channel views, wartime installations, and chalk downland. Sights: Battle of Britain Memorial, Cliff Top Cafe, and World War Two sound mirror. Lunch in Folkestone, or Folkestone Warren. Folkestone lunch stop: the Cliff Top Cafe. Folkestone Warren lunch stop: the Royal Oak. 15 km.

**[Sandling to Folkestone Central via Folkestone Warren Halt railway station](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-or-dover/)**: Rambler favourite! Dismantled railway, chalky escarpment ridge, steep climbs, open downland with Channel views, cliff top promenade, shingle beach, and historic harbour. Sights: [Folkestone](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folkestone), [Sandgate Branch](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandgate_Branch), [Elham Valley Railway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elham_Valley_Railway), [Folkestone Warren Halt railway station](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folkestone_Warren_Halt_railway_station), Folkestone Harbour Arm, and Battle of Britain Memorial. Steep climbs near start. Lunch in Etchinghill: the [Gatekeeper Inn](http://www.gatekeeperinn.co.uk/). 17 km.

**[Sandling to Folkestone Central via the Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-via-the-sea/)**: Disused railway trackbed, orchards, fields, open downland, ridge with sea views, shingle beach, traffic-free seafront promenade, and cliff gardens. Sights: Saltwood Castle, Sandgate Castle, Folkestone Harbour, Mermaid Beach, and Sunny Sands. Two steep hill climbs. Best in Jul–Sep. Lunch in Sandgate, or Folkestone. Sandgate lunch stops: the Earl of Clarendon, the Famous Ship Inn, or the Boat House. Folkestone lunch stops: the Mermaids Cafe Bar, or the HatHats Coffee. 11 km.

**Sandling to Folkestone Central via White Cliffs of Dover**: A variant of [Sandling to Folkestone or Dover](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-or-dover/). Rambler favourite! Escarpment ridge, steep climbs, open downland, White Cliffs cliff-top path, Channel views, and beach and harbour. Sights: [Folkestone](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folkestone), [Sandgate Branch](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandgate_Branch), [Elham Valley Railway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elham_Valley_Railway), Battle of Britain Memorial, White Cliffs of Dover, and Folkestone Harbour Arm. Steep climbs near start. Lunch in Etchinghill: the [Gatekeeper Inn](http://www.gatekeeperinn.co.uk/). 20 km.

**Sandling to Folkestone Central via the Sea & Harbour Arm**: A longer variant of [Sandling to Folkestone via the Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-via-the-sea/). Disused railway trackbed, orchards, fields, open downland, shingle beach, seafront promenade, refurbished harbour area, historic old streets, and clifftop esplanade. Sights: Saltwood Castle, Sandgate Castle, Folkestone Harbour, Harbour Arm, Mermaid Beach, and Sunny Sands. Two steep hill climbs. Best in Jul–Sep. Lunch in Sandgate, or Folkestone. Sandgate lunch stops: the Earl of Clarendon, the Famous Ship Inn, or the Boat House. Folkestone lunch stops: the Mermaids Cafe Bar, the HatHats Coffee, the Harbour Arm, or the Steep Street Coffee House. 13 km.
FKC.publicWalksCircular: **Folkestone Circular via Castle Hill**: Rambler favourite! Verdant coastal cliffs, rolling hills, and suburban streets. Begins, necessarily, with a lengthy suburban segment, but then becomes very beautiful and surprisingly quiet. Particularly lovely on the walk along the cliffs east of Folkestone, where there are also a number of lunch spot choices. The narrow overgrown paths are best. But note that there is a LOT of steep ascents and descents on this walk. 16 km. 370 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2913988093)**

FKW.name: Folkestone West
FKW.adminWalksAll:
**Sandling to Folkestone West**: A shorter variant of [Sandling to Folkestone or Dover](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-or-dover/). Rambler favourite! Dismantled railway, chalky escarpment ridge, steep climbs, open downland, and long pavement finish. Sights: [Sandgate Branch](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandgate_Branch) and [Elham Valley Railway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elham_Valley_Railway). Long pavement to station. Lunch in Etchinghill: the [Gatekeeper Inn](http://www.gatekeeperinn.co.uk/). 11 km.

**Wye to Folkestone West via NDW**: A longer variant of [Sandling to Wye](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-wye/). North Downs ridge, valley, disused railway, spectacular Channel views, and road walking at end. Sights: Channel Tunnel Terminal. Road walking at end.

FLE.name: Fleet
FLE.adminWalksAll:
**[Fleet Circular via Tweseldown Racecourse](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/fleet-circular/)**: Heathland, woodland, military training lands, levada water channel, canal towpath, residential outskirts, and nature reserves. Sights: [Fleet Pond Nature Reserve](http://fleetpond.org.uk/history-of-the-pond), [Basingstoke Canal](https://basingstoke-canal.org.uk), Tweseldown Racecourse, Beacon Hill levada, Zebon Copse Local Nature Reserve, Naishes Lane SANGS, and Basingbourne Heath. MoD closures apply. Lunch in Fleet, Tweseldown, Ewshot, or Church Crookham. Fleet lunch stop: the [Foresters](https://foresters-dining.co.uk). Tweseldown lunch stop: the [Tweseldown](https://www.thetweseldown.co.uk/index). Ewshot lunch stop: the Windmill. Church Crookham lunch stop: the [Fox & Hounds](http://www.foxandhoundscc.co.uk). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

**Fleet Circular via Fleet Pond Nature Reserve**: An alternative variant of [Fleet Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/fleet-circular/). Canal towpath, woodland edge, residential streets, nature reserve, and housing development. Sights: [Fleet Pond Nature Reserve](http://fleetpond.org.uk/history-of-the-pond), [Basingstoke Canal](https://basingstoke-canal.org.uk), Zebon Copse Local Nature Reserve, and Basingbourne Heath. MoD closures apply. Lunch in Church Crookham: the [Fox & Hounds](http://www.foxandhoundscc.co.uk). 15 km.

**Farnham to Fleet**: A shorter variant of [Farnham to Blackwater](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farnham-to-blackwater/). Rambler favourite! Deer park, heathland, MOD training area, Iron Age hillfort, reservoirs, stream valleys, woodland, and Fleet Pond nature reserve. Sights: Caesar's Camp, Fleet Pond Nature Reserve, and Farnham Park. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Church Crookham: the [Foresters](https://www.foresters-dining.co.uk/). 15 km. 3 hours.
FLE.publicWalksCircular: **[Fleet Circular via Tweseldown Racecourse](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/fleet-circular/)**: Heathland, woodland, military training lands, levada water channel, canal towpath, residential outskirts, and nature reserves. Sights: [Fleet Pond Nature Reserve](http://fleetpond.org.uk/history-of-the-pond), [Basingstoke Canal](https://basingstoke-canal.org.uk), Tweseldown Racecourse, Beacon Hill levada, Zebon Copse Local Nature Reserve, Naishes Lane SANGS, and Basingbourne Heath. MoD closures apply. Lunch in Fleet, Tweseldown, Ewshot, or Church Crookham. Fleet lunch stop: the [Foresters](https://foresters-dining.co.uk). Tweseldown lunch stop: the [Tweseldown](https://www.thetweseldown.co.uk/index). Ewshot lunch stop: the Windmill. Church Crookham lunch stop: the [Fox & Hounds](http://www.foxandhoundscc.co.uk). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

FLT.name: Flitwick
FLT.adminWalksAll:
**[Flitwick Circular via Old St. Mary's Church](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/flitwick-circular/)**: River valley, wooded wetland fringes, quiet villages, rolling fields, escarpment ridge, nature reserve lakes, and ancient woodland. Sights: De Grey Mausoleum, Wrest House and Gardens, Clophill Lakes Nature Reserve, Old St. Mary's Church, Ailesbury Mausoleum & Crypt, Cainhoe Castle, Flitwick Moor, and Maulden Wood. Can be muddy. Lunch in Clophill: the Stone Jug, or the [Flying Horse](http://www.theflyinghorse.co.uk/). 25 km. 5.5 hours.

**Flitwick Circular via De Grey Mausoleum & Clophill Lakes Nature Reserve**: A shorter variant of [Flitwick Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/flitwick-circular/). River valley, wooded wetland fringes, quiet villages, rolling fields, and nature reserve lakes. Sights: De Grey Mausoleum, Wrest House and Gardens, Clophill Lakes Nature Reserve, and Flitwick Moor. Lunch in Clophill: the Stone Jug, or the [Flying Horse](http://www.theflyinghorse.co.uk/). 18 km.

**[Lidlington to Flitwick](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lidlington-to-flitwick/)**: Undulating Greensand Ridge, mixed woods, open parkland, heathland, historic market town, ancient woodland, and flat moors and wetland nature reserve. Sights: Cooper's Hill Nature Reserve, Houghton House, Ampthill historic centre, Ailesbury Mausoleum & Crypt, Flitwick Moor, and King's Wood. Can be muddy. 22 km. 5 hours.

**[Harlington to Flitwick](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/harlington-to-flitwick/)**: Steep rolling downlands, chalk escarpments, hilltop views, grassy fields, pastures, and ancient woodland. Sights: Sundon Hills, Sharpenhoe Clappers, Sharpenhoe Neolithic Hill Fort, and Flitwick Wood. Can be hilly. Lunch in Pulloxhill, or Westoning. Pulloxhill lunch stop: the [Cross Keys](http://www.thecrosskeys.co.uk/). Westoning lunch stops: the [Chequers](http://thechequerswestoning.co.uk/), or the [Bell](https://www.thebellwestoning.co.uk/). 24 km. 5 hours.
FLT.publicWalksCircular: **[Flitwick Circular via Old St. Mary's Church](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/flitwick-circular/)**: River valley, wooded wetland fringes, quiet villages, rolling fields, escarpment ridge, nature reserve lakes, and ancient woodland. Sights: De Grey Mausoleum, Wrest House and Gardens, Clophill Lakes Nature Reserve, Old St. Mary's Church, Ailesbury Mausoleum & Crypt, Cainhoe Castle, Flitwick Moor, and Maulden Wood. Can be muddy. Lunch in Clophill: the Stone Jug, or the [Flying Horse](http://www.theflyinghorse.co.uk/). 25 km. 5.5 hours.

FLX.name: Felixstowe
FLX.privateNote: Trainline says 1h54m — Liverpool St via Ipswich
FLX.adminWalksAll: **[Wickham Market to Felixstowe](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wickham-market-to-felixstowe/)**: Farmland, Suffolk heathland (Sandlings), river bank, grassy sea wall, shingle beach, isolated coastline, and urban Felixstowe approaches. Sights: Sandlings Walk, Suffolk Coast Path, Havergate Island RSPB Reserve, Shingle Street, Bawdsey Manor, Orford Ness, Martello Towers, Hollesley Marshes Nature Reserve, and Butley Ferry. Long shingle sections. Lunch in Tunstall, or Chillesford. Tunstall lunch stop: the [Green Man](http://www.greenmantunstall.co.uk/). Chillesford lunch stop: the [Froize Freehouse](http://froize.co.uk/). 35 km.
FLX.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Wickham Market to Felixstowe](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wickham-market-to-felixstowe/)**: Farmland, Suffolk heathland (Sandlings), river bank, grassy sea wall, shingle beach, isolated coastline, and urban Felixstowe approaches. Sights: Sandlings Walk, Suffolk Coast Path, Havergate Island RSPB Reserve, Shingle Street, Bawdsey Manor, Orford Ness, Martello Towers, Hollesley Marshes Nature Reserve, and Butley Ferry. Long shingle sections. Lunch in Tunstall, or Chillesford. Tunstall lunch stop: the [Green Man](http://www.greenmantunstall.co.uk/). Chillesford lunch stop: the [Froize Freehouse](http://froize.co.uk/). 35 km.

FMR.name: Falmer
FMR.adminWalksAll: **Hassocks to Falmer**: A shorter variant of [Hassocks to Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hassocks-to-brighton/). South Downs valleys, open chalk downland, ancient woods, parkland, and university campus. Sights: St Margaret's Church, Ditchling Beacon, Ditchling Beacon Nature Reserve, Stanmer House, Stanmer Church, University of Sussex, Swan Inn, and St Laurence Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Stanmer, Falmer, or Falmer South. Stanmer lunch stops: the Stanmer House, or the Stanmer Tea Rooms. Falmer lunch stop: the Swan Inn. Falmer South lunch stops: the Falmer Cafe, or the Park Farm Shop. 12 km. 3.5 hours.
FMR.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Hassocks to Falmer**: A shorter variant of [Hassocks to Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hassocks-to-brighton/). South Downs valleys, open chalk downland, ancient woods, parkland, and university campus. Sights: St Margaret's Church, Ditchling Beacon, Ditchling Beacon Nature Reserve, Stanmer House, Stanmer Church, University of Sussex, Swan Inn, and St Laurence Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Stanmer, Falmer, or Falmer South. Stanmer lunch stops: the Stanmer House, or the Stanmer Tea Rooms. Falmer lunch stop: the Swan Inn. Falmer South lunch stops: the Falmer Cafe, or the Park Farm Shop. 12 km. 3.5 hours.

FNC.name: Farncombe
FNC.adminWalksAll: **Milford to Farncombe**: Sandy bridleways, sunken footpaths, woodland, open fields, arboretum lakes, and canal towpath. Sights: Enton Mill, Winkworth Arboretum, and Farncombe Boat House. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Hambledon, or Hascombe. Hambledon lunch stop: the Merry Harriers. Hascombe lunch stop: the White Horse. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).
FNC.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Milford to Farncombe**: Sandy bridleways, sunken footpaths, woodland, open fields, arboretum lakes, and canal towpath. Sights: Enton Mill, Winkworth Arboretum, and Farncombe Boat House. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Hambledon, or Hascombe. Hambledon lunch stop: the Merry Harriers. Hascombe lunch stop: the White Horse. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).

FNH.name: Farnham
FNH.adminWalksAll:
**Farnham to Godalming**: Rambler favourite! River valley, forest tracks, sandy bridleways, open parkland, country houses, and wooded valleys. Sights: [Waverley Abbey](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/waverley-abbey/), Moor Park House, Church of St Nicholas, and Farnham Castle. Can be muddy. Lunch in Tilford: the Barley Mow. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 18 km. 180 m uphill. 5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920859171)**

**Farnham Circular**: Pretty valley, wooded heathland, and village green at Tilford. Sights: Puttenham Common. Lunch in Charleshill: the Donkey. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km.

**[Farnham to Blackwater](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farnham-to-blackwater/)**: Rambler favourite! Deer park, heathland, MOD training area, Iron Age hillfort, reservoirs, stream valleys, woodland, nature reserve, wet meadows, lakeside sandy shores, and common. Sights: Caesar's Camp, Fleet Pond Nature Reserve, Farnham Park, and Foxlease Meadows. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Church Crookham: the [Foresters](https://www.foresters-dining.co.uk/). 23 km. 5.5 hours.

**Farnham to Aldershot**: A shorter variant of [Farnham to Blackwater](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farnham-to-blackwater/). Rambler favourite! Deer park, heathland, MOD training area, Iron Age hillfort, copse, and urban parks. Sights: Caesar's Camp and Farnham Park. MOD closures apply. 11 km.

**Farnham to Fleet**: A shorter variant of [Farnham to Blackwater](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farnham-to-blackwater/). Rambler favourite! Deer park, heathland, MOD training area, Iron Age hillfort, reservoirs, stream valleys, woodland, and Fleet Pond nature reserve. Sights: Caesar's Camp, Fleet Pond Nature Reserve, and Farnham Park. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Church Crookham: the [Foresters](https://www.foresters-dining.co.uk/). 15 km. 3 hours.

**[Bentley to Farnham via Devil's Jumps](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bentley-to-farnham/)**: Rambler favourite! Ancient forests, heathland ridges, Scots pine, heather, large ponds with swimming, remote commons, and quiet woodland paths and lanes. Sights: Alice Holt Forest, Frensham Great Pond, Devil's Jumps, Hankley Common D-Day training site, and Waverley Abbey. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Churt, or Tilford. Churt lunch stop: the [Bel and the Dragon](https://belandthedragon-churt.co.uk/). Tilford lunch stop: the [Barley Mow](http://www.thebarleymowtilford.com/). 26 km. 6 hours.

**Guildford to Farnham via Loseley Park**: River valley, gentle pastures, woods, remote heathlands, heather-covered moors, and sandy Greensand soils. Sights: Loseley Park, Watts Gallery, Watts Chapel, and Puttenham Common. Can be muddy. Best in Jul–Sep. Lunch in Compton, or Puttenham. Compton lunch stop: the Watts Gallery tea shop. Puttenham lunch stop: the Good Intent. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 22 km.

**[Haslemere to Farnham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haslemere-to-farnham/)**: Wooded ridge, heathland valleys, lowland peat bog, large commons, MOD terrain, quiet woodland paths and lanes, and river valley. Sights: Temple of the Four Winds, Gibbet Hill, Devil's Punch Bowl, Thursley Common, Hankley Common, Atlantic Wall, and Waverley Abbey. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Hindhead, Thursley, or Tilford. Hindhead lunch stops: the [Devil's Punch Bowl Café](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/hindhead-commons-and-the-devils-punch-bowl), or the [Devil's Punchbowl Hotel](http://www.devilspunchbowlhotel.co.uk/). Thursley lunch stop: the [Three Horseshoes](http://www.threehorseshoesthursley.com/). Tilford lunch stops: the [Duke of Cambridge](http://www.dukeofcambridgetilford.co.uk/), or the [Barley Mow](http://www.thebarleymowtilford.com/). 23 km. 5.5 hours.

**Bentley to Farnham via Frensham Little Pond**: A shorter variant of [Bentley to Farnham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bentley-to-farnham/). Rambler favourite! Ancient forests, heathland, Scots pine, large ponds with swimming, woodland paths and lanes, and skipping Devil's Jumps and Hankley Common. Sights: Alice Holt Forest, Frensham Great Pond, and Frensham Little Pond. Lunch in Churt, or Tilford. Churt lunch stop: the [Bel and the Dragon](https://belandthedragon-churt.co.uk/). Tilford lunch stop: the [Barley Mow](http://www.thebarleymowtilford.com/). 19 km.

**Bentley to Farnham via Waverley Abbey**: An alternative variant of [Bentley to Farnham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bentley-to-farnham/). Rambler favourite! Greensand Way, River Wey, optional out-and-back to Waverley Abbey, and cuts steep ascents near Farnham. Sights: Waverley Abbey. Lunch in Tilford: the [Barley Mow](http://www.thebarleymowtilford.com/).

**Guildford to Farnham via Hog's Back**: Dramatic ridge of the Hog's Back, heathlands, woods, moors, and with magnificent views north and south. Sights: Hog's Back, Watts Gallery, Watts Chapel, and Puttenham Common. Steep; more strenuous. Lunch in Compton, or Puttenham. Compton lunch stop: the Watts Gallery tea shop. Puttenham lunch stop: the Good Intent. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 21 km.
FNH.publicWalksS2S: **Farnham to Godalming**: Rambler favourite! River valley, forest tracks, sandy bridleways, open parkland, country houses, and wooded valleys. Sights: [Waverley Abbey](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/waverley-abbey/), Moor Park House, Church of St Nicholas, and Farnham Castle. Can be muddy. Lunch in Tilford: the Barley Mow. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 18 km. 180 m uphill. 5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920859171)**

FNR.name: Farningham Road
FNR.adminWalksAll:
**Farningham Road Circular via Farningham Woods Nature Reserve**: A variant of [Farningham Road to Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farningham-road-to-otford/). Ancient woodland, riverside Darent Valley Path, Eynsford loop, village of Horton Kirby, and recreation ground. Sights: [Farningham Woods Nature Reserve](https://explorekent.org/activities/farningham-woods), [Darent Valley Path](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpdarentvalleypath.html), [Eynsford Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/eynsford-castle), Farningham Cattle Screen, Farningham Mill, and 100 Year Wood. Motocross path closures possible. Lunch in Farningham, or Eynsford. Farningham lunch stops: the [Lion Hotel](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thelionfarningham), or the [Pied Bull](https://piedbullfarningham.co.uk). Eynsford lunch stops: the [Castle Hotel](https://www.castlehotelkent.com), the [Malt Shovel](https://themaltshoveleynsford.co.uk), or the [Plough Inn](https://www.theploughinneynsford.co.uk). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**Farningham Road Circular via Darent Valley Path**: A shorter variant of [Farningham Road to Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farningham-road-to-otford/). Direct route from station, riverside Darent Valley Path, Eynsford loop, and village of Horton Kirby. Sights: [Darent Valley Path](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpdarentvalleypath.html), [Eynsford Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/eynsford-castle), Farningham Cattle Screen, and 100 Year Wood. Lunch in Farningham: the [Lion Hotel](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thelionfarningham), or the [Pied Bull](https://piedbullfarningham.co.uk). 11 km. 2.5 hours.

**Farningham Road Circular via Nine Hole Wood**: An alternative variant of [Farningham Road to Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farningham-road-to-otford/). Ancient woodland, riverside Darent Valley Path, Farningham village, Sparepenny Lane hillside, and Horton Kirby. Sights: [Farningham Woods Nature Reserve](https://explorekent.org/activities/farningham-woods), [Darent Valley Path](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpdarentvalleypath.html), Farningham Cattle Screen, Farningham Mill, Nine Hole Wood, and 100 Year Wood. Motocross path closures possible. Lunch in Farningham: the [Lion Hotel](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thelionfarningham), or the [Pied Bull](https://piedbullfarningham.co.uk). 10 km. 2.5 hours.

**[Farningham Road to Sole Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farningham-road-to-sole-street/)**: Urban sprawl, housing developments, busy roads, arable fields, and occasional glimpses of historic rural Kent. Sights: Wheatsheaf pub and Chapter House Farm. Lunch in Southfleet, or Sole Street. Southfleet lunch stops: the [Ship Inn](https://www.shipatsouthfleet.co.uk/), or the [Manor Farm Barn](https://www.manorfarmbarnsouthfleet.co.uk/). Sole Street lunch stop: the [Railway Inn](http://www.railwayinnsolestreet.co.uk/). 17 km.

**[Farningham Road to Otford via South Darenth Viaduct](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farningham-road-to-otford/)**: Ancient woodland, field edges, riverside Darent Valley Path, low hills, chalk downland, quaint villages, golf course, and nature reserves. Sights: [Farningham Woods Nature Reserve](https://explorekent.org/activities/farningham-woods), [Darent Valley Path](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpdarentvalleypath.html), [Eynsford Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/eynsford-castle), [Eagle Heights](https://www.eagleheights.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/lullingstone-country-park), South Darenth Viaduct, Franks Hall, Farningham Cattle Screen, Farningham Mill, Ss Peter and Paul, Farningham, St Martin of Tours, Lullingstone Castle, [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm), Ss Peter and Paul, Shoreham, The Mount Vineyard, White Hill Nature Reserve, Fackenden Down, Otford Solar System, Otford Pond, St Bartholomew, Otford, Otford Palace, Kings Arms, and Water House. Motocross path closures possible. Lunch in Farningham, or Eynsford. Farningham lunch stops: the [Lion Hotel](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thelionfarningham), or the [Pied Bull](https://piedbullfarningham.co.uk). Eynsford lunch stops: the [Castle Hotel](https://www.castlehotelkent.com), the [Malt Shovel](https://themaltshoveleynsford.co.uk), or the [Plough Inn](https://www.theploughinneynsford.co.uk). 20 km. 5 hours.

**Farningham Road to Shoreham via Darent Valley Path**: A shorter variant of [Farningham Road to Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farningham-road-to-otford/). Direct route from station, riverside Darent Valley Path, low hills, and quaint village of Shoreham. Sights: [Darent Valley Path](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpdarentvalleypath.html), [Eagle Heights](https://www.eagleheights.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/lullingstone-country-park), Lullingstone Castle, [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm), and Ss Peter and Paul, Shoreham. Lunch in Farningham: the [Lion Hotel](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thelionfarningham), or the [Pied Bull](https://piedbullfarningham.co.uk). 13 km. 3 hours.

**Farningham Road to Shoreham via Farningham Woods Nature Reserve**: A shorter variant of [Farningham Road to Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farningham-road-to-otford/). Ancient woodland, riverside Darent Valley Path, low hills, and quaint village of Shoreham. Sights: [Farningham Woods Nature Reserve](https://explorekent.org/activities/farningham-woods), [Darent Valley Path](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpdarentvalleypath.html), [Eynsford Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/eynsford-castle), [Eagle Heights](https://www.eagleheights.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/lullingstone-country-park), Lullingstone Castle, [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm), Ss Peter and Paul, Shoreham, and The Mount Vineyard. Motocross path closures possible. Lunch in Farningham: the [Lion Hotel](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thelionfarningham), or the [Pied Bull](https://piedbullfarningham.co.uk). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Farningham Road to Otford via Darent Valley Path**: A shorter variant of [Farningham Road to Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farningham-road-to-otford/). Direct route from station, riverside Darent Valley Path, low hills, chalk downland, and quaint villages. Sights: [Darent Valley Path](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpdarentvalleypath.html), [Eynsford Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/eynsford-castle), [Eagle Heights](https://www.eagleheights.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/lullingstone-country-park), Lullingstone Castle, [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm), White Hill Nature Reserve, Fackenden Down, Otford Solar System, and Otford Palace. Lunch in Farningham: the [Lion Hotel](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thelionfarningham), or the [Pied Bull](https://piedbullfarningham.co.uk). 17 km. 4 hours.

**Farningham Road to Otford via Farningham Woods Nature Reserve**: A variant of [Farningham Road to Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farningham-road-to-otford/). Ancient woodland, riverside Darent Valley Path, low hills, golf course, and valley floor route to Otford. Sights: [Farningham Woods Nature Reserve](https://explorekent.org/activities/farningham-woods), [Darent Valley Path](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpdarentvalleypath.html), [Eynsford Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/eynsford-castle), [Eagle Heights](https://www.eagleheights.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/lullingstone-country-park), Lullingstone Castle, Otford Solar System, and Otford Palace. Motocross path closures possible. Lunch in Farningham: the [Lion Hotel](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thelionfarningham), or the [Pied Bull](https://piedbullfarningham.co.uk). 18 km.

FOH.name: Forest Hill
FOH.adminWalksAll:
**[Crystal Palace to Forest Hill via Dulwich Picture Gallery](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/crystal-palace-to-forest-hill/)**: Suburban parks, ancient woodland, leafy avenues, Victorian gardens, and urban pavements. Sights: [Crystal Palace Museum](https://www.crystalpalacemuseum.org.uk), [Crystal Palace Dinosaurs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crystal_Palace_Dinosaurs), [Dulwich Picture Gallery](https://www.dulwichpicturegallery.org.uk), [Dulwich College](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dulwich_College), [Horniman Museum](https://www.horniman.ac.uk), Crystal Palace Park, Sydenham Hill Wood, Cox's Walk Footbridge, Dulwich Park, Old College, Belair House, Horniman Gardens, and Dulwich toll-gate. Lunch in Dulwich Village: the [Crown and Greyhound](https://www.thecrownandgreyhound.co.uk). 13 km. 3.5 hours.

**Crystal Palace to Forest Hill via Crystal Palace Museum**: A shorter variant of [Crystal Palace to Forest Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/crystal-palace-to-forest-hill/). Suburban parks, ancient woodland, Victorian gardens, and urban pavements. Sights: [Crystal Palace Museum](https://www.crystalpalacemuseum.org.uk), [Crystal Palace Dinosaurs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crystal_Palace_Dinosaurs), [Horniman Museum](https://www.horniman.ac.uk), Crystal Palace Park, Sydenham Hill Wood, Cox's Walk Footbridge, and Horniman Gardens. Lunch in Sydenham Hill: the [Wood House](https://www.thewoodhousedulwich.co.uk). 7 km. 2 hours.
FOH.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Crystal Palace to Forest Hill via Dulwich Picture Gallery](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/crystal-palace-to-forest-hill/)**: Suburban parks, ancient woodland, leafy avenues, Victorian gardens, and urban pavements. Sights: [Crystal Palace Museum](https://www.crystalpalacemuseum.org.uk), [Crystal Palace Dinosaurs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crystal_Palace_Dinosaurs), [Dulwich Picture Gallery](https://www.dulwichpicturegallery.org.uk), [Dulwich College](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dulwich_College), [Horniman Museum](https://www.horniman.ac.uk), Crystal Palace Park, Sydenham Hill Wood, Cox's Walk Footbridge, Dulwich Park, Old College, Belair House, Horniman Gardens, and Dulwich toll-gate. Lunch in Dulwich Village: the [Crown and Greyhound](https://www.thecrownandgreyhound.co.uk). 13 km. 3.5 hours.

FPK.name: Finsbury Park
FPK.adminWalksAll:
**Finsbury Park to Tottenham Hale**: A variant of [Alexandra Palace to Tottenham Hale](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/alexandra-palace-to-tottenham-hale/). Urban park, reservoir wetlands, residential streets, riverside path, and open marshland. Sights: [Woodberry Wetlands](https://www.wildlondon.org.uk/woodberry-wetlands-nature-reserve), [Walthamstow Marshes Nature Reserve](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/walthamstow-marshes), [Walthamstow Wetlands](https://www.wildlondon.org.uk/walthamstow-wetlands-nature-reserve), Springfield Park, Coppermill Tower, and Engine House. Lunch in Woodberry Wetlands: the Coal House Café. 8 km.

**Finsbury Park to Hampstead**: A longer variant of [Highgate to Hampstead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/highgate-to-hampstead/). Disused railway trackbed nature reserve, urban green spaces, woods, heathland, parkland, ponds, garden suburb streets, and panoramic hilltops. Sights: [Parkland Walk](https://www.parkland-walk.org.uk), [Hampstead Garden Suburb](http://www.hgs.org.uk/history/index.html), [Free Church](https://www.hgsfreechurch.org.uk), [St Jude-on-the-Hill](https://www.stjudeonthehill.com), [Hampstead Heath](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hampstead_Heath), [Golders Hill Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Golders_Hill_Park), [The Pergola](https://www.historic-uk.com/HistoryMagazine/DestinationsUK/Hampstead-Pergola-Hill-Gardens), [Kenwood House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/kenwood), [Burgh House](https://www.burghhouse.org.uk), [Fenton House and Garden](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/fenton-house-and-garden), [St John-at-Hampstead](https://hampsteadparishchurch.org.uk), Highgate Wood, The Archer, Hill Garden, and Parliament Hill. Can be muddy. Lunch in North End Road, or Golders Hill Park. North End Road lunch stop: the [Old Bull & Bush](https://www.thebullandbush.co.uk). Golders Hill Park lunch stop: the Refreshment House. 19 km. 5 hours.

**Alexandra Palace to Finsbury Park**: A variant of [Alexandra Palace to Tottenham Hale](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/alexandra-palace-to-tottenham-hale/). Disused railway trackbed nature reserve, ancient woodland, and urban park. Sights: [Alexandra Palace](https://www.alexandrapalace.com), [Parkland Walk](https://www.parkland-walk.org.uk), Highgate Wood, and Finsbury Park. Lunch in Finsbury Park: the Finsbury Park Café. 8 km.

FRI.name: Frinton-on-Sea
FRI.adminWalksAll: **Walton-on-the-Naze to Frinton-on-Sea**: A longer variant of [Walton-on-the-Naze Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/walton-on-the-naze-circular/). Flat coastal seafront, beach huts, and sandy shore southward to Frinton. Check tide timings. Stone Point spit section may not be reliable — hasn't been walk-checked recently.
FRI.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Walton-on-the-Naze to Frinton-on-Sea**: A longer variant of [Walton-on-the-Naze Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/walton-on-the-naze-circular/). Flat coastal seafront, beach huts, and sandy shore southward to Frinton. Check tide timings. Stone Point spit section may not be reliable — hasn't been walk-checked recently.

FRT.name: Frant
FRT.adminWalksAll:
**Frant to Tunbridge Wells via Eridge Rocks**: A shorter variant of [Tunbridge Wells Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-circular/). High Weald hills, landscaped parkland, bluebell woods, restored heathland, sandstone outcrops, river valley, wooded common, and colonnaded town walkway. Sights: [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), The Pantiles, High Rocks, and Eridge Old Park. Busy road at start. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Eridge Green: the [Nevill Crest and Gun](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/nevillcrestandgun). 17 km.

**Frant to Tunbridge Wells via Harrison's Rocks**: An alternative variant of [Tunbridge Wells Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-circular/). High Weald hills, landscaped parkland, bluebell woods, restored heathland, sandstone outcrops, river valley, wooded common, and colonnaded town walkway. Sights: [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Groombridge Place](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groombridge_Place), The Pantiles, St Alban Frant, High Rocks, and Eridge Old Park. Busy road at start. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Eridge Green: the [Nevill Crest and Gun](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/nevillcrestandgun). 21 km. 5.5 hours.

**Wadhurst to Frant**: A shorter variant of [Wadhurst to Tunbridge Wells](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-to-tunbridge-wells/). High Weald ridges, wooded valleys, open fields, descent through woodland, and brief roadside stretch. Sights: [Sussex Border Path](https://www.sussexborderpath.co.uk), Eridge Old Park, Saxonbury Hill Tower, The Mount, St Alban, Frant, High Weald Landscape Trail, Tunbridge Wells Circular Walk, and [Brecknock Arms](https://www.thebrecknockarmspub.com). No pavement on final road. Lunch in Frant: the George Inn, or the [Abergavenny Arms](https://www.abergavennyarms.co.uk). 11 km. 3 hours.
FRT.publicWalksS2S:
**Frant to Tunbridge Wells via Eridge Rocks**: A shorter variant of [Tunbridge Wells Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-circular/). High Weald hills, landscaped parkland, bluebell woods, restored heathland, sandstone outcrops, river valley, wooded common, and colonnaded town walkway. Sights: [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), The Pantiles, High Rocks, and Eridge Old Park. Busy road at start. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Eridge Green: the [Nevill Crest and Gun](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/nevillcrestandgun). 17 km.

**Frant to Tunbridge Wells via Harrison's Rocks**: An alternative variant of [Tunbridge Wells Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-circular/). High Weald hills, landscaped parkland, bluebell woods, restored heathland, sandstone outcrops, river valley, wooded common, and colonnaded town walkway. Sights: [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Groombridge Place](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groombridge_Place), The Pantiles, St Alban Frant, High Rocks, and Eridge Old Park. Busy road at start. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Eridge Green: the [Nevill Crest and Gun](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/nevillcrestandgun). 21 km. 5.5 hours.
FRT.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Wadhurst to Frant**: A shorter variant of [Wadhurst to Tunbridge Wells](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-to-tunbridge-wells/). High Weald ridges, wooded valleys, open fields, descent through woodland, and brief roadside stretch. Sights: [Sussex Border Path](https://www.sussexborderpath.co.uk), Eridge Old Park, Saxonbury Hill Tower, The Mount, St Alban, Frant, High Weald Landscape Trail, Tunbridge Wells Circular Walk, and [Brecknock Arms](https://www.thebrecknockarmspub.com). No pavement on final road. Lunch in Frant: the George Inn, or the [Abergavenny Arms](https://www.abergavennyarms.co.uk). 11 km. 3 hours.

FSB.name: Fishbourne
FSB.adminWalksAll: **Fishbourne Circular via Chichester Harbour**: Sea. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 30 July 2023.* 11 km. 10 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921494524)**
FSB.publicWalksCircular: **Fishbourne Circular via Chichester Harbour**: Sea. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 11 km. 10 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921494524)**

FSK.name: Fiskerton
FSK.adminWalksAll:
**[Fiskerton Circular via Southwell](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: River, lanes, and racecourse. Sights: Southwell Minster, Southwell Racecourse, and River Greet. Lunch in Southwell: the pubs and cafes. 13 km. 60 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/311%20Fiskerton%20Southwell.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925785026)**

**[Burton Joyce to Fiskerton](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Riverside, good paths, and open views. Sights: Gunthorpe Lock, Gunthorpe village, and River Trent. Irregular train times. Lunch in Gunthorpe, or Fiskerton. Gunthorpe lunch stops: the pubs, or the cafe at the lock. Fiskerton lunch stop: the pub. 16 km. 10 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/404%20Burton%20Joyce%20to%20Fiskerton.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925786946)**
FSK.publicWalksCircular: **[Fiskerton Circular via Southwell](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: River, lanes, and racecourse. Sights: Southwell Minster, Southwell Racecourse, and River Greet. Lunch in Southwell: the pubs and cafes. 13 km. 60 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/311%20Fiskerton%20Southwell.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925785026)**
FSK.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Burton Joyce to Fiskerton](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Riverside, good paths, and open views. Sights: Gunthorpe Lock, Gunthorpe village, and River Trent. Irregular train times. Lunch in Gunthorpe, or Fiskerton. Gunthorpe lunch stops: the pubs, or the cafe at the lock. Fiskerton lunch stop: the pub. 16 km. 10 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/404%20Burton%20Joyce%20to%20Fiskerton.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925786946)**

GBS.name: Goring-by-Sea
GBS.adminWalksAll:
**Goring-by-Sea to Worthing**: From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 18 June 2022.* 19 km. 310 m uphill. 5.5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921495389)**

**Goring-by-Sea to Littlehampton via Highdown Gardens**: A shorter variant of [Angmering to Littlehampton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/angmering-to-littlehampton/). Residential streets, open downland, farmland, seafront promenade, and riverside walk. Sights: [Highdown Gardens](https://highdowngardens.co.uk), [Highdown Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Highdown_Hill), Iron Age hill fort, Miller's Tomb, Rampion Wind Farm, Ecclesden Mill, England Coast Path, Rustington Convalescent Home, [East Beach Café](https://eastbeachcafe.co.uk), and [Harbour Lights Café](https://www.harbourlightscafe.co.uk). Lunch in Highdown Hill: the [Highdown](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/highdown). 12 km. 3 hours.

**Goring-by-Sea to Littlehampton via Ilex Avenue**: An alternative variant of [Angmering to Littlehampton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/angmering-to-littlehampton/). Residential streets, tree-lined bridleway, coastal plain, open downland, shingle beach, seafront promenade, and riverside walk. Sights: Ilex Avenue, Goring Gap, St Andrew, Ferring, [Highdown Gardens](https://highdowngardens.co.uk), [Highdown Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Highdown_Hill), Iron Age hill fort, Miller's Tomb, Rampion Wind Farm, Ecclesden Mill, St Margaret, Angmering, St Mary the Virgin, East Preston, England Coast Path, Rustington Convalescent Home, Lighthouse, River Arun, [East Beach Café](https://eastbeachcafe.co.uk), and [Harbour Lights Café](https://www.harbourlightscafe.co.uk). Lunch in Highdown Hill, or Angmering. Highdown Hill lunch stop: the [Highdown](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/highdown). Angmering lunch stops: the [Spotted Cow](https://www.spottedcowangmering.co.uk/index), or the [Lamb at Angmering](https://www.thelamb-angmering.com). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Arundel to Goring-by-Sea via Roman Catholic Cathedral](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/arundel-to-goring-by-sea/)**: River path, wooded estate tracks, open downland, chalk hills, coastal plain, and seaside seafront loop. Sights: [Arundel Castle](https://www.arundelcastle.org), [Angmering Park Estate](https://www.angmeringwalks.com/angmering-park-estate), [Highdown Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Highdown_Hill), [Highdown Gardens](https://highdowngardens.co.uk), Iron Age hill fort, Roman Catholic Cathedral, St Nicholas, St John the Divine, Miller's Tomb, Ilex Avenue, and Rampion Wind Farm. Can be muddy. Lunch in Patching, Highdown, or Goring-by-Sea. Patching lunch stop: the [World's End](https://www.worldsendpatching.co.uk). Highdown lunch stop: the [Highdown](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/highdown). Goring-by-Sea lunch stop: the [Bulls Head](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/west-sussex/bull). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Arundel to Goring-by-Sea via Arundel Castle**: A shorter variant of [Arundel to Goring-by-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/arundel-to-goring-by-sea/). River path, wooded estate tracks, open downland, chalk hills, and coastal plain. Sights: [Arundel Castle](https://www.arundelcastle.org), [Angmering Park Estate](https://www.angmeringwalks.com/angmering-park-estate), [Highdown Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Highdown_Hill), [Highdown Gardens](https://highdowngardens.co.uk), Iron Age hill fort, St John the Divine, and Miller's Tomb. Can be muddy. Lunch in Patching, or Highdown. Patching lunch stop: the [World's End](https://www.worldsendpatching.co.uk). Highdown lunch stop: the [Highdown](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/highdown). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**Angmering to Goring-by-Sea**: An alternative variant of [Arundel to Goring-by-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/arundel-to-goring-by-sea/). Residential streets, village lanes, bridleways, open downland, chalk hills, and coastal plain. Sights: [Highdown Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Highdown_Hill), [Highdown Gardens](https://highdowngardens.co.uk), Iron Age hill fort, Ecclesden Mill, St Margaret, and Miller's Tomb. Lunch in Highdown, or Angmering. Highdown lunch stop: the [Highdown](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/highdown). Angmering lunch stops: the [Lamb at Angmering](https://www.thelamb-angmering.com), or the [Spotted Cow](https://www.spottedcowangmering.co.uk/index). 11 km. 2.5 hours.
GBS.publicWalksS2S: **Goring-by-Sea to Worthing**: From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 19 km. 310 m uphill. 5.5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921495389)**

GDN.name: Godstone
GDN.adminWalksAll:
**Godstone Circular**: A variant of [Godstone to Oxted](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/godstone-to-oxted/). Low-lying farmland, river valley, woodland, farm tracks, meadows, and railway embankment. Sights: [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), St George's Church Crowhurst, Lagham Manor, and Greenwich Meridian Trail. Can be muddy. Lunch in Staffhurst Wood: the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted). 14 km. 3 hours.

**[Godstone to Oxted via Tandridge Yew](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/godstone-to-oxted/)**: Low-lying farmland, river valley, woodland, low ridges, golf course, ancient churchyards, and old coaching village. Sights: [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), [Tandridge Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/tandridge-churchyard-765), [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), St George's Church Crowhurst, St Peter's Church Tandridge, Lagham Manor, Greenwich Meridian Trail, Greensand Way, Oxted Mill, and St Silvan's House. Can be muddy. Lunch in Staffhurst Wood: the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted). 17 km. 4.5 hours.

**Godstone to Hurst Green**: A shorter variant of [Godstone to Oxted](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/godstone-to-oxted/). Low-lying farmland, river valley, woodland, village green, and playing fields. Sights: [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), St George's Church Crowhurst, Lagham Manor, and Greenwich Meridian Trail. Can be muddy. Lunch in Staffhurst Wood: the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted). 13 km. 3 hours.

**Godstone to Oxted via Haycutter**: A shorter variant of [Godstone to Oxted](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/godstone-to-oxted/). Low-lying farmland, river valley, woodland, country lanes, and village green. Sights: [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), St George's Church Crowhurst, Lagham Manor, Oxted Mill, Greensand Way, and Haycutter. Can be muddy. Lunch in Staffhurst Wood: the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**Hurst Green to Godstone**: A variant of [Oxted to Lingfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxted-to-lingfield/). Hillside paths, Low Weald farmland, ancient woodland, and flat fields alongside railway. Sights: [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), Tenchleys Manor, and St George's Church. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Staffhurst Wood: the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted). 13 km.

**Oxted to Godstone**: An alternative variant of [Oxted to Lingfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxted-to-lingfield/). Meadows, wooded Greensand Ridge, gently rolling Low Weald farmland, ancient woodland, and flat fields alongside railway. Sights: [St Peter's Church](https://www.stpeterslimpsfield.org/history-of-st-peters.html), [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), Tenchleys Manor, and St George's Church. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Staffhurst Wood: the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted). 16 km.
GDN.publicWalksS2S: **[Godstone to Oxted via Tandridge Yew](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/godstone-to-oxted/)**: Low-lying farmland, river valley, woodland, low ridges, golf course, ancient churchyards, and old coaching village. Sights: [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), [Tandridge Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/tandridge-churchyard-765), [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), St George's Church Crowhurst, St Peter's Church Tandridge, Lagham Manor, Greenwich Meridian Trail, Greensand Way, Oxted Mill, and St Silvan's House. Can be muddy. Lunch in Staffhurst Wood: the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted). 17 km. 4.5 hours.

GER.name: Gerrards Cross
GER.adminWalksAll: **Gerrards Cross to Cookham**: Parkland, lakes, woods, beech forest, open fields, Thames riverside, village lanes, and National Trust common.
