Sights: Bulstrode Park, Bulstrode Manor, Hedgerley Green Nature Reserve, St Mary the Virgin, Egypt Wood, Burnham Beeches, Church of St Nicholas, Holy Trinity Church, Stanley Spencer Gallery, Stanley Spencer's memorial stone, and Cookham Moor. Can be muddy. Best in Nov. Lunch in Hedgerley, or Littleworth Common. Hedgerley lunch stop: the White Horse. Littleworth Common lunch stop: the Blackwood Arms. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 15 km. 4.5 hours.
GER.publicWalksS2S: **Gerrards Cross to Cookham**: Parkland, lakes, woods, beech forest, open fields, Thames riverside, village lanes, and National Trust common. Sights: Bulstrode Park, Bulstrode Manor, Hedgerley Green Nature Reserve, St Mary the Virgin, Egypt Wood, Burnham Beeches, Church of St Nicholas, Holy Trinity Church, Stanley Spencer Gallery, Stanley Spencer's memorial stone, and Cookham Moor. Can be muddy. Best in Nov. Lunch in Hedgerley, or Littleworth Common. Hedgerley lunch stop: the White Horse. Littleworth Common lunch stop: the Blackwood Arms. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 15 km. 4.5 hours.

GFD.name: Greenford
GFD.adminWalksAll: **[Greenford to Osterley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/greenford-to-osterley/)**: Urban parks, open meadows, river towpath, Grand Union Canal, flat West London countryside, and stately home grounds. Sights: Osterley Park, Osterley House, Hanwell Locks, and Brent River Park. Lunch in Tentelow Lane: the Plough. 12 km. 3 hours.
GFD.publicWalksS2S: **[Greenford to Osterley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/greenford-to-osterley/)**: Urban parks, open meadows, river towpath, Grand Union Canal, flat West London countryside, and stately home grounds. Sights: Osterley Park, Osterley House, Hanwell Locks, and Brent River Park. Lunch in Tentelow Lane: the Plough. 12 km. 3 hours.

GLD.name: Guildford
GLD.adminWalksAll:
**Guildford Circular via Watt’s Chapel**: Rambler favourite! From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 1 May 2021.* 13 km. 140 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921496141)**

**Gomshall to Guildford**: Fields, wooded valley, pine woods, heathland common, stud farms, and canal and riverside path into a historic town. Sights: Church of St James, Albury Park, Catholic Apostolic Church, Saxon Church of Ss Peter and Paul, Chinthurst Hill Tower, Great Tangley Manor House, Yvonne Arnaud Theatre, and St Catherine's Lock. Can be muddy. Lunch in Albury Heath, or Shere. Albury Heath lunch stop: the William IV. Shere lunch stops: the White Horse, or the William Bray. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 15 km. 110 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921052519)**

**[Guildford Circular via Chantries Hill Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/guildford-via-chantries-hill-circular/)**: Rambler favourite! Hilly Surrey Hills AONB, River Wey Navigation, wooded escarpment, heathland, bluebell and beech woods, sandy soils, and pretty villages. Sights: St Martha's-on-the-Hill church and Chantries Hill. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Albury: the Drummond at Albury. 20 km.

**[Guildford Circular via Albury Park](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/guildford-circular-via-albury-park/)**: North Downs chalk ridge, Greensand hills, mixed woodland, heathland, parkland, river valleys, water meadows, and industrial ruins. Sights: Guildford Castle, Pewley Down, [Chilworth Gunpowder Mills](https://www.chilworthgunpowdermills.co.uk), Chilworth Manor, Albury Park, [Ss Peter and Paul (Saxon Church)](https://www.alburyoldsaxonchurch.org), [Catholic Apostolic Church](https://www.alburychurches.org/catholic-apostolic-church-apostles-henry-drummond), Silent Pool, Albury Organic Vineyard, Optohedron, Newlands Corner, [St Martha-on-the-Hill](https://www.parishofchilworth.org.uk/history/st-marthas/full-history), St Catherine's Chapel, Shalford Water Meadows, River Wey Navigation, Alice sculptures, Tunsgate Arch, and Guildhall. Can be muddy. Lunch in Little London: the [William IV](https://www.thewilliamivpub.com). 23 km. 6 hours.

**Guildford Circular via Watts Gallery**: Gentle pastures, woods, river valley, Loseley Park estate, and village lanes. Sights: Watts Gallery, Watts Chapel, and Loseley Park. Can be muddy. Lunch in Compton: the Watts Gallery tea shop, or the Withies Inn. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 13 km.

**Guildford to Gomshall via Guildford Castle**: Sandy ridges, downland escarpments, wooded sections, open downland, quiet valley, and Surrey Hills AONB. Sights: Guildford Castle, Pewley Down, St Martha-on-the-Hill Church, and Shere. Can be muddy. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in Shere, or Gomshall. Shere lunch stops: the White Horse, the William Bray, the Dabbling Duck, or the Hilly's Tea Shop. Gomshall lunch stops: the Compasses Inn, or the Gomshall Mill. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 19 km.

**[Guildford to Horsley via Chantries Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/guildford-to-horsley/)**: North Downs ridge, escarpments, bluebell woods, downland slopes, upland country with long views, wooded stretches, and estate grounds. Sights: Chantries Hill, St Martha's-on-the-Hill Church, Newlands Corner, West Horsley Place, and Grange Park Opera. Steep hill climbs. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Albury, Chilworth, or Newlands Corner. Albury lunch stop: the Drummond at Albury. Chilworth lunch stop: the Percy Arms. Newlands Corner lunch stop: the Plucky Pheasant. 21 km.

**Guildford to Farnham via Loseley Park**: River valley, gentle pastures, woods, remote heathlands, heather-covered moors, and sandy Greensand soils. Sights: Loseley Park, Watts Gallery, Watts Chapel, and Puttenham Common. Can be muddy. Best in Jul–Sep. Lunch in Compton, or Puttenham. Compton lunch stop: the Watts Gallery tea shop. Puttenham lunch stop: the Good Intent. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 22 km.

**Guildford to Shalford**: A shorter variant of [Guildford via Chantries Hill Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/guildford-via-chantries-hill-circular/). Rambler favourite! Hilly Surrey Hills, wooded escarpment, heathland, and Shalford watermeadows. Sights: St Martha's-on-the-Hill church and Chantries Hill. Can be muddy. Lunch in Albury: the Drummond at Albury. 17 km.

**Guildford to Horsley via St Martha's-on-the-Hill Church**: A shorter variant of [Guildford to Horsley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/guildford-to-horsley/). North Downs ridge, downland escarpment, wooded stretches, upland country with long views, and estate grounds. Sights: St Martha's-on-the-Hill Church, Newlands Corner, and West Horsley Place. Steep hill climbs. Lunch in Newlands Corner: the Plucky Pheasant. 17 km.

**Guildford to Chilworth via Pewley Down**: Short climb, downland, hilltop church, and descent to station village. Sights: Pewley Down and St Martha-on-the-Hill Church. Lunch in Chilworth: the Percy Arms. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 6 km.

**Guildford to Horsley via Pewley Down**: A shorter variant of [Guildford to Horsley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/guildford-to-horsley/). Pewley Down, downland, North Downs ridge, wooded stretches, upland country, and estate grounds. Sights: Pewley Down, St Martha's-on-the-Hill Church, Newlands Corner, and West Horsley Place. Steep hill climbs. Lunch in Albury: the Drummond at Albury. 19 km.

**Guildford to Farnham via Hog's Back**: Dramatic ridge of the Hog's Back, heathlands, woods, moors, and with magnificent views north and south. Sights: Hog's Back, Watts Gallery, Watts Chapel, and Puttenham Common. Steep; more strenuous. Lunch in Compton, or Puttenham. Compton lunch stop: the Watts Gallery tea shop. Puttenham lunch stop: the Good Intent. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 21 km.

**Guildford to Box Hill & Westhumble**: Sandy ridges, downland escarpments, extended North Downs Way, open downland, and wooded sections. Sights: Guildford Castle, Pewley Down, St Martha-on-the-Hill Church, Shere, and [Denbies Wine Estate](http://www.denbies.co.uk). Lunch in Shere, or Gomshall. Shere lunch stops: the White Horse, the William Bray, the Dabbling Duck, or the Hilly's Tea Shop. Gomshall lunch stops: the Compasses Inn, or the Gomshall Mill. A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 23 km.

**Chilworth to Guildford via Chantries Hill Circular**: A shorter variant of [Guildford via Chantries Hill Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/guildford-via-chantries-hill-circular/). Rambler favourite! Shorter start, skips Chantries Hill and St Martha's Hill, heathland and woods, and River Wey into Guildford. Lunch in Albury: the Drummond at Albury. 14 km.

**Chilworth to Guildford via Albury Park**: A shorter variant of [Guildford Circular via Albury Park](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/guildford-circular-via-albury-park/). Greensand hills, mixed woodland, heathland, parkland, river valley, industrial ruins, and water meadows. Sights: [Chilworth Gunpowder Mills](https://www.chilworthgunpowdermills.co.uk), Chilworth Manor, Albury Park, [Ss Peter and Paul (Saxon Church)](https://www.alburyoldsaxonchurch.org), [Catholic Apostolic Church](https://www.alburychurches.org/catholic-apostolic-church-apostles-henry-drummond), Silent Pool, Albury Organic Vineyard, Optohedron, Newlands Corner, [St Martha-on-the-Hill](https://www.parishofchilworth.org.uk/history/st-marthas/full-history), St Catherine's Chapel, Shalford Water Meadows, River Wey Navigation, and Alice sculptures. Can be muddy. Lunch in Little London: the [William IV](https://www.thewilliamivpub.com). 19 km. 5 hours.

**Wanborough to Guildford**: Open fields, North Downs ridge, vineyard, golf course, mixed woodland, and River Wey towpath all the way to Guildford. Sights: Wanborough Church, Wanborough Manor House, Greyfriars Vineyard, [Watts Gallery](http://www.wattsgallery.org.uk/en-gb/visit/), and Watts Chapel. Can be muddy. Lunch in Compton: the [Withies Inn](http://www.thewithiesinn.com/), or the [Watts Gallery Tea Shop](http://www.wattsgallery.org.uk/en-gb/visit/tea-shop/). A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 19 km.

**Holmwood to Guildford via Leith Hill Tower**: A longer variant of [Holmwood to Shamley Green](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/holmwood-to-shamley-green/). Sandy Greensand Ridge, beech and sweet chestnut woods, heathland, several big climbs and viewpoints, green valleys, and extended route to Guildford. Sights: Leith Hill Tower, Holmbury Hill, and Pitch Hill. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Holmbury St Mary: the Heartwork Coffee, the King's Head, or the Royal Oak. 28 km.

**Holmwood to Guildford via Church of St James**: Extended route combining two walks through Surrey hills, woods, heathland, and riverside paths. Sights: Church of St James, Albury Park, Chinthurst Hill Tower, and Yvonne Arnaud Theatre. Can be muddy. Lunch in Albury Heath: the William IV. A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 48 km.
GLD.publicWalksCircular: **Guildford Circular via Watt’s Chapel**: Rambler favourite! From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 13 km. 140 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921496141)**
GLD.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Gomshall to Guildford**: Fields, wooded valley, pine woods, heathland common, stud farms, and canal and riverside path into a historic town. Sights: Church of St James, Albury Park, Catholic Apostolic Church, Saxon Church of Ss Peter and Paul, Chinthurst Hill Tower, Great Tangley Manor House, Yvonne Arnaud Theatre, and St Catherine's Lock. Can be muddy. Lunch in Albury Heath, or Shere. Albury Heath lunch stop: the William IV. Shere lunch stops: the White Horse, or the William Bray. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 15 km. 110 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921052519)**

GLY.name: Glynde
GLY.privateNote: LON Trainline says: 1h25m — Victoria via Lewes
GLY.adminWalksAll:
**Glynde to Seaford via Firle Place**: Rambler favourite! South Downs ridge, chalk downland, Firle Park, river valley, ancient villages, forest, coastal cliffs, shingle beach, and esplanade. Sights: Firle Place, Church of St Peter, Alfriston Clergy House, Parish Church of St Andrew, and Seaford Museum. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch in Alfriston, or Litlington. Alfriston lunch stops: the George Inn, the Star Inn, the Ye Olde Smugglers Inn, the Wingrove Inn, the Chestnuts Tea Room, the Singing Kettle, or the Poco Restaurant & Wine Bar. Litlington lunch stops: the Plough and Harrow pub, or the Littlington Tea Gardens. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 23 km. 7 hours.

**Glynde to Southease via Glynde and Southease**: A shorter variant of [Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-glynde-and-southease/). Rambler favourite! Downland ridge, South Downs Way, open chalk paths, and panoramic views. Lunch in Southease: the YHA Courtyard Cafe. 6 km.

**Glynde to Lewes**: A shorter variant of [Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-glynde-and-southease/). Rambler favourite! Downland ridge via Mount Caburn, steep descent into Lewes, and chalk paths. Sights: Mount Caburn. Steep descent. Lunch in Glynde: the Trevor Arms, the Steamworks, or the Little Cottage Tea Rooms. 6 km.

**Glynde to Seaford omitting West Firle**: A shorter variant of [Lewes to Seaford via West Firle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-seaford-via-west-firle/). South Downs ridges, open downland, hidden valley, and coastal approach. Sights: Mount Caburn Iron Age Hill Fort and St Andrew's Church Bishopstone. No lunch-time pub. 11 km.

**Glynde to Southease via West Firle Circular**: South Downs Way chalk ridge and extensive views inland and to Newhaven and the Channel. Sights: Firle Place. Lunch in Firle: the Ram Inn. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 11 km.

**Glynde to Seaford via West Firle**: A shorter variant of [Lewes to Seaford via West Firle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-seaford-via-west-firle/). South Downs ridges, open downland, hidden valley, and coastal approach. Sights: Firle Park, St Peter's Church, St Andrew's Church Bishopstone, and Firle Tower. Steep climbs. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in West Firle: the Ram Inn. 16 km.

**Glynde to Eastbourne**: An alternative variant of [Southease to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-eastbourne/). Open treeless downland ridges, steep ascents, scenic Cuckmere Valley, coastal promenade, beach, and pier. Sights: Firle Beacon, Long Burgh Long Barrow, Long Man of Wilmington, Windover Hill, Beachy Head, Wish Tower, Eastbourne Pier, and Italian Gardens. Steep ascents. Lunch in Alfriston: the Pubs, tea rooms and village shop. 27 km.

**Bishopstone to Glynde**: A shorter variant of [Bishopstone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/). Downland climbs, South Downs Way ridge walking westward, and descent to Glynde. Sights: Long Burgh Barrow. 16 km.

**Lewes to Glynde via West Firle**: A shorter variant of [Lewes to Seaford via West Firle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-seaford-via-west-firle/). Circular ridge path, open downland, and valley descent into Glynde. Sights: Mount Caburn Iron Age Hill Fort. Lunch in Glynde: the [Trevor Arms](https://www.thetrevor.co.uk/), or the [Steamworks](https://steamworksbars.co.uk/about-us/glynde-steamworks-is-open-6472323). 6 km.

**Lewes to Glynde via West Firle Circular**: Steep hill with views, secluded valley, and chalk downland. Sights: Lewes Castle and Mount Caburn. Steep hill. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 5 km.
GLY.publicWalksS2S: **Glynde to Seaford via Firle Place**: Rambler favourite! South Downs ridge, chalk downland, Firle Park, river valley, ancient villages, forest, coastal cliffs, shingle beach, and esplanade. Sights: Firle Place, Church of St Peter, Alfriston Clergy House, Parish Church of St Andrew, and Seaford Museum. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch in Alfriston, or Litlington. Alfriston lunch stops: the George Inn, the Star Inn, the Ye Olde Smugglers Inn, the Wingrove Inn, the Chestnuts Tea Room, the Singing Kettle, or the Poco Restaurant & Wine Bar. Litlington lunch stops: the Plough and Harrow pub, or the Littlington Tea Gardens. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 23 km. 7 hours.

GMN.name: Great Missenden
GMN.adminWalksAll:
**Great Missenden to Amersham via Penn Wood**: Open Chilterns farmland, beech woods, rolling fields, quiet villages, and a ridge approach to old Amersham. Sights: [Parish Church of St Peter and St Paul](http://www.missendenchurch.org.uk/), [St John the Baptist Church](http://www.littlemissendenparish.org.uk/), [All Saints Church](http://www.coleshill.org/all_saints_church.htm), [Parish Church of St Mary](http://stmarysamersham.co.uk/), Penn Wood, and Roald Dahl Museum and Story Centre. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Penn Street, Little Missenden, or Winchmore Hill. Penn Street lunch stops: the [Squirrel](http://www.thesquirrelpub.co.uk/), or the [Hit or Miss Inn](http://www.ourpubs.co.uk/). Little Missenden lunch stops: the Red Lion, or the Crown. Winchmore Hill lunch stop: the Potters Arms. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 17 km. 220 m uphill. 5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920274295)**

**Great Missenden Circular Walk**: Chilterns hills, fields, and woods on a circular route from Great Missenden. Sights: Great Missenden Abbey and [Roald Dahl Museum](http://www.roalddahlmuseum.org/cafe/). A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km.

**Great Missenden to Amersham via St John the Baptist Church**: Road and waymarked path close to A413 and through Little Missenden into old Amersham. Sights: [St John the Baptist Church](http://www.littlemissendenparish.org.uk/) and [Parish Church of St Mary](http://stmarysamersham.co.uk/). Lunch in Little Missenden: the Red Lion, or the Crown. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 10 km.

**Chesham to Great Missenden via St John the Baptist Church**: Undulating Chilterns, sloping fields, woods, hamlets, village greens, and beech woodland on Frith Hill. Sights: St Mary Church, St John the Baptist Church, Great Missenden Abbey, and [Roald Dahl Museum](http://www.roalddahlmuseum.org/cafe/). Steep descent into Great Missenden. Lunch in The Lee: the [Cock and Rabbit Inn](http://www.graziemille.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 15 km. 5 hours.

**Princes Risborough to Great Missenden**: Sloping fields, beech woods, hamlets, upmarket farms, cottages, and two short steep hills with fine views. Sights: [Lacey Green Windmill](http://www.laceygreenwindmill.org.uk/), [Roald Dahl Museum and Story Centre](http://www.roalddahlmuseum.org/), and Prestwood Local Nature Reserve. Can be muddy. Best in Oct–Nov. Lunch in Bryant's Bottom, or Prestwood. Bryant's Bottom lunch stop: the Gate Inn. Prestwood lunch stop: the Polecat Inn. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 15 km. 4.5 hours.

**Chesham to Great Missenden via St Mary Church**: Undulating Chilterns, sloping fields, woods, and hamlets. Sights: St Mary Church and Great Missenden Abbey. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 9 km. 3 hours.

**Amersham to Great Missenden**: Open Chilterns farmland, beech woods, rolling fields, quiet villages, and ridge descent to Great Missenden. Sights: [Parish Church of St Mary](http://stmarysamersham.co.uk/), [All Saints Church](http://www.coleshill.org/all_saints_church.htm), [St John the Baptist Church](http://www.littlemissendenparish.org.uk/), [Parish Church of St Peter and St Paul](http://www.missendenchurch.org.uk/), and Roald Dahl Museum and Story Centre. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Penn Street, or Little Missenden. Penn Street lunch stops: the [Squirrel](http://www.thesquirrelpub.co.uk/), or the [Hit or Miss Inn](http://www.ourpubs.co.uk/). Little Missenden lunch stop: the Red Lion. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 5 hours.

**Wendover to Great Missenden**: An alternative variant of [Wendover Circular via The Lee and Swan Bottom](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wendover-circular-via-the-lee-and-swan-bottom/). Chiltern Hills, grassy fields, varied woodland, beautiful valleys, and scenic descent into Great Missenden. Sights: Boddington Hill Iron Age Hillfort and St. Mary's Church. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in The Lee: the Cock and Rabbit Inn. 21 km.
GMN.publicWalksS2S: **Great Missenden to Amersham via Penn Wood**: Open Chilterns farmland, beech woods, rolling fields, quiet villages, and a ridge approach to old Amersham. Sights: [Parish Church of St Peter and St Paul](http://www.missendenchurch.org.uk/), [St John the Baptist Church](http://www.littlemissendenparish.org.uk/), [All Saints Church](http://www.coleshill.org/all_saints_church.htm), [Parish Church of St Mary](http://stmarysamersham.co.uk/), Penn Wood, and Roald Dahl Museum and Story Centre. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Penn Street, Little Missenden, or Winchmore Hill. Penn Street lunch stops: the [Squirrel](http://www.thesquirrelpub.co.uk/), or the [Hit or Miss Inn](http://www.ourpubs.co.uk/). Little Missenden lunch stops: the Red Lion, or the Crown. Winchmore Hill lunch stop: the Potters Arms. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 17 km. 220 m uphill. 5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920274295)**

GMV.name: Great Malvern
GMV.adminWalksAll:
**[Great Malvern Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/malvern-hills-from-great-malvern-or-colwall/)**: Steep dramatic hills rising from flat vales, ancient woodland, open grassy commons with bracken and heather, scrub and rock ridge, and rolling pastures. Sights: Great Malvern Priory, [St. Ann's Well](http://stannswell.co.uk/), Worcestershire Beacon, Herefordshire Beacon, and British Camp. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Malvern: the [Wyche Inn](http://www.thewycheinn.co.uk/), the [Café H2O at the Malvern Hills GeoCentre](https://www.wyche-innovation.com/cafe-h2o), the [Malvern Hills Hotel & Restaurants](http://malvernhillshotel.co.uk/), or the Sally's Place. 20 km. 6.5 hours.

**Colwall to Great Malvern**: An alternative variant of [Malvern Hills from Great Malvern or Colwall](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/malvern-hills-from-great-malvern-or-colwall/). Fields, oak woods at westerly base of hills, ridge tops, open commons, and scrub and bracken slopes. Sights: Herefordshire Beacon, British Camp, Worcestershire Beacon, [St. Ann's Well](http://stannswell.co.uk/), and Great Malvern Priory. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Malvern: the [Malvern Hills Hotel & Restaurants](http://malvernhillshotel.co.uk/), the Sally's Place, the [Wyche Inn](http://www.thewycheinn.co.uk/), or the [Café H2O at the Malvern Hills GeoCentre](https://www.wyche-innovation.com/cafe-h2o). 18 km. 5.5 hours.
GMV.publicWalksCircular: **[Great Malvern Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/malvern-hills-from-great-malvern-or-colwall/)**: Steep dramatic hills rising from flat vales, ancient woodland, open grassy commons with bracken and heather, scrub and rock ridge, and rolling pastures. Sights: Great Malvern Priory, [St. Ann's Well](http://stannswell.co.uk/), Worcestershire Beacon, Herefordshire Beacon, and British Camp. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Malvern: the [Wyche Inn](http://www.thewycheinn.co.uk/), the [Café H2O at the Malvern Hills GeoCentre](https://www.wyche-innovation.com/cafe-h2o), the [Malvern Hills Hotel & Restaurants](http://malvernhillshotel.co.uk/), or the Sally's Place. 20 km. 6.5 hours.

GNH.name: Greenhithe
GNH.adminWalksAll: **[Dartford to Greenhithe](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dartford-to-greenhithe/)**: Flat riverside paths, flood defences, marshland, industrial edge-land, and wide Thames estuary views. Sights: Queen Elizabeth Bridge, Dartford Marshes, Littlebrook Power Station, and [Thames Path](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thames_Path). Flood risk — check warnings. Lunch in Greenhithe: the Asda café. 11 km.
GNH.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Dartford to Greenhithe](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dartford-to-greenhithe/)**: Flat riverside paths, flood defences, marshland, industrial edge-land, and wide Thames estuary views. Sights: Queen Elizabeth Bridge, Dartford Marshes, Littlebrook Power Station, and [Thames Path](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thames_Path). Flood risk — check warnings. Lunch in Greenhithe: the Asda café. 11 km.

GOD.name: Godalming
GOD.adminWalksAll:
**Farnham to Godalming**: Rambler favourite! River valley, forest tracks, sandy bridleways, open parkland, country houses, and wooded valleys. Sights: [Waverley Abbey](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/waverley-abbey/), Moor Park House, Church of St Nicholas, and Farnham Castle. Can be muddy. Lunch in Tilford: the Barley Mow. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 18 km. 180 m uphill. 5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920859171)**

**Wanborough to Godalming via Compton**: Open fields, North Downs ridge, vineyard, golf course, mixed woodland, riverside path along the River Wey, and historic market town. Sights: Wanborough Church, Wanborough Manor House, Greyfriars Vineyard, [Watts Gallery](http://www.wattsgallery.org.uk/en-gb/visit/), Watts Chapel, Phillips Memorial Cloister, and Church of St Peter and St Paul. Can be muddy. Lunch in Compton: the [Withies Inn](http://www.thewithiesinn.com/), or the [Watts Gallery Tea Shop](http://www.wattsgallery.org.uk/en-gb/visit/tea-shop/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 12 km. 150 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920224124)**

**Milford to Godalming**: Sandy bridleways, sunken footpaths, woodland, open fields, arboretum lakes, canal towpath, and historic town streets. Sights: Enton Mill, St Peter's Church, Hambledon, Greensand Way, The Hurtwood, [St Peter's Church, Hascombe](http://www.openchurchestrust.org.uk/Churches/Hascombe.htm), Winkworth Arboretum, Old Cider House, [River Wey and Godalming Navigation](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-riverwey_godalmingnavigations_dapdunewharf), and Church of St Peter and St Paul. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Hambledon, Hascombe, or Winkworth Arboretum. Hambledon lunch stop: the Merry Harriers. Hascombe lunch stop: the White Horse. Winkworth Arboretum lunch stop: the Winkworth Arboretum Cafe. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 18 km. 5 hours.

**Witley to Godalming**: Sandy bridleways, sunken footpaths, woodland, open fields, arboretum lakes, canal towpath, and historic town streets. Sights: [St Peter's Church, Hascombe](http://www.openchurchestrust.org.uk/Churches/Hascombe.htm), Winkworth Arboretum, [River Wey and Godalming Navigation](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-riverwey_godalmingnavigations_dapdunewharf), and Church of St Peter and St Paul. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Hascombe: the White Horse. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).
GOD.publicWalksS2SEnding:
**Farnham to Godalming**: Rambler favourite! River valley, forest tracks, sandy bridleways, open parkland, country houses, and wooded valleys. Sights: [Waverley Abbey](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/waverley-abbey/), Moor Park House, Church of St Nicholas, and Farnham Castle. Can be muddy. Lunch in Tilford: the Barley Mow. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 18 km. 180 m uphill. 5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920859171)**

**Wanborough to Godalming via Compton**: Open fields, North Downs ridge, vineyard, golf course, mixed woodland, riverside path along the River Wey, and historic market town. Sights: Wanborough Church, Wanborough Manor House, Greyfriars Vineyard, [Watts Gallery](http://www.wattsgallery.org.uk/en-gb/visit/), Watts Chapel, Phillips Memorial Cloister, and Church of St Peter and St Paul. Can be muddy. Lunch in Compton: the [Withies Inn](http://www.thewithiesinn.com/), or the [Watts Gallery Tea Shop](http://www.wattsgallery.org.uk/en-gb/visit/tea-shop/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 12 km. 150 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920224124)**

GOM.name: Gomshall
GOM.adminWalksAll:
**Gomshall Circular via Shere**: An essential walk! Woods, quaint village, and hilly. Sights: [Shere](https://www.visitsurrey.com/listing/shere/83223101/) (a beautiful, quintessential English village). Can be muddy. Lunch in Shere: the Dabbling Duck (very nice; reservations recommended). From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 1 February 2026.* 10 km. 170 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920217304)**

**Gomshall to Guildford**: Fields, wooded valley, pine woods, heathland common, stud farms, and canal and riverside path into a historic town. Sights: Church of St James, Albury Park, Catholic Apostolic Church, Saxon Church of Ss Peter and Paul, Chinthurst Hill Tower, Great Tangley Manor House, Yvonne Arnaud Theatre, and St Catherine's Lock. Can be muddy. Lunch in Albury Heath, or Shere. Albury Heath lunch stop: the William IV. Shere lunch stops: the White Horse, or the William Bray. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 15 km. 110 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921052519)**

**Gomshall Circular**: Circular downland walk from valley village and wooded and open sections. Sights: St Martha-on-the-Hill Church. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 9 km.

**[Gomshall to Dorking via Holmbury Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/gomshall-to-dorking/)**: Greensand Ridge, heathland, hilltop moorland, woodland trails, suburban streets, and a high street. Sights: Holmbury Hill, Leith Hill Tower, The Nower, and Hurt Wood. Can be muddy. Lunch in Holmbury St. Mary: the [Kings Head](https://thekingsheadhsm.com/), the [Royal Oak](https://royaloakholmbury.co.uk/), or the [Heartwork Coffee Bar](https://www.heartworkcoffee.co.uk/). 23 km.

**Gomshall to Box Hill & Westhumble**: North Downs Way, open and wooded downland, and extended ridge walking. Sights: [Denbies Wine Estate](http://www.denbies.co.uk). A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 13 km.

**Gomshall to Dorking via Piney Copse**: An alternative variant of [Dorking Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-circular/). Piney Copse woodland, Abinger Roughs mixed woodland and grassland, open hillside, river valleys, Greensand Hills, and wooded ridges. Sights: Piney Copse, [Abinger Roughs](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/abinger-roughs-and-netley-park), Wilberforce Memorial, Abinger Manor, St James, Abinger, Friday Street, Hammer Pond, Bury Hill Fisheries, The Nower, Greensand Way, Rose Hill Arch, and St Martin's Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Abinger Common: the [Abinger Hatch](https://theabingerhatch.co.uk). 15 km. 4 hours.

**Gomshall to Shalford**: Fields, wooded valley, pine woods, heathland common, stud farms, and riverside path to a village station. Sights: Church of St James, Albury Park, Chinthurst Hill Tower, and Great Tangley Manor House. Can be muddy. Lunch in Albury Heath, or Shere. Albury Heath lunch stop: the William IV. Shere lunch stops: the White Horse, or the William Bray. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).

**Holmwood to Gomshall**: Broadleaf woods, heathland, sandy bridleways, open valley, fields, and a picturesque Surrey Hills village. Sights: Leith Hill Tower, [Leith Hill Rhododendron Wood](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/leith-hill/features/the-rhododendron-wood-at-leith-hill), Leith Hill Place, Church of St John the Evangelist, Wotton House, Deerleap Wood, Monument to Samuel Wilberforce, Gomshall Mill, and Piney Copse. Can be muddy. Best in May, Oct and Nov. Lunch in Coldharbour, or Wotton. Coldharbour lunch stop: the Plough Inn. Wotton lunch stop: the Wotton Hatch. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**Guildford to Gomshall via Guildford Castle**: Sandy ridges, downland escarpments, wooded sections, open downland, quiet valley, and Surrey Hills AONB. Sights: Guildford Castle, Pewley Down, St Martha-on-the-Hill Church, and Shere. Can be muddy. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in Shere, or Gomshall. Shere lunch stops: the White Horse, the William Bray, the Dabbling Duck, or the Hilly's Tea Shop. Gomshall lunch stops: the Compasses Inn, or the Gomshall Mill. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 19 km.

**Witley to Gomshall**: A longer variant of [Witley to Ewhurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/witley-to-ewhurst/). Rural Surrey Hills, Wey Valley, small villages, forested ridge, and long extension north to Gomshall. Sights: [Hurtwood Inn](http://www.hurtwoodinn.com/) and [Gomshall Mill](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/gomshallmill/). Very long day. Lunch in Hascombe, or Shamley Green. Hascombe lunch stop: the [White Horse](https://www.whitehorsehascombe.co.uk). Shamley Green lunch stops: the [Bricklayers Arms](http://www.bricklayersarmspub.co.uk), or the [Red Lion](https://www.redlionshamleygreen.com). 25 km.
GOM.publicWalksS2S: **Gomshall to Guildford**: Fields, wooded valley, pine woods, heathland common, stud farms, and canal and riverside path into a historic town. Sights: Church of St James, Albury Park, Catholic Apostolic Church, Saxon Church of Ss Peter and Paul, Chinthurst Hill Tower, Great Tangley Manor House, Yvonne Arnaud Theatre, and St Catherine's Lock. Can be muddy. Lunch in Albury Heath, or Shere. Albury Heath lunch stop: the William IV. Shere lunch stops: the White Horse, or the William Bray. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 15 km. 110 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921052519)**
GOM.publicWalksCircular: **Gomshall Circular via Shere**: An essential walk! Woods, quaint village, and hilly. Sights: [Shere](https://www.visitsurrey.com/listing/shere/83223101/) (a beautiful, quintessential English village). Can be muddy. Lunch in Shere: the Dabbling Duck (very nice; reservations recommended). From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 10 km. 170 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920217304)**

GOR.name: Goring & Streatley
GOR.adminWalksAll:
**Pangbourne to Goring via Streatley Hill**: Rambler favourite! Woods, hilly, river, and quaint village. Relatively busy area. Plenty of lunch venues in Goring. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 11 October 2025.* 12 km. 190 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2618431014)**

**[Goring & Streatley Circular via Bell Inn](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/goring-circular/)**: Undulating downs, Thames-side views, Ridgeway path, chalk hills, and quaint villages. Sights: [St Mary's Church Aldworth](http://www.aldworthvillage.org/index.php/st-marys-church/the-giants/), [Bell Inn](https://www.facebook.com/The-Bell-Inn-Aldworth-102844626417649/), and Streatley Warren. Steep descents. Lunch in Aldworth: the [Bell Inn](https://www.facebook.com/The-Bell-Inn-Aldworth-102844626417649/), or the [Four Points Inn](https://www.thefourpointsinn.co.uk/). 18 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Goring & Streatley Circular via Hill Bottom](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/goring-circular-via-hill-bottom/)**: Rolling Chilterns countryside, beech forest trails, quiet country lanes, and three pubs en route. Sights: Pierreponts. Muddy after rain. Best in Apr–May. 18 km.

**Goring & Streatley Circular via St Mary's Church Aldworth**: A shorter variant of [Goring Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/goring-circular/). Undulating downs, hillier start over Lough Down, shorter afternoon return, and Thames-side views. Sights: [St Mary's Church Aldworth](http://www.aldworthvillage.org/index.php/st-marys-church/the-giants/). Steep climbs. Lunch in Aldworth: the [Bell Inn](https://www.facebook.com/The-Bell-Inn-Aldworth-102844626417649/). 12 km.

**Goring & Streatley Circular via Streatley Warren**: A longer variant of [Goring Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/goring-circular/). Ridgeway path extended to Streatley Warren, chalk downland, nature reserve, and Thames views. Sights: Streatley Warren and [St Mary's Church Aldworth](http://www.aldworthvillage.org/index.php/st-marys-church/the-giants/). Seasonal path closure. Best in Oct–Mar. Lunch in Aldworth: the [Bell Inn](https://www.facebook.com/The-Bell-Inn-Aldworth-102844626417649/). 19 km.

**Goring & Streatley Circular via Beetle and Wedge**: A variant of [Cholsey to Goring](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cholsey-to-goring/). Circular route via Moulsford and finishing with a southerly stretch along the Thames. Sights: [Beetle and Wedge](http://www.beetleandwedge.co.uk/). 20 km.

**[Cholsey to Goring & Streatley via Wallingford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cholsey-to-goring-via-wallingford/)**: Thames riverside path, gently rolling Oxfordshire countryside, open fields, and attractive villages. Sights: Pierreponts café. Long, exposed walk. Best in Apr, May, Jun, Sep, Oct and Nov. Lunch in Wallingford, or South Stoke. Wallingford lunch stop: the Boat House. South Stoke lunch stop: the Perch and Pike. 20 km.

**[Cholsey to Goring & Streatley via St Mary's Church, Cholsey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cholsey-to-goring/)**: Gentle Oxfordshire countryside, wide grassy horse training gallops, Ridgeway stretch on Berkshire Downs, and riverside walk along the Thames. Sights: St Mary's Church, Cholsey and [Beetle and Wedge](http://www.beetleandwedge.co.uk/). Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in Blewbury: the [Red Lion](http://www.theredlionblewbury.co.uk/). 23 km.

**Pangbourne to Goring & Streatley**: A shorter variant of [Pangbourne via Goring Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pangbourne-via-goring-circular/). Part-wooded ridges, open pastures, wildflower nature reserve, and Thames-side village. Sights: Whitchurch Bridge, Hartslock Nature Reserve, and Goring Gap. Lunch in Goring-on-Thames: the [Catherine Wheel](http://www.tcwgoring.co.uk/index), the [Chocolate Café](https://www.thechocolatecafeclub.co.uk/goring/), the [Miller of Mansfield](https://themillerofmansfield.com/), or the [Pierreponts Café](http://pierreponts.co.uk/). 8 km.
GOR.publicWalksS2S: **Pangbourne to Goring via Streatley Hill**: Rambler favourite! Woods, hilly, river, and quaint village. Relatively busy area. Plenty of lunch venues in Goring. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 12 km. 190 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2618431014)**

GRC.name: Great Chesterford
GRC.adminWalksAll:
**[Great Chesterford to Newport](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/great-chesterford-to-newport/)**: Rolling Essex farmland, grassy field edges, small woods, river valleys, historic market town, parkland, and pylons over crop fields. Sights: All Saints Church, Great Chesterford, St Mary the Virgin, Little Chesterford, Bridge End Garden, [Fry Art Gallery](https://fryartgallery.org), [St Mary the Virgin, Saffron Walden](https://www.stmaryssaffronwalden.org/Groups/313211/History_and_Guides.aspx), Walden Castle, [Saffron Walden Museum](https://www.saffronwaldenmuseum.org), Turf Maze, Old Sun Inn, Market Place, [Audley End House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/audley-end-house-and-gardens), Audley End Miniature Railway, King Edward VI Almshouses, St Mary the Virgin, Newport, and Monk's Barn. Can be muddy. Lunch in Saffron Walden: the [Eight Bells](https://www.theeightbellssaffronwalden.com), the Kings Arms, the [Cross Keys Hotel](https://www.theoldcrosskeys.co.uk), or the [Temeraire](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/the-temeraire-saffron-walden). 17 km. 4 hours.

**Great Chesterford to Audley End**: A longer variant of [Great Chesterford to Newport](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/great-chesterford-to-newport/). Rolling Essex farmland, grassy field edges, small woods, river valleys, historic market town, parkland, and village with thatched cottages. Sights: All Saints Church, Great Chesterford, St Mary the Virgin, Little Chesterford, Bridge End Garden, [Fry Art Gallery](https://fryartgallery.org), [St Mary the Virgin, Saffron Walden](https://www.stmaryssaffronwalden.org/Groups/313211/History_and_Guides.aspx), Walden Castle, [Saffron Walden Museum](https://www.saffronwaldenmuseum.org), Turf Maze, Old Sun Inn, [Audley End House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/audley-end-house-and-gardens), Audley End Miniature Railway, King Edward VI Almshouses, St Mary the Virgin, Newport, Monk's Barn, and St Mary the Virgin, Wendens Ambo. Can be muddy. Lunch in Saffron Walden: the [Eight Bells](https://www.theeightbellssaffronwalden.com), the Kings Arms, the [Cross Keys Hotel](https://www.theoldcrosskeys.co.uk), or the [Temeraire](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/the-temeraire-saffron-walden). 20 km. 5 hours.
GRC.publicWalksS2S: **[Great Chesterford to Newport](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/great-chesterford-to-newport/)**: Rolling Essex farmland, grassy field edges, small woods, river valleys, historic market town, parkland, and pylons over crop fields. Sights: All Saints Church, Great Chesterford, St Mary the Virgin, Little Chesterford, Bridge End Garden, [Fry Art Gallery](https://fryartgallery.org), [St Mary the Virgin, Saffron Walden](https://www.stmaryssaffronwalden.org/Groups/313211/History_and_Guides.aspx), Walden Castle, [Saffron Walden Museum](https://www.saffronwaldenmuseum.org), Turf Maze, Old Sun Inn, Market Place, [Audley End House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/audley-end-house-and-gardens), Audley End Miniature Railway, King Edward VI Almshouses, St Mary the Virgin, Newport, and Monk's Barn. Can be muddy. Lunch in Saffron Walden: the [Eight Bells](https://www.theeightbellssaffronwalden.com), the Kings Arms, the [Cross Keys Hotel](https://www.theoldcrosskeys.co.uk), or the [Temeraire](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/the-temeraire-saffron-walden). 17 km. 4 hours.

GRN.name: Grindleford
GRN.adminWalksAll:
**[Grindleford Circular via Chatsworth House](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/grindleford-circular-via-chatsworth-house/)**: Ancient oak woodland, gritstone escarpments (edges), open moorland, landscaped parkland, river valley, marshland, wooded wetland, and quiet pastures. Sights: [Chatsworth House](https://www.chatsworth.org/), Curbar Edge, Birchen Edge, Hunting Tower, Nelson's Monument, and Chatsworth Park. Can be muddy. Lunch in Curbar, Baslow, or Chatsworth Estate. Curbar lunch stop: the Jolly's Coffee & Cake. Baslow lunch stop: the [Robin Hood Inn](https://www.robinhoodbaslow.co.uk/). Chatsworth Estate lunch stops: the [Carriage House](https://www.chatsworth.org/), the [Parlour](https://www.chatsworth.org/), or the [Cavendish](https://www.chatsworth.org/). 24 km. 6.5 hours.

**Grindleford Circular via Chatsworth House & White Edge**: An alternative variant of [Grindleford Circular via Chatsworth House](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/grindleford-circular-via-chatsworth-house/). Ancient oak woodland, gritstone escarpments, open upland moorland (Big Moor), exposed White Edge ridge, river valley, and landscaped parkland. Sights: [Chatsworth House](https://www.chatsworth.org/), Curbar Edge, White Edge, Big Moor, and Chatsworth Park. More exposed, hilly. Lunch in Curbar, Baslow, or Chatsworth Estate. Curbar lunch stop: the Jolly's Coffee & Cake. Baslow lunch stop: the [Robin Hood Inn](https://www.robinhoodbaslow.co.uk/). Chatsworth Estate lunch stops: the [Carriage House](https://www.chatsworth.org/), the [Parlour](https://www.chatsworth.org/), or the [Cavendish](https://www.chatsworth.org/). 26 km.

**Hathersage to Grindleford**: A shorter variant of [Hathersage Circular via Stanage Edge Higger Tor and Padley Gorge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hathersage-circular-via-stanage-edge-higger-tor-and-padley-gorge/). Green valley, gritstone escarpment edge, open moorland plateaus, ancient hill fort, and narrow wooded gorge with tumbling waterfalls. Sights: Stanage Edge, North Lees Hall, Brontë Cottage, Higger Tor, Carl Wark, Longshaw Estate, Padley Gorge, Padley Chapel, and Grindleford Station Café. Can be muddy. Lunch in Longshaw: the [Fox House Inn](http://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/yorkshire/thefoxhouselongshaw), or the [Longshaw Tea Room](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/longshaw-burbage-and-the-eastern-moors/features/eating-and-shopping-at-longshaw). 14 km.
GRN.publicWalksCircular: **[Grindleford Circular via Chatsworth House](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/grindleford-circular-via-chatsworth-house/)**: Ancient oak woodland, gritstone escarpments (edges), open moorland, landscaped parkland, river valley, marshland, wooded wetland, and quiet pastures. Sights: [Chatsworth House](https://www.chatsworth.org/), Curbar Edge, Birchen Edge, Hunting Tower, Nelson's Monument, and Chatsworth Park. Can be muddy. Lunch in Curbar, Baslow, or Chatsworth Estate. Curbar lunch stop: the Jolly's Coffee & Cake. Baslow lunch stop: the [Robin Hood Inn](https://www.robinhoodbaslow.co.uk/). Chatsworth Estate lunch stops: the [Carriage House](https://www.chatsworth.org/), the [Parlour](https://www.chatsworth.org/), or the [Cavendish](https://www.chatsworth.org/). 24 km. 6.5 hours.

GRV.name: Gravesend
GRV.adminWalksAll: **[Higham to Gravesend](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/higham-to-gravesend/)**: Flat estuary landscape, gravel tracks, grassy paths, marshes, open water, mud flats, RSPB reserve, industrial heritage, and big skies. Sights: [Cliffe Pools Nature Reserve](http://www.rspb.org.uk/reserves/guide/c/cliffepools/about.aspx), [St Helen's Church Cliffe](http://www.cliffehistory.co.uk/martin.html), Cliffe Fort, St Marys Church Lower Higham, and Brennan Torpedo Tracks. Can be muddy. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. 18 km.
GRV.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Higham to Gravesend](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/higham-to-gravesend/)**: Flat estuary landscape, gravel tracks, grassy paths, marshes, open water, mud flats, RSPB reserve, industrial heritage, and big skies. Sights: [Cliffe Pools Nature Reserve](http://www.rspb.org.uk/reserves/guide/c/cliffepools/about.aspx), [St Helen's Church Cliffe](http://www.cliffehistory.co.uk/martin.html), Cliffe Fort, St Marys Church Lower Higham, and Brennan Torpedo Tracks. Can be muddy. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. 18 km.

GRY.name: Grays
GRY.adminWalksAll: **[Rainham to Grays](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rainham-to-grays/)**: Flat riverside path, marshland, industrial scenery, flood defences, graffiti art walls, former wharves, and Thames views throughout. Sights: [Rainham Hall](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/rainham-hall/), [St Helen's and St Giles' Church](http://www.essexchurches.info/church.asp?p=Rainham), [Rainham Marshes RSPB Nature Reserve](http://www.rspb.org.uk/reserves/guide/r/rainhammarshes/), Purfleet Heritage Museum, Royal Hotel, Dartford Bridge, [St Clement's Church](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Clement%27s_Church,_West_Thurrock), and Concrete barges. Flood risk eastern half. Lunch in Purfleet, or Grays. Purfleet lunch stops: the RSPB Café, or the Royal Hotel. Grays lunch stop: the Wharf. 16 km.
GRY.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Rainham to Grays](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rainham-to-grays/)**: Flat riverside path, marshland, industrial scenery, flood defences, graffiti art walls, former wharves, and Thames views throughout. Sights: [Rainham Hall](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/rainham-hall/), [St Helen's and St Giles' Church](http://www.essexchurches.info/church.asp?p=Rainham), [Rainham Marshes RSPB Nature Reserve](http://www.rspb.org.uk/reserves/guide/r/rainhammarshes/), Purfleet Heritage Museum, Royal Hotel, Dartford Bridge, [St Clement's Church](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Clement%27s_Church,_West_Thurrock), and Concrete barges. Flood risk eastern half. Lunch in Purfleet, or Grays. Purfleet lunch stops: the RSPB Café, or the Royal Hotel. Grays lunch stop: the Wharf. 16 km.

GSD.name: Garsdale
GSD.adminWalksAll:
**[Garsdale Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/baugh-fell/)**: Wild moorland, pathless plateau, rough pasture, lonely valleys, tarns, infant river fords, and a waterfall. Sights: Baugh Fell, Tarn Rigg Hill, Knoutberry Haw, West Baugh Fell Tarn, Grisedale, Ure Force, Dandrymire Viaduct, and [Moorcock Inn](https://www.the-moorcock.co.uk/). Pathless, rough ground. 19 km. 6 hours.

**[Garsdale to Kirkby Stephen](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wild-boar-fell/)**: Remote moorland ridge, lonely dales, upland fells, scarp edges, river pastures, commons, and bucolic valley farmland. Sights: Pendragon Castle, Wild Boar Fell, Swarth Fell, and Mallerstang Edge. Can be boggy. 23 km. 6 hours.

**Kirkby Stephen to Garsdale**: A longer variant of [Nine Standards](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/nine-standards/). High exposed upland watershed, moorland, open fells, source of Eden River, and good track above Mallerstang Valley. Sights: Nine Standards, High Seat, Hugh Seat, and Hell Gill Bridge. Exposed high ground. 29 km. 8 hours.
GSD.publicWalksS2S: **[Garsdale to Kirkby Stephen](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wild-boar-fell/)**: Remote moorland ridge, lonely dales, upland fells, scarp edges, river pastures, commons, and bucolic valley farmland. Sights: Pendragon Castle, Wild Boar Fell, Swarth Fell, and Mallerstang Edge. Can be boggy. 23 km. 6 hours.
GSD.publicWalksCircular: **[Garsdale Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/baugh-fell/)**: Wild moorland, pathless plateau, rough pasture, lonely valleys, tarns, infant river fords, and a waterfall. Sights: Baugh Fell, Tarn Rigg Hill, Knoutberry Haw, West Baugh Fell Tarn, Grisedale, Ure Force, Dandrymire Viaduct, and [Moorcock Inn](https://www.the-moorcock.co.uk/). Pathless, rough ground. 19 km. 6 hours.

GSN.name: Garston
GSN.adminWalksAll:
**Garston to St Albans via Moor Mill**: Common woodland, farmland tracks, river meadows, water meadows, quiet residential roads, and historic city streets. Sights: Bricket Wood Common, Munden House, Moor Mill, Netherwylde Farm, [Verulamium Museum](http://www.stalbansmuseums.org.uk/Sites/Verulamium-Museum), [Verulamium](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Verulamium), [Roman Theatre](http://www.romantheatre.co.uk/), St Albans Cathedral, Great Gateway, Ye Olde Fighting Cocks, Clock Tower, Roman Wall, Hypocaust, and Sopwell Nunnery. Can be very muddy. Lunch in Bricket Wood, or Park Street. Bricket Wood lunch stop: the Moor Mill. Park Street lunch stops: the Overdraught, or the Falcon. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 12 km. 60 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920312228)**

**Garston to Watford North**: An alternative variant of [Abbey Trail](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/). 4 km.

**Garston to St Albans Abbey**: Common woodland, farmland tracks, river meadows, water meadows, quiet residential roads, and historic city streets. Sights: Bricket Wood Common, Munden House, Moor Mill, Netherwylde Farm, [Verulamium Museum](http://www.stalbansmuseums.org.uk/Sites/Verulamium-Museum), [Verulamium](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Verulamium), [Roman Theatre](http://www.romantheatre.co.uk/), St Albans Cathedral, Great Gateway, Ye Olde Fighting Cocks, Clock Tower, Roman Wall, Hypocaust, and Sopwell Nunnery. Can be very muddy. Lunch in Bricket Wood, or Park Street. Bricket Wood lunch stop: the Moor Mill. Park Street lunch stops: the Overdraught, or the Falcon. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).

**Bricket Wood to Garston**: An alternative variant of [Abbey Trail](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/). 4 km.
GSN.publicWalksS2S: **Garston to St Albans via Moor Mill**: Common woodland, farmland tracks, river meadows, water meadows, quiet residential roads, and historic city streets. Sights: Bricket Wood Common, Munden House, Moor Mill, Netherwylde Farm, [Verulamium Museum](http://www.stalbansmuseums.org.uk/Sites/Verulamium-Museum), [Verulamium](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Verulamium), [Roman Theatre](http://www.romantheatre.co.uk/), St Albans Cathedral, Great Gateway, Ye Olde Fighting Cocks, Clock Tower, Roman Wall, Hypocaust, and Sopwell Nunnery. Can be very muddy. Lunch in Bricket Wood, or Park Street. Bricket Wood lunch stop: the Moor Mill. Park Street lunch stops: the Overdraught, or the Falcon. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 12 km. 60 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920312228)**

HAG.name: Hagley
HAG.adminWalksAll:
**Hagley Circular via the Clent Hills & Calcot Hill**: A longer variant of [Hagley to Barnt Green via the Clent Hills](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hagley-to-barnt-green-via-the-clent-hills/). Open hilltops, ridge paths, woods, pastures, valley paths, quiet lanes, and country park fringe. Sights: [Clent Hills](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clent_Hills), [Hagley Park](https://www.hagleypark.co.uk/), The Four Stones, Walton Hill, Calcot Hill, and Hagley Hall. Lunch in Clent: the [Vine Inn](https://www.vineinnclent.com/), the [Hill Tavern](https://hilltavernclent.co.uk/), the [Four Stones Coffee & Brunch](https://fourstonesclent.co.uk/), or the [Woodman at Clent](https://www.thewoodmanclent.co.uk/). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Hagley to Barnt Green](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hagley-to-barnt-green-via-the-clent-hills/)**: Ridge walk over North Worcestershire Hills, open hilltops, scrubland, gorse, woods, pastures, motorway bridge crossing, country parks, and heathland with bilberry and heather. Sights: [Clent Hills](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clent_Hills), [Wychbury Hill](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wychbury_Hill), [Hagley Park](https://www.hagleypark.co.uk/), The Four Stones, Walton Hill, Waseley Hills Country Park, Beacon Hill Castle, Lickey Hills Country Park, Bilberry Hill, and Cofton Hill. Steep descents. 19 km. 5 hours.
HAG.publicWalksS2S: **[Hagley to Barnt Green](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hagley-to-barnt-green-via-the-clent-hills/)**: Ridge walk over North Worcestershire Hills, open hilltops, scrubland, gorse, woods, pastures, motorway bridge crossing, country parks, and heathland with bilberry and heather. Sights: [Clent Hills](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clent_Hills), [Wychbury Hill](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wychbury_Hill), [Hagley Park](https://www.hagleypark.co.uk/), The Four Stones, Walton Hill, Waseley Hills Country Park, Beacon Hill Castle, Lickey Hills Country Park, Bilberry Hill, and Cofton Hill. Steep descents. 19 km. 5 hours.

HAI.name: Halling
HAI.adminWalksAll: **Otford to Halling**: A longer variant of [Otford to Snodland](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/otford-to-snodland/). North Downs ridge, southern escarpment, fields, woods, open views south, forest, country park, and open countryside. Sights: Trosley Country Park. Lunch in Wrotham, or Vigo Village. Wrotham lunch stop: the [Rose and Crown](https://www.roseandcrownwrotham.co.uk/). Vigo Village lunch stop: the Village. 23 km.
HAI.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Otford to Halling**: A longer variant of [Otford to Snodland](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/otford-to-snodland/). North Downs ridge, southern escarpment, fields, woods, open views south, forest, country park, and open countryside. Sights: Trosley Country Park. Lunch in Wrotham, or Vigo Village. Wrotham lunch stop: the [Rose and Crown](https://www.roseandcrownwrotham.co.uk/). Vigo Village lunch stop: the Village. 23 km.

HAV.name: Havant
HAV.adminWalksAll: **[Portsmouth Harbour to Havant](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/portsmouth-to-havant-via-hayling-island/)**: Flat coastal walk, seafront promenade, sandy beaches, short ferry crossing, disused railway path, and harbourside shore path. Sights: Spinnaker Tower, Southsea Castle, South Parade Pier, Lumps Fort, Eastney Batteries, Fort Cumberland, [Hayling Island Branch Line](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hayling_Island_Branch_Line), and St Nicholas Chapel. Lunch in Old Portsmouth, Hayling Island, or Langstone. Old Portsmouth lunch stop: the [Spice Island](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/hampshire/spice-island). Hayling Island lunch stops: the [Ferry Boat Inn](http://www.theferryboatinnhaylingisland.co.uk), or the [Inn on the Beach](http://www.innonthebeach.co.uk). Langstone lunch stops: the [Ship Inn](http://www.shiplangstone.co.uk), or the Royal Oak. 20 km.
HAV.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Portsmouth Harbour to Havant](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/portsmouth-to-havant-via-hayling-island/)**: Flat coastal walk, seafront promenade, sandy beaches, short ferry crossing, disused railway path, and harbourside shore path. Sights: Spinnaker Tower, Southsea Castle, South Parade Pier, Lumps Fort, Eastney Batteries, Fort Cumberland, [Hayling Island Branch Line](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hayling_Island_Branch_Line), and St Nicholas Chapel. Lunch in Old Portsmouth, Hayling Island, or Langstone. Old Portsmouth lunch stop: the [Spice Island](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/hampshire/spice-island). Hayling Island lunch stops: the [Ferry Boat Inn](http://www.theferryboatinnhaylingisland.co.uk), or the [Inn on the Beach](http://www.innonthebeach.co.uk). Langstone lunch stops: the [Ship Inn](http://www.shiplangstone.co.uk), or the Royal Oak. 20 km.

HBN.name: Hollingbourne
HBN.adminWalksAll:
**[Hollingbourne Circular via Hollingbourne Meadows](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hollingbourne-circular/)**: Open farm fields, North Downs ridge, chalk grassland, woodland, parkland, moated castle grounds, and meadows. Sights: [Hucking Estate](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/hucking-estate), [Hollingbourne Meadows](http://www.hollingbournemeadowstrust.co.uk), [Leeds Castle](https://leeds-castle.com), The Shepherd, Hollingbourne Manor, and All Saints Hollingbourne. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hollingbourne: the [Park Gate Inn](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/theparkgateinnhollingbourne#). 15 km. 4 hours.

**Hollingbourne Circular via Hucking Estate**: A longer variant of [Hollingbourne Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hollingbourne-circular/). Open farm fields, North Downs ridge, chalk grassland, grassy rides, woodland paths, open valley, castle grounds, and meadows. Sights: [Hucking Estate](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/hucking-estate), St Margaret Hucking, The Shepherd, The Tranchet Axe, The Wild Boar Family, [Leeds Castle](https://leeds-castle.com), Hollingbourne Manor, and All Saints Hollingbourne. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hucking: the [Hook & Hatchet Inn](https://www.hookandhatchetpub.co.uk). 20 km. 5 hours.

**Hollingbourne Circular via Thurnham Castle & St Margaret Hucking**: A longer variant of [Hollingbourne Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hollingbourne-circular/). Open farm fields, North Downs ridge, sunken lanes, hollows, chalk grassland, grassy rides, woodland, open valley, parkland, castle grounds, and meadows. Sights: Thurnham Castle, White Horse Wood Country Park, [Hucking Estate](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/hucking-estate), St Margaret Hucking, The Shepherd, The Tranchet Axe, The Wild Boar Family, [Leeds Castle](https://leeds-castle.com), Hollingbourne Manor, and All Saints Hollingbourne. Can be muddy. Lunch in Thurnham, or Hucking. Thurnham lunch stop: the [Black Horse](https://www.blackhorsekent.co.uk). Hucking lunch stop: the [Hook & Hatchet Inn](https://www.hookandhatchetpub.co.uk). 26 km. 6.5 hours.

**Lenham to Hollingbourne**: A longer variant of [Lenham to Charing](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lenham-to-charing/). North Downs footpaths, little-used field paths, rape seed fields, and woods. Sights: Lenham Cross and Stalisfield Green. Can be overgrown. Lunch in Warren Street, Lenham, Wormshill, Hollingbourne, or Harrietsham. Warren Street, Lenham lunch stop: the [Harrow Hill Hotel & Inn](http://www.harrowhillhotel.com/). Wormshill lunch stop: the [Blacksmiths Arms](https://whatpub.com/pubs/SWA/4470/blacksmiths-arms-wormshill). Hollingbourne lunch stop: the [Dirty Habit](https://elitepubs.com/the-dirty-habit/). Harrietsham lunch stop: the [Ringlestone Inn](https://www.theringlestone.co.uk/). 14 km. 4.5 hours.
HBN.publicWalksCircular: **[Hollingbourne Circular via Hollingbourne Meadows](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hollingbourne-circular/)**: Open farm fields, North Downs ridge, chalk grassland, woodland, parkland, moated castle grounds, and meadows. Sights: [Hucking Estate](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/hucking-estate), [Hollingbourne Meadows](http://www.hollingbournemeadowstrust.co.uk), [Leeds Castle](https://leeds-castle.com), The Shepherd, Hollingbourne Manor, and All Saints Hollingbourne. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hollingbourne: the [Park Gate Inn](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/theparkgateinnhollingbourne#). 15 km. 4 hours.

HCN.name: Headcorn
HCN.adminWalksAll: **[Staplehurst to Headcorn](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/staplehurst-to-headcorn/)**: Fields, low-lying farmland, bluebell woods, and Kentish Weald villages. Sights: Sissinghurst Castle Gardens. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Frittenden: the Bell and Jorrocks. 13 km.
HCN.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Staplehurst to Headcorn](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/staplehurst-to-headcorn/)**: Fields, low-lying farmland, bluebell woods, and Kentish Weald villages. Sights: Sissinghurst Castle Gardens. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Frittenden: the Bell and Jorrocks. 13 km.

HDM.name: Haddenham and Thame Parkway
HDM.adminWalksAll:
**[Haddenham and Thame Parkway Circular via Brill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haddenham-circular-via-brill/)**: Rolling arable farmland, hilltop villages, hayfields, meadows, pastures, and far-reaching panoramic views. Sights: Brill Windmill, Brill Common, and Chilton House. Can be muddy. Lunch in Brill, or Ashendon. Brill lunch stops: the [Pointer](http://www.thepointerbrill.co.uk/), or the [Pheasant](https://www.thepheasantbrill.co.uk/). Ashendon lunch stop: the [Ash Tree](https://www.theashtree.co.uk/). 27 km. 6.5 hours.

**Haddenham and Thame Parkway Circular via Gibraltar and Ford**: A shorter variant of [Haddenham to Aylesbury via Gibraltar and Ford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haddenham-to-aylesbury-via-gibraltar-and-ford/). Flat Thame Valley farmland, tranquil hamlets, pastures, and bus return along the A418. Sights: Notley Abbey and Dinton Hall. Lunch in Cuddington, Gibraltar, Dinton, or Ford. Cuddington lunch stop: the [Crown at Cuddington](https://www.thecrowncuddington.com/). Gibraltar lunch stop: the [Bottle & Glass](http://www.thebottleandglass.com/). Dinton lunch stop: the [Seven Stars](http://www.sevenstarsdinton.com/). Ford lunch stop: the [Dinton Hermit Inn](https://www.thedintonhermit.co.uk/).

**[Haddenham and Thame Parkway to Aylesbury via Gibraltar and Ford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haddenham-to-aylesbury-via-gibraltar-and-ford/)**: Flat Thame Valley farmland, tranquil hamlets, pastures, community woodland, and stately mansion grounds. Sights: Notley Abbey, Dinton Hall, [Hartwell House](http://www.hartwell-house.com/spa-cafe-and-bar/), and [King's Head](http://www.kingsheadaylesbury.co.uk/). Lunch in Cuddington, Gibraltar, Dinton, or Ford. Cuddington lunch stop: the [Crown at Cuddington](https://www.thecrowncuddington.com/). Gibraltar lunch stop: the [Bottle & Glass](http://www.thebottleandglass.com/). Dinton lunch stop: the [Seven Stars](http://www.sevenstarsdinton.com/). Ford lunch stop: the [Dinton Hermit Inn](https://www.thedintonhermit.co.uk/). 23 km. 5 hours.

**[Haddenham and Thame Parkway to Aylesbury via Waddesdon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haddenham-to-aylesbury-via-waddesdon/)**: Thame Valley floodplain, energetic climbs, open vistas, Rothschild estate parkland, tree-lined avenue, and river and brook paths into a market town. Sights: [Waddesdon Manor](http://www.waddesdon.org.uk/), Eythrope Park, and Upper Winchendon. Lunch in Waddesdon: the [Waddesdon Manor](http://www.waddesdon.org.uk/), the [Five Arrows Hotel and Restaurant](https://fivearrowshotel.co.uk/), the [Bow](https://www.thebowwaddesdon.co.uk/), the 94 Coffee Shop, or the [Lion](http://www.thelionwaddesdon.co.uk/). 25 km. 6 hours.

**Haddenham and Thame Parkway to Aylesbury Vale Parkway**: An alternative variant of [Haddenham to Aylesbury via Waddesdon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haddenham-to-aylesbury-via-waddesdon/). Thame Valley floodplain, energetic climbs, open vistas, Rothschild estate parkland, and tree-lined avenue. Sights: [Waddesdon Manor](http://www.waddesdon.org.uk/), Eythrope Park, and Upper Winchendon. Lunch in Waddesdon: the [Waddesdon Manor](http://www.waddesdon.org.uk/), the [Five Arrows Hotel and Restaurant](https://fivearrowshotel.co.uk/), the [Bow](https://www.thebowwaddesdon.co.uk/), the 94 Coffee Shop, or the [Lion](http://www.thelionwaddesdon.co.uk/). 19 km.

Sights: Brill Windmill, Brill Common, and Chilton House. Can be muddy. Lunch in Brill, or Ashendon. Brill lunch stops: the [Pointer](http://www.thepointerbrill.co.uk/), or the [Pheasant](https://www.thepheasantbrill.co.uk/). Ashendon lunch stop: the [Ash Tree](https://www.theashtree.co.uk/). 27 km. 6.5 hours.

HEV.name: Hever
HEV.adminWalksAll:
**Hever to Leigh via Chiddingstone Castle**: Undulating hills, open fields, beech woods, rivers, ponds, quaint villages, and historic estates. Sights: [Hever Castle](http://www.hevercastle.co.uk/), St Peter's Church, Chiddingstone Castle, St Mary's Church, Chiding Stone, [Penshurst Place](http://www.penshurstplace.com/), St John the Baptist Church, and Luke Tapestry. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chiddingstone, or Penshurst. Chiddingstone lunch stops: the [Castle Inn](http://www.thecastleinnchiddingstone.com/), or the Tulip Tree Tearooms. Penshurst lunch stop: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](http://www.leicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Hever to Dormans](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-dormans/)**: Undulating countryside, woods, farmland, fields, streams, historic manor houses, iron-age hillfort, remote border area of Kent, and Surrey and Sussex. Sights: Cowden, St Mary Magdalene Church, Sussex Border Path, Waystrode Manor, Crippenden Manor, Vanguard Way, Dry Hill Fort, and Greathed Manor. Can be muddy. Lunch in Cowden: the [Fountain](https://www.thefountaininncowden.com/). 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Hever to Ashurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-ashurst/)**: Low hills, wooded valleys, farm fields, bluebell woods, manor house grounds, golf course, orchards, and remote High Weald countryside. Sights: Crippenden Manor, Leighton Manor, Waystrode Manor, Sussex Border Path, Sussex House Farm, Heathersome's Wood, Bolebroke Castle, [Orchard Tea Room](https://www.perryhillorchards.co.uk/orchard-tea-room), St Mary Magdalene, and [Cowden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cowden). Can be muddy. Lunch in Cowden: the [Fountain](https://www.thefountaincowden.co.uk). 16 km. 4 hours.

**[Hever to Tonbridge via Tonbridge Castle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-tonbridge/)**: Gentle farmland, orchards, river valleys, parkland, country park lakes, and riverside paths into a market town. Sights: [Hever Castle](https://www.hevercastle.co.uk), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Tonbridge Castle](https://www.tonbridgecastle.org), St Peter's Church Hever, St John the Baptist Church Penshurst, Leicester Square Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Chippens Bank House, Withers, Straight Mile, and Powder Mills. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**Hever to Leigh via Hever Castle**: Undulating hills, open fields, beech woods, rivers, ponds, and historic estates. Sights: [Hever Castle](http://www.hevercastle.co.uk/), St Peter's Church, [Penshurst Place](http://www.penshurstplace.com/), and St John the Baptist Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](http://www.leicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 12 km.

**Hever to Penshurst via Penshurst Place**: A shorter variant of [Hever to Tonbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-tonbridge/). Farmland, river valley, parkland, and historic village. Sights: [Hever Castle](https://www.hevercastle.co.uk), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), St Peter's Church Hever, St John the Baptist Church Penshurst, Leicester Square Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Chippens Bank House, and Withers. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). 12 km. 3 hours.

**Hever to Cowden**: A shorter variant of [Hever to Ashurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-ashurst/). Low hills, wooded valleys, farm fields, manor house grounds, and remote High Weald countryside. Sights: Crippenden Manor, Leighton Manor, Waystrode Manor, Sussex Border Path, St Mary Magdalene, and [Cowden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cowden). Can be muddy. Lunch in Cowden: the [Fountain](https://www.thefountaincowden.co.uk). 11 km. 2.5 hours.

**Hever to Tonbridge via Chiddingstone Castle**: Undulating hills, open fields, rivers, Haysden Country Park, and historic estates. Sights: [Hever Castle](http://www.hevercastle.co.uk/), Chiddingstone Castle, [Penshurst Place](http://www.penshurstplace.com/), and Haysden Country Park. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chiddingstone, or Penshurst. Chiddingstone lunch stop: the [Castle Inn](http://www.thecastleinnchiddingstone.com/). Penshurst lunch stop: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](http://www.leicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk/). A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 21 km.

**Hever to Penshurst via Chiddingstone Castle**: Fields, woods, and country lanes across to Penshurst Station. Sights: [Hever Castle](http://www.hevercastle.co.uk/), Chiddingstone Castle, and St Mary's Church. Lunch in Chiddingstone: the [Castle Inn](http://www.thecastleinnchiddingstone.com/). An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).

**[Cowden to Hever](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cowden-to-hever/)**: Remote wooded valleys, sandstone outcrops, undulating High Weald farmland, vineyard, Eden Valley meadows, quaint village streets, and historic castle grounds. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), [Hever Castle](https://www.hevercastle.co.uk), St John the Baptist, Penshurst, St Mary, Chiddingstone, St Peter, Hever, Chiding Stone, Leicester Square, and Harden Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst, or Hoath Corner. Penshurst lunch stops: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). Hoath Corner lunch stop: the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Edenbridge Town to Hever via Edenbridge**: An alternative variant of [Edenbridge Figure of 8](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edenbridge-figure-of-8/). Water meadows, riverside paths, farm airstrip, and flat Eden Valley fields to Hever. Sights: [Edenbridge](https://www.visitedenbridge.com), [River Eden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Eden,_Kent), [Eden Valley Museum](https://evmt.org.uk), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), Stone Bridge, Gabriel's Fishery, and Devil's Den. Can be waterlogged. Lunch in Edenbridge: the [Old Eden](https://www.theoldeden.com), or the [Ye Olde Crown](https://www.yeoldecrown.co.uk). 12 km. 2.5 hours.

**Edenbridge Town to Hever via Great Stone Bridge Trust**: A longer variant of [Edenbridge Figure of 8](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edenbridge-figure-of-8/). Gentle farmland, water meadows, riverside paths, farm airstrip, flat Eden Valley, and fields to Hever. Sights: [Edenbridge](https://www.visitedenbridge.com), [River Eden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Eden,_Kent), [Eden Valley Museum](https://evmt.org.uk), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), Stone Bridge, Great Stone Bridge Trust, Edenbridge Water Meadows, Gabriel's Fishery, and Devil's Den. Can be waterlogged. Lunch in Edenbridge: the [Old Eden](https://www.theoldeden.com), or the [Ye Olde Crown](https://www.yeoldecrown.co.uk). 18 km. 4 hours.
