HEX.name: Hexham
HEX.adminWalksAll:
**Hexham Circular (HWP: Walwick to Planetrees)**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). River Tyne, quiet country lanes, North Tyne Valley, ridge, Wall stretches, and village of Acomb. Sights: Chesters Fort, Brunton Turret, and Carrowburgh Fort. Lunch in Chollerford, or Acomb. Chollerford lunch stops: the Riverside Kitchen Café, or the George Hotel. Acomb lunch stops: the Miners Arms, the Sun Inn, or the Queens Arms Hotel. 20 km.

**Hexham to Corbridge**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). River Tyne, country lanes, North Tyne Valley views, Wall stretches, ridge, and historic town of Corbridge. Sights: Chesters Fort, Brunton Turret, St. Oswald's Church, Halton Chesters Fort, Aydon Castle, Halton Castle, and Halton Church. Lunch in Wall, or near Halton Chesters. Wall lunch stop: the Hadrian Hotel. near Halton Chesters lunch stop: the Errington Coffee House. 21 km.

**Bardon Mill to Hexham**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). Common, upland plateau, crags, scenic Wall, gentle descent into North Tyne Valley, riverside, and country lanes. Sights: Housesteads Fort, Sycamore Gap, Carrowburgh Fort, Mithraeum, Black Carts Turret, Chesters Fort, Brunton Turret, and Vindolanda Roman Fort. Lunch in Housesteads, Chesters, or Chollerford. Housesteads lunch stop: the Housesteads Fort Café. Chesters lunch stop: the Chesters Fort Tearoom. Chollerford lunch stops: the Riverside Kitchen Café, or the George Hotel. 28 km.
HEX.publicWalksS2S: **Hexham to Corbridge**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). River Tyne, country lanes, North Tyne Valley views, Wall stretches, ridge, and historic town of Corbridge. Sights: Chesters Fort, Brunton Turret, St. Oswald's Church, Halton Chesters Fort, Aydon Castle, Halton Castle, and Halton Church. Lunch in Wall, or near Halton Chesters. Wall lunch stop: the Hadrian Hotel. near Halton Chesters lunch stop: the Errington Coffee House. 21 km.
HEX.publicWalksCircular: **Hexham Circular (HWP: Walwick to Planetrees)**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). River Tyne, quiet country lanes, North Tyne Valley, ridge, Wall stretches, and village of Acomb. Sights: Chesters Fort, Brunton Turret, and Carrowburgh Fort. Lunch in Chollerford, or Acomb. Chollerford lunch stops: the Riverside Kitchen Café, or the George Hotel. Acomb lunch stops: the Miners Arms, the Sun Inn, or the Queens Arms Hotel. 20 km.
HEX.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Bardon Mill to Hexham**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). Common, upland plateau, crags, scenic Wall, gentle descent into North Tyne Valley, riverside, and country lanes. Sights: Housesteads Fort, Sycamore Gap, Carrowburgh Fort, Mithraeum, Black Carts Turret, Chesters Fort, Brunton Turret, and Vindolanda Roman Fort. Lunch in Housesteads, Chesters, or Chollerford. Housesteads lunch stop: the Housesteads Fort Café. Chesters lunch stop: the Chesters Fort Tearoom. Chollerford lunch stops: the Riverside Kitchen Café, or the George Hotel. 28 km.

HGM.name: Higham
HGM.adminWalksAll: **[Higham to Gravesend](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/higham-to-gravesend/)**: Flat estuary landscape, gravel tracks, grassy paths, marshes, open water, mud flats, RSPB reserve, industrial heritage, and big skies. Sights: [Cliffe Pools Nature Reserve](http://www.rspb.org.uk/reserves/guide/c/cliffepools/about.aspx), [St Helen's Church Cliffe](http://www.cliffehistory.co.uk/martin.html), Cliffe Fort, St Marys Church Lower Higham, and Brennan Torpedo Tracks. Can be muddy. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. 18 km.
HGM.publicWalksS2S: **[Higham to Gravesend](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/higham-to-gravesend/)**: Flat estuary landscape, gravel tracks, grassy paths, marshes, open water, mud flats, RSPB reserve, industrial heritage, and big skies. Sights: [Cliffe Pools Nature Reserve](http://www.rspb.org.uk/reserves/guide/c/cliffepools/about.aspx), [St Helen's Church Cliffe](http://www.cliffehistory.co.uk/martin.html), Cliffe Fort, St Marys Church Lower Higham, and Brennan Torpedo Tracks. Can be muddy. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. 18 km.

HGS.name: Hastings
HGS.adminWalksAll:
**Winchelsea to Hastings**: Rambler favourite! From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 29 June 2024.* 18 km. 320 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/693693647)**

**[Hastings Circular via Fairlight Glen Beach](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hastings-circular-via-fairlight-glen-beach/)**: Hilly clifftop path, steep glens, shingle and sandy beach, forested inland valleys, coastal headlands, bracken, and eroding cliffs. Sights: Hastings Castle, Jerwood Art Gallery, Fairlight Glen Beach, [St Andrew's Church](http://www.sussexparishchurches.org/content/view/170/34/), and [Hastings County Park](http://www.1066online.com/HASTINGS-MAIN/Places-to-Visit/visit-hastings/country-park.html). Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch in Fairlight: the [Coastguards Tea Rooms](http://www.coastguardstearooms.co.uk/). 13 km.

**Hastings Circular via St Clements Caves**: Rambler favourite! Clifftop coastal path, steep climbs, Hastings Old Town, caves, and castle. Sights: Hastings Castle and St Clements Caves. Crumbly cliff edges. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 9 km. 3 hours.

**Hastings to Rye**: Rambler favourite! Clifftop coastal path, steep climbs, remote naturist beach, flat marshland, ancient hilltop towns, and panoramic viewpoints. Sights: Hastings Castle, St Clements Caves, New Gate, St. Thomas' Church, The Look Out, and [Hastings Country Park](https://www.hastingscountryparkhistory.com/index.php). Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch in Fairlight, or Pett Level. Fairlight lunch stop: the [Cove](https://www.thecovefairlight.co.uk). Pett Level lunch stops: the [Coastguard Café](https://www.coastguardcafe.co.uk/), or the [Eater's](https://www.facebook.com/eatersatpett/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 19 km. 6 hours.

**Hastings to Winchelsea**: Rambler favourite! Clifftop coastal path, steep climbs, naturist beach, flat marshland, and ancient hilltop town. Sights: Hastings Castle, St Clements Caves, New Gate, and St. Thomas' Church. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch in Fairlight: the [Cove](https://www.thecovefairlight.co.uk). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km.

**Winchelsea to Hastings via All Saints Church**: Rambler favourite! Flat river levels, farmland, hilly coastal cliffs, wooded glens, naturist beach, and historic old town. Sights: All Saints Church, Hog Hill windmill, [Hastings Country Park](https://www.hastingscountryparkhistory.com/index.php), Fairlight Glen nudist beach, and Hastings Castle. Steep cliff climbs. Best in Mar–Aug. Lunch in Icklesham, Pett, Pett Level, or Fairlight Cove. Icklesham lunch stop: the Queens Head. Pett lunch stop: the Royal Oak Inn. Pett Level lunch stops: the [C-Side cafe](https://www.csidecafe.co.uk/), or the [Cockles and Dreams](https://www.facebook.com/cocklesanddreams/). Fairlight Cove lunch stop: the [Cove Inn](https://www.thecovefairlight.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 20 km. 6 hours.

**[Winchelsea to Hastings via Three Oaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings-via-three-oaks/)**: Undulating ridge, pastureland, farms, woods, hilly territory with fine views, coastal cliff path with steep ascents and descents, and historic walled town. Sights: Winchelsea, New Gate, Icklesham Church, Doleham station, Fairlight Hall, Fairlight Glen, Ecclesbourne Glen, East Hill Lift, and Hastings Old Town. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Three Oaks, or Fairlight. Three Oaks lunch stop: the Three Oaks pub. Fairlight lunch stops: the Coastguard Cafe, or the Grumpy Cook Cafe. 26 km.

**[Eastbourne to Hastings](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eastbourne-to-hastings-via-bexhill/)**: Flat coastal seafront promenades, shingle beach, sandy beach at low tide, sea wall paths, quiet coast roads, and low cliffs. Sights: [Eastbourne Pier](http://www.eastbournepier.com/), [Eastbourne Harbour](http://www.eastbourneharbour.com/walks.php), [Martello Towers](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Martello_tower), [Pevensey Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/daysout/properties/pevensey-castle/), [De La Warr Pavilion](http://www.dlwp.com/), and Hastings Pier. Shingle heavy going. Lunch in Pevensey Bay, Norman's Bay, Cooden Beach, Bexhill, or St Leonard's. Pevensey Bay lunch stops: the Aqua Bar, the Moorings, or the [Castle Inn](http://castleinnpevenseybay.co.uk/). Norman's Bay lunch stop: the [Star Inn](https://www.thestarinnnormansbay.co.uk/). Cooden Beach lunch stop: the [Relais Cooden Beach](https://www.therelaisretreats.com/coodenbeach/). Bexhill lunch stop: the [De La Warr Pavilion Cafe, Bar & Kitchen](http://www.dlwp.com/). St Leonard's lunch stops: the [Farmyard](https://farmyardwine.com/), or the [Royal](http://theroyalstleonards.co.uk/). 26 km.

**Rye to Hastings**: Rambler favourite! Flat levels, farmland, hilly coastal cliffs, wooded glens, naturist beach, and historic old town. Sights: All Saints Church, Hog Hill windmill, [Hastings Country Park](https://www.hastingscountryparkhistory.com/index.php), Fairlight Glen nudist beach, and Hastings Castle. Steep cliff climbs. Best in Mar–Aug. Lunch in Icklesham, Pett, Pett Level, or Fairlight Cove. Icklesham lunch stop: the Queens Head. Pett lunch stop: the Royal Oak Inn. Pett Level lunch stops: the [C-Side cafe](https://www.csidecafe.co.uk/), or the [Cockles and Dreams](https://www.facebook.com/cocklesanddreams/). Fairlight Cove lunch stop: the [Cove Inn](https://www.thecovefairlight.co.uk/). A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 24 km.

**Three Oaks to Hastings**: A shorter variant of [Winchelsea to Hastings via Three Oaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings-via-three-oaks/). Varied hilly terrain, woods, cliff-top coast path, steep ascents and descents, and old town. Sights: Guestling Church, Fairlight Hall, Fairlight Glen, Ecclesbourne Glen, East Hill Lift, and Hastings Old Town. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Fairlight: the Coastguard Cafe, or the Grumpy Cook Cafe. 13 km.

**Cooden Beach to Hastings**: A shorter variant of [Eastbourne to Hastings via Bexhill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eastbourne-to-hastings-via-bexhill/). Seafront promenade, sandy paths, secluded bays, low cliffs, and Hastings promenade. Sights: [De La Warr Pavilion](http://www.dlwp.com/) and Hastings Pier. Lunch in Bexhill: the [De La Warr Pavilion Cafe, Bar & Kitchen](http://www.dlwp.com/). 11 km.
HGS.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Winchelsea to Hastings**: Rambler favourite! From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 18 km. 320 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/693693647)**

HHE.name: Haywards Heath
HHE.adminWalksAll:
**[Haywards Heath Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haywards-heath-circular-via-lindfield/)**: Low hills, Ouse Valley, woodland, nature reserves, fields, hedgerows, historic villages, and canal-era river crossings. Sights: Scrase Valley Local Nature Reserve, Eastern Road Local Nature Reserve, Sussex Ouse Valley Way, [Bluebell Railway](https://www.bluebell-railway.com), Bluebell Vineyard, Freshfield Lane Brickworks, [Lindfield](http://www.westsussex.info/lindfield.shtml), All Saints Lindfield, Church House, Toll House, and Ouse Navigation. Can be muddy. Lunch in Freshfield Bridges: the [Sloop Inn](https://www.thesloopinn.com). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Haywards Heath to Balcombe via the Ouse valley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haywards-heath-to-balcombe/)**: Rolling High Weald hills, woodland, farmland, river valley, golf course, level crossing, and quiet lanes. Sights: [Ouse Valley Viaduct](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ouse_Valley_Viaduct), [Sussex Ouse Valley Way](http://www.sussexousevalleyway.co.uk/index.htm), and [High Weald Landscape Trail](http://www.highweald.org/home/news/1850-new-online-guide-for-landscape-trail-.html). Can be muddy. 10 km. 2.5 hours.

**[Haywards Heath to Balcombe via Ardingly](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haywards-heath-to-balcombe-via-ardingly/)**: Undulating High Weald farmland, woodland, golf course, reservoir dam wall, fields, and a private school grounds. Sights: Ardingly Reservoir, [South of England Event Centre](https://southofenglandeventcentre.co.uk/events), Ardingly College, Ouse Valley Viaduct, Stone Hall, Balcombe Place, St Peter, Ardingly, Balcombe Lake, Sussex Ouse Valley Way, and High Weald Landscape Trail. Can be muddy. Lunch in Ardingly Reservoir, or Ardingly. Ardingly Reservoir lunch stop: the [Ardingly Activity Centre Café](https://www.ardinglyactivitycentre.co.uk/our-cafe). Ardingly lunch stops: the [Ardingly Inn](https://theardinglyinn.co.uk), the Fellows Bakery, or the Ardingly Café. 16 km. 4 hours.

**[Wivelsfield to Haywards Heath via Cuckfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wivelsfield-to-haywards-heath/)**: Woods, wildflower meadows, farmland, High Weald ridge, golf course, and suburban road sections. Sights: [Bedelands Farm Local Nature Reserve](https://bh-green-circle.org.uk/sites/bedelands-local-nature-reserve), [Holy Trinity Church](https://www.holytrinitycuckfield.org/welcome/church-history-2), [Cuckfield](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cuckfield), [Cuckfield Museum](https://cuckfieldmuseum.org), [High Weald Landscape Trail](https://highweald.org/things-to-do/high-weald-landscape-trail), [Borde Hill Garden](https://www.bordehill.co.uk), Ockenden Manor, Cuckfield Park, New England Wood, and Ouse Valley Viaduct. Can be muddy. Lunch in Cuckfield: the [Talbot](https://www.thetalbotcuckfield.co.uk), the Cuckfield Pantry & Tearoom, or the [Tom's Food](https://tomsfood.co.uk/home). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Wivelsfield to Haywards Heath via Bedelands Farm Local Nature Reserve**: A shorter variant of [Wivelsfield to Haywards Heath](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wivelsfield-to-haywards-heath/). Woods, wildflower meadows, farmland, High Weald, parkland, and suburban road sections. Sights: [Bedelands Farm Local Nature Reserve](https://bh-green-circle.org.uk/sites/bedelands-local-nature-reserve), [Holy Trinity Church](https://www.holytrinitycuckfield.org/welcome/church-history-2), [Cuckfield Museum](https://cuckfieldmuseum.org), [Borde Hill Garden](https://www.bordehill.co.uk), Ockenden Manor, and Ouse Valley Viaduct. Can be muddy. Lunch in Cuckfield: the [Talbot](https://www.thetalbotcuckfield.co.uk), the Cuckfield Pantry & Tearoom, or the [Tom's Food](https://tomsfood.co.uk/home). 14 km. 3.5 hours.
HHE.publicWalksS2S:
**[Haywards Heath to Balcombe via the Ouse valley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haywards-heath-to-balcombe/)**: Rolling High Weald hills, woodland, farmland, river valley, golf course, level crossing, and quiet lanes. Sights: [Ouse Valley Viaduct](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ouse_Valley_Viaduct), [Sussex Ouse Valley Way](http://www.sussexousevalleyway.co.uk/index.htm), and [High Weald Landscape Trail](http://www.highweald.org/home/news/1850-new-online-guide-for-landscape-trail-.html). Can be muddy. 10 km. 2.5 hours.

**[Haywards Heath to Balcombe via Ardingly](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haywards-heath-to-balcombe-via-ardingly/)**: Undulating High Weald farmland, woodland, golf course, reservoir dam wall, fields, and a private school grounds. Sights: Ardingly Reservoir, [South of England Event Centre](https://southofenglandeventcentre.co.uk/events), Ardingly College, Ouse Valley Viaduct, Stone Hall, Balcombe Place, St Peter, Ardingly, Balcombe Lake, Sussex Ouse Valley Way, and High Weald Landscape Trail. Can be muddy. Lunch in Ardingly Reservoir, or Ardingly. Ardingly Reservoir lunch stop: the [Ardingly Activity Centre Café](https://www.ardinglyactivitycentre.co.uk/our-cafe). Ardingly lunch stops: the [Ardingly Inn](https://theardinglyinn.co.uk), the Fellows Bakery, or the Ardingly Café. 16 km. 4 hours.
HHE.publicWalksCircular: **[Haywards Heath Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haywards-heath-circular-via-lindfield/)**: Low hills, Ouse Valley, woodland, nature reserves, fields, hedgerows, historic villages, and canal-era river crossings. Sights: Scrase Valley Local Nature Reserve, Eastern Road Local Nature Reserve, Sussex Ouse Valley Way, [Bluebell Railway](https://www.bluebell-railway.com), Bluebell Vineyard, Freshfield Lane Brickworks, [Lindfield](http://www.westsussex.info/lindfield.shtml), All Saints Lindfield, Church House, Toll House, and Ouse Navigation. Can be muddy. Lunch in Freshfield Bridges: the [Sloop Inn](https://www.thesloopinn.com). 18 km. 4.5 hours.
HHE.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Wivelsfield to Haywards Heath via Cuckfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wivelsfield-to-haywards-heath/)**: Woods, wildflower meadows, farmland, High Weald ridge, golf course, and suburban road sections. Sights: [Bedelands Farm Local Nature Reserve](https://bh-green-circle.org.uk/sites/bedelands-local-nature-reserve), [Holy Trinity Church](https://www.holytrinitycuckfield.org/welcome/church-history-2), [Cuckfield](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cuckfield), [Cuckfield Museum](https://cuckfieldmuseum.org), [High Weald Landscape Trail](https://highweald.org/things-to-do/high-weald-landscape-trail), [Borde Hill Garden](https://www.bordehill.co.uk), Ockenden Manor, Cuckfield Park, New England Wood, and Ouse Valley Viaduct. Can be muddy. Lunch in Cuckfield: the [Talbot](https://www.thetalbotcuckfield.co.uk), the Cuckfield Pantry & Tearoom, or the [Tom's Food](https://tomsfood.co.uk/home). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

HIA.name: Hampton-in-Arden
HIA.adminWalksAll: **[Hampton-in-Arden Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-850)**: Ancient village, tarmacked lanes, River Blythe crossing, nature reserve pools and hides, packhorse bridge, and open countryside. Sights: [Marsh Lane Nature Reserve](https://www.packingtonestate.co.uk), [St. Mary & St. Bartholomew](https://www.hamptoninardenchurch.org.uk), and [Hampton Manor](https://www.hamptonmanor.com). Can be muddy. Lunch in Hampton-in-Arden: the [White Lion](https://www.thewhitelioninn.com), the [Hampton Corner Shop](https://www.hamptoncornershop.com), the [Sandwiches @ No.6](https://www.facebook.com/sandwichesatnumbersix), or the [Peel's at Hampton Manor](https://www.hamptonmanor.com). 5 km. 1.5 hours.
HIA.publicWalksCircular: **[Hampton-in-Arden Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-850)**: Ancient village, tarmacked lanes, River Blythe crossing, nature reserve pools and hides, packhorse bridge, and open countryside. Sights: [Marsh Lane Nature Reserve](https://www.packingtonestate.co.uk), [St. Mary & St. Bartholomew](https://www.hamptoninardenchurch.org.uk), and [Hampton Manor](https://www.hamptonmanor.com). Can be muddy. Lunch in Hampton-in-Arden: the [White Lion](https://www.thewhitelioninn.com), the [Hampton Corner Shop](https://www.hamptoncornershop.com), the [Sandwiches @ No.6](https://www.facebook.com/sandwichesatnumbersix), or the [Peel's at Hampton Manor](https://www.hamptonmanor.com). 5 km. 1.5 hours.

HIR.name: Horton-in-Ribblesdale
HIR.adminWalksAll:
**[Horton-in-Ribblesdale Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pen-y-ghent-and-plover-hill/)**: Open moorland, remote valleys, engineered hill paths, ancient trading routes, rocky scrambles, boggy plateau, and sweeping Dales views. Sights: Pen-y-ghent, Plover Hill, Hull Pot, and Pendle Hill. Can be boggy. 17 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Horton-in-Ribblesdale to Ribblehead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ingleborough-via-moughton-scars-and-gaping-gill/)**: Limestone pavements, caves, dramatic gorge, moorland fell, high exposed plateau, steep engineered paths, quiet valley, and Iron Age hillfort remains. Sights: Moughton Scars, Trow Gill, Gaping Gill, Ingleborough, and Ribblehead Viaduct. Steep ascent/descent. 20 km. 6 hours.

**[Horton-in-Ribblesdale to Settle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/fountains-fell-and-catrigg-force/)**: Wild remote fell, moorland plateau, Pennine Way paths, lonely pastures, limestone country, waterfall, elevated bridleways above valley, and views of green dales and tarn. Sights: Fountains Fell, Pen-y-ghent, Malham Tarn, Catrigg Force, and Winskill Stones National Nature Reserve. Can be boggy/rough. 24 km. 6 hours.
HIR.publicWalksS2S:
**[Horton-in-Ribblesdale to Ribblehead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ingleborough-via-moughton-scars-and-gaping-gill/)**: Limestone pavements, caves, dramatic gorge, moorland fell, high exposed plateau, steep engineered paths, quiet valley, and Iron Age hillfort remains. Sights: Moughton Scars, Trow Gill, Gaping Gill, Ingleborough, and Ribblehead Viaduct. Steep ascent/descent. 20 km. 6 hours.

**[Horton-in-Ribblesdale to Settle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/fountains-fell-and-catrigg-force/)**: Wild remote fell, moorland plateau, Pennine Way paths, lonely pastures, limestone country, waterfall, elevated bridleways above valley, and views of green dales and tarn. Sights: Fountains Fell, Pen-y-ghent, Malham Tarn, Catrigg Force, and Winskill Stones National Nature Reserve. Can be boggy/rough. 24 km. 6 hours.
HIR.publicWalksCircular: **[Horton-in-Ribblesdale Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pen-y-ghent-and-plover-hill/)**: Open moorland, remote valleys, engineered hill paths, ancient trading routes, rocky scrambles, boggy plateau, and sweeping Dales views. Sights: Pen-y-ghent, Plover Hill, Hull Pot, and Pendle Hill. Can be boggy. 17 km. 4.5 hours.

HIT.name: Hitchin
HIT.adminWalksAll:
**[Hitchin Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hitchin-circular/)**: Chilterns chalk ridge, farm tracks, field boundaries, grassy lanes, woods, steep chalk downland, flat-bottomed valleys, alder and willow fen woodland, chalk river, and historic market town. Sights: Pegsdon Hills, Knocking Hoe National Nature Reserve, Motte-and-double bailey, Abandoned medieval village, Oughtonhead Common, River Oughton, and St. Mary's Church. Can be hilly. Lunch in Great Offley, Pegsdon, or Pirton. Great Offley lunch stops: the [Red Lion](https://redliongreatoffley.co.uk/), or the [Green Man](http://www.chefandbrewer.com/pub/green-man-great-offley-hitchin/s3444/). Pegsdon lunch stop: the [View](https://theviewrestaurant.co.uk/). Pirton lunch stop: the [Motte & Bailey](https://themotteandbaileypirton.com/). 25 km. 6 hours.

**Knebworth to Hitchin**: A variant of [Knebworth Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knebworth-circular/). Woodland, open fields, deer park, bluebell woods, small Hertfordshire villages, and historic market town. Sights: Knebworth House, St Mary and Thomas of Canterbury church, St Paul's Walden Bury, All Saints Church St Paul's Walden, Stagenhoe, Hitch Wood, Wain Wood, Hitchin Priory, St Mary's Church Hitchin, and Red Lion pub Preston. Inquisitive cattle. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Preston, or Hitchin. Preston lunch stop: the Red Lion. Hitchin lunch stop: the Halsey's Deli & Eatery. 19 km.
HIT.publicWalksCircular: **[Hitchin Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hitchin-circular/)**: Chilterns chalk ridge, farm tracks, field boundaries, grassy lanes, woods, steep chalk downland, flat-bottomed valleys, alder and willow fen woodland, chalk river, and historic market town. Sights: Pegsdon Hills, Knocking Hoe National Nature Reserve, Motte-and-double bailey, Abandoned medieval village, Oughtonhead Common, River Oughton, and St. Mary's Church. Can be hilly. Lunch in Great Offley, Pegsdon, or Pirton. Great Offley lunch stops: the [Red Lion](https://redliongreatoffley.co.uk/), or the [Green Man](http://www.chefandbrewer.com/pub/green-man-great-offley-hitchin/s3444/). Pegsdon lunch stop: the [View](https://theviewrestaurant.co.uk/). Pirton lunch stop: the [Motte & Bailey](https://themotteandbaileypirton.com/). 25 km. 6 hours.

HLB.name: Hildenborough
HLB.adminWalksAll:
**Hildenborough to Penshurst**: An alternative variant of [Tonbridge to Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-to-penshurst/). Roadside walking, fields, parkland, riverside paths, low hills, vineyards, and farmland. Sights: Hall Place, St Mary, Leigh, [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), Harden Vineyard, St Mary, Chiddingstone, Chiding Stone, and Penshurst Platform Gallery. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst, or Hoath Corner. Penshurst lunch stops: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). Hoath Corner lunch stop: the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Hildenborough to Tonbridge via Hall Place**: An alternative variant of [Tonbridge to Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-to-penshurst/). Roadside walking, fields, parkland, riverside paths, parkland, playing fields, and town centre. Sights: Hall Place, St Mary, Leigh, [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), St John the Baptist, Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Penshurst Park, Tonbridge Castle, and Tonbridge School. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst, or Leigh. Penshurst lunch stops: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). Leigh lunch stop: the [Fleur De Lis](https://fleurdelisleigh.co.uk). 18 km. 4 hours.

**Hildenborough to Tonbridge via Hadlow Tower**: A longer variant of [Hildenborough to Hadlow or Tonbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hildenborough-to-hadlow-or-tonbridge/). Wealden countryside, meadows, farm fields, woods, and long riverside path along the River Medway into Tonbridge. Sights: [Hadlow Tower](http://www.thehadlowtower.co.uk/), [Broadview Gardens](https://www.hadlow.ac.uk/about-us/broadview-gardens-and-garden-centre), St Giles, Shipbourne, Hadlow College, St Mary, Hadlow, Hadlow Castle, Tonbridge Castle, and Tonbridge Memorial Garden. Can be muddy. Lunch in Shipbourne, or Dunk's Green. Shipbourne lunch stop: the [Chaser Inn](https://www.thechaser.co.uk/). Dunk's Green lunch stop: the [Kentish Rifleman](https://www.thekentishrifleman.co.uk/). 20 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Tunbridge Wells to Hildenborough](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-to-hildenborough/)**: Town commons, woodland tracks, wild flower meadows, country park, river paths, and quiet roads. Sights: Haysden Country Park, Brookhurst Field, St Lawrence Church, Wealdway, and Eden Valley Walk. Can be muddy. Lunch in Bidborough, or Hildenborough. Bidborough lunch stop: the [Kentish Hare](http://www.thekentishhare.com/). Hildenborough lunch stop: the [Plough at Leigh](http://www.theploughatleigh.com/). 17 km.
HLB.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Tunbridge Wells to Hildenborough](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-to-hildenborough/)**: Town commons, woodland tracks, wild flower meadows, country park, river paths, and quiet roads. Sights: Haysden Country Park, Brookhurst Field, St Lawrence Church, Wealdway, and Eden Valley Walk. Can be muddy. Lunch in Bidborough, or Hildenborough. Bidborough lunch stop: the [Kentish Hare](http://www.thekentishhare.com/). Hildenborough lunch stop: the [Plough at Leigh](http://www.theploughatleigh.com/). 17 km.

HLM.name: Holmwood
HLM.adminWalksAll:
**[Holmwood to Reigate](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/holmwood-to-reigate/)**: Gently undulating Low Weald countryside, ancient semi-natural woodland, village green, river crossing, and Greensand ridge with views of North Downs and South Downs. Sights: Hammond's Copse Nature Reserve and Reigate Priory. Lunch in Leigh: the Plough. 16 km. 4 hours.

**Holmwood to Gomshall**: Broadleaf woods, heathland, sandy bridleways, open valley, fields, and a picturesque Surrey Hills village. Sights: Leith Hill Tower, [Leith Hill Rhododendron Wood](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/leith-hill/features/the-rhododendron-wood-at-leith-hill), Leith Hill Place, Church of St John the Evangelist, Wotton House, Deerleap Wood, Monument to Samuel Wilberforce, Gomshall Mill, and Piney Copse. Can be muddy. Best in May, Oct and Nov. Lunch in Coldharbour, or Wotton. Coldharbour lunch stop: the Plough Inn. Wotton lunch stop: the Wotton Hatch. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**Holmwood to Guildford via Leith Hill Tower**: A longer variant of [Holmwood to Shamley Green](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/holmwood-to-shamley-green/). Sandy Greensand Ridge, beech and sweet chestnut woods, heathland, several big climbs and viewpoints, green valleys, and extended route to Guildford. Sights: Leith Hill Tower, Holmbury Hill, and Pitch Hill. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Holmbury St Mary: the Heartwork Coffee, the King's Head, or the Royal Oak. 28 km.

**Holmwood to Guildford via Church of St James**: Extended route combining two walks through Surrey hills, woods, heathland, and riverside paths. Sights: Church of St James, Albury Park, Chinthurst Hill Tower, and Yvonne Arnaud Theatre. Can be muddy. Lunch in Albury Heath: the William IV. A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 48 km.
HLM.publicWalksS2S:
**[Holmwood to Reigate](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/holmwood-to-reigate/)**: Gently undulating Low Weald countryside, ancient semi-natural woodland, village green, river crossing, and Greensand ridge with views of North Downs and South Downs. Sights: Hammond's Copse Nature Reserve and Reigate Priory. Lunch in Leigh: the Plough. 16 km. 4 hours.

**Holmwood to Gomshall**: Broadleaf woods, heathland, sandy bridleways, open valley, fields, and a picturesque Surrey Hills village. Sights: Leith Hill Tower, [Leith Hill Rhododendron Wood](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/leith-hill/features/the-rhododendron-wood-at-leith-hill), Leith Hill Place, Church of St John the Evangelist, Wotton House, Deerleap Wood, Monument to Samuel Wilberforce, Gomshall Mill, and Piney Copse. Can be muddy. Best in May, Oct and Nov. Lunch in Coldharbour, or Wotton. Coldharbour lunch stop: the Plough Inn. Wotton lunch stop: the Wotton Hatch. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 4.5 hours.

HLN.name: Harlington
HLN.adminWalksAll:
**Harlington Circular**: An alternative variant of [Harlington to Flitwick](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/harlington-to-flitwick/). Steep rolling downlands, chalk escarpments, hilltop views, grassy fields, and pastures. Sights: Sundon Hills, Sharpenhoe Clappers, and Sharpenhoe Neolithic Hill Fort. Can be hilly. Lunch in Pulloxhill, or Westoning. Pulloxhill lunch stop: the [Cross Keys](http://www.thecrosskeys.co.uk/). Westoning lunch stops: the [Chequers](http://thechequerswestoning.co.uk/), or the [Bell](https://www.thebellwestoning.co.uk/). 17 km.

**[Harlington to Flitwick](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/harlington-to-flitwick/)**: Steep rolling downlands, chalk escarpments, hilltop views, grassy fields, pastures, and ancient woodland. Sights: Sundon Hills, Sharpenhoe Clappers, Sharpenhoe Neolithic Hill Fort, and Flitwick Wood. Can be hilly. Lunch in Pulloxhill, or Westoning. Pulloxhill lunch stop: the [Cross Keys](http://www.thecrosskeys.co.uk/). Westoning lunch stops: the [Chequers](http://thechequerswestoning.co.uk/), or the [Bell](https://www.thebellwestoning.co.uk/). 24 km. 5 hours.

**[Leagrave to Harlington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leagrave-to-harlington/)**: Meadows, solitary chalk hills, ancient woodlands, steep chalk escarpments, chalk downland, Neolithic hill forts, ancient trackways, rolling grassy fields, and hilltop village. Sights: Barton Hills, Sharpenhoe Clappers, Sharpenhoe Hill Fort, Warden Hill, Galley Hill, Icknield Way, and [Millhouse Coffee Shop at The Olde Watermill Dickensian Village](http://www.oldewatermill.co.uk/). Steep chalk escarpments. Lunch in Hexton: the [Raven of Hexton](https://www.theravenhexton.com/). 23 km. 5 hours.
HLN.publicWalksS2S: **[Harlington to Flitwick](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/harlington-to-flitwick/)**: Steep rolling downlands, chalk escarpments, hilltop views, grassy fields, pastures, and ancient woodland. Sights: Sundon Hills, Sharpenhoe Clappers, Sharpenhoe Neolithic Hill Fort, and Flitwick Wood. Can be hilly. Lunch in Pulloxhill, or Westoning. Pulloxhill lunch stop: the [Cross Keys](http://www.thecrosskeys.co.uk/). Westoning lunch stops: the [Chequers](http://thechequerswestoning.co.uk/), or the [Bell](https://www.thebellwestoning.co.uk/). 24 km. 5 hours.
HLN.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Leagrave to Harlington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leagrave-to-harlington/)**: Meadows, solitary chalk hills, ancient woodlands, steep chalk escarpments, chalk downland, Neolithic hill forts, ancient trackways, rolling grassy fields, and hilltop village. Sights: Barton Hills, Sharpenhoe Clappers, Sharpenhoe Hill Fort, Warden Hill, Galley Hill, Icknield Way, and [Millhouse Coffee Shop at The Olde Watermill Dickensian Village](http://www.oldewatermill.co.uk/). Steep chalk escarpments. Lunch in Hexton: the [Raven of Hexton](https://www.theravenhexton.com/). 23 km. 5 hours.

HMC.name: Hampton Court
HMC.adminWalksAll:
**[Hampton Court Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hampton-court-circular/)**: Flat royal parkland, riverside Thames Path, lime tree avenues, deer park, ornamental ponds, formal baroque gardens, and woodland plantations. Sights: [Hampton Court Palace](https://www.hrp.org.uk/hampton-court-palace), [Bushy Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/visit/parks/bushy-park), Tijou Screen, Privy Garden, Long Water, Diana Fountain, Chestnut Avenue, Water Gardens, Lion Gates, Woodland Gardens, Maze, Great Vine, Banqueting House, Great Fountain Garden, Royal Tennis Court, Pond Gardens, and Canadian Totem Pole. Lunch in Bushy Park: the [Pheasantry café](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/visit/parks/bushy-park/pheasantry-cafe). 12 km. 2.5 hours.

**Hampton Court to Hampton Wick**: An alternative variant of [Hampton Court Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hampton-court-circular/). Flat royal parkland, lime tree avenues, deer park, ornamental ponds, woodland plantations, and suburban streets. Sights: [Hampton Court Palace](https://www.hrp.org.uk/hampton-court-palace), [Bushy Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/visit/parks/bushy-park), Tijou Screen, Privy Garden, Long Water, Diana Fountain, Chestnut Avenue, Water Gardens, Woodland Gardens, and Canadian Totem Pole. Lunch in Bushy Park, or Hampton Wick. Bushy Park lunch stop: the [Pheasantry café](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/visit/parks/bushy-park/pheasantry-cafe). Hampton Wick lunch stops: the [White Hart Hotel](https://www.whiteharthoteluk.co.uk), the [Swan](https://www.theswanhamptonwick.co.uk), or the [Foresters Arms](https://www.the-foresters.com). 13 km. 3 hours.

**[West Byfleet to Hampton Court](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/west-byfleet-to-hampton-court/)**: Flat canal towpath, riverside Thames Path, tarmaced and paved surfaces, waterside scenery, locks, islands, and houseboats. Sights: [Coxes Lock](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coxes_Lock), [D'Oyly Carte Island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/D%27Oyly_Carte_Island), [Hampton Court Palace](https://www.hrp.org.uk/hampton-court-palace), New Haw Lock, and Desborough Island. Lunch in West Byfleet, New Haw Lock, Weybridge, D'Oyly Carte Island, Walton, Between Walton and West Molesey, West Molesey, or Hampton Court. West Byfleet lunch stop: the Station. New Haw Lock lunch stop: the White Hart. Weybridge lunch stop: the Old Crown. D'Oyly Carte Island lunch stop: the [D'Oyle's cafe](https://www.doylycarteisland.com/doylys). Walton lunch stops: the Anglers, or the Swan. Between Walton and West Molesey lunch stop: the Weir. West Molesey lunch stops: the [Miss Polly cafe](https://www.misspollycafe.co.uk/miss-polly-riverside), or the [Eight on the River](https://www.eightontheriver.com/). Hampton Court lunch stops: the Mitre, or the Cardinal Wolsey. 17 km.

**Kingston to Hampton Court**: A longer variant of [Wimbledon to Kingston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-kingston/). Thames riverside path, deer park, and formal palace gardens. Sights: Hampton Court Palace and Bushy Park.
HMC.publicWalksCircular: **[Hampton Court Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hampton-court-circular/)**: Flat royal parkland, riverside Thames Path, lime tree avenues, deer park, ornamental ponds, formal baroque gardens, and woodland plantations. Sights: [Hampton Court Palace](https://www.hrp.org.uk/hampton-court-palace), [Bushy Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/visit/parks/bushy-park), Tijou Screen, Privy Garden, Long Water, Diana Fountain, Chestnut Avenue, Water Gardens, Lion Gates, Woodland Gardens, Maze, Great Vine, Banqueting House, Great Fountain Garden, Royal Tennis Court, Pond Gardens, and Canadian Totem Pole. Lunch in Bushy Park: the [Pheasantry café](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/visit/parks/bushy-park/pheasantry-cafe). 12 km. 2.5 hours.

Sights: [Coxes Lock](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coxes_Lock), [D'Oyly Carte Island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/D%27Oyly_Carte_Island), [Hampton Court Palace](https://www.hrp.org.uk/hampton-court-palace), New Haw Lock, and Desborough Island. New Haw Lock lunch stop: the White Hart. Weybridge lunch stop: the Old Crown. D'Oyly Carte Island lunch stop: the [D'Oyle's cafe](https://www.doylycarteisland.com/doylys). Hampton Court lunch stops: the Mitre, or the Cardinal Wolsey. 17 km.

HMD.name: Hampden Park
HMD.adminWalksAll: **[Hampden Park to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hampden-park-to-eastbourne/)**: Parks, woods, chalk downland ridges, open grassland, chalk heath, dense beech forest, coastal farmland, golf course, and Victorian seaside town. Sights: Hampden Park, Lullington Heath National Nature Reserve, Friston Forest, St Andrew's Church, Jevington, Friston Place, Belle Tout Lighthouse, Birling Gap, Eastbourne cricket ground, Eastbourne Town Hall, Gildredge Park, and Little Chelsea. Steep descents. Lunch in Jevington, or East Dean. Jevington lunch stop: the [Eight Bells](https://eightbellsjevington.com/). East Dean lunch stops: the [Tiger Inn](https://www.beachyhead.org.uk/the-tiger-inn/), the Hikers' Rest Coffee Shop, the Thai Terre, or the Beehive on the Green. 26 km.
HMD.publicWalksS2S: **[Hampden Park to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hampden-park-to-eastbourne/)**: Parks, woods, chalk downland ridges, open grassland, chalk heath, dense beech forest, coastal farmland, golf course, and Victorian seaside town. Sights: Hampden Park, Lullington Heath National Nature Reserve, Friston Forest, St Andrew's Church, Jevington, Friston Place, Belle Tout Lighthouse, Birling Gap, Eastbourne cricket ground, Eastbourne Town Hall, Gildredge Park, and Little Chelsea. Steep descents. Lunch in Jevington, or East Dean. Jevington lunch stop: the [Eight Bells](https://eightbellsjevington.com/). East Dean lunch stops: the [Tiger Inn](https://www.beachyhead.org.uk/the-tiger-inn/), the Hikers' Rest Coffee Shop, the Thai Terre, or the Beehive on the Green. 26 km.

HMT.name: Ham Street
HMT.adminWalksAll:
**Ham Street Circular via Ham Street Woods National Nature Reserve & Saxon Shore Way**: A shorter variant of [Ham Street to Appledore](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ham-street-to-appledore/). Woodland, canal towpath, farm fields, isolated church, and Romney Marsh flatlands. Sights: [Ham Street Woods National Nature Reserve](https://explorekent.org/activities/ham-street-woods-national-nature-reserve), [Royal Military Canal](http://www.rmcp.co.uk/the-royal-military-canal), [Saxon Shore Way](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saxon_Shore_Way), St Matthew's Church, and St Mary's Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Warehorne: the [Woolpack Inn](https://www.woolpackwarehorne.co.uk). 12 km. 3 hours.

**Ham Street Circular via Gusbourne Vineyard**: A longer variant of [Ham Street to Appledore](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ham-street-to-appledore/). Woodland, canal towpath, vineyard, farm fields, historic village, and Romney Marsh flatlands. Sights: [Ham Street Woods National Nature Reserve](https://explorekent.org/activities/ham-street-woods-national-nature-reserve), [Royal Military Canal](http://www.rmcp.co.uk/the-royal-military-canal), [Saxon Shore Way](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saxon_Shore_Way), [Gusbourne Vineyard](https://www.gusbourne.com), St Matthew's Church, St Mary's Church, Ss Peter and Paul Church, and Miss Mollett's High Class Tea Room. Can be muddy. Lunch in Appledore: the [Black Lion](https://www.blacklionappledore.co.uk). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Ham Street to Appledore via Ham Street Woods National Nature Reserve](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ham-street-to-appledore/)**: Woodland National Nature Reserve, Royal Military Canal towpath, farm fields, vineyard, Romney Marsh flatlands, and a historic village main street. Sights: [Ham Street Woods National Nature Reserve](https://explorekent.org/activities/ham-street-woods-national-nature-reserve), [Royal Military Canal](http://www.rmcp.co.uk/the-royal-military-canal), [Saxon Shore Way](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saxon_Shore_Way), [Gusbourne Vineyard](https://www.gusbourne.com), St Matthew's Church, St Mary's Church, Ss Peter and Paul Church, and Miss Mollett's High Class Tea Room. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Warehorne: the [Woolpack Inn](https://www.woolpackwarehorne.co.uk). 14 km. 3 hours.

**Ham Street to Appledore via Horne's Place Chapel**: A longer variant of [Ham Street to Appledore](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ham-street-to-appledore/). Woodland, Royal Military Canal towpath, vineyard, Park Wood, Heron Wood, farm fields, and Romney Marsh flatlands. Sights: [Ham Street Woods National Nature Reserve](https://explorekent.org/activities/ham-street-woods-national-nature-reserve), [Royal Military Canal](http://www.rmcp.co.uk/the-royal-military-canal), [Saxon Shore Way](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saxon_Shore_Way), [Gusbourne Vineyard](https://www.gusbourne.com), [Horne's Place Chapel](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/hornes-place-chapel), St Matthew's Church, St Mary's Church, Ss Peter and Paul Church, and Miss Mollett's High Class Tea Room. Can be muddy. Lunch in Warehorne: the [Woolpack Inn](https://www.woolpackwarehorne.co.uk). 20 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Ashford to Ham Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashford-to-ham-street/)**: River path, urban suburbs, new housing estates, tranquil pastures, ancient woodland, and mud-prone bridleway. Sights: Ham Street Woods National Nature Reserve, Victoria Park, Buxford Fish Pass, St Mary's Church Great Chart, and [Curious Brewery Taproom & Restaurant](https://curiousbrewery.com/taproom-restaurant/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Great Chart, Kingsnorth, or Ham Street. Great Chart lunch stop: the [Swan and Dog](https://www.swananddog.co.uk/). Kingsnorth lunch stop: the [Queen's Head](https://www.kentvenues.co.uk/venues/queens-head-kingsnorth). Ham Street lunch stop: the [Duke's Head](https://dukesheadhamstreet.co.uk/). 17 km.

**Pluckley to Ham Street**: A longer variant of [Pluckley to Ashford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pluckley-to-ashford/). Pastures, orchards, woods, commons, bogs, parkland estate, and continuing Greensand Way to Ham Street. Sights: Godinton Park and Hothfield Bogs. Lunch in Pluckley, Little Chart, or Great Chart. Pluckley lunch stops: the [Dering Arms](http://www.deringarms.com/), or the [Black Horse](https://theblackhorseatpluckley.co.uk/). Little Chart lunch stop: the [Swan Inn](https://www.theswaninnlittlechart.co.uk/index). Great Chart lunch stop: the [Swan and Dog](https://www.swananddog.co.uk/). 25 km.
HMT.publicWalksS2S: **[Ham Street to Appledore via Ham Street Woods National Nature Reserve](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ham-street-to-appledore/)**: Woodland National Nature Reserve, Royal Military Canal towpath, farm fields, vineyard, Romney Marsh flatlands, and a historic village main street. Sights: [Ham Street Woods National Nature Reserve](https://explorekent.org/activities/ham-street-woods-national-nature-reserve), [Royal Military Canal](http://www.rmcp.co.uk/the-royal-military-canal), [Saxon Shore Way](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saxon_Shore_Way), [Gusbourne Vineyard](https://www.gusbourne.com), St Matthew's Church, St Mary's Church, Ss Peter and Paul Church, and Miss Mollett's High Class Tea Room. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Warehorne: the [Woolpack Inn](https://www.woolpackwarehorne.co.uk). 14 km. 3 hours.

Kingsnorth lunch stop: the [Queen's Head](https://www.kentvenues.co.uk/venues/queens-head-kingsnorth). Ham Street lunch stop: the [Duke's Head](https://dukesheadhamstreet.co.uk/). 17 km.

HNB.name: Herne Bay
HNB.adminWalksAll:
**[Birchington-on-Sea to Herne Bay](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/birchington-to-herne-bay/)**: Entirely flat coastal walk; seafront promenade, raised sea wall, remote shingle beach, grassy clifftop slopes, and a seaside resort. Sights: [Reculver Towers and Roman Fort](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reculver), Herne Bay Pier, Kentish Flats offshore windfarm, and Maunsell Forts. Lunch in Reculver: the [King Ethelbert Inn](https://www.kingethelbertinn.co.uk/), or the [HatHats Coffee Company](https://hathats.co.uk/pages/reculver). 14 km. 5 hours.

**Margate to Herne Bay**: A longer variant of [Birchington to Herne Bay](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/birchington-to-herne-bay/). Flat seafront promenades, quiet residential clifftops, small headlands, and sandy beaches linking coastal towns. Sights: [Reculver Towers and Roman Fort](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reculver), Turner Contemporary Gallery, and Herne Bay Pier. Lunch in Reculver: the [King Ethelbert Inn](https://www.kingethelbertinn.co.uk/), or the [HatHats Coffee Company](https://hathats.co.uk/pages/reculver). 18 km.
HNB.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Birchington-on-Sea to Herne Bay](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/birchington-to-herne-bay/)**: Entirely flat coastal walk; seafront promenade, raised sea wall, remote shingle beach, grassy clifftop slopes, and a seaside resort. Sights: [Reculver Towers and Roman Fort](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reculver), Herne Bay Pier, Kentish Flats offshore windfarm, and Maunsell Forts. Lunch in Reculver: the [King Ethelbert Inn](https://www.kingethelbertinn.co.uk/), or the [HatHats Coffee Company](https://hathats.co.uk/pages/reculver). 14 km. 5 hours.

HND.name: Hanborough
HND.adminWalksAll:
**Hanborough Circular via Blenheim Palace**: An essential walk! It's an easy walk from the station to the beautiful, vast (and free) grounds of the finest example of English baroque architecture. For a somewhat extortionate fee you can also visit the house, but you'd probably want a whole day for that. Sights: [Blenheim Palace](https://www.blenheimpalace.com). From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 1 January 2024.* 12 km. 110 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920249192)**

**Hanborough to Charlbury**: Rolling Oxfordshire farmland, landscaped parkland, lakeside paths, open fields, ancient hedgerows, Roman road, Cotswold stone villages, and deer park. Sights: Blenheim Palace, Column of Victory, Great Bridge, Cornbury Park, St Mary the Virgin Church, and Charlbury Railway Station. Can be muddy. Best in Sep. Lunch in Stonesfield, or Finstock. Stonesfield lunch stop: the White Horse. Finstock lunch stop: the Plough Inn. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 22 km. 6 hours.

**Charlbury to Hanborough**: Rolling Oxfordshire farmland, landscaped parkland, lakeside paths, open fields, ancient hedgerows, Roman road, Cotswold stone villages, and deer park. Sights: Blenheim Palace, Column of Victory, Great Bridge, Cornbury Park, and St Mary the Virgin Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Finstock, or Stonesfield. Finstock lunch stop: the Plough Inn. Stonesfield lunch stop: the White Horse. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 21 km.
HND.publicWalksCircular: **Hanborough Circular via Blenheim Palace**: An essential walk! It's an easy walk from the station to the beautiful, vast (and free) grounds of the finest example of English baroque architecture. For a somewhat extortionate fee you can also visit the house, but you'd probably want a whole day for that. Sights: [Blenheim Palace](https://www.blenheimpalace.com). From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 12 km. 110 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920249192)**

HNL.name: Henley-in-Arden
HNL.adminWalksAll: **[Henley-in-Arden Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-817)**: Historic high street, farmland, Millennium Way, rural lanes, fields, and woodland edges. Sights: [Henley Mount](https://www.visit-henley.co.uk/the-mount/), Beaudesert Castle, [Henley's Churches](https://www.visit-henley.co.uk/heritage-trail/), [Henley Museum](https://www.heritagehenley.org.uk), Henley Mile, St John the Baptist Church, and Guildhall. Can be overgrown summer. Lunch in Henley-in-Arden: the [Henley Kitchen & Bakery](https://www.facebook.com/hkandb/), the BD Sandwiches, the Henley Butchers & Deli, the [White Swan](https://www.thewhiteswanhotel.com), the Nag's Head, or the [Henley Ice Cream](https://www.henleyicecream.co.uk). 8 km. 2.5 hours.
HNL.publicWalksCircular: **[Henley-in-Arden Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-817)**: Historic high street, farmland, Millennium Way, rural lanes, fields, and woodland edges. Sights: [Henley Mount](https://www.visit-henley.co.uk/the-mount/), Beaudesert Castle, [Henley's Churches](https://www.visit-henley.co.uk/heritage-trail/), [Henley Museum](https://www.heritagehenley.org.uk), Henley Mile, St John the Baptist Church, and Guildhall. Can be overgrown summer. Lunch in Henley-in-Arden: the [Henley Kitchen & Bakery](https://www.facebook.com/hkandb/), the BD Sandwiches, the Henley Butchers & Deli, the [White Swan](https://www.thewhiteswanhotel.com), the Nag's Head, or the [Henley Ice Cream](https://www.henleyicecream.co.uk). 8 km. 2.5 hours.

HOK.name: Hook
HOK.adminWalksAll:
**[Hook to Winchfield via Odiham High Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hook-to-winchfield/)**: Heath, river meadows, canal towpath, parkland estate, fields, and country lanes. Sights: [Bartley Heath](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/hook-common-and-bartley-heath), [Basingstoke Canal](https://www.canalguide.co.uk/canals/basingstokcanal.html), [Odiham Castle](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/odihamcastle), [Odiham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Odiham), [Dogmersfield Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dogmersfield_Park), Greywell Tunnel, St Mary the Virgin, Greywell Mill, Pest House, All Saints Church, Odiham High Street, Tundry Pond, Hunting Lodge, Old Thatch Cottage, and St Mary the Virgin Winchfield. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lodge Farm, North Warnborough, Greywell, or Odiham. Lodge Farm lunch stop: the [Newlyns Farm Shop & Café](https://www.newlyns-farmshop.co.uk/visit-us). North Warnborough lunch stop: the [Mill House](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/millhouse). Greywell lunch stop: the Fox & Goose. Odiham lunch stops: the [Bel & the Dragon](https://belandthedragon-odiham.co.uk), the [Red Lion](https://www.redlionodiham.co.uk), or the [Waterwitch](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/hampshire/waterwitch). 20 km. 4.5 hours.

**Hook to Winchfield via Bartley Heath**: A shorter variant of [Hook to Winchfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hook-to-winchfield/). Heath, river meadows, canal towpath, Odiham Common, and country lanes. Sights: [Bartley Heath](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/hook-common-and-bartley-heath), [Basingstoke Canal](https://www.canalguide.co.uk/canals/basingstokcanal.html), [Odiham Castle](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/odihamcastle), [Odiham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Odiham), Greywell Tunnel, St Mary the Virgin, Greywell Mill, Pest House, All Saints Church, Hunting Lodge, Old Thatch Cottage, and St Mary the Virgin Winchfield. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lodge Farm, North Warnborough, Greywell, or Odiham. Lodge Farm lunch stop: the [Newlyns Farm Shop & Café](https://www.newlyns-farmshop.co.uk/visit-us). North Warnborough lunch stop: the [Mill House](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/millhouse). Greywell lunch stop: the Fox & Goose. Odiham lunch stops: the [Bel & the Dragon](https://belandthedragon-odiham.co.uk), the [Red Lion](https://www.redlionodiham.co.uk), or the [Waterwitch](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/hampshire/waterwitch). 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**Hook to Winchfield via All Saints Church Odiham**: A longer variant of [Hook to Winchfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hook-to-winchfield/). Heath, river meadows, canal towpath, parkland estate, rural fields, village lanes, and woodland. Sights: [Bartley Heath](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/hook-common-and-bartley-heath), [Basingstoke Canal](https://www.canalguide.co.uk/canals/basingstokcanal.html), [Odiham Castle](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/odihamcastle), [Odiham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Odiham), [Dogmersfield Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dogmersfield_Park), Greywell Tunnel, St Mary the Virgin, Greywell Mill, Pest House, All Saints Church Odiham, All Saints Church Dogmersfield, Tundry Pond, Hunting Lodge, Old Thatch Cottage, and St Mary the Virgin Winchfield. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lodge Farm, North Warnborough, Greywell, Odiham, Crookham Wharf, or Dogmersfield. Lodge Farm lunch stop: the [Newlyns Farm Shop & Café](https://www.newlyns-farmshop.co.uk/visit-us). North Warnborough lunch stop: the [Mill House](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/millhouse). Greywell lunch stop: the Fox & Goose. Odiham lunch stops: the [Bel & the Dragon](https://belandthedragon-odiham.co.uk), the [Red Lion](https://www.redlionodiham.co.uk), or the [Waterwitch](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/hampshire/waterwitch). Crookham Wharf lunch stop: the [Exchequer](https://www.exchequercrookham.co.uk). Dogmersfield lunch stop: the [Queens Head](https://www.queensheadpub.co.uk). 24 km. 5.5 hours.
HOK.publicWalksS2S: **[Hook to Winchfield via Odiham High Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hook-to-winchfield/)**: Heath, river meadows, canal towpath, parkland estate, fields, and country lanes. Sights: [Bartley Heath](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/hook-common-and-bartley-heath), [Basingstoke Canal](https://www.canalguide.co.uk/canals/basingstokcanal.html), [Odiham Castle](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/odihamcastle), [Odiham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Odiham), [Dogmersfield Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dogmersfield_Park), Greywell Tunnel, St Mary the Virgin, Greywell Mill, Pest House, All Saints Church, Odiham High Street, Tundry Pond, Hunting Lodge, Old Thatch Cottage, and St Mary the Virgin Winchfield. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lodge Farm, North Warnborough, Greywell, or Odiham. Lodge Farm lunch stop: the [Newlyns Farm Shop & Café](https://www.newlyns-farmshop.co.uk/visit-us). North Warnborough lunch stop: the [Mill House](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/millhouse). Greywell lunch stop: the Fox & Goose. Odiham lunch stops: the [Bel & the Dragon](https://belandthedragon-odiham.co.uk), the [Red Lion](https://www.redlionodiham.co.uk), or the [Waterwitch](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/hampshire/waterwitch). 20 km. 4.5 hours.

HOP.name: Hope
HOP.adminWalksAll:
**[Hope to Hathersage](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hope-to-hathersage-or-bamford-via-castleton/)**: Limestone and gritstone uplands, pastures with panoramic views, landslip area, dry limestone gorge, collapsed cave dale, upland moor, and scenic river descent. Sights: Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, Speedwell Cavern, Treak Cliff Cavern, Peak Cavern, Cave Dale, Peveril Castle, Odin Mine, Hope Cement Works, and [David Mellor Design Museum](https://www.davidmellordesign.com/design-museum-cafe). Can be muddy. Lunch in Castleton, or Bradwell. Castleton lunch stops: the Castleton Fish & Chips, the Dolly Tearooms, the Peak District National Park Visitor Centre Café, the Three Roofs Café, the Castle, the Bull's Head, the Castleton Maid Ice Cream, the Peveril Tea Rooms, the George Inn, the Rose Cottage Café, the 1530 The Restaurant, the Tilly's of Castleton, the Ye Olde Nag's Head, the Baytree Tea Room & Gifts, the Rambler's Rest, the Peak Hotel, or the Ye Olde Cheshire Cheese. Bradwell lunch stops: the [Ye Olde Bowling Green Inn](https://www.yeoldebowlinggreenbradwell.co.uk/), the White Hart Inn, the Brook Café, or the [Shoulder of Mutton Inn](https://www.shoulderbradwell.pub/). 27 km. 7 hours.

**Hope to Edale**: An alternative variant of [Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bamford-to-edale-via-win-hill-and-great-ridge/). Steep ascent to heathery ridge, exposed moorland, gritstone escarpments, and ridgeline with panoramic valley views. Sights: Win Hill Pike, The Great Ridge, Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, and Lose Hill. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Hope: the [Cheshire Cheese Inn](http://www.thecheshirecheeseinn.co.uk/). 15 km.

**Hope to Bamford**: An alternative variant of [Hope to Hathersage or Bamford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hope-to-hathersage-or-bamford-via-castleton/). Limestone and gritstone uplands, pastures with panoramic views, landslip area, dry limestone gorge, collapsed cave dale, upland moor, and descent to Bamford. Sights: Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, Cave Dale, Peveril Castle, and Hope Cement Works. Can be muddy. Lunch in Castleton, or Bradwell. Castleton lunch stops: the Castleton Fish & Chips, the Dolly Tearooms, the Peak District National Park Visitor Centre Café, the Three Roofs Café, the Castle, or the Bull's Head. Bradwell lunch stops: the [Ye Olde Bowling Green Inn](https://www.yeoldebowlinggreenbradwell.co.uk/), or the [Shoulder of Mutton Inn](https://www.shoulderbradwell.pub/).
HOP.publicWalksS2S: **[Hope to Hathersage](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hope-to-hathersage-or-bamford-via-castleton/)**: Limestone and gritstone uplands, pastures with panoramic views, landslip area, dry limestone gorge, collapsed cave dale, upland moor, and scenic river descent. Sights: Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, Speedwell Cavern, Treak Cliff Cavern, Peak Cavern, Cave Dale, Peveril Castle, Odin Mine, Hope Cement Works, and [David Mellor Design Museum](https://www.davidmellordesign.com/design-museum-cafe). Can be muddy. Lunch in Castleton, or Bradwell. Castleton lunch stops: the Castleton Fish & Chips, the Dolly Tearooms, the Peak District National Park Visitor Centre Café, the Three Roofs Café, the Castle, the Bull's Head, the Castleton Maid Ice Cream, the Peveril Tea Rooms, the George Inn, the Rose Cottage Café, the 1530 The Restaurant, the Tilly's of Castleton, the Ye Olde Nag's Head, the Baytree Tea Room & Gifts, the Rambler's Rest, the Peak Hotel, or the Ye Olde Cheshire Cheese. Bradwell lunch stops: the [Ye Olde Bowling Green Inn](https://www.yeoldebowlinggreenbradwell.co.uk/), the White Hart Inn, the Brook Café, or the [Shoulder of Mutton Inn](https://www.shoulderbradwell.pub/). 27 km. 7 hours.

HOT.name: Henley-on-Thames
HOT.privateNote:
Trainline says
London Paddington > Twyford > Henley-on-Thames: 49m
CLJ > Victoria > Paddington > Twyford > Henley-on-Thames: 1h46m
HOT.adminWalksAll:
**Henley Circular via Hambleden**: Rambler favourite! Thames towpath, open riverside meadows, quaint brick-and-flint hamlet, ancient woodland, wooded hillside terraces, parkland estate, and Georgian market town. Sights: Temple Island, Hambleden Lock, Hambleden Mill, St Mary the Virgin Church, Hambleden, St Mary the Virgin Church, Fawley, and Fawley Mausoleum. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hambleden: the Stag & Huntsman. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 24 km. 310 m uphill. 6.5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920170092)**

**Henley-on-Thames to Marlow via Hambleden**: Rambler favourite! From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 28 September 2024.* 15 km. 130 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/849768913)**

**Shiplake to Henley via Greys Court**: Riverside Thames path, bluebell and beech woods, open agricultural fields, gentle hills, golf course, historic village, and market town riverfront. Sights: Shiplake Lock, Shiplake Church, Shiplake College, St Nicholas Church, Greys Court, Lambridge Wood, Friar Park, Friar Park's lodge house, St Mary the Virgin, and Red Lion Hotel. Can flood after rain. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Rotherfield Greys, or Binfield Heath. Rotherfield Greys lunch stop: the Maltsters Arms. Binfield Heath lunch stop: the Bottle & Glass Inn. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 17 km. 170 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920707447)**

**Henley-on-Thames Circular via Hambleden Circular**: Thames riverbank, beech woods, valley farmland, watermeadows, arable fields, and gentle hills. Sights: Temple Island, Hambleden, Hambleden Mill, Hambleden Weir, Hambleden Lock, Remenham Hill, and Henley bridge. Best in Nov. Lunch in Hambleden, or Aston. Hambleden lunch stops: the Stag and Huntsman, or the Hambleden Village Stores. Aston lunch stop: the Flower Pot Hotel. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 14 km.

**[Henley-on-Thames Circular via Rotherfield Greys](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/henley-circular-via-rotherfield-greys/)**: Pleasant valleys, undulating woodland, bluebell woods, open fields, Thames riverbank, and weir crossing. Sights: Marsh Lock and [Bottle & Glass Inn](https://www.bottleandglassinn.com/). Marsh Lock bridge closed. Lunch in Binfield Heath: the [Bottle & Glass](https://www.bottleandglassinn.com/). 15 km.

**Henley-on-Thames Circular via Stonor Circular**: Rolling grassy hills, hidden valleys, mixed woodland, beech woods, farmland, and panoramic views. Sights: [Stonor Park](http://www.stonor.com), Abandoned Church at Bix Bottom, and George Harrison's former house. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Nettlebed: the White Hart. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 23 km.

**[Henley-on-Thames Circular via Turville Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/henley-via-turville-circular/)**: Thames Valley and Chilterns, open valley countryside, attractive woodland, Thames path, and picturesque villages and hamlets. Sights: Stonor House, St Bartholomew's Church, St Mary and the Virgin Church, and Turville windmill. Best in Jun–Aug. 24 km.

**Henley-on-Thames Circular via Great Wood & St Nicholas Church, Remenham**: Rambler favourite! Parkland estate, ancient woodland, wooded hillside terraces, quaint brick-and-flint hamlet, weir crossings, and Thames towpath return. Sights: Temple Island, Hambleden Lock, Hambleden Mill, St Mary the Virgin Church, Hambleden, St Mary the Virgin Church, Fawley, and St Nicholas Church, Remenham. Can be muddy. Best in Dec–Feb. Lunch in Hambleden: the Stag & Huntsman. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**Henley-on-Thames Circular via Hambleden Circular & Marsh Lock**: Thames riverbank, beech woods, valley farmland, watermeadows, arable fields, gentle hills, and riverside tarmac path. Sights: Temple Island, Hambleden, Hambleden Mill, Hambleden Weir, and Marsh Lock. Best in Nov. Lunch in Hambleden, or Aston. Hambleden lunch stops: the Stag and Huntsman, or the Hambleden Village Stores. Aston lunch stop: the Flower Pot Hotel. A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 17 km.

**Henley-on-Thames to Pangbourne**: Rambler favourite! Thames riverside, ancient streets, broad valleys, beech woods, fields, small villages, alpaca farm, and toll bridge. Sights: St Nicholas' Church, All Saints Church, Bozedown Alpaca Farm, Whitchurch Toll Bridge, and Parish Church of Whitchurch-on-Thames. Can be muddy. Lunch in Rotherfield Greys, Peppard Common, or Kingwood. Rotherfield Greys lunch stop: the [Maltster's Arms](http://www.maltstersarmsrotherfield.co.uk/). Peppard Common lunch stop: the [Red Lion](https://www.theredlionpub.uk/). Kingwood lunch stop: the [Unicorn](http://www.unicornkingwood.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 19 km. 5.5 hours.

**Henley-on-Thames to Shiplake via Great Wood**: Rambler favourite! Thames riverside path extension from Henley to Shiplake station. Sights: Temple Island, Hambleden Lock, and St Mary the Virgin Church, Hambleden. Lunch in Hambleden: the Stag & Huntsman. A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).

**Henley-on-Thames to Shiplake via Rotherfield Greys**: A shorter variant of [Henley Circular via Rotherfield Greys](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/henley-circular-via-rotherfield-greys/). Pleasant valleys, undulating woodland, bluebell woods, open fields, and ending at Shiplake station. Sights: [Bottle & Glass Inn](https://www.bottleandglassinn.com/) and Baskerville. Lunch in Binfield Heath: the [Bottle & Glass](https://www.bottleandglassinn.com/). 13 km.

**Henley-on-Thames to Marlow**: Thames riverbank, beech woods, wooded hills, and valley farmland. Sights: Temple Island, Hambleden, Hambleden Mill, and Hambleden Weir. Best in Nov. Lunch in Hambleden: the Stag and Huntsman, or the Hambleden Village Stores. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km.

**[Reading to Henley-on-Thames via Sonning Lock](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/reading-to-henley/)**: Flat Thames towpath, riverside parks, wooded sections, pasture fields, meadows, and riverside gardens. Sights: Sonning Lock, Sonning Bridge, Shiplake Lock, Marsh Lock, and [Thames Path](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thames_Path). Flood risk sections. Lunch in Sonning: the Bull, or the Coppa Club. 16 km.

**Marlow to Henley-on-Thames via Hurley Lock**: Thames riverbank, riverside villages, and Remenham Hill. Sights: Hurley Lock and Bisham Abbey. Lunch in Aston: the Flower Pot Hotel. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 13 km.

**Shiplake to Henley-on-Thames via High Wood**: Bluebell woods, quiet lanes, open fields, beech woods, golf course, and historic town riverfront. Sights: High Wood, Greys Court, Lambridge Wood, Friar Park, and St Mary the Virgin. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Binfield Heath, or Rotherfield Greys. Binfield Heath lunch stop: the Bottle & Glass Inn. Rotherfield Greys lunch stop: the Maltsters Arms. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km.

**Marlow to Henley-on-Thames via Hambleden Parish Church**: Thames Path towing path, riverside meadows, and Hambleden Lock. Sights: Hurley Lock, Bisham Abbey, and Hambleden Parish Church. Lunch in Aston, or Hambleden. Aston lunch stop: the Flower Pot Hotel. Hambleden lunch stop: the Stag and Huntsman. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 15 km.
