HPD.name: Harpenden
HPD.adminWalksAll: **Harpenden to St Albans via Sanbridge**: Mostly fields and country lanes. Some beautiful new bluebell woods, stunning historic city streeets, commons, golf courses, and parkland. Sights: [Bluebell Woods in Heartwood Forest](https://heartwood.woodlandtrust.org.uk/), [Childwickbury Estate](https://www.google.com/maps/place/?q=place_id:ChIJxz4q38U4dkgRO4Z_rruVnV8), and [St Albans Cathedral and Abbey Church of St Alban](https://www.stalbanscathedral.org/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Sandridge: the [Queens Head](https://linktr.ee/TheQueensHeadSandridge), the [Heartwood Tea Rooms](http://heartwoodtearooms.com/), the [Green Man](http://www.greenmansandridge.co.uk/), or the [Rose & Crown](http://www.theroseandcrownsandridge.co.uk/). End-of-walk rests: [Mad Squirrel Tap](https://www.madsquirrelstalbans.co.uk/), [Abigail's Cafe](https://www.google.com/maps/place/?q=place_id:ChIJfy5BmiM_dkgRUNtX7o50Yx4), [The Peahen](https://www.mcmullens.co.uk/local-pub/peahen/), [The Snug](https://www.google.com/maps/place/?q=place_id:ChIJmT8rc8JHdkgR6s4QOCS4Wkw), or [The Boot](http://www.bootcantina.com/). *Last reviewed 4 May 2026.* 17 km. 130 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2930623590)**
HPD.publicWalksS2S: **Harpenden to St Albans via Sanbridge**: Mostly fields and country lanes. Some beautiful new bluebell woods, stunning historic city streeets, commons, golf courses, and parkland. Sights: [Bluebell Woods in Heartwood Forest](https://heartwood.woodlandtrust.org.uk/), [Childwickbury Estate](https://www.google.com/maps/place/?q=place_id:ChIJxz4q38U4dkgRO4Z_rruVnV8), and [St Albans Cathedral and Abbey Church of St Alban](https://www.stalbanscathedral.org/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Sandridge: the [Queens Head](https://linktr.ee/TheQueensHeadSandridge), the [Heartwood Tea Rooms](http://heartwoodtearooms.com/), the [Green Man](http://www.greenmansandridge.co.uk/), or the [Rose & Crown](http://www.theroseandcrownsandridge.co.uk/). End-of-walk rests: [Mad Squirrel Tap](https://www.madsquirrelstalbans.co.uk/), [Abigail's Cafe](https://www.google.com/maps/place/?q=place_id:ChIJfy5BmiM_dkgRUNtX7o50Yx4), [The Peahen](https://www.mcmullens.co.uk/local-pub/peahen/), [The Snug](https://www.google.com/maps/place/?q=place_id:ChIJmT8rc8JHdkgR6s4QOCS4Wkw), or [The Boot](http://www.bootcantina.com/). 17 km. 130 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2930623590)**

HRH.name: Horsham
HRH.adminWalksAll:
**[Balcombe to Horsham via Chesworth Farm Riverside Fields](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/balcombe-to-horsham/)**: Wealden woods, farmland fields, streams, ponds, riverside nature reserve, and old market town. Sights: [High Weald](http://www.highweald.org/), [Nymans Estate](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/nymans), [St Leonard's Forest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Leonard%27s_Forest), [Chesworth Farm Riverside Fields](https://friendsofchesworthfarm.com/), [River Arun](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Arun), [Horsham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Horsham), and [Black Jug](http://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/blackjug/). Can be very overgrown. Lunch in Plummers Plain: the [Wheatsheaf](http://www.thewheatsheafplummersplain.co.uk/). 27 km.

**Ockley to Horsham**: Fields, oak woods, gentle hills, farmland, suburban Horsham, and parkland. Sights: [Parish Church of St John the Baptist](https://friendsofokewoodchurch.org/#history), [Hannah Peschar Sculpture Garden](https://www.hannahpescharsculpture.com/), Horsham Park, Leith Hill, and Warnham Lodge Estate water tower. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Walliswood, or Okewood Hill. Walliswood lunch stop: the Scarlett Arms. Okewood Hill lunch stop: the Punchbowl. A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 20 km.

**Balcombe to Horsham via High Weald**: A shorter variant of [Balcombe to Horsham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/balcombe-to-horsham/). Wealden woods, farmland, streams, and direct route to Horsham station. Sights: [High Weald](http://www.highweald.org/), [Nymans Estate](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/nymans), and [St Leonard's Forest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Leonard%27s_Forest). Can be very overgrown. Lunch in Plummers Plain: the [Wheatsheaf](http://www.thewheatsheafplummersplain.co.uk/). 21 km.
HRH.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Balcombe to Horsham via Chesworth Farm Riverside Fields](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/balcombe-to-horsham/)**: Wealden woods, farmland fields, streams, ponds, riverside nature reserve, and old market town. Sights: [High Weald](http://www.highweald.org/), [Nymans Estate](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/nymans), [St Leonard's Forest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Leonard%27s_Forest), [Chesworth Farm Riverside Fields](https://friendsofchesworthfarm.com/), [River Arun](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Arun), [Horsham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Horsham), and [Black Jug](http://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/blackjug/). Can be very overgrown. Lunch in Plummers Plain: the [Wheatsheaf](http://www.thewheatsheafplummersplain.co.uk/). 27 km.

HRM.name: Harrietsham
HRM.adminWalksAll:
**[Harrietsham to Pluckley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/harrietsham-to-pluckley/)**: Gentle ridge, apple orchards, pasture, escarpment views, bluebell woods, Kentish farmland, and churches attached to farms and manor houses. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Fairbourne Heath: the Pepper Box Inn. 15 km.

**Yalding to Harrietsham**: A longer variant of [Yalding to Sutton Valence](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-to-sutton-vallance/). Flat Medway Valley start, gentle Greensand Ridge climb, arable fields, apple orchards, scenic ridge walking, coppiced woodland with wood anemone, and then descent to Harrietsham. Sights: Boughton Monchelsea church, Buttercup Goat Sanctuary, and Pepper Box pub. Best in Mar–Apr. Lunch in Linton: the Bull. 24 km.
HRM.publicWalksS2S: **[Harrietsham to Pluckley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/harrietsham-to-pluckley/)**: Gentle ridge, apple orchards, pasture, escarpment views, bluebell woods, Kentish farmland, and churches attached to farms and manor houses. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Fairbourne Heath: the Pepper Box Inn. 15 km.

HSG.name: Hathersage
HSG.adminWalksAll:
**[Hathersage Circular via Stanage Edge Higger Tor and Padley Gorge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hathersage-circular-via-stanage-edge-higger-tor-and-padley-gorge/)**: Green valley, gritstone escarpment edge, open moorland plateaus, ancient hill fort, narrow wooded gorge with tumbling waterfalls, and quiet river pastures. Sights: Stanage Edge, North Lees Hall, Brontë Cottage, Camp Green, Higger Tor, Carl Wark, Longshaw Estate, Padley Gorge, and Padley Chapel. Can be muddy. Lunch in Longshaw: the [Fox House Inn](http://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/yorkshire/thefoxhouselongshaw), or the [Longshaw Tea Room](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/longshaw-burbage-and-the-eastern-moors/features/eating-and-shopping-at-longshaw). 20 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Hathersage Circular via Bretton Clough and Eyam](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hathersage-circular/)**: Rolling hills, drystone walls, meadows, deep wooded valleys, open moorland, heathery blanket peat bog, fast-flowing streams, and mining heritage spoil heaps. Sights: Carl Wark, Higger Tor, Highlow Brook, Bretton Clough, Eyam, Eyam Moor, Wet Withens Bronze Age stone circle, Eyam Moor Barrow, and [David Mellor Design Museum](https://www.davidmellordesign.com/shops/hathersage). Can be muddy. Lunch in Bretton, Great Hucklow, or Foolow. Bretton lunch stop: the [Barrel Inn](https://barrelinn.co.uk/). Great Hucklow lunch stop: the [Queen Anne Inn](http://queenanneinn.co.uk/). Foolow lunch stop: the [Bulls Head Inn](https://thebullsheadfoolow.co.uk/). 22 km. 6 hours.

**[Hathersage to Bamford via Derwent Edge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hathersage-to-bamford-via-bamford-and-hordron-edges/)**: Gritstone edges, open moorland, pastures with views, ancient oak woods, plantation woods, reservoir shore, and Hope Valley. Sights: Stanage Edge, Ladybower Reservoir, Derwent Edge, Hordron Edge Stone Circle, Bamford Edge, and Ashopton Viaduct. Steep descents. Lunch in Bamford: the [Ladybower Inn](https://www.ladybowerinn.com). 25 km. 6.5 hours.

**Hathersage to Grindleford**: A shorter variant of [Hathersage Circular via Stanage Edge Higger Tor and Padley Gorge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hathersage-circular-via-stanage-edge-higger-tor-and-padley-gorge/). Green valley, gritstone escarpment edge, open moorland plateaus, ancient hill fort, and narrow wooded gorge with tumbling waterfalls. Sights: Stanage Edge, North Lees Hall, Brontë Cottage, Higger Tor, Carl Wark, Longshaw Estate, Padley Gorge, Padley Chapel, and Grindleford Station Café. Can be muddy. Lunch in Longshaw: the [Fox House Inn](http://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/yorkshire/thefoxhouselongshaw), or the [Longshaw Tea Room](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/longshaw-burbage-and-the-eastern-moors/features/eating-and-shopping-at-longshaw). 14 km.

**Hathersage to Bamford via Stanage Edge**: A shorter variant of [Hathersage to Bamford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hathersage-to-bamford-via-bamford-and-hordron-edges/). Gritstone edges, open moorland, pastures with views, ancient oak woods, reservoir shore, and Hope Valley. Sights: Stanage Edge, Ladybower Reservoir, Bamford Edge, and Ashopton Viaduct. Steep descents. Lunch in Bamford: the [Ladybower Inn](https://www.ladybowerinn.com). 20 km.

**Hathersage to Bamford via Hordron Edge Stone Circle**: A variant of [Hathersage to Bamford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hathersage-to-bamford-via-bamford-and-hordron-edges/). Open moorland, prehistoric cairns, stone circles, standing stones, pathless sections, and Moscar and Bamford Moors. Sights: Hordron Edge Stone Circle and Ladybower Reservoir. Avoid Ground Nesting Season. Avoid Mar–Jul. Lunch in Bamford: the [Ladybower Inn](https://www.ladybowerinn.com).

**[Hope to Hathersage](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hope-to-hathersage-or-bamford-via-castleton/)**: Limestone and gritstone uplands, pastures with panoramic views, landslip area, dry limestone gorge, collapsed cave dale, upland moor, and scenic river descent. Sights: Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, Speedwell Cavern, Treak Cliff Cavern, Peak Cavern, Cave Dale, Peveril Castle, Odin Mine, Hope Cement Works, and [David Mellor Design Museum](https://www.davidmellordesign.com/design-museum-cafe). Can be muddy. Lunch in Castleton, or Bradwell. Castleton lunch stops: the Castleton Fish & Chips, the Dolly Tearooms, the Peak District National Park Visitor Centre Café, the Three Roofs Café, the Castle, the Bull's Head, the Castleton Maid Ice Cream, the Peveril Tea Rooms, the George Inn, the Rose Cottage Café, the 1530 The Restaurant, the Tilly's of Castleton, the Ye Olde Nag's Head, the Baytree Tea Room & Gifts, the Rambler's Rest, the Peak Hotel, or the Ye Olde Cheshire Cheese. Bradwell lunch stops: the [Ye Olde Bowling Green Inn](https://www.yeoldebowlinggreenbradwell.co.uk/), the White Hart Inn, the Brook Café, or the [Shoulder of Mutton Inn](https://www.shoulderbradwell.pub/). 27 km. 7 hours.
HSG.publicWalksS2S: **[Hathersage to Bamford via Derwent Edge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hathersage-to-bamford-via-bamford-and-hordron-edges/)**: Gritstone edges, open moorland, pastures with views, ancient oak woods, plantation woods, reservoir shore, and Hope Valley. Sights: Stanage Edge, Ladybower Reservoir, Derwent Edge, Hordron Edge Stone Circle, Bamford Edge, and Ashopton Viaduct. Steep descents. Lunch in Bamford: the [Ladybower Inn](https://www.ladybowerinn.com). 25 km. 6.5 hours.

**[Hathersage Circular via Stanage Edge Higger Tor and Padley Gorge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hathersage-circular-via-stanage-edge-higger-tor-and-padley-gorge/)**: Green valley, gritstone escarpment edge, open moorland plateaus, ancient hill fort, narrow wooded gorge with tumbling waterfalls, and quiet river pastures. Sights: Stanage Edge, North Lees Hall, Brontë Cottage, Camp Green, Higger Tor, Carl Wark, Longshaw Estate, Padley Gorge, and Padley Chapel. Can be muddy. Lunch in Longshaw: the [Fox House Inn](http://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/yorkshire/thefoxhouselongshaw), or the [Longshaw Tea Room](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/longshaw-burbage-and-the-eastern-moors/features/eating-and-shopping-at-longshaw). 20 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Hathersage Circular via Bretton Clough and Eyam](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hathersage-circular/)**: Rolling hills, drystone walls, meadows, deep wooded valleys, open moorland, heathery blanket peat bog, fast-flowing streams, and mining heritage spoil heaps. Sights: Carl Wark, Higger Tor, Highlow Brook, Bretton Clough, Eyam, Eyam Moor, Wet Withens Bronze Age stone circle, Eyam Moor Barrow, and [David Mellor Design Museum](https://www.davidmellordesign.com/shops/hathersage). Can be muddy. Lunch in Bretton, Great Hucklow, or Foolow. Bretton lunch stop: the [Barrel Inn](https://barrelinn.co.uk/). Great Hucklow lunch stop: the [Queen Anne Inn](http://queenanneinn.co.uk/). Foolow lunch stop: the [Bulls Head Inn](https://thebullsheadfoolow.co.uk/). 22 km. 6 hours.
HSG.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Hope to Hathersage](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hope-to-hathersage-or-bamford-via-castleton/)**: Limestone and gritstone uplands, pastures with panoramic views, landslip area, dry limestone gorge, collapsed cave dale, upland moor, and scenic river descent. Sights: Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, Speedwell Cavern, Treak Cliff Cavern, Peak Cavern, Cave Dale, Peveril Castle, Odin Mine, Hope Cement Works, and [David Mellor Design Museum](https://www.davidmellordesign.com/design-museum-cafe). Can be muddy. Lunch in Castleton, or Bradwell. Castleton lunch stops: the Castleton Fish & Chips, the Dolly Tearooms, the Peak District National Park Visitor Centre Café, the Three Roofs Café, the Castle, the Bull's Head, the Castleton Maid Ice Cream, the Peveril Tea Rooms, the George Inn, the Rose Cottage Café, the 1530 The Restaurant, the Tilly's of Castleton, the Ye Olde Nag's Head, the Baytree Tea Room & Gifts, the Rambler's Rest, the Peak Hotel, or the Ye Olde Cheshire Cheese. Bradwell lunch stops: the [Ye Olde Bowling Green Inn](https://www.yeoldebowlinggreenbradwell.co.uk/), the White Hart Inn, the Brook Café, or the [Shoulder of Mutton Inn](https://www.shoulderbradwell.pub/). 27 km. 7 hours.

HSK.name: Hassocks
HSK.adminWalksAll:
**Hassocks Circular via Ditchling Beacon**: Quaint village, woods, and hilly. Sights: [Ditchling](https://www.visitditchling.co.uk/) (beautiful, multiple café/restaurants). Can be muddy. *Last reviewed 27 February 2026.* 16 km. 380 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2794137045)**

**Hassocks to Lewes**: South Downs chalk grassland ridge, panoramic views to sea and inland, dew ponds, Iron Age fort, wooded climbs, riverside embankment, and historic town. Sights: Church of St John the Baptist, [Jill Windmill](http://www.jillwindmill.org.uk), Jack Windmill, Ditchling Beacon, [Chattri Memorial](http://www.chattri.com/), [Old Hamsey Church](http://www.friendsofhamsey.org/history/), Anne of Cleves House, Lewes Castle, Barbican House Museum, Lewes Priory, St John Sub Castro, and Bull House. Can be slippery. Lunch in Plumpton: the Half Moon. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 17 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Hassocks to Brighton via St Margaret's Church](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hassocks-to-brighton/)**: South Downs valleys, open chalk downland, ancient woods, parkland, university campus, suburban streets, and seafront. Sights: St Margaret's Church, Ditchling Beacon, Ditchling Beacon Nature Reserve, Stanmer House, Stanmer Church, University of Sussex, Swan Inn, Amex Stadium, Kemp Town Racecourse, Brighton Pier, Royal Pavilion, The Lanes, and Brighton College. Can be muddy. Lunch in Stanmer, Falmer, or Falmer South. Stanmer lunch stops: the Stanmer House, or the Stanmer Tea Rooms. Falmer lunch stop: the Swan Inn. Falmer South lunch stops: the Falmer Cafe, or the Park Farm Shop. 22 km. 6 hours.

**Hassocks to Plumpton**: South Downs chalk grassland ridge, dew ponds, wooded descent, farmland paths, and racecourse. Sights: Church of St John the Baptist, [Jill Windmill](http://www.jillwindmill.org.uk), Jack Windmill, Ditchling Beacon, and [Chattri Memorial](http://www.chattri.com/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Plumpton: the Half Moon. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 13 km.

**Hassocks to Falmer**: A shorter variant of [Hassocks to Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hassocks-to-brighton/). South Downs valleys, open chalk downland, ancient woods, parkland, and university campus. Sights: St Margaret's Church, Ditchling Beacon, Ditchling Beacon Nature Reserve, Stanmer House, Stanmer Church, University of Sussex, Swan Inn, and St Laurence Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Stanmer, Falmer, or Falmer South. Stanmer lunch stops: the Stanmer House, or the Stanmer Tea Rooms. Falmer lunch stop: the Swan Inn. Falmer South lunch stops: the Falmer Cafe, or the Park Farm Shop. 12 km. 3.5 hours.

**Hassocks to Shoreham-by-Sea**: Chalk downland hills, escarpment ridge, quiet road with valley views, farm tracks, nature reserve, and Adur river path. Sights: Wolstonbury Hill, Devil's Dyke, Edburton Hill, Mill Hill Nature Reserve, and Bramber Castle. Lunch in Devil's Dyke area, or Devil's Dyke. Devil's Dyke area lunch stop: the Wildflower Cafe. Devil's Dyke lunch stop: the Devil's Dyke pub. A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 20 km.

**Hassocks to Brighton via Clayton Windmills**: An alternative variant of [Hassocks to Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hassocks-to-brighton/). Residential paths, woodland, open downland ridge, chalk track along South Downs Way, and windmills. Sights: Clayton Windmills, Jack Windmill, Jill Windmill, Church of St John the Baptist, Butcher's Wood, Keymer Post, and Ditchling Beacon Nature Reserve. Can be muddy. Lunch in Stanmer, or Falmer. Stanmer lunch stops: the Stanmer House, or the Stanmer Tea Rooms. Falmer lunch stop: the Swan Inn.

**[Burgess Hill to Hassocks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/burgess-hill-to-hassocks/)**: Rolling farmland, chalky downland, wooded copses, open hilltops, golf courses, picturesque villages, and panoramic South Downs ridge. Sights: Ditchling Beacon, Wolstonbury Hill, Jack and Jill Windmills, Hurstpierpoint College, Sussex Border Path, Indian War Memorial, and Holy Trinity Church. Can be very muddy. Lunch in Ditchling, Pyecombe, Hurstpierpoint, or Clayton. Ditchling lunch stop: the [Bull](https://www.thebullditchling.com/). Pyecombe lunch stop: the [Plough](http://www.theploughpyecombe.co.uk/). Hurstpierpoint lunch stops: the [New Inn](https://www.thenewinnhurst.com/), the [Horse Inn Hurst](https://whatpub.com/pubs/BRI/418/horse-inn-hurst-hurstpierpoint), the [Poacher](https://whatpub.com/pubs/BRI/20/poacher-hurstpierpoint), or the [Morleys Bistro](https://morleys-bistro.com/). Clayton lunch stop: the [Jack and Jill](http://www.thejackandjillinn.co.uk/). 26 km. 7 hours.
HSK.publicWalksCircular: **Hassocks Circular via Ditchling Beacon**: Quaint village, woods, and hilly. Sights: [Ditchling](https://www.visitditchling.co.uk/) (beautiful, multiple café/restaurants). Can be muddy. 16 km. 380 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2794137045)**

HSL.name: Haslemere
HSL.adminWalksAll:
**Milford to Haslemere via the Devil's Punch Bowl**: Rambler favourite! Remote heathland, pretty lakes, sandy bridleways, ancient woodland, heather and gorse commons, and a dramatic natural amphitheatre. Sights: [Unknown Sailor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unknown_Sailor), Church of St Michael and All Angels, Devil's Punch Bowl, and Haslemere Museum. Can be muddy. Best in Jul–Aug. Lunch in Thursley: the [Three Horseshoes](http://threehorseshoesthursley.com/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 270 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2905513618)**

**Liphook to Haslemere via Fernhurst**: Mixed oak, beech and chestnut woods, bridleways, streams, open countryside, horse paddocks, and a final climb into a market town. Sights: Shulbrede Priory, Haslemere Museum, Valewood Park, and Black Down. Can be muddy. Lunch in Fernhurst: the Red Lion, or the Fern (Cafe) at the Pavilion. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 290 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920301093)**

**Haslemere Circular**: Mixed National Trust woodland, heathland with bracken, gorse, heather and bilberry, hilltop plateau, open views, rhododendron tunnels, and undulating bridleways. Sights: [Black Down](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blackdown,_West_Sussex), Temple of the Winds, [Haslemere Museum](http://www.haslemeremuseum.co.uk/), Marley Common, and Valewood Park. Can be muddy. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in Fernhurst: the Red Lion, or the Fern (Cafe) at the Pavilion. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 13 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Haslemere to Farnham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haslemere-to-farnham/)**: Wooded ridge, heathland valleys, lowland peat bog, large commons, MOD terrain, quiet woodland paths and lanes, and river valley. Sights: Temple of the Four Winds, Gibbet Hill, Devil's Punch Bowl, Thursley Common, Hankley Common, Atlantic Wall, and Waverley Abbey. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Hindhead, Thursley, or Tilford. Hindhead lunch stops: the [Devil's Punch Bowl Café](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/hindhead-commons-and-the-devils-punch-bowl), or the [Devil's Punchbowl Hotel](http://www.devilspunchbowlhotel.co.uk/). Thursley lunch stop: the [Three Horseshoes](http://www.threehorseshoesthursley.com/). Tilford lunch stops: the [Duke of Cambridge](http://www.dukeofcambridgetilford.co.uk/), or the [Barley Mow](http://www.thebarleymowtilford.com/). 23 km. 5.5 hours.

**Witley to Haslemere via Crown Inn**: Gentle farmland, bridleways, mixed woodland, open fields, National Trust copses, and a medieval village green. Sights: Crown Inn, St Mary's Church, Sydenhurst Estate, Combe Court, Frillinghurst Wood, Swan Barn Farm, Witley Copse, Haslemere Museum, and RSPCA Lockwood Equine Centre. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chiddingfold: the Crown Inn, the Swan, or the Elliott's Coffee Shop. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 13 km. 4 hours.

**[Witley to Haslemere via Thursley, Surrey Hills](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/witley-to-haslemere-via-thursley-surrey-hills/)**: Wooded countryside, heathland, sunken byway, prolonged hill climb, moorland rim, greensand hills, and quaint village. Sights: [Thursley](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thursley), [Gibbet Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gibbet_Hill,_Hindhead), Devil's Punch Bowl, [Sailor's Stone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unknown_Sailor), St Michael and All Angels Church, and [Fly Agaric](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amanita_muscaria). Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Thursley: the Three Horseshoes. 17 km.

**Milford to Haslemere via Devil's Punch Bowl**: Rambler favourite! Heathland, rim path with views over Devil's Punch Bowl, and shorter and easier than main descent route. Sights: Devil's Punch Bowl and Church of St Michael and All Angels. Lunch in Thursley: the [Three Horseshoes](http://threehorseshoesthursley.com/). A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).

**Milford to Haslemere via Gibbet Hill**: Rambler favourite! Heathland ridge, historic gibbet site, viewpoint summit, and rim path with valleys left and right. Sights: Gibbet Hill, Sailor's Stone, and Devil's Punch Bowl. Lunch in Thursley: the [Three Horseshoes](http://threehorseshoesthursley.com/). A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).
HSL.publicWalksS2SEnding:
**Milford to Haslemere via the Devil's Punch Bowl**: Rambler favourite! Remote heathland, pretty lakes, sandy bridleways, ancient woodland, heather and gorse commons, and a dramatic natural amphitheatre. Sights: [Unknown Sailor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unknown_Sailor), Church of St Michael and All Angels, Devil's Punch Bowl, and Haslemere Museum. Can be muddy. Best in Jul–Aug. Lunch in Thursley: the [Three Horseshoes](http://threehorseshoesthursley.com/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 270 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2905513618)**

**Liphook to Haslemere via Fernhurst**: Mixed oak, beech and chestnut woods, bridleways, streams, open countryside, horse paddocks, and a final climb into a market town. Sights: Shulbrede Priory, Haslemere Museum, Valewood Park, and Black Down. Can be muddy. Lunch in Fernhurst: the Red Lion, or the Fern (Cafe) at the Pavilion. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 290 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920301093)**

HSY.name: Horsley
HSY.adminWalksAll:
**[Horsley to West Byfleet](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/horsley-to-west-byfleet/)**: Woods, fields, open sandy heathland, commons, canal towpath, and suburban road. Sights: Chatley Semaphore Tower, [RHS Garden Wisley](http://www.rhs.org.uk/Gardens/Wisley), Ockham Mill, River Wey and Wey Navigation, and Hatchford Park. Can be muddy. Lunch in Martyr's Green, Ockham Common car park, or Pyrford Lock. Martyr's Green lunch stop: the [Black Swan](http://www.blackswanockham.com/). Ockham Common car park lunch stop: the Ockham Bites. Pyrford Lock lunch stop: the [Anchor](http://anchorpyrford.co.uk/). 15 km.

**[Guildford to Horsley via Chantries Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/guildford-to-horsley/)**: North Downs ridge, escarpments, bluebell woods, downland slopes, upland country with long views, wooded stretches, and estate grounds. Sights: Chantries Hill, St Martha's-on-the-Hill Church, Newlands Corner, West Horsley Place, and Grange Park Opera. Steep hill climbs. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Albury, Chilworth, or Newlands Corner. Albury lunch stop: the Drummond at Albury. Chilworth lunch stop: the Percy Arms. Newlands Corner lunch stop: the Plucky Pheasant. 21 km.

**Chilworth to Horsley**: A shorter variant of [Guildford to Horsley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/guildford-to-horsley/). North Downs ridge, downland escarpment, wooded stretches, upland country, and estate grounds. Sights: Chilworth Gunpowder Mills, Newlands Corner, and West Horsley Place. Lunch in Albury, or Chilworth. Albury lunch stop: the Drummond at Albury. Chilworth lunch stop: the Percy Arms. 14 km.

**Guildford to Horsley via St Martha's-on-the-Hill Church**: A shorter variant of [Guildford to Horsley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/guildford-to-horsley/). North Downs ridge, downland escarpment, wooded stretches, upland country with long views, and estate grounds. Sights: St Martha's-on-the-Hill Church, Newlands Corner, and West Horsley Place. Steep hill climbs. Lunch in Newlands Corner: the Plucky Pheasant. 17 km.

**Guildford to Horsley via Pewley Down**: A shorter variant of [Guildford to Horsley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/guildford-to-horsley/). Pewley Down, downland, North Downs ridge, wooded stretches, upland country, and estate grounds. Sights: Pewley Down, St Martha's-on-the-Hill Church, Newlands Corner, and West Horsley Place. Steep hill climbs. Lunch in Albury: the Drummond at Albury. 19 km.

Lunch in Martyr's Green, Ockham Common car park, or Pyrford Lock. Martyr's Green lunch stop: the [Black Swan](http://www.blackswanockham.com/). Ockham Common car park lunch stop: the Ockham Bites. Pyrford Lock lunch stop: the [Anchor](http://anchorpyrford.co.uk/). 15 km.

Sights: Chantries Hill, St Martha's-on-the-Hill Church, Newlands Corner, West Horsley Place, and Grange Park Opera. Steep hill climbs. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Albury, Chilworth, or Newlands Corner. Albury lunch stop: the Drummond at Albury. Chilworth lunch stop: the Percy Arms. Newlands Corner lunch stop: the Plucky Pheasant. 21 km.

HTN.name: Hatton
HTN.adminWalksAll: **[Hatton Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-1238)**: Canal towpath, tunnel sections, narrow path sections, low bridges, and Warwickshire countryside. Sights: [Hatton Locks](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), Church of St. Mary the Virgin, [Baddesley Clinton](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/baddesley-clinton), [The Grand Union Canal](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Hay Wood](https://www.forestryengland.uk/hay-wood), and Shakespeare Hall. Narrow path sections. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Shrewley, Kingswood Junction, Lapworth, Kingswood, or Rowington. Shrewley lunch stops: the Shrewley Village Store, or the [Durham Ox](https://www.durhamoxshrewley.co.uk). Kingswood Junction lunch stop: the Canal Shop and Moorings. Lapworth lunch stop: the Lapworth Village Shop. Kingswood lunch stops: the Navigation Inn, the Punchbowl, or the Boot Inn. Rowington lunch stop: the [Tom O' the Wood](https://www.tomothewood.co.uk). 8 km. 2.5 hours.
HTN.publicWalksCircular: **[Hatton Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-1238)**: Canal towpath, tunnel sections, narrow path sections, low bridges, and Warwickshire countryside. Sights: [Hatton Locks](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), Church of St. Mary the Virgin, [Baddesley Clinton](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/baddesley-clinton), [The Grand Union Canal](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Hay Wood](https://www.forestryengland.uk/hay-wood), and Shakespeare Hall. Narrow path sections. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Shrewley, Kingswood Junction, Lapworth, Kingswood, or Rowington. Shrewley lunch stops: the Shrewley Village Store, or the [Durham Ox](https://www.durhamoxshrewley.co.uk). Kingswood Junction lunch stop: the Canal Shop and Moorings. Lapworth lunch stop: the Lapworth Village Shop. Kingswood lunch stops: the Navigation Inn, the Punchbowl, or the Boot Inn. Rowington lunch stop: the [Tom O' the Wood](https://www.tomothewood.co.uk). 8 km. 2.5 hours.

HUN.name: Huntingdon
HUN.adminWalksAll:
**[Huntingdon Circular via Portholme Meadow](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/huntingdon-circular/)**: Flat riverside paths, water meadows, farmland tracks, tarmac lanes, nature reserve, and market town streets. Sights: [Huntingdon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Huntingdon), [Houghton Mill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/cambridgeshire/houghton-mill-and-waterclose-meadows), [Cromwell Museum](https://www.cromwellmuseum.org), Castle Hills, Old Bridge, All Saints Church, Hartford, St Mary the Virgin, Houghton, Godmanchester Nature Reserve, St Mary the Virgin, Godmanchester, Chinese Bridge, Portholme Meadow, All Saints Church, Huntingdon, and Sebastopol Cannon. Prone to flooding. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Wyton, or Houghton. Wyton lunch stop: the [Three Jolly Butchers](https://3jbs.co.uk). Houghton lunch stop: the [Three Horseshoes Inn](https://www.threehorseshoesinnhoughton.co.uk). 19 km. 4 hours.

**Huntingdon Circular via St Ives**: A longer variant of [Huntingdon Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/huntingdon-circular/). Flat riverside paths, water meadows, village lanes, meadow crossings, market town streets, and nature reserve. Sights: [Huntingdon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Huntingdon), [St Ives](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Ives,_Cambridgeshire), [Norris Museum](https://www.norrismuseum.org.uk), [Houghton Mill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/cambridgeshire/houghton-mill-and-waterclose-meadows), [The Manor Garden](https://www.greenknowe.co.uk), [Cromwell Museum](https://www.cromwellmuseum.org), Castle Hills, Old Bridge, All Saints Church, Hartford, St Mary the Virgin, Houghton, St Margaret of Antioch, Hemingford Abbots, Hemingford Manor Garden, St James Church, Hemingford Grey, Bridge Chapel, Old Mill, St Ives, All Saints Church, St Ives, Holt Island, Godmanchester Nature Reserve, St Mary the Virgin, Godmanchester, Chinese Bridge, All Saints Church, Huntingdon, and Sebastopol Cannon. Prone to flooding. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Wyton, Houghton, Hemingford Abbots, or Hemingford Grey. Wyton lunch stop: the [Three Jolly Butchers](https://3jbs.co.uk). Houghton lunch stop: the [Three Horseshoes Inn](https://www.threehorseshoesinnhoughton.co.uk). Hemingford Abbots lunch stop: the [Axe & Compass](https://axeandcompass.co.uk). Hemingford Grey lunch stop: the [Cock](https://thecockhemingford.co.uk). 25 km. 5.5 hours.
HUN.publicWalksCircular: **[Huntingdon Circular via Portholme Meadow](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/huntingdon-circular/)**: Flat riverside paths, water meadows, farmland tracks, tarmac lanes, nature reserve, and market town streets. Sights: [Huntingdon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Huntingdon), [Houghton Mill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/cambridgeshire/houghton-mill-and-waterclose-meadows), [Cromwell Museum](https://www.cromwellmuseum.org), Castle Hills, Old Bridge, All Saints Church, Hartford, St Mary the Virgin, Houghton, Godmanchester Nature Reserve, St Mary the Virgin, Godmanchester, Chinese Bridge, Portholme Meadow, All Saints Church, Huntingdon, and Sebastopol Cannon. Prone to flooding. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Wyton, or Houghton. Wyton lunch stop: the [Three Jolly Butchers](https://3jbs.co.uk). Houghton lunch stop: the [Three Horseshoes Inn](https://www.threehorseshoesinnhoughton.co.uk). 19 km. 4 hours.

HUR.name: Hurst Green
HUR.adminWalksAll:
**Hurst Green to Penshurst**: 21 km. 350 m uphill. 6 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2934416642)**

**[Hurst Green to Sevenoaks via Toy's Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hurst-green-to-sevenoaks/)**: Greensand ridge, chart hills, undulating woodland, commons, deer park, historic estates, and suburban old town. Sights: Chartwell, Toy's Hill, Ide Hill, Knole Park, and Knole Palace. Steep climbs. Lunch in Chartwell, or Toys Hill. Chartwell lunch stop: the NT restaurant at Chartwell. Toys Hill lunch stop: the [Fox & Hounds](http://www.foxhoundstoyshill.co.uk/). 18 km.

**[Hurst Green to Riddlesdown](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hurst-green-to-riddlesdown/)**: Heathland common, golf courses, farmland, North Downs escarpment, open downland, woodland strips, and views across the High Weald and South Downs. Sights: Limpsfield Common, River Eden, Titsey church, Tatsfield church, Henley Wood Enclosure, and Riddlesdown. Can be muddy. Lunch in Tatsfield: the Old Ship. 21 km. 5.5 hours.

**Hurst Green to Godstone**: A variant of [Oxted to Lingfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxted-to-lingfield/). Hillside paths, Low Weald farmland, ancient woodland, and flat fields alongside railway. Sights: [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), Tenchleys Manor, and St George's Church. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Staffhurst Wood: the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted). 13 km.

**Hurst Green to Lingfield**: A shorter variant of [Oxted to Lingfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxted-to-lingfield/). Hillside paths, Low Weald farmland, ancient woodland, old manor houses, flat plain, and nature reserve. Sights: [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), [Lingfield Nature Reserve](https://www.lingfieldreserves.org.uk), Tenchleys Manor, St George's Church, Crowhurst Place, Ss Peter and Paul, New Place, Old Cage, Community Orchard, and Lingfield Oak. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Staffhurst Wood: the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Hurst Green to Oxted**: Greensand hills, woodland, open country, and village paths. Can be muddy. Lunch in Limpsfield Chart: the [Carpenters Arms](http://carpenterslimpsfield.co.uk/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 9 km. 3 hours.

**Hurst Green to Upper Warlingham**: A shorter variant of [Hurst Green to Riddlesdown](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hurst-green-to-riddlesdown/). Heathland common, golf courses, farmland, North Downs escarpment, open downland, woodland strips, and views across the Caterham Valley. Sights: Limpsfield Common, River Eden, Titsey church, Tatsfield church, and Henley Wood Enclosure. Steep descent. Lunch in Tatsfield: the Old Ship. 19 km.

**Hurst Green to Sevenoaks via Chartwell**: Easterly direction and mainly woodland walking. Sights: [Chartwell](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk) and Ide Hill. Lunch in Chartwell, or Ide Hill. Chartwell lunch stop: the [NT restaurant at Chartwell](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk). Ide Hill lunch stop: the [Cock Inn](https://cockinnidehill.co.uk/). An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 21 km.

**Godstone to Hurst Green**: A shorter variant of [Godstone to Oxted](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/godstone-to-oxted/). Low-lying farmland, river valley, woodland, village green, and playing fields. Sights: [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), St George's Church Crowhurst, Lagham Manor, and Greenwich Meridian Trail. Can be muddy. Lunch in Staffhurst Wood: the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted). 13 km. 3 hours.
HUR.publicWalksS2S: **Hurst Green to Penshurst**: 21 km. 350 m uphill. 6 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2934416642)**

HWH.name: Haltwhistle
HWH.adminWalksAll:
**Haltwhistle to Bardon Mill**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). Burn gorge, upland plateau, Roman military zone, crags, Sycamore Gap, lough, and open moorland. Sights: Haltwhistle Burn Roman Fortlet, Great Chesters Fort, Sycamore Gap, Crag Lough, and Housesteads Fort. Lunch in Walltown Quarry, near Haltwhistle, near Steel Rigg, or Housesteads. Walltown Quarry lunch stop: the Roman Army Museum Tea Room. near Haltwhistle lunch stop: the Milecastle Inn. near Steel Rigg lunch stops: the Twice Brewed Inn, or the Once Brewed Coffee and Bakehouse at The Sill. Housesteads lunch stop: the Housesteads Fort Café. 18 km.

**Brampton to Haltwhistle**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). Ridge, wooded gorge, river, Roman remains, quarry crags, upland moorland, and market town. Sights: Lanercost Priory, Birdoswald Fort, Willowford Bridge, Milecastle 48, Thirlwall Castle, Roman Army Museum, and Great Chesters Fort. Lunch in Haytongate, Birdoswald, Gilsland, Greenhead, or Walltown Quarry. Haytongate lunch stop: the Haytongate Farm Snack Hut. Birdoswald lunch stop: the Birdoswald Fort Café. Gilsland lunch stops: the House of Meg Café, the Samson Inn, or the Bridge Inn. Greenhead lunch stops: the Greenhead Hotel, or the Greenhead Tea Room. Walltown Quarry lunch stop: the Roman Army Museum Tea Room. 28 km.
HWH.publicWalksS2S: **Haltwhistle to Bardon Mill**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). Burn gorge, upland plateau, Roman military zone, crags, Sycamore Gap, lough, and open moorland. Sights: Haltwhistle Burn Roman Fortlet, Great Chesters Fort, Sycamore Gap, Crag Lough, and Housesteads Fort. Lunch in Walltown Quarry, near Haltwhistle, near Steel Rigg, or Housesteads. Walltown Quarry lunch stop: the Roman Army Museum Tea Room. near Haltwhistle lunch stop: the Milecastle Inn. near Steel Rigg lunch stops: the Twice Brewed Inn, or the Once Brewed Coffee and Bakehouse at The Sill. Housesteads lunch stop: the Housesteads Fort Café. 18 km.
HWH.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Brampton to Haltwhistle**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). Lunch in Haytongate, Birdoswald, Gilsland, Greenhead, or Walltown Quarry. Haytongate lunch stop: the Haytongate Farm Snack Hut. Birdoswald lunch stop: the Birdoswald Fort Café. Gilsland lunch stops: the House of Meg Café, the Samson Inn, or the Bridge Inn. Greenhead lunch stops: the Greenhead Hotel, or the Greenhead Tea Room. Walltown Quarry lunch stop: the Roman Army Museum Tea Room. 28 km.

HWN.name: Harlow Town
HWN.adminWalksAll: **[Harlow Town Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/harlow-circular/)**: Flat, easy farmland, open fields, riverside, and quiet villages. Sights: Gilston Park House and Hunsdon Airfield. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hunsdon: the Crown, or the [Fox & Hounds](http://www.foxandhounds-hunsdon.co.uk/). 14 km.
HWN.publicWalksCircular: **[Harlow Town Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/harlow-circular/)**: Flat, easy farmland, open fields, riverside, and quiet villages. Sights: Gilston Park House and Hunsdon Airfield. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hunsdon: the Crown, or the [Fox & Hounds](http://www.foxandhounds-hunsdon.co.uk/). 14 km.

HWW.name: How Wood
HWW.adminWalksAll: **Park Street to How Wood**: An alternative variant of [Abbey Trail](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/).
HWW.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Park Street to How Wood**: An alternative variant of [Abbey Trail](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/).

HYS.name: Hayes
HYS.adminWalksAll:
**[Hayes Circular Walk](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hayes-circular/)**: Three commons, coppiced woodland, open heathland, gorse, ponds, riverside paths, and suburban road crossings. Sights: Hayes Common, Keston Ponds, Caesar's Well, Keston Common, West Wickham Common, Ravensbourne Lodge, and Iron Age mound and ditch. Busy road crossings. Lunch in Keston: the [Fox Inn](https://thefoxkeston.co.uk/), the [Greyhound](https://www.greyhound.pub/), the [Triple Two Cafe](https://tripletwocoffee.co.uk/keston/), or the [Heathfields](https://heathfieldskeston.co.uk/). 9 km.

**[Hayes to Knockholt](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hayes-to-knockholt/)**: Fields, woods, valleys, heathland, commons, and London Green Belt countryside. Sights: [Down House](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/server.php?show=nav.14922), Keston Ponds, Caesar's Well, Wilberforce Oak, Holwood House, Downe Bank Nature Reserve, St Peter and St Paul Church, and Hayes Common. Can be waterlogged. Lunch in Cudham, or Downe. Cudham lunch stop: the [Blacksmith's Arms](http://www.theblacksmithsarms.co.uk/). Downe lunch stops: the Queens Head, or the George & Dragon. 16 km.

**[Whyteleafe to Hayes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whyteleafe-to-hayes/)**: Gently rolling countryside, shady footpaths and bridleways, open valleys, woods, chalk downland, and quiet rural hamlets near the London/Surrey border. Sights: [Biggin Hill Airport](https://www.military-airshows.co.uk/ven10.htm), Hutchinson's Bank Local Nature Reserve, Greatpark Clock Tower, Coal Tax posts, Hayes Common, London Outer Orbital Path, Leaves Green Circular Walk, and Nash Circular Walk. Can be muddy. Lunch in Fickleshole, or Great Farleigh Green. Fickleshole lunch stop: the [White Bear](https://www.whitebearwarlingham.co.uk). Great Farleigh Green lunch stop: the [Harrow](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/theharrowwarlingham). 16 km. 4 hours.
HYS.publicWalksS2S: **[Hayes to Knockholt](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hayes-to-knockholt/)**: Fields, woods, valleys, heathland, commons, and London Green Belt countryside. Can be waterlogged. Lunch in Cudham, or Downe. Cudham lunch stop: the [Blacksmith's Arms](http://www.theblacksmithsarms.co.uk/). Downe lunch stops: the Queens Head, or the George & Dragon. 16 km.

ILN.name: Ilkeston
ILN.adminWalksAll: **[Long Eaton to Ilkeston via Erewash Valley](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Canal, industrial towns, and disused railway viaduct. Sights: Bennerley Viaduct, Erewash Canal, and Erewash Valley Trail. 15 km. 30 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/416%20Erewash%20Valley.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925788454)**
ILN.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Long Eaton to Ilkeston via Erewash Valley](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Canal, industrial towns, and disused railway viaduct. Sights: Bennerley Viaduct, Erewash Canal, and Erewash Valley Trail. 15 km. 30 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/416%20Erewash%20Valley.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925788454)**

INT.name: Ingatestone
INT.adminWalksAll:
**[Ingatestone to Battlesbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ingatestone-to-battlesbridge/)**: Wid Valley fields, riverside paths, cattle farm estate, bluebell woods, tree-lined tracks, open fields with far views, and Crouch Valley finish. Sights: Ingatestone Hall, St. Mary's Church Buttsbury, Stock Tower Mill, and Hanningfield Reservoir Nature Discovery Centre. Can be muddy. Lunch in Stock, or South Hanningfield. Stock lunch stops: the [Bakers Arms](http://www.bakersarms.co.uk/), the [Hoop](http://www.thehoop.co.uk/), the [Dandelion & Burdock Kitchen & Lounge](https://www.dandelion-burdock.co.uk/), the [Bear Inn](http://www.thebearstock.com/), or the [Harvard Inn](https://theharvardinn.co.uk/). South Hanningfield lunch stop: the [Old Windmill](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/oldwindmill/). 24 km. 5.5 hours.

**Ingatestone to Wickford**: An alternative variant of [Ingatestone to Battlesbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ingatestone-to-battlesbridge/). Wid Valley fields, riverside paths, cattle farm estate, bluebell woods, tree-lined tracks, and Crowsheath Community Woodland. Sights: Ingatestone Hall, St. Mary's Church Buttsbury, Stock Tower Mill, and Hanningfield Reservoir Nature Discovery Centre. Can be muddy. Lunch in Stock, or South Hanningfield. Stock lunch stops: the [Bakers Arms](http://www.bakersarms.co.uk/), the [Hoop](http://www.thehoop.co.uk/), the [Dandelion & Burdock Kitchen & Lounge](https://www.dandelion-burdock.co.uk/), the [Bear Inn](http://www.thebearstock.com/), or the [Harvard Inn](https://theharvardinn.co.uk/). South Hanningfield lunch stop: the [Old Windmill](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/oldwindmill/). 18 km.

**[Epping to Ingatestone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/epping-to-ingatestone-via-chipping-ongar/)**: Mildly undulating Essex countryside, ancient woods, arable fields, historic villages, old churches, manor houses, and London Clay subsoil. Sights: Chipping Ongar Motte-and-Double-Bailey, St. Andrew's Church, Greensted-juxta-Ongar, St. Laurence Church, Blackmore, and Gernon Bushes SSSI. Can be muddy. Lunch in Coopersale: the Theydon Oak. 26 km. 6 hours.
INT.publicWalksS2S: **[Ingatestone to Battlesbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ingatestone-to-battlesbridge/)**: Wid Valley fields, riverside paths, cattle farm estate, bluebell woods, tree-lined tracks, open fields with far views, and Crouch Valley finish. Sights: Ingatestone Hall, St. Mary's Church Buttsbury, Stock Tower Mill, and Hanningfield Reservoir Nature Discovery Centre. Can be muddy. Lunch in Stock, or South Hanningfield. Stock lunch stops: the [Bakers Arms](http://www.bakersarms.co.uk/), the [Hoop](http://www.thehoop.co.uk/), the [Dandelion & Burdock Kitchen & Lounge](https://www.dandelion-burdock.co.uk/), the [Bear Inn](http://www.thebearstock.com/), or the [Harvard Inn](https://theharvardinn.co.uk/). South Hanningfield lunch stop: the [Old Windmill](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/oldwindmill/). 24 km. 5.5 hours.

Lunch in Coopersale: the Theydon Oak. 26 km. 6 hours.

ISP.name: Islip
ISP.adminWalksAll:
**[Islip Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/islip-circular/)**: Rolling countryside, fields, woods, rivers, wetland nature reserve, quiet villages, and old churches. Sights: [RSPB Otmoor](https://www.rspb.org.uk/reserves-and-events/reserves-a-z/otmoor-reserve/), [Otmoor Birding Blog](http://otmoorbirding.blogspot.com/), St Nicholas Church Islip, Wetland Watch bird hide, Church of the Assumption of the Blessed Virgin Mary, Holy Rood Church Woodeaton, Woodeaton Manor House Folly, and Red Lion. Can be muddy. Lunch in Beckley: the [Abingdon Arms](https://www.theabingdonarms.co.uk/). 17 km.

**Islip to Oxford Parkway**: A variant of [Islip Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/islip-circular/). Rolling countryside, fields, woods, rivers, wetland nature reserve, quiet villages, and old churches. Sights: [RSPB Otmoor](https://www.rspb.org.uk/reserves-and-events/reserves-a-z/otmoor-reserve/), St Nicholas Church Islip, Wetland Watch bird hide, Church of the Assumption of the Blessed Virgin Mary, and Holy Rood Church Woodeaton. Can be muddy. Lunch in Beckley: the [Abingdon Arms](https://www.theabingdonarms.co.uk/).

KBW.name: Knebworth
KBW.adminWalksAll:
**[Knebworth Circular via Graffidge Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knebworth-circular/)**: Undulating Hertfordshire countryside, woodland, large open arable fields, quiet lanes, deer park, and pretty villages. Sights: Knebworth House, St Mary and Thomas of Canterbury church, St Paul's Walden Bury, All Saints Church St Paul's Walden, Hoo Park, The Bury Codicote, and Graffidge Wood. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in St Paul's Walden, Whitwell, Codicote, Rabley Heath, or Knebworth. St Paul's Walden lunch stop: the Strathmore Arms. Whitwell lunch stops: the Bull Inn, or the Emily's Tea Shop. Codicote lunch stops: the Goat, or the Globe. Rabley Heath lunch stop: the Robin Hood & Little John. Knebworth lunch stops: the Station pub, or the Sugar Boutique. 19 km.

**Knebworth Circular via Knebworth House**: An alternative variant of [Knebworth Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knebworth-circular/). Undulating Hertfordshire countryside, woodland, large open arable fields, quiet lanes, deer park, and pretty villages. Sights: Knebworth House, St Mary and Thomas of Canterbury church, St Paul's Walden Bury, All Saints Church St Paul's Walden, Hoo Park, and The Bury Codicote. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Whitwell, St Paul's Walden, Codicote, or Knebworth. Whitwell lunch stops: the Bull Inn, or the Emily's Tea Shop. St Paul's Walden lunch stop: the Strathmore Arms. Codicote lunch stop: the Goat. Knebworth lunch stops: the Station pub, or the Sugar Boutique. 19 km.

**[Knebworth to Welwyn Garden City via Brocket Hall](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knebworth-to-welwyn-garden-city/)**: Gently undulating woodland, open fields, deer park, golf course, nature reserve, and formal garden city streets. Sights: Knebworth House, St Mary and Thomas of Canterbury Church, Ayot St Lawrence Palladian Church, Old St Lawrence Church, Brocket Hall, Shaw's Corner, and Sherrardspark Wood. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Ayot St Lawrence: the Brocket Arms. 21 km.

**Knebworth to Hitchin**: A variant of [Knebworth Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knebworth-circular/). Woodland, open fields, deer park, bluebell woods, small Hertfordshire villages, and historic market town. Sights: Knebworth House, St Mary and Thomas of Canterbury church, St Paul's Walden Bury, All Saints Church St Paul's Walden, Stagenhoe, Hitch Wood, Wain Wood, Hitchin Priory, St Mary's Church Hitchin, and Red Lion pub Preston. Inquisitive cattle. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Preston, or Hitchin. Preston lunch stop: the Red Lion. Hitchin lunch stop: the Halsey's Deli & Eatery. 19 km.

**Knebworth to Welwyn Garden City via Ayot Greenway**: A shorter variant of [Knebworth to Welwyn Garden City](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knebworth-to-welwyn-garden-city/). Gently undulating woodland, open fields, deer park, and disused railway trackbed through tree-lined cutting. Sights: Knebworth House, St Mary and Thomas of Canterbury Church, Ayot St Lawrence Palladian Church, Old St Lawrence Church, Shaw's Corner, and Ayot Greenway. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Ayot St Lawrence: the Brocket Arms. 19 km.

Sights: Knebworth House, St Mary and Thomas of Canterbury Church, Ayot St Lawrence Palladian Church, Old St Lawrence Church, Brocket Hall, Shaw's Corner, and Sherrardspark Wood. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Ayot St Lawrence: the Brocket Arms. 21 km.

Knebworth lunch stops: the Station pub, or the Sugar Boutique. 19 km.

KCK.name: Knockholt
KCK.adminWalksAll:
**[Knockholt Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knockholt-circular/)**: Rural fields with wildflowers, bluebell woods, and several village churches. Sights: [Down House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/home-of-charles-darwin-down-house/). Numerous stiles throughout. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Downe, or Cudham. Downe lunch stops: the [Queens Head](https://www.queensheaddowne.com/), or the [George & Dragon](https://www.georgeanddragondowne.com/). Cudham lunch stop: the [Blacksmiths Arms](https://www.theblacksmithsarmscudham.com/). 18 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Knockholt to Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knockholt-to-otford/)**: Wooded hills, steep ridges, hidden downland valley, bluebell woods, beech and sweet chestnut woodland, and fields. Sights: Magpie Bottom, Shoreham Aircraft Museum, and Mount Vineyard. Descents can be slippery. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in Shoreham: the Kings Arms, the Samuel Palmer, or the Crown. 12 km.

**Knockholt to Shoreham**: A shorter variant of [Knockholt to Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knockholt-to-otford/). Wooded hills, steep ridge climb, and descent into village. Sights: Shoreham Aircraft Museum. Descent can be slippery. Lunch in Shoreham: the Kings Arms, the Samuel Palmer, or the Crown. 5 km.

**[Hayes to Knockholt](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hayes-to-knockholt/)**: Fields, woods, valleys, heathland, commons, and London Green Belt countryside. Sights: [Down House](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/server.php?show=nav.14922), Keston Ponds, Caesar's Well, Wilberforce Oak, Holwood House, Downe Bank Nature Reserve, St Peter and St Paul Church, and Hayes Common. Can be waterlogged. Lunch in Cudham, or Downe. Cudham lunch stop: the [Blacksmith's Arms](http://www.theblacksmithsarms.co.uk/). Downe lunch stops: the Queens Head, or the George & Dragon. 16 km.

**[Caterham to Knockholt](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/caterham-to-knockholt/)**: North Downs escarpment, woods carpeted with wild garlic and bluebells, open pasture, arable fields, fine southward views, and some M25 road noise early on. Sights: [Titsey Place](http://www.titsey.org/), [North Downs Way](http://www.nationaltrail.co.uk/northdowns/), [Botley Hill](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Botley_Hill), Oxted Downs, Chevening House, and Titsey Brewing Taproom. Can be slippery. Lunch in Caterham area, Botley Hill, or Titsey. Caterham area lunch stop: the [Godstone Vineyards](http://www.godstonevineyards.uk/). Botley Hill lunch stop: the [Botley Hill Farmhouse](http://www.botleyhill-farmhouse.co.uk/). Titsey lunch stop: the [Titsey Brewing Taproom](https://titseybrewingco.com/taproom/). 26 km.

Sights: Magpie Bottom, Shoreham Aircraft Museum, and Mount Vineyard. Descents can be slippery. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in Shoreham: the Kings Arms, the Samuel Palmer, or the Crown. 12 km.

KEL.name: Kelvedon
KEL.adminWalksAll:
**[Kelvedon Circular via Grange Barn](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kelvedon-circular/)**: Gentle valley, river stretches, country lanes, green lanes, field boundaries, rolling Essex countryside, fields, and woods. Sights: Grange Barn, Paycocke's, Marks Hall Gardens and Arboretum, All Saints Church, and Coggeshall Abbey. Can be muddy. Lunch in Coggeshall, or Marks Hall. Coggeshall lunch stops: the [West Street Vineyard](http://www.weststreetvineyard.co.uk/), the [Ranfield's Brasserie](https://ranfieldsbrasserie.co.uk/), the Bakes and Blooms, the [Chapel Inn](http://www.thechapelinn.com), the [White Hart Hotel](https://www.greenekinginns.co.uk/hotels/white-hart-coggeshall/), or the Woolpack Inn. Marks Hall lunch stop: the [Orchard Kitchen Café](https://www.markshall.org.uk/plan-your-visit/cafe/). 23 km. 5 hours.

**Kelvedon Circular via Nunn's Bridge**: A longer variant of [Kelvedon Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kelvedon-circular/). Gentle valley, river stretches, wooded valley closely following river, country lanes, field boundaries, and rolling Essex countryside. Sights: Grange Barn, Paycocke's, Nunn's Bridge, Marks Hall Gardens and Arboretum, All Saints Church, and Coggeshall Abbey. Can be muddy. Lunch in Coggeshall, or Marks Hall. Coggeshall lunch stops: the [West Street Vineyard](http://www.weststreetvineyard.co.uk/), the [Ranfield's Brasserie](https://ranfieldsbrasserie.co.uk/), the Bakes and Blooms, the [Chapel Inn](http://www.thechapelinn.com), the [White Hart Hotel](https://www.greenekinginns.co.uk/hotels/white-hart-coggeshall/), or the Woolpack Inn. Marks Hall lunch stop: the [Orchard Kitchen Café](https://www.markshall.org.uk/plan-your-visit/cafe/). 25 km.

KEM.name: Kemble
KEM.adminWalksAll: **[Kemble Circular via Thames Head and Cirencester](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kemble-circular/)**: Cotswolds farmland, disused canal towpath, ancient hillfort, beech woods, high watershed, grand parkland vistas, market town streets, quiet fields, and upper Thames riverbank. Sights: Thames Head, Lyd Well, Trewsbury Camp Hillfort, Thames & Severn Canal, Sapperton Canal Tunnel, Hailey Wood, Cirencester Park, Queen Anne Monument, Roman Amphitheatre, Roman Town Wall, and [Thames Path](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thames_Path). Can flood at Thames Head. Lunch in Kemble, or Sapperton. Kemble lunch stop: the [Thames Head Inn](https://www.arkells.com/pub/the-thames-head-inn-cirencester.htm). Sapperton lunch stop: the [Bell at Sapperton](https://www.bellsapperton.co.uk/). 27 km. 6 hours.
KEM.publicWalksCircular: **[Kemble Circular via Thames Head and Cirencester](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kemble-circular/)**: Cotswolds farmland, disused canal towpath, ancient hillfort, beech woods, high watershed, grand parkland vistas, market town streets, quiet fields, and upper Thames riverbank. Sights: Thames Head, Lyd Well, Trewsbury Camp Hillfort, Thames & Severn Canal, Sapperton Canal Tunnel, Hailey Wood, Cirencester Park, Queen Anne Monument, Roman Amphitheatre, Roman Town Wall, and [Thames Path](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thames_Path). Can flood at Thames Head. Lunch in Kemble, or Sapperton. Kemble lunch stop: the [Thames Head Inn](https://www.arkells.com/pub/the-thames-head-inn-cirencester.htm). Sapperton lunch stop: the [Bell at Sapperton](https://www.bellsapperton.co.uk/). 27 km. 6 hours.

Sights: Pytchley, Broughton, and Great Cransley. 18 km. 190 m uphill. 5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/418%20Kettering%20Circular.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925789056)**

KGM.name: Kingham
KGM.adminWalksAll:
**[Kingham to Charlbury via Bruern Abbey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kingham-to-charlbury/)**: Gentle rolling hills, riverside meadows, woods, fields, Cotswold stone villages, and bridleways shared with horse riders. Sights: Bruern Abbey, Shipton under Wychwood, and Ascott under Wychwood. Can be very muddy. Best in Apr, Jun, Jul and Aug. 17 km.

**[Kingham to Moreton-in-Marsh via Adelstrop](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kingham-to-moreton-in-marsh-via-adelstrop/)**: Fields near river Evenlode, quiet tarmac lanes, flower-filled meadows, gentle hills, and picturesque Cotswolds villages and churches. Sights: St Nicholas Church, Adelstrop village and station sign, [St Mary Magdalene Church](http://www.britainexpress.com/counties/glouces/churches/Adlestrop.htm), [Chastleton House](http://www.oxfordshirecotswolds.org/things-to-do/attractions/chastleton-house-p51773), and St Mary's Church, Chastleton. Can be boggy. Best in Jun and Aug. Lunch in Lower Oddington, or Upper Oddington. Lower Oddington lunch stop: the [Fox Inn](https://thefoxatoddington.com/). Upper Oddington lunch stop: the [Horse and Groom](https://www.horseandgroomcotswolds.co.uk/). 17 km. 5 hours.

**[Kingham to Charlbury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kingham-to-charlbury-long/)**: Peaceful linear walk through Cotswold countryside, fields, lanes, and villages. Lunch in Burford, or Swinbrook. Burford lunch stop: the Various. Swinbrook lunch stop: the [Swan Inn](https://www.theswanswinbrook.co.uk/). 34 km.

KGS.name: King's Sutton
KGS.adminWalksAll:
**[King's Sutton Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kings-sutton-circular/)**: Open fields, Cotswold villages, long views, and gentle countryside. Sights: Aynho Park, Aynho Grammar School, Kings Sutton Church, Croughton Church, and Souldern House. Lunch in Aynho, Souldern, Croughton, or Charlton. Aynho lunch stop: the [Cartwright Hotel and Restaurant](http://www.cartwright-hotel.co.uk/). Souldern lunch stop: the [Fox Inn](http://www.thefoxatsouldern.co.uk/). Croughton lunch stop: the [Blackbird Inn](https://whatpub.com/pubs/NTH/153/blackbird-croughton). Charlton lunch stop: the [Rose & Crown](http://theroseandcrowncharlton.co.uk/). 17 km.

**King's Sutton to Banbury**: A longer variant of [Kings Sutton Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kings-sutton-circular/). Open fields, Cotswold villages, long views, and finishing into Banbury. Sights: Aynho Park, Aynho Grammar School, Kings Sutton Church, Croughton Church, and Souldern House. Lunch in Aynho, Souldern, Croughton, or Charlton. Aynho lunch stop: the [Cartwright Hotel and Restaurant](http://www.cartwright-hotel.co.uk/). Sights: Aynho Park, Aynho Grammar School, Kings Sutton Church, Croughton Church, and Souldern House. Lunch in Aynho, Souldern, Croughton, or Charlton. Aynho lunch stop: the [Cartwright Hotel and Restaurant](http://www.cartwright-hotel.co.uk/). Souldern lunch stop: the [Fox Inn](http://www.thefoxatsouldern.co.uk/). Croughton lunch stop: the [Blackbird Inn](https://whatpub.com/pubs/NTH/153/blackbird-croughton). Charlton lunch stop: the [Rose & Crown](http://theroseandcrowncharlton.co.uk/). 17 km.

KIT.name: Kintbury
KIT.privateNote:
LON Trainline says: 1h5m — Paddington via Newbury
CLJ trainline: 1h57 CLJ>VIC>PAD>NBY>KIT
KIT.adminWalksAll:
**Kintbury Circular via Combe Gibbet**: From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 3 January 2022.* 16 km. 250 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921496591)**

**Kintbury Circular Walk with Shortcut**: Ridge walk to Inkpen Common and shortened loop cutting out Hampstead Park and canal section. Sights: Inkpen Hill, Walbury Hill, and Inkpen Common. Can be muddy. Lunch in Inkpen: the [Crown and Garter](https://www.crownandgarter.co.uk/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 17 km.

**Kintbury Circular Walk**: Ridge walk to Walbury Hill, descent via Inkpen Common, villages, parkland, and canal towpath. Sights: Inkpen Hill, Walbury Hill, Inkpen Common, Hampstead Park, and Kennet and Avon Canal. Can be muddy. Lunch in Inkpen, or Hampstead Marshall. Inkpen lunch stop: the [Crown and Garter](https://www.crownandgarter.co.uk/). Hampstead Marshall lunch stop: the [White Hart](https://www.saviourwhitehart.co.uk/). A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 23 km.

**Kintbury to Bedwyn via Bedwyn Brail Roman Villa**: Quiet woods and pastures, long open ridge with panoramic views, forest walk, and canalside village. Sights: Inkpen Hill, St Mary's Great Bedwyn, Kennet and Avon Canal, and Bedwyn Brail Roman Villa. Can be muddy. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 21 km. 6.5 hours.

**Kintbury to Bedwyn via Inkpen Hill**: Quiet woods and pastures and ridge walk shortened by descending to Shalbourne valley earlier. Sights: Inkpen Hill, St Mary's Great Bedwyn, and Kennet and Avon Canal. Can be muddy. Lunch in Shalbourne: the [Plough Inn](http://www.ploughshalbourne.co.uk/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km.
KIT.publicWalksCircular: **Kintbury Circular via Combe Gibbet**: From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 16 km. 250 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921496591)**

KLY.name: Kenley
KLY.adminWalksAll:
**[Kenley to Caterham via Woldingham Garden Village](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kenley-to-caterham/)**: Chalk downland, meadows, bluebell woods, hillside paths, undulating valleys, residential alleyways, and hilltop village. Sights: [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), Sanderstead to Whyteleafe Countryside Area, King's Wood, All Saints Warlingham, Blanchman's Farm Nature Reserve, Woldingham Garden Village, Peace Centre, Madeira Walk, St Paul Woldingham, Marden Park, and Woldingham School. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Warlingham: the [Horseshoe](https://www.baronspubs.com/horseshoe), or the [White Lion](https://www.emberinns.co.uk/nationalsearch/southeast/the-white-lion-warlingham#). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**Kenley to Caterham via Riddlesdown**: A shorter variant of [Kenley to Caterham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kenley-to-caterham/). Chalk downland, meadows, bluebell woods, direct valley route through Marden Park, and hillside paths. Sights: [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), Sanderstead to Whyteleafe Countryside Area, King's Wood, All Saints Warlingham, Blanchman's Farm Nature Reserve, Marden Park, and Woldingham School. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Warlingham: the [Horseshoe](https://www.baronspubs.com/horseshoe), or the [White Lion](https://www.emberinns.co.uk/nationalsearch/southeast/the-white-lion-warlingham#). 12 km. 3 hours.
KLY.publicWalksS2S: **[Kenley to Caterham via Woldingham Garden Village](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kenley-to-caterham/)**: Chalk downland, meadows, bluebell woods, hillside paths, undulating valleys, residential alleyways, and hilltop village. Sights: [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), Sanderstead to Whyteleafe Countryside Area, King's Wood, All Saints Warlingham, Blanchman's Farm Nature Reserve, Woldingham Garden Village, Peace Centre, Madeira Walk, St Paul Woldingham, Marden Park, and Woldingham School. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Warlingham: the [Horseshoe](https://www.baronspubs.com/horseshoe), or the [White Lion](https://www.emberinns.co.uk/nationalsearch/southeast/the-white-lion-warlingham#). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

KND.name: Kingswood
KND.adminWalksAll:
**Kingswood Circular**: An alternative variant of [Chipstead to Kingswood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chipstead-to-kingswood/). Golf course fairways, open farmland, quiet country lanes, long strip woodland, and semi-open downland. Sights: Well House Inn, St Margaret's Well, Shabden Park Estate, Legal & General House, and Kingswood Arms. Can be muddy. Lunch in Mugswell: the [Well House Inn](https://thewellhouseinn.co.uk). 10 km. 2.5 hours.

**Kingswood to Chipstead**: An alternative variant of [Chipstead to Kingswood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chipstead-to-kingswood/). Golf course fairways, open farmland, quiet country lanes, long strip woodland, and open chalk downs. Sights: Well House Inn, St Margaret's Well, Shabden Park Estate, Chipstead Downs SSSI, and Ramblers Rest. Can be muddy. Lunch in Mugswell: the [Well House Inn](https://thewellhouseinn.co.uk). 8 km. 2 hours.

**[Chipstead to Kingswood via Stagbury Downs](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chipstead-to-kingswood/)**: Ancient woodland, open chalk downs, open farmland, golf course fairways, quiet country lanes, and semi-open downland. Sights: [Banstead Woods Nature Trail](https://www.reigatesociety.org.uk/Walks/BansteadWood_NatureTrail_Online.pdf), Narnia sculptures, Chipstead Downs SSSI, Stagbury Downs, Shabden Park Estate, Old School House, Well House Inn, St Margaret's Well, Legal & General House, and Kingswood Arms. Can be muddy. Lunch in Mugswell: the [Well House Inn](https://thewellhouseinn.co.uk). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Chipstead to Kingswood via Banstead Woods Nature Trail**: A shorter variant of [Chipstead to Kingswood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chipstead-to-kingswood/). Ancient woodland, open chalk downs, open farmland, long strip woodland, and semi-open downland. Sights: [Banstead Woods Nature Trail](https://www.reigatesociety.org.uk/Walks/BansteadWood_NatureTrail_Online.pdf), Narnia sculptures, Chipstead Downs SSSI, Shabden Park Estate, Legal & General House, and Kingswood Arms. Can be muddy. Lunch in Kingswood: the [Kingswood Arms](https://www.kingswoodarms.co.uk). 11 km. 3 hours.

**Riddlesdown to Kingswood**: Ancient commons, open grazing land, bluebell woods, chalk downland, and extended after-lunch route to Kingswood. Sights: Chaldon Church, Farthing Down, Kenley Common, Coulsdon Common, and Happy Valley. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in Coulsdon Common, or Kenley Common. Coulsdon Common lunch stop: the Fox. Kenley Common lunch stop: the [Wattenden Arms](http://www.thewattendenarmskenley.co.uk/). A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse).
KND.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Chipstead to Kingswood via Stagbury Downs](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chipstead-to-kingswood/)**: Ancient woodland, open chalk downs, open farmland, golf course fairways, quiet country lanes, and semi-open downland. Sights: [Banstead Woods Nature Trail](https://www.reigatesociety.org.uk/Walks/BansteadWood_NatureTrail_Online.pdf), Narnia sculptures, Chipstead Downs SSSI, Stagbury Downs, Shabden Park Estate, Old School House, Well House Inn, St Margaret's Well, Legal & General House, and Kingswood Arms. Can be muddy. Lunch in Mugswell: the [Well House Inn](https://thewellhouseinn.co.uk). 16 km. 4 hours.

KNG.name: Kingston
KNG.adminWalksAll:
**Kingston to Hampton Court**: A longer variant of [Wimbledon to Kingston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-kingston/). Thames riverside path, deer park, and formal palace gardens. Sights: Hampton Court Palace and Bushy Park.

**[Wimbledon to Kingston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-kingston/)**: Common heathland, woodland, royal deer park, formal gardens, Thames riverside path, and open grassland. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](http://www.cannizaropark.org.uk/index.htm), [Cannizaro House](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cannizaro_Park), [Richmond Park](http://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond_park/), [Southside House](http://www.southsidehouse.com/), [Caesar's Camp](http://www.themodernantiquarian.com/site/2393/caesars_camp_wimbledon.html), Isabella Plantation, Pembroke Lodge, King Henry VIII Mound, Ham House, Teddington Lock, and Hampton Court Palace. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Wimbledon Common, Richmond Park, or Richmond Hill. Wimbledon Common lunch stop: the Fox and Grapes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Pembroke Lodge. Richmond Hill lunch stop: the Roebuck. 18 km.

**West Byfleet to Kingston**: A longer variant of [West Byfleet to Hampton Court](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/west-byfleet-to-hampton-court/). Flat canal towpath, Thames riverside path, deer park, and town centre. Sights: [Coxes Lock](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coxes_Lock), [D'Oyly Carte Island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/D%27Oyly_Carte_Island), [Hampton Court Palace](https://www.hrp.org.uk/hampton-court-palace), Hampton Court Park, and Desborough Island. Lunch in West Byfleet, New Haw Lock, Weybridge, D'Oyly Carte Island, Walton, Between Walton and West Molesey, or West Molesey. West Byfleet lunch stop: the Station. New Haw Lock lunch stop: the White Hart. Weybridge lunch stop: the Old Crown. D'Oyly Carte Island lunch stop: the [D'Oyle's cafe](https://www.doylycarteisland.com/doylys). Walton lunch stops: the Anglers, or the Swan. Between Walton and West Molesey lunch stop: the Weir. West Molesey lunch stops: the [Miss Polly cafe](https://www.misspollycafe.co.uk/miss-polly-riverside), or the [Eight on the River](https://www.eightontheriver.com/). 21 km.
KNG.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Wimbledon to Kingston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-kingston/)**: Common heathland, woodland, royal deer park, formal gardens, Thames riverside path, and open grassland. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](http://www.cannizaropark.org.uk/index.htm), [Cannizaro House](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cannizaro_Park), [Richmond Park](http://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond_park/), [Southside House](http://www.southsidehouse.com/), [Caesar's Camp](http://www.themodernantiquarian.com/site/2393/caesars_camp_wimbledon.html), Isabella Plantation, Pembroke Lodge, King Henry VIII Mound, Ham House, Teddington Lock, and Hampton Court Palace. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Wimbledon Common, Richmond Park, or Richmond Hill. Wimbledon Common lunch stop: the Fox and Grapes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Pembroke Lodge. Richmond Hill lunch stop: the Roebuck. 18 km.

KNW.name: Kenilworth
KNW.adminWalksAll: **[Kenilworth Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-852)**: Historic market town, farmland, open countryside, woodland, brooks, and bridleways. Sights: [Abbey Fields](https://www.friendsofabbeyfields.org.uk), [Kenilworth Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk), [WW2 Anti Aircraft Battery (Bannerhill Battery, Goodrest Farm)](https://www.kenilworth-ww2.co.uk), and [Faerie Tale Farm](https://www.faerietalealpacas.co.uk). Can be muddy. Lunch in Kenilworth: the [Virgins & Castle](https://www.virginsandcastle.com), the Cross, or the [Pomeroy's](https://www.pomeroysbistro.co.uk). 11 km. 3.5 hours.
KNW.publicWalksCircular: **[Kenilworth Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-852)**: Historic market town, farmland, open countryside, woodland, brooks, and bridleways. Sights: [Abbey Fields](https://www.friendsofabbeyfields.org.uk), [Kenilworth Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk), [WW2 Anti Aircraft Battery (Bannerhill Battery, Goodrest Farm)](https://www.kenilworth-ww2.co.uk), and [Faerie Tale Farm](https://www.faerietalealpacas.co.uk). Can be muddy. Lunch in Kenilworth: the [Virgins & Castle](https://www.virginsandcastle.com), the Cross, or the [Pomeroy's](https://www.pomeroysbistro.co.uk). 11 km. 3.5 hours.

KSW.name: Kirkby Stephen
KSW.adminWalksAll:
**[Kirkby Stephen Circular via Eden River gorge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/nine-standards/)**: Upland fell, exposed moorland, peat bog, limestone country with shake holes and swallow holes, river gorge, narrow woodland path, scenic valley, heathery commons, and pastures. Sights: Nine Standards, Eden River gorge, Ladthwaite Beck waterfall, Dukerdale, and Podgill Viaduct. Can be boggy. 18 km. 5 hours.

**Kirkby Stephen Circular via Nine Standards**: An alternative variant of [Nine Standards](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/nine-standards/). Upland fell, lanes and paths into town, and historic market town. Sights: Nine Standards. Can be boggy. 20 km.

**Kirkby Stephen to Garsdale**: A longer variant of [Nine Standards](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/nine-standards/). High exposed upland watershed, moorland, open fells, source of Eden River, and good track above Mallerstang Valley. Sights: Nine Standards, High Seat, Hugh Seat, and Hell Gill Bridge. Exposed high ground. 29 km. 8 hours.

**[Garsdale to Kirkby Stephen](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wild-boar-fell/)**: Remote moorland ridge, lonely dales, upland fells, scarp edges, river pastures, commons, and bucolic valley farmland. Sights: Pendragon Castle, Wild Boar Fell, Swarth Fell, and Mallerstang Edge. Can be boggy. 23 km. 6 hours.
KSW.publicWalksCircular: **[Kirkby Stephen Circular via Eden River gorge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/nine-standards/)**: Upland fell, exposed moorland, peat bog, limestone country with shake holes and swallow holes, river gorge, narrow woodland path, scenic valley, heathery commons, and pastures. Sights: Nine Standards, Eden River gorge, Ladthwaite Beck waterfall, Dukerdale, and Podgill Viaduct. Can be boggy. 18 km. 5 hours.
KSW.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Garsdale to Kirkby Stephen](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wild-boar-fell/)**: Remote moorland ridge, lonely dales, upland fells, scarp edges, river pastures, commons, and bucolic valley farmland. Sights: Pendragon Castle, Wild Boar Fell, Swarth Fell, and Mallerstang Edge. Can be boggy. 23 km. 6 hours.

KWB.name: Kew Bridge
KWB.adminWalksAll:
**Ealing Broadway to Kew Bridge**: A shorter variant of [Ealing Broadway Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ealing-broadway-circular/). Canal towpath, river Thames towpath, urban parks, and suburban streets ending at Kew Bridge Station. Sights: Pitshanger Manor, Grand Union Canal, Boston Manor, Syon House, Richmond Old Deer Park, Kew Gardens, and Kew Bridge Steam Museum. Lunch in Syon Park, or Isleworth. Syon Park lunch stop: the Syon Park Refectory. Isleworth lunch stop: the London Apprentice.

**Wimbledon to Kew Bridge**: An alternative variant of [Wimbledon to Kingston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-kingston/). Common heathland, woodland, royal deer park, and Thames riverside path northward to Kew. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](http://www.cannizaropark.org.uk/index.htm), [Richmond Park](http://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond_park/), Isabella Plantation, and Pembroke Lodge. Lunch in Wimbledon Common, or Richmond Park. Wimbledon Common lunch stop: the Fox and Grapes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Pembroke Lodge.
