Common heathland, woodland, royal deer park, and Thames riverside path northward to Kew. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](http://www.cannizaropark.org.uk/index.htm), [Richmond Park](http://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond_park/), Isabella Plantation, and Pembroke Lodge. Lunch in Wimbledon Common, or Richmond Park. Wimbledon Common lunch stop: the Fox and Grapes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Pembroke Lodge.

KWG.name: Kew Gardens
KWG.adminWalksAll: **Ealing Broadway to Kew Gardens**: A shorter variant of [Ealing Broadway Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ealing-broadway-circular/). Canal towpath, river Thames towpath, urban parks, and suburban streets ending at Kew Gardens station. Sights: Pitshanger Manor, Grand Union Canal, Boston Manor, Syon House, Richmond Old Deer Park, and Kew Gardens. Lunch in Syon Park, or Isleworth. Syon Park lunch stop: the Syon Park Refectory. Sights: Pitshanger Manor, Grand Union Canal, Boston Manor, Syon House, Richmond Old Deer Park, and Kew Gardens. Lunch in Syon Park, or Isleworth. Syon Park lunch stop: the Syon Park Refectory. Isleworth lunch stop: the London Apprentice.

LAC.name: Lancing
LAC.adminWalksAll: **Amberley to Lancing**: A shorter variant of [Amberley to Shoreham By Sea or Lancing](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/amberley-to-shoreham-by-sea-or-lancing/). Rambler favourite! Open chalk ridge, South Downs escarpment, wide well-marked paths, exposed hilltops, and suburban road walking into Lancing. Sights: [Chanctonbury Ring](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chanctonbury_Ring), Bramber Castle, and Lancing College. Very exposed in wind. Lunch in Washington: the [Frankland Arms](https://thefranklandarms.com/). 22 km.
LAC.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Amberley to Lancing**: A shorter variant of [Amberley to Shoreham By Sea or Lancing](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/amberley-to-shoreham-by-sea-or-lancing/). Rambler favourite! Open chalk ridge, South Downs escarpment, wide well-marked paths, exposed hilltops, and suburban road walking into Lancing. Sights: [Chanctonbury Ring](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chanctonbury_Ring), Bramber Castle, and Lancing College. Very exposed in wind. Lunch in Washington: the [Frankland Arms](https://thefranklandarms.com/). 22 km.

LAI.name: Laindon
LAI.adminWalksAll: **[Laindon Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/laindon-circular/)**: Hilly Essex countryside, ancient and modern woods, flower-rich meadows, farmland, panoramic Thames Estuary views, and conservation village. Sights: Langdon Hills Country Park, Langdon Nature Reserve, One Tree Hill, Horndon-on-the-Hill conservation area, and [Langdon Visitor Centre](http://www.essexwt.org.uk/reserves/langdon). Can be muddy. Lunch in Horndon-on-the-Hill: the [Bell Inn](http://www.bell-inn.co.uk/), or the [Swan](https://www.theswanpubhorndon.co.uk/). 20 km. 5 hours.
LAI.publicWalksCircular: **[Laindon Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/laindon-circular/)**: Hilly Essex countryside, ancient and modern woods, flower-rich meadows, farmland, panoramic Thames Estuary views, and conservation village. Sights: Langdon Hills Country Park, Langdon Nature Reserve, One Tree Hill, Horndon-on-the-Hill conservation area, and [Langdon Visitor Centre](http://www.essexwt.org.uk/reserves/langdon). Can be muddy. Lunch in Horndon-on-the-Hill: the [Bell Inn](http://www.bell-inn.co.uk/), or the [Swan](https://www.theswanpubhorndon.co.uk/). 20 km. 5 hours.

LBG.name: London Bridge
LBG.adminWalksAll: **London Bridge to Cutty Sark for Maritime Greenwich**: A shorter variant of [Westminster to Greenwich via the south bank](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-bridge-to-greenwich-via-the-south-bank/). Flat riverside pavement, converted Victorian wharves, residential riverside developments, industrial heritage, and pocket parks. Sights: [HMS Belfast](http://www.iwm.org.uk/visits/hms-belfast), City Hall, [Tower Bridge](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tower_Bridge), Design Museum, [Brunel Museum](http://www.brunel-museum.org.uk/), Surrey Docks Farm, Statue of Peter the Great, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, [Cutty Sark](http://www.rmg.co.uk/cuttysark), [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), and Royal Observatory. Lunch in Bermondsey, or Rotherhithe. Bermondsey lunch stop: the Angel Pub. Rotherhithe lunch stops: the [Mayflower pub](http://www.mayflowerpub.co.uk/), or the Salt Quay Pub.
LBG.publicWalksS2S: **London Bridge to Cutty Sark for Maritime Greenwich**: A shorter variant of [Westminster to Greenwich via the south bank](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-bridge-to-greenwich-via-the-south-bank/). Flat riverside pavement, converted Victorian wharves, residential riverside developments, industrial heritage, and pocket parks. Sights: [HMS Belfast](http://www.iwm.org.uk/visits/hms-belfast), City Hall, [Tower Bridge](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tower_Bridge), Design Museum, [Brunel Museum](http://www.brunel-museum.org.uk/), Surrey Docks Farm, Statue of Peter the Great, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, [Cutty Sark](http://www.rmg.co.uk/cuttysark), [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), and Royal Observatory. Lunch in Bermondsey, or Rotherhithe. Bermondsey lunch stop: the Angel Pub. Rotherhithe lunch stops: the [Mayflower pub](http://www.mayflowerpub.co.uk/), or the Salt Quay Pub.

LBO.name: Loughborough
LBO.adminWalksAll: **[Loughborough Circular via Walton on the Wolds](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Canal, footpaths, and roadside footpath. Sights: Grand Union Canal, Cotes Bridge, and Walton on the Wolds. Last section on busy road. 15 km. 70 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/409%20Lboro%20Circular.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925787180)**
LBO.publicWalksCircular: **[Loughborough Circular via Walton on the Wolds](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Canal, footpaths, and roadside footpath. Sights: Grand Union Canal, Cotes Bridge, and Walton on the Wolds. Last section on busy road. 15 km. 70 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/409%20Lboro%20Circular.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925787180)**

LBZ.name: Leighton Buzzard
LBZ.adminWalksAll:
**Bow Brickhill to Leighton Buzzard**: Woodland paths, pine and beech woods, greensand ridge with valley views, riverside meadows, and canal towpath. Sights: Back Wood, Duncombe Wood, Rushmere Country Park, Greensand Ridge Walk, River Ouzel, Grand Union Canal, and Leighton Lock. Can be muddy. Best avoided in winter. Lunch in Great Brickhill, or Rushmere Country Park. Great Brickhill lunch stop: the Old Red Lion. Rushmere Country Park lunch stop: the Tree Tops Cafe. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 4 hours.

**[Cheddington to Leighton Buzzard](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cheddington-to-leighton-buzzard/)**: Rolling fields, wooded areas, hilltop villages, panoramic views of the Chilterns and Aylesbury Vale, and level descent into Linslade. Sights: All Saints Church, Wing. Arable field crossings. Lunch in Wingrave: the [Rose & Crown](https://roseandcrownwingrave.net/). 24 km. 5.5 hours.
LBZ.publicWalksS2SEnding:
**Bow Brickhill to Leighton Buzzard**: Woodland paths, pine and beech woods, greensand ridge with valley views, riverside meadows, and canal towpath. Sights: Back Wood, Duncombe Wood, Rushmere Country Park, Greensand Ridge Walk, River Ouzel, Grand Union Canal, and Leighton Lock. Can be muddy. Best avoided in winter. Lunch in Great Brickhill, or Rushmere Country Park. Great Brickhill lunch stop: the Old Red Lion. Rushmere Country Park lunch stop: the Tree Tops Cafe. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 4 hours.

**[Cheddington to Leighton Buzzard](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cheddington-to-leighton-buzzard/)**: Rolling fields, wooded areas, hilltop villages, panoramic views of the Chilterns and Aylesbury Vale, and level descent into Linslade. Sights: All Saints Church, Wing. Arable field crossings. Lunch in Wingrave: the [Rose & Crown](https://roseandcrownwingrave.net/). 24 km. 5.5 hours.

LEA.name: Leagrave
LEA.adminWalksAll: **[Leagrave to Harlington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leagrave-to-harlington/)**: Meadows, solitary chalk hills, ancient woodlands, steep chalk escarpments, chalk downland, Neolithic hill forts, ancient trackways, rolling grassy fields, and hilltop village. Sights: Barton Hills, Sharpenhoe Clappers, Sharpenhoe Hill Fort, Warden Hill, Galley Hill, Icknield Way, and [Millhouse Coffee Shop at The Olde Watermill Dickensian Village](http://www.oldewatermill.co.uk/). Steep chalk escarpments. Lunch in Hexton: the [Raven of Hexton](https://www.theravenhexton.com/). 23 km. 5 hours.
LEA.publicWalksS2S: **[Leagrave to Harlington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leagrave-to-harlington/)**: Meadows, solitary chalk hills, ancient woodlands, steep chalk escarpments, chalk downland, Neolithic hill forts, ancient trackways, rolling grassy fields, and hilltop village. Sights: Barton Hills, Sharpenhoe Clappers, Sharpenhoe Hill Fort, Warden Hill, Galley Hill, Icknield Way, and [Millhouse Coffee Shop at The Olde Watermill Dickensian Village](http://www.oldewatermill.co.uk/). Steep chalk escarpments. Lunch in Hexton: the [Raven of Hexton](https://www.theravenhexton.com/). 23 km. 5 hours.

LEI.name: Leicester
LEI.adminWalksAll: **[Barrow upon Soar to Leicester](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Flat and on decent paths. Sights: Cossington Lock, Watermead Country Park, National Space Centre, and Abbey Park. 19 km. 40 m uphill. 5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/103%20Barrow%20on%20Soar%20to%20Leicester.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925759790)**
LEI.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Barrow upon Soar to Leicester](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Flat and on decent paths. Sights: Cossington Lock, Watermead Country Park, National Space Centre, and Abbey Park. 19 km. 40 m uphill. 5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/103%20Barrow%20on%20Soar%20to%20Leicester.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925759790)**

LEN.name: Lenham
LEN.adminWalksAll:
**[Lenham to Charing via Stalisfield Green](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lenham-to-charing/)**: North Downs footpaths, little-used field paths, rape seed fields, and woods. Sights: Lenham Cross, Stalisfield Green, and Charing village. Can be overgrown. Lunch in Warren Street, Lenham, or Stalisfield Green. Warren Street, Lenham lunch stop: the [Harrow Hill Hotel & Inn](http://www.harrowhillhotel.com/). Stalisfield Green lunch stop: the [Plough](http://www.theploughinnstalisfield.co.uk/). 13 km. 4.5 hours.

**Lenham to Hollingbourne**: A longer variant of [Lenham to Charing](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lenham-to-charing/). North Downs footpaths, little-used field paths, rape seed fields, and woods. Sights: Lenham Cross and Stalisfield Green. Can be overgrown. Lunch in Warren Street, Lenham, Wormshill, Hollingbourne, or Harrietsham. Warren Street, Lenham lunch stop: the [Harrow Hill Hotel & Inn](http://www.harrowhillhotel.com/). Wormshill lunch stop: the [Blacksmiths Arms](https://whatpub.com/pubs/SWA/4470/blacksmiths-arms-wormshill). Hollingbourne lunch stop: the [Dirty Habit](https://elitepubs.com/the-dirty-habit/). Harrietsham lunch stop: the [Ringlestone Inn](https://www.theringlestone.co.uk/). 14 km. 4.5 hours.

**Lenham to Charing via Lenham Cross**: A shorter variant of [Lenham to Charing](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lenham-to-charing/). North Downs footpaths, little-used field paths, and woods. Sights: Lenham Cross. Can be overgrown. Lunch in Warren Street, Lenham, or Stalisfield Green. Warren Street, Lenham lunch stop: the [Harrow Hill Hotel & Inn](http://www.harrowhillhotel.com/). Stalisfield Green lunch stop: the [Plough](http://www.theploughinnstalisfield.co.uk/). 9 km. 3 hours.
LEN.publicWalksS2S: **[Lenham to Charing via Stalisfield Green](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lenham-to-charing/)**: North Downs footpaths, little-used field paths, rape seed fields, and woods. Sights: Lenham Cross, Stalisfield Green, and Charing village. Can be overgrown. Lunch in Warren Street, Lenham, or Stalisfield Green. Warren Street, Lenham lunch stop: the [Harrow Hill Hotel & Inn](http://www.harrowhillhotel.com/). Stalisfield Green lunch stop: the [Plough](http://www.theploughinnstalisfield.co.uk/). 13 km. 4.5 hours.

LES.name: Leigh-on-Sea
LES.adminWalksAll:
**[Leigh-on-Sea to Shoeburyness](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-on-sea-to-shoeburyness/)**: Flat coastal path, seaside promenade, harbour, mudflats, beach huts, residential streets, open grassy seafront, and army training area margins. Sights: Southend Pier. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Chalkwell, Westcliff, Southend-on-Sea, Southchurch, or Shoebury Common. Chalkwell lunch stop: the Tides cafe. Westcliff lunch stop: the Beach Hut. Southend-on-Sea lunch stop: the Oliver's cafe. Southchurch lunch stops: the Billy Hundreds, or the Ocean Beach. Shoebury Common lunch stop: the Uncle Tom's Cabin. 12 km.

**Benfleet to Leigh-on-Sea via Leigh on Sea & Hadleigh Castle**: A shorter variant of [Benfleet Circular via Leigh on Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/benfleet-circular-via-leigh-on-sea/). Hilly woodland, narrow grassy paths, Hadleigh Country Park, and estuary views. Sights: Hadleigh Castle. Can be muddy. Best in May–Oct. Lunch in Leigh-on-Sea: the Crooked Billet, or the Osborne Bros. 8 km.

**Benfleet to Leigh-on-Sea via Leigh on Sea & Benfleet Creek**: An alternative variant of [Benfleet Circular via Leigh on Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/benfleet-circular-via-leigh-on-sea/). Completely flat estuary path along Benfleet Creek shoreline. Sights: Benfleet Creek. Lunch in Leigh-on-Sea: the Crooked Billet. 6 km.
LES.publicWalksS2S: **[Leigh-on-Sea to Shoeburyness](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-on-sea-to-shoeburyness/)**: Flat coastal path, seaside promenade, harbour, mudflats, beach huts, residential streets, open grassy seafront, and army training area margins. Sights: Southend Pier. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Chalkwell, Westcliff, Southend-on-Sea, Southchurch, or Shoebury Common. Chalkwell lunch stop: the Tides cafe. Westcliff lunch stop: the Beach Hut. Southend-on-Sea lunch stop: the Oliver's cafe. Southchurch lunch stops: the Billy Hundreds, or the Ocean Beach. Shoebury Common lunch stop: the Uncle Tom's Cabin. 12 km.

LET.name: Letchworth Garden City
LET.adminWalksAll: **[Arlesey to Letchworth Garden City](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/arlesey-to-letchworth-garden-city/)**: Chalk stream meadows, field boundaries, hilltop village, open farmland with far views, green lanes, lavender farm, and Garden City. Sights: Hiz River, RAF Henlow Camp, Meppershall Manor House, Meppershall motte-and-double bailey, All Saints' Church, Shillington, Pirton motte-and-double bailey, Hitchin Lavender (Cadwell Farm), and Letchworth Garden City. Lunch in Shillington, or Pirton. Shillington lunch stops: the Crown, or the Musgrave Arms. Pirton lunch stop: the [Motte & Bailey](https://motteandbaileypub.com/). 25 km. 5.5 hours.
LET.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Arlesey to Letchworth Garden City](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/arlesey-to-letchworth-garden-city/)**: Chalk stream meadows, field boundaries, hilltop village, open farmland with far views, green lanes, lavender farm, and Garden City. Sights: Hiz River, RAF Henlow Camp, Meppershall Manor House, Meppershall motte-and-double bailey, All Saints' Church, Shillington, Pirton motte-and-double bailey, Hitchin Lavender (Cadwell Farm), and Letchworth Garden City. Lunch in Shillington, or Pirton. Shillington lunch stops: the Crown, or the Musgrave Arms. Pirton lunch stop: the [Motte & Bailey](https://motteandbaileypub.com/). 25 km. 5.5 hours.

LFD.name: Lingfield
LFD.adminWalksAll:
**Lingfield Circular**: A longer variant of [Dormans to Lingfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dormans-to-lingfield/). Undulating woodland, railway cuttings and viaducts, golf course, racecourse straight, farmland, nature reserve, village conservation area, and water meadow. Sights: Cook's Pond Viaduct, [Lingfield Park Racecourse](https://www.lingfieldpark.co.uk), [Lingfield Nature Reserve](https://www.lingfieldreserves.org.uk), Ss Peter and Paul, New Place, Old Cage, Lingfield Oak, Community Orchard, and Lingfield College. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lingfield: the [Star](https://www.thestarlingfield.co.uk). 20 km. 4.5 hours.

**Lingfield to Dormans**: A longer variant of [Dormans to Lingfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dormans-to-lingfield/). Undulating woodland, railway cuttings and viaducts, golf course, racecourse straight, farmland, nature reserve, village conservation area, water meadow, and fields and copses. Sights: Cook's Pond Viaduct, [Lingfield Park Racecourse](https://www.lingfieldpark.co.uk), [Lingfield Nature Reserve](https://www.lingfieldreserves.org.uk), Ss Peter and Paul, New Place, Old Cage, Lingfield Oak, Community Orchard, [Old House at Home](https://www.oldhouseathome.co.uk), Young Epilepsy, and Lingfield College. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lingfield: the [Star](https://www.thestarlingfield.co.uk). 24 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Dormans to Lingfield via Lingfield Old Town conservation area](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dormans-to-lingfield/)**: Undulating woodland, railway cuttings and viaducts, golf course, racecourse straight, farmland, nature reserve, and village conservation area. Sights: Cook's Pond Viaduct, [Lingfield Park Racecourse](https://www.lingfieldpark.co.uk), [Lingfield Nature Reserve](https://www.lingfieldreserves.org.uk), Ss Peter and Paul, New Place, Lingfield Old Town conservation area, Old Cage, Lingfield Oak, Community Orchard, and Lingfield College. Can be muddy. Avoid August Bank Holiday. Lunch in Lingfield: the [Star](https://www.thestarlingfield.co.uk). 16 km. 4 hours.

**[Oxted to Lingfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxted-to-lingfield/)**: Meadows, wooded Greensand Ridge, gently rolling Low Weald farmland, ancient woodland, old farmsteads and manor houses, flat plain, and nature reserve. Sights: [St Peter's Church](https://www.stpeterslimpsfield.org/history-of-st-peters.html), [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), [Lingfield Nature Reserve](https://www.lingfieldreserves.org.uk), Tenchleys Manor, St George's Church, Crowhurst Place, Ss Peter and Paul, New Place, Old Cage, Community Orchard, and Lingfield Oak. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Staffhurst Wood: the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

**Dormans to Lingfield via Cook's Pond Viaduct**: A shorter variant of [Dormans to Lingfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dormans-to-lingfield/). Undulating woodland, railway cuttings and viaducts, golf course, racecourse straight, nature reserve, and village conservation area. Sights: Cook's Pond Viaduct, [Lingfield Park Racecourse](https://www.lingfieldpark.co.uk), [Lingfield Nature Reserve](https://www.lingfieldreserves.org.uk), Ss Peter and Paul, New Place, Old Cage, Lingfield Oak, and Community Orchard. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lingfield: the [Star](https://www.thestarlingfield.co.uk). 11 km. 2.5 hours.

**Hurst Green to Lingfield**: A shorter variant of [Oxted to Lingfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxted-to-lingfield/). Hillside paths, Low Weald farmland, ancient woodland, old manor houses, flat plain, and nature reserve. Sights: [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), [Lingfield Nature Reserve](https://www.lingfieldreserves.org.uk), Tenchleys Manor, St George's Church, Crowhurst Place, Ss Peter and Paul, New Place, Old Cage, Community Orchard, and Lingfield Oak. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Staffhurst Wood: the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted). 16 km. 4 hours.
LFD.publicWalksS2SEnding:
**[Dormans to Lingfield via Lingfield Old Town conservation area](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dormans-to-lingfield/)**: Undulating woodland, railway cuttings and viaducts, golf course, racecourse straight, farmland, nature reserve, and village conservation area. Sights: Cook's Pond Viaduct, [Lingfield Park Racecourse](https://www.lingfieldpark.co.uk), [Lingfield Nature Reserve](https://www.lingfieldreserves.org.uk), Ss Peter and Paul, New Place, Lingfield Old Town conservation area, Old Cage, Lingfield Oak, Community Orchard, and Lingfield College. **[Oxted to Lingfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxted-to-lingfield/)**: Meadows, wooded Greensand Ridge, gently rolling Low Weald farmland, ancient woodland, old farmsteads and manor houses, flat plain, and nature reserve. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Staffhurst Wood: the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

LGE.name: Long Eaton
LGE.adminWalksAll:
**[Long Eaton to Ilkeston via Erewash Valley](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Canal, industrial towns, and disused railway viaduct. Sights: Bennerley Viaduct, Erewash Canal, and Erewash Valley Trail. 15 km. 30 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/416%20Erewash%20Valley.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925788454)**

**[Derby to Long Eaton along waterways](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Waterways — River Derwent, Derby & Sandiacre Canal, and Erewash Canal. Sights: River Derwent, Derby & Sandiacre Canal, and Erewash Canal. 18 km. 10 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/210%20Derby%20to%20Long%20Eaton.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925784648)**

Sights: River Derwent, Derby & Sandiacre Canal, and Erewash Canal. 18 km. 10 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/210%20Derby%20to%20Long%20Eaton.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925784648)**

LGM.name: Langley Mill
LGM.adminWalksAll: **[Langley Mill Circular](https://www.visitambervalley.com/media/1114/13-langley-mill.pdf)**: 35% hard surfaced 35%. Made up level surfaces 30% fields/countryside. Flat with one significant rising section. 7 km.
LGM.publicWalksCircular: **[Langley Mill Circular](https://www.visitambervalley.com/media/1114/13-langley-mill.pdf)**: 35% hard surfaced 35%. Made up level surfaces 30% fields/countryside. Flat with one significant rising section. 7 km.

LGW.name: Langwathby
LGW.adminWalksAll:
**[Langwathby to Appleby via Little Dun Fell](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cross-fell-and-great-dun-fell/)**: Moorland ridge, high plateau, boulder-filled fell tops, upland hay meadows, disused mine scars, rough pastures, and engineered Pennine Way paths. Sights: Cross Fell, Great Dun Fell, Little Dun Fell, Knock Fell, and High Cup Nick. Exposed, boggy, foggy. Avoid winter/early spring. 33 km. 8.5 hours.

**Langwathby to Appleby via Green Castle Crag**: A variant of [Cross Fell and Great Dun Fell](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cross-fell-and-great-dun-fell/). Tarmac service track from radar station, beautiful gorge, rim of Knock Ore Gill, rough pastures, and paths to the Pennine Way. Sights: Cross Fell, Great Dun Fell, Green Castle Crag, and Knock Ore Gill. Exposed, boggy, foggy.
LGW.publicWalksS2S: **[Langwathby to Appleby via Little Dun Fell](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cross-fell-and-great-dun-fell/)**: Moorland ridge, high plateau, boulder-filled fell tops, upland hay meadows, disused mine scars, rough pastures, and engineered Pennine Way paths. Sights: Cross Fell, Great Dun Fell, Little Dun Fell, Knock Fell, and High Cup Nick. Exposed, boggy, foggy. Avoid winter/early spring. 33 km. 8.5 hours.

LHD.name: Leatherhead
LHD.adminWalksAll:
**Leatherhead to Box Hill & Westhumble**: Gentle river valley start, steep chalk climbs after lunch, open downland ridges, yew and box woodland, and stepping stones. Sights: Town Bridge, Thorncroft Manor, Norbury Park, St Michael & All Angels Church, White Hill, Juniper Top, Box Hill, Stepping Stones, Denbies Wine Estate, and Leopold Salomons Memorial. Steep descents, can be slippery. Lunch in Mickleham: the Running Horses, or the King William IV. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 11 km. 3.5 hours.

**Box Hill & Westhumble to Leatherhead**: Steep chalk hills, open downland, yew and box woodland, grassy ridges with panoramic views, gentle river valley fields, and historic parkland. Sights: Box Hill, Stepping Stones, Juniper Top, White Hill, St Michael & All Angels Church, Thorncroft Manor, Leopold Salomons Memorial, Norbury Park, Denbies Wine Estate, Box Hill & Westhumble Railway Station, Town Bridge, and King George V Memorial Gardens. Steep descents, can be slippery. Lunch in Mickleham: the Running Horses, or the King William IV. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 11 km. 3.5 hours.
LHD.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Box Hill & Westhumble to Leatherhead**: Steep chalk hills, open downland, yew and box woodland, grassy ridges with panoramic views, gentle river valley fields, and historic parkland. Sights: Box Hill, Stepping Stones, Juniper Top, White Hill, St Michael & All Angels Church, Thorncroft Manor, Leopold Salomons Memorial, Norbury Park, Denbies Wine Estate, Box Hill & Westhumble Railway Station, Town Bridge, and King George V Memorial Gardens. Steep descents, can be slippery. Lunch in Mickleham: the Running Horses, or the King William IV. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 11 km. 3.5 hours.

LID.name: Lidlington
LID.adminWalksAll: **[Lidlington to Flitwick](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lidlington-to-flitwick/)**: Undulating Greensand Ridge, mixed woods, open parkland, heathland, historic market town, ancient woodland, and flat moors and wetland nature reserve. Sights: Cooper's Hill Nature Reserve, Houghton House, Ampthill historic centre, Ailesbury Mausoleum & Crypt, Flitwick Moor, and King's Wood. Can be muddy. 22 km. 5 hours.
LID.publicWalksS2S: **[Lidlington to Flitwick](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lidlington-to-flitwick/)**: Undulating Greensand Ridge, mixed woods, open parkland, heathland, historic market town, ancient woodland, and flat moors and wetland nature reserve. Sights: Cooper's Hill Nature Reserve, Houghton House, Ampthill historic centre, Ailesbury Mausoleum & Crypt, Flitwick Moor, and King's Wood. Can be muddy. 22 km. 5 hours.

LIH.name: Leigh
LIH.privateNote: Trainline says 52m — London Bridge via Tonbridge
LIH.adminWalksAll:
**Leigh to Tunbridge Wells via Penshurst Place**: Rolling Kent hills, pastoral river valleys, parkland, woods, lakes, and commons extending into town. Sights: [Penshurst Place](http://www.penshurstplace.com/), St John the Baptist Church, Leicester Square (Penshurst), River Medway, All Souls Chapel, Poundsbridge, Pantiles, King Charles the Martyr Church, and [The Opera House](http://www.jdwetherspoon.co.uk/pub/theoperahouse). Can be muddy. Lunch in Smart's Hill, or Penshurst. Smart's Hill lunch stops: the [Spotted Dog](http://www.spotteddogpub.co.uk/), or the [Bottle House Inn](http://www.thebottlehouseinnpenshurst.co.uk/). Penshurst lunch stop: the [Leicester Arms](http://www.leicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 15 km. 220 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921069953)**

**Leigh Circular via Penshurst Place & Chiddingstone Village**: A variant of [Leigh and Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-and-penshurst/). Rambler favourite! Parkland, river valley, farmland, woods, and hamlet lanes. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Village](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/chiddingstone-village), and St John the Baptist. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hoath Corner: the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com). 16 km.

**Leigh Circular via Chiddingstone Castle**: A longer variant of [Leigh and Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-and-penshurst/). Rambler favourite! Parkland, river valley, farmland, woods, vineyard, and picturesque village streets. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Village](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/chiddingstone-village), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), St John the Baptist, and Harden Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hoath Corner: the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Leigh to Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-and-penshurst/)**: Rambler favourite! Parkland, river valley, gentle farmland slopes, vineyard, woods, and hamlet lanes. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Village](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/chiddingstone-village), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), St John the Baptist, and Harden Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hoath Corner: the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com). 13 km. 3 hours.

**Leigh to Sevenoaks**: Rolling Kentish farmland, fields, oasthouses, bluebell woods, steep greensand hillside, and deer park. Sights: St Mary's Church, Hall Place, Knole Park, Knole House, and Brewhouse Tearoom. Can be muddy. Lunch in Underriver: the White Rock. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 4.5 hours.

**Leigh to Tonbridge**: A shorter variant of [Hever to Tonbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-tonbridge/). Parkland estate, river valley, country park lakes, and riverside paths into a market town. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Tonbridge Castle](https://www.tonbridgecastle.org), St John the Baptist Church Penshurst, Leicester Square Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Straight Mile, and Powder Mills. Lunch in Penshurst: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). 12 km. 3 hours.

**Hever to Leigh via Chiddingstone Castle**: Undulating hills, open fields, beech woods, rivers, ponds, quaint villages, and historic estates. Sights: [Hever Castle](http://www.hevercastle.co.uk/), St Peter's Church, Chiddingstone Castle, St Mary's Church, Chiding Stone, [Penshurst Place](http://www.penshurstplace.com/), St John the Baptist Church, and Luke Tapestry. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chiddingstone, or Penshurst. Chiddingstone lunch stops: the [Castle Inn](http://www.thecastleinnchiddingstone.com/), or the Tulip Tree Tearooms. Penshurst lunch stop: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](http://www.leicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**Penshurst to Leigh**: An alternative variant of [Leigh and Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-and-penshurst/). Rambler favourite! Low-lying Eden valley farmland, gentle ridges, woods, vineyard, and hamlet lanes. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Village](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/chiddingstone-village), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), and Harden Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hoath Corner: the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**Hever to Leigh via Hever Castle**: Undulating hills, open fields, beech woods, rivers, ponds, and historic estates. Sights: [Hever Castle](http://www.hevercastle.co.uk/), St Peter's Church, [Penshurst Place](http://www.penshurstplace.com/), and St John the Baptist Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](http://www.leicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 12 km.

**Tunbridge Wells to Leigh**: An alternative variant of [Tunbridge Wells to Hildenborough](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-to-hildenborough/). Town commons, woodland tracks, wild flower meadows, country park, river and lake paths, and quiet roads. Sights: Haysden Country Park, Brookhurst Field, St Lawrence Church, Wealdway, and Eden Valley Walk. Can be muddy. Lunch in Bidborough: the [Kentish Hare](http://www.thekentishhare.com/). 14 km.
LIH.publicWalksS2S: **Leigh to Tunbridge Wells via Penshurst Place**: Rolling Kent hills, pastoral river valleys, parkland, woods, lakes, and commons extending into town. Sights: [Penshurst Place](http://www.penshurstplace.com/), St John the Baptist Church, Leicester Square (Penshurst), River Medway, All Souls Chapel, Poundsbridge, Pantiles, King Charles the Martyr Church, and [The Opera House](http://www.jdwetherspoon.co.uk/pub/theoperahouse). Can be muddy. Lunch in Smart's Hill, or Penshurst. Smart's Hill lunch stops: the [Spotted Dog](http://www.spotteddogpub.co.uk/), or the [Bottle House Inn](http://www.thebottlehouseinnpenshurst.co.uk/). Penshurst lunch stop: the [Leicester Arms](http://www.leicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 15 km. 220 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921069953)**

LIP.name: Liphook
LIP.adminWalksAll: **Liphook to Haslemere via Fernhurst**: Mixed oak, beech and chestnut woods, bridleways, streams, open countryside, horse paddocks, and a final climb into a market town. Sights: Shulbrede Priory, Haslemere Museum, Valewood Park, and Black Down. Can be muddy. Lunch in Fernhurst: the Red Lion, or the Fern (Cafe) at the Pavilion. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 290 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920301093)**
LIP.publicWalksS2S: **Liphook to Haslemere via Fernhurst**: Mixed oak, beech and chestnut woods, bridleways, streams, open countryside, horse paddocks, and a final climb into a market town. Sights: Shulbrede Priory, Haslemere Museum, Valewood Park, and Black Down. Can be muddy. Lunch in Fernhurst: the Red Lion, or the Fern (Cafe) at the Pavilion. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 290 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920301093)**

LIS.name: Liss
LIS.adminWalksAll:
**Petersfield to Liss via Edward Thomas Memorial Stone**: Rolling Hampshire countryside, wooded hangers, steep hillside ascent, unspoilt farmland, and quiet villages. Sights: Shoulder of Mutton Hill, Edward Thomas Memorial Stone, All Saints' Church, Steep, Petersfield Market Square, and Equestrian Statue of King William III. Steep ascent, slippery winter. Lunch in Hawkley: the [Hawkley Inn](http://hawkleyinn.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km. 5 hours.

**Petersfield to Liss via Shoulder of Mutton Hill**: Rolling Hampshire countryside, wooded hangers, and shortcut via Pococks Lane and Upland Lane. Sights: Shoulder of Mutton Hill. Steep ascent, slippery winter. Lunch in Hawkley: the [Hawkley Inn](http://hawkleyinn.co.uk/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 11 km.
LIS.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Petersfield to Liss via Edward Thomas Memorial Stone**: Rolling Hampshire countryside, wooded hangers, steep hillside ascent, unspoilt farmland, and quiet villages. Sights: Shoulder of Mutton Hill, Edward Thomas Memorial Stone, All Saints' Church, Steep, Petersfield Market Square, and Equestrian Statue of King William III. Steep ascent, slippery winter. Lunch in Hawkley: the [Hawkley Inn](http://hawkleyinn.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km. 5 hours.

LIT.name: Littlehampton
LIT.adminWalksAll:
**[Littlehampton to Bognor Regis](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/littlehampton-to-bognor-regis/)**: Coastal seafront promenades, shingle beaches, sand dunes, sea wall paths, quiet seafront homes, and south coast seaside resorts. Sights: [West Beach Cafe](https://www.instagram.com/west.beachcafe/), Climping Beach, Pagham Harbour, and Bognor Regis Pier. Check tide timings. Lunch in Littlehampton, Littlehampton West Beach, Felpham, or Bognor Regis. Littlehampton lunch stops: the Steam Packet, or the Arun View Inn. Littlehampton West Beach lunch stop: the [West Beach Cafe](https://www.instagram.com/west.beachcafe/). Felpham lunch stops: the Boat House Cafe, or the [Lobster Pot](http://www.lobsterpotfelpham.com/). Bognor Regis lunch stop: the Regis (Brewers Fayre). 12 km.

**[Angmering to Littlehampton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/angmering-to-littlehampton/)**: Open downland, coastal plain, quiet alleyways, farmland, residential streets, shingle beach, seafront promenade, and riverside walk. Sights: [Highdown Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Highdown_Hill), [Highdown Gardens](https://highdowngardens.co.uk), [Littlehampton](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Littlehampton), Iron Age hill fort, Miller's Tomb, Ecclesden Mill, Rampion Wind Farm, St Margaret, Angmering, St Mary the Virgin, East Preston, England Coast Path, Rustington Convalescent Home, Lighthouse, River Arun, Millennium Clock Tower, [East Beach Café](https://eastbeachcafe.co.uk), and [Harbour Lights Café](https://www.harbourlightscafe.co.uk). Lunch in Ferring, Highdown Hill, or Angmering. Ferring lunch stop: the [Henty Arms](https://www.hentyarms.com). Highdown Hill lunch stop: the [Highdown](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/highdown). Angmering lunch stops: the [Spotted Cow](https://www.spottedcowangmering.co.uk/index), or the [Lamb at Angmering](https://www.thelamb-angmering.com). 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Shoreham-by-Sea to Littlehampton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shoreham-by-sea-to-littlehampton/)**: Flat coastal promenade, shingle beach, sandy beach, grassy seafront paths, and seaside resorts. Sights: Shoreham Fort, Worthing Pier, Widewater Lagoon Nature Reserve, [Perch on the Pier](https://perchonthepier.com/), [Sea Lane Cafe](http://www.sealanecafe.co.uk/), [Blue Bird Cafe](https://thebluebirdcafeferring.co.uk/), and [East Beach Cafe](http://www.eastbeachcafe.co.uk/). Check tide timings. Lunch in Lancing, Worthing, Goring, Ferring, or Littlehampton. Lancing lunch stop: the Perch at Lancing. Worthing lunch stops: the Coast Cafe, or the [Perch on the Pier](https://perchonthepier.com/). Goring lunch stop: the [Sea Lane Cafe](http://www.sealanecafe.co.uk/). Ferring lunch stop: the [Blue Bird Cafe](https://thebluebirdcafeferring.co.uk/). Littlehampton lunch stop: the [East Beach Cafe](http://www.eastbeachcafe.co.uk/). 22 km.

**Goring-by-Sea to Littlehampton via Highdown Gardens**: A shorter variant of [Angmering to Littlehampton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/angmering-to-littlehampton/). Residential streets, open downland, farmland, seafront promenade, and riverside walk. Sights: [Highdown Gardens](https://highdowngardens.co.uk), [Highdown Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Highdown_Hill), Iron Age hill fort, Miller's Tomb, Rampion Wind Farm, Ecclesden Mill, England Coast Path, Rustington Convalescent Home, [East Beach Café](https://eastbeachcafe.co.uk), and [Harbour Lights Café](https://www.harbourlightscafe.co.uk). Lunch in Highdown Hill: the [Highdown](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/highdown). 12 km. 3 hours.

**Worthing to Littlehampton**: A shorter variant of [Shoreham By Sea to Littlehampton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shoreham-by-sea-to-littlehampton/). Flat coastal promenade, grassy paths, shingle and sandy beach, and seaside resort. Sights: [Sea Lane Cafe](http://www.sealanecafe.co.uk/), [Blue Bird Cafe](https://thebluebirdcafeferring.co.uk/), and [East Beach Cafe](http://www.eastbeachcafe.co.uk/). Check tide timings. Lunch in Goring, Ferring, or Littlehampton. Goring lunch stop: the [Sea Lane Cafe](http://www.sealanecafe.co.uk/). Ferring lunch stop: the [Blue Bird Cafe](https://thebluebirdcafeferring.co.uk/). Littlehampton lunch stop: the [East Beach Cafe](http://www.eastbeachcafe.co.uk/). 13 km.

**Goring-by-Sea to Littlehampton via Ilex Avenue**: An alternative variant of [Angmering to Littlehampton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/angmering-to-littlehampton/). Residential streets, tree-lined bridleway, coastal plain, open downland, shingle beach, seafront promenade, and riverside walk. Sights: Ilex Avenue, Goring Gap, St Andrew, Ferring, [Highdown Gardens](https://highdowngardens.co.uk), [Highdown Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Highdown_Hill), Iron Age hill fort, Miller's Tomb, Rampion Wind Farm, Ecclesden Mill, St Margaret, Angmering, St Mary the Virgin, East Preston, England Coast Path, Rustington Convalescent Home, Lighthouse, River Arun, [East Beach Café](https://eastbeachcafe.co.uk), and [Harbour Lights Café](https://www.harbourlightscafe.co.uk). Lunch in Highdown Hill, or Angmering. Highdown Hill lunch stop: the [Highdown](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/highdown). Angmering lunch stops: the [Spotted Cow](https://www.spottedcowangmering.co.uk/index), or the [Lamb at Angmering](https://www.thelamb-angmering.com). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

Sights: Shoreham Fort, Worthing Pier, Widewater Lagoon Nature Reserve, [Perch on the Pier](https://perchonthepier.com/), [Sea Lane Cafe](http://www.sealanecafe.co.uk/), [Blue Bird Cafe](https://thebluebirdcafeferring.co.uk/), and [East Beach Cafe](http://www.eastbeachcafe.co.uk/). LMS.name: Leamington Spa
LMS.adminWalksAll: **[Leamington Spa Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-678)**: Spa town, Grand Union Canal towpath, River Leam, local nature reserve, country park, open fields, and village paths. Sights: [Leam Valley Nature Reserve](https://www.warwickshirewildlifetrust.org.uk/LeamValley), [Newbold Comyn](https://www.royal-leamington-spa.co.uk/visiting/parks-gardens/newbold-comyn/), [Jephson Gardens](https://www.warwickdc.gov.uk/info/20312/jephson_gardens), and [Royal Pump Rooms](https://www.warwickdc.gov.uk/royalpumprooms). Can be muddy. Lunch in Offchurch, or Leamington Spa. Offchurch lunch stop: the [Stag at Offchurch](https://www.thestagatoffchurch.com). Leamington Spa lunch stop: the [Cenu Cacao](https://www.cenucacao.co.uk). 11 km. 4 hours.

Sights: [Leam Valley Nature Reserve](https://www.warwickshirewildlifetrust.org.uk/LeamValley), [Newbold Comyn](https://www.royal-leamington-spa.co.uk/visiting/parks-gardens/newbold-comyn/), [Jephson Gardens](https://www.warwickdc.gov.uk/info/20312/jephson_gardens), and [Royal Pump Rooms](https://www.warwickdc.gov.uk/royalpumprooms). LNY.name: Langley
LNY.adminWalksAll:
**Langley to Burnham station**: A shorter variant of [West Drayton to Cookham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/west-drayton-to-cookham/). Country parks, lowland heaths, mature beech woodland, and farmland lanes. Sights: [Langley Park County Park](http://www.buckscc.gov.uk/leisure-and-culture/country-parks/langley-park/), Black Park County Park, and [Burnham Beeches](http://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/burnham-beeches-and-stoke-common/Pages/default.aspx). Lunch in Fulmer, Farnham Common, or Burnham Beeches. Fulmer lunch stop: the [Black Horse](http://www.theblackhorsefulmer.co.uk/). Farnham Common lunch stop: the [Forresters](http://www.theforesterspub.com/). Burnham Beeches lunch stop: the Beeches Cafe. 18 km.

**Langley to Cookham**: A shorter variant of [West Drayton to Cookham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/west-drayton-to-cookham/). Lowland heaths, mature beech woodland, country parks, commons, and historic riverside village. Sights: [Langley Park County Park](http://www.buckscc.gov.uk/leisure-and-culture/country-parks/langley-park/), Black Park County Park, [Burnham Beeches](http://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/burnham-beeches-and-stoke-common/Pages/default.aspx), Lord Boston's Victorian Folly, and River Thames. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in Fulmer, Farnham Common, Burnham Beeches, Littleworth Common, or Cookham. Fulmer lunch stop: the [Black Horse](http://www.theblackhorsefulmer.co.uk/). Farnham Common lunch stop: the [Forresters](http://www.theforesterspub.com/). Burnham Beeches lunch stop: the Beeches Cafe. Littleworth Common lunch stop: the Blackwood Arms. Cookham lunch stops: the Infusions, the Kings Arms, or the Bel and the Dragon. 22 km.

LOW.name: Lowdham
LOW.adminWalksAll: **[Lowdham to Burton Joyce](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Countryside and river. Sights: River Trent, Gonalston, Caythorpe, and Gunthorpe. Lunch in Burton Joyce: the pub. 14 km. 30 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/417%20Lowdham%20to%20Burton%20Joyce.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925788637)**
