LPW.name: Lapworth
LPW.adminWalksAll: **[Lapworth Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-854)**: Rural fields, canal towpath, flight of locks, Kingswood Arm, country lanes, and Tudor mansion grounds. Sights: Church of St. Mary the Virgin, [Packwood House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/packwood-house), [Kingswood Arm](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), and [Baddesley Clinton](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/baddesley-clinton). Can be very muddy. Lunch in Lapworth: the [Punch Bowl](https://www.punchbowllapworth.co.uk), the [Navigation Inn](https://www.navigationlapworth.co.uk), or the [Boot](https://www.bootinnlapworth.co.uk). 6 km. 2 hours.
LPW.publicWalksCircular: **[Lapworth Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-854)**: Rural fields, canal towpath, flight of locks, Kingswood Arm, country lanes, and Tudor mansion grounds. Sights: Church of St. Mary the Virgin, [Packwood House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/packwood-house), [Kingswood Arm](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), and [Baddesley Clinton](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/baddesley-clinton). Can be very muddy. Lunch in Lapworth: the [Punch Bowl](https://www.punchbowllapworth.co.uk), the [Navigation Inn](https://www.navigationlapworth.co.uk), or the [Boot](https://www.bootinnlapworth.co.uk). 6 km. 2 hours.

LST.name: Liverpool Street
LST.adminWalksAll:
**Liverpool Street to Moorgate**: A longer variant of [The London Stations](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-stations/). Flat urban streets, shopping arcade, and City plazas. Sights: Broadgate Shopping Centre, Broadgate Circle, and Moorgate station. 0 km.

**[Kings Cross to Liverpool Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-stations/)**: Entirely flat, urban city-centre streets, royal parks, riverside Thames path, canal towpath, and historic financial district. Sights: Kings Cross station, St Pancras station, Kings Cross Central, Granary Square, Crick Institute, Euston station, Regent's Park, Queen Mary's Gardens, Regent's Park Open Air Theatre, Marylebone station, Great Central Hotel, Paddington Basin, Paddington station, Elizabeth Line station, Kensington Gardens, Italian Gardens, Serpentine, Serpentine Lido, Princess Diana Memorial, Hyde Park, Kensington Palace, Buckingham Palace, Queen's Gallery, St James's Park, Horseguard's Parade, Banqueting House, Trafalgar Square, Nelson's Column, Charing Cross station, Hungerford Bridge, Golden Jubilee Bridges, London Eye, Royal Festival Hall, National Theatre, Oxo Tower, Waterloo station, Tate Modern, Millennium Bridge, Shakespeare's Globe, Blackfriars station, Founders Arms, Southwark Bridge, Cannon Street station, Borough Market, Shard, London Bridge station, Hays Galleria, HMS Belfast, Tower Bridge, Tower of London, Fenchurch Street station, Leadenhall Market, Lloyd's Building, Cheesegrater, Gherkin, Walkie-Talkie, Skygarden, St Helen's Bishopsgate, St Ethelburga's, and Liverpool Street station. Lunch in Regent's Park, Marylebone, Hyde Park, St James's Park, Blackfriars, Cannon Street, or London Bridge. Regent's Park lunch stop: the Garden Cafe. Marylebone lunch stop: the Gino's Cafe Bar. Hyde Park lunch stop: the Lido Cafe. St James's Park lunch stop: the St James's Cafe. Blackfriars lunch stop: the Founders Arms. Cannon Street lunch stop: the Bankers. London Bridge lunch stop: the Borough Market. 21 km.

**Charing Cross to Liverpool Street**: A shorter variant of [The London Stations](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-stations/). Flat riverside Thames path, South Bank, City of London streets, and historic financial district. Sights: Charing Cross station, Hungerford Bridge, Golden Jubilee Bridges, London Eye, Royal Festival Hall, National Theatre, Oxo Tower, Waterloo station, Tate Modern, Millennium Bridge, Shakespeare's Globe, Blackfriars station, Founders Arms, Southwark Bridge, Cannon Street station, Borough Market, Shard, London Bridge station, Hays Galleria, HMS Belfast, Tower Bridge, Tower of London, Fenchurch Street station, Leadenhall Market, Lloyd's Building, Cheesegrater, Gherkin, Walkie-Talkie, Skygarden, St Helen's Bishopsgate, St Ethelburga's, and Liverpool Street station. Lunch in Blackfriars, Cannon Street, or London Bridge. Blackfriars lunch stop: the Founders Arms. Cannon Street lunch stop: the Bankers. London Bridge lunch stop: the Borough Market. 8 km.
LST.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Kings Cross to Liverpool Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-stations/)**: Entirely flat, urban city-centre streets, royal parks, riverside Thames path, canal towpath, and historic financial district. Sights: Kings Cross station, St Pancras station, Kings Cross Central, Granary Square, Crick Institute, Euston station, Regent's Park, Queen Mary's Gardens, Regent's Park Open Air Theatre, Marylebone station, Great Central Hotel, Paddington Basin, Paddington station, Elizabeth Line station, Kensington Gardens, Italian Gardens, Serpentine, Serpentine Lido, Princess Diana Memorial, Hyde Park, Kensington Palace, Buckingham Palace, Queen's Gallery, St James's Park, Horseguard's Parade, Banqueting House, Trafalgar Square, Nelson's Column, Charing Cross station, Hungerford Bridge, Golden Jubilee Bridges, London Eye, Royal Festival Hall, National Theatre, Oxo Tower, Waterloo station, Tate Modern, Millennium Bridge, Shakespeare's Globe, Blackfriars station, Founders Arms, Southwark Bridge, Cannon Street station, Borough Market, Shard, London Bridge station, Hays Galleria, HMS Belfast, Tower Bridge, Tower of London, Fenchurch Street station, Leadenhall Market, Lloyd's Building, Cheesegrater, Gherkin, Walkie-Talkie, Skygarden, St Helen's Bishopsgate, St Ethelburga's, and Liverpool Street station. Lunch in Regent's Park, Marylebone, Hyde Park, St James's Park, Blackfriars, Cannon Street, or London Bridge. Regent's Park lunch stop: the Garden Cafe. Marylebone lunch stop: the Gino's Cafe Bar. Hyde Park lunch stop: the Lido Cafe. St James's Park lunch stop: the St James's Cafe. Blackfriars lunch stop: the Founders Arms. Cannon Street lunch stop: the Bankers. London Bridge lunch stop: the Borough Market. 21 km.

LTK.name: Little Kimble
LTK.adminWalksAll: **[Little Kimble to Saunderton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/little-kimble-to-saunderton/)**: Gentle Chilterns hills, beech woods, open stretches, fine views, and undulating but not taxing gradients. Bring a compass. Best in Oct–Nov. Lunch in Parslow's Hillock, Lower Cadsden, or Lacey Green. Parslow's Hillock lunch stop: the [Pink and Lily](http://pink-lily.com/). Lower Cadsden lunch stop: the [Plough](http://theplough.pub/). Lacey Green lunch stop: the [Whip Inn](http://thewhipinn.co.uk/). 17 km. 5 hours.
LTK.publicWalksS2S: **[Little Kimble to Saunderton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/little-kimble-to-saunderton/)**: Gentle Chilterns hills, beech woods, open stretches, fine views, and undulating but not taxing gradients. Bring a compass. Best in Oct–Nov. Lunch in Parslow's Hillock, Lower Cadsden, or Lacey Green. Parslow's Hillock lunch stop: the [Pink and Lily](http://pink-lily.com/). Lower Cadsden lunch stop: the [Plough](http://theplough.pub/). Lacey Green lunch stop: the [Whip Inn](http://thewhipinn.co.uk/). 17 km. 5 hours.

LTV.name: Lichfield Trent Valley
LTV.adminWalksAll: **[Lichfield Circular via Whittington Heath](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Golf club, heath, woodland, and Heart of England Way. Sights: Hopwas Hayes Wood and Whittington Heath. MOD firing range — check access. 15 km. 120 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/305%20Lichfield.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925785196)**
LTV.publicWalksCircular: **[Lichfield Circular via Whittington Heath](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Golf club, heath, woodland, and Heart of England Way. Sights: Hopwas Hayes Wood and Whittington Heath. MOD firing range — check access. 15 km. 120 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/305%20Lichfield.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925785196)**

LWM.name: Llantwit Major
LWM.adminWalksAll:
**[Barry to Llantwit Major](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/barry-to-llantwit-major/)**: Coastal headlands, pebble and sandy beaches, flat rock-shelf beaches, low cliffs, striated limestone cliffs, parkland, quiet cliff paths, wetland, and historic town centre. Sights: [Barry Docks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Docks), [Barry Island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Island), [Porthkerry Country Park](http://www.valeofglamorgan.gov.uk/en/enjoying/Coast-and-Countryside/Porthkerry-Country-Park/Porthkerry-Country-Park.aspx), [Porthkerry Viaduct](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Porthkerry_Viaduct), [The Bulwarks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Bulwarks,_Porthkerry), [St Illtyd's Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Illtyd's_Church,_Llantwit_Major), Cold Knap Point, Rhoose Point, and Summerhouse Point. Check tide timings. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in East Aberthaw: the [Blue Anchor](http://www.blueanchoraberthaw.com). 21 km.

**Rhoose Cardiff International Airport to Llantwit Major**: A shorter variant of [Barry to Llantwit Major](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/barry-to-llantwit-major/). Cliff tops, flat rock-shelf beaches, striated limestone cliffs, wetland, inland diversion past power station, dramatic coastal scenery, and historic town centre. Sights: Summerhouse Point, [St Illtyd's Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Illtyd's_Church,_Llantwit_Major), and Llantwit Major beach. Check tide timings. Lunch in East Aberthaw: the [Blue Anchor](http://www.blueanchoraberthaw.com). 15 km.
LWM.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Barry to Llantwit Major](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/barry-to-llantwit-major/)**: Coastal headlands, pebble and sandy beaches, flat rock-shelf beaches, low cliffs, striated limestone cliffs, parkland, quiet cliff paths, wetland, and historic town centre. Sights: [Barry Docks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Docks), [Barry Island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Island), [Porthkerry Country Park](http://www.valeofglamorgan.gov.uk/en/enjoying/Coast-and-Countryside/Porthkerry-Country-Park/Porthkerry-Country-Park.aspx), [Porthkerry Viaduct](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Porthkerry_Viaduct), [The Bulwarks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Bulwarks,_Porthkerry), [St Illtyd's Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Illtyd's_Church,_Llantwit_Major), Cold Knap Point, Rhoose Point, and Summerhouse Point. Check tide timings. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in East Aberthaw: the [Blue Anchor](http://www.blueanchoraberthaw.com). 21 km.

LWS.name: Lewes
LWS.adminWalksAll:
**Lewes to Southease via Mt Caburn**: An essential walk! Escarpments, three distinct downland ridges, wide open chalk paths, panoramic views, historic town streets, and gentle valleys. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 17 October 2021.* 16 km. 380 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921497025)**

**[Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-glynde-and-southease/)**: Rambler favourite! Three distinct downland ridges, wide open chalk paths, panoramic views, historic town streets, and gentle valleys. Sights: Mount Caburn and Lewes. Three steep climbs. Lunch in Rodmell, Southease, or Glynde. Rodmell lunch stop: the Abergavenny Arms. Southease lunch stop: the YHA Courtyard Cafe. Glynde lunch stops: the Trevor Arms, the Steamworks, or the Little Cottage Tea Rooms. 23 km.

**[Lewes Circular via Swanborough and Black Cap](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-swanborough-and-black-cap/)**: Open chalk downland, South Downs ridgeline, woods, wetland, bridleways, old racecourse, and town streets. Sights: Priory of St Pancras, Cockshut Stream Restoration Project, Lewes Old Racecourse, Lewes Castle, Barbican House Museum, Black Cap, Battle of Lewes viewpoint, Baxter's Field, and Ashcombe Windmill. Can be muddy. Lunch in Plumpton, or Falmer. Plumpton lunch stop: the Half Moon. Falmer lunch stop: the Swan Inn. 23 km.

**Lewes Circular via West Firle Circular**: Steep hills, chalk ridge, South Downs Way, secluded valley, flat river levee, and historic town. Sights: Lewes Castle, Mount Caburn, Firle Place, Monk's House, Harveys Brewery, Parish Church of St Thomas a Becket, and White Hart Hotel. Steep hills. Avoid Dec/Jan. Lunch in Firle: the Ram Inn. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 23 km. 6 hours.

**Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease & West Firle**: A longer variant of [Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-glynde-and-southease/). Rambler favourite! Three downland ridges, extended South Downs Way, pretty village of West Firle, chalk paths, and panoramic views. Sights: Mount Caburn and West Firle. Very long walk. Lunch in Rodmell, West Firle, or Glynde. Rodmell lunch stop: the Abergavenny Arms. West Firle lunch stop: the Ram Inn. Glynde lunch stop: the Trevor Arms. 29 km.

**Lewes Circular via Northease Manor**: Lower-lying inland route, Northease Manor, South Downs villages, and chalk downland. Sights: Lewes Castle, Northease Manor, and Monk's House. Lunch in Rodmell: the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/). An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse).

**[Lewes to Seaford via West Firle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-seaford-via-west-firle/)**: South Downs ridges, open downland, sweeping valley, remote hidden valley, coastal shingle beach, and recreation ground. Sights: Mount Caburn Iron Age Hill Fort, Firle Park, St Peter's Church, St Andrew's Church Bishopstone, and Firle Tower. Steep climbs. Lunch in West Firle: the Ram Inn. 22 km. 6 hours.

**Lewes to Southease via Glynde**: A shorter variant of [Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-glynde-and-southease/). Rambler favourite! Downland ridge, long gradual descent to Rodmell, chalk paths, and open views. Sights: Mount Caburn. Lunch in Rodmell, or Southease. Rodmell lunch stop: the Abergavenny Arms. Southease lunch stop: the YHA Courtyard Cafe. 11 km.

**Lewes to Southease via Lewes Priory**: Chalk grassland ridges, South Downs views, historic town, picturesque village, and return by train from Southease. Sights: Lewes Castle, Lewes Priory, Anne of Cleves House, and Monk's House. Lunch in Rodmell: the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 12 km.

**Lewes to Southease via Lewes Castle**: A variant of [Southease to Rottingdean or Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-rottingdean-or-brighton/). Chalk downs, South Downs Way, and Ouse valley. Sights: Lewes Castle. 11 km.

**Lewes to Plumpton**: A variant of [Lewes Circular via Swanborough and Black Cap](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-swanborough-and-black-cap/). Open chalk downland, South Downs ridgeline, woods, wetland, and farmland paths. Sights: Priory of St Pancras, Cockshut Stream Restoration Project, Black Cap, Lewes Castle, and Ashcombe Windmill. Can be muddy. Lunch in Plumpton: the Half Moon. 17 km.

**Lewes to Seaford omitting West Firle**: A variant of [Lewes to Seaford via West Firle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-seaford-via-west-firle/). South Downs ridges, open downland, remote valley, and coastal approach. Sights: Mount Caburn Iron Age Hill Fort and St Andrew's Church Bishopstone. Steep climbs. Lunch in Glynde: the [Trevor Arms](https://www.thetrevor.co.uk/), or the [Steamworks](https://steamworksbars.co.uk/about-us/glynde-steamworks-is-open-6472323). 18 km.

**Lewes to Brighton**: A variant of [Southease to Rottingdean or Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-rottingdean-or-brighton/). Chalk downs, open escarpment, quaint village, seafront promenade, and urban Brighton. Sights: Lewes Castle, Kipling Gardens, Brighton Pier, and [Brighton Royal Pavilion](http://www.royalpavilion.org.uk/). Lunch in Kingston: the Juggs. 19 km.

**Lewes to Glynde via West Firle**: A shorter variant of [Lewes to Seaford via West Firle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-seaford-via-west-firle/). Circular ridge path, open downland, and valley descent into Glynde. Sights: Mount Caburn Iron Age Hill Fort. Lunch in Glynde: the [Trevor Arms](https://www.thetrevor.co.uk/), or the [Steamworks](https://steamworksbars.co.uk/about-us/glynde-steamworks-is-open-6472323). 6 km.

**Lewes to Southease via Kipling Gardens**: A variant of [Southease to Rottingdean or Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-rottingdean-or-brighton/). Chalk downs, open escarpment, quaint village, heart of downs, and Ouse valley. Sights: Lewes Castle and Kipling Gardens. Lunch in Kingston: the Juggs. 20 km.

**Lewes to Glynde via West Firle Circular**: Steep hill with views, secluded valley, and chalk downland. Sights: Lewes Castle and Mount Caburn. Steep hill. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 5 km.

**Lewes to Cooksbridge via Swanborough and Black Cap & Old Hamsey Church**: A variant of [Lewes Circular via Swanborough and Black Cap](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-swanborough-and-black-cap/). Open chalk downland, South Downs ridgeline, River Ouse bank, farmland, and country lanes. Sights: Priory of St Pancras, Cockshut Stream Restoration Project, Black Cap, Old Hamsey Church, and Ashcombe Windmill. Track slippery when wet. Lunch in Offham: the Blacksmith's Arms. 24 km.

**Lewes to Seaford Walk via South Downs**: Chalk grassland ridges, South Downs, Southease station midpoint, and extended coastal finish at Seaford. Sights: Lewes Castle and Monk's House. Lunch in Rodmell: the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/). A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 24 km.

**Lewes to Seaford via St Andrew's Church**: River valley, ridge-top South Downs, open downland with coastal views, pretty villages, and seaside esplanade. Sights: Monk's House and St Andrew's Church. Lunch in Rodmell: the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/). A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 24 km. 6.5 hours.

**Hassocks to Lewes**: South Downs chalk grassland ridge, panoramic views to sea and inland, dew ponds, Iron Age fort, wooded climbs, riverside embankment, and historic town. Sights: Church of St John the Baptist, [Jill Windmill](http://www.jillwindmill.org.uk), Jack Windmill, Ditchling Beacon, [Chattri Memorial](http://www.chattri.com/), [Old Hamsey Church](http://www.friendsofhamsey.org/history/), Anne of Cleves House, Lewes Castle, Barbican House Museum, Lewes Priory, St John Sub Castro, and Bull House. Can be slippery. Lunch in Plumpton: the Half Moon. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 17 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Uckfield to Lewes via Glyndebourne opera house](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-lewes/)**: River valleys, fields, woods, golf course, open chalk downland, South Downs escarpment, and historic town. Sights: Horsted Place, Lavender Line (Dingley Dell terminal), Glyndebourne opera house, Glyndebourne Wind Turbine, Southeram Nature Reserve, Lewes Castle, and Caburn Hillfort. Can be muddy. Lunch in Barcombe Mills: the Anchor Inn. 20 km.

**Glynde to Lewes**: A shorter variant of [Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-glynde-and-southease/). Rambler favourite! Downland ridge via Mount Caburn, steep descent into Lewes, and chalk paths. Sights: Mount Caburn. Steep descent. Lunch in Glynde: the Trevor Arms, the Steamworks, or the Little Cottage Tea Rooms. 6 km.

**Uckfield to Lewes via Barcombe Mills sluices and river pools**: An alternative variant of [Uckfield to Lewes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-lewes/). River valleys, fields, mill streams, riverside path, small villages, and historic town centre. Sights: Barcombe Mills sluices and river pools, St Mary the Virgin church, St Peter church, Hamsey, Lewes Castle, Lewes Barbican, Horsted Place, and Lavender Line (Dingley Dell terminal). Lunch in Barcombe Mills: the Anchor Inn. 18 km.

**Southease to Lewes**: Flat valley walk along River Ouse levee into historic Lewes. Sights: Lewes Castle and Monk's House. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 6 km.
LWS.publicWalksS2S: **Lewes to Southease via Mt Caburn**: An essential walk! Escarpments, three distinct downland ridges, wide open chalk paths, panoramic views, historic town streets, and gentle valleys. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 16 km. 380 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921497025)**

LYP.name: Lymington Pier
LYP.adminWalksAll:
**Lymington Pier to Lymington Town via Shirley Holms**: A shorter variant of [Brockenhurst to Lymington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/brockenhurst-to-lymington/). River estuary path, wet woodland, Iron Age hill fort, farm fields, heathland, and coastal saltmarsh embankments. Sights: [Walhampton Monument](https://burrardneale.wordpress.com/history-of-the-walhampton-monument), [Lymington Reedbeds](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/lymington-reedbeds-nature-reserve), [Buckland Rings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Buckland_Rings), Shirley Holms, [Lymington–Keyhaven Marshes](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/lymingtonkeyhaven), Salt Boiling Houses, Moses Dock, [Lymington Sea Water Baths](https://www.lymingtonseawaterbaths.org.uk), and Lymington Yacht Haven. Wet woodland sections. Lunch in Chequers Green: the [Chequers Inn](http://www.chequersinnlymington.com). 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**Lymington Pier to Lymington Town via Hurst Castle**: An alternative variant of [Brockenhurst to Lymington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/brockenhurst-to-lymington/). River estuary path, wet woodland, Iron Age hill fort, farm fields, coastal marshes, shingle spit, and saltmarsh embankments. Sights: [Walhampton Monument](https://burrardneale.wordpress.com/history-of-the-walhampton-monument), [Lymington Reedbeds](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/lymington-reedbeds-nature-reserve), [Buckland Rings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Buckland_Rings), [Hurst Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/hurst-castle), Hurst Point Lighthouse, Fort Albert, Keyhaven Bird Observatory, The Pans & Avon Water Wetlands, [Lymington–Keyhaven Marshes](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/lymingtonkeyhaven), Salt Boiling Houses, Moses Dock, [Lymington Sea Water Baths](https://www.lymingtonseawaterbaths.org.uk), and Lymington Yacht Haven. Wet woodland sections. Lunch in Keyhaven: the [Gun Inn](https://www.theguninn.co.uk). 21 km. 4.5 hours.
LYP.publicWalksS2S:
**Lymington Pier to Lymington Town via Shirley Holms**: A shorter variant of [Brockenhurst to Lymington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/brockenhurst-to-lymington/). River estuary path, wet woodland, Iron Age hill fort, farm fields, heathland, and coastal saltmarsh embankments. Sights: [Walhampton Monument](https://burrardneale.wordpress.com/history-of-the-walhampton-monument), [Lymington Reedbeds](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/lymington-reedbeds-nature-reserve), [Buckland Rings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Buckland_Rings), Shirley Holms, [Lymington–Keyhaven Marshes](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/lymingtonkeyhaven), Salt Boiling Houses, Moses Dock, [Lymington Sea Water Baths](https://www.lymingtonseawaterbaths.org.uk), and Lymington Yacht Haven. Wet woodland sections. Lunch in Chequers Green: the [Chequers Inn](http://www.chequersinnlymington.com). 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**Lymington Pier to Lymington Town via Hurst Castle**: An alternative variant of [Brockenhurst to Lymington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/brockenhurst-to-lymington/). River estuary path, wet woodland, Iron Age hill fort, farm fields, coastal marshes, shingle spit, and saltmarsh embankments. Sights: [Walhampton Monument](https://burrardneale.wordpress.com/history-of-the-walhampton-monument), [Lymington Reedbeds](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/lymington-reedbeds-nature-reserve), [Buckland Rings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Buckland_Rings), [Hurst Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/hurst-castle), Hurst Point Lighthouse, Fort Albert, Keyhaven Bird Observatory, The Pans & Avon Water Wetlands, [Lymington–Keyhaven Marshes](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/lymingtonkeyhaven), Salt Boiling Houses, Moses Dock, [Lymington Sea Water Baths](https://www.lymingtonseawaterbaths.org.uk), and Lymington Yacht Haven. Wet woodland sections. Lunch in Keyhaven: the [Gun Inn](https://www.theguninn.co.uk). 21 km. 4.5 hours.

LYT.name: Lymington Town
LYT.adminWalksAll:
**Lymington to Brockenhurst via Roydon Woods**: Can be EXTREME mud in Lymington Reeds Beds Nature Reserve. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 3 February 2024.* 12 km. 90 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921509210)**

**Lymington Town to New Milton**: A variant of [Lymington to Barton-on-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lymington-to-barton-on-sea/). Coastal path, salt marshes, nature reserve, esplanade, low eroding cliffs, and then inland road walking to station. Sights: Hurst Castle, [Hordle Cliff](https://wessexcoastgeology.soton.ac.uk/hordle.htm), and [Beachcomber Café](http://www.beachcomber-cafe.co.uk/). Eroding cliff edges. Lunch in Keyhaven, or Milford-on-Sea. Keyhaven lunch stop: the [Gun Inn](http://www.theguninn.com/). Milford-on-Sea lunch stops: the [Marine](http://www.themarinemos.co.uk/), or the Needles Eye Café. 18 km.

**[Brockenhurst to Lymington Town via Lymington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/brockenhurst-to-lymington/)**: Landscaped parkland, mixed woodland, open heathland, farm fields, coastal saltmarsh embankments, and sailing town. Sights: New Zealand Cemetery, St Nicholas Church, Brockenhurst Park, [Roydon Woods Nature Reserve](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/roydon-woods-nature-reserve), Roydon Manor, Shirley Holms, [Lymington–Keyhaven Marshes](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/lymingtonkeyhaven), [Lymington Sea Water Baths](https://www.lymingtonseawaterbaths.org.uk), [Lymington](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lymington), Salt Boiling Houses, Moses Dock, Lymington Yacht Haven, and Royal Lymington Yacht Club. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chequers Green: the [Chequers Inn](http://www.chequersinnlymington.com). 20 km. 4.5 hours.

**Lymington Pier to Lymington Town via Shirley Holms**: A shorter variant of [Brockenhurst to Lymington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/brockenhurst-to-lymington/). River estuary path, wet woodland, Iron Age hill fort, farm fields, heathland, and coastal saltmarsh embankments. Sights: [Walhampton Monument](https://burrardneale.wordpress.com/history-of-the-walhampton-monument), [Lymington Reedbeds](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/lymington-reedbeds-nature-reserve), [Buckland Rings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Buckland_Rings), Shirley Holms, [Lymington–Keyhaven Marshes](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/lymingtonkeyhaven), Salt Boiling Houses, Moses Dock, [Lymington Sea Water Baths](https://www.lymingtonseawaterbaths.org.uk), and Lymington Yacht Haven. Wet woodland sections. Lunch in Chequers Green: the [Chequers Inn](http://www.chequersinnlymington.com). 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**Lymington Pier to Lymington Town via Hurst Castle**: An alternative variant of [Brockenhurst to Lymington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/brockenhurst-to-lymington/). River estuary path, wet woodland, Iron Age hill fort, farm fields, coastal marshes, shingle spit, and saltmarsh embankments. Sights: [Walhampton Monument](https://burrardneale.wordpress.com/history-of-the-walhampton-monument), [Lymington Reedbeds](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/lymington-reedbeds-nature-reserve), [Buckland Rings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Buckland_Rings), [Hurst Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/hurst-castle), Hurst Point Lighthouse, Fort Albert, Keyhaven Bird Observatory, The Pans & Avon Water Wetlands, [Lymington–Keyhaven Marshes](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/lymingtonkeyhaven), Salt Boiling Houses, Moses Dock, [Lymington Sea Water Baths](https://www.lymingtonseawaterbaths.org.uk), and Lymington Yacht Haven. Wet woodland sections. Lunch in Keyhaven: the [Gun Inn](https://www.theguninn.co.uk). 21 km. 4.5 hours.

**Brockenhurst to Lymington Town via Hurst Castle**: A longer variant of [Brockenhurst to Lymington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/brockenhurst-to-lymington/). Landscaped parkland, woodland, heathland, farm fields, coastal marshes, shingle spit, and saltmarsh embankments. Sights: New Zealand Cemetery, St Nicholas Church, Brockenhurst Park, [Roydon Woods Nature Reserve](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/roydon-woods-nature-reserve), Roydon Manor, Shirley Holms, [Hurst Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/hurst-castle), Hurst Point Lighthouse, Fort Albert, Keyhaven Bird Observatory, The Pans & Avon Water Wetlands, [Lymington–Keyhaven Marshes](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/lymingtonkeyhaven), Salt Boiling Houses, Moses Dock, [Lymington Sea Water Baths](https://www.lymingtonseawaterbaths.org.uk), and Lymington Yacht Haven. Can be muddy. Lunch in Keyhaven: the [Gun Inn](https://www.theguninn.co.uk). 25 km. 5.5 hours.
LYT.publicWalksS2S: **Lymington to Brockenhurst via Roydon Woods**: Can be EXTREME mud in Lymington Reeds Beds Nature Reserve. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 12 km. 90 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921509210)**

MAC.name: Macclesfield
MAC.adminWalksAll:
**[Macclesfield Circular via Teggs Nose and Kerridge Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/macclesfield-circular-via-teggs-nose-and-kerridge-hill/)**: Steep residential roads, rolling pastures, quarried hilltop, moorland edges, forest, isolated chapel, reservoir, long ridgeline, and canal towpath. Sights: Tegg's Nose Country Park, Macclesfield Forest, Forest Chapel, Lamaload Reservoir, White Nancy, Kerridge Ridge, and Macclesfield Canal. Steep ascents. Lunch in Macclesfield Forest, Bottom-of-the-Oven, or Rainow. Macclesfield Forest lunch stop: the [Forest Distillery](https://theforestdistillery.com/). Bottom-of-the-Oven lunch stop: the [Stanley Arms Hotel](https://www.stanleyarms.com/). Rainow lunch stop: the Robin Hood. 24 km. 7 hours.

**[Macclesfield Circular via The Dane Valley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/macclesfield-circular-via-the-dane-valley/)**: Macclesfield Canal towpath, rolling pastures, wooded valleys, exposed ridge, Dane Valley, tributary valleys, moorland fringes, gritstone ridges, and reservoir. Sights: Macclesfield Canal, [Sutton Hall](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/suttonhall/), Croker Hill, Wincle Minn, Greasley Hollow, Dane Valley, Bosley Cloud, and Shutlingsloe. Can be muddy. Lunch in Dane Bridge, Wincle, or Wincle. Dane Bridge, Wincle lunch stop: the [Wincle Beer Company](https://www.winclebeer.co.uk/). Wincle lunch stop: the [Ship Inn](https://theshipinnwinclefood.co.uk/). 28 km. 7.5 hours.

**Macclesfield Circular via Teggs Nose and Kerridge Hill & Shining Tor**: A longer variant of [Macclesfield Circular via Teggs Nose and Kerridge Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/macclesfield-circular-via-teggs-nose-and-kerridge-hill/). Steep residential roads, rolling pastures, quarried hilltop, moorland, forest, isolated chapel, high moor to Shining Tor, reservoir, long ridgeline, and canal towpath. Sights: Tegg's Nose Country Park, Macclesfield Forest, Forest Chapel, Shining Tor, [Cat & Fiddle](https://theforestdistillery.com/cat-fiddle/), Lamaload Reservoir, White Nancy, Kerridge Ridge, and Macclesfield Canal. Very steep ascents. Lunch in Buxton New Road, Macclesfield Forest, or Bottom-of-the-Oven. Buxton New Road lunch stops: the [Cat & Fiddle](https://theforestdistillery.com/cat-fiddle/), or the [Peak View Restaurant & Tea Room](http://www.peakviewtearooms.co.uk/). Macclesfield Forest lunch stop: the [Forest Distillery](https://theforestdistillery.com/). Bottom-of-the-Oven lunch stop: the [Stanley Arms Hotel](https://www.stanleyarms.com/). 31 km.

**[Macclesfield to Buxton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/macclesfield-to-buxton/)**: Residential roads, pastures, rolling hills, quarried hilltop, forest tracks, exposed moorland, lonely upland valleys, and riverside approach into a spa town. Sights: Tegg's Nose Country Park, Macclesfield Forest, Forest Chapel, Shining Tor, Goytsclough Quarry, Pavilion Gardens, [Cat & Fiddle](https://theforestdistillery.com/cat-fiddle/), and [Peak View Restaurant & Tea Room](http://www.peakviewtearooms.co.uk/). Exposed in bad weather. Lunch in Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest: the [Forest Distillery](https://theforestdistillery.com/), or the [Stanley Arms Hotel](https://www.stanleyarms.com/). 25 km. 7 hours.

**[Buxton to Macclesfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-to-macclesfield/)**: Moorland, exposed ridges, gritstone uplands, wooded valleys, rolling pastures, ancient packhorse routes, and quarried country park. Sights: Shining Tor, Buxton Crescent Hotel, [Cat & Fiddle](https://theforestdistillery.com/the-bar-at-cat-and-fiddle/), Forest Chapel, Macclesfield Forest, and Tegg's Nose Country Park. Very exposed moorland. Lunch in Buxton New Road, or Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest. Buxton New Road lunch stop: the [Peak View Restaurant & Tea Room](http://www.peakviewtearooms.co.uk). Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest lunch stops: the [Forest Distillery](https://theforestdistillery.com), or the Stanley Arms Hotel. 24 km. 6.5 hours.

MAI.name: Maidenhead
MAI.adminWalksAll:
**[Maidenhead to Marlow via Winter Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/maidenhead-to-marlow/)**: Thames riverside path, wooded escarpment views, short steep hill, and pretty 18th-century town. Sights: Maidenhead Rail Bridge, Boulters Lock, Cliveden, Winter Hill, and Marlow Bridge. Riverside can flood. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Maidenhead, or Cookham. Maidenhead lunch stop: the Boathouse. Cookham lunch stops: the Bel and the Dragon, the Kings Arms, the Crown, or the Teapot Tea Shop. 14 km.

**Maidenhead to Marlow via Maidenhead Rail Bridge**: A shorter variant of [Maidenhead to Marlow](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/maidenhead-to-marlow/). Thames riverside path all the way, flat, passing Boulters Lock, and Cookham and Bourne End. Sights: Maidenhead Rail Bridge, Boulters Lock, Cliveden, and Marlow Bridge. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Cookham: the Bel and the Dragon, the Kings Arms, the Crown, or the Teapot Tea Shop. 14 km.

**Maidenhead to Bourne End via Maidenhead Rail Bridge**: A shorter variant of [Maidenhead to Marlow](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/maidenhead-to-marlow/). Thames riverside path all the way, flat, and passing Boulters Lock and Cookham. Sights: Maidenhead Rail Bridge, Boulters Lock, and Cliveden. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Cookham: the Bel and the Dragon, the Kings Arms, the Crown, or the Teapot Tea Shop. 9 km.

**Maidenhead to Cookham via Maidenhead Rail Bridge**: A shorter variant of [Maidenhead to Marlow](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/maidenhead-to-marlow/). Thames riverside path, wooded escarpment views, and Boulters Lock. Sights: Maidenhead Rail Bridge, Boulters Lock, and Cliveden. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Cookham: the Bel and the Dragon, the Kings Arms, the Crown, or the Teapot Tea Shop. 8 km.

**Maidenhead to Bourne End via Winter Hill**: A longer variant of [Maidenhead to Marlow](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/maidenhead-to-marlow/). Thames riverside path, hill, town, and then riverside return to Bourne End. Sights: Maidenhead Rail Bridge, Boulters Lock, Cliveden, Winter Hill, and Marlow Bridge. Riverside can flood. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Cookham: the Bel and the Dragon, the Kings Arms, the Crown, or the Teapot Tea Shop. 19 km.

**Maidenhead to Cookham via Winter Hill**: A longer variant of [Maidenhead to Marlow](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/maidenhead-to-marlow/). Thames riverside path, hill, town, and then riverside return to Cookham. Sights: Maidenhead Rail Bridge, Boulters Lock, Cliveden, Winter Hill, and Marlow Bridge. Riverside can be slithery. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Cookham: the Bel and the Dragon, the Kings Arms, the Crown, or the Teapot Tea Shop. 21 km.

**Cookham to Maidenhead**: Hills, escarpment with valley views, beech woods, open fields, riverside Thames Path, wooded estate banks, and town outskirts. Sights: Stanley Spencer Gallery, Holy Trinity Church, Cliveden Estate, Winter Hill, Boulter's Lock, Bisham Woods, Cock Marsh, and Brunel's Maidenhead Railway Bridge. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Cookham Dean, Cock Marsh, or Cookham. Cookham Dean lunch stops: the Jolly Farmer, or the Mole & Badger. Cock Marsh lunch stop: the Bounty. Cookham lunch stops: the Kings Arms, the Ferry, or the Bel and the Dragon. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 5 hours.

**Marlow to Maidenhead via Thames Path**: Flat riverside Thames Path, meadows, riverside gardens, wooded estate banks, and town outskirts. Sights: Holy Trinity Church, Stanley Spencer Gallery, Cliveden Estate, and Boulter's Lock. Lunch in Bourne End, or Cookham. Bourne End lunch stop: the Bounty Inn. Cookham lunch stops: the Kings Arms, the Ferry, or the Bel and the Dragon. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km.

Sights: Maidenhead Rail Bridge, Boulters Lock, Cliveden, Winter Hill, and Marlow Bridge. Riverside can flood. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Maidenhead, or Cookham. Maidenhead lunch stop: the Boathouse. MAR.name: Margate
MAR.adminWalksAll:
**Ramsgate to Margate**: Low chalk cliffs, sandy bays and coves, cliff-top grassland, seaside promenades, and historic coastal towns. Sights: Turner Contemporary Gallery, Dickens House Museum, Kingsgate Castle, North Foreland Lighthouse, and [Royal Victoria Pavilion](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/all-pubs/england/kent/the-royal-victoria-pavilion-ramsgate). Check tide timings. Very busy; infinite cafés/restaurants. Lunch in Ramsgate, Dumpton Gap, Louisa Bay, Broadstairs, Kingsgate Bay, or Botany Bay. Ramsgate lunch stops: the [Royal Victoria Pavillion](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/all-pubs/england/kent/the-royal-victoria-pavilion-ramsgate), or the [Royal Harbour Brasserie](http://www.royalharbourbrasserie.co.uk/). Dumpton Gap lunch stop: the [Sams](https://twitter.com/dumptoncafe). Louisa Bay lunch stop: the [Louisa Bay Cafe](https://www.facebook.com/Louisa-Bay-Cafe-260058987367161/). Broadstairs lunch stop: the Charles Dickens. Kingsgate Bay lunch stop: the [Captain Digby](http://www.captaindigby.co.uk/). Botany Bay lunch stop: the [Botany Bay](https://www.botanybayhotel.co.uk/). *Last reviewed 25 February 2026.* 16 km. 120 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2797333523)**

**Margate to Ramsgate**: An alternative variant of [Ramsgate to Margate](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ramsgate-to-margate/). Rambler favourite! Low chalk cliffs, sandy bays and coves, cliff-top grassland, seaside promenades, and historic coastal towns. Sights: Turner Contemporary Gallery, Kingsgate Castle, North Foreland Lighthouse, and Dickens House Museum. Check tide timings. Lunch in Kingsgate Bay, Botany Bay, Louisa Bay, Dumpton Gap, or Ramsgate. Kingsgate Bay lunch stop: the [Captain Digby](http://www.captaindigby.co.uk/). Botany Bay lunch stop: the [Botany Bay](https://www.botanybayhotel.co.uk/). Louisa Bay lunch stop: the [Louisa Bay Cafe](https://www.facebook.com/Louisa-Bay-Cafe-260058987367161/). Dumpton Gap lunch stop: the [Sams](https://twitter.com/dumptoncafe). Ramsgate lunch stops: the [Royal Victoria Pavillion](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/all-pubs/england/kent/the-royal-victoria-pavilion-ramsgate), or the [Royal Harbour Brasserie](http://www.royalharbourbrasserie.co.uk/). 15 km.

**Margate to Herne Bay**: A longer variant of [Birchington to Herne Bay](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/birchington-to-herne-bay/). Flat seafront promenades, quiet residential clifftops, small headlands, and sandy beaches linking coastal towns. Sights: [Reculver Towers and Roman Fort](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reculver), Turner Contemporary Gallery, and Herne Bay Pier. Lunch in Reculver: the [King Ethelbert Inn](https://www.kingethelbertinn.co.uk/), or the [HatHats Coffee Company](https://hathats.co.uk/pages/reculver). 18 km.

MDE.name: Maidstone East
MDE.adminWalksAll:
**[Aylesford to Maidstone East](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/aylesford-to-maidstone/)**: River meadows, chalk downland, wooded North Downs escarpment, open hilltop, farmland, riverside towpath, and country park. Sights: [The Friars](https://www.thefriars.org.uk), [Kit's Coty House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/kits-coty-house-and-little-kits-coty-house/history), [Cobtree Manor Park](https://www.cobtreecafe.co.uk/about-cobtree-manor-park), [Whatman Park](https://www.maidstoneriverpark.co.uk/the-park/whatman-park), Aylesford Bridge, Ss Peter and Paul, Kit's Coty Vineyard, St Mary Burham, Little Kit's Coty House, White Horse Stone, Allington Castle, Kent Life, Allington Sluice Gate, Brenchley Gardens, Iguanodon, Battle of Maidstone memorial, Maidstone Museum, and Battle of Medway memorial. Can be muddy. Lunch in Eccles, Burham, or Burham Common. Eccles lunch stop: the Red Bull. Burham lunch stops: the [Butcher's Block](https://thebutchersblockpub.co.uk), or the [Windmill](https://windmillburham.com). Burham Common lunch stop: the [Robin Hood](https://www.robinhood-pub.co.uk). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

**[East Malling to Maidstone East](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-malling-to-maidstone/)**: Orchards, coppiced woodland, water meadows, riverside paths, low hills, and pedestrianised town centre. Sights: [East Malling Research Station (Horticultural Research Institute)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Malling_Research_Station), [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), [Maidstone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maidstone), St Mary's Abbey, Manor Park Country Park, St Leonard's Tower, Warren Woods Nature Park, Douce's Manor, St Mary the Virgin, Nettlestead Place, Medway Valley Walk, Tutsham Hall, Smiths Hall, Barham Court, Teston Bridge, Maidstone Millennium River Park, Barming Bridge, East Farleigh Bridge, East Farleigh Lock, Lockmeadow Millennium Bridge, The Stag, All Saints Church, Archbishop's Palace, Russian Cannon, Maidstone Town Hall, Maidstone Museum, Battle of Maidstone memorial, Brenchley Gardens, and Iguanodon. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh, or Wateringbury. West Farleigh lunch stop: the [Tickled Trout](https://thetickledtrout.com). Wateringbury lunch stops: the [River Kitchen](https://www.theriverkitchen.co.uk), or the Ramblers Rest Café. 19 km. 4.5 hours.

**West Malling to Maidstone East**: A longer variant of [East Malling to Maidstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-malling-to-maidstone/). Orchards, country park, coppiced woodland, water meadows, riverside paths, low hills, and pedestrianised town centre. Sights: [East Malling Research Station (Horticultural Research Institute)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Malling_Research_Station), [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), [Maidstone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maidstone), St Mary's Abbey, Manor Park Country Park, St Leonard's Tower, Douce's Manor, Warren Woods Nature Park, St Mary the Virgin, Nettlestead Place, Medway Valley Walk, Tutsham Hall, Smiths Hall, Barham Court, Teston Bridge, Maidstone Millennium River Park, Barming Bridge, East Farleigh Bridge, East Farleigh Lock, Lockmeadow Millennium Bridge, The Stag, All Saints Church, Archbishop's Palace, Russian Cannon, Maidstone Town Hall, Maidstone Museum, Battle of Maidstone memorial, Brenchley Gardens, and Iguanodon. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh, or Wateringbury. West Farleigh lunch stop: the [Tickled Trout](https://thetickledtrout.com). Wateringbury lunch stops: the [River Kitchen](https://www.theriverkitchen.co.uk), or the Ramblers Rest Café. 21 km. 5 hours.

MDG.name: Midgham
MDG.adminWalksAll:
**[Newbury Racecourse to Midgham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/newbury-racecourse-to-woolhampton/)**: Gentle climbs, heathland common, woodland, river meadows, and canal. Sights: Greenham Common and Cruise Missile Bunkers. Swing bridge delays. Lunch in Ashford Hill: the [Ship Inn](https://whatpub.com/pubs/HAN/034/ship-inn-ashford-hill). 18 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Aldermaston to Midgham via Stanford Dingley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/aldermaston-to-woolhampton-via-stanford-dingley/)**: Undulating West Berkshire Downs, ancient woodlands, commons, fields, chalk stream valleys, heathland, canal towpath, and landscaped parkland. Sights: Bucklebury Common, Bucklebury Estate, Avenue of Oaks, Kennet & Avon Canal, and Midgham Park. Can be muddy. Lunch in Beenham, Chapel Row, Bucklebury, Stanford Dingley, Bucklebury, or Upper Bucklebury. Beenham lunch stop: the [Six Bells](http://www.thesixbells.co.uk/). Chapel Row, Bucklebury lunch stops: the [Blackbird Cafe](http://www.theblackbirdcafe.co.uk/), or the [Bladebone at Bucklebury](http://www.thebladebone.com/). Stanford Dingley lunch stops: the [Old Boot](http://www.oldbootinnstanforddingley.co.uk/), or the [Bull Inn](https://thebullinnpub.com/). Bucklebury lunch stop: the [Bucklebury Farm Café](https://buckleburyfarm.co.uk/cafe). Upper Bucklebury lunch stop: the [Cottage Inn](http://www.thecottageinnupperbucklebury.co.uk/). 20 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Aldermaston to Midgham via Frilsham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/aldermaston-to-woolhampton-via-frilsham/)**: Mildly undulating West Berkshire Downs, ancient woodlands, commons, chalk stream valleys, water meadows, heathland, landscaped park, and canal towpath. Sights: Bucklebury Common, Avenue of Oaks, Kennet & Avon Canal, and Midgham Park. Can be muddy. Lunch in Beenham, Chapel Row, Bucklebury, or Frilsham. Beenham lunch stop: the [Six Bells](http://www.thesixbells.co.uk/). Chapel Row, Bucklebury lunch stops: the [Blackbird Cafe](http://www.theblackbirdcafe.co.uk/), or the [Bladebone at Bucklebury](http://www.thebladebone.com/). Frilsham lunch stop: the [Pot Kiln](http://www.potkiln.org/). 23 km. 5.5 hours.

**Newbury to Midgham**: A variant of [Newbury Racecourse to Woolhampton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/newbury-racecourse-to-woolhampton/). Gentle climbs, heathland common, woodland, river meadows, and canal. Sights: Greenham Common and Cruise Missile Bunkers. Swing bridge delays. Lunch in Ashford Hill: the [Ship Inn](https://whatpub.com/pubs/HAN/034/ship-inn-ashford-hill). 18 km.

**Mortimer to Midgham**: Gentle farmland, Roman earthworks, pine woods, commons, river weirs, and short canalside towpath. Sights: St Mary's Church, Stratfield Mortimer, Calleva Amphitheatre, Roman Walls of Calleva, St Mary the Virgin Church, Silchester, Kennet & Avon Canal Trust Information Centre, and Padworth Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Silchester Common: the Calleva Arms. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 19 km.

**Aldermaston to Midgham via Frilsham & Grimsbury Castle**: A longer variant of [Aldermaston to Woolhampton via Frilsham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/aldermaston-to-woolhampton-via-frilsham/). Undulating ancient woodlands, heathland, chalk stream valleys, and Iron Age hillfort. Sights: Grimsbury Castle. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hermitage: the [Fox Inn](http://www.hermitage-fox.co.uk/), or the White Horse of Hermitage. 27 km.

MDN.name: Maiden Newton
MDN.adminWalksAll:
**[Maiden Newton Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/maiden-newton-circular-or-to-dorchester/)**: Chalk downland, dry hanging valleys, rounded hills, broad ridges, chalk streams, picturesque villages, and historic churches. Sights: The Cerne Giant, Cerne Abbey, Hog Cliff National Nature Reserve, and Giant Hill. Six hilly ascents. Lunch in Sydling St. Nicholas, or Cerne Abbas. Sydling St. Nicholas lunch stop: the [Greyhound Inn](https://dorsetgreyhound.co.uk). Cerne Abbas lunch stops: the [Royal Oak](https://theroyaloakcerneabbas.co.uk), the [Giant Inn (aka The Red Lion)](https://www.thegiantcerneabbas.co.uk), the [New Inn](https://www.thenewinncerneabbas.co.uk/index), or the [Abbots Tearoom](https://www.abbotsbedandbreakfast.co.uk). 28 km. 7 hours.

**Maiden Newton to Dorchester South**: An alternative variant of [Maiden Newton Circular or to Dorchester](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/maiden-newton-circular-or-to-dorchester/). Chalk downland, Cerne Valley, historic Dorchester city centre, and two modest climbs with fine views. Sights: The Cerne Giant, Cerne Abbey, Hog Cliff National Nature Reserve, and Giant Hill. Hilly ascents. Lunch in Sydling St. Nicholas, or Cerne Abbas. Sydling St. Nicholas lunch stop: the [Greyhound Inn](https://dorsetgreyhound.co.uk). Cerne Abbas lunch stops: the [Royal Oak](https://theroyaloakcerneabbas.co.uk), the [Giant Inn (aka The Red Lion)](https://www.thegiantcerneabbas.co.uk), the [New Inn](https://www.thenewinncerneabbas.co.uk/index), or the [Abbots Tearoom](https://www.abbotsbedandbreakfast.co.uk).

MDW.name: Maidstone West
MDW.adminWalksAll:
**Maidstone West to Wateringbury via Teston Lock**: A shorter variant of [Wateringbury Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wateringbury-circular/). River Medway towpath, riverside paths, flat valley walking, and water meadows. Sights: Archbishop's Palace, All Saints, Maidstone, The Stag, Lockmeadow Millennium Bridge, Tovil Railway Bridge, East Farleigh Lock, East Farleigh Bridge, Barming Bridge, Teston Lock, Teston Bridge, and Maidstone Millennium River Park. 9 km. 2 hours.

**Maidstone West to Wateringbury via Archbishop's Palace**: A longer variant of [Wateringbury Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wateringbury-circular/). River Medway towpath, riverside paths, low hills, orchard country, country lanes, and water meadows. Sights: Archbishop's Palace, All Saints, Maidstone, The Stag, Lockmeadow Millennium Bridge, Tovil Railway Bridge, East Farleigh Lock, East Farleigh Bridge, Barming Bridge, [Horseshoes Inn](https://www.thehorseshoeseastfarleigh.co.uk), Teston Bridge, Tutsham Hall, and Maidstone Millennium River Park. Can be muddy. Lunch in East Farleigh: the [Horseshoes Inn](https://www.thehorseshoeseastfarleigh.co.uk), the Bull Inn, or the Victory. 17 km. 4 hours.

**Maidstone West to Wateringbury via North Pole**: A longer variant of [Wateringbury Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wateringbury-circular/). River Medway towpath and valley, riverside paths, low hills, orchard country, woodland, water meadows, and country lanes. Sights: Archbishop's Palace, All Saints, Maidstone, The Stag, Lockmeadow Millennium Bridge, East Farleigh Bridge, East Farleigh Lock, [North Pole](https://northpolepub.co.uk), Teston Bridge, Teston Bridge Country Park, Tutsham Hall, Ss Peter and Paul, Teston, Barham Court, and [Horseshoes Inn](https://www.thehorseshoeseastfarleigh.co.uk). Can be muddy. Lunch in Red Hill, or East Farleigh. Red Hill lunch stop: the [North Pole](https://northpolepub.co.uk). East Farleigh lunch stop: the [Horseshoes Inn](https://www.thehorseshoeseastfarleigh.co.uk). 25 km. 6 hours.

**East Malling to Maidstone West**: An alternative variant of [East Malling to Maidstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-malling-to-maidstone/). Orchards, coppiced woodland, water meadows, and riverside paths into Maidstone. Sights: Teston Bridge, East Farleigh Bridge, Lockmeadow Millennium Bridge, and Archbishop's Palace. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh: the [Tickled Trout](https://thetickledtrout.com).

MEP.name: Meopham
MEP.adminWalksAll:
**[Meopham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/meopham-to-rochester/)**: Chalk grassland, ancient woodland, community woodland, landscaped parkland, wildflower meadows, farm fields, country park, and busy road stretches. Sights: [Jeskyns Community Woodland](https://www.forestryengland.uk/jeskyns), [Ashenbank Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/ashenbank-wood), [Cobham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cobham,_Kent), [Cobham Park (Cobham Wood and Mausoleum)](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/cobham-wood-and-mausoleum), Darnley Mausoleum, [Ranscombe Farm Nature Reserve](https://www.plantlife.org.uk/our-work/ranscombe-farm-nature-reserve), [Camer Park](https://explorekent.org/activities/camer-park), St Mary Magdalene Church, New College of Cobham, Nurstead Court, Bronze Age Barrow, Cobham Hall, and Luddesdown Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch in Cobham: the [Leather Bottle](https://www.theleatherbottle.pub/index), the Darnley Arms, or the [Ship Inn](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/kent/ship-inn). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

**Meopham to Rochester**: An alternative variant of [Meopham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/meopham-to-rochester/). Chalk grassland, ancient woodland, community woodland, landscaped parkland, wildflower meadows, motorway viaduct crossing, salt marsh, riverside path, and historic town. Sights: [Jeskyns Community Woodland](https://www.forestryengland.uk/jeskyns), [Ashenbank Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/ashenbank-wood), [Cobham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cobham,_Kent), [Cobham Park (Cobham Wood and Mausoleum)](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/cobham-wood-and-mausoleum), Darnley Mausoleum, [Ranscombe Farm Nature Reserve](https://www.plantlife.org.uk/our-work/ranscombe-farm-nature-reserve), [Medway Viaduct](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Medway_Viaducts), [Rochester](https://www.visitmedway.org/see-and-do/destination-medway/rochester), [Rochester Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/rochester-castle), [Rochester Cathedral](https://www.rochestercathedral.org/visitinginformation), La Providence, Baty's Marsh, St Mary Magdalene Church, New College of Cobham, Bronze Age Barrow, and Cobham Hall. Motorway viaduct crossing. Lunch in Cobham: the [Leather Bottle](https://www.theleatherbottle.pub/index), the Darnley Arms, or the [Ship Inn](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/kent/ship-inn). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

MES.name: Melton
MES.adminWalksAll:
**Melton Circular**: A shorter variant of [Melton to Woodbridge via Sutton Hoo](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/melton-to-woodbridge-via-sutton-hoo/). Lowland heathland, river marshes, estuary walls, reedbeds, and return via England Coast Path. Sights: [Sutton Hoo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sutton_Hoo) and Bromeswell Green Nature Reserve. Lunch in Bromeswell: the [Unruly Pig Pub & Kitchen](https://theunrulypig.co.uk/). 17 km.

**[Melton to Woodbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/melton-to-woodbridge-via-sutton-hoo/)**: Lowland heathland, river marshes, estuary walls, reedbeds, poplar rows, quiet lanes, heathland golf course, and compact riverside town. Sights: [Sutton Hoo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sutton_Hoo), [Woodbridge Tide Mill](https://www.riverdeben.org/), Bromeswell Green Nature Reserve, and Tranmer House. Café in ticketed area. Lunch in Bromeswell, or Lower Ufford. Bromeswell lunch stop: the [Unruly Pig Pub & Kitchen](https://theunrulypig.co.uk/). Lower Ufford lunch stop: the [Ufford White Lion](https://uffordwhitelion.co.uk/). 20 km. 4.5 hours.
MES.publicWalksS2S: **[Melton to Woodbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/melton-to-woodbridge-via-sutton-hoo/)**: Lowland heathland, river marshes, estuary walls, reedbeds, poplar rows, quiet lanes, heathland golf course, and compact riverside town. Sights: [Sutton Hoo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sutton_Hoo), [Woodbridge Tide Mill](https://www.riverdeben.org/), Bromeswell Green Nature Reserve, and Tranmer House. Café in ticketed area. Lunch in Bromeswell, or Lower Ufford. Bromeswell lunch stop: the [Unruly Pig Pub & Kitchen](https://theunrulypig.co.uk/). Lower Ufford lunch stop: the [Ufford White Lion](https://uffordwhitelion.co.uk/). 20 km. 4.5 hours.

MGN.name: Marston Green
MGN.adminWalksAll: **[Marston Green Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-819)**: Suburban paths, airport perimeter fence, woodland, open parkland, country park, lakes, and meadows. Sights: [Aircraft Spotting (Runway 15 viewing area)](https://www.airportspotting.com/where-to-spot-birmingham-airport/), [Sheldon Country Park](https://www.birmingham.gov.uk/sheldonpark), [Elmdon Park](https://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/Elmdon-Nature-Park), Elmdon Hall (remains), St Nicolas's Church, Old Walled Garden (Local Nature Reserve), and [Landrover Experience](https://www.experience.landrover.com). Can be noisy (airport). Lunch in Marston Green, or Sheldon. Marston Green lunch stop: the [Picnic Basket](https://www.thepicnicbasket.co.uk). Sheldon lunch stop: the [Tradita Cafe & restaurant](https://www.tradita.co.uk). 8 km. 2.5 hours.

MHM.name: Merstham
MHM.adminWalksAll:
**Merstham to Tattenham Corner**: Epic scenery sadly ruined by the constant drone of the M25. North Downs ridge, parkland, open heathland, wooded paths, racecourse downland, and London panoramas. Sights: [Gatton Park](https://gattonpark.co.uk), [Reigate Fort](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/reigate-hill-and-gatton-park/features/reigate-fort-at-reigate-hill-and-gatton-park), [Banstead Commons](https://bansteadcommons.org.uk), [First World War Centenary Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/langley-vale-wood), [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets), Millennium Stones, Inglis Folly, and Langley Vale Wood. Race days restrict access. Lunch in Mogador: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). *Last reviewed 5 April 2026.* 15 km. 230 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2863296429)**

**Merstham to Tadworth**: A shorter variant of [Merstham to Tattenham Corner](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/merstham-to-tattenham-corner/). North Downs ridge, parkland, open heathland, and wooded paths. Sights: [Gatton Park](https://gattonpark.co.uk), [Reigate Fort](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/reigate-hill-and-gatton-park/features/reigate-fort-at-reigate-hill-and-gatton-park), and [Banstead Commons](https://bansteadcommons.org.uk). Lunch in Mogador: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). 11 km. 3 hours.

**Merstham to Redhill via Ss Peter and Paul**: A variant of [Redhill Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/redhill-circular/). Grassy embankment, wooded open spaces, wetland lakes, restored sand quarries, restored grassland, wooded paths, farm fields, wooded commons, and suburban streets. Sights: Merstham Bund, Spynes Mere Nature Reserve, Mercers Country Park, Ss Peter and Paul, Nutfield Priory, Royal Earlswood Park, Earlswood Common, Upper Earlswood Lake, Redhill Common, and King George VI Memorial. Road noise near M25. Lunch in Nutfield Marsh, or Nutfield. Nutfield Marsh lunch stop: the [Inn on the Pond](https://www.innonthepond.co.uk). Nutfield lunch stop: the [Queens Head](https://www.queensheadpublichouse.co.uk). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**Merstham to Tattenham Corner via Gatton Park**: A variant of [Merstham to Tattenham Corner](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/merstham-to-tattenham-corner/). North Downs ridge, parkland, open heathland, wooded paths, and direct racecourse crossing. Sights: [Gatton Park](https://gattonpark.co.uk), [Reigate Fort](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/reigate-hill-and-gatton-park/features/reigate-fort-at-reigate-hill-and-gatton-park), [Banstead Commons](https://bansteadcommons.org.uk), and [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets). Race days restrict access. Lunch in Mogador: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). 16 km.

**Merstham to Epsom Downs**: A variant of [Merstham to Tattenham Corner](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/merstham-to-tattenham-corner/). North Downs ridge, parkland, open heathland, racecourse downland, and quiet residential street to Epsom Downs station. Sights: [Gatton Park](https://gattonpark.co.uk), [Reigate Fort](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/reigate-hill-and-gatton-park/features/reigate-fort-at-reigate-hill-and-gatton-park), and [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets). Race days restrict access. Lunch in Mogador: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). 17 km.

**Merstham to Epsom**: A variant of [Merstham to Tattenham Corner](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/merstham-to-tattenham-corner/). North Downs ridge, parkland, open heathland, racecourse downland, and residential streets into Epsom town centre. Sights: [Gatton Park](https://gattonpark.co.uk), [Reigate Fort](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/reigate-hill-and-gatton-park/features/reigate-fort-at-reigate-hill-and-gatton-park), [Banstead Commons](https://bansteadcommons.org.uk), [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets), The Durdans, and Rosebery Park. Race days restrict access. Lunch in Mogador: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Merstham to Redhill via St Mary the Virgin**: A variant of [Redhill Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/redhill-circular/). Grassy embankment, wooded open spaces, wetland lakes, restored sand quarries, Greensand Ridge, farm fields, wooded commons, and suburban streets. Sights: Merstham Bund, Spynes Mere Nature Reserve, Mercers Country Park, St Mary the Virgin, Castle Hill, Nutfield Priory, Royal Earlswood Park, Earlswood Common, Upper Earlswood Lake, Redhill Common, and King George VI Memorial. Road noise near M25. Lunch in Nutfield Marsh, or Bletchingley. Nutfield Marsh lunch stop: the [Inn on the Pond](https://www.innonthepond.co.uk). Bletchingley lunch stops: the [Whyte Harte Hotel](https://whytehartehotel.co.uk), the Lamingtons Tea Room, or the [Red Lion](https://www.redlionbletchingley.co.uk). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Box Hill & Westhumble to Merstham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-to-merstham/)**: North Downs ridge, wooded escarpment, open hilltops, undulating ridge paths, rural farmland, parkland, and golf course. Sights: Box Hill, Reigate Hill, Colley Hill, Gatton Park, and Reigate Fort. Hilly, multiple ascents. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Reigate Hill, or Merstham. Reigate Hill lunch stop: the [Chalk Hare](http://www.chalkhare.co.uk/). Merstham lunch stop: the Feathers. 16 km.

**Tadworth to Merstham**: A shorter variant of [Tattenham Corner Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tattenham-corner-circular/). Heathland, North Downs escarpment with fine views, parkland, and golf course. Sights: Banstead Heath, Tadworth Windmill, Margery Wood, North Downs Way, Colley Hill, Inglis Folly, Memorial Glade, Reigate Fort, Gatton Park, Millennium Stones, Quality Street, and Merstham Village Garden. M25 noise audible. Lunch in Mogador: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). 11 km. 2.5 hours.

**Tattenham Corner to Merstham**: An alternative variant of [Tattenham Corner Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tattenham-corner-circular/). Downland, racecourse gallops, WWI memorial woodland, heathland, North Downs escarpment with fine views, parkland, and golf course. Sights: [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets), [Langley Vale Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/langley-vale-wood), Regiment of Trees, Witness, Jutland Wood, Tadworth Windmill, Banstead Heath, Margery Wood, North Downs Way, Colley Hill, Inglis Folly, Memorial Glade, Reigate Fort, Gatton Park, Millennium Stones, Royal Alexandra & Albert School, Quality Street, and Merstham Village Garden. M25 noise audible. Lunch in Walton on the Hill, or Mogador. Walton on the Hill lunch stops: the [Blue Ball](https://theblueballtadworth.co.uk/food-drink), the Village Café, or the [Bell](http://www.thebell-therat.co.uk). Mogador lunch stop: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Dorking West to Merstham**: A variant of [Box Hill to Merstham and Caterham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-to-merstham/). Vineyard, North Downs ridge, wooded escarpment, open hilltops, and parkland. Sights: Denbies Vineyard, Box Hill, Reigate Hill, Gatton Park, and Reigate Fort. Hilly, multiple ascents. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Reigate Hill, or Merstham. Reigate Hill lunch stop: the [Chalk Hare](http://www.chalkhare.co.uk/). Merstham lunch stop: the Feathers.
MHM.publicWalksS2S: **Merstham to Tattenham Corner**: Epic scenery sadly ruined by the constant drone of the M25. North Downs ridge, parkland, open heathland, wooded paths, racecourse downland, and London panoramas. Sights: [Gatton Park](https://gattonpark.co.uk), [Reigate Fort](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/reigate-hill-and-gatton-park/features/reigate-fort-at-reigate-hill-and-gatton-park), [Banstead Commons](https://bansteadcommons.org.uk), [First World War Centenary Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/langley-vale-wood), [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets), Millennium Stones, Inglis Folly, and Langley Vale Wood. Race days restrict access. Lunch in Mogador: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). 15 km. 230 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2863296429)**

MHR.name: Market Harborough
MHR.adminWalksAll: **[105. Mkt Haboro Circular 9m](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Views and old-railway cycle/walking path. Sights: Brampton Valley Way, Dingley Church, Braybrooke, and Great Oxendon. 15 km. 150 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/105%20Mkt%20Hboro%20Circular%209m.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925764901)**

MIJ.name: Mitcham Junction
MIJ.adminWalksAll:
**[Mitcham Junction to Colliers Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mitcham-junction-to-colliers-wood/)**: Flat urban riverside parks, nature reserves, chalk stream, industrial heritage, and south London commons and green corridors. Sights: [Wandle Valley Regional Park](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk), [Wandle Trail](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk/projects/wandle-trail), [Beddington Farmlands](https://bfnr.org.uk), [Sutton Ecology Centre](https://www.sutton.gov.uk/w/biodiversity-and-wildlife-sutton-ecology-centre), [Honeywood Museum](https://friendsofhoneywood.co.uk/index.html), [Carshalton Water Tower](https://carshaltonwatertower.co.uk/index.html), [Morden Hall Park](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/morden-hall-park), [Deen City Farm](https://www.deencityfarm.co.uk), [Merton Abbey Mills](https://www.mertonabbeymills.org.uk), Carew Manor, Watermeads Nature Reserve, Ravensbury Park, Wilderness Island, Morden Hall, Snuff Mill, and Merton Priory. Lunch in Carshalton: the [Greyhound Hotel](https://www.thegreyhoundhotel.com), the [Hope](https://hopecarshalton.co.uk), or the [Sun](https://www.thesuncarshalton.com/food). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**Mitcham Junction to Morden**: A shorter variant of [Mitcham Junction to Colliers Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mitcham-junction-to-colliers-wood/). Flat urban riverside parks, nature reserves, chalk stream, industrial heritage, and south London commons and green corridors. Sights: [Wandle Valley Regional Park](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk), [Wandle Trail](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk/projects/wandle-trail), [Beddington Farmlands](https://bfnr.org.uk), [Sutton Ecology Centre](https://www.sutton.gov.uk/w/biodiversity-and-wildlife-sutton-ecology-centre), [Honeywood Museum](https://friendsofhoneywood.co.uk/index.html), [Carshalton Water Tower](https://carshaltonwatertower.co.uk/index.html), [Morden Hall Park](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/morden-hall-park), Carew Manor, Watermeads Nature Reserve, Ravensbury Park, and Wilderness Island. Lunch in Carshalton: the [Greyhound Hotel](https://www.thegreyhoundhotel.com), the [Hope](https://hopecarshalton.co.uk), or the [Sun](https://www.thesuncarshalton.com/food). 13 km.
MIJ.publicWalksS2S: **[Mitcham Junction to Colliers Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mitcham-junction-to-colliers-wood/)**: Flat urban riverside parks, nature reserves, chalk stream, industrial heritage, and south London commons and green corridors. Sights: [Wandle Valley Regional Park](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk), [Wandle Trail](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk/projects/wandle-trail), [Beddington Farmlands](https://bfnr.org.uk), [Sutton Ecology Centre](https://www.sutton.gov.uk/w/biodiversity-and-wildlife-sutton-ecology-centre), [Honeywood Museum](https://friendsofhoneywood.co.uk/index.html), [Carshalton Water Tower](https://carshaltonwatertower.co.uk/index.html), [Morden Hall Park](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/morden-hall-park), [Deen City Farm](https://www.deencityfarm.co.uk), [Merton Abbey Mills](https://www.mertonabbeymills.org.uk), Carew Manor, Watermeads Nature Reserve, Ravensbury Park, Wilderness Island, Morden Hall, Snuff Mill, and Merton Priory. Lunch in Carshalton: the [Greyhound Hotel](https://www.thegreyhoundhotel.com), the [Hope](https://hopecarshalton.co.uk), or the [Sun](https://www.thesuncarshalton.com/food). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

MIM.name: Moreton-in-Marsh
MIM.adminWalksAll:
**[Moreton-in-Marsh Circular via Longborough Opera House](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/moreton-in-marsh-circular/)**: Gentle Cotswold hills, open fields, ridge with fine views, and honey-coloured villages. Sights: Longborough Opera House and Blockley. Lunch in Longborough: the [Coach and Horses Inn](http://coachnhorseslongborough.co.uk). 20 km.

**[Moreton-in-Marsh Circular via Chipping Campden](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/moreton-in-marsh-circular/)**: Gentle rolling Cotswold hills, sloping fields, honey-coloured villages, picturesque valleys, and dramatic dips. Sights: Chipping Campden and Blockley. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chipping Campden: the [Eight Bells](https://www.eightbellsinn.co.uk/). 25 km.

**[Moreton-in-Marsh Circular via the Escarpement](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/moreton-in-marsh-circular-via-the-escarpment/)**: Cotswold hills, escarpment, open fields, bucolic villages, racehorse training facility, Cotswold stone quarry, and long-grass paths in summer. Sights: Redesdale Market Hall, Curfew Tower, Stanway Watermill, Stanway House, Snowshill Manor, Monarch's Way, and Cotswold Way. Can be overgrown. Lunch in Ford, or Stanton. Ford lunch stop: the [Plough Inn](http://theploughinnford.co.uk). Stanton lunch stop: the [Mount Inn](http://themountinn.co.uk). 42 km.

**Moreton-in-Marsh Circular via Broad Campden**: A shorter variant of [Moreton-in-Marsh Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/moreton-in-marsh-circular/). Gentle Cotswold hills, rolling fields, and honey-coloured villages including Paxford and Broad Campden. Sights: Broad Campden and Blockley. Lunch in Paxford, or Broad Campden. Paxford lunch stop: the [Churchill Arms](https://www.churchillarms.co/). Broad Campden lunch stop: the [Bakers Arms](https://www.bakersarmscampden.com/). 20 km.

**Moreton-in-Marsh Circular via Dover's Hill**: A longer variant of [Moreton-in-Marsh Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/moreton-in-marsh-circular/). Gentle rolling Cotswold hills, sloping fields, honey-coloured villages, escarpment views, and Dover's Hill. Sights: Dover's Hill, Chipping Campden, and Blockley. Lunch in Chipping Campden: the [Eight Bells](https://www.eightbellsinn.co.uk/). 28 km.

**[Kingham to Moreton-in-Marsh via Adelstrop](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kingham-to-moreton-in-marsh-via-adelstrop/)**: Fields near river Evenlode, quiet tarmac lanes, flower-filled meadows, gentle hills, and picturesque Cotswolds villages and churches. Sights: St Nicholas Church, Adelstrop village and station sign, [St Mary Magdalene Church](http://www.britainexpress.com/counties/glouces/churches/Adlestrop.htm), [Chastleton House](http://www.oxfordshirecotswolds.org/things-to-do/attractions/chastleton-house-p51773), and St Mary's Church, Chastleton. Can be boggy. Best in Jun and Aug. Lunch in Lower Oddington, or Upper Oddington. Lower Oddington lunch stop: the [Fox Inn](https://thefoxatoddington.com/). Upper Oddington lunch stop: the [Horse and Groom](https://www.horseandgroomcotswolds.co.uk/). 17 km. 5 hours.

Sights: Longborough Opera House and Blockley. Lunch in Longborough: the [Coach and Horses Inn](http://coachnhorseslongborough.co.uk). 20 km.

Ford lunch stop: the [Plough Inn](http://theploughinnford.co.uk). Stanton lunch stop: the [Mount Inn](http://themountinn.co.uk). 42 km.
