Chalk paths can be slippery. Lunch in Godstone: the [Hare & Hounds](https://www.hareandhoundsgodstone.co.uk), the [White Hart](https://www.thewhitehartongodstonegreen.com), or the [Bell Inn](https://www.thebellgodstone.co.uk). 16 km. 4 hours.

**[Earlswood to Oxted](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/earlswood-to-oxted/)**: Gentle greensand ridge, pasture, arable fields, woodland, bluebell woods, escarpment views, and low-lying meadows. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Bletchingley, or Tandridge. Bletchingley lunch stops: the Red Lion, the Whyte Hart Hotel, or the Lamingtons Tea Room. Tandridge lunch stop: the Barley Mow. 16 km.

**[Godstone to Oxted via Tandridge Yew](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/godstone-to-oxted/)**: Low-lying farmland, river valley, woodland, low ridges, golf course, ancient churchyards, and old coaching village. Sights: [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), [Tandridge Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/tandridge-churchyard-765), [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), St George's Church Crowhurst, St Peter's Church Tandridge, Lagham Manor, Greenwich Meridian Trail, Greensand Way, Oxted Mill, and St Silvan's House. Can be muddy. Lunch in Staffhurst Wood: the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted). 17 km. 4.5 hours.

PAD.name: Paddington
PAD.adminWalksAll:
**Paddington to Charing Cross**: A shorter variant of [The London Stations](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-stations/). Flat urban streets, Hyde Park, Kensington Gardens, Belgravia, and St James's Park. Sights: Paddington station, Elizabeth Line station, Kensington Gardens, Italian Gardens, Serpentine, Serpentine Lido, Princess Diana Memorial, Hyde Park, Kensington Palace, Buckingham Palace, Queen's Gallery, St James's Park, Horseguard's Parade, Banqueting House, Victoria station, Trafalgar Square, Nelson's Column, and Charing Cross station. Lunch in Hyde Park, or St James's Park. Hyde Park lunch stop: the Lido Cafe. St James's Park lunch stop: the St James's Cafe. 7 km.

**Kings Cross to Paddington**: A shorter variant of [The London Stations](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-stations/). Flat urban streets, canal towpath, and Regent's Park. Sights: Kings Cross station, St Pancras station, Granary Square, Crick Institute, Euston station, Regent's Park, Queen Mary's Gardens, Regent's Park Open Air Theatre, Marylebone station, Paddington Basin, and Paddington station. Lunch in Regent's Park, or Marylebone. Regent's Park lunch stop: the Garden Cafe. Marylebone lunch stop: the Gino's Cafe Bar. 5 km.
PAD.publicWalksS2S: **Paddington to Charing Cross**: A shorter variant of [The London Stations](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-stations/). Flat urban streets, Hyde Park, Kensington Gardens, Belgravia, and St James's Park. Sights: Paddington station, Elizabeth Line station, Kensington Gardens, Italian Gardens, Serpentine, Serpentine Lido, Princess Diana Memorial, Hyde Park, Kensington Palace, Buckingham Palace, Queen's Gallery, St James's Park, Horseguard's Parade, Banqueting House, Victoria station, Trafalgar Square, Nelson's Column, and Charing Cross station. Lunch in Hyde Park, or St James's Park. Hyde Park lunch stop: the Lido Cafe. St James's Park lunch stop: the St James's Cafe. 7 km.
PAD.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Kings Cross to Paddington**: A shorter variant of [The London Stations](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-stations/). Flat urban streets, canal towpath, and Regent's Park. Sights: Kings Cross station, St Pancras station, Granary Square, Crick Institute, Euston station, Regent's Park, Queen Mary's Gardens, Regent's Park Open Air Theatre, Marylebone station, Paddington Basin, and Paddington station. Lunch in Regent's Park, or Marylebone. Regent's Park lunch stop: the Garden Cafe. Marylebone lunch stop: the Gino's Cafe Bar. 5 km.

PAN.name: Pangbourne
PAN.adminWalksAll:
**Pangbourne Circular via Cold Harbour**: River Thames valley, wooded hillsides, chalk nature reserve, open fields, quiet lanes, and pretty villages. Sights: Whitchurch Toll Bridge, St Mary's Church, Hartslock Nature Reserve, Great Chalk Wood, Church of St John the Baptist, Modern Artists Gallery, George Hotel, and Elephant Hotel. Can be muddy. Note that the lunch stops listed are at the start/end station rather than at the natural midpoint of the route. Lunch in Pangbourne, or Whitchurch. Pangbourne lunch stop: the Swan. Whitchurch lunch stop: the Greyhound. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 10 km. 150 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920257524)**

**Pangbourne to Goring via Streatley Hill**: Rambler favourite! Woods, hilly, river, and quaint village. Relatively busy area. Plenty of lunch venues in Goring. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 11 October 2025.* 12 km. 190 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2618431014)**

**[Pangbourne Circular via Goring Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pangbourne-via-goring-circular/)**: Part-wooded ridges, open pastures, wildflower nature reserve, Thames river meadows, beech woods, and Thames-side villages. Sights: Whitchurch Bridge, Hartslock Nature Reserve, Hartslock Wood, Goring Gap, Mapledurham Estate, and [YHA Streatley](http://www.yha.org.uk/hostel/streatley). Can be muddy. Lunch in Whitchurch Hill, Goring-on-Thames, or Streatley. Whitchurch Hill lunch stop: the [Sun Inn](https://www.thesunwhitchurchhill.co.uk/). Goring-on-Thames lunch stops: the [Catherine Wheel](http://www.tcwgoring.co.uk/index), the [Chocolate Café](https://www.thechocolatecafeclub.co.uk/goring/), the [Miller of Mansfield](https://themillerofmansfield.com/), or the [Pierreponts Café](http://pierreponts.co.uk/). Streatley lunch stop: the [Swan at Streatley](https://www.coppaclub.co.uk/theswanatstreatley/). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Pangbourne Circular via Whitchurch Toll Bridge**: River Thames valley, wooded hillsides, chalk nature reserve, open fields, and quiet lanes. Sights: Whitchurch Toll Bridge, St Mary's Church, Hartslock Nature Reserve, and Great Chalk Wood. Can be muddy. Lunch in Pangbourne: the Swan. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 10 km.

**Pangbourne to Goring & Streatley**: A shorter variant of [Pangbourne via Goring Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pangbourne-via-goring-circular/). Part-wooded ridges, open pastures, wildflower nature reserve, and Thames-side village. Sights: Whitchurch Bridge, Hartslock Nature Reserve, and Goring Gap. Lunch in Goring-on-Thames: the [Catherine Wheel](http://www.tcwgoring.co.uk/index), the [Chocolate Café](https://www.thechocolatecafeclub.co.uk/goring/), the [Miller of Mansfield](https://themillerofmansfield.com/), or the [Pierreponts Café](http://pierreponts.co.uk/). 8 km.

**Pangbourne to Tilehurst**: A longer variant of [Pangbourne via Goring Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pangbourne-via-goring-circular/). Part-wooded ridges, river meadows, Thames Path, wooded hills, and Thames-side villages. Sights: Whitchurch Bridge, Hartslock Nature Reserve, Mapledurham Estate, and Goring Gap. Lunch in Goring-on-Thames, or Streatley. Goring-on-Thames lunch stops: the [Catherine Wheel](http://www.tcwgoring.co.uk/index), the [Chocolate Café](https://www.thechocolatecafeclub.co.uk/goring/), the [Miller of Mansfield](https://themillerofmansfield.com/), or the [Pierreponts Café](http://pierreponts.co.uk/). Streatley lunch stop: the [Swan at Streatley](https://www.coppaclub.co.uk/theswanatstreatley/). 21 km.

**Henley-on-Thames to Pangbourne**: Rambler favourite! Thames riverside, ancient streets, broad valleys, beech woods, fields, small villages, alpaca farm, and toll bridge. Sights: St Nicholas' Church, All Saints Church, Bozedown Alpaca Farm, Whitchurch Toll Bridge, and Parish Church of Whitchurch-on-Thames. Can be muddy. Lunch in Rotherfield Greys, Peppard Common, or Kingwood. Rotherfield Greys lunch stop: the [Maltster's Arms](http://www.maltstersarmsrotherfield.co.uk/). Peppard Common lunch stop: the [Red Lion](https://www.theredlionpub.uk/). Kingwood lunch stop: the [Unicorn](http://www.unicornkingwood.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 19 km. 5.5 hours.
PAN.publicWalksCircular: **Pangbourne Circular via Cold Harbour**: River Thames valley, wooded hillsides, chalk nature reserve, open fields, quiet lanes, and pretty villages. Sights: Whitchurch Toll Bridge, St Mary's Church, Hartslock Nature Reserve, Great Chalk Wood, Church of St John the Baptist, Modern Artists Gallery, George Hotel, and Elephant Hotel. Can be muddy. Note that the lunch stops listed are at the start/end station rather than at the natural midpoint of the route. Lunch in Pangbourne, or Whitchurch. Pangbourne lunch stop: the Swan. Whitchurch lunch stop: the Greyhound. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 10 km. 150 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920257524)**
PAN.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Pangbourne to Goring via Streatley Hill**: Rambler favourite! Woods, hilly, river, and quaint village. Relatively busy area. Plenty of lunch venues in Goring. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 12 km. 190 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2618431014)**

PBR.name: Potters Bar
PBR.adminWalksAll: **Potters Bar Circular via Gobions Wood**: Almost worthy because of the great and tranquil beauty of Gobions Wood, and it being only 20m from Kings Cross on a quick train. Unfortunately the surrounding fields and busy commuter roads that make up the bulk of the area don’t have enough going for them to put this on the recommendation list. Sights: [Gobions Wood](https://www.hertswildlifetrust.org.uk/nature-reserves/gobions-wood). 8 km. 60 m uphill. 2.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925781855)**
PBR.publicWalksCircular: **Potters Bar Circular via Gobions Wood**: Almost worthy because of the great and tranquil beauty of Gobions Wood, and it being only 20m from Kings Cross on a quick train. Unfortunately the surrounding fields and busy commuter roads that make up the bulk of the area don’t have enough going for them to put this on the recommendation list. Sights: [Gobions Wood](https://www.hertswildlifetrust.org.uk/nature-reserves/gobions-wood). 8 km. 60 m uphill. 2.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925781855)**

PDW.name: Paddock Wood
PDW.adminWalksAll:
**[Paddock Wood to Yalding via St Mary, Hunton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/paddock-wood-to-yalding/)**: Flat flood plain, riverside meadows, tree-lined field edges, mixed woods, open countryside, village streets, and gentle Greensand Ridge slopes. Sights: [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), [Yalding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yalding), Twyford Bridge, Yalding Lees, Ss Peter and Paul, Yalding, Yalding Town Bridge, Warde's Moat, St Mary, Hunton, Greensand Way, Medway Valley Walk, Cheveney Mill, and Yalding Fen. Lunch in Twyford Bridge: the [Boathouse](https://www.boathouseyalding.co.uk). 18 km. 4 hours.

**Paddock Wood to Yalding via River Medway**: A shorter variant of [Paddock Wood to Yalding](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/paddock-wood-to-yalding/). Flat flood plain, riverside meadows, tree-lined field edges, mixed woods, open countryside, and village streets. Sights: [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), [Yalding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yalding), Twyford Bridge, Yalding Lees, Ss Peter and Paul, Yalding, Yalding Town Bridge, Warde's Moat, Medway Valley Walk, Cheveney Mill, and Yalding Fen. Lunch in Twyford Bridge: the [Boathouse](https://www.boathouseyalding.co.uk). 12 km. 2.5 hours.

**Paddock Wood to Wateringbury via River Medway**: A longer variant of [Paddock Wood to Yalding](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/paddock-wood-to-yalding/). Flat flood plain, riverside meadows, tree-lined field edges, mixed woods, open countryside, village streets, and extended riverside path to Wateringbury. Sights: [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), [Yalding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yalding), Twyford Bridge, Yalding Lees, Ss Peter and Paul, Yalding, Yalding Town Bridge, Warde's Moat, Medway Valley Walk, Cheveney Mill, Yalding Fen, Kenward House, Nettlestead Place, and St Mary the Virgin, Nettlestead. Lunch in Twyford Bridge: the [Boathouse](https://www.boathouseyalding.co.uk). 15 km.

**Paddock Wood to Wateringbury via St Mary, Hunton**: A longer variant of [Paddock Wood to Yalding](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/paddock-wood-to-yalding/). Flat flood plain, riverside meadows, tree-lined field edges, mixed woods, open countryside, village streets, Greensand Ridge slopes, and extended riverside path to Wateringbury. Sights: [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), [Yalding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yalding), Twyford Bridge, Yalding Lees, Ss Peter and Paul, Yalding, Yalding Town Bridge, Warde's Moat, St Mary, Hunton, Greensand Way, Medway Valley Walk, Cheveney Mill, Yalding Fen, Kenward House, Nettlestead Place, and St Mary the Virgin, Nettlestead. Lunch in Twyford Bridge: the [Boathouse](https://www.boathouseyalding.co.uk). 20 km.

**Yalding to Paddock Wood**: A longer variant of [Yalding Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-circular/). Flat riverside paths, farmland, meadows, woods, and quiet lanes. Sights: [Teston Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teston_Bridge), [Nettlestead Church](http://nettlesteadchurch.co.uk/), [Teston Bridge Country Park](http://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/teston-bridge-country-park), and [Tea Pot Island](http://www.teapotisland.co.uk/). Lunch in West Farleigh, or East Peckham. West Farleigh lunch stop: the [Tickled Trout](http://www.thetickledtrout.com/). East Peckham lunch stop: the [Man of Kent](http://themanofkentpub.com/).
PDW.publicWalksS2S: **[Paddock Wood to Yalding via St Mary, Hunton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/paddock-wood-to-yalding/)**: Flat flood plain, riverside meadows, tree-lined field edges, mixed woods, open countryside, village streets, and gentle Greensand Ridge slopes. Sights: [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), [Yalding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yalding), Twyford Bridge, Yalding Lees, Ss Peter and Paul, Yalding, Yalding Town Bridge, Warde's Moat, St Mary, Hunton, Greensand Way, Medway Valley Walk, Cheveney Mill, and Yalding Fen. Lunch in Twyford Bridge: the [Boathouse](https://www.boathouseyalding.co.uk). 18 km. 4 hours.

PET.name: Petts Wood
PET.adminWalksAll: **[Petts Wood Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/petts-wood-circular/)**: Suburban woodlands, wide woodland tracks, heathland, valleys, moated manor ruins, and open green spaces. Sights: [Scadbury Park](https://www.scadbury-park.org.uk/), [Chislehurst Caves](http://chislehurst-caves.co.uk/), William Willett Memorial Sundial, Scadbury Park Moated Manor House Ruins, and St Nicholas Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chislehurst: the [Bull's Head](https://www.thebullsheadhotel.co.uk/). 12 km.
PET.publicWalksCircular: **[Petts Wood Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/petts-wood-circular/)**: Suburban woodlands, wide woodland tracks, heathland, valleys, moated manor ruins, and open green spaces. Sights: [Scadbury Park](https://www.scadbury-park.org.uk/), [Chislehurst Caves](http://chislehurst-caves.co.uk/), William Willett Memorial Sundial, Scadbury Park Moated Manor House Ruins, and St Nicholas Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chislehurst: the [Bull's Head](https://www.thebullsheadhotel.co.uk/). 12 km.

PEV.name: Pevensey and Westham
PEV.adminWalksAll:
**Pevensey to Battle**: An essential walk! Sights: [Battle Abbey](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/1066-battle-of-hastings-abbey-and-battlefield/) (the site of the Battle of Hastings, upon which William the Conqeuror built his mighty Battle Abbey in papally-imposed contrition for the blood he had shed). From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 1 May 2023.* 28 km. 350 m uphill. 7.5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920345301)**

**Pevensey and Westham to Eastbourne**: A shorter variant of [Eastbourne to Hastings via Bexhill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eastbourne-to-hastings-via-bexhill/). Coastal path south-west from Pevensey, seafront promenade, sandy beach, and Eastbourne pier. Sights: [Pevensey Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/daysout/properties/pevensey-castle/) and [Eastbourne Pier](http://www.eastbournepier.com/). Lunch in Pevensey Bay: the Aqua Bar, or the [Castle Inn](http://castleinnpevenseybay.co.uk/). 9 km.
PEV.publicWalksS2S: **Pevensey to Battle**: An essential walk! Sights: [Battle Abbey](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/1066-battle-of-hastings-abbey-and-battlefield/) (the site of the Battle of Hastings, upon which William the Conqeuror built his mighty Battle Abbey in papally-imposed contrition for the blood he had shed). From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 28 km. 350 m uphill. 7.5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920345301)**

PEW.name: Pewsey
PEW.adminWalksAll:
**[Pewsey Circular via Giant's Grave](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pewsey-circular/)**: Chalk downland escarpments, open ridges, steep serrated downs, Vale of Pewsey farmland, canal towpath, prehistoric earthworks, and hillforts. Sights: Wansdyke, Milk Hill, Alton Barnes White Horse, Kennet & Avon Canal, Giant's Grave, and Pewsey Downs. Lunch in Honeystreet: the [Honeystreet Mill Café](https://www.honeystreetmill.co.uk/about-3), or the [Barge Inn](https://thebargeinnhoneystreet.uk/). 27 km. 6 hours.

**[Pewsey Circular via Avebury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pewsey-circular-via-avebury/)**: Open Wiltshire downland, Marlborough Downs ridges, bluebell woods, sarsen stone fields, ancient earthworks, Vale of Pewsey, and canal towpath. Sights: Fyfield Down Sarsen Stones Field, Avebury Standing Stones, Avebury Bank & Ditch Earthworks, Silbury Hill, West Kennett Long Barrow, The Sanctuary, Windmill Hill, West Woods, Wansdyke, Milk Hill, and Kennet & Avon Canal. Lunch in Lockeridge, or Avebury. Lockeridge lunch stop: the [Who'd A Thought It](http://www.thewhodathoughtit.co.uk/index). Avebury lunch stops: the [Circles Restaurant (at Avebury Museum)](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/wiltshire/avebury), or the [Red Lion](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/wiltshire/red-lion). 36 km. 8.5 hours.

**Pewsey Circular via Wansdyke**: A shorter variant of [Pewsey Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pewsey-circular/). Chalk downland escarpments, open ridges, Vale of Pewsey farmland, canal towpath, and prehistoric earthworks. Sights: Wansdyke, Milk Hill, Alton Barnes White Horse, and Kennet & Avon Canal. Lunch in Honeystreet: the [Honeystreet Mill Café](https://www.honeystreetmill.co.uk/about-3), or the [Barge Inn](https://thebargeinnhoneystreet.uk/). 19 km.
PEW.publicWalksCircular:
**[Pewsey Circular via Giant's Grave](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pewsey-circular/)**: Chalk downland escarpments, open ridges, steep serrated downs, Vale of Pewsey farmland, canal towpath, prehistoric earthworks, and hillforts. Sights: Wansdyke, Milk Hill, Alton Barnes White Horse, Kennet & Avon Canal, Giant's Grave, and Pewsey Downs. Lunch in Honeystreet: the [Honeystreet Mill Café](https://www.honeystreetmill.co.uk/about-3), or the [Barge Inn](https://thebargeinnhoneystreet.uk/). 27 km. 6 hours.

**[Pewsey Circular via Avebury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pewsey-circular-via-avebury/)**: Open Wiltshire downland, Marlborough Downs ridges, bluebell woods, sarsen stone fields, ancient earthworks, Vale of Pewsey, and canal towpath. Sights: Fyfield Down Sarsen Stones Field, Avebury Standing Stones, Avebury Bank & Ditch Earthworks, Silbury Hill, West Kennett Long Barrow, The Sanctuary, Windmill Hill, West Woods, Wansdyke, Milk Hill, and Kennet & Avon Canal. Lunch in Lockeridge, or Avebury. Lockeridge lunch stop: the [Who'd A Thought It](http://www.thewhodathoughtit.co.uk/index). Avebury lunch stops: the [Circles Restaurant (at Avebury Museum)](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/wiltshire/avebury), or the [Red Lion](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/wiltshire/red-lion). 36 km. 8.5 hours.

PFL.name: Purfleet
PFL.adminWalksAll:
**[Rainham to Purfleet via RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rainham-to-purfleet-via-rspb-rainham-marshes/)**: Completely flat, tarmac and hard surfaces, riverside sea wall, reed beds, marshes, and industrial-edge landscape with Thames views. Sights: [RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/rainham-marshes), Concrete Barges, The Diver, Purfleet Heritage Museum, Purfleet Heritage Park, Coldharbour Point beacon, [London LOOP Section 24](https://tfl.gov.uk/modes/walking/loop-walk), and [NCN Route 13](https://www.sustrans.org.uk/find-a-route-on-the-national-cycle-network/route-13/). Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Feb. Lunch in Purfleet: the [Royal Hotel Purfleet](http://theroyalpurfleet.co.uk/). 12 km. 3 hours.

**Rainham to Purfleet via Rainham Hall**: A shorter variant of [Rainham to Grays](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rainham-to-grays/). Flat riverside path, marshland, RSPB nature reserve, and Thames views. Sights: [Rainham Hall](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/rainham-hall/), [St Helen's and St Giles' Church](http://www.essexchurches.info/church.asp?p=Rainham), [Rainham Marshes RSPB Nature Reserve](http://www.rspb.org.uk/reserves/guide/r/rainhammarshes/), Purfleet Heritage Museum, Royal Hotel, and Concrete barges. Lunch in Purfleet: the RSPB Café, or the Royal Hotel. 8 km.
PFL.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Rainham to Purfleet via RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rainham-to-purfleet-via-rspb-rainham-marshes/)**: Completely flat, tarmac and hard surfaces, riverside sea wall, reed beds, marshes, and industrial-edge landscape with Thames views. Sights: [RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/rainham-marshes), Concrete Barges, The Diver, Purfleet Heritage Museum, Purfleet Heritage Park, Coldharbour Point beacon, [London LOOP Section 24](https://tfl.gov.uk/modes/walking/loop-walk), and [NCN Route 13](https://www.sustrans.org.uk/find-a-route-on-the-national-cycle-network/route-13/). Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Feb. Lunch in Purfleet: the [Royal Hotel Purfleet](http://theroyalpurfleet.co.uk/). 12 km. 3 hours.

PHR.name: Penshurst
PHR.privateNote:
LON Trainline says 56m — London Bridge via Tonbridge

CLJ>PHR issue. App says 1 hour and 49 minutes from Clapham Junction. Two changes: London Bridge and Tonbridge. But trainline says  1h7 CLJ>Redhill>PHR
PHR.adminWalksAll:
**Penshurst Circular via Chiddingstone**: Rambler favourite! Stately home. Sights: [Chiddingstone](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/chiddingstone-village) (lovely medieval village). Multiple cafés & restaurants in Chiddingstone. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 26 July 2025.* 14 km. 130 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/251970499)**

**Hurst Green to Penshurst**: 21 km. 350 m uphill. 6 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2934416642)**

**[Penshurst Circular via Bough Beech](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/penshurst-circular/)**: Low-lying farmland, woodland, reservoir causeway, field edges, river meadows, and attractive Kent villages. Sights: [Bore Place](https://www.boreplace.org/visit/walks-at-bore-place), Bough Beech Reservoir, [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), Chiding Stone, St Luke, Chiddingstone Causeway, St Mary, Chiddingstone, Larkin's Brewery, Penshurst Platform Gallery, Whistler's Farmhouse, and Penshurst Airfield. Can be muddy. Lunch in Bough Beech, or Chiddingstone. Bough Beech lunch stop: the [Wheatsheaf](https://www.wheatsheafboughbeech.co.uk). Chiddingstone lunch stop: the [Castle Inn](https://thecastleinnchiddingstone.co.uk). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**Penshurst Circular via Chiddingstone Village**: An alternative variant of [Leigh and Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-and-penshurst/). Rambler favourite! Low-lying farmland, Eden valley, woods, vineyard, and picturesque village streets. Sights: [Chiddingstone Village](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/chiddingstone-village), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), and Harden Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hoath Corner: the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com). 12 km. 3 hours.

**Penshurst Circular via Penshurst Place**: A shorter variant of [Leigh and Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-and-penshurst/). Rambler favourite! Low-lying Eden valley farmland and village streets. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com). Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk). 9 km.

**Penshurst Circular via Bough Beech & Hever Castle Golf Course**: A longer variant of [Penshurst Circular via Bough Beech](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/penshurst-circular/). Low-lying farmland, woodland, golf course, reservoir paths, river meadows, and Chiddingstone Nature Reserve. Sights: [Bore Place](https://www.boreplace.org/visit/walks-at-bore-place), Bough Beech Reservoir, Whistler's Farmhouse, Hever Castle Golf Course, St Paul, Four Elms, St Luke, Chiddingstone Causeway, Chiddingstone Nature Reserve, and Penshurst Platform Gallery. Can be muddy. Lunch in Four Elms, or Bough Beech. Four Elms lunch stop: the Four Elms Inn. Bough Beech lunch stop: the [Wheatsheaf](https://www.wheatsheafboughbeech.co.uk). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

**Penshurst to Leigh**: An alternative variant of [Leigh and Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-and-penshurst/). Rambler favourite! Low-lying Eden valley farmland, gentle ridges, woods, vineyard, and hamlet lanes. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Village](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/chiddingstone-village), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), and Harden Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hoath Corner: the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Leigh to Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-and-penshurst/)**: Rambler favourite! Parkland, river valley, gentle farmland slopes, vineyard, woods, and hamlet lanes. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Village](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/chiddingstone-village), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), St John the Baptist, and Harden Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hoath Corner: the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com). 13 km. 3 hours.

**[Tonbridge to Penshurst via Harden Vineyard](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-to-penshurst/)**: Lakes, water meadows, riverside paths, low hills, orchards, vineyards, farmland, and picturesque villages. Sights: [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, Harden Vineyard, Larkin's Brewery, St Mary, Chiddingstone, Chiding Stone, and Penshurst Platform Gallery. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst, or Hoath Corner. Penshurst lunch stops: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). Hoath Corner lunch stop: the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com). 18 km. 4 hours.

**Hever to Penshurst via Penshurst Place**: A shorter variant of [Hever to Tonbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-tonbridge/). Farmland, river valley, parkland, and historic village. Sights: [Hever Castle](https://www.hevercastle.co.uk), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), St Peter's Church Hever, St John the Baptist Church Penshurst, Leicester Square Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Chippens Bank House, and Withers. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). 12 km. 3 hours.

**Tonbridge to Penshurst via Haysden Country Park**: A shorter variant of [Tonbridge to Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-to-penshurst/). Lakes, water meadows, riverside paths, Eden Valley Walk, farmland, and picturesque villages. Sights: [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, St Mary, Chiddingstone, Chiding Stone, and Penshurst Platform Gallery. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). 15 km.

**Hildenborough to Penshurst**: An alternative variant of [Tonbridge to Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-to-penshurst/). Roadside walking, fields, parkland, riverside paths, low hills, vineyards, and farmland. Sights: Hall Place, St Mary, Leigh, [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), Harden Vineyard, St Mary, Chiddingstone, Chiding Stone, and Penshurst Platform Gallery. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst, or Hoath Corner. Penshurst lunch stops: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). Hoath Corner lunch stop: the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Hever to Penshurst via Chiddingstone Castle**: Fields, woods, and country lanes across to Penshurst Station. Sights: [Hever Castle](http://www.hevercastle.co.uk/), Chiddingstone Castle, and St Mary's Church. Lunch in Chiddingstone: the [Castle Inn](http://www.thecastleinnchiddingstone.com/). An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).
PHR.publicWalksCircular: **Penshurst Circular via Chiddingstone**: Rambler favourite! Stately home. Sights: [Chiddingstone](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/chiddingstone-village) (lovely medieval village). Multiple cafés & restaurants in Chiddingstone. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 14 km. 130 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/251970499)**
PHR.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Hurst Green to Penshurst**: 21 km. 350 m uphill. 6 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2934416642)**

PKT.name: Park Street
PKT.adminWalksAll:
**Park Street to Bricket Wood**: An alternative variant of [Abbey Trail](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/). 4 km.

**Park Street to How Wood**: An alternative variant of [Abbey Trail](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/).

**St Albans Abbey to Park Street**: An alternative variant of [Abbey Trail](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/). 4 km.
PKT.publicWalksS2S:
**Park Street to Bricket Wood**: An alternative variant of [Abbey Trail](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/). 4 km.

**Park Street to How Wood**: An alternative variant of [Abbey Trail](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/).
PKT.publicWalksS2SEnding: **St Albans Abbey to Park Street**: An alternative variant of [Abbey Trail](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/). 4 km.

PLC.name: Pluckley
PLC.adminWalksAll:
**Pluckley Circular via Dering windows**: Flat Low Weald farmland, orchards, quiet lanes, ancient pubs, quaint Kent villages, and a fine viewpoint near Pluckley. Sights: Dering windows, St Nicholas Church, St Mary the Virgin, Pluckley Station, and H E Bates' home in Little Chart Forstal. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Little Chart, or Pluckley. Little Chart lunch stop: the Swan Inn. Pluckley lunch stops: the Black Horse, or the Dering Arms. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 11 km.

**[Pluckley to Ashford International](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pluckley-to-ashford/)**: Pastures, orchards, woods, commons, bogs, parkland estate, and riverside parks into a town centre. Sights: Godinton Park, Hothfield Bogs, and Sheerland House. Lunch in Pluckley, Little Chart, Great Chart, or Ashford. Pluckley lunch stops: the [Dering Arms](http://www.deringarms.com/), or the [Black Horse](https://theblackhorseatpluckley.co.uk/). Little Chart lunch stop: the [Swan Inn](https://www.theswaninnlittlechart.co.uk/index). Great Chart lunch stop: the [Swan and Dog](https://www.swananddog.co.uk/). Ashford lunch stops: the [Curious Brewery Taproom & Restaurant](https://curiousbrewery.com/taproom-restaurant/), or the [Everest Inn](https://everestinnkent.co.uk/). 16 km.

**Pluckley to Ham Street**: A longer variant of [Pluckley to Ashford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pluckley-to-ashford/). Pastures, orchards, woods, commons, bogs, parkland estate, and continuing Greensand Way to Ham Street. Sights: Godinton Park and Hothfield Bogs. Lunch in Pluckley, Little Chart, or Great Chart. Pluckley lunch stops: the [Dering Arms](http://www.deringarms.com/), or the [Black Horse](https://theblackhorseatpluckley.co.uk/). Little Chart lunch stop: the [Swan Inn](https://www.theswaninnlittlechart.co.uk/index). Great Chart lunch stop: the [Swan and Dog](https://www.swananddog.co.uk/). 25 km.

**[Harrietsham to Pluckley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/harrietsham-to-pluckley/)**: Gentle ridge, apple orchards, pasture, escarpment views, bluebell woods, Kentish farmland, and churches attached to farms and manor houses. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Fairbourne Heath: the Pepper Box Inn. 15 km.
PLC.publicWalksS2S: **[Pluckley to Ashford International](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pluckley-to-ashford/)**: Pastures, orchards, woods, commons, bogs, parkland estate, and riverside parks into a town centre. Sights: Godinton Park, Hothfield Bogs, and Sheerland House. Lunch in Pluckley, Little Chart, Great Chart, or Ashford. Pluckley lunch stops: the [Dering Arms](http://www.deringarms.com/), or the [Black Horse](https://theblackhorseatpluckley.co.uk/). Little Chart lunch stop: the [Swan Inn](https://www.theswaninnlittlechart.co.uk/index). Great Chart lunch stop: the [Swan and Dog](https://www.swananddog.co.uk/). Ashford lunch stops: the [Curious Brewery Taproom & Restaurant](https://curiousbrewery.com/taproom-restaurant/), or the [Everest Inn](https://everestinnkent.co.uk/). 16 km.
PLC.publicWalksCircular: **Pluckley Circular via Dering windows**: Flat Low Weald farmland, orchards, quiet lanes, ancient pubs, quaint Kent villages, and a fine viewpoint near Pluckley. Sights: Dering windows, St Nicholas Church, St Mary the Virgin, Pluckley Station, and H E Bates' home in Little Chart Forstal. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Little Chart, or Pluckley. Little Chart lunch stop: the Swan Inn. Pluckley lunch stops: the Black Horse, or the Dering Arms. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 11 km.
PLC.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Harrietsham to Pluckley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/harrietsham-to-pluckley/)**: Gentle ridge, apple orchards, pasture, escarpment views, bluebell woods, Kentish farmland, and churches attached to farms and manor houses. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Fairbourne Heath: the Pepper Box Inn. 15 km.

PLD.name: Portslade
PLD.adminWalksAll: **Newhaven Town to Portslade (via Brighton and Hove)**: A longer variant of [Newhaven to Brighton and Portslade](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/newhaven-to-brighton-and-portslade/). Harbourside path, white chalk cliffs, clifftop and undercliff concrete paths, shingle beach, busy promenade, beach huts, and wide seafront promenade. Sights: [Newhaven Fort](http://www.newhavenfort.org.uk), Brighton Pier, Brighton Marina, Brighton Pavilion, The Lanes, Saltdean Lido, i360 Tower, and Brighton West Pier. Exposed in high wind. Lunch in Newhaven, Peacehaven, Telescombe, Saltdean, Rottingdean, Brighton Marina, or Hove. Newhaven lunch stop: the Hope Inn. Peacehaven lunch stops: the [Peacehaven](https://www.stonehouserestaurants.co.uk/nationalsearch/southeast/thepeacehavenpeacehaven), or the Smuggler's Rest. Telescombe lunch stop: the [Telescombe Tavern](http://telscombetavern.wix.com/thetelscombetavern). Saltdean lunch stop: the [Whitecliffs](https://www.facebook.com/whitecliffssaltdean/). Rottingdean lunch stops: the Molly's at the Beach, or the White Horse. Brighton Marina lunch stop: the West Quay. Hove lunch stop: the [Big Beach Cafe](http://www.bigbeachcafe.com/). 23 km.
PLD.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Newhaven Town to Portslade (via Brighton and Hove)**: A longer variant of [Newhaven to Brighton and Portslade](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/newhaven-to-brighton-and-portslade/). Harbourside path, white chalk cliffs, clifftop and undercliff concrete paths, shingle beach, busy promenade, beach huts, and wide seafront promenade. Sights: [Newhaven Fort](http://www.newhavenfort.org.uk), Brighton Pier, Brighton Marina, Brighton Pavilion, The Lanes, Saltdean Lido, i360 Tower, and Brighton West Pier. Exposed in high wind. Lunch in Newhaven, Peacehaven, Telescombe, Saltdean, Rottingdean, Brighton Marina, or Hove. Newhaven lunch stop: the Hope Inn. Peacehaven lunch stops: the [Peacehaven](https://www.stonehouserestaurants.co.uk/nationalsearch/southeast/thepeacehavenpeacehaven), or the Smuggler's Rest. Telescombe lunch stop: the [Telescombe Tavern](http://telscombetavern.wix.com/thetelscombetavern). Saltdean lunch stop: the [Whitecliffs](https://www.facebook.com/whitecliffssaltdean/). Rottingdean lunch stops: the Molly's at the Beach, or the White Horse. Brighton Marina lunch stop: the West Quay. Hove lunch stop: the [Big Beach Cafe](http://www.bigbeachcafe.com/). 23 km.

PLG.name: Polegate
PLG.adminWalksAll:
**Polegate to Eastbourne**: A longer variant of [Polegate to Birling Gap](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/polegate-to-birling-gap/). South Downs downland and coastal clifftop path from Birling Gap eastward to Eastbourne. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch in East Dean: the [Tiger Inn](https://www.beachyhead.org.uk/the-tiger-inn/), or the [Deli on the Green](https://www.deli-onthegreen.com/).

**Berwick to Polegate**: Rambler favourite! Farmland, chalk downland, Folkington village, and suburban roads at finish. Sights: [Long Man of Wilmington](http://www.villagenet.co.uk/sevensisters/villages/wilmington.php). Suburban roads at end. Lunch in Wilmington: the [Long Man Inn](https://longmaninn.co.uk/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 11 km.
PLG.publicWalksS2S: **Polegate to Eastbourne**: A longer variant of [Polegate to Birling Gap](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/polegate-to-birling-gap/). South Downs downland and coastal clifftop path from Birling Gap eastward to Eastbourne. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch in East Dean: the [Tiger Inn](https://www.beachyhead.org.uk/the-tiger-inn/), or the [Deli on the Green](https://www.deli-onthegreen.com/).
PLG.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Berwick to Polegate**: Rambler favourite! Farmland, chalk downland, Folkington village, and suburban roads at finish. Sights: [Long Man of Wilmington](http://www.villagenet.co.uk/sevensisters/villages/wilmington.php). Suburban roads at end. Lunch in Wilmington: the [Long Man Inn](https://longmaninn.co.uk/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 11 km.

PMH.name: Portsmouth Harbour
PMH.adminWalksAll: **[Portsmouth Harbour to Havant](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/portsmouth-to-havant-via-hayling-island/)**: Flat coastal walk, seafront promenade, sandy beaches, short ferry crossing, disused railway path, and harbourside shore path. Sights: Spinnaker Tower, Southsea Castle, South Parade Pier, Lumps Fort, Eastney Batteries, Fort Cumberland, [Hayling Island Branch Line](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hayling_Island_Branch_Line), and St Nicholas Chapel. Lunch in Old Portsmouth, Hayling Island, or Langstone. Old Portsmouth lunch stop: the [Spice Island](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/hampshire/spice-island). Hayling Island lunch stops: the [Ferry Boat Inn](http://www.theferryboatinnhaylingisland.co.uk), or the [Inn on the Beach](http://www.innonthebeach.co.uk). Langstone lunch stops: the [Ship Inn](http://www.shiplangstone.co.uk), or the Royal Oak. 20 km.
PMH.publicWalksS2S: **[Portsmouth Harbour to Havant](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/portsmouth-to-havant-via-hayling-island/)**: Flat coastal walk, seafront promenade, sandy beaches, short ferry crossing, disused railway path, and harbourside shore path. Sights: Spinnaker Tower, Southsea Castle, South Parade Pier, Lumps Fort, Eastney Batteries, Fort Cumberland, [Hayling Island Branch Line](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hayling_Island_Branch_Line), and St Nicholas Chapel. Lunch in Old Portsmouth, Hayling Island, or Langstone. Old Portsmouth lunch stop: the [Spice Island](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/hampshire/spice-island). Hayling Island lunch stops: the [Ferry Boat Inn](http://www.theferryboatinnhaylingisland.co.uk), or the [Inn on the Beach](http://www.innonthebeach.co.uk). Langstone lunch stops: the [Ship Inn](http://www.shiplangstone.co.uk), or the Royal Oak. 20 km.

PMP.name: Plumpton
PMP.adminWalksAll:
**Hassocks to Plumpton**: South Downs chalk grassland ridge, dew ponds, wooded descent, farmland paths, and racecourse. Sights: Church of St John the Baptist, [Jill Windmill](http://www.jillwindmill.org.uk), Jack Windmill, Ditchling Beacon, and [Chattri Memorial](http://www.chattri.com/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Plumpton: the Half Moon. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 13 km.

**Lewes to Plumpton**: A variant of [Lewes Circular via Swanborough and Black Cap](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-swanborough-and-black-cap/). Open chalk downland, South Downs ridgeline, woods, wetland, and farmland paths. Sights: Priory of St Pancras, Cockshut Stream Restoration Project, Black Cap, Lewes Castle, and Ashcombe Windmill. Can be muddy. Lunch in Plumpton: the Half Moon. 17 km.
PMP.publicWalksS2SEnding:
**Hassocks to Plumpton**: South Downs chalk grassland ridge, dew ponds, wooded descent, farmland paths, and racecourse. Sights: Church of St John the Baptist, [Jill Windmill](http://www.jillwindmill.org.uk), Jack Windmill, Ditchling Beacon, and [Chattri Memorial](http://www.chattri.com/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Plumpton: the Half Moon. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 13 km.

**Lewes to Plumpton**: A variant of [Lewes Circular via Swanborough and Black Cap](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-swanborough-and-black-cap/). Open chalk downland, South Downs ridgeline, woods, wetland, and farmland paths. Sights: Priory of St Pancras, Cockshut Stream Restoration Project, Black Cap, Lewes Castle, and Ashcombe Windmill. Can be muddy. Lunch in Plumpton: the Half Moon. 17 km.

PRR.name: Princes Risborough
PRR.adminWalksAll:
**Princes Risborough Circular via Ninn Wood**: *Last reviewed 19 February 2022.* 15 km. 280 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921497906)**

**Princes Risborough to Wendover via Whiteleaf Cross**: Rambler favourite! Beech woods, chalk downlands, high ridgeline, nature reserves, escarpments with wide views, and a pleasant old market town. Sights: Whiteleaf Cross, Chequers, Coombe Hill, and Boer War Monument. Can be muddy. Lunch in Smokey Row (Great Kimble), or Lower Cadsden. Smokey Row (Great Kimble) lunch stop: the Swan. Lower Cadsden lunch stop: the Plough. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**Princes Risborough to Great Missenden**: Sloping fields, beech woods, hamlets, upmarket farms, cottages, and two short steep hills with fine views. Sights: [Lacey Green Windmill](http://www.laceygreenwindmill.org.uk/), [Roald Dahl Museum and Story Centre](http://www.roalddahlmuseum.org/), and Prestwood Local Nature Reserve. Can be muddy. Best in Oct–Nov. Lunch in Bryant's Bottom, or Prestwood. Bryant's Bottom lunch stop: the Gate Inn. Prestwood lunch stop: the Polecat Inn. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 15 km. 4.5 hours.

**Saunderton to Princes Risborough**: Rambler favourite! Rolling Chiltern Hills, open ridge, and secluded valleys. Sights: Holy Trinity Church and Lyde Gardens. Lunch in Bledlow: the [Lions](http://www.lionsofbledlow.co.uk). An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 13 km.
PRR.publicWalksCircular: **Princes Risborough Circular via Ninn Wood**: 15 km. 280 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921497906)**

PSH.name: Pershore
PSH.adminWalksAll: **[Evesham to Pershore via Dumbleton and Bredon Hills](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/evesham-to-pershore-via-dumbleton-and-bredon-hills/)**: Vale of Evesham market gardens, river meadows, pastures, minor hills, country estate, woods, and Iron Age hillfort summit with panoramic views. Sights: Evesham Abbey Precinct, Dumbleton Hall, Bredon Hill Iron Age Hillfort, and Pershore Abbey. Can be muddy. Lunch in Ashton-Under-Hill: the [Star Inn](http://thestar-ashtonunderhill.co.uk/). 34 km. 8 hours.
PSH.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Evesham to Pershore via Dumbleton and Bredon Hills](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/evesham-to-pershore-via-dumbleton-and-bredon-hills/)**: Vale of Evesham market gardens, river meadows, pastures, minor hills, country estate, woods, and Iron Age hillfort summit with panoramic views. Sights: Evesham Abbey Precinct, Dumbleton Hall, Bredon Hill Iron Age Hillfort, and Pershore Abbey. Can be muddy. Lunch in Ashton-Under-Hill: the [Star Inn](http://thestar-ashtonunderhill.co.uk/). 34 km. 8 hours.

PTR.name: Petersfield
PTR.adminWalksAll:
**Petersfield Circular**: Steep hillside ascent of Shoulder of Mutton Hill, gentle gradients, one short steep downhill section, and wooded hangers. Sights: Shoulder of Mutton Hill and Edward Thomas Memorial Stone. Steep descent, slippery winter. Lunch in Steep: the [Harrow Inn](http://www.harrow-inn.co.uk/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 10 km. 3 hours.

**[Petersfield to Rowlands Castle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/petersfield-to-rowlands-castle/)**: Undulating ramble through western South Downs, gentle hills, hilltop views, and farmland. Can be muddy. Lunch in Buriton, or Chalton. Buriton lunch stop: the Five Bells. Chalton lunch stop: the Red Lion. 16 km. 5 hours.

**Petersfield to Liss via Edward Thomas Memorial Stone**: Rolling Hampshire countryside, wooded hangers, steep hillside ascent, unspoilt farmland, and quiet villages. Sights: Shoulder of Mutton Hill, Edward Thomas Memorial Stone, All Saints' Church, Steep, Petersfield Market Square, and Equestrian Statue of King William III. Steep ascent, slippery winter. Lunch in Hawkley: the [Hawkley Inn](http://hawkleyinn.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km. 5 hours.

**Petersfield to Liss via Shoulder of Mutton Hill**: Rolling Hampshire countryside, wooded hangers, and shortcut via Pococks Lane and Upland Lane. Sights: Shoulder of Mutton Hill. Steep ascent, slippery winter. Lunch in Hawkley: the [Hawkley Inn](http://hawkleyinn.co.uk/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 11 km.

**Petersfield to Alton**: Rolling Hampshire countryside, wooded hangers, villages, open farmland, and the Alton Circular afternoon route. Sights: Shoulder of Mutton Hill. Very long walk. A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 23 km.

**Rowlands Castle to Petersfield**: A shorter variant of [Rowlands Castle Grand Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rowlands-castle-grand-circular/). South Downs ridge, steep climbs, yew tree woods, open hilltops, escarpments, farmland, and heath near Petersfield. Sights: [Stansted House and Park](https://www.stanstedpark.co.uk/), St Mary Stoughton, [Kingley Vale National Nature Reserve](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kingley_Vale_National_Nature_Reserve), [Devil's Humps](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Devil%27s_Humps,_Stoughton), [Beacon Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beacon_Hill,_West_Sussex), and [Uppark House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/uppark-house-and-garden). Steep climbs throughout. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Hooksway, or South Harting. Hooksway lunch stop: the [Royal Oak](http://royaloakhooksway.co.uk/). South Harting lunch stop: the [White Horse](https://www.thewhitehorse.co.uk/). 33 km.
PTR.publicWalksS2S:
**[Petersfield to Rowlands Castle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/petersfield-to-rowlands-castle/)**: Undulating ramble through western South Downs, gentle hills, hilltop views, and farmland. Can be muddy. Lunch in Buriton, or Chalton. Buriton lunch stop: the Five Bells. Chalton lunch stop: the Red Lion. 16 km. 5 hours.

**Petersfield to Liss via Edward Thomas Memorial Stone**: Rolling Hampshire countryside, wooded hangers, steep hillside ascent, unspoilt farmland, and quiet villages. Sights: Shoulder of Mutton Hill, Edward Thomas Memorial Stone, All Saints' Church, Steep, Petersfield Market Square, and Equestrian Statue of King William III. Steep ascent, slippery winter. Lunch in Hawkley: the [Hawkley Inn](http://hawkleyinn.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km. 5 hours.

PUL.name: Pulborough
PUL.adminWalksAll:
**[Pulborough Circular via Greatham Bridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pulborough-circular/)**: Water meadows, riverside embankments, low hills, parkland, airfield edge, heathland, woodland, and thatched village. Sights: Stopham Bridge, Greatham Bridge, [Amberley Wildbrooks Nature Reserve](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/amberley-wildbrooks), Amberley Castle, St Michael, Amberley, [Parham House](https://www.parhaminsussex.co.uk), Parham Airfield, [RSPB Pulborough Brooks](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/pulborough-brooks), Wiggonholt church, St Mary, Pulborough, and Park Mound. Can be boggy. Lunch in Amberley, or Cootham. Amberley lunch stops: the [Black Horse](https://www.amberleyblackhorse.co.uk), or the [Sportsman](https://thesportsmansussex.co.uk). Cootham lunch stop: the [Crown Inn](https://crowncootham.co.uk). 21 km. 5 hours.

**Pulborough Circular via Petworth House**: A longer variant of [Pulborough to Petworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pulborough-to-petworth/). Low hills, wooded commons, farmland, Rother valley, Hesworth Common, medieval bridge, riverside, and South Downs views. Sights: [Petworth House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/sussex/petworth), Petworth Park, Hesworth Common, St Mary the Virgin (Fittleworth), St Mary the Virgin (Stopham), Stopham Bridge, Stopham Vineyard, Toat Monument, Pallingham Bridge, Upper Pond, and Upperton Monument. Can be muddy. Lunch in Petworth: the [Angel Inn](https://www.angelinnpetworth.co.uk), or the [Star](https://www.starpetworth.co.uk). 23 km. 6 hours.

**[Pulborough to Amberley via Pulborough Brooks RSPB Nature Reserve](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pulborough-to-amberley/)**: Quaint historic village centre, gentle hills, marshland RSPB reserve, quiet roads through woodland, and South Downs ridge with panoramic views of the Arun valley. Sights: Pulborough Brooks RSPB Nature Reserve and South Downs. Can be muddy. Lunch in Nutbourne, Marehill, or Pulborough Brooks. Nutbourne lunch stop: the Rising Sun. Marehill lunch stop: the White Horse. Pulborough Brooks lunch stop: the RSPB Visitors Centre, Pulborough Brooks. 16 km.

**Pulborough to Amberley via Stopham Bridge**: A shorter variant of [Pulborough Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pulborough-circular/). Low hills, riverside embankments, water meadows, and thatched village. Sights: Stopham Bridge, Greatham Bridge, [Amberley Wildbrooks Nature Reserve](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/amberley-wildbrooks), Amberley Castle, and St Michael, Amberley. Can be boggy. Lunch in Amberley: the [Black Horse](https://www.amberleyblackhorse.co.uk), or the [Sportsman](https://thesportsmansussex.co.uk). 12 km. 2.5 hours.

**Pulborough to Amberley via South Stoke Barn**: A longer variant of [Pulborough Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pulborough-circular/). Low hills, riverside embankments, water meadows, woodland paths, ancient barns, and riverside hamlets. Sights: Stopham Bridge, Greatham Bridge, [Amberley Wildbrooks Nature Reserve](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/amberley-wildbrooks), Amberley Castle, St Michael, Amberley, South Stoke Barn, St Leonard, South Stoke, Gurkha Bridge, St Mary the Virgin, North Stoke, and Amberley Museum and Heritage Centre. Can be boggy. Lunch in Amberley, or Houghton. Amberley lunch stop: the [Black Horse](https://www.amberleyblackhorse.co.uk). Houghton lunch stop: the [George & Dragon](https://www.thegeorgeanddragonhoughton.co.uk). 18 km. 4 hours.

**Pulborough to Amberley via Amberley Village Tea Room**: A longer variant of [Pulborough to Amberley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pulborough-to-amberley/). Quaint historic village centre, gentle hills, marshland RSPB reserve, quiet roads through woodland, South Downs ridge, picturesque village heart, watermeadows, and River Arun bank. Sights: Pulborough Brooks RSPB Nature Reserve, South Downs, Amberley Village Tea Room, and River Arun. Watermeadows flood in winter. Avoid winter/wet weather. Lunch in Nutbourne, Marehill, or Pulborough Brooks. Nutbourne lunch stop: the Rising Sun. Marehill lunch stop: the White Horse. Pulborough Brooks lunch stop: the RSPB Visitors Centre, Pulborough Brooks. 19 km.

**[Amberley to Pulborough via Glatting Beacon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/amberley-to-pulborough/)**: Steep chalky tracks, ridge-top views, hanging woods, heathery commons, river valley, medieval bridge, and parkland with ponds. Sights: Bignor Hill, Glatting Beacon, Barlavington Down, Duncton Mill, Burton Park, Stopham Bridge, and Stopham Church. Steep chalky tracks. Lunch in Duncton: the [Cricketers](http://www.thecricketersduncton.co.uk/). 25 km. 6 hours.

**Amberley to Pulborough via Amberley Castle**: A shorter variant of [Pulborough Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pulborough-circular/). Water meadows, riverside embankments, parkland, airfield edge, heathland, and woodland. Sights: Amberley Castle, St Michael, Amberley, [Parham House](https://www.parhaminsussex.co.uk), Parham Airfield, [RSPB Pulborough Brooks](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/pulborough-brooks), Wiggonholt church, and St Mary, Pulborough. Can be boggy. Lunch in Amberley, or Cootham. Amberley lunch stops: the [Black Horse](https://www.amberleyblackhorse.co.uk), or the [Sportsman](https://thesportsmansussex.co.uk). Cootham lunch stop: the [Crown Inn](https://crowncootham.co.uk). 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**Amberley to Pulborough via Bignor**: A shorter variant of [Amberley to Pulborough](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/amberley-to-pulborough/). Steep descent through hanging woods, quiet villages, heathery commons, and river valley. Sights: Bignor Hill, Bignor, Sutton Common, and Stopham Bridge. Streambed wading required. Lunch in Sutton: the [White Horse](https://www.whitehorseinn-sutton.co.uk/). 19 km.
PUL.publicWalksS2S: **[Pulborough to Amberley via Pulborough Brooks RSPB Nature Reserve](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pulborough-to-amberley/)**: Quaint historic village centre, gentle hills, marshland RSPB reserve, quiet roads through woodland, and South Downs ridge with panoramic views of the Arun valley. Sights: Pulborough Brooks RSPB Nature Reserve and South Downs. Can be muddy. Lunch in Nutbourne, Marehill, or Pulborough Brooks. Nutbourne lunch stop: the Rising Sun. Marehill lunch stop: the White Horse. Pulborough Brooks lunch stop: the RSPB Visitors Centre, Pulborough Brooks. 16 km.
PUL.publicWalksCircular: **[Pulborough Circular via Greatham Bridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pulborough-circular/)**: Water meadows, riverside embankments, low hills, parkland, airfield edge, heathland, woodland, and thatched village. Sights: Stopham Bridge, Greatham Bridge, [Amberley Wildbrooks Nature Reserve](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/amberley-wildbrooks), Amberley Castle, St Michael, Amberley, [Parham House](https://www.parhaminsussex.co.uk), Parham Airfield, [RSPB Pulborough Brooks](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/pulborough-brooks), Wiggonholt church, St Mary, Pulborough, and Park Mound. Can be boggy. Lunch in Amberley, or Cootham. Amberley lunch stops: the [Black Horse](https://www.amberleyblackhorse.co.uk), or the [Sportsman](https://thesportsmansussex.co.uk). Cootham lunch stop: the [Crown Inn](https://crowncootham.co.uk). 21 km. 5 hours.
PUL.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Amberley to Pulborough via Glatting Beacon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/amberley-to-pulborough/)**: Steep chalky tracks, ridge-top views, hanging woods, heathery commons, river valley, medieval bridge, and parkland with ponds. Sights: Bignor Hill, Glatting Beacon, Barlavington Down, Duncton Mill, Burton Park, Stopham Bridge, and Stopham Church. Steep chalky tracks. Lunch in Duncton: the [Cricketers](http://www.thecricketersduncton.co.uk/). 25 km. 6 hours.

PUT.name: Putney
PUT.adminWalksAll:
**[Putney to Richmond](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/putney-to-richmond/)**: Flat, all-weather Thames riverside paths, tree-lined embankments, wealthy suburbs, semi-rural stretches, historic bridges, and urban parks. Sights: St Mary's Church Putney, All Saints Church Fulham, [Fulham Palace](http://www.fulhampalace.org/), [Hammersmith Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hammersmith_Bridge), [William Morris Society](http://www.williammorrissociety.org/), [Emery Walker Trust](https://www.emerywalker.org.uk/), [Water and Steam Museum](http://www.waterandsteam.org.uk/), [Kew Gardens](https://www.kew.org/), [Syon Park](http://www.syonpark.co.uk/), [Richmond Lock and Footbridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richmond_Lock_and_Footbridge), [Richmond Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richmond_Bridge,_London), and London Wetlands Centre. Best in Dec–Feb. Lunch in Putney, or Richmond. Putney lunch stop: the [Putney Pantry](http://www.putneypantry.com/). Richmond lunch stop: the [White Cross](http://thewhitecrossrichmond.com/). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Wimbledon to Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-putney/)**: Suburban streets, open common, landscaped park, woodland riverside path, Royal Park grassland, suburban roads, and riverside Thames path. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](https://cannizaropark.com/about-cannizaro-park), [Wimbledon Common](https://www.wpcc.org.uk), [Richmond Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/visit/parks/richmond-park), [Barnes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barnes,_London), [Barnes Common](https://barnescommon.org.uk), [London Wetland Centre](https://www.wwt.org.uk/wetland-centres/london), [Oxford and Cambridge Boat Race](https://www.theboatrace.org/the-course), [Beverley Brook Walk](https://www.merton.gov.uk/system/files?file=beverley_brook_walk_web.pdf), Millennium Fountain, Cannizaro Park Aviary, Sunken Garden, Mediterranean Garden, Italian Garden, Rose Garden, Diana and the Fawn statue, Caesar's Camp Iron Age hill fort, Barnes Common Old Cemetery, Craven Cottage, St Mary's Church Putney, All Saints Church Putney, and Gustav Holst blue plaque. Can be muddy. Lunch in Richmond Park, or Barnes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Roehampton Gate café. Barnes lunch stops: the [Crossing](https://www.thecrossing-barnes.co.uk), the [Ye White Hart](https://www.whitehartbarnes.co.uk), the [Watermans Arms](https://www.watermansarms.co.uk), the [Bull's Head](https://www.thebullsheadbarnes.com), the [Coach & Horses](https://www.coachandhorsesbarnes.co.uk), or the [Sun Inn](https://www.thesuninnbarnes.co.uk). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**Stoneleigh to Putney**: An alternative variant of [Beverley Brook](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/beverley-brook/). Suburban streets, riverside brookside paths, commons, parkland, and Thames-side esplanade. Sights: Wimbledon Common, Richmond Park, Barnes Common, and Beverley Meads LNR. Can be muddy. Lunch in Worcester Park, Motspur Park, New Malden, West Wimbledon, London SW15, East Sheen, or Barnes. Worcester Park lunch stop: the [Brook Pub & Dining](https://thebrook worcesterpark.co.uk/). Motspur Park lunch stops: the Blossom & Brew, the Café Rich Point, or the [Earl Beatty](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/surrey/earl-beatty). New Malden lunch stop: the [Glasshouse](https://www.glasshouse.pub/). West Wimbledon lunch stop: the [Da Mimmo](https://www.damimmowimbledon.com/). London SW15 lunch stop: the [Roehampton Gate Café](https://www.colicci.co.uk/roehampton-gate-cafe). East Sheen lunch stop: the [Palewell Café on the Common](https://www.colicci.co.uk/roehampton-gate-cafe). Barnes lunch stops: the Café Loris, or the [Stag's Head](https://www.stagsheadbarnes.co.uk/).
PUT.publicWalksS2S: **[Putney to Richmond](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/putney-to-richmond/)**: Flat, all-weather Thames riverside paths, tree-lined embankments, wealthy suburbs, semi-rural stretches, historic bridges, and urban parks. Sights: St Mary's Church Putney, All Saints Church Fulham, [Fulham Palace](http://www.fulhampalace.org/), [Hammersmith Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hammersmith_Bridge), [William Morris Society](http://www.williammorrissociety.org/), [Emery Walker Trust](https://www.emerywalker.org.uk/), [Water and Steam Museum](http://www.waterandsteam.org.uk/), [Kew Gardens](https://www.kew.org/), [Syon Park](http://www.syonpark.co.uk/), [Richmond Lock and Footbridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richmond_Lock_and_Footbridge), [Richmond Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richmond_Bridge,_London), and London Wetlands Centre. Best in Dec–Feb. Lunch in Putney, or Richmond. Putney lunch stop: the [Putney Pantry](http://www.putneypantry.com/). Richmond lunch stop: the [White Cross](http://thewhitecrossrichmond.com/). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

Can be muddy. Lunch in Richmond Park, or Barnes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Roehampton Gate café. Barnes lunch stops: the [Crossing](https://www.thecrossing-barnes.co.uk), the [Ye White Hart](https://www.whitehartbarnes.co.uk), the [Watermans Arms](https://www.watermansarms.co.uk), the [Bull's Head](https://www.thebullsheadbarnes.com), the [Coach & Horses](https://www.coachandhorsesbarnes.co.uk), or the [Sun Inn](https://www.thesuninnbarnes.co.uk). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

RAM.name: Ramsgate
RAM.adminWalksAll:
**Ramsgate to Margate**: Low chalk cliffs, sandy bays and coves, cliff-top grassland, seaside promenades, and historic coastal towns. Sights: Turner Contemporary Gallery, Dickens House Museum, Kingsgate Castle, North Foreland Lighthouse, and [Royal Victoria Pavilion](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/all-pubs/england/kent/the-royal-victoria-pavilion-ramsgate). Check tide timings. Very busy; infinite cafés/restaurants. Lunch in Ramsgate, Dumpton Gap, Louisa Bay, Broadstairs, Kingsgate Bay, or Botany Bay. Ramsgate lunch stops: the [Royal Victoria Pavillion](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/all-pubs/england/kent/the-royal-victoria-pavilion-ramsgate), or the [Royal Harbour Brasserie](http://www.royalharbourbrasserie.co.uk/). Dumpton Gap lunch stop: the [Sams](https://twitter.com/dumptoncafe). Louisa Bay lunch stop: the [Louisa Bay Cafe](https://www.facebook.com/Louisa-Bay-Cafe-260058987367161/). Broadstairs lunch stop: the Charles Dickens. Kingsgate Bay lunch stop: the [Captain Digby](http://www.captaindigby.co.uk/). Botany Bay lunch stop: the [Botany Bay](https://www.botanybayhotel.co.uk/). *Last reviewed 25 February 2026.* 16 km. 120 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2797333523)**

**Margate to Ramsgate**: An alternative variant of [Ramsgate to Margate](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ramsgate-to-margate/). Rambler favourite! Low chalk cliffs, sandy bays and coves, cliff-top grassland, seaside promenades, and historic coastal towns. Sights: Turner Contemporary Gallery, Kingsgate Castle, North Foreland Lighthouse, and Dickens House Museum. Check tide timings. Lunch in Kingsgate Bay, Botany Bay, Louisa Bay, Dumpton Gap, or Ramsgate. Kingsgate Bay lunch stop: the [Captain Digby](http://www.captaindigby.co.uk/). Botany Bay lunch stop: the [Botany Bay](https://www.botanybayhotel.co.uk/). Louisa Bay lunch stop: the [Louisa Bay Cafe](https://www.facebook.com/Louisa-Bay-Cafe-260058987367161/). Dumpton Gap lunch stop: the [Sams](https://twitter.com/dumptoncafe). Ramsgate lunch stops: the [Royal Victoria Pavillion](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/all-pubs/england/kent/the-royal-victoria-pavilion-ramsgate), or the [Royal Harbour Brasserie](http://www.royalharbourbrasserie.co.uk/). 15 km.
