RBR.name: Robertsbridge
RBR.adminWalksAll:
**[Robertsbridge Circular via Brightling](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/robertsbridge-circular-via-brightling/)**: East Sussex countryside, rolling hills, fields, and woodland around Brightling. No lunch stop. 18 km. 5.5 hours.

**Robertsbridge Circular via Rother Valley Railway**: Rolling hills, woods, hop fields, orchards, Rother Valley, and gentle undulating farmland. Sights: [Bodiam Castle](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/sussex/bodiam-castle), St Mary's Church, Salehurst, and Rother Valley Railway. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hawkhurst Fish Farm: the [Waterside Café](http://hawkhurstfishfarm.co.uk/waterside-cafe). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 18 km. 6 hours.

**Robertsbridge Circular via Bodiam Castle**: Rolling hills, direct route to castle, Rother Valley, and gentle return. Sights: [Bodiam Castle](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/sussex/bodiam-castle). Lunch in Bodiam: the Castle Inn. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 14 km. 4.5 hours.

**Robertsbridge Circular via Hurst Green**: Rolling High Weald hills and alternative morning route via Hurst Green village. Sights: [Bodiam Castle](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/sussex/bodiam-castle) and St Mary's Church, Salehurst. Lunch in Hurst Green: the [Eurasia at The Royal George](http://www.eurasiahurstgreen.co.uk/). A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 18 km. 6 hours.

**[Robertsbridge to Uckfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/robertsbridge-to-uckfield/)**: Linear walk across East Sussex, farmland, fields, villages, and countryside between Robertsbridge and Uckfield. Can be overgrown. Lunch in Wood's Corner, Heathfield, Cade Street, Heathfield, Old Heathfield, Heathfield, Heathfield, Waldron, Heathfield, or Blackboys, Uckfield. Wood's Corner, Heathfield lunch stop: the [Swan Inn](https://swaninndallington.co.uk/). Cade Street, Heathfield lunch stop: the [Half Moon Inn](https://www.halfmoon-inn.co.uk/). Old Heathfield, Heathfield lunch stop: the [Star Inn](https://www.starinnoldheathfield.co.uk/). Heathfield lunch stop: the [Runt In Tun](https://www.runtintun.co.uk/). Waldron, Heathfield lunch stop: the [Star Inn](https://www.thestarinnwaldron.co.uk/). Blackboys, Uckfield lunch stop: the [Blackboys Inn](https://www.theblackboys.co.uk/). 38 km.

**Robertsbridge to Battle via Battle**: Linear southerly walk, rolling High Weald countryside, and historic town finish. Sights: Battle. Lunch in Staplecross, or Seddlescombe. Staplecross lunch stop: the [Cross Inn](http://www.thecrossinn.com/). Seddlescombe lunch stop: the [Queens Head](http://www.thequeensheadsedlescombe.com/). An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 17 km. 5.5 hours.

**Robertsbridge to Stonegate**: An alternative variant of [Robertsbridge Circular via Brightling](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/robertsbridge-circular-via-brightling/). East Sussex countryside, rolling hills, fields, and woodland via Burwash to Stonegate. Lunch in Burwash: the Wheel Inn. 18 km. 5.5 hours.

**Robertsbridge to Wadhurst**: A variant of [Etchingham to Wadhurst via Ticehurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/etchingham-to-wadhurst-via-ticehurst/). Hilly Wealden pasture and woodland, panoramic valley views, converted oast houses, cobnut orchard, back lanes with wildflower verges, and reservoir shoreline. Sights: Bewl Water, Ticehurst village, and Wadhurst village. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Ticehurst, or Three Leg Cross. Ticehurst lunch stop: the Bell. Three Leg Cross lunch stop: the Bull Inn. 21 km.

**[Stonegate to Robertsbridge via Batemans](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/stonegate-to-robertsbridge/)**: Hilly High Weald, valleys, ridgetop village, hornbeam woods, country estate, and panoramic ridge views. Sights: Batemans. Can be muddy. Best in Oct, Nov and Apr. Lunch in Burwash, or Robertsbridge. Burwash lunch stops: the Rose & Crown, the Bear Inn, or the Blacksmiths Tea and Coffee Shop. Robertsbridge lunch stops: the Ostrich, or the George Inn. 14 km.

**Wadhurst to Robertsbridge**: High Weald AONB, rolling hills, woods, Bewl Water lakeside, Sussex Border Path, and quiet farmland. Sights: [Bewl Water](http://www.bewl.co.uk) and Church of St Peter and St Paul. Can be muddy. Lunch in Cousley Wood, or Bewl Water. Cousley Wood lunch stop: the [Old Vine](http://www.theoldvineinn.co.uk/). Bewl Water lunch stop: the [Look Out restaurant](http://www.bewl.co.uk). A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 34 km. 7 hours.
RBR.publicWalksS2S: **[Robertsbridge to Uckfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/robertsbridge-to-uckfield/)**: Linear walk across East Sussex, farmland, fields, villages, and countryside between Robertsbridge and Uckfield. Can be overgrown. Lunch in Wood's Corner, Heathfield, Cade Street, Heathfield, Old Heathfield, Heathfield, Heathfield, Waldron, Heathfield, or Blackboys, Uckfield. Wood's Corner, Heathfield lunch stop: the [Swan Inn](https://swaninndallington.co.uk/). Cade Street, Heathfield lunch stop: the [Half Moon Inn](https://www.halfmoon-inn.co.uk/). Old Heathfield, Heathfield lunch stop: the [Star Inn](https://www.starinnoldheathfield.co.uk/). Heathfield lunch stop: the [Runt In Tun](https://www.runtintun.co.uk/). Waldron, Heathfield lunch stop: the [Star Inn](https://www.thestarinnwaldron.co.uk/). Blackboys, Uckfield lunch stop: the [Blackboys Inn](https://www.theblackboys.co.uk/). 38 km.
RBR.publicWalksCircular:
**[Robertsbridge Circular via Brightling](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/robertsbridge-circular-via-brightling/)**: East Sussex countryside, rolling hills, fields, and woodland around Brightling. No lunch stop. 18 km. 5.5 hours.

**Robertsbridge Circular via Rother Valley Railway**: Rolling hills, woods, hop fields, orchards, Rother Valley, and gentle undulating farmland. Sights: [Bodiam Castle](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/sussex/bodiam-castle), St Mary's Church, Salehurst, and Rother Valley Railway. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hawkhurst Fish Farm: the [Waterside Café](http://hawkhurstfishfarm.co.uk/waterside-cafe). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 18 km. 6 hours.
RBR.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Stonegate to Robertsbridge via Batemans](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/stonegate-to-robertsbridge/)**: Hilly High Weald, valleys, ridgetop village, hornbeam woods, country estate, and panoramic ridge views. Sights: Batemans. Can be muddy. Best in Oct, Nov and Apr. Lunch in Burwash, or Robertsbridge. Burwash lunch stops: the Rose & Crown, the Bear Inn, or the Blacksmiths Tea and Coffee Shop. Robertsbridge lunch stops: the Ostrich, or the George Inn. 14 km.

RDD.name: Riddlesdown
RDD.adminWalksAll:
**Riddlesdown to Coulsdon South**: Ancient commons, open grazing land, bluebell woods, chalk downland, and quiet suburban fringe. Sights: Chaldon Church, Farthing Down, Kenley Common, Coulsdon Common, Riddlesdown, and Happy Valley. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in Coulsdon Common, or Kenley Common. Coulsdon Common lunch stop: the Fox. Kenley Common lunch stop: the [Wattenden Arms](http://www.thewattendenarmskenley.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km. 5 hours.

**Riddlesdown to Kingswood**: Ancient commons, open grazing land, bluebell woods, chalk downland, and extended after-lunch route to Kingswood. Sights: Chaldon Church, Farthing Down, Kenley Common, Coulsdon Common, and Happy Valley. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in Coulsdon Common, or Kenley Common. Coulsdon Common lunch stop: the Fox. Kenley Common lunch stop: the [Wattenden Arms](http://www.thewattendenarmskenley.co.uk/). A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse).

**[Hurst Green to Riddlesdown](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hurst-green-to-riddlesdown/)**: Heathland common, golf courses, farmland, North Downs escarpment, open downland, woodland strips, and views across the High Weald and South Downs. Sights: Limpsfield Common, River Eden, Titsey church, Tatsfield church, Henley Wood Enclosure, and Riddlesdown. Can be muddy. Lunch in Tatsfield: the Old Ship. 21 km. 5.5 hours.
RDD.publicWalksS2S: **Riddlesdown to Coulsdon South**: Ancient commons, open grazing land, bluebell woods, chalk downland, and quiet suburban fringe. Sights: Chaldon Church, Farthing Down, Kenley Common, Coulsdon Common, Riddlesdown, and Happy Valley. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in Coulsdon Common, or Kenley Common. Coulsdon Common lunch stop: the Fox. Kenley Common lunch stop: the [Wattenden Arms](http://www.thewattendenarmskenley.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km. 5 hours.
RDD.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Hurst Green to Riddlesdown](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hurst-green-to-riddlesdown/)**: Heathland common, golf courses, farmland, North Downs escarpment, open downland, woodland strips, and views across the High Weald and South Downs. Sights: Limpsfield Common, River Eden, Titsey church, Tatsfield church, Henley Wood Enclosure, and Riddlesdown. Can be muddy. Lunch in Tatsfield: the Old Ship. 21 km. 5.5 hours.

RDG.name: Reading
RDG.adminWalksAll: **[Reading to Henley-on-Thames via Sonning Lock](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/reading-to-henley/)**: Flat Thames towpath, riverside parks, wooded sections, pasture fields, meadows, and riverside gardens. Sights: Sonning Lock, Sonning Bridge, Shiplake Lock, Marsh Lock, and [Thames Path](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thames_Path). Flood risk sections. Lunch in Sonning: the Bull, or the Coppa Club. 16 km.
RDG.publicWalksS2S: **[Reading to Henley-on-Thames via Sonning Lock](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/reading-to-henley/)**: Flat Thames towpath, riverside parks, wooded sections, pasture fields, meadows, and riverside gardens. Sights: Sonning Lock, Sonning Bridge, Shiplake Lock, Marsh Lock, and [Thames Path](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thames_Path). Flood risk sections. Lunch in Sonning: the Bull, or the Coppa Club. 16 km.

RDH.name: Redhill
RDH.adminWalksAll:
**Coulsdon to Redhill via the Chaldon Way**: It's too apparent that you haven't left London. *Last reviewed 18 December 2021.* 15 km. 180 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921483662)**

**[Redhill Circular via St Mary the Virgin](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/redhill-circular/)**: Nature reserves, wetland lakes, restored sand quarries, Greensand Ridge, farm fields, wooded commons, suburban streets, and pedestrianised town centre. Sights: The Moors Nature Reserve, Holmethorpe Lagoons Nature Reserve, Mercers Country Park, Spynes Mere Nature Reserve, St Mary the Virgin, Castle Hill, Nutfield Priory, Royal Earlswood Park, Earlswood Common, Upper Earlswood Lake, Redhill Common, and King George VI Memorial. Can be muddy. Lunch in Nutfield Marsh, or Bletchingley. Nutfield Marsh lunch stop: the [Inn on the Pond](https://www.innonthepond.co.uk). Bletchingley lunch stops: the [Whyte Harte Hotel](https://whytehartehotel.co.uk), the Lamingtons Tea Room, or the [Red Lion](https://www.redlionbletchingley.co.uk). 20 km. 5 hours.

**Redhill Circular via Ss Peter and Paul**: A shorter variant of [Redhill Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/redhill-circular/). Nature reserves, wetland lakes, restored sand quarries, restored grassland, Greensand Ridge, wooded paths, farm fields, wooded commons, and suburban streets. Sights: The Moors Nature Reserve, Holmethorpe Lagoons Nature Reserve, Mercers Country Park, Spynes Mere Nature Reserve, Ss Peter and Paul, Nutfield Priory, Royal Earlswood Park, Earlswood Common, Upper Earlswood Lake, Redhill Common, and King George VI Memorial. Can be muddy. Lunch in Nutfield Marsh, or Nutfield. Nutfield Marsh lunch stop: the [Inn on the Pond](https://www.innonthepond.co.uk). Nutfield lunch stop: the [Queens Head](https://www.queensheadpublichouse.co.uk). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Merstham to Redhill via Ss Peter and Paul**: A variant of [Redhill Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/redhill-circular/). Grassy embankment, wooded open spaces, wetland lakes, restored sand quarries, restored grassland, wooded paths, farm fields, wooded commons, and suburban streets. Sights: Merstham Bund, Spynes Mere Nature Reserve, Mercers Country Park, Ss Peter and Paul, Nutfield Priory, Royal Earlswood Park, Earlswood Common, Upper Earlswood Lake, Redhill Common, and King George VI Memorial. Road noise near M25. Lunch in Nutfield Marsh, or Nutfield. Nutfield Marsh lunch stop: the [Inn on the Pond](https://www.innonthepond.co.uk). Nutfield lunch stop: the [Queens Head](https://www.queensheadpublichouse.co.uk). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**Merstham to Redhill via St Mary the Virgin**: A variant of [Redhill Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/redhill-circular/). Grassy embankment, wooded open spaces, wetland lakes, restored sand quarries, Greensand Ridge, farm fields, wooded commons, and suburban streets. Sights: Merstham Bund, Spynes Mere Nature Reserve, Mercers Country Park, St Mary the Virgin, Castle Hill, Nutfield Priory, Royal Earlswood Park, Earlswood Common, Upper Earlswood Lake, Redhill Common, and King George VI Memorial. Road noise near M25. Lunch in Nutfield Marsh, or Bletchingley. Nutfield Marsh lunch stop: the [Inn on the Pond](https://www.innonthepond.co.uk). Bletchingley lunch stops: the [Whyte Harte Hotel](https://whytehartehotel.co.uk), the Lamingtons Tea Room, or the [Red Lion](https://www.redlionbletchingley.co.uk). 18 km. 4.5 hours.
RDH.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Coulsdon to Redhill via the Chaldon Way**: It's too apparent that you haven't left London. 15 km. 180 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921483662)**

REI.name: Reigate
REI.adminWalksAll:
**[Dorking to Reigate](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-to-reigate/)**: Mainly level riverside path, sandstone Greensand Way, picturesque Surrey villages, and parkland grounds. Sights: Betchworth Castle, St Michael's Church, and Reigate Priory. Lunch in Betchworth, Brockham, or Reigate Heath. Betchworth lunch stop: the [Dolphin Inn](http://www.youngs.co.uk/pub-detail.asp?PubID=387). Brockham lunch stop: the [Royal Oak](http://www.royaloak-brockham.co.uk/). Reigate Heath lunch stop: the [Skimmington Castle](http://www.skimmingtoncastle.co.uk/). 12 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Holmwood to Reigate](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/holmwood-to-reigate/)**: Gently undulating Low Weald countryside, ancient semi-natural woodland, village green, river crossing, and Greensand ridge with views of North Downs and South Downs. Sights: Hammond's Copse Nature Reserve and Reigate Priory. Lunch in Leigh: the Plough. 16 km. 4 hours.
REI.publicWalksS2SEnding:

Sights: Betchworth Castle, St Michael's Church, and Reigate Priory. Lunch in Betchworth, Brockham, or Reigate Heath. Sights: Hammond's Copse Nature Reserve and Reigate Priory. Lunch in Leigh: the Plough. 16 km. 4 hours.

RHD.name: Ribblehead
RHD.adminWalksAll:
**[Ribblehead Circular via Whernside](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whernside/)**: Moorland, mosses, heather, cotton grass, high plateau with tarns, elevated scarp path, steep descent on engineered path, farm tracks, limestone country with pavements, shake holes, sink holes, and dry streambeds. Sights: Whernside, Ribblehead Viaduct, Artengill Railway Viaduct, and Low Force Waterfall. Can be boggy. 20 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Ribblehead Circular via Ingleton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ingleborough-via-ingleton/)**: High moorland, steep mountain ascent, exposed plateau, limestone pavement, erratics, shake holes, pot holes, farm tracks, wooded gorge option, and valley return under viaduct. Sights: Ingleborough, Ribblehead Viaduct, Ribblehead Limestone Quarry National Nature Reserve, Park Fell, Simon Fell, Scales Moor limestone plateau, Ingleton Waterfalls Trail, and Ribblehead Station. Very exposed summit. Lunch in Ingleton: the [Wheatsheaf Inn](https://www.thewheatsheaf-ingleton.co.uk/), the [Peaks & Troughs Bar & Kitchen](https://www.peakstroughs.co.uk/), the Village Kitchen, the Ingleton Chippy, the Ingleton Pizza, the G&T Café and Kitchen, or the Three Horse Shoes. 24 km. 7 hours.

**Ribblehead to Bentham**: An alternative variant of [Ingleborough via Ingleton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ingleborough-via-ingleton/). High moorland, steep mountain ascent, exposed plateau, and largely flat finish from Ingleton to Bentham. Sights: Ingleborough, Ribblehead Viaduct, Park Fell, and Simon Fell. Very exposed summit. Lunch in Ingleton: the [Wheatsheaf Inn](https://www.thewheatsheaf-ingleton.co.uk/), the [Peaks & Troughs Bar & Kitchen](https://www.peakstroughs.co.uk/), the Village Kitchen, the Ingleton Chippy, the Ingleton Pizza, the G&T Café and Kitchen, or the Three Horse Shoes. 17 km.

**[Dent to Ribblehead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/great-knoutberry-hill/)**: Grassy moorland, high fells, quiet roads, good tracks, open views, railway viaducts, becks, and pastures. Sights: Dent Head Viaduct, Ribblehead Viaduct, Great Knoutberry Hill, Blea Moor Railway Tunnel, [Station Inn](https://www.thestationinnribblehead.com/), and Ribblehead Station Visitor Centre and Tea Room. Can be boggy. 18 km. 5 hours.

**[Horton-in-Ribblesdale to Ribblehead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ingleborough-via-moughton-scars-and-gaping-gill/)**: Limestone pavements, caves, dramatic gorge, moorland fell, high exposed plateau, steep engineered paths, quiet valley, and Iron Age hillfort remains. Sights: Moughton Scars, Trow Gill, Gaping Gill, Ingleborough, and Ribblehead Viaduct. Steep ascent/descent. 20 km. 6 hours.

RIA.name: Rhoose Cardiff International Airport
RIA.adminWalksAll:
**Rhoose Cardiff International Airport to Llantwit Major**: A shorter variant of [Barry to Llantwit Major](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/barry-to-llantwit-major/). Cliff tops, flat rock-shelf beaches, striated limestone cliffs, wetland, inland diversion past power station, dramatic coastal scenery, and historic town centre. Sights: Summerhouse Point, [St Illtyd's Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Illtyd's_Church,_Llantwit_Major), and Llantwit Major beach. Check tide timings. Lunch in East Aberthaw: the [Blue Anchor](http://www.blueanchoraberthaw.com). 15 km.

**Barry to Rhoose Cardiff International Airport**: A shorter variant of [Barry to Llantwit Major](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/barry-to-llantwit-major/). Coastal headlands, pebble beach, low cliffs, parkland, railway viaduct, and cliff tops above former quarries. Sights: [Barry Docks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Docks), [Barry Island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Island), Cold Knap Point, [Porthkerry Country Park](http://www.valeofglamorgan.gov.uk/en/enjoying/Coast-and-Countryside/Porthkerry-Country-Park/Porthkerry-Country-Park.aspx), [Porthkerry Viaduct](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Porthkerry_Viaduct), [The Bulwarks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Bulwarks,_Porthkerry), and Rhoose Point. Check tide timings.
RIA.publicWalksS2S: **Rhoose Cardiff International Airport to Llantwit Major**: A shorter variant of [Barry to Llantwit Major](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/barry-to-llantwit-major/). Cliff tops, flat rock-shelf beaches, striated limestone cliffs, wetland, inland diversion past power station, dramatic coastal scenery, and historic town centre. Sights: Summerhouse Point, [St Illtyd's Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Illtyd's_Church,_Llantwit_Major), and Llantwit Major beach. Check tide timings. Lunch in East Aberthaw: the [Blue Anchor](http://www.blueanchoraberthaw.com). 15 km.

Sights: [Barry Docks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Docks), [Barry Island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Island), Cold Knap Point, [Porthkerry Country Park](http://www.valeofglamorgan.gov.uk/en/enjoying/Coast-and-Countryside/Porthkerry-Country-Park/Porthkerry-Country-Park.aspx), [Porthkerry Viaduct](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Porthkerry_Viaduct), [The Bulwarks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Bulwarks,_Porthkerry), and Rhoose Point. Check tide timings.

RID.name: Ridgmont
RID.adminWalksAll:
**[Stewartby to Ridgmont](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/stewartby-to-ridgmont/)**: Flat farmland, lake shores, ancient woods, open countryside, quiet lanes, and Sustrans cycle tracks. Sights: Stewartby Lake, Stewartby Wind Turbine, Marston Thrift SSSI, Holcot Wood, Church of St Peter and St Paul, and Ridgmont Station Heritage Centre. Can be muddy. Lunch in Cranfield, or Marston Moretaine. Cranfield lunch stops: the [Swan/Gurkha Kitchen](https://www.gurkhakitchencranfield.co.uk/), or the Cross Keys. Marston Moretaine lunch stop: the Exhibition. 19 km.

**[Woburn Sands to Ridgmont](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/woburn-sands-to-ridgmont/)**: Farmland, conifer plantation, semi-ancient woodland, deer park, open grassland, safari park perimeter, and muddy field paths. Sights: Aspley Woods, Danesborough Hill Fort, Woburn Abbey, Church of St John the Baptist, Woburn Safari Park, Ridgmont Station Heritage Centre, and St James' Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Church End, Eversholt, or Woburn. Church End, Eversholt lunch stop: the [Green Man](https://thegreenmaneversholt.co.uk/). Woburn lunch stops: the Fratellis, or the Stop 'n' Stuff. 20 km.
RID.publicWalksS2SEnding:

Sights: Stewartby Lake, Stewartby Wind Turbine, Marston Thrift SSSI, Holcot Wood, Church of St Peter and St Paul, and Ridgmont Station Heritage Centre. Can be muddy. Marston Moretaine lunch stop: the Exhibition. 19 km.

Sights: Aspley Woods, Danesborough Hill Fort, Woburn Abbey, Church of St John the Baptist, Woburn Safari Park, Ridgmont Station Heritage Centre, and St James' Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Church End, Eversholt, or Woburn. Church End, Eversholt lunch stop: the [Green Man](https://thegreenmaneversholt.co.uk/). Woburn lunch stops: the Fratellis, or the Stop 'n' Stuff. 20 km.

RLN.name: Rowlands Castle
RLN.adminWalksAll:
**[Rowlands Castle Circular via Stansted House](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rowlands-castle-circular/)**: Park- and woodland, steep lonely valleys, open fields, hamlets, beech avenue, and South Downs escarpment views. Sights: Stansted House, Stansted Forest, and South Downs Way. Steep climbs and descents. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in West Marden, or Hooksway. West Marden lunch stop: the [Victoria Inn](https://victoriainnwestmarden.com/). Hooksway lunch stop: the [Royal Oak](http://www.royaloakhooksway.co.uk/). 25 km. 6 hours.

**[Rowlands Castle Circular via Stansted House and Park](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rowlands-castle-grand-circular/)**: South Downs ridge, steep climbs, yew tree woods, open hilltops, escarpments, ancient burial mounds, farmland, hedged lanes, and quiet hamlet tracks. Sights: [Stansted House and Park](https://www.stanstedpark.co.uk/), St Mary Stoughton, [Kingley Vale National Nature Reserve](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kingley_Vale_National_Nature_Reserve), [Devil's Humps](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Devil%27s_Humps,_Stoughton), [Beacon Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beacon_Hill,_West_Sussex), [Uppark House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/uppark-house-and-garden), [Harris Well](http://www.smuggling.co.uk/gazetteer_se_19.html), [Red Lion Inn](http://redlionchalton.co.uk/), St Michael's Church Chalton, and [St Hubert's Church](http://www.hampshire-history.com/st-huberts-church-idsworth/). Steep climbs throughout. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Hooksway, or South Harting. Hooksway lunch stop: the [Royal Oak](http://royaloakhooksway.co.uk/). South Harting lunch stop: the [White Horse](https://www.thewhitehorse.co.uk/). 37 km.

**Rowlands Castle Circular via Pen Hill**: A longer variant of [Rowlands Castle Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rowlands-castle-circular/). Narrow wood-fringed valley, main South Downs escarpment, undulating South Downs Way, and far views from Pen Hill and Beacon Hill. Sights: Pen Hill, Beacon Hill, South Downs Way, Stansted House, and Stansted Forest. Steep climbs and descents. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in West Marden, or Hooksway. West Marden lunch stop: the [Victoria Inn](https://victoriainnwestmarden.com/). Hooksway lunch stop: the [Royal Oak](http://www.royaloakhooksway.co.uk/). 29 km.

**Rowlands Castle to Petersfield**: A shorter variant of [Rowlands Castle Grand Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rowlands-castle-grand-circular/). South Downs ridge, steep climbs, yew tree woods, open hilltops, escarpments, farmland, and heath near Petersfield. Sights: [Stansted House and Park](https://www.stanstedpark.co.uk/), St Mary Stoughton, [Kingley Vale National Nature Reserve](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kingley_Vale_National_Nature_Reserve), [Devil's Humps](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Devil%27s_Humps,_Stoughton), [Beacon Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beacon_Hill,_West_Sussex), and [Uppark House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/uppark-house-and-garden). Steep climbs throughout. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Hooksway, or South Harting. Hooksway lunch stop: the [Royal Oak](http://royaloakhooksway.co.uk/). South Harting lunch stop: the [White Horse](https://www.thewhitehorse.co.uk/). 33 km.

**[Petersfield to Rowlands Castle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/petersfield-to-rowlands-castle/)**: Undulating ramble through western South Downs, gentle hills, hilltop views, and farmland. Can be muddy. Lunch in Buriton, or Chalton. Buriton lunch stop: the Five Bells. Chalton lunch stop: the Red Lion. 16 km. 5 hours.

Sights: [Stansted House and Park](https://www.stanstedpark.co.uk/), St Mary Stoughton, [Kingley Vale National Nature Reserve](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kingley_Vale_National_Nature_Reserve), [Devil's Humps](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Devil%27s_Humps,_Stoughton), [Beacon Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beacon_Hill,_West_Sussex), [Uppark House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/uppark-house-and-garden), [Harris Well](http://www.smuggling.co.uk/gazetteer_se_19.html), [Red Lion Inn](http://redlionchalton.co.uk/), St Michael's Church Chalton, and [St Hubert's Church](http://www.hampshire-history.com/st-huberts-church-idsworth/). Steep climbs throughout. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Hooksway, or South Harting. Hooksway lunch stop: the [Royal Oak](http://royaloakhooksway.co.uk/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Buriton, or Chalton. Buriton lunch stop: the Five Bells. Chalton lunch stop: the Red Lion. 16 km. 5 hours.

RMD.name: Richmond
RMD.adminWalksAll:
**[Richmond Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/richmond-circular/)**: River Thames path, open parkland, woodland, deer park, grassy slopes, and hilltop views. Sights: [Richmond Park](http://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond-park), Pembroke Lodge, Isabella Plantation, Richmond Green, and Richmond Hill. Lunch in Richmond Park: the [Pembroke Lodge Tea Rooms](http://www.pembroke-lodge.co.uk/the-tea-rooms). 12 km. 3 hours.

**[Putney to Richmond](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/putney-to-richmond/)**: Flat, all-weather Thames riverside paths, tree-lined embankments, wealthy suburbs, semi-rural stretches, historic bridges, and urban parks. Sights: St Mary's Church Putney, All Saints Church Fulham, [Fulham Palace](http://www.fulhampalace.org/), [Hammersmith Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hammersmith_Bridge), [William Morris Society](http://www.williammorrissociety.org/), [Emery Walker Trust](https://www.emerywalker.org.uk/), [Water and Steam Museum](http://www.waterandsteam.org.uk/), [Kew Gardens](https://www.kew.org/), [Syon Park](http://www.syonpark.co.uk/), [Richmond Lock and Footbridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richmond_Lock_and_Footbridge), [Richmond Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richmond_Bridge,_London), and London Wetlands Centre. Best in Dec–Feb. Lunch in Putney, or Richmond. Putney lunch stop: the [Putney Pantry](http://www.putneypantry.com/). Richmond lunch stop: the [White Cross](http://thewhitecrossrichmond.com/). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**Ealing Broadway to Richmond**: A shorter variant of [Ealing Broadway Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ealing-broadway-circular/). Canal towpath, river Thames towpath, urban parks, and suburban streets ending at Richmond via Old Deer Park. Sights: Pitshanger Manor, Grand Union Canal, Boston Manor, Syon House, and Richmond Old Deer Park. Lunch in Syon Park, or Isleworth. Syon Park lunch stop: the Syon Park Refectory. Isleworth lunch stop: the London Apprentice.

Sights: [Richmond Park](http://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond-park), Pembroke Lodge, Isabella Plantation, Richmond Green, and Richmond Hill. Lunch in Richmond Park: the [Pembroke Lodge Tea Rooms](http://www.pembroke-lodge.co.uk/the-tea-rooms). 12 km. 3 hours.

Sights: St Mary's Church Putney, All Saints Church Fulham, [Fulham Palace](http://www.fulhampalace.org/), [Hammersmith Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hammersmith_Bridge), [William Morris Society](http://www.williammorrissociety.org/), [Emery Walker Trust](https://www.emerywalker.org.uk/), [Water and Steam Museum](http://www.waterandsteam.org.uk/), [Kew Gardens](https://www.kew.org/), [Syon Park](http://www.syonpark.co.uk/), [Richmond Lock and Footbridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richmond_Lock_and_Footbridge), [Richmond Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richmond_Bridge,_London), and London Wetlands Centre. Best in Dec–Feb. Lunch in Putney, or Richmond. Putney lunch stop: the [Putney Pantry](http://www.putneypantry.com/). Richmond lunch stop: the [White Cross](http://thewhitecrossrichmond.com/). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

RNM.name: Rainham
RNM.adminWalksAll:
**Rainham Circular**: An alternative variant of [Rainham to Purfleet via RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rainham-to-purfleet-via-rspb-rainham-marshes/). Completely flat, tarmac cycleway, sea wall, marshes, landfill edge, and riverside views. Sights: [RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/rainham-marshes), Concrete Barges, The Diver, Wennington Marsh, and Rainham Silt Lagoons. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Feb. Lunch in Rainham: the [Phoenix](https://www.craftunionpubs.com/phoenix-rainham).

**[Rainham to Purfleet via RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rainham-to-purfleet-via-rspb-rainham-marshes/)**: Completely flat, tarmac and hard surfaces, riverside sea wall, reed beds, marshes, and industrial-edge landscape with Thames views. Sights: [RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/rainham-marshes), Concrete Barges, The Diver, Purfleet Heritage Museum, Purfleet Heritage Park, Coldharbour Point beacon, [London LOOP Section 24](https://tfl.gov.uk/modes/walking/loop-walk), and [NCN Route 13](https://www.sustrans.org.uk/find-a-route-on-the-national-cycle-network/route-13/). Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Feb. Lunch in Purfleet: the [Royal Hotel Purfleet](http://theroyalpurfleet.co.uk/). 12 km. 3 hours.

**[Rainham to Grays](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rainham-to-grays/)**: Flat riverside path, marshland, industrial scenery, flood defences, graffiti art walls, former wharves, and Thames views throughout. Sights: [Rainham Hall](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/rainham-hall/), [St Helen's and St Giles' Church](http://www.essexchurches.info/church.asp?p=Rainham), [Rainham Marshes RSPB Nature Reserve](http://www.rspb.org.uk/reserves/guide/r/rainhammarshes/), Purfleet Heritage Museum, Royal Hotel, Dartford Bridge, [St Clement's Church](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Clement%27s_Church,_West_Thurrock), and Concrete barges. Flood risk eastern half. Lunch in Purfleet, or Grays. Purfleet lunch stops: the RSPB Café, or the Royal Hotel. Grays lunch stop: the Wharf. 16 km.

**Rainham to Purfleet via Rainham Hall**: A shorter variant of [Rainham to Grays](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rainham-to-grays/). Flat riverside path, marshland, RSPB nature reserve, and Thames views. Sights: [Rainham Hall](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/rainham-hall/), [St Helen's and St Giles' Church](http://www.essexchurches.info/church.asp?p=Rainham), [Rainham Marshes RSPB Nature Reserve](http://www.rspb.org.uk/reserves/guide/r/rainhammarshes/), Purfleet Heritage Museum, Royal Hotel, and Concrete barges. Lunch in Purfleet: the RSPB Café, or the Royal Hotel. 8 km.

**Hornchurch to Rainham**: A longer variant of [Rainham to Purfleet via RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rainham-to-purfleet-via-rspb-rainham-marshes/). Flat, tarmac, marshes, riverside, reed beds, and with an Underground-to-station start via the Ingrebourne Valley. Sights: [RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/rainham-marshes), Concrete Barges, and The Diver. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Feb. Lunch in Purfleet: the [Royal Hotel Purfleet](http://theroyalpurfleet.co.uk/). 36 km.
RNM.publicWalksS2S:
**[Rainham to Purfleet via RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rainham-to-purfleet-via-rspb-rainham-marshes/)**: Completely flat, tarmac and hard surfaces, riverside sea wall, reed beds, marshes, and industrial-edge landscape with Thames views. Sights: [RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/rainham-marshes), Concrete Barges, The Diver, Purfleet Heritage Museum, Purfleet Heritage Park, Coldharbour Point beacon, [London LOOP Section 24](https://tfl.gov.uk/modes/walking/loop-walk), and [NCN Route 13](https://www.sustrans.org.uk/find-a-route-on-the-national-cycle-network/route-13/). Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Feb. Lunch in Purfleet: the [Royal Hotel Purfleet](http://theroyalpurfleet.co.uk/). 12 km. 3 hours.

**[Rainham to Grays](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rainham-to-grays/)**: Flat riverside path, marshland, industrial scenery, flood defences, graffiti art walls, former wharves, and Thames views throughout. Sights: [Rainham Hall](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/rainham-hall/), [St Helen's and St Giles' Church](http://www.essexchurches.info/church.asp?p=Rainham), [Rainham Marshes RSPB Nature Reserve](http://www.rspb.org.uk/reserves/guide/r/rainhammarshes/), Purfleet Heritage Museum, Royal Hotel, Dartford Bridge, [St Clement's Church](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Clement%27s_Church,_West_Thurrock), and Concrete barges. Flood risk eastern half. Lunch in Purfleet, or Grays. Purfleet lunch stops: the RSPB Café, or the Royal Hotel. Grays lunch stop: the Wharf. 16 km.

ROM.name: Romsey
ROM.adminWalksAll: **[Mottisfont & Dunbridge to Romsey via War Memorial Park](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mottisfont-to-romsey/)**: Rolling chalk hills, ancient and bluebell woodlands, river meadows, managed farmland, and a picturesque market town. Sights: [Mottisfont Abbey](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont), St. Andrew's Church, Romsey Abbey, King John's House, Tudor Cottage, War Memorial Park, and Sadler's Mill. Can be muddy. Lunch in Mottisfont, Dunbridge, or Kimbridge. Mottisfont lunch stops: the [Old Kitchen Café](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont), or the [Coach House Café](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont). Dunbridge lunch stop: the [Mill Arms](http://www.millarmsdunbridge.co.uk/). Kimbridge lunch stops: the [Kimbridge Tea Rooms and Café](https://www.idealcollection.co.uk/venues/kimbridge-barn/), or the [Bear and Ragged Staff](http://www.bear-and-ragged-staff.co.uk/). 22 km. 5 hours.
ROM.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Mottisfont & Dunbridge to Romsey via War Memorial Park](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mottisfont-to-romsey/)**: Rolling chalk hills, ancient and bluebell woodlands, river meadows, managed farmland, and a picturesque market town. Sights: [Mottisfont Abbey](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont), St. Andrew's Church, Romsey Abbey, King John's House, Tudor Cottage, War Memorial Park, and Sadler's Mill. Can be muddy. Lunch in Mottisfont, Dunbridge, or Kimbridge. Mottisfont lunch stops: the [Old Kitchen Café](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont), or the [Coach House Café](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont). Dunbridge lunch stop: the [Mill Arms](http://www.millarmsdunbridge.co.uk/). Kimbridge lunch stops: the [Kimbridge Tea Rooms and Café](https://www.idealcollection.co.uk/venues/kimbridge-barn/), or the [Bear and Ragged Staff](http://www.bear-and-ragged-staff.co.uk/). 22 km. 5 hours.

RTR.name: Rochester
RTR.adminWalksAll: **Meopham to Rochester**: An alternative variant of [Meopham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/meopham-to-rochester/). Chalk grassland, ancient woodland, community woodland, landscaped parkland, wildflower meadows, motorway viaduct crossing, salt marsh, riverside path, and historic town. Sights: [Jeskyns Community Woodland](https://www.forestryengland.uk/jeskyns), [Ashenbank Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/ashenbank-wood), [Cobham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cobham,_Kent), [Cobham Park (Cobham Wood and Mausoleum)](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/cobham-wood-and-mausoleum), Darnley Mausoleum, [Ranscombe Farm Nature Reserve](https://www.plantlife.org.uk/our-work/ranscombe-farm-nature-reserve), [Medway Viaduct](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Medway_Viaducts), [Rochester](https://www.visitmedway.org/see-and-do/destination-medway/rochester), [Rochester Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/rochester-castle), [Rochester Cathedral](https://www.rochestercathedral.org/visitinginformation), La Providence, Baty's Marsh, St Mary Magdalene Church, New College of Cobham, Bronze Age Barrow, and Cobham Hall. Motorway viaduct crossing. Lunch in Cobham: the [Leather Bottle](https://www.theleatherbottle.pub/index), the Darnley Arms, or the [Ship Inn](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/kent/ship-inn). 19 km. 4.5 hours.
RTR.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Meopham to Rochester**: An alternative variant of [Meopham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/meopham-to-rochester/). Chalk grassland, ancient woodland, community woodland, landscaped parkland, wildflower meadows, motorway viaduct crossing, salt marsh, riverside path, and historic town. Sights: [Jeskyns Community Woodland](https://www.forestryengland.uk/jeskyns), [Ashenbank Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/ashenbank-wood), [Cobham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cobham,_Kent), [Cobham Park (Cobham Wood and Mausoleum)](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/cobham-wood-and-mausoleum), Darnley Mausoleum, [Ranscombe Farm Nature Reserve](https://www.plantlife.org.uk/our-work/ranscombe-farm-nature-reserve), [Medway Viaduct](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Medway_Viaducts), [Rochester](https://www.visitmedway.org/see-and-do/destination-medway/rochester), [Rochester Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/rochester-castle), [Rochester Cathedral](https://www.rochestercathedral.org/visitinginformation), La Providence, Baty's Marsh, St Mary Magdalene Church, New College of Cobham, Bronze Age Barrow, and Cobham Hall. Motorway viaduct crossing. Lunch in Cobham: the [Leather Bottle](https://www.theleatherbottle.pub/index), the Darnley Arms, or the [Ship Inn](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/kent/ship-inn). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

RUG.name: Rugby
RUG.adminWalksAll: **[Rugby Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-821)**: Town streets, river valley, Oxford Canal towpath, open fields, and village. Sights: [Oxford Canal](www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Rugby Art Gallery & Museum](https://www.ragm.co.uk/), and [St Mary's Church](www.warwickshirechurches.weebly.com). Can be muddy. Lunch in Rugby, Clifton-upon-Dunsmore, or Oxford Canal bridge 66. Rugby lunch stop: the [Victoria Inn](www.atomicbrewery.co.uk). Clifton-upon-Dunsmore lunch stops: the [Clifton Village Stores](www.facebook.com/cliftonvillagestores/), or the Bull Inn. Oxford Canal bridge 66 lunch stop: the [Canal Lounge](www.cliftoncruisers.com/cafe/). 4 km. 1.5 hours.
RUG.publicWalksCircular: **[Rugby Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-821)**: Town streets, river valley, Oxford Canal towpath, open fields, and village. Sights: [Oxford Canal](www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Rugby Art Gallery & Museum](https://www.ragm.co.uk/), and [St Mary's Church](www.warwickshirechurches.weebly.com). Can be muddy. Lunch in Rugby, Clifton-upon-Dunsmore, or Oxford Canal bridge 66. Rugby lunch stop: the [Victoria Inn](www.atomicbrewery.co.uk). Clifton-upon-Dunsmore lunch stops: the [Clifton Village Stores](www.facebook.com/cliftonvillagestores/), or the Bull Inn. Oxford Canal bridge 66 lunch stop: the [Canal Lounge](www.cliftoncruisers.com/cafe/). 4 km. 1.5 hours.

RYE.name: Rye
RYE.adminWalksAll:
**[Rye Circular via St Mary the Virgin](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rye-figure-of-8/)**: Medieval hilltop town, reclaimed marshland, shingle beach, saline lagoons, freshwater gravel pits, reedbeds, riverside embankments, and coastal nature reserve. Sights: [St Mary the Virgin](https://www.ryeparishchurch.org.uk/history.htm), [Rye Castle Museum](http://ryemuseum.co.uk), [Lamb House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/sussex/lamb-house), [Rye Heritage Centre](https://www.ryeheritage.co.uk), [Rye Harbour Nature Reserve](https://rye.sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk), Camber Castle, Castle Water, Mary Stanford Lifeboat House, Ypres Tower, Landgate, Mermaid Inn, Rye Windmill, Saltcote Place, Church of the Holy Spirit, and Martello Tower. Can be muddy. Lunch in Rye Harbour: the [William the Conqueror](https://www.williamtheconqueror.co.uk), the Bosun's Bite, or the [Lime Kiln Cafe](https://rye.sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/discovery-centre/cafe). 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**Rye Circular via All Saints Church**: Rambler favourite! Flat levels, farmland, coastal path to Pett Level, and return via connecting walk. Sights: All Saints Church and Hog Hill windmill. Lunch in Icklesham, or Pett. Icklesham lunch stop: the Queens Head. Pett lunch stop: the Royal Oak Inn. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 21 km.

**Rye Circular via Camber Sands**: A shorter variant of [Rye, Dungeness and Lydd-on-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rye-dungeness-lydd-on-sea/). River estuary, sand dunes, beach, and grassy marshland. Sights: Camber Sands and Romney Marsh Windfarm. Check tide timings. Lunch in Camber: the [Owl](http://theowlcamber.co.uk/), the [Gallivant](https://thegallivant.co.uk/), the Rye Bay Café/Bistro, or the Dunes Bar & Restaurant. 17 km.

**Rye Circular via Scots Float Sluice**: A longer variant of [Rye Figure of 8](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rye-figure-of-8/). Medieval hilltop town, reclaimed marshland, shingle beach, riverside embankments, agricultural fields, wooded hillsides, and village lanes via Playden. Sights: [St Mary the Virgin](https://www.ryeparishchurch.org.uk/history.htm), [Rye Castle Museum](http://ryemuseum.co.uk), [Lamb House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/sussex/lamb-house), [Rye Heritage Centre](https://www.ryeheritage.co.uk), [Rye Harbour Nature Reserve](https://rye.sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk), Camber Castle, Castle Water, Mary Stanford Lifeboat House, Ypres Tower, Landgate, Mermaid Inn, Rye Windmill, Saltcote Place, Scots Float Sluice, St Michael Playden, Church of the Holy Spirit, and Martello Tower. Embankment path closed until 2026/27. Lunch in Rye Harbour: the [William the Conqueror](https://www.williamtheconqueror.co.uk), the Bosun's Bite, or the [Lime Kiln Cafe](https://rye.sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/discovery-centre/cafe). 21 km. 4.5 hours.

**Rye to Hastings**: Rambler favourite! Flat levels, farmland, hilly coastal cliffs, wooded glens, naturist beach, and historic old town. Sights: All Saints Church, Hog Hill windmill, [Hastings Country Park](https://www.hastingscountryparkhistory.com/index.php), Fairlight Glen nudist beach, and Hastings Castle. Steep cliff climbs. Best in Mar–Aug. Lunch in Icklesham, Pett, Pett Level, or Fairlight Cove. Icklesham lunch stop: the Queens Head. Pett lunch stop: the Royal Oak Inn. Pett Level lunch stops: the [C-Side cafe](https://www.csidecafe.co.uk/), or the [Cockles and Dreams](https://www.facebook.com/cocklesanddreams/). Fairlight Cove lunch stop: the [Cove Inn](https://www.thecovefairlight.co.uk/). A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 24 km.

**Hastings to Rye**: Rambler favourite! Clifftop coastal path, steep climbs, remote naturist beach, flat marshland, ancient hilltop towns, and panoramic viewpoints. Sights: Hastings Castle, St Clements Caves, New Gate, St. Thomas' Church, The Look Out, and [Hastings Country Park](https://www.hastingscountryparkhistory.com/index.php). Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch in Fairlight, or Pett Level. Fairlight lunch stop: the [Cove](https://www.thecovefairlight.co.uk). Pett Level lunch stops: the [Coastguard Café](https://www.coastguardcafe.co.uk/), or the [Eater's](https://www.facebook.com/eatersatpett/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 19 km. 6 hours.
RYE.publicWalksCircular: **[Rye Circular via St Mary the Virgin](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rye-figure-of-8/)**: Medieval hilltop town, reclaimed marshland, shingle beach, saline lagoons, freshwater gravel pits, reedbeds, riverside embankments, and coastal nature reserve. Sights: [St Mary the Virgin](https://www.ryeparishchurch.org.uk/history.htm), [Rye Castle Museum](http://ryemuseum.co.uk), [Lamb House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/sussex/lamb-house), [Rye Heritage Centre](https://www.ryeheritage.co.uk), [Rye Harbour Nature Reserve](https://rye.sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk), Camber Castle, Castle Water, Mary Stanford Lifeboat House, Ypres Tower, Landgate, Mermaid Inn, Rye Windmill, Saltcote Place, Church of the Holy Spirit, and Martello Tower. Can be muddy. Lunch in Rye Harbour: the [William the Conqueror](https://www.williamtheconqueror.co.uk), the Bosun's Bite, or the [Lime Kiln Cafe](https://rye.sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/discovery-centre/cafe). 16 km. 3.5 hours.

RYN.name: Roydon
RYN.adminWalksAll: **[Roydon to Sawbridgeworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/roydon-to-sawbridgeworth/)**: Gently rolling Hertfordshire countryside, wheat fields, woods, waterways, green lanes, quiet country roads, river stretches, and navigation canal. Sights: [Henry Moore Foundation](https://henry-moore.org/studios-and-gardens/). Cattle in pastures. Avoid end Oct–Easter. Lunch in Perry Green: the Henry Moore Foundation Visitor Café. 22 km. 5 hours.
RYN.publicWalksS2S: **[Roydon to Sawbridgeworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/roydon-to-sawbridgeworth/)**: Gently rolling Hertfordshire countryside, wheat fields, woods, waterways, green lanes, quiet country roads, river stretches, and navigation canal. Sights: [Henry Moore Foundation](https://henry-moore.org/studios-and-gardens/). Cattle in pastures. Avoid end Oct–Easter. Lunch in Perry Green: the Henry Moore Foundation Visitor Café. 22 km. 5 hours.

SAA.name: St Albans Abbey
SAA.adminWalksAll:
**St Albans Abbey to Park Street**: An alternative variant of [Abbey Trail](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/). 4 km.

**[Watford Junction to St Albans Abbey](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/)**: Way-marked trail through the Ver and Colne Valleys and linking all seven Abbey Line stations — can be done end-to-end or as individual sections with a train hop back. Sights: Sopwell Nunnery and St Albans Abbey. Sections can become waterlogged after heavy rain, sometimes without obvious alternative routes. 14 km.

**Garston to St Albans Abbey**: Common woodland, farmland tracks, river meadows, water meadows, quiet residential roads, and historic city streets. Sights: Bricket Wood Common, Munden House, Moor Mill, Netherwylde Farm, [Verulamium Museum](http://www.stalbansmuseums.org.uk/Sites/Verulamium-Museum), [Verulamium](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Verulamium), [Roman Theatre](http://www.romantheatre.co.uk/), St Albans Cathedral, Great Gateway, Ye Olde Fighting Cocks, Clock Tower, Roman Wall, Hypocaust, and Sopwell Nunnery. Can be very muddy. Lunch in Bricket Wood, or Park Street. Bricket Wood lunch stop: the Moor Mill. Park Street lunch stops: the Overdraught, or the Falcon. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).
SAA.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Watford Junction to St Albans Abbey](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/)**: Way-marked trail through the Ver and Colne Valleys and linking all seven Abbey Line stations — can be done end-to-end or as individual sections with a train hop back. Sights: Sopwell Nunnery and St Albans Abbey. Sections can become waterlogged after heavy rain, sometimes without obvious alternative routes. 14 km.

SAC.name: St Albans City
SAC.adminWalksAll:
**St Albans Circular via Gorhambury House**: Historic city. Much of the walk is through St Albans, but it's a beautiful city so this is no bad thing. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 22 August 2020.* 13 km. 120 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921498612)**

**Harpenden to St Albans via Sanbridge**: Mostly fields and country lanes. Some beautiful new bluebell woods, stunning historic city streeets, commons, golf courses, and parkland. Sights: [Bluebell Woods in Heartwood Forest](https://heartwood.woodlandtrust.org.uk/), [Childwickbury Estate](https://www.google.com/maps/place/?q=place_id:ChIJxz4q38U4dkgRO4Z_rruVnV8), and [St Albans Cathedral and Abbey Church of St Alban](https://www.stalbanscathedral.org/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Sandridge: the [Queens Head](https://linktr.ee/TheQueensHeadSandridge), the [Heartwood Tea Rooms](http://heartwoodtearooms.com/), the [Green Man](http://www.greenmansandridge.co.uk/), or the [Rose & Crown](http://www.theroseandcrownsandridge.co.uk/). End-of-walk rests: [Mad Squirrel Tap](https://www.madsquirrelstalbans.co.uk/), [Abigail's Cafe](https://www.google.com/maps/place/?q=place_id:ChIJfy5BmiM_dkgRUNtX7o50Yx4), [The Peahen](https://www.mcmullens.co.uk/local-pub/peahen/), [The Snug](https://www.google.com/maps/place/?q=place_id:ChIJmT8rc8JHdkgR6s4QOCS4Wkw), or [The Boot](http://www.bootcantina.com/). *Last reviewed 4 May 2026.* 17 km. 130 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2930623590)**

**Garston to St Albans via Moor Mill**: Common woodland, farmland tracks, river meadows, water meadows, quiet residential roads, and historic city streets. Sights: Bricket Wood Common, Munden House, Moor Mill, Netherwylde Farm, [Verulamium Museum](http://www.stalbansmuseums.org.uk/Sites/Verulamium-Museum), [Verulamium](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Verulamium), [Roman Theatre](http://www.romantheatre.co.uk/), St Albans Cathedral, Great Gateway, Ye Olde Fighting Cocks, Clock Tower, Roman Wall, Hypocaust, and Sopwell Nunnery. Can be very muddy. Lunch in Bricket Wood, or Park Street. Bricket Wood lunch stop: the Moor Mill. Park Street lunch stops: the Overdraught, or the Falcon. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 12 km. 60 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920312228)**
SAC.publicWalksCircular: **St Albans Circular via Gorhambury House**: Historic city. Much of the walk is through St Albans, but it's a beautiful city so this is no bad thing. Sights: Bricket Wood Common, Munden House, Moor Mill, Netherwylde Farm, [Verulamium Museum](http://www.stalbansmuseums.org.uk/Sites/Verulamium-Museum), [Verulamium](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Verulamium), [Roman Theatre](http://www.romantheatre.co.uk/), St Albans Cathedral, Great Gateway, Ye Olde Fighting Cocks, Clock Tower, Roman Wall, Hypocaust, and Sopwell Nunnery. Can be very muddy. Lunch in Bricket Wood, or Park Street. Bricket Wood lunch stop: the Moor Mill. Park Street lunch stops: the Overdraught, or the Falcon. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 12 km. 60 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920312228)**

SAL.name: Salisbury
SAL.adminWalksAll:
**Salisbury to Stonehenge via Old Sarum**: An essential walk! Have lunch a café or pub next to the river. Return from Stonehenge to Salisbury by bus (very frequent & reliable but [check times](https://www.connectingwiltshire.co.uk)). From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 7 April 2022.* 16 km. 220 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921513301)**

**[Salisbury Circular via Cranborne Chase Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/salisbury-circular/)**: Historic city, open fields, river valley, pretty villages, chalk downs, ancient drove tracks, steep hillside nature reserve, and water meadows. Sights: Salisbury Cathedral, [Old Mill at Harnham](http://oldmillhotelsalisbury.co.uk/), Harnham Slope Nature Reserve, and Cranborne Chase Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. Strenuous afternoon climbs. Lunch in Coombe Bissett, Bishopstone, or Broad Chalke. Coombe Bissett lunch stops: the [Fox & Goose](http://www.foxandgoose-coombebissett.co.uk/), or the [Coombe Bissett Stores](http://www.coombebissettstores.co.uk/). Bishopstone lunch stop: the [White Hart](http://www.thewhitehart-bishopstone.co.uk/). Broad Chalke lunch stops: the [Queen's Head](http://queensheadbroadchalke.co.uk/), or the [Chalke Valley Stores](http://www.chalkevalleystores.co.uk/). 32 km. 7.5 hours.

**Salisbury to Dean**: An alternative variant of [Dean to Mottisfont](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dean-to-mottisfont/). Chalk downlands, rolling hills, varied woods, field boundaries, farm tracks, and following the Clarendon Way. Sights: Clarendon Palace and [New Art Centre at Roche Court](http://sculpture.uk.com/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Pitton, or Winterslow. Pitton lunch stop: the [Silver Plough](http://silverplough-pitton.co.uk/). Winterslow lunch stop: the [Lord Nelson](http://www.the-lord-nelson.co.uk/). 24 km.

**Salisbury to Mottisfont & Dunbridge**: An alternative variant of [Dean to Mottisfont](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dean-to-mottisfont/). Chalk downlands, rolling hills, varied woods, field boundaries, farm tracks, steep-sided chalk downs, river meadows, Test Valley chalk stream, and sculpture park. Sights: Clarendon Palace, [New Art Centre at Roche Court](http://sculpture.uk.com/), [Mottisfont Abbey & Gardens](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont), and St. Andrews Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Pitton, or Winterslow. Pitton lunch stop: the [Silver Plough](http://silverplough-pitton.co.uk/). Winterslow lunch stop: the [Lord Nelson](http://www.the-lord-nelson.co.uk/). 37 km.
SAL.publicWalksCircular: **Salisbury to Stonehenge via Old Sarum**: An essential walk! Have lunch a café or pub next to the river. Return from Stonehenge to Salisbury by bus (very frequent & reliable but [check times](https://www.connectingwiltshire.co.uk)). From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 16 km. 220 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921513301)**

SAV.name: Stratford-upon-Avon
SAV.adminWalksAll: **[Stratford-upon-Avon Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-1237)**: Medieval market town, canal basin, riverside path, parks and gardens, and historic streets. Sights: [Shakespeare's Birthplace](https://www.shakespeare.org.uk), New Place, [Shakespeare's Schoolroom & Guildhall](https://www.shakespearesschoolroom.org), Royal Shakespeare Theatre, Swan Theatre, [Holy Trinity Church](https://www.stratford-upon-avon.org), [Bancroft Gardens](https://www.visitstratforduponavon.co.uk), Gower Memorial, Shakespeare Memorial Fountain, Hall's Croft, Guild Chapel, and Nash's House. Lunch in Stratford-upon-Avon: the [Dirty Duck](https://www.greeneking-pubs.co.uk/pubs/warwickshire/dirty-duck/), or the [Shakespaw Cat Cafe](https://www.shakespawcatcafe.com). 4 km. 1.5 hours.
SAV.publicWalksCircular: **[Stratford-upon-Avon Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-1237)**: Medieval market town, canal basin, riverside path, parks and gardens, and historic streets. Sights: [Shakespeare's Birthplace](https://www.shakespeare.org.uk), New Place, [Shakespeare's Schoolroom & Guildhall](https://www.shakespearesschoolroom.org), Royal Shakespeare Theatre, Swan Theatre, [Holy Trinity Church](https://www.stratford-upon-avon.org), [Bancroft Gardens](https://www.visitstratforduponavon.co.uk), Gower Memorial, Shakespeare Memorial Fountain, Hall's Croft, Guild Chapel, and Nash's House. Lunch in Stratford-upon-Avon: the [Dirty Duck](https://www.greeneking-pubs.co.uk/pubs/warwickshire/dirty-duck/), or the [Shakespaw Cat Cafe](https://www.shakespawcatcafe.com). 4 km. 1.5 hours.

SAW.name: Sawbridgeworth
SAW.adminWalksAll:
**Bishop’s Stortford to Sawbridgworth**: From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 22 November 2020.* 7 km. 10 m uphill. 2 hours. Easy. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921491885)**

**[Sawbridgeworth to Bishop's Stortford via St. Andrew's Church](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sawbridgeworth-to-bishops-stortford/)**: Gently rolling arable countryside, green lanes, quiet country roads, bluebell woods, river navigation towpath, and marshland nature reserves. Sights: Henry Moore Foundation, St. Andrew's Church, and Thorley Hall and Church. Lunch in Perry Green, Much Hadham, or Green Tye. Perry Green lunch stop: the [Rosey Lea Café](https://henry-moore.org/studios-and-gardens/plan-your-visit-studios-gardens/rosey-lea-cafe/). Much Hadham lunch stop: the Bull Inn. Green Tye lunch stop: the [Prince of Wales](http://www.thepow.co.uk/). 23 km. 5 hours.

**[Roydon to Sawbridgeworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/roydon-to-sawbridgeworth/)**: Gently rolling Hertfordshire countryside, wheat fields, woods, waterways, green lanes, quiet country roads, river stretches, and navigation canal. Sights: [Henry Moore Foundation](https://henry-moore.org/studios-and-gardens/). Cattle in pastures. Avoid end Oct–Easter. Lunch in Perry Green: the Henry Moore Foundation Visitor Café. 22 km. 5 hours.
SAW.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Bishop’s Stortford to Sawbridgworth**: From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 7 km. 10 m uphill. 2 hours. Easy. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921491885)**

SDA.name: Snodland
SDA.adminWalksAll:
**Snodland to Sole Street**: North Downs, wooded areas, open fields, hilly sections, pastoral valleys, and organic farmland. Sights: Snodland Clock Tower, Hall House, Luddesdown Court, and St Peter and Paul church. Nettles in summer. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 13 km. 4 hours.

**[Otford to Snodland](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/otford-to-snodland/)**: North Downs ridge, southern escarpment, fields, woods, open views south, forest, country park, open countryside, and urban streets. Sights: Trosley Country Park. Lunch in Wrotham, or Vigo Village. Wrotham lunch stop: the [Rose and Crown](https://www.roseandcrownwrotham.co.uk/). Vigo Village lunch stop: the Village. 21 km.
SDA.publicWalksS2S: **Snodland to Sole Street**: North Downs, wooded areas, open fields, hilly sections, pastoral valleys, and organic farmland. Sights: Snodland Clock Tower, Hall House, Luddesdown Court, and St Peter and Paul church. Nettles in summer. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 13 km. 4 hours.
SDA.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Otford to Snodland](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/otford-to-snodland/)**: North Downs ridge, southern escarpment, fields, woods, open views south, forest, country park, open countryside, and urban streets. Sights: Trosley Country Park. Lunch in Wrotham, or Vigo Village. Wrotham lunch stop: the [Rose and Crown](https://www.roseandcrownwrotham.co.uk/). Vigo Village lunch stop: the Village. 21 km.

SDG.name: Sandling
SDG.adminWalksAll:
**[Sandling to Folkestone Central via Folkestone Warren Halt railway station](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-or-dover/)**: Rambler favourite! Dismantled railway, chalky escarpment ridge, steep climbs, open downland with Channel views, cliff top promenade, shingle beach, and historic harbour. Sights: [Folkestone](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folkestone), [Sandgate Branch](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandgate_Branch), [Elham Valley Railway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elham_Valley_Railway), [Folkestone Warren Halt railway station](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folkestone_Warren_Halt_railway_station), Folkestone Harbour Arm, and Battle of Britain Memorial. Steep climbs near start. Lunch in Etchinghill: the [Gatekeeper Inn](http://www.gatekeeperinn.co.uk/). 17 km.

**[Sandling to Folkestone Central via the Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-via-the-sea/)**: Disused railway trackbed, orchards, fields, open downland, ridge with sea views, shingle beach, traffic-free seafront promenade, and cliff gardens. Sights: Saltwood Castle, Sandgate Castle, Folkestone Harbour, Mermaid Beach, and Sunny Sands. Two steep hill climbs. Best in Jul–Sep. Lunch in Sandgate, or Folkestone. Sandgate lunch stops: the Earl of Clarendon, the Famous Ship Inn, or the Boat House. Folkestone lunch stops: the Mermaids Cafe Bar, or the HatHats Coffee. 11 km.

**[Sandling to Wye](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-wye/)**: North Downs escarpment ridge, open downland, woods, sloping fields, and fine views over Kent coast. Sights: Tickled Trout. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Stowting, or Brabourne. Stowting lunch stop: the Tiger Inn. Brabourne lunch stop: the Five Bells Inn. 20 km.

**Sandling to Folkestone West**: A shorter variant of [Sandling to Folkestone or Dover](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-or-dover/). Rambler favourite! Dismantled railway, chalky escarpment ridge, steep climbs, open downland, and long pavement finish. Sights: [Sandgate Branch](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandgate_Branch) and [Elham Valley Railway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elham_Valley_Railway). Long pavement to station. Lunch in Etchinghill: the [Gatekeeper Inn](http://www.gatekeeperinn.co.uk/). 11 km.

**Sandling to Folkestone Central via White Cliffs of Dover**: A variant of [Sandling to Folkestone or Dover](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-or-dover/). Rambler favourite! Escarpment ridge, steep climbs, open downland, White Cliffs cliff-top path, Channel views, and beach and harbour. Sights: [Folkestone](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folkestone), [Sandgate Branch](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandgate_Branch), [Elham Valley Railway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elham_Valley_Railway), Battle of Britain Memorial, White Cliffs of Dover, and Folkestone Harbour Arm. Steep climbs near start. Lunch in Etchinghill: the [Gatekeeper Inn](http://www.gatekeeperinn.co.uk/). 20 km.

**Sandling to Dover Priory**: A longer variant of [Sandling to Folkestone or Dover](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-or-dover/). Rambler favourite! Escarpment ridge, steep climbs, White Cliffs cliff-top path, remote chalk downland, housing estate outskirts, and Western Heights fortifications. Sights: [Folkestone](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folkestone), [Sandgate Branch](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandgate_Branch), [Elham Valley Railway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elham_Valley_Railway), Battle of Britain Memorial, White Cliffs of Dover, and Western Heights. Steep climbs near start. Lunch in Etchinghill, or White Cliffs. Etchinghill lunch stop: the [Gatekeeper Inn](http://www.gatekeeperinn.co.uk/). White Cliffs lunch stop: the Clifftop Cafe. 21 km.

**Sandling to Folkestone Central via the Sea & Harbour Arm**: A longer variant of [Sandling to Folkestone via the Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-via-the-sea/). Disused railway trackbed, orchards, fields, open downland, shingle beach, seafront promenade, refurbished harbour area, historic old streets, and clifftop esplanade. Sights: Saltwood Castle, Sandgate Castle, Folkestone Harbour, Harbour Arm, Mermaid Beach, and Sunny Sands. Two steep hill climbs. Best in Jul–Sep. Lunch in Sandgate, or Folkestone. Sandgate lunch stops: the Earl of Clarendon, the Famous Ship Inn, or the Boat House. Folkestone lunch stops: the Mermaids Cafe Bar, the HatHats Coffee, the Harbour Arm, or the Steep Street Coffee House. 13 km.

**Wye to Sandling**: An alternative variant of [Sandling to Wye](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-wye/). North Downs escarpment ridge, open downland, woods, sloping fields, and fine views over Kent coast. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Stowting, or Brabourne. Stowting lunch stop: the Tiger Inn. Brabourne lunch stop: the Five Bells Inn. 20 km.
SDG.publicWalksS2S:
**[Sandling to Folkestone Central via Folkestone Warren Halt railway station](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-or-dover/)**: Rambler favourite! Dismantled railway, chalky escarpment ridge, steep climbs, open downland with Channel views, cliff top promenade, shingle beach, and historic harbour. Sights: [Folkestone](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folkestone), [Sandgate Branch](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandgate_Branch), [Elham Valley Railway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elham_Valley_Railway), [Folkestone Warren Halt railway station](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folkestone_Warren_Halt_railway_station), Folkestone Harbour Arm, and Battle of Britain Memorial. Steep climbs near start. Lunch in Etchinghill: the [Gatekeeper Inn](http://www.gatekeeperinn.co.uk/). 17 km.

**[Sandling to Folkestone Central via the Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-via-the-sea/)**: Disused railway trackbed, orchards, fields, open downland, ridge with sea views, shingle beach, traffic-free seafront promenade, and cliff gardens. Sights: Saltwood Castle, Sandgate Castle, Folkestone Harbour, Mermaid Beach, and Sunny Sands. Two steep hill climbs. Best in Jul–Sep. Lunch in Sandgate, or Folkestone. Sandgate lunch stops: the Earl of Clarendon, the Famous Ship Inn, or the Boat House. Folkestone lunch stops: the Mermaids Cafe Bar, or the HatHats Coffee. 11 km.

**[Sandling to Wye](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-wye/)**: North Downs escarpment ridge, open downland, woods, sloping fields, and fine views over Kent coast. Sights: Tickled Trout. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Stowting, or Brabourne. Stowting lunch stop: the Tiger Inn. Brabourne lunch stop: the Five Bells Inn. 20 km.

SDR.name: Saunderton
SDR.adminWalksAll:
**Saunderton Circular via Bledlow Circular**: Rambler favourite! Rolling Chiltern Hills, open ridge, secluded valleys, gentle gradients with one or two steep hills, and fine valley views. Sights: Holy Trinity Church, Lyde Gardens, and St Mary's Church. Lunch in Bledlow, or Bledlow Ridge. Bledlow lunch stop: the [Lions](http://www.lionsofbledlow.co.uk). Bledlow Ridge lunch stop: the [Boot](http://theboot-bledlowridge.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 17 km. 5.5 hours.

**Saunderton Circular via West Wycombe Circular**: Chiltern woodland, sloping meadows, commons, and a short ridge walk. Sights: [Hughenden Manor](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/hughenden), [West Wycombe Caves](https://www.hellfirecaves.co.uk), West Wycombe Park, Dashwood Mausoleum, St Lawrence Church, and Iron Age hill fort. Very muddy in winter. Lunch in Hughenden, Downley Common, or West Wycombe. Hughenden lunch stop: the Tea Room at Hughenden Manor. Downley Common lunch stop: the [Le De Spencer's Arms](http://www.ledespencersarms.co.uk/). West Wycombe lunch stop: the [George and Dragon Hotel](http://www.georgeanddragonhotel.com). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km. 5 hours.

**Saunderton to Princes Risborough**: Rambler favourite! Rolling Chiltern Hills, open ridge, and secluded valleys. Sights: Holy Trinity Church and Lyde Gardens. Lunch in Bledlow: the [Lions](http://www.lionsofbledlow.co.uk). An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 13 km.

**[Little Kimble to Saunderton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/little-kimble-to-saunderton/)**: Gentle Chilterns hills, beech woods, open stretches, fine views, and undulating but not taxing gradients. Bring a compass. Best in Oct–Nov. Lunch in Parslow's Hillock, Lower Cadsden, or Lacey Green. Parslow's Hillock lunch stop: the [Pink and Lily](http://pink-lily.com/). Lower Cadsden lunch stop: the [Plough](http://theplough.pub/). Lacey Green lunch stop: the [Whip Inn](http://thewhipinn.co.uk/). 17 km. 5 hours.

Bring a compass. Best in Oct–Nov. Lunch in Parslow's Hillock, Lower Cadsden, or Lacey Green. Parslow's Hillock lunch stop: the [Pink and Lily](http://pink-lily.com/). Lower Cadsden lunch stop: the [Plough](http://theplough.pub/). Lacey Green lunch stop: the [Whip Inn](http://thewhipinn.co.uk/). 17 km. 5 hours.

SDY.name: Sandy
SDY.adminWalksAll:
**[Sandy to Arlesey via Glebe Meadows](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandy-to-arlesey/)**: Flat river meadows, commons, heathland, acid grassland, woods, and riverside paths along the River Ivel. Sights: [The Lodge](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/the-lodge), [Jordans Mill](https://jordansmill.com), [Henlow Grange](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Henlow_Grange), [Kingfisher Way](https://enjoybiggleswade.co.uk/local-walking-routes), [River Ivel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Ivel), Holme Mills, Ivel Mill, Biggleswade Common, Galley Hill hill fort, River Hiz, and Glebe Meadows. Can be boggy. Lunch in Jordans Mill: the [Jordans Mill Riverside Café](https://jordansmill.com/plan-your-visit/whats-on#riverside-cafe). 17 km. 4 hours.

**Sandy to Biggleswade**: A shorter variant of [Sandy to Arlesey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandy-to-arlesey/). Flat river meadows, commons, heathland, woods, riverside paths, and conservation site perimeter. Sights: [The Lodge](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/the-lodge), [Broom East Wildlife Conservation Site](https://www.birdingplaces.eu/en/birdingplaces/united-kingdom/broom-lakes), [Jordans Mill](https://jordansmill.com), [Kingfisher Way](https://enjoybiggleswade.co.uk/local-walking-routes), [River Ivel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Ivel), Ivel Mill, Biggleswade Common, and Galley Hill hill fort. Can be boggy. Lunch in Jordans Mill: the [Jordans Mill Riverside Café](https://jordansmill.com/plan-your-visit/whats-on#riverside-cafe). 14 km. 3 hours.

**Sandy to Arlesey via Broom East Wildlife Conservation Site**: A longer variant of [Sandy to Arlesey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandy-to-arlesey/). Flat river meadows, commons, heathland, woods, riverside paths, and market town streets. Sights: [The Lodge](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/the-lodge), [Broom East Wildlife Conservation Site](https://www.birdingplaces.eu/en/birdingplaces/united-kingdom/broom-lakes), [Jordans Mill](https://jordansmill.com), [Henlow Grange](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Henlow_Grange), [Kingfisher Way](https://enjoybiggleswade.co.uk/local-walking-routes), [River Ivel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Ivel), Ivel Mill, Holme Mills, Biggleswade Common, Galley Hill hill fort, Crown Hotel, and River Hiz. Can be boggy. Lunch in Biggleswade: the [Crown Hotel](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/the-crown-hotel-biggleswade), the [White Hart](https://www.greeneking-pubs.co.uk/pubs/bedfordshire/white-hart), the [Rosso Lounge](https://thelounges.co.uk/rosso), or the [Surfin Cafe](https://www.surfincafe.co.uk). 20 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Biggleswade to Sandy via Shuttleworth Collection](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/biggleswade-to-sandy/)**: Flat arable farmland, grassy tracks, woodland trails, thatched villages, and low greensand ridge. Sights: [Old Warden Aerodrome](https://oldwardenaerodrome.co.uk), [Shuttleworth Collection](https://www.shuttleworth.org), [Landmark Trust properties](https://www.landmarktrust.org.uk/search-and-book/location/england/bedfordshire/#Search), [Greensand Ridge Walk](https://www.centralbedfordshire.gov.uk/info/82/countryside/434/walking/2), Keeper's Cottage, Queen Anne's Summerhouse, Shuttleworth House, Abbey Church of St Leonard, Airship Hangars, St Mary the Virgin, The Riddy, and Captain Sir William Peel. Airshow closures apply. Lunch in Old Warden: the [Hare & Hounds](https://www.hareandhoundsoldwarden.com). 18 km. 4 hours.

**Biggleswade to Sandy via Old Warden Aerodrome**: A shorter variant of [Biggleswade to Sandy](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/biggleswade-to-sandy/). Flat arable farmland, grassy bridleways, village greens, and thatched cottages. Sights: [Old Warden Aerodrome](https://oldwardenaerodrome.co.uk), St Mary the Virgin, The Riddy, and Captain Sir William Peel. Lunch in Northill: the [Crown at Northill](https://crownnorthill.co.uk). 11 km. 2.5 hours.
SDY.publicWalksS2S: **[Sandy to Arlesey via Glebe Meadows](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandy-to-arlesey/)**: Flat river meadows, commons, heathland, acid grassland, woods, and riverside paths along the River Ivel. Sights: [The Lodge](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/the-lodge), [Jordans Mill](https://jordansmill.com), [Henlow Grange](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Henlow_Grange), [Kingfisher Way](https://enjoybiggleswade.co.uk/local-walking-routes), [River Ivel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Ivel), Holme Mills, Ivel Mill, Biggleswade Common, Galley Hill hill fort, River Hiz, and Glebe Meadows. Can be boggy. Lunch in Jordans Mill: the [Jordans Mill Riverside Café](https://jordansmill.com/plan-your-visit/whats-on#riverside-cafe). 17 km. 4 hours.
