Airshow closures apply. Lunch in Old Warden: the [Hare & Hounds](https://www.hareandhoundsoldwarden.com). 18 km. 4 hours.

SED.name: Shelford
SED.adminWalksAll:
**Shelford to Cambridge via All Saints Church Little Shelford**: Flat farmland, permissive river paths, open fields, light woodland, and historic city centre streets. Sights: Church of St Mary the Virgin, All Saints Church Little Shelford, Church of St Edmunds Hauxton, All Saints Church Haslingfield, Travelling Telescope, Parish Church of St Andrew and St Mary Grantchester, Orchard Tea Rooms, Old Vicarage Grantchester, Rupert Brooke statue, Queens College, King's College, Clare College, Wren Library, Clare Bridge, Trinity Hall, Gate of Honour, Gonville and Caius College, Trinity College, St John's College, Sidney Sussex College, Great St Mary's Church, Church of St Edward, Saint Bene't's Church, Eagle pub, Corpus Christi College, Old Cavendish Lab, Pembroke College, Peterhouse, Little St Mary's Church, Fitzwilliam Museum, and Cambridge University Botanic Gardens. High tarmac count. Lunch in Haslingfield, or Grantchester. Haslingfield lunch stop: the Little Rose. Grantchester lunch stops: the Rupert Brooke, the Red Lion, or the Green Man. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 23 km. 6 hours.

**Shelford to Cambridge via Church of St Mary the Virgin**: Flat road and field paths and skipping college tour and heading direct to Cambridge station. Sights: Church of St Mary the Virgin, Church of St Edmunds Hauxton, and Orchard Tea Rooms. High tarmac count. Lunch in Haslingfield: the Little Rose. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 19 km.
SED.publicWalksS2S: **Shelford to Cambridge via All Saints Church Little Shelford**: Flat farmland, permissive river paths, open fields, light woodland, and historic city centre streets. Sights: Church of St Mary the Virgin, All Saints Church Little Shelford, Church of St Edmunds Hauxton, All Saints Church Haslingfield, Travelling Telescope, Parish Church of St Andrew and St Mary Grantchester, Orchard Tea Rooms, Old Vicarage Grantchester, Rupert Brooke statue, Queens College, King's College, Clare College, Wren Library, Clare Bridge, Trinity Hall, Gate of Honour, Gonville and Caius College, Trinity College, St John's College, Sidney Sussex College, Great St Mary's Church, Church of St Edward, Saint Bene't's Church, Eagle pub, Corpus Christi College, Old Cavendish Lab, Pembroke College, Peterhouse, Little St Mary's Church, Fitzwilliam Museum, and Cambridge University Botanic Gardens. High tarmac count. Lunch in Haslingfield, or Grantchester. Haslingfield lunch stop: the Little Rose. Grantchester lunch stops: the Rupert Brooke, the Red Lion, or the Green Man. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 23 km. 6 hours.

SEE.name: Southease
SEE.privateNote: LON Trainline says 1h12m — Victoria via Lewes. Supposedly the RTT data should fix this.
SEE.adminWalksAll:
**Lewes to Southease via Mt Caburn**: An essential walk! Escarpments, three distinct downland ridges, wide open chalk paths, panoramic views, historic town streets, and gentle valleys. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 17 October 2021.* 16 km. 380 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921497025)**

**[Southease Circular via King George V Memorial](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-circular/)**: South Downs hills, chalk paths, river embankment, coastal clifftop, Undercliff sea-level promenade, open downland, arable fields, and quiet downland valleys. Sights: Centenary Park, Meridian Monument, King George V Memorial, Undercliff Walk, Telscombe Tye, St Peter's Church Southease, Piddinghoe Church, Harvey's Cross, Breaky Bottom Vineyard, and Rottingdean Windmill. Can be muddy. Lunch in Peacehaven, or Telscombe Cliffs. Peacehaven lunch stops: the Papachino's Gateway Cafe, the Dewdrop Inn, the [Burger and Bird](https://www.burgerandbird.com/), the Big Mouths Burger Bar, the [Stonehouse Pizza and Carvery](https://www.stonehouserestaurants.co.uk/nationalsearch/southeast/thepeacehavenpeacehaven), or the [Maloncho](https://www.maloncho-restaurant.co.uk/). Telscombe Cliffs lunch stops: the [Kappadokya](https://www.kappadokya.co.uk/), the Thai Elephant Two, the Tavern at Telscombe Cliffs, or the [Smugglers' Rest](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thesmugglersrestpeacehaven#/). 23 km.

**Southease Circular via Monk's House**: A shorter variant of [Southease to Rottingdean or Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-rottingdean-or-brighton/). Flat Ouse valley, riverbank, chalk downs, South Downs Way, and circular route back to station. Sights: Monk's House and Lewes Castle. Can be muddy. Lunch in Rodmell, or Kingston. Rodmell lunch stop: the Abergavenny Arms. Kingston lunch stop: the Juggs. 15 km.

**Southease Circular via Centenary Park & Breaky Bottom Vineyard**: A variant of [Southease Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-circular/). South Downs hills, chalk paths, river embankment, coastal clifftop, open downland, and short section of concrete track. Sights: Centenary Park, Meridian Monument, Undercliff Walk, Telscombe Tye, St Peter's Church Southease, and Breaky Bottom Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch in Peacehaven, or Telscombe Cliffs. Peacehaven lunch stops: the Papachino's Gateway Cafe, or the Dewdrop Inn. Telscombe Cliffs lunch stop: the [Smugglers' Rest](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thesmugglersrestpeacehaven#/). 18 km.

**Southease Circular via Kipling Gardens**: A longer variant of [Southease to Rottingdean or Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-rottingdean-or-brighton/). Flat Ouse valley, riverbank, chalk downs, seafront, Saltdean, and circular route back over downs to station. Sights: Monk's House, Lewes Castle, and Kipling Gardens. Can be muddy. Lunch in Rodmell, or Kingston. Rodmell lunch stop: the Abergavenny Arms. Kingston lunch stop: the Juggs. 23 km.

**Southease Circular via Balsdean deserted village**: A longer variant of [Southease Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-circular/). South Downs hills, chalk bridleways, open downland valleys, and wider views to both sides of the Downs. Sights: Centenary Park, Meridian Monument, Undercliff Walk, Telscombe Tye, St Peter's Church Southease, Balsdean deserted village, and Rottingdean Windmill. Can be muddy. Lunch in Telscombe Cliffs: the [Smugglers' Rest](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thesmugglersrestpeacehaven#/). 26 km.

**Southease to Seaford via Monk's House**: River valley, ridge-top South Downs, open downland with coastal views, pretty villages, and seaside esplanade. Sights: Monk's House and St Andrew's Church. Lunch in Rodmell: the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 18 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Southease to Eastbourne via Long Man of Wilmington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-eastbourne/)**: Open treeless downland ridges, steep ascents, scenic Cuckmere Valley, coastal promenade, beach, and pier. Sights: Firle Beacon, Long Burgh Long Barrow, Long Man of Wilmington, Windover Hill, Beachy Head, Wish Tower, Eastbourne Pier, and Italian Gardens. Steep ascents. Lunch in Alfriston: the Pubs, tea rooms and village shop. 27 km.

**Southease to Seaford via St Andrew's Church**: Direct route across South Downs, open downland with coastal views, and seaside esplanade. Sights: St Andrew's Church. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 12 km. 4 hours.

**Southease to Bishopstone**: River valley, ridge-top South Downs, open downland, and pretty village. Sights: Monk's House and St Andrew's Church. Lunch in Rodmell: the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km.

**Southease to Lewes**: Flat valley walk along River Ouse levee into historic Lewes. Sights: Lewes Castle and Monk's House. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 6 km.

**Southease to Brighton**: A longer variant of [Southease to Rottingdean or Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-rottingdean-or-brighton/). Flat Ouse valley, riverbank, chalk downs, open escarpment, quaint village, seafront promenade, and urban Brighton. Sights: Monk's House, Lewes Castle, Kipling Gardens, Brighton Pier, and [Brighton Royal Pavilion](http://www.royalpavilion.org.uk/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Rodmell, or Kingston. Rodmell lunch stop: the Abergavenny Arms. Kingston lunch stop: the Juggs. 23 km.

**Southease to Eastbourne via Cuckmere Haven**: A longer variant of [Southease to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-eastbourne/). Open downland ridges, steep ascents, Cuckmere Haven, Seven Sisters chalk cliffs, and coastal path. Sights: Firle Beacon, Long Burgh Long Barrow, Cuckmere Haven, Seven Sisters, Beachy Head, and Eastbourne Pier. Very hilly afternoon. Lunch in Alfriston: the Pubs, tea rooms and village shop. 33 km.

**Lewes to Southease via Glynde**: A shorter variant of [Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-glynde-and-southease/). Rambler favourite! Downland ridge, long gradual descent to Rodmell, chalk paths, and open views. Sights: Mount Caburn. Lunch in Rodmell, or Southease. Rodmell lunch stop: the Abergavenny Arms. Southease lunch stop: the YHA Courtyard Cafe. 11 km.

**Glynde to Southease via Glynde and Southease**: A shorter variant of [Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-glynde-and-southease/). Rambler favourite! Downland ridge, South Downs Way, open chalk paths, and panoramic views. Lunch in Southease: the YHA Courtyard Cafe. 6 km.

**Lewes to Southease via Lewes Priory**: Chalk grassland ridges, South Downs views, historic town, picturesque village, and return by train from Southease. Sights: Lewes Castle, Lewes Priory, Anne of Cleves House, and Monk's House. Lunch in Rodmell: the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 12 km.

**Glynde to Southease via West Firle Circular**: South Downs Way chalk ridge and extensive views inland and to Newhaven and the Channel. Sights: Firle Place. Lunch in Firle: the Ram Inn. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 11 km.

**Lewes to Southease via Lewes Castle**: A variant of [Southease to Rottingdean or Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-rottingdean-or-brighton/). Chalk downs, South Downs Way, and Ouse valley. Sights: Lewes Castle. 11 km.

**Bishopstone to Southease**: A shorter variant of [Bishopstone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/). Downland climbs, South Downs Way ridge walking, and descent to Southease. Sights: Long Burgh Barrow. 16 km.

**Lewes to Southease via Kipling Gardens**: A variant of [Southease to Rottingdean or Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-rottingdean-or-brighton/). Chalk downs, open escarpment, quaint village, heart of downs, and Ouse valley. Sights: Lewes Castle and Kipling Gardens. Lunch in Kingston: the Juggs. 20 km.

Escarpments, three distinct downland ridges, wide open chalk paths, panoramic views, historic town streets, and gentle valleys. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 16 km. 380 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921497025)**

SEF.name: Seaford
SEF.privateNote: LON Trainline says 1h25m - Victoria via Lewes
SEF.adminWalksAll:
**Eastbourne to Seaford via Beachy Head**: An essential walk! Spectacular white cliffs in a well-protected national landscape. Immense white chalk cliffs, sea, marshes, meadows, and beachfront. Best on sunny high summer days when the chalk is gleaming. Seven Sisters extremely busy on Summer weekends/bank holidays. Best in May–Jul. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 18 March 2024.* 22 km. 290 m uphill. 6 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/1472764500)**

**[Seaford Circular via Alfriston and Bishopstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/seaford-circular/)**: Steep coastal cliffs, chalk downland, open panoramic ridgelines, vineyard tracks, village greens, shingle beach, and seafront promenade. Sights: Seaford Head, Martello Tower, Hope Gap, Rathfinny Vineyard, Alfriston Church, George Inn, South Hill Barn, and Bishopstone Church. Steep coastal climbs. Lunch in Alfriston, South Hill Barn, or Seaford. Alfriston lunch stop: the George Inn. South Hill Barn lunch stop: the Brewster's Coffee Co. Seaford lunch stop: the Papuchinos at the Galley. 25 km.

**Seaford to Eastbourne**: Rambler favourite! Dramatic clifftop chalk downs, undulating Seven Sisters cliffs, shingle beaches, river haven, and elegant seafront promenade. Sights: [Birling Gap](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/birling-gap-and-the-seven-sisters), Seven Sisters, Belle Tout, and Martello Tower. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch in Exceat: the Cuckmere Inn, the Saltmarsh Kitchen, or the Grab and Go. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 21 km.

**Seaford to Berwick**: An alternative variant of [Berwick to Seaford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-seaford/). Coastal chalk cliffs, downland, river valley, open fields, and village lanes. Sights: Seaford Head, Seven Sisters, High and Over Hill, Cuckmere Haven, Berwick church, and Martello Tower. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch in Alfriston, or Berwick. Alfriston lunch stop: the George Inn. Berwick lunch stops: the Cricketer's Arms, or the Berwick Arms. 16 km.

**Glynde to Seaford via Firle Place**: Rambler favourite! South Downs ridge, chalk downland, Firle Park, river valley, ancient villages, forest, coastal cliffs, shingle beach, and esplanade. Sights: Firle Place, Church of St Peter, Alfriston Clergy House, Parish Church of St Andrew, and Seaford Museum. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch in Alfriston, or Litlington. Alfriston lunch stops: the George Inn, the Star Inn, the Ye Olde Smugglers Inn, the Wingrove Inn, the Chestnuts Tea Room, the Singing Kettle, or the Poco Restaurant & Wine Bar. Litlington lunch stops: the Plough and Harrow pub, or the Littlington Tea Gardens. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 23 km. 7 hours.

**Southease to Seaford via Monk's House**: River valley, ridge-top South Downs, open downland with coastal views, pretty villages, and seaside esplanade. Sights: Monk's House and St Andrew's Church. Lunch in Rodmell: the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 18 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Berwick to Seaford via Long Man of Wilmington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-seaford/)**: South Downs chalk hills, open downland, river valley, coastal cliffs, shingle beach, and picturesque village. Sights: Long Man of Wilmington, Windover Hill, High and Over Hill, Berwick church, Cuckmere Haven, Seven Sisters, Seaford Head, and Martello Tower. Can be muddy. Lunch in Wilmington, Alfriston, Exceat, or Seaford. Wilmington lunch stop: the Long Man Inn. Alfriston lunch stop: the George Inn. Exceat lunch stop: the Cuckmere Inn. Seaford lunch stops: the Trawlers, the Plough Inn, or the Steamworks. 20 km.

**[Lewes to Seaford via West Firle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-seaford-via-west-firle/)**: South Downs ridges, open downland, sweeping valley, remote hidden valley, coastal shingle beach, and recreation ground. Sights: Mount Caburn Iron Age Hill Fort, Firle Park, St Peter's Church, St Andrew's Church Bishopstone, and Firle Tower. Steep climbs. Lunch in West Firle: the Ram Inn. 22 km. 6 hours.

**Southease to Seaford via St Andrew's Church**: Direct route across South Downs, open downland with coastal views, and seaside esplanade. Sights: St Andrew's Church. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 12 km. 4 hours.

**Glynde to Seaford omitting West Firle**: A shorter variant of [Lewes to Seaford via West Firle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-seaford-via-west-firle/). South Downs ridges, open downland, hidden valley, and coastal approach. Sights: Mount Caburn Iron Age Hill Fort and St Andrew's Church Bishopstone. No lunch-time pub. 11 km.

**Glynde to Seaford via West Firle**: A shorter variant of [Lewes to Seaford via West Firle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-seaford-via-west-firle/). South Downs ridges, open downland, hidden valley, and coastal approach. Sights: Firle Park, St Peter's Church, St Andrew's Church Bishopstone, and Firle Tower. Steep climbs. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in West Firle: the Ram Inn. 16 km.

**Lewes to Seaford omitting West Firle**: A variant of [Lewes to Seaford via West Firle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-seaford-via-west-firle/). South Downs ridges, open downland, remote valley, and coastal approach. Sights: Mount Caburn Iron Age Hill Fort and St Andrew's Church Bishopstone. Steep climbs. Lunch in Glynde: the [Trevor Arms](https://www.thetrevor.co.uk/), or the [Steamworks](https://steamworksbars.co.uk/about-us/glynde-steamworks-is-open-6472323). 18 km.

**Berwick to Seaford via Berwick church**: An alternative variant of [Berwick to Seaford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-seaford/). Fields, downland, village lanes, river valley, coastal cliffs, and shingle beach. Sights: Berwick church, High and Over Hill, Cuckmere Haven, Seven Sisters, Seaford Head, and Martello Tower. Can be muddy. Lunch in Berwick, Alfriston, Exceat, or Seaford. Berwick lunch stop: the Cricketer's Arms. Alfriston lunch stop: the George Inn. Exceat lunch stop: the Cuckmere Inn. Seaford lunch stops: the Trawlers, the Plough Inn, or the Steamworks. 18 km.

**Bishopstone to Seaford via Alfriston and Bishopstone**: An alternative variant of [Seaford Circular via Alfriston and Bishopstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/seaford-circular/). Seafront, chalk downland, vineyard tracks, open ridgelines, and coastal cliff finish over Seaford Head. Sights: Seaford Head, Rathfinny Vineyard, Alfriston Church, George Inn, and Bishopstone Church. Steep coastal climbs. Lunch in Alfriston: the George Inn. 22 km.

**Bishopstone to Seaford via Saxon Church of St Andrew**: A longer variant of [Bishopstone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/). All main walk terrain plus seafront promenade into Seaford. Sights: Saxon Church of St Andrew, Long Burgh Barrow, and St Michael and All Angels church. Can be overgrown. Lunch in Berwick: the [Cricketers' Arms](https://www.cricketersberwick.co.uk/). 23 km.

**Lewes to Seaford Walk via South Downs**: Chalk grassland ridges, South Downs, Southease station midpoint, and extended coastal finish at Seaford. Sights: Lewes Castle and Monk's House. Lunch in Rodmell: the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/). A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 24 km.

**Lewes to Seaford via St Andrew's Church**: River valley, ridge-top South Downs, open downland with coastal views, pretty villages, and seaside esplanade. Sights: Monk's House and St Andrew's Church. Lunch in Rodmell: the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/). A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 24 km. 6.5 hours.

Immense white chalk cliffs, sea, marshes, meadows, and beachfront. Best on sunny high summer days when the chalk is gleaming. Seven Sisters extremely busy on Summer weekends/bank holidays. Best in May–Jul. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 22 km. 290 m uphill. 6 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/1472764500)**

SEH.name: Shoreham
SEH.privateNote: Trainline says 37m — London Bridge via Swanley
SEH.adminWalksAll:
**[Shoreham Circular via World Garden](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shoreham-circular/)**: Steep-sided chalk hills, ancient woodland, chalk grassland, open countryside, valley floor, riverside paths, golf course, and lavender fields. Sights: Ss Peter and Paul, Shoreham, [The Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/find-a-kent-country-park/lullingstone-country-park), [Lullingstone Castle](https://www.lullingstonecastle.co.uk), Tudor Gatehouse, St Botolph, Lullingstone, World Garden, [Darent Valley Path](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpdarentvalleypath.html), [Castle Farm](https://www.castlefarmkent.co.uk), [Otford Solar System](https://www.otford.org/solarsystem), Otford Palace, St Bartholomew, Otford, Otford Pond, Shoreham Cross, [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm), Broughton Manor, Pickmoss, Water House, and Holmesdale. Steep descents. Lunch in Shoreham: the Crown, the [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarms-shoreham.foodndrink.uk), the [Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk), or the [Samuel Palmer](https://thesamuelpalmer.com). 20 km. 5 hours.

**Shoreham Circular via Samuel Palmer Trail**: An alternative variant of [Shoreham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shoreham-circular/). Chalk grassland, ancient woodland, valley floor, riverside paths, hillside, lavender fields, and Samuel Palmer Trail landscapes. Sights: Ss Peter and Paul, Shoreham, [The Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/find-a-kent-country-park/lullingstone-country-park), [Samuel Palmer Trail](https://darent-valley.org.uk/explore-the-darent-valley/samuel-palmer-trail), [Castle Farm](https://www.castlefarmkent.co.uk), [Otford Solar System](https://www.otford.org/solarsystem), Otford Palace, Shoreham Cross, and [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm). Steep descents. Lunch in Shoreham: the [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarms-shoreham.foodndrink.uk), the [Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk), the [Samuel Palmer](https://thesamuelpalmer.com), or the Crown. 15 km. 4 hours.

**Shoreham Circular via Ss Peter and Paul, Shoreham**: A shorter variant of [Shoreham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shoreham-circular/). Riverside paths, chalk woodland, chalk grassland, ancient trees, lavender fields, and valley floor. Sights: Ss Peter and Paul, Shoreham, [The Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/find-a-kent-country-park/lullingstone-country-park), [Lullingstone Castle](https://www.lullingstonecastle.co.uk), Tudor Gatehouse, St Botolph, Lullingstone, [Castle Farm](https://www.castlefarmkent.co.uk), and [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm). Lunch in Shoreham: the Crown, the [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarms-shoreham.foodndrink.uk), the [Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk), or the [Samuel Palmer](https://thesamuelpalmer.com). 10 km. 2.5 hours.

**Shoreham Circular via White Hill Nature Reserve**: A shorter variant of [Shoreham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shoreham-circular/). Chalk hillside nature reserves, wildflower meadows, wooded hills, village streets, and riverside paths. Sights: [Otford Solar System](https://www.otford.org/solarsystem), Otford Palace, St Bartholomew, Otford, Otford Pond, Shoreham Cross, [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm), White Hill Nature Reserve, and Fackenden Down. Steep descents. Lunch in Shoreham: the Crown, the [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarms-shoreham.foodndrink.uk), the [Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk), or the [Samuel Palmer](https://thesamuelpalmer.com). 9 km. 2.5 hours.

**Eynsford to Shoreham**: A shorter variant of [Eynsford Circular via Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eynsford-circular-via-otford/). Rambler favourite! Secluded valleys, chalk downland ridges, woodland, chalk grassland nature reserves, and riverside village paths. Sights: [Otford Solar System](https://www.otford.org/solarsystem), Otford Palace, [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm), and [Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk). Can be muddy. Lunch in Otford: the [Bull](https://thebull-otford.co.uk), or the [Olive Tree](https://hospicesofhope.co.uk/our-shops/#our-bistros). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Otford to Shoreham**: Rambler favourite! North Downs hills, chalk woodland, bluebell woods, open commons, grassy fields, and Shoreham village. Sights: Church of St Peter and St Paul and Shoreham Memorial Cross. Can be muddy. Lunch in Shoreham: the King's Arms, the Crown, or the Samuel Palmer. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 8 km.

**Farningham Road to Shoreham via Darent Valley Path**: A shorter variant of [Farningham Road to Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farningham-road-to-otford/). Direct route from station, riverside Darent Valley Path, low hills, and quaint village of Shoreham. Sights: [Darent Valley Path](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpdarentvalleypath.html), [Eagle Heights](https://www.eagleheights.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/lullingstone-country-park), Lullingstone Castle, [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm), and Ss Peter and Paul, Shoreham. Lunch in Farningham: the [Lion Hotel](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thelionfarningham), or the [Pied Bull](https://piedbullfarningham.co.uk). 13 km. 3 hours.

**Farningham Road to Shoreham via Farningham Woods Nature Reserve**: A shorter variant of [Farningham Road to Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farningham-road-to-otford/). Ancient woodland, riverside Darent Valley Path, low hills, and quaint village of Shoreham. Sights: [Farningham Woods Nature Reserve](https://explorekent.org/activities/farningham-woods), [Darent Valley Path](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpdarentvalleypath.html), [Eynsford Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/eynsford-castle), [Eagle Heights](https://www.eagleheights.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/lullingstone-country-park), Lullingstone Castle, [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm), Ss Peter and Paul, Shoreham, and The Mount Vineyard. Motocross path closures possible. Lunch in Farningham: the [Lion Hotel](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thelionfarningham), or the [Pied Bull](https://piedbullfarningham.co.uk). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Knockholt to Shoreham**: A shorter variant of [Knockholt to Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knockholt-to-otford/). Wooded hills, steep ridge climb, and descent into village. Sights: Shoreham Aircraft Museum. Descent can be slippery. Lunch in Shoreham: the Kings Arms, the Samuel Palmer, or the Crown. 5 km.
SEH.publicWalksCircular: **[Shoreham Circular via World Garden](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shoreham-circular/)**: Steep-sided chalk hills, ancient woodland, chalk grassland, open countryside, valley floor, riverside paths, golf course, and lavender fields. Sights: Ss Peter and Paul, Shoreham, [The Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/find-a-kent-country-park/lullingstone-country-park), [Lullingstone Castle](https://www.lullingstonecastle.co.uk), Tudor Gatehouse, St Botolph, Lullingstone, World Garden, [Darent Valley Path](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpdarentvalleypath.html), [Castle Farm](https://www.castlefarmkent.co.uk), [Otford Solar System](https://www.otford.org/solarsystem), Otford Palace, St Bartholomew, Otford, Otford Pond, Shoreham Cross, [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm), Broughton Manor, Pickmoss, Water House, and Holmesdale. Steep descents. Lunch in Shoreham: the Crown, the [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarms-shoreham.foodndrink.uk), the [Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk), or the [Samuel Palmer](https://thesamuelpalmer.com). 20 km. 5 hours.

SET.name: Settle
SET.adminWalksAll:
**[Settle Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/settle-circular-via-malham/)**: Limestone outcrops, crags, pavements, caves, dry valleys, steep pastures, gravel farm tracks, stony ground, moorland, and panoramic upland views. Sights: Malham Cove, Malham Tarn, Janet's Foss, Gordale Scar, Water Sinks, and Watlowes dry valley. Hard on feet/knees. Lunch in Malham: the [Old Barn Tea Room](https://oldbarnmalham.co.uk/), the [Buck Inn](https://www.vixen-pubs.co.uk/buck-inn-pub-malham), the [Lister Arms](https://listerarms.co.uk/), or the [Beck Hall Restaurant](https://www.beckhallmalham.com/restaurant/). 22 km. 6 hours.

**[Horton-in-Ribblesdale to Settle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/fountains-fell-and-catrigg-force/)**: Wild remote fell, moorland plateau, Pennine Way paths, lonely pastures, limestone country, waterfall, elevated bridleways above valley, and views of green dales and tarn. Sights: Fountains Fell, Pen-y-ghent, Malham Tarn, Catrigg Force, and Winskill Stones National Nature Reserve. Can be boggy/rough. 24 km. 6 hours.
SET.publicWalksCircular: **[Settle Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/settle-circular-via-malham/)**: Limestone outcrops, crags, pavements, caves, dry valleys, steep pastures, gravel farm tracks, stony ground, moorland, and panoramic upland views. Sights: Malham Cove, Malham Tarn, Janet's Foss, Gordale Scar, Water Sinks, and Watlowes dry valley. Hard on feet/knees. Lunch in Malham: the [Old Barn Tea Room](https://oldbarnmalham.co.uk/), the [Buck Inn](https://www.vixen-pubs.co.uk/buck-inn-pub-malham), the [Lister Arms](https://listerarms.co.uk/), or the [Beck Hall Restaurant](https://www.beckhallmalham.com/restaurant/). 22 km. 6 hours.
SET.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Horton-in-Ribblesdale to Settle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/fountains-fell-and-catrigg-force/)**: Wild remote fell, moorland plateau, Pennine Way paths, lonely pastures, limestone country, waterfall, elevated bridleways above valley, and views of green dales and tarn. Sights: Fountains Fell, Pen-y-ghent, Malham Tarn, Catrigg Force, and Winskill Stones National Nature Reserve. Can be boggy/rough. 24 km. 6 hours.

SEV.name: Sevenoaks
SEV.adminWalksAll:
**Borough Green to Sevenoaks via Ightham Mote**: An essential walk! Greensand hills, apple orchards, woodland paths, streams, deer park, open parkland, and historic manor grounds. Sights: [Ightham Mote](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-ighthammote) (smallish but charming 14th C. manor; you can easily fit a full visit into your walk), [Knole House](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-knole) (sprawling ancient aristocratic seat; well worth a visit but requires an entire day in itself), Knole Park, Plaxtol Parish Church, and Fairlawne House. Can be muddy. Lunch in Ightham Mote, or Shipbourne. Ightham Mote lunch stop: the [National Trust café](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-ighthammote). Shipbourne lunch stop: the Chaser Inn. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 25 May 2025.* 15 km. 250 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2902569123)**

**[Sevenoaks Circular via Knole House](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sevenoaks-circular/)**: Rambler favourite! Deer park, fields, woods, coppiced woodland, escarpment ridge, bluebell and wild garlic woods, and Weald views. Sights: Knole House, Knole Park, Ightham Mote, and One Tree Hill. Some mud inevitable. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Knole House, Godden Green, or Ightham Mote. Knole House lunch stop: the Brewhouse Cafe. Godden Green lunch stop: the Bucks Head. Ightham Mote lunch stop: the Ightham Mote restaurant. 16 km.

**[Yalding to Sevenoaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-to-sevenoaks/)**: Rambler favourite! Undulating Kent farmland, orchards, bluebell woods, greensand escarpment, and deer park. Sights: Ightham Mote, Knole House, and One Tree Hill. Waymarking can disappear. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in West Peckham, Dunks Green, or Shipbourne. West Peckham lunch stop: the Swan. Dunks Green lunch stop: the Kentish Rifleman. Shipbourne lunch stop: the Chaser Inn. 21 km.

**Leigh to Sevenoaks**: Rolling Kentish farmland, fields, oasthouses, bluebell woods, steep greensand hillside, and deer park. Sights: St Mary's Church, Hall Place, Knole Park, Knole House, and Brewhouse Tearoom. Can be muddy. Lunch in Underriver: the White Rock. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Hurst Green to Sevenoaks via Toy's Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hurst-green-to-sevenoaks/)**: Greensand ridge, chart hills, undulating woodland, commons, deer park, historic estates, and suburban old town. Sights: Chartwell, Toy's Hill, Ide Hill, Knole Park, and Knole Palace. Steep climbs. Lunch in Chartwell, or Toys Hill. Chartwell lunch stop: the NT restaurant at Chartwell. Toys Hill lunch stop: the [Fox & Hounds](http://www.foxhoundstoyshill.co.uk/). 18 km.

**Edenbridge to Sevenoaks**: Open fields, NE ascent to Toy's Hill, and easterly woodland walking after Ide Hill. Sights: Toy's Hill and Ide Hill. Lunch in Ide Hill: the [Cock Inn](https://cockinnidehill.co.uk/). An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 17 km.

**Hurst Green to Sevenoaks via Chartwell**: Easterly direction and mainly woodland walking. Sights: [Chartwell](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk) and Ide Hill. Lunch in Chartwell, or Ide Hill. Chartwell lunch stop: the [NT restaurant at Chartwell](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk). Ide Hill lunch stop: the [Cock Inn](https://cockinnidehill.co.uk/). An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 21 km.
SEV.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Borough Green to Sevenoaks via Ightham Mote**: An essential walk! Greensand hills, apple orchards, woodland paths, streams, deer park, open parkland, and historic manor grounds. Sights: [Ightham Mote](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-ighthammote) (smallish but charming 14th C. manor; you can easily fit a full visit into your walk), [Knole House](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-knole) (sprawling ancient aristocratic seat; well worth a visit but requires an entire day in itself), Knole Park, Plaxtol Parish Church, and Fairlawne House. Can be muddy. Lunch in Ightham Mote, or Shipbourne. Ightham Mote lunch stop: the [National Trust café](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-ighthammote). Shipbourne lunch stop: the Chaser Inn. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 15 km. 250 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2902569123)**

SFR.name: Shalford
SFR.adminWalksAll:
**[Shalford Circular via Blackheath Common War Memorial](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shalford-circular/)**: Sandy hills, heathland, woods, river valley, water meadows, historic mill ruins, open downland, and quiet farmland. Sights: [Shalford Mill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/shalford-mill), [St Martha-on-the-Hill](https://www.parishofchilworth.org.uk/history/st-marthas/full-history), [Chilworth Gunpowder Mills](https://www.chilworthgunpowdermills.co.uk), Incorporating Mills, Chilworth Manor, St Catherine's Chapel, Blackheath Common War Memorial, and Guildford Cathedral. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chilworth: the [Percy Arms](https://www.thepercyarms.net). 22 km. 5.5 hours.

**Shalford Circular via Shalford Mill**: A shorter variant of [Shalford Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shalford-circular/). Sandy hills, woods, river valley, historic mill ruins, open downland, and water meadows. Sights: [Shalford Mill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/shalford-mill), [St Martha-on-the-Hill](https://www.parishofchilworth.org.uk/history/st-marthas/full-history), [Chilworth Gunpowder Mills](https://www.chilworthgunpowdermills.co.uk), Incorporating Mills, Chilworth Manor, and St Catherine's Chapel. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chilworth: the [Percy Arms](https://www.thepercyarms.net). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**Shalford to Chilworth**: A shorter variant of [Shalford Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shalford-circular/). Sandy hills, heathland, woods, river valley, historic mill ruins, and open downland. Sights: [Shalford Mill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/shalford-mill), [St Martha-on-the-Hill](https://www.parishofchilworth.org.uk/history/st-marthas/full-history), [Chilworth Gunpowder Mills](https://www.chilworthgunpowdermills.co.uk), Incorporating Mills, Chilworth Manor, and Blackheath Common War Memorial. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chilworth: the [Percy Arms](https://www.thepercyarms.net). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**Chilworth to Shalford via Chantries Hill Circular**: A shorter variant of [Guildford via Chantries Hill Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/guildford-via-chantries-hill-circular/). Rambler favourite! Short walk through Albury, heathland and woods, and Shalford watermeadows. Lunch in Albury: the Drummond at Albury. 11 km.

**Guildford to Shalford**: A shorter variant of [Guildford via Chantries Hill Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/guildford-via-chantries-hill-circular/). Rambler favourite! Hilly Surrey Hills, wooded escarpment, heathland, and Shalford watermeadows. Sights: St Martha's-on-the-Hill church and Chantries Hill. Can be muddy. Lunch in Albury: the Drummond at Albury. 17 km.

**Chilworth to Shalford via Chilworth Gunpowder Mills**: A shorter variant of [Shalford Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shalford-circular/). Heathland, sandy woods, river valley, historic mill ruins, open downland, and water meadows. Sights: [Chilworth Gunpowder Mills](https://www.chilworthgunpowdermills.co.uk), Incorporating Mills, Blackheath Common War Memorial, and St Catherine's Chapel. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chilworth: the [Percy Arms](https://www.thepercyarms.net). 13 km. 3.5 hours.

**Gomshall to Shalford**: Fields, wooded valley, pine woods, heathland common, stud farms, and riverside path to a village station. Sights: Church of St James, Albury Park, Chinthurst Hill Tower, and Great Tangley Manor House. Can be muddy. Lunch in Albury Heath, or Shere. Albury Heath lunch stop: the William IV. Shere lunch stops: the White Horse, or the William Bray. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).
SFR.publicWalksCircular: **[Shalford Circular via Blackheath Common War Memorial](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shalford-circular/)**: Sandy hills, heathland, woods, river valley, water meadows, historic mill ruins, open downland, and quiet farmland. Sights: [Shalford Mill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/shalford-mill), [St Martha-on-the-Hill](https://www.parishofchilworth.org.uk/history/st-marthas/full-history), [Chilworth Gunpowder Mills](https://www.chilworthgunpowdermills.co.uk), Incorporating Mills, Chilworth Manor, St Catherine's Chapel, Blackheath Common War Memorial, and Guildford Cathedral. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chilworth: the [Percy Arms](https://www.thepercyarms.net). 22 km. 5.5 hours.

SHF.name: Sheffield
SHF.adminWalksAll:
**[Sheffield to Bamford via Stanage Edge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sheffield-to-bamford-via-stanage-edge/)**: Urban streets, landscaped Victorian cemetery, municipal parkland, narrow wooded valley, open moorland, rugged gritstone edges, and a scenic descent into lush valley. Sights: [Victorian General Cemetery](http://gencem.org/), [Endcliffe Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Endcliffe_Park), [Porter Valley](https://www.fopv.org.uk/), [Peak District](http://www.peakdistrict.gov.uk/), Burbage Rocks, and Stanage Edge. Can be muddy. Lunch in Fulwood, Sheffield, Ringinglow, or Longshaw. Fulwood, Sheffield lunch stop: the [Forge Dam Café](https://forgedamcafe.co.uk/). Ringinglow lunch stop: the [Norfolk Arms](http://norfolkarms.com/). Longshaw lunch stop: the [Fox House Inn](http://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/yorkshire/thefoxhouselongshaw). 25 km. 6.5 hours.

**Sheffield to Dore and Totley**: A shorter variant of [Sheffield Circular via Porter and Gleadless](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sheffield-circular-via-porter-and-gleadless/). Ancient escarpment woodlands, wooded valleys, moorland fringe, panoramic views, and urban parks. Sights: [Sheffield General Cemetery](http://gencem.org/), Endcliffe Park, [Porter Valley](https://www.fopv.org.uk/), [Peak District](http://www.peakdistrict.gov.uk/), [Ecclesall Woods](http://www.friendsofecclesallwoods.org.uk/about/ecclesall), and Whirlow Brook Park. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Sheffield, Fulwood, Sheffield, Ringinglow, Sheffield, Whirlow Brook Park, Sheffield, or Dore, Sheffield. Sheffield lunch stop: the Endcliffe Park Café. Fulwood, Sheffield lunch stop: the [Forge Dam Café](https://forgedamcafe.co.uk/). Ringinglow, Sheffield lunch stop: the [Norfolk Arms](http://norfolkarms.com/). Whirlow Brook Park, Sheffield lunch stop: the Shelter Café. Dore, Sheffield lunch stop: the [Summer House](http://www.thesummerhousesheffield.com/).

**Sheffield to Bamford via Stanage Edge & Carl Wark**: A variant of [Sheffield to Bamford via Stanage Edge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sheffield-to-bamford-via-stanage-edge/). Urban streets, wooded valley, open moorland, rocky tors, gritstone edges, and valley descent. Sights: Carl Wark, Higger Tor, and Stanage Edge. Can be muddy. Lunch in Longshaw: the [Fox House Inn](http://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/yorkshire/thefoxhouselongshaw).
SHF.publicWalksS2S: **[Sheffield to Bamford via Stanage Edge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sheffield-to-bamford-via-stanage-edge/)**: Urban streets, landscaped Victorian cemetery, municipal parkland, narrow wooded valley, open moorland, rugged gritstone edges, and a scenic descent into lush valley. Sights: [Victorian General Cemetery](http://gencem.org/), [Endcliffe Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Endcliffe_Park), [Porter Valley](https://www.fopv.org.uk/), [Peak District](http://www.peakdistrict.gov.uk/), Burbage Rocks, and Stanage Edge. Can be muddy. Lunch in Fulwood, Sheffield, Ringinglow, or Longshaw. Fulwood, Sheffield lunch stop: the [Forge Dam Café](https://forgedamcafe.co.uk/). Ringinglow lunch stop: the [Norfolk Arms](http://norfolkarms.com/). Longshaw lunch stop: the [Fox House Inn](http://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/yorkshire/thefoxhouselongshaw). 25 km. 6.5 hours.

SHI.name: Shiplake
SHI.adminWalksAll:
**Shiplake to Henley via Greys Court**: Riverside Thames path, bluebell and beech woods, open agricultural fields, gentle hills, golf course, historic village, and market town riverfront. Sights: Shiplake Lock, Shiplake Church, Shiplake College, St Nicholas Church, Greys Court, Lambridge Wood, Friar Park, Friar Park's lodge house, St Mary the Virgin, and Red Lion Hotel. Can flood after rain. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Rotherfield Greys, or Binfield Heath. Rotherfield Greys lunch stop: the Maltsters Arms. Binfield Heath lunch stop: the Bottle & Glass Inn. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 17 km. 170 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920707447)**

**Shiplake Circular**: Riverside Thames path, bluebell and beech woods, open agricultural fields, gentle hills, market town, and return riverside towpath. Sights: Shiplake Lock, Shiplake Church, Greys Court, Lambridge Wood, Friar Park, and St Mary the Virgin. Footbridge may be closed. Lunch in Rotherfield Greys: the Maltsters Arms. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 21 km.

**Shiplake to Henley-on-Thames via High Wood**: Bluebell woods, quiet lanes, open fields, beech woods, golf course, and historic town riverfront. Sights: High Wood, Greys Court, Lambridge Wood, Friar Park, and St Mary the Virgin. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Binfield Heath, or Rotherfield Greys. Binfield Heath lunch stop: the Bottle & Glass Inn. Rotherfield Greys lunch stop: the Maltsters Arms. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km.

**Henley-on-Thames to Shiplake via Great Wood**: Rambler favourite! Thames riverside path extension from Henley to Shiplake station. Sights: Temple Island, Hambleden Lock, and St Mary the Virgin Church, Hambleden. Lunch in Hambleden: the Stag & Huntsman. A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).

**Henley-on-Thames to Shiplake via Rotherfield Greys**: A shorter variant of [Henley Circular via Rotherfield Greys](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/henley-circular-via-rotherfield-greys/). Pleasant valleys, undulating woodland, bluebell woods, open fields, and ending at Shiplake station. Sights: [Bottle & Glass Inn](https://www.bottleandglassinn.com/) and Baskerville. Lunch in Binfield Heath: the [Bottle & Glass](https://www.bottleandglassinn.com/). 13 km.
SHI.publicWalksS2S: **Shiplake to Henley via Greys Court**: Riverside Thames path, bluebell and beech woods, open agricultural fields, gentle hills, golf course, historic village, and market town riverfront. Sights: Shiplake Lock, Shiplake Church, Shiplake College, St Nicholas Church, Greys Court, Lambridge Wood, Friar Park, Friar Park's lodge house, St Mary the Virgin, and Red Lion Hotel. Can flood after rain. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Rotherfield Greys, or Binfield Heath. Rotherfield Greys lunch stop: the Maltsters Arms. Binfield Heath lunch stop: the Bottle & Glass Inn. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 17 km. 170 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920707447)**

SHO.name: Sholing
SHO.adminWalksAll: **Botley to Sholing (reverse walk with extension)**: Farmland, woodland, riverside causeway, stony beach, coastal country park, shoreline tarmac path, and wooded valley following the Itchen Way. Sights: Manor Farm and Country Park, St Bartholomew's Church, Warsash Ferry, Hamble Common, Netley Chapel, Netley Castle, Westwood Woodland Park, Cruise Liner Terminal, and Itchen Way. Check tide timings. Lunch in Lower Swanwick: the Harper's Steakhouse (formerly The Navigator), the Boat House Cafe and Restaurant, the Banana Wharf, or the Mermaids at Universal. A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 20 km.
SHO.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Botley to Sholing (reverse walk with extension)**: Farmland, woodland, riverside causeway, stony beach, coastal country park, shoreline tarmac path, and wooded valley following the Itchen Way. Sights: Manor Farm and Country Park, St Bartholomew's Church, Warsash Ferry, Hamble Common, Netley Chapel, Netley Castle, Westwood Woodland Park, Cruise Liner Terminal, and Itchen Way. Check tide timings. Lunch in Lower Swanwick: the Harper's Steakhouse (formerly The Navigator), the Boat House Cafe and Restaurant, the Banana Wharf, or the Mermaids at Universal. A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 20 km.

SHW.name: Shawford
SHW.adminWalksAll: **Winchester to Shawford**: A shorter variant of [Winchester Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchester-circular/). Historic cathedral city streets, watermeadows, iron age hillfort, and river valley. Sights: Winchester Cathedral, Great Hall, King Arthur's Round Table, Westgate, and St Catherine's Hill. M3 motorway noise. Lunch in Shawford: the Bridge. 8 km.
SHW.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Winchester to Shawford**: A shorter variant of [Winchester Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchester-circular/). Historic cathedral city streets, watermeadows, iron age hillfort, and river valley. Sights: Winchester Cathedral, Great Hall, King Arthur's Round Table, Westgate, and St Catherine's Hill. M3 motorway noise. Lunch in Shawford: the Bridge. 8 km.

SLO.name: Slough
SLO.adminWalksAll: **[Slough to Egham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/slough-to-egham-via-windsor-great-park/)**: Flat urban start, parkland, deer park, historic royal landscape, ornamental gardens, and lake. Sights: Windsor Castle, Savill Gardens, Valley Gardens, Virginia Water lake, and [The Royal Landscape](http://www.theroyallandscape.co.uk/visiting/). 19 km. 4 hours.
SLO.publicWalksS2S: **[Slough to Egham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/slough-to-egham-via-windsor-great-park/)**: Flat urban start, parkland, deer park, historic royal landscape, ornamental gardens, and lake. Sights: Windsor Castle, Savill Gardens, Valley Gardens, Virginia Water lake, and [The Royal Landscape](http://www.theroyallandscape.co.uk/visiting/). 19 km. 4 hours.

SMD.name: Stamford
SMD.privateNote: THIS IS A BLOODY TEST. IF IT PERSISTS, PUBLISH THINGY IS WORKING.
SMD.adminWalksAll: **[Stamford Circular via Pilsgate](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Old railway, Roman road, park grounds, and meadows. Sights: Burghley Park, Wothorpe Towers, Torpel Way, Ermine Street, Hereward Way, and Jurassic Way. 17 km. 90 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/415%20Stamford.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925788133)**

SMT.name: St Margarets
SMT.adminWalksAll:
**[St Margarets Circular via Hollycross Lake](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/st-margarets-circular/)**: Rolling East Hertfordshire countryside, wheat fields, woods, ancient tracks, green lanes, river valleys along Ash, Rib and Lea rivers, and scenic nature reserve. Sights: Thundridge Old Church, Amwell Nature Reserve, and Hollycross Lake. Can be muddy. Lunch in Thundridge, or Wadesmill. Thundridge lunch stop: the [Ermines Café & Stores](https://erminesnum39.wixsite.com/ermines-cafe). Wadesmill lunch stop: the [Feathers Inn](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/hertfordshire/feathers). 24 km. 5.5 hours.

**St Margarets Circular via Amwell Nature Reserve**: A shorter variant of [St Margarets Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/st-margarets-circular/). Rolling East Hertfordshire countryside, wheat fields, woods, river valleys, and figure-of-eight loop. Sights: Amwell Nature Reserve. Can be muddy. Lunch in Wareside: the [Chequers Inn](http://www.chequerswareside.com/). 12 km.

**St Margarets Circular via Thundridge Old Church**: A shorter variant of [St Margarets Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/st-margarets-circular/). Rolling East Hertfordshire countryside, wheat fields, woods, river valleys, and hourglass-shaped loop. Sights: Thundridge Old Church and Amwell Nature Reserve. Can be muddy. Lunch in Wareside: the [Chequers Inn](http://www.chequerswareside.com/). 19 km.

Sights: Thundridge Old Church, Amwell Nature Reserve, and Hollycross Lake. Can be muddy. Lunch in Thundridge, or Wadesmill. Thundridge lunch stop: the [Ermines Café & Stores](https://erminesnum39.wixsite.com/ermines-cafe). Wadesmill lunch stop: the [Feathers Inn](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/hertfordshire/feathers). 24 km. 5.5 hours.

SND.name: Sandhurst
SND.adminWalksAll:
**Sandhurst to Crowthorne**: A variant of [Blackwater to Wokingham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackwater-to-wokingham/). Roads from station, gravel-pit lakes, nature reserves, country lanes, mixed woodland, heathland, and pine woods. Sights: [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), Ambarrow Court Local Nature Reserve, Wellington College, and St James's Church. Lunch in Finchampstead: the [Queen's Oak](https://www.thequeensoak.com), or the [Greyhound](https://greyhoundfinchampstead.co.uk). 14 km.

**Sandhurst to Wokingham via Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre**: A variant of [Blackwater to Wokingham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackwater-to-wokingham/). Roads from station, Horseshoe Lake circuit, fields, pine woodland, heathland, golf course, soft fruit farms, and market town. Sights: [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), Ambarrow Court Local Nature Reserve, Heathlake Nature Reserve, and [Wokingham](https://www.wokinghamsociety.org.uk/history.html). Lunch in Horseshoe Lake: the [Lake View Café](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk/lakeviewcafe). 14 km.

**Sandhurst to Wokingham via Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve**: A variant of [Blackwater to Wokingham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackwater-to-wokingham/). Roads from station, gravel-pit lakes, nature reserves, country lanes, mixed and pine woodland, heathland, golf course, soft fruit farms, and historic market town. Sights: [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), Ambarrow Court Local Nature Reserve, Wellington College, Heathlake Nature Reserve, [Wokingham](https://www.wokinghamsociety.org.uk/history.html), St James's Church, Wellingtonia Avenue, Wokingham Town Hall, Tudor House, and Rose Street. Lunch in Finchampstead: the [Queen's Oak](https://www.thequeensoak.com), or the [Greyhound](https://greyhoundfinchampstead.co.uk). 22 km.

**Crowthorne to Sandhurst**: A shorter variant of [Crowthorne to Blackwater via Eversley Cross](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/crowthorne-to-blackwater-via-eversley-cross/). Pine woods, heathland, lakes and flooded gravel pits, riverside meadows, and town roads. Sights: [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), Wellingtonia Avenue, and Martineau Cottages. Flood risk in places. Lunch in Eversley Cross: the [Frog and Wicket](https://www.thefrogandwicket.co.uk), or the [Chequers](https://www.thechequerseversleycross.co.uk). 13 km.

SNG.name: Sunningdale
SNG.adminWalksAll:
**Sunningdale to Windsor**: Parkland, lake shores, ornamental gardens, light woodland, deer park, tree-lined avenue, and historic town streets. Sights: Holy Trinity Sunningdale Church, Coworth Park, Virginia Water Lake, Valley Gardens, Totem Pole, Obelisk, [Savill Gardens](http://www.windsorgreatpark.co.uk/), Cow Pond, Royal Lodge, Copper Horse, Long Walk, Windsor Castle, and Guildhall. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Savill Gardens: the Savill Garden Restaurant. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 90 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921106856)**

**Sunningdale to Windsor and Eton Central**: Parkland, lake shores, ornamental gardens, light woodland, deer park, tree-lined avenue, and historic town streets. Sights: Holy Trinity Sunningdale Church, Coworth Park, Virginia Water Lake, Valley Gardens, Totem Pole, Obelisk, [Savill Gardens](http://www.windsorgreatpark.co.uk/), Cow Pond, Royal Lodge, Copper Horse, Long Walk, Windsor Castle, and Guildhall. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Savill Gardens: the Savill Garden Restaurant. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Sunningdale to Staines**: Parkland, fields, quiet lanes, riverside meadows, Thames Path, and light woodland. Sights: Holy Trinity Sunningdale Church, Coworth Park, Virginia Water Lake, Valley Gardens, Totem Pole, Obelisk, [Savill Gardens](http://www.windsorgreatpark.co.uk/), [Runnymede](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Runnymede), John F Kennedy Memorial, and [Magna Carta Memorial](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Magna_Carta). Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Savill Gardens, or Englefield Green. Savill Gardens lunch stop: the Savill Garden Restaurant. Englefield Green lunch stop: the Bailiwick. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 4 hours.

Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Savill Gardens: the Savill Garden Restaurant. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 90 m uphill. 4 hours. SNL.name: Stoneleigh
SNL.adminWalksAll:
**[Stoneleigh to Putney Bridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/beverley-brook/)**: Suburban streets, riverside brookside paths, commons, parkland, re-naturalised meanders, wet woodland, and Thames-side esplanade. Sights: Wimbledon Common, Richmond Park, Barnes Common, Beverley Meads LNR, Craven Cottage, and Barn Elms. Can be muddy. Lunch in Worcester Park, Motspur Park, New Malden, West Wimbledon, London SW15, East Sheen, or Barnes. Worcester Park lunch stop: the [Brook Pub & Dining](https://thebrook worcesterpark.co.uk/). Motspur Park lunch stops: the Blossom & Brew, the Café Rich Point, or the [Earl Beatty](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/surrey/earl-beatty). New Malden lunch stop: the [Glasshouse](https://www.glasshouse.pub/). West Wimbledon lunch stop: the [Da Mimmo](https://www.damimmowimbledon.com/). London SW15 lunch stop: the [Roehampton Gate Café](https://www.colicci.co.uk/roehampton-gate-cafe). East Sheen lunch stop: the [Palewell Café on the Common](https://www.colicci.co.uk/roehampton-gate-cafe). Barnes lunch stops: the Café Loris, or the [Stag's Head](https://www.stagsheadbarnes.co.uk/). 21 km. 4.5 hours.

**Stoneleigh to New Malden**: A shorter variant of [Beverley Brook](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/beverley-brook/). Suburban streets and brookside paths through built-up areas. Lunch in Worcester Park, Motspur Park, or New Malden. Worcester Park lunch stop: the [Brook Pub & Dining](https://thebrook worcesterpark.co.uk/). Motspur Park lunch stops: the Blossom & Brew, the Café Rich Point, or the [Earl Beatty](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/surrey/earl-beatty). New Malden lunch stop: the [Glasshouse](https://www.glasshouse.pub/). 8 km.

**Stoneleigh to Putney**: An alternative variant of [Beverley Brook](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/beverley-brook/). Suburban streets, riverside brookside paths, commons, parkland, and Thames-side esplanade. Sights: Wimbledon Common, Richmond Park, Barnes Common, and Beverley Meads LNR. Can be muddy. Lunch in Worcester Park, Motspur Park, New Malden, West Wimbledon, London SW15, East Sheen, or Barnes. Worcester Park lunch stop: the [Brook Pub & Dining](https://thebrook worcesterpark.co.uk/). Motspur Park lunch stops: the Blossom & Brew, the Café Rich Point, or the [Earl Beatty](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/surrey/earl-beatty). New Malden lunch stop: the [Glasshouse](https://www.glasshouse.pub/). West Wimbledon lunch stop: the [Da Mimmo](https://www.damimmowimbledon.com/). London SW15 lunch stop: the [Roehampton Gate Café](https://www.colicci.co.uk/roehampton-gate-cafe). East Sheen lunch stop: the [Palewell Café on the Common](https://www.colicci.co.uk/roehampton-gate-cafe). Barnes lunch stops: the Café Loris, or the [Stag's Head](https://www.stagsheadbarnes.co.uk/).

SOB.name: Southbourne
SOB.adminWalksAll:
**[Southbourne to Emsworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southbourne-to-emsworth-via-thorney-island/)**: Flat coastal sea wall, tidal harbour, salt marsh, sand dunes, beach, mudflats, tidal lake, and historic village high street. Sights: St Nicholas Church, Pilsey Island RSPB Reserve, Brook Meadow Local Nature Reserve, and [Chichester Harbour](http://www.conservancy.co.uk). Check tide timings. Lunch in Southbourne, or Emsworth. Southbourne lunch stop: the [Traveller's Joy](http://www.travellersjoyemsworth.co.uk/). Emsworth lunch stops: the [Lord Raglan](http://www.lord-raglan-emsworth.co.uk/), the [Ship Inn](http://www.shipinnemsworth.co.uk/), the [Driftwood Cafe](http://www.driftwood-cafe.co.uk/), the [Greenhouse Cafe](http://greenhousecafe.co.uk/), the [Coal Exchange](http://www.thecoalexchange.co.uk/), the [Blue Bell](http://www.bluebellinnemsworth.co.uk/), the [Crown Inn](http://www.thecrownemsworth.com/), or the [Railway Inn](http://www.therailwayemsworth.co.uk/). 15 km.

**Southbourne to Chichester**: Flat tidal harbour shoreline, raised sea walls, mudflats, fields, light woodland, duck pond, and pedestrianised historic city centre. Sights: Parish Church of St Mary, Chidham, Holy Trinity Church, Fishbourne Roman Palace, Bishop's Palace Gardens, Chichester Cathedral, Pallant House Gallery, Market Cross, and Festival Theatre. Can be muddy. Best at high tide. Lunch in Chidham, or Bosham. Chidham lunch stop: the Old House at Home. Bosham lunch stops: the Anchor Bleu, the Shoreside Cafe, the Bosham Walk Arts & Crafts Centre café, or the Cumberland Gallery coffee shop. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 18 km. 5 hours.

**Southbourne to Bosham**: Flat tidal harbour shoreline, raised sea walls, mudflats, fields, and quaint harbourside village. Sights: Parish Church of St Mary, Chidham and Holy Trinity Church. Can be muddy. Best at high tide. Lunch in Chidham, or Bosham. Chidham lunch stop: the Old House at Home. Bosham lunch stops: the Anchor Bleu, or the Shoreside Cafe. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).

SOF.name: South Woodham Ferrers
SOF.adminWalksAll:
**South Woodham Ferrers Circular**: An alternative variant of [South Woodham Ferrers to North Fambridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/south-woodham-ferrers-to-north-fambridge/). Rolling hills, arable fields, vineyards, bluebell woods, and Essex countryside. Sights: RHS Garden Hyde Hall. Lunch in East Hanningfield: the [Vita Bella](https://www.vita-bella.co.uk/). 19 km.

**[South Woodham Ferrers to North Fambridge via Martins Lane Estate](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/south-woodham-ferrers-to-north-fambridge/)**: Rolling hills, arable fields, vineyards, bluebell woods, disused railway line, tidal riverside, and grassy salt marshes. Sights: RHS Garden Hyde Hall, Blue House Farm Nature Reserve, Martins Lane Estate, and Crows Lane Estate. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in East Hanningfield: the [Vita Bella](https://www.vita-bella.co.uk/). 25 km. 5.5 hours.

**South Woodham Ferrers to North Fambridge via RHS Garden Hyde Hall & Blue House Farm Nature Reserve**: A longer variant of [South Woodham Ferrers to North Fambridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/south-woodham-ferrers-to-north-fambridge/). Rolling hills, arable fields, vineyards, bluebell woods, extended riverside walking, and permissive paths around nature reserve with bird hides. Sights: RHS Garden Hyde Hall and Blue House Farm Nature Reserve. Lunch in East Hanningfield: the [Vita Bella](https://www.vita-bella.co.uk/). 28 km.

**Wickford to South Woodham Ferrers**: An alternative variant of [Wickford to Battlesbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wickford-to-battlesbridge/). Flat brook-side path, fields, fenland nature reserve, saltmarshes, and tidal River Crouch with mudflats and creeks. Sights: Woodham Fenn Nature Reserve and Marsh Farm Country Park. Best at low tide. Lunch in Hanningfield Reservoir, or South Hanningfield. Hanningfield Reservoir lunch stop: the [Café on the Water (& The Tackle Shop)](https://www.watersideparksuk.com/park/hanningfield/cafe-on-the-water/). South Hanningfield lunch stop: the [Old Windmill](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/oldwindmill/). 29 km.
SOF.publicWalksS2S: **[South Woodham Ferrers to North Fambridge via Martins Lane Estate](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/south-woodham-ferrers-to-north-fambridge/)**: Rolling hills, arable fields, vineyards, bluebell woods, disused railway line, tidal riverside, and grassy salt marshes. Sights: RHS Garden Hyde Hall, Blue House Farm Nature Reserve, Martins Lane Estate, and Crows Lane Estate. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in East Hanningfield: the [Vita Bella](https://www.vita-bella.co.uk/). 25 km. 5.5 hours.

SOG.name: Stonegate
SOG.adminWalksAll:
**Stonegate Circular via Bateman's**: Rolling hills, tranquil valleys, classic English woodland, pastureland, and unspoiled village. Sights: [Bateman's](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk) and Church of St. Bartholomew. Nettles and brambles. Lunch in Burwash, or Burwash Weald. Burwash lunch stops: the Rose and Crown, or the [Bear Inn](http://www.bear-inn-hotel-burwash.co.uk/). Burwash Weald lunch stop: the [Wheel Inn](http://www.thewheelburwashweald.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 15 km. 5 hours.

**[Stonegate to Robertsbridge via Batemans](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/stonegate-to-robertsbridge/)**: Hilly High Weald, valleys, ridgetop village, hornbeam woods, country estate, and panoramic ridge views. Sights: Batemans. Can be muddy. Best in Oct, Nov and Apr. Lunch in Burwash, or Robertsbridge. Burwash lunch stops: the Rose & Crown, the Bear Inn, or the Blacksmiths Tea and Coffee Shop. Robertsbridge lunch stops: the Ostrich, or the George Inn. 14 km.

**Stonegate to Wadhurst**: Rolling hills, tranquil valleys, woodland, pastureland, and remote wilderness sections. Sights: [Bateman's](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk) and Church of St. Bartholomew. Nettles and brambles. Lunch in Burwash Weald: the [Wheel Inn](http://www.thewheelburwashweald.co.uk/). An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 32 km.

**Wadhurst to Stonegate**: High Weald AONB, rolling hills, woods, quiet farmland, and historic villages. Sights: Church of St Peter and St Paul and Church of Saint Dunstans. Can be muddy. Lunch in Mayfield: the [Rose & Crown Inn](http://www.roseandcrownmayfield.co.uk/). An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km.

**Robertsbridge to Stonegate**: An alternative variant of [Robertsbridge Circular via Brightling](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/robertsbridge-circular-via-brightling/). East Sussex countryside, rolling hills, fields, and woodland via Burwash to Stonegate. Lunch in Burwash: the Wheel Inn. 18 km. 5.5 hours.
SOG.publicWalksS2S: **[Stonegate to Robertsbridge via Batemans](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/stonegate-to-robertsbridge/)**: Hilly High Weald, valleys, ridgetop village, hornbeam woods, country estate, and panoramic ridge views. Sights: Batemans. Can be muddy. Best in Oct, Nov and Apr. Lunch in Burwash, or Robertsbridge. Burwash lunch stops: the Rose & Crown, the Bear Inn, or the Blacksmiths Tea and Coffee Shop. Robertsbridge lunch stops: the Ostrich, or the George Inn. 14 km.
SOG.publicWalksCircular: **Stonegate Circular via Bateman's**: Rolling hills, tranquil valleys, classic English woodland, pastureland, and unspoiled village. Sights: [Bateman's](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk) and Church of St. Bartholomew. Nettles and brambles. Lunch in Burwash, or Burwash Weald. Burwash lunch stops: the Rose and Crown, or the [Bear Inn](http://www.bear-inn-hotel-burwash.co.uk/). Burwash Weald lunch stop: the [Wheel Inn](http://www.thewheelburwashweald.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 15 km. 5 hours.

SOL.name: Solihull
SOL.adminWalksAll: **[Solihull Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-855)**: Town centre, parks, ornamental gardens, lake, River Blythe, woodland, and open fields. Sights: [St. Alphege Church](http://www.solihullparish.org.uk), [Manor House](http://www.themanorhousesolihull.com), [Malvern Park](http://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/), [Parkridge Nature Reserve](http://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/), [Malvern & Brueton Park](http://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/), and [Tudor Grange Park](http://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Solihull, or Brueton Park, Solihull. Solihull lunch stops: the [Taste Collective](http://www.tastecollectivesolihull.co.uk), the [Jamaya](http://www.jamaya.co.uk), the Tap & Tandoor, the [Manor House Tea Room](http://www.themanorhousesolihull.com), or the Vita. Brueton Park, Solihull lunch stop: the Parkridge Café. 8 km. 3 hours.
SOL.publicWalksCircular: **[Solihull Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-855)**: Town centre, parks, ornamental gardens, lake, River Blythe, woodland, and open fields. Sights: [St. Alphege Church](http://www.solihullparish.org.uk), [Manor House](http://www.themanorhousesolihull.com), [Malvern Park](http://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/), [Parkridge Nature Reserve](http://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/), [Malvern & Brueton Park](http://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/), and [Tudor Grange Park](http://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Solihull, or Brueton Park, Solihull. Solihull lunch stops: the [Taste Collective](http://www.tastecollectivesolihull.co.uk), the [Jamaya](http://www.jamaya.co.uk), the Tap & Tandoor, the [Manor House Tea Room](http://www.themanorhousesolihull.com), or the Vita. Brueton Park, Solihull lunch stop: the Parkridge Café. 8 km. 3 hours.

SOR.name: Sole Street
SOR.adminWalksAll:
**Sole Street Circular Walk**: North Downs, wooded ridge, pastoral valleys, and Weald Way path. Sights: Luddesdown Court and St Peter and Paul church. Best in Apr–May. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 13 km.

**Sole Street Circular via Jeskyns Community Woodland**: A shorter variant of [Meopham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/meopham-to-rochester/). Chalk grassland, ancient woodland, community woodland, landscaped parkland, wildflower meadows, farm fields, and country park. Sights: [Jeskyns Community Woodland](https://www.forestryengland.uk/jeskyns), [Ashenbank Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/ashenbank-wood), [Cobham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cobham,_Kent), [Cobham Park (Cobham Wood and Mausoleum)](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/cobham-wood-and-mausoleum), Darnley Mausoleum, [Ranscombe Farm Nature Reserve](https://www.plantlife.org.uk/our-work/ranscombe-farm-nature-reserve), St Mary Magdalene Church, New College of Cobham, Bronze Age Barrow, Cobham Hall, and Luddesdown Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch in Cobham: the [Leather Bottle](https://www.theleatherbottle.pub/index), the Darnley Arms, or the [Ship Inn](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/kent/ship-inn). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Snodland to Sole Street**: North Downs, wooded areas, open fields, hilly sections, pastoral valleys, and organic farmland. Sights: Snodland Clock Tower, Hall House, Luddesdown Court, and St Peter and Paul church. Nettles in summer. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 13 km. 4 hours.

**[Farningham Road to Sole Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farningham-road-to-sole-street/)**: Urban sprawl, housing developments, busy roads, arable fields, and occasional glimpses of historic rural Kent. Sights: Wheatsheaf pub and Chapter House Farm. Lunch in Southfleet, or Sole Street. Southfleet lunch stops: the [Ship Inn](https://www.shipatsouthfleet.co.uk/), or the [Manor Farm Barn](https://www.manorfarmbarnsouthfleet.co.uk/). Sole Street lunch stop: the [Railway Inn](http://www.railwayinnsolestreet.co.uk/). 17 km.

**[Cuxton to Sole Street via Cobham Church](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cuxton-to-sole-street/)**: Open downland views, wooded ridge, bluebell woods, organic vineyard, scenic ridges, and village churchyards. Sights: Cobham Church and Leather Bottle pub. Some strenuous climbs. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Luddesdown, or Cobham. Luddesdown lunch stop: the Golden Lion. Cobham lunch stop: the Leather Bottle. 17 km.

**Cuxton to Sole Street**: A shorter variant of [Cuxton to Sole Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cuxton-to-sole-street/). Open downland views, wooded ridge, bluebell woods, and organic vineyard. Some strenuous climbs. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Luddesdown, or Sole Street. Luddesdown lunch stop: the Golden Lion. Sole Street lunch stop: the Railway Inn. 11 km.

SPO.name: Spondon
SPO.adminWalksAll: **[Spondon Circular via Dale Abbey](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Villages, park grounds, and ornamental lake. Sights: Dale Abbey, All Saints Church, Hermitage Cave, Ockbrook Moravian Settlement, and Locko Park. 16 km. 180 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/410%20Spondon%20Circular.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925787589)**
SPO.publicWalksCircular: **[Spondon Circular via Dale Abbey](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Villages, park grounds, and ornamental lake. Sights: Dale Abbey, All Saints Church, Hermitage Cave, Ockbrook Moravian Settlement, and Locko Park. 16 km. 180 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/410%20Spondon%20Circular.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925787589)**

SPU.name: Staplehurst
SPU.adminWalksAll: **[Staplehurst to Headcorn](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/staplehurst-to-headcorn/)**: Fields, low-lying farmland, bluebell woods, and Kentish Weald villages. Sights: Sissinghurst Castle Gardens. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Frittenden: the Bell and Jorrocks. 13 km.
SPU.publicWalksS2S: **[Staplehurst to Headcorn](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/staplehurst-to-headcorn/)**: Fields, low-lying farmland, bluebell woods, and Kentish Weald villages. Sights: Sissinghurst Castle Gardens. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Frittenden: the Bell and Jorrocks. 13 km.

SRG.name: Seer Green and Jordans
SRG.adminWalksAll: **Beaconsfield to Seer Green and Jordans**: Gently rolling wooded hills, open fields, and Buckinghamshire countryside. Sights: Bekonscot Model Village and Railway, Hodgemoor Woods, Milton's Cottage, Friends Meeting House, Quaker Burial Ground, and Mayflower Barn. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chalfont St Giles, or Three Households. Chalfont St Giles lunch stops: the Merlins Cave, the Deli on the Green, or the Feathers pub. Three Households lunch stop: the White Hart Inn. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).
SRG.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Beaconsfield to Seer Green and Jordans**: Gently rolling wooded hills, open fields, and Buckinghamshire countryside.
