Lunch in Chalfont St Giles, or Three Households. Chalfont St Giles lunch stops: the Merlins Cave, the Deli on the Green, or the Feathers pub. Three Households lunch stop: the White Hart Inn. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).

SRL.name: Shirley
SRL.adminWalksAll: **[Shirley Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-822)**: Canal towpath, Christmas tree plantations, farmland, forest, and lakeside paths. Sights: [Stratford-upon-Avon Canal](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Earlswood Lakes](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), and [Earlswood Lakes Craft Centre](https://www.facebook.com/earlswoodlakescraftcentre/). Trip hazard: anchored cable. Lunch in Shirley, Dickens Heath, or Earlswood. Shirley lunch stops: the Two Mugs, or the Frankie's Café. Dickens Heath lunch stops: the Moreton's Kitchen Bar & Deli, or the Coffee Cloud. Earlswood lunch stops: the [Bulls Head](https://www.bullsheadearlswood.co.uk), the [Blue Bell Cider House](https://www.bluebellciderhouse.org), or the [Reservoir](https://www.hungryhorse.co.uk). 8 km. 3 hours.
SRL.publicWalksCircular: **[Shirley Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-822)**: Canal towpath, Christmas tree plantations, farmland, forest, and lakeside paths. Sights: [Stratford-upon-Avon Canal](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Earlswood Lakes](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), and [Earlswood Lakes Craft Centre](https://www.facebook.com/earlswoodlakescraftcentre/). Trip hazard: anchored cable. Lunch in Shirley, Dickens Heath, or Earlswood. Shirley lunch stops: the Two Mugs, or the Frankie's Café. Dickens Heath lunch stops: the Moreton's Kitchen Bar & Deli, or the Coffee Cloud. Earlswood lunch stops: the [Bulls Head](https://www.bullsheadearlswood.co.uk), the [Blue Bell Cider House](https://www.bluebellciderhouse.org), or the [Reservoir](https://www.hungryhorse.co.uk). 8 km. 3 hours.

SRY.name: Shoeburyness
SRY.adminWalksAll: **[Leigh-on-Sea to Shoeburyness](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-on-sea-to-shoeburyness/)**: Flat coastal path, seaside promenade, harbour, mudflats, beach huts, residential streets, open grassy seafront, and army training area margins. Sights: Southend Pier. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Chalkwell, Westcliff, Southend-on-Sea, Southchurch, or Shoebury Common. Chalkwell lunch stop: the Tides cafe. Westcliff lunch stop: the Beach Hut. Southend-on-Sea lunch stop: the Oliver's cafe. Southchurch lunch stops: the Billy Hundreds, or the Ocean Beach. Shoebury Common lunch stop: the Uncle Tom's Cabin. 12 km.
SRY.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Leigh-on-Sea to Shoeburyness](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-on-sea-to-shoeburyness/)**: Flat coastal path, seaside promenade, harbour, mudflats, beach huts, residential streets, open grassy seafront, and army training area margins. Sights: Southend Pier. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Chalkwell, Westcliff, Southend-on-Sea, Southchurch, or Shoebury Common. Chalkwell lunch stop: the Tides cafe. Westcliff lunch stop: the Beach Hut. Southend-on-Sea lunch stop: the Oliver's cafe. Southchurch lunch stops: the Billy Hundreds, or the Ocean Beach. Shoebury Common lunch stop: the Uncle Tom's Cabin. 12 km.

SSE.name: Shoreham-by-Sea
SSE.adminWalksAll:
**[Shoreham-by-Sea to Littlehampton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shoreham-by-sea-to-littlehampton/)**: Flat coastal promenade, shingle beach, sandy beach, grassy seafront paths, and seaside resorts. Sights: Shoreham Fort, Worthing Pier, Widewater Lagoon Nature Reserve, [Perch on the Pier](https://perchonthepier.com/), [Sea Lane Cafe](http://www.sealanecafe.co.uk/), [Blue Bird Cafe](https://thebluebirdcafeferring.co.uk/), and [East Beach Cafe](http://www.eastbeachcafe.co.uk/). Check tide timings. Lunch in Lancing, Worthing, Goring, Ferring, or Littlehampton. Lancing lunch stop: the Perch at Lancing. Worthing lunch stops: the Coast Cafe, or the [Perch on the Pier](https://perchonthepier.com/). Goring lunch stop: the [Sea Lane Cafe](http://www.sealanecafe.co.uk/). Ferring lunch stop: the [Blue Bird Cafe](https://thebluebirdcafeferring.co.uk/). Littlehampton lunch stop: the [East Beach Cafe](http://www.eastbeachcafe.co.uk/). 22 km.

**Shoreham-by-Sea to Worthing**: A shorter variant of [Shoreham By Sea to Littlehampton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shoreham-by-sea-to-littlehampton/). Flat coastal path, shingle beach, seafront promenade, and seaside resort. Sights: Shoreham Fort, Widewater Lagoon Nature Reserve, Worthing Pier, and [Perch on the Pier](https://perchonthepier.com/). Check tide timings. Lunch in Lancing, or Worthing. Lancing lunch stop: the Perch at Lancing. Worthing lunch stops: the Coast Cafe, or the [Perch on the Pier](https://perchonthepier.com/). 10 km.

**[Amberley to Shoreham-by-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/amberley-to-shoreham-by-sea-or-lancing/)**: Rambler favourite! Open chalk ridge, South Downs escarpment, wide well-marked paths, exposed hilltops, riverside estuary path, and suburban road walking near finish. Sights: [Chanctonbury Ring](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chanctonbury_Ring), [St Botolph's Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Botolph%27s_Church,_Botolphs), Bramber Castle, Lancing College, and Old Shoreham Bridge. Very exposed in wind. Lunch in Washington: the [Frankland Arms](https://thefranklandarms.com/). 25 km.

**Hassocks to Shoreham-by-Sea**: Chalk downland hills, escarpment ridge, quiet road with valley views, farm tracks, nature reserve, and Adur river path. Sights: Wolstonbury Hill, Devil's Dyke, Edburton Hill, Mill Hill Nature Reserve, and Bramber Castle. Lunch in Devil's Dyke area, or Devil's Dyke. Devil's Dyke area lunch stop: the Wildflower Cafe. Devil's Dyke lunch stop: the Devil's Dyke pub. A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 20 km.
SSE.publicWalksS2S: **[Shoreham-by-Sea to Littlehampton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shoreham-by-sea-to-littlehampton/)**: Flat coastal promenade, shingle beach, sandy beach, grassy seafront paths, and seaside resorts. Sights: Shoreham Fort, Worthing Pier, Widewater Lagoon Nature Reserve, [Perch on the Pier](https://perchonthepier.com/), [Sea Lane Cafe](http://www.sealanecafe.co.uk/), [Blue Bird Cafe](https://thebluebirdcafeferring.co.uk/), and [East Beach Cafe](http://www.eastbeachcafe.co.uk/). Check tide timings. Lunch in Lancing, Worthing, Goring, Ferring, or Littlehampton. Lancing lunch stop: the Perch at Lancing. Worthing lunch stops: the Coast Cafe, or the [Perch on the Pier](https://perchonthepier.com/). Goring lunch stop: the [Sea Lane Cafe](http://www.sealanecafe.co.uk/). Ferring lunch stop: the [Blue Bird Cafe](https://thebluebirdcafeferring.co.uk/). Littlehampton lunch stop: the [East Beach Cafe](http://www.eastbeachcafe.co.uk/). 22 km.
SSE.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Amberley to Shoreham-by-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/amberley-to-shoreham-by-sea-or-lancing/)**: Rambler favourite! STY.name: Stratford-upon-Avon Parkway
STY.adminWalksAll: **[Stratford-upon-Avon Parkway Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-823)**: Canal towpath, village streets, kissing gate, and lock-side path. Sights: [Stratford-upon-Avon Canal](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [St Andrew's Church](https://www.britishlistedbuildings.co.uk), [Mary Arden's Farm](https://www.shakespeare.org.uk), [Stratford Armouries](https://www.stratfordarmouries.co.uk), and [Shakespeare Distillery](https://www.shakespearedistillery.com). Limited refreshment options. Lunch in Wilmcote: the [Mary Arden Inn](https://www.mary-arden.co.uk), or the Wilmcote stores. 3 km. 2.5 hours.

Sights: [Stratford-upon-Avon Canal](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [St Andrew's Church](https://www.britishlistedbuildings.co.uk), [Mary Arden's Farm](https://www.shakespeare.org.uk), [Stratford Armouries](https://www.stratfordarmouries.co.uk), and [Shakespeare Distillery](https://www.shakespearedistillery.com). SUY.name: Sudbury
SUY.adminWalksAll:
**Bures to Sudbury via Lamarsh**: Gently rolling farmland, river meadows, woodland, open fields, historic market town, and pastoral common lands. Sights: Holy Innocents Church Lamarsh, St Mary's Church Great Henny, Gainsborough's House, St Gregory's Church Sudbury, Sudbury Common Lands, and Loshes Woodland. Can be muddy. Lunch in Bulmer Tye, Lamarsh, or Henny Street. Bulmer Tye lunch stop: the Fox. Lamarsh lunch stop: the Lamarsh Lion. Henny Street lunch stop: the Henny Swan. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 140 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920318605)**

**Bures to Sudbury via Holy Innocents Church Lamarsh**: Gently rolling farmland, river meadows, open countryside, and historic market town. Sights: Holy Innocents Church Lamarsh, St Mary's Church Great Henny, Gainsborough's House, St Gregory's Church Sudbury, and Sudbury Common Lands. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lamarsh, or Henny Street. Lamarsh lunch stop: the Lamarsh Lion. Henny Street lunch stop: the Henny Swan. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 12 km.
SUY.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Bures to Sudbury via Lamarsh**: Gently rolling farmland, river meadows, woodland, open fields, historic market town, and pastoral common lands. Sights: Holy Innocents Church Lamarsh, St Mary's Church Great Henny, Gainsborough's House, St Gregory's Church Sudbury, Sudbury Common Lands, and Loshes Woodland. Can be muddy. Lunch in Bulmer Tye, Lamarsh, or Henny Street. Bulmer Tye lunch stop: the Fox. Lamarsh lunch stop: the Lamarsh Lion. Henny Street lunch stop: the Henny Swan. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 140 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920318605)**

SWR.name: Stewartby
SWR.adminWalksAll: **[Stewartby to Ridgmont](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/stewartby-to-ridgmont/)**: Flat farmland, lake shores, ancient woods, open countryside, quiet lanes, and Sustrans cycle tracks. Sights: Stewartby Lake, Stewartby Wind Turbine, Marston Thrift SSSI, Holcot Wood, Church of St Peter and St Paul, and Ridgmont Station Heritage Centre. Can be muddy. Lunch in Cranfield, or Marston Moretaine. Cranfield lunch stops: the [Swan/Gurkha Kitchen](https://www.gurkhakitchencranfield.co.uk/), or the Cross Keys. Marston Moretaine lunch stop: the Exhibition. 19 km.
SWR.publicWalksS2S: **[Stewartby to Ridgmont](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/stewartby-to-ridgmont/)**: Flat farmland, lake shores, ancient woods, open countryside, quiet lanes, and Sustrans cycle tracks. Sights: Stewartby Lake, Stewartby Wind Turbine, Marston Thrift SSSI, Holcot Wood, Church of St Peter and St Paul, and Ridgmont Station Heritage Centre. Can be muddy. Lunch in Cranfield, or Marston Moretaine. Cranfield lunch stops: the [Swan/Gurkha Kitchen](https://www.gurkhakitchencranfield.co.uk/), or the Cross Keys. Marston Moretaine lunch stop: the Exhibition. 19 km.

SYS.name: Syston
SYS.adminWalksAll: **[Syston to Melton](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Flat and footpaths. Sights: Kirby Bellars Church, Rearsby, Brooksby, Rotherby, and Frisby on the Wreake. Winter flooding possible. 20 km. 70 m uphill. 5.5 hours. Hard. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/107%20Syston%20to%20Melton%2013m.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925768336)**

[GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/107%20Syston%20to%20Melton%2013m.gpx). **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925768336)**

TAD.name: Tadworth
TAD.adminWalksAll:
**[Tadworth Circular via Headley Heath and Box Hill Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tadworth-via-headley-heath-and-box-hill-circular/)**: Open chalk downland, heathland, mixed woodland, forested hills, escarpment edges, and quiet lanes. Sights: [Headley Heath](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/headley-heath/), [Box Hill](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Box_Hill,_Surrey), White Hill, Juniper Top, and Stane Street. Easy to disorientate. Lunch in Headley, Box Hill, Walton on the Hill, or Tadworth. Headley lunch stop: the [Cock Inn](https://cockinnheadley.co.uk/). Box Hill lunch stop: the [National Trust Box Hill](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/box-hill). Walton on the Hill lunch stop: the [Chalk Hare](http://www.chalkhare.co.uk/). Tadworth lunch stops: the [Chequers](https://www.chequerspub.co.uk/), the [Blue Ball](https://theblueballtadworth.co.uk/), or the [Bell](http://www.thebell-therat.co.uk/). 19 km.

**Tadworth Circular via Banstead Commons**: A shorter variant of [Merstham to Tattenham Corner](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/merstham-to-tattenham-corner/). Rambler favourite! Open heathland, wooded bridleways, Banstead Commons, and gentle slopes. Sights: [Banstead Commons](https://bansteadcommons.org.uk). Can be muddy. Lunch in Mogador: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). 8 km. 2 hours.

**Tadworth to Merstham**: A shorter variant of [Tattenham Corner Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tattenham-corner-circular/). Heathland, North Downs escarpment with fine views, parkland, and golf course. Sights: Banstead Heath, Tadworth Windmill, Margery Wood, North Downs Way, Colley Hill, Inglis Folly, Memorial Glade, Reigate Fort, Gatton Park, Millennium Stones, Quality Street, and Merstham Village Garden. M25 noise audible. Lunch in Mogador: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). 11 km. 2.5 hours.

**Tattenham Corner to Tadworth**: A shorter variant of [Tattenham Corner Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tattenham-corner-circular/). Downland, racecourse gallops, WWI memorial woodland and wildflower meadows, heathland, and gentle paths. Sights: [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets), [Langley Vale Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/langley-vale-wood), Regiment of Trees, Witness, Jutland Wood, Tadworth Windmill, and Banstead Heath. Can be muddy. Lunch in Walton on the Hill: the [Blue Ball](https://theblueballtadworth.co.uk/food-drink), the Village Café, or the [Bell](http://www.thebell-therat.co.uk). 13 km. 3 hours.

**Merstham to Tadworth**: A shorter variant of [Merstham to Tattenham Corner](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/merstham-to-tattenham-corner/). North Downs ridge, parkland, open heathland, and wooded paths. Sights: [Gatton Park](https://gattonpark.co.uk), [Reigate Fort](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/reigate-hill-and-gatton-park/features/reigate-fort-at-reigate-hill-and-gatton-park), and [Banstead Commons](https://bansteadcommons.org.uk). Lunch in Mogador: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). 11 km. 3 hours.
TAD.publicWalksCircular: **[Tadworth Circular via Headley Heath and Box Hill Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tadworth-via-headley-heath-and-box-hill-circular/)**: Open chalk downland, heathland, mixed woodland, forested hills, escarpment edges, and quiet lanes. Sights: [Headley Heath](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/headley-heath/), [Box Hill](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Box_Hill,_Surrey), White Hill, Juniper Top, and Stane Street. Easy to disorientate. Lunch in Headley, Box Hill, Walton on the Hill, or Tadworth. Headley lunch stop: the [Cock Inn](https://cockinnheadley.co.uk/). Box Hill lunch stop: the [National Trust Box Hill](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/box-hill). Walton on the Hill lunch stop: the [Chalk Hare](http://www.chalkhare.co.uk/). Tadworth lunch stops: the [Chequers](https://www.chequerspub.co.uk/), the [Blue Ball](https://theblueballtadworth.co.uk/), or the [Bell](http://www.thebell-therat.co.uk/). 19 km.

TAP.name: Taplow
TAP.adminWalksAll: **Cookham to Taplow**: Hills, escarpment, beech woods, open fields, Thames riverside, country park, and residential roads. Sights: Stanley Spencer Gallery, Holy Trinity Church, Cliveden Estate, Boulter's Lock, Ray Mill Island, and Taplow Riverside. Lunch in Cookham: the Kings Arms, the Ferry, or the Bel and the Dragon. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).
TAP.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Cookham to Taplow**: Hills, escarpment, beech woods, open fields, Thames riverside, country park, and residential roads. Sights: Stanley Spencer Gallery, Holy Trinity Church, Cliveden Estate, Boulter's Lock, Ray Mill Island, and Taplow Riverside. Lunch in Cookham: the Kings Arms, the Ferry, or the Bel and the Dragon. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).

TAT.name: Tattenham Corner
TAT.adminWalksAll:
**Merstham to Tattenham Corner**: Epic scenery sadly ruined by the constant drone of the M25. North Downs ridge, parkland, open heathland, wooded paths, racecourse downland, and London panoramas. Sights: [Gatton Park](https://gattonpark.co.uk), [Reigate Fort](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/reigate-hill-and-gatton-park/features/reigate-fort-at-reigate-hill-and-gatton-park), [Banstead Commons](https://bansteadcommons.org.uk), [First World War Centenary Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/langley-vale-wood), [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets), Millennium Stones, Inglis Folly, and Langley Vale Wood. Race days restrict access. Lunch in Mogador: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). *Last reviewed 5 April 2026.* 15 km. 230 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2863296429)**

**[Tattenham Corner Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tattenham-corner-circular/)**: Downland, racecourse gallops, WWI memorial woodland and wildflower meadows, heathland, riding stables, and undulating farm tracks. Sights: [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets), [Langley Vale Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/langley-vale-wood), First World War Centenary Wood, Regiment of Trees, Witness, Jutland Wood, Tadworth Camp, Coal Tax post, Banstead Heath, Tadworth Windmill, and Emily Davison commemorative plaque. Can be muddy. Lunch in Walton on the Hill: the [Blue Ball](https://theblueballtadworth.co.uk/food-drink), the Village Café, or the [Bell](http://www.thebell-therat.co.uk). 17 km. 4 hours.

**Tattenham Corner to Tadworth**: A shorter variant of [Tattenham Corner Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tattenham-corner-circular/). Downland, racecourse gallops, WWI memorial woodland and wildflower meadows, heathland, and gentle paths. Sights: [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets), [Langley Vale Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/langley-vale-wood), Regiment of Trees, Witness, Jutland Wood, Tadworth Windmill, and Banstead Heath. Can be muddy. Lunch in Walton on the Hill: the [Blue Ball](https://theblueballtadworth.co.uk/food-drink), the Village Café, or the [Bell](http://www.thebell-therat.co.uk). 13 km. 3 hours.

**Tattenham Corner to Merstham**: An alternative variant of [Tattenham Corner Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tattenham-corner-circular/). Downland, racecourse gallops, WWI memorial woodland, heathland, North Downs escarpment with fine views, parkland, and golf course. Sights: [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets), [Langley Vale Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/langley-vale-wood), Regiment of Trees, Witness, Jutland Wood, Tadworth Windmill, Banstead Heath, Margery Wood, North Downs Way, Colley Hill, Inglis Folly, Memorial Glade, Reigate Fort, Gatton Park, Millennium Stones, Royal Alexandra & Albert School, Quality Street, and Merstham Village Garden. M25 noise audible. Lunch in Walton on the Hill, or Mogador. Walton on the Hill lunch stops: the [Blue Ball](https://theblueballtadworth.co.uk/food-drink), the Village Café, or the [Bell](http://www.thebell-therat.co.uk). Mogador lunch stop: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Tattenham Corner to Epsom Downs**: A variant of [Tattenham Corner Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tattenham-corner-circular/). Downland, racecourse gallops, WWI memorial woodland and wildflower meadows, heathland, golf course, and residential streets. Sights: [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets), [Langley Vale Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/langley-vale-wood), Regiment of Trees, Witness, Jutland Wood, Tadworth Windmill, and Banstead Heath. Can be muddy. Lunch in Walton on the Hill: the [Blue Ball](https://theblueballtadworth.co.uk/food-drink), the Village Café, or the [Bell](http://www.thebell-therat.co.uk). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

**Merstham to Tattenham Corner via Gatton Park**: A variant of [Merstham to Tattenham Corner](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/merstham-to-tattenham-corner/). North Downs ridge, parkland, open heathland, wooded paths, and direct racecourse crossing. Sights: [Gatton Park](https://gattonpark.co.uk), [Reigate Fort](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/reigate-hill-and-gatton-park/features/reigate-fort-at-reigate-hill-and-gatton-park), [Banstead Commons](https://bansteadcommons.org.uk), and [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets). Race days restrict access. Lunch in Mogador: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). 16 km.
TAT.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Merstham to Tattenham Corner**: Epic scenery sadly ruined by the constant drone of the M25. North Downs ridge, parkland, open heathland, wooded paths, racecourse downland, and London panoramas. Sights: [Gatton Park](https://gattonpark.co.uk), [Reigate Fort](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/reigate-hill-and-gatton-park/features/reigate-fort-at-reigate-hill-and-gatton-park), [Banstead Commons](https://bansteadcommons.org.uk), [First World War Centenary Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/langley-vale-wood), [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets), Millennium Stones, Inglis Folly, and Langley Vale Wood. Race days restrict access. Lunch in Mogador: the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com). 15 km. 230 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2863296429)**

TBW.name: Tunbridge Wells
TBW.adminWalksAll:
**Tunbridge Wells Circular via Groombridge**: Rambler favourite! Escarpments. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 27 May 2023.* 23 km. 300 m uphill. 6.5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/693628580)**

**Leigh to Tunbridge Wells via Penshurst Place**: Rolling Kent hills, pastoral river valleys, parkland, woods, lakes, and commons extending into town. Sights: [Penshurst Place](http://www.penshurstplace.com/), St John the Baptist Church, Leicester Square (Penshurst), River Medway, All Souls Chapel, Poundsbridge, Pantiles, King Charles the Martyr Church, and [The Opera House](http://www.jdwetherspoon.co.uk/pub/theoperahouse). Can be muddy. Lunch in Smart's Hill, or Penshurst. Smart's Hill lunch stops: the [Spotted Dog](http://www.spotteddogpub.co.uk/), or the [Bottle House Inn](http://www.thebottlehouseinnpenshurst.co.uk/). Penshurst lunch stop: the [Leicester Arms](http://www.leicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 15 km. 220 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921069953)**

**[Tunbridge Wells Circular via Tunbridge Wells](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-circular/)**: High Weald hills, landscaped parkland, bluebell woods, restored heathland, sandstone outcrops, river valley, wooded common, and colonnaded town walkway. Sights: [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Groombridge Place](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groombridge_Place), [Tunbridge Wells](https://visittunbridgewells.com), The Pantiles, Calverley Grounds, St Alban Frant, High Rocks, Eridge Old Park, Eridge Park, Birchden Vineyards, and St Thomas the Apostle. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Eridge Green, or Frant. Eridge Green lunch stop: the [Nevill Crest and Gun](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/nevillcrestandgun). Frant lunch stop: the George Inn. 23 km. 6 hours.

**Tunbridge Wells Circular via Eridge Rocks**: A shorter variant of [Tunbridge Wells Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-circular/). High Weald hills, landscaped parkland, bluebell woods, restored heathland, sandstone outcrops, river valley, wooded common, and colonnaded town walkway. Sights: [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Groombridge Place](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groombridge_Place), The Pantiles, Calverley Grounds, St Alban Frant, High Rocks, and Eridge Old Park. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Eridge Green, or Frant. Eridge Green lunch stop: the [Nevill Crest and Gun](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/nevillcrestandgun). Frant lunch stop: the George Inn. 19 km.

**[Tunbridge Wells to Hildenborough](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-to-hildenborough/)**: Town commons, woodland tracks, wild flower meadows, country park, river paths, and quiet roads. Sights: Haysden Country Park, Brookhurst Field, St Lawrence Church, Wealdway, and Eden Valley Walk. Can be muddy. Lunch in Bidborough, or Hildenborough. Bidborough lunch stop: the [Kentish Hare](http://www.thekentishhare.com/). Hildenborough lunch stop: the [Plough at Leigh](http://www.theploughatleigh.com/). 17 km.

**Tunbridge Wells to Tonbridge**: An alternative variant of [Tunbridge Wells to Hildenborough](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-to-hildenborough/). Town commons, woodland tracks, wild flower meadows, country park, river and lake paths, and urban roads. Sights: Haysden Country Park, Brookhurst Field, St Lawrence Church, Wealdway, and Eden Valley Walk. Can be muddy. Lunch in Bidborough: the [Kentish Hare](http://www.thekentishhare.com/). 14 km.

**Tunbridge Wells to Leigh**: An alternative variant of [Tunbridge Wells to Hildenborough](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-to-hildenborough/). Town commons, woodland tracks, wild flower meadows, country park, river and lake paths, and quiet roads. Sights: Haysden Country Park, Brookhurst Field, St Lawrence Church, Wealdway, and Eden Valley Walk. Can be muddy. Lunch in Bidborough: the [Kentish Hare](http://www.thekentishhare.com/). 14 km.

**[Wadhurst to Tunbridge Wells via Tunbridge Wells](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-to-tunbridge-wells/)**: High Weald ridges, wooded valleys, open fields, landscaped parkland, and back streets and alleyways of a spa town. Sights: [Sussex Border Path](https://www.sussexborderpath.co.uk), [Tunbridge Wells](https://www.visittunbridgewells.com/explore), Eridge Old Park, Saxonbury Hill Tower, The Mount, St Alban, Frant, Nevill Crest, High Weald Landscape Trail, Tunbridge Wells Circular Walk, [Juliets](https://www.julietsandmore.com), and [Opera House](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/all-pubs/england/kent/opera-house-royal-tunbridge-wells). Can be boggy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Frant: the George Inn, or the [Abergavenny Arms](https://www.abergavennyarms.co.uk). 13 km. 3.5 hours.

**Wadhurst to Tunbridge Wells via Dunorlan Park**: A longer variant of [Wadhurst to Tunbridge Wells](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-to-tunbridge-wells/). High Weald ridges, wooded valleys, parkland, recreation grounds, suburban roads, and Victorian park gardens. Sights: [Sussex Border Path](https://www.sussexborderpath.co.uk), Eridge Old Park, Saxonbury Hill Tower, The Mount, St Alban, Frant, Nevill Crest, High Weald Landscape Trail, Tunbridge Wells Circular Walk, [Dunorlan Park](https://tunbridgewells.gov.uk/community-and-leisure/parks-and-play-areas/dunorlan-park), [Calverley Grounds](https://tunbridgewells.gov.uk/community-and-leisure/parks-and-play-areas/calverley-grounds), and Calverley Park. Can be boggy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Frant: the George Inn, or the [Abergavenny Arms](https://www.abergavennyarms.co.uk). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Frant to Tunbridge Wells via Eridge Rocks**: A shorter variant of [Tunbridge Wells Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-circular/). High Weald hills, landscaped parkland, bluebell woods, restored heathland, sandstone outcrops, river valley, wooded common, and colonnaded town walkway. Sights: [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), The Pantiles, High Rocks, and Eridge Old Park. Busy road at start. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Eridge Green: the [Nevill Crest and Gun](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/nevillcrestandgun). 17 km.

**Frant to Tunbridge Wells via Harrison's Rocks**: An alternative variant of [Tunbridge Wells Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-circular/). High Weald hills, landscaped parkland, bluebell woods, restored heathland, sandstone outcrops, river valley, wooded common, and colonnaded town walkway. Sights: [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Groombridge Place](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groombridge_Place), The Pantiles, St Alban Frant, High Rocks, and Eridge Old Park. Busy road at start. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Eridge Green: the [Nevill Crest and Gun](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/nevillcrestandgun). 21 km. 5.5 hours.
TBW.publicWalksCircular: **Tunbridge Wells Circular via Groombridge**: Rambler favourite! Escarpments. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 23 km. 300 m uphill. 6.5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/693628580)**

Sights: [Penshurst Place](http://www.penshurstplace.com/), St John the Baptist Church, Leicester Square (Penshurst), River Medway, All Souls Chapel, Poundsbridge, Pantiles, King Charles the Martyr Church, and [The Opera House](http://www.jdwetherspoon.co.uk/pub/theoperahouse). Can be muddy. Lunch in Smart's Hill, or Penshurst. Smart's Hill lunch stops: the [Spotted Dog](http://www.spotteddogpub.co.uk/), or the [Bottle House Inn](http://www.thebottlehouseinnpenshurst.co.uk/). Penshurst lunch stop: the [Leicester Arms](http://www.leicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 15 km. 220 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921069953)**

TEY.name: Teynham
TEY.adminWalksAll: **[Teynham to Faversham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/teynham-to-faversham/)**: Flat orchards and fruit farms, tidal river seawall, coastal creeks, mudflats, salt marshes, drainage ditches, dykes, windswept grazing marshes, and historic market town streets. Sights: Oare Marshes Nature Reserve and Faversham Market Place. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lynsted, Lewson Street, Conyer, or Oare. Lynsted lunch stop: the [Black Lion](https://blacklionlynsted.co.uk/). Lewson Street lunch stop: the [Plough Inn](http://www.ploughinnlewsonst.co.uk/). Conyer lunch stop: the [Ship Inn at Conyer](https://www.theshipinnconyerquay.co.uk/). Oare lunch stops: the [Three Mariners at Oare](https://www.threemarinersoare.co.uk/), or the [Castle Inn](https://www.castleoarefaversham.co.uk/). 24 km. 5.5 hours.
TEY.publicWalksS2S: **[Teynham to Faversham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/teynham-to-faversham/)**: Flat orchards and fruit farms, tidal river seawall, coastal creeks, mudflats, salt marshes, drainage ditches, dykes, windswept grazing marshes, and historic market town streets. Sights: Oare Marshes Nature Reserve and Faversham Market Place. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lynsted, Lewson Street, Conyer, or Oare. Lynsted lunch stop: the [Black Lion](https://blacklionlynsted.co.uk/). Lewson Street lunch stop: the [Plough Inn](http://www.ploughinnlewsonst.co.uk/). Conyer lunch stop: the [Ship Inn at Conyer](https://www.theshipinnconyerquay.co.uk/). Oare lunch stops: the [Three Mariners at Oare](https://www.threemarinersoare.co.uk/), or the [Castle Inn](https://www.castleoarefaversham.co.uk/). 24 km. 5.5 hours.

THA.name: Thatcham
THA.adminWalksAll: **Newbury Racecourse to Thatcham**: A longer variant of [Newbury Racecourse to Woolhampton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/newbury-racecourse-to-woolhampton/). Gentle climbs, heathland common, woodland, river meadows, and canal. Sights: Greenham Common and Cruise Missile Bunkers. Lunch in Ashford Hill: the [Ship Inn](https://whatpub.com/pubs/HAN/034/ship-inn-ashford-hill). 22 km.
THA.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Newbury Racecourse to Thatcham**: A longer variant of [Newbury Racecourse to Woolhampton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/newbury-racecourse-to-woolhampton/). Gentle climbs, heathland common, woodland, river meadows, and canal. Sights: Greenham Common and Cruise Missile Bunkers. Lunch in Ashford Hill: the [Ship Inn](https://whatpub.com/pubs/HAN/034/ship-inn-ashford-hill). 22 km.

THE.name: Theale
THE.adminWalksAll: **Mortimer to Theale**: Gentle farmland, Roman earthworks, pine woods, commons, river weirs, and canalside towpath. Sights: St Mary's Church, Stratfield Mortimer, Calleva Amphitheatre, Roman Walls of Calleva, St Mary the Virgin Church, Silchester, Kennet & Avon Canal Trust Information Centre, and Padworth Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Silchester Common: the Calleva Arms. A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 22 km.
THE.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Mortimer to Theale**: Gentle farmland, Roman earthworks, pine woods, commons, river weirs, and canalside towpath. Sights: St Mary's Church, Stratfield Mortimer, Calleva Amphitheatre, Roman Walls of Calleva, St Mary the Virgin Church, Silchester, Kennet & Avon Canal Trust Information Centre, and Padworth Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Silchester Common: the Calleva Arms. A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 22 km.

THL.name: Tile Hill
THL.adminWalksAll: **[Tile Hill Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-913)**: Suburban streets, deciduous and coniferous woodland, open fields, scrub grassland, and sports ground. Sights: [Tile Hill Wood](https://www.coventry.gov.uk), Plants Hill Wood, Pig Wood, [St Oswald's Church](https://www.stoswalds.co.uk), and Massey Ferguson Factory (plaque). Can be muddy. Lunch in Tile Hill, or Burton Green. Tile Hill lunch stops: the [Auction House](https://www.blackrosepubs.com/theauctionhouse), the [Penny Farthing](https://www.pennyfarthingpubcoventry.co.uk), the [Rupee Lounge](https://www.therupeelounge.co.uk), or the Subway. Burton Green lunch stop: the [Hickory's Smokehouse](https://www.hickorys.co.uk). 6 km. 2 hours.
THL.publicWalksCircular: **[Tile Hill Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-913)**: Suburban streets, deciduous and coniferous woodland, open fields, scrub grassland, and sports ground. Sights: [Tile Hill Wood](https://www.coventry.gov.uk), Plants Hill Wood, Pig Wood, [St Oswald's Church](https://www.stoswalds.co.uk), and Massey Ferguson Factory (plaque). Can be muddy. Lunch in Tile Hill, or Burton Green. Tile Hill lunch stops: the [Auction House](https://www.blackrosepubs.com/theauctionhouse), the [Penny Farthing](https://www.pennyfarthingpubcoventry.co.uk), the [Rupee Lounge](https://www.therupeelounge.co.uk), or the Subway. Burton Green lunch stop: the [Hickory's Smokehouse](https://www.hickorys.co.uk). 6 km. 2 hours.

TIL.name: Tilbury Town
TIL.adminWalksAll: **[Tilbury Town to East Tilbury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tilbury-to-east-tilbury-via-2-forts-way/)**: Flat Thames Estuary coast path, riverside sea wall, wild flower meadows, salt marsh views, former landfill landscape, and brief inland section. Sights: [Tilbury Fort](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tilbury_Fort), [Coalhouse Fort](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coalhouse_Fort), [Thurrock Thameside Nature Park](https://www.essexwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/thurrock-thameside), St Catherine's Church, and East Tilbury Radar Tower. Check tide timings. Lunch in Tilbury Fort, or Coalhouse Fort. Tilbury Fort lunch stop: the World's End. Coalhouse Fort lunch stop: the [Ship](https://theshipeasttilburyvillage.co.uk). 13 km.
TIL.publicWalksS2S: **[Tilbury Town to East Tilbury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tilbury-to-east-tilbury-via-2-forts-way/)**: Flat Thames Estuary coast path, riverside sea wall, wild flower meadows, salt marsh views, former landfill landscape, and brief inland section. Sights: [Tilbury Fort](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tilbury_Fort), [Coalhouse Fort](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coalhouse_Fort), [Thurrock Thameside Nature Park](https://www.essexwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/thurrock-thameside), St Catherine's Church, and East Tilbury Radar Tower. Check tide timings. Lunch in Tilbury Fort, or Coalhouse Fort. Tilbury Fort lunch stop: the World's End. Coalhouse Fort lunch stop: the [Ship](https://theshipeasttilburyvillage.co.uk). 13 km.

TIS.name: Tisbury
TIS.adminWalksAll:
**[Tisbury Circular via Hindon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-hindon/)**: Dry chalky U-shaped downland valleys, wooded ridges, grassy valleys, picturesque villages, rolling hills, and river valley. Sights: Fonthill Estate, Place Farm, and Fonthill House. Lunch in West Hatch, Hindon, or Fonthill Gifford. West Hatch lunch stop: the [Pythouse Kitchen Garden Cafe](http://pythousekitchengarden.co.uk/). Hindon lunch stops: the [Grosvenor Arms](http://www.grosvenorarmshindon.co.uk/), or the [Lamb](http://www.lambhindon.co.uk/). Fonthill Gifford lunch stop: the [Beckford Arms](http://www.beckfordarms.com/). 22 km. 5 hours.

**[Tisbury Circular via Donhead St. Andrew](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-donhead-st-andrew/)**: Rolling downland, wooded estates, large country parks, open ridges, Neolithic hill fort, steep descents, and unspoilt village. Sights: [Old Wardour Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/old-wardour-castle/) and New Wardour Castle. Can be muddy. 23 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Tisbury Circular via Dinton and Fovant](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-dinton-and-fovant/)**: Nadder Valley, undulating West Wiltshire Downs, grassy U-shaped valleys, bluebell woods, wildflower meadows, pastures, and chalk downland. Sights: Philipps House, Dinton Park, Dinton Mill, and Fovant Badges. Lunch in Dinton: the Wyndham Arms. 24 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Tisbury Circular via Alvediston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-alvediston/)**: Chalky downland valleys, open hilltops, coombes, wooded Iron Age hill fort, undulating countryside, and expansive views. Plenty of ascent. Lunch in Swallowcliffe, or Ebbesbourne Wake. Swallowcliffe lunch stop: the [Royal Oak](http://www.royaloakswallowcliffe.com/). Ebbesbourne Wake lunch stop: the [Horseshoe Inn](http://thehorseshoe-inn.co.uk/). 26 km. 6.5 hours.

**[Tisbury Circular via Ludwell](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-ludwell/)**: Upper Nadder Valley, Wardour Park, rolling downland, steep chalk escarpments, ridge walking, woods, boggy brook crossings, and unspoilt village. Sights: New Wardour Castle, Old Wardour Castle, Win Green Hill, White Sheet Hill, and Castle Ditches Iron Age Hillfort. Steep rough descents. Lunch in Ludwell, or Berwick St. John. Ludwell lunch stop: the [Grove Arms](https://www.thegrovearms.co.uk/). Berwick St. John lunch stop: the [Talbot Inn](http://www.talbotinnberwickstjohn.co.uk/). 26 km. 6.5 hours.

**Tisbury Circular via Dinton and Fovant & Chiselbury Iron Age hill fort**: A longer variant of [Tisbury Circular via Dinton and Fovant](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-dinton-and-fovant/). Nadder Valley, undulating West Wiltshire Downs, grassy valleys, bluebell woods, wildflower meadows, chalk downland, and Fovant Down hilltop. Sights: Philipps House, Dinton Park, Dinton Mill, Fovant Badges, and Chiselbury Iron Age hill fort. Lunch in Dinton: the Wyndham Arms. 29 km.
TIS.publicWalksCircular:
**[Tisbury Circular via Hindon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-hindon/)**: Dry chalky U-shaped downland valleys, wooded ridges, grassy valleys, picturesque villages, rolling hills, and river valley. Sights: Fonthill Estate, Place Farm, and Fonthill House. Lunch in West Hatch, Hindon, or Fonthill Gifford. Fonthill Gifford lunch stop: the [Beckford Arms](http://www.beckfordarms.com/). 22 km. 5 hours.

**[Tisbury Circular via Donhead St. Andrew](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-donhead-st-andrew/)**: Rolling downland, wooded estates, large country parks, open ridges, Neolithic hill fort, steep descents, and unspoilt village. Sights: [Old Wardour Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/old-wardour-castle/) and New Wardour Castle. Can be muddy. 23 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Tisbury Circular via Dinton and Fovant](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-dinton-and-fovant/)**: Nadder Valley, undulating West Wiltshire Downs, grassy U-shaped valleys, bluebell woods, wildflower meadows, pastures, and chalk downland. Sights: Philipps House, Dinton Park, Dinton Mill, and Fovant Badges. Lunch in Dinton: the Wyndham Arms. 24 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Tisbury Circular via Alvediston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-alvediston/)**: Chalky downland valleys, open hilltops, coombes, wooded Iron Age hill fort, undulating countryside, and expansive views. Plenty of ascent. Lunch in Swallowcliffe, or Ebbesbourne Wake. Swallowcliffe lunch stop: the [Royal Oak](http://www.royaloakswallowcliffe.com/). Ebbesbourne Wake lunch stop: the [Horseshoe Inn](http://thehorseshoe-inn.co.uk/). 26 km. 6.5 hours.

**[Tisbury Circular via Ludwell](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-ludwell/)**: Upper Nadder Valley, Wardour Park, rolling downland, steep chalk escarpments, ridge walking, woods, boggy brook crossings, and unspoilt village. Sights: New Wardour Castle, Old Wardour Castle, Win Green Hill, White Sheet Hill, and Castle Ditches Iron Age Hillfort. Steep rough descents. Lunch in Ludwell, or Berwick St. John. Ludwell lunch stop: the [Grove Arms](https://www.thegrovearms.co.uk/). Berwick St. John lunch stop: the [Talbot Inn](http://www.talbotinnberwickstjohn.co.uk/). 26 km. 6.5 hours.

TLH.name: Tilehurst
TLH.adminWalksAll: **Pangbourne to Tilehurst**: A longer variant of [Pangbourne via Goring Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pangbourne-via-goring-circular/). Part-wooded ridges, river meadows, Thames Path, wooded hills, and Thames-side villages. Sights: Whitchurch Bridge, Hartslock Nature Reserve, Mapledurham Estate, and Goring Gap. Lunch in Goring-on-Thames, or Streatley. Goring-on-Thames lunch stops: the [Catherine Wheel](http://www.tcwgoring.co.uk/index), the [Chocolate Café](https://www.thechocolatecafeclub.co.uk/goring/), the [Miller of Mansfield](https://themillerofmansfield.com/), or the [Pierreponts Café](http://pierreponts.co.uk/). Streatley lunch stop: the [Swan at Streatley](https://www.coppaclub.co.uk/theswanatstreatley/). 21 km.
TLH.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Pangbourne to Tilehurst**: A longer variant of [Pangbourne via Goring Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pangbourne-via-goring-circular/). Part-wooded ridges, river meadows, Thames Path, wooded hills, and Thames-side villages. Sights: Whitchurch Bridge, Hartslock Nature Reserve, Mapledurham Estate, and Goring Gap. Lunch in Goring-on-Thames, or Streatley. Goring-on-Thames lunch stops: the [Catherine Wheel](http://www.tcwgoring.co.uk/index), the [Chocolate Café](https://www.thechocolatecafeclub.co.uk/goring/), the [Miller of Mansfield](https://themillerofmansfield.com/), or the [Pierreponts Café](http://pierreponts.co.uk/). Streatley lunch stop: the [Swan at Streatley](https://www.coppaclub.co.uk/theswanatstreatley/). 21 km.

TLK.name: The Lakes
TLK.adminWalksAll: **[The Lakes Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-825)**: Reservoirs, lakeside paths, ancient woodland, meadows, heathland remnant, and field edges. Sights: [Earlswood Lakes](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Clowes Wood](https://www.warwickshirewildlifetrust.org.uk), [Earlswood Moathouse](https://www.warwickshirewildlifetrust.org.uk), and [Earlswood Lakes Craft Centre](https://www.facebook.com/earlswoodlakescraftcentre/). Can be muddy. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in Earlswood, or Earlswood Lakes Craft Centre. Earlswood lunch stops: the [Red Lion](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk), the Reservoir, or the [Limes Country Lodge](https://www.limescountrylodge.co.uk). Earlswood Lakes Craft Centre lunch stop: the [Manor House Tearooms](https://www.facebook.com/earlswoodlakescraftcentre/). 6 km. 2 hours.
TLK.publicWalksCircular: **[The Lakes Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-825)**: Reservoirs, lakeside paths, ancient woodland, meadows, heathland remnant, and field edges. Sights: [Earlswood Lakes](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Clowes Wood](https://www.warwickshirewildlifetrust.org.uk), [Earlswood Moathouse](https://www.warwickshirewildlifetrust.org.uk), and [Earlswood Lakes Craft Centre](https://www.facebook.com/earlswoodlakescraftcentre/). Can be muddy. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch in Earlswood, or Earlswood Lakes Craft Centre. Earlswood lunch stops: the [Red Lion](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk), the Reservoir, or the [Limes Country Lodge](https://www.limescountrylodge.co.uk). Earlswood Lakes Craft Centre lunch stop: the [Manor House Tearooms](https://www.facebook.com/earlswoodlakescraftcentre/). 6 km. 2 hours.

TOK.name: Three Oaks
TOK.adminWalksAll:
**Three Oaks to Hastings**: A shorter variant of [Winchelsea to Hastings via Three Oaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings-via-three-oaks/). Varied hilly terrain, woods, cliff-top coast path, steep ascents and descents, and old town. Sights: Guestling Church, Fairlight Hall, Fairlight Glen, Ecclesbourne Glen, East Hill Lift, and Hastings Old Town. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Fairlight: the Coastguard Cafe, or the Grumpy Cook Cafe. 13 km.

**Winchelsea to Three Oaks**: A shorter variant of [Winchelsea to Hastings via Three Oaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings-via-three-oaks/). Undulating ridge, pastureland, farms, woods, and hilly territory with fine views over the Brede valley. Sights: Winchelsea, New Gate, and Icklesham Church. Lunch in Icklesham, or Three Oaks. Icklesham lunch stop: the Queen's Head. Three Oaks lunch stop: the Three Oaks pub. 12 km.
TOK.publicWalksS2S: **Three Oaks to Hastings**: A shorter variant of [Winchelsea to Hastings via Three Oaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings-via-three-oaks/). Varied hilly terrain, woods, cliff-top coast path, steep ascents and descents, and old town. Sights: Guestling Church, Fairlight Hall, Fairlight Glen, Ecclesbourne Glen, East Hill Lift, and Hastings Old Town. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Fairlight: the Coastguard Cafe, or the Grumpy Cook Cafe. 13 km.
TOK.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Winchelsea to Three Oaks**: A shorter variant of [Winchelsea to Hastings via Three Oaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings-via-three-oaks/). Undulating ridge, pastureland, farms, woods, and hilly territory with fine views over the Brede valley. Sights: Winchelsea, New Gate, and Icklesham Church. Lunch in Icklesham, or Three Oaks. Icklesham lunch stop: the Queen's Head. Three Oaks lunch stop: the Three Oaks pub. 12 km.

TOM.name: Tottenham Hale
TOM.adminWalksAll:
**[Alexandra Palace to Tottenham Hale](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/alexandra-palace-to-tottenham-hale/)**: Urban north London, disused railway trackbed nature reserve, ancient woodland, reservoir wetlands, residential streets, riverside path, and open parkland. Sights: [Alexandra Palace](https://www.alexandrapalace.com), [Parkland Walk](https://www.parkland-walk.org.uk), [Woodberry Wetlands](https://www.wildlondon.org.uk/woodberry-wetlands-nature-reserve), [Walthamstow Marshes Nature Reserve](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/walthamstow-marshes), [Walthamstow Wetlands](https://www.wildlondon.org.uk/walthamstow-wetlands-nature-reserve), Highgate Wood, Finsbury Park, Springfield Park, Coppermill Tower, and Engine House. Lunch in Finsbury Park, or Woodberry Wetlands. Finsbury Park lunch stop: the Finsbury Park Café. Woodberry Wetlands lunch stop: the Coal House Café. 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**Highgate to Tottenham Hale**: A shorter variant of [Alexandra Palace to Tottenham Hale](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/alexandra-palace-to-tottenham-hale/). Disused railway trackbed nature reserve, urban parks, reservoir wetlands, residential streets, and riverside path. Sights: [Parkland Walk](https://www.parkland-walk.org.uk), [Woodberry Wetlands](https://www.wildlondon.org.uk/woodberry-wetlands-nature-reserve), [Walthamstow Marshes Nature Reserve](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/walthamstow-marshes), [Walthamstow Wetlands](https://www.wildlondon.org.uk/walthamstow-wetlands-nature-reserve), Finsbury Park, Springfield Park, and Coppermill Tower. Lunch in Finsbury Park, or Woodberry Wetlands. Finsbury Park lunch stop: the Finsbury Park Café. Woodberry Wetlands lunch stop: the Coal House Café. 11 km. 2.5 hours.

**Finsbury Park to Tottenham Hale**: A variant of [Alexandra Palace to Tottenham Hale](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/alexandra-palace-to-tottenham-hale/). Urban park, reservoir wetlands, residential streets, riverside path, and open marshland. Sights: [Woodberry Wetlands](https://www.wildlondon.org.uk/woodberry-wetlands-nature-reserve), [Walthamstow Marshes Nature Reserve](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/walthamstow-marshes), [Walthamstow Wetlands](https://www.wildlondon.org.uk/walthamstow-wetlands-nature-reserve), Springfield Park, Coppermill Tower, and Engine House. Lunch in Woodberry Wetlands: the Coal House Café. 8 km.
TOM.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Alexandra Palace to Tottenham Hale](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/alexandra-palace-to-tottenham-hale/)**: Urban north London, disused railway trackbed nature reserve, ancient woodland, reservoir wetlands, residential streets, riverside path, and open parkland. Sights: [Alexandra Palace](https://www.alexandrapalace.com), [Parkland Walk](https://www.parkland-walk.org.uk), [Woodberry Wetlands](https://www.wildlondon.org.uk/woodberry-wetlands-nature-reserve), [Walthamstow Marshes Nature Reserve](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/walthamstow-marshes), [Walthamstow Wetlands](https://www.wildlondon.org.uk/walthamstow-wetlands-nature-reserve), Highgate Wood, Finsbury Park, Springfield Park, Coppermill Tower, and Engine House. Lunch in Finsbury Park, or Woodberry Wetlands. Finsbury Park lunch stop: the Finsbury Park Café. Woodberry Wetlands lunch stop: the Coal House Café. 15 km. 3.5 hours.

TON.name: Tonbridge
TON.adminWalksAll:
**[Tonbridge Circular via Tudeley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-circular-via-tudeley/)**: Parkland, farm fields, paddocks, orchards, country park, riverside paths, residential streets, and fishing and sailing lakes. Sights: [All Saints Church, Tudeley](https://www.tudeley.org/Groups/363846/Chagall_Windows.aspx), [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), Tonbridge Castle, Somerhill House, Hadlow Tower, Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, Stone Lock, Heusenstamm Wood, and Tonbridge Memorial Garden. Lunch in Tudeley Hale: the [Poacher & Partridge](https://elitepubs.com/venue/the-poacher-and-partridge). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

**Tonbridge Circular via Eden Valley Walk**: A variant of [Tonbridge to Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-to-penshurst/). Lakes, water meadows, riverside paths, parkland, playing fields, and town centre. Sights: [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, St John the Baptist, Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Penshurst Park, Tonbridge Castle, and Tonbridge School. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst, or Leigh. Penshurst lunch stops: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). Leigh lunch stop: the [Fleur De Lis](https://fleurdelisleigh.co.uk). 18 km. 4 hours.

**Tonbridge Circular via Tudeley & St Thomas à Becket**: A longer variant of [Tonbridge Circular via Tudeley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-circular-via-tudeley/). Parkland, farm fields, paddocks, orchards, low wooded hills, country lanes, country park, riverside paths, and sailing lakes. Sights: [All Saints Church, Tudeley](https://www.tudeley.org/Groups/363846/Chagall_Windows.aspx), St Thomas à Becket, [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), Tonbridge Castle, Somerhill House, Hadlow Tower, Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, Stone Lock, and Heusenstamm Wood. Tricky navigation, limited waymarking. Lunch in Tudeley, or Capel. Tudeley lunch stop: the [George & Dragon](https://www.gd-tudeley.com). Capel lunch stop: the [Dovecote Inn](https://www.dovecote-capel.co.uk). 25 km. 6 hours.

**[Tonbridge to Penshurst via Harden Vineyard](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-to-penshurst/)**: Lakes, water meadows, riverside paths, low hills, orchards, vineyards, farmland, and picturesque villages. Sights: [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, Harden Vineyard, Larkin's Brewery, St Mary, Chiddingstone, Chiding Stone, and Penshurst Platform Gallery. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst, or Hoath Corner. Penshurst lunch stops: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). Hoath Corner lunch stop: the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com). 18 km. 4 hours.

**Tonbridge to Penshurst via Haysden Country Park**: A shorter variant of [Tonbridge to Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-to-penshurst/). Lakes, water meadows, riverside paths, Eden Valley Walk, farmland, and picturesque villages. Sights: [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, St Mary, Chiddingstone, Chiding Stone, and Penshurst Platform Gallery. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). 15 km.

**[Hever to Tonbridge via Tonbridge Castle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-tonbridge/)**: Gentle farmland, orchards, river valleys, parkland, country park lakes, and riverside paths into a market town. Sights: [Hever Castle](https://www.hevercastle.co.uk), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Tonbridge Castle](https://www.tonbridgecastle.org), St Peter's Church Hever, St John the Baptist Church Penshurst, Leicester Square Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Chippens Bank House, Withers, Straight Mile, and Powder Mills. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**Leigh to Tonbridge**: A shorter variant of [Hever to Tonbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-tonbridge/). Parkland estate, river valley, country park lakes, and riverside paths into a market town. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Tonbridge Castle](https://www.tonbridgecastle.org), St John the Baptist Church Penshurst, Leicester Square Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Straight Mile, and Powder Mills. Lunch in Penshurst: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). 12 km. 3 hours.

**Tunbridge Wells to Tonbridge**: An alternative variant of [Tunbridge Wells to Hildenborough](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-to-hildenborough/). Town commons, woodland tracks, wild flower meadows, country park, river and lake paths, and urban roads. Sights: Haysden Country Park, Brookhurst Field, St Lawrence Church, Wealdway, and Eden Valley Walk. Can be muddy. Lunch in Bidborough: the [Kentish Hare](http://www.thekentishhare.com/). 14 km.

**Hildenborough to Tonbridge via Hall Place**: An alternative variant of [Tonbridge to Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-to-penshurst/). Roadside walking, fields, parkland, riverside paths, parkland, playing fields, and town centre. Sights: Hall Place, St Mary, Leigh, [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), St John the Baptist, Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Penshurst Park, Tonbridge Castle, and Tonbridge School. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst, or Leigh. Penshurst lunch stops: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). Leigh lunch stop: the [Fleur De Lis](https://fleurdelisleigh.co.uk). 18 km. 4 hours.

**Hildenborough to Tonbridge via Hadlow Tower**: A longer variant of [Hildenborough to Hadlow or Tonbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hildenborough-to-hadlow-or-tonbridge/). Wealden countryside, meadows, farm fields, woods, and long riverside path along the River Medway into Tonbridge. Sights: [Hadlow Tower](http://www.thehadlowtower.co.uk/), [Broadview Gardens](https://www.hadlow.ac.uk/about-us/broadview-gardens-and-garden-centre), St Giles, Shipbourne, Hadlow College, St Mary, Hadlow, Hadlow Castle, Tonbridge Castle, and Tonbridge Memorial Garden. Can be muddy. Lunch in Shipbourne, or Dunk's Green. Shipbourne lunch stop: the [Chaser Inn](https://www.thechaser.co.uk/). Dunk's Green lunch stop: the [Kentish Rifleman](https://www.thekentishrifleman.co.uk/). 20 km. 4.5 hours.

**Hever to Tonbridge via Chiddingstone Castle**: Undulating hills, open fields, rivers, Haysden Country Park, and historic estates. Sights: [Hever Castle](http://www.hevercastle.co.uk/), Chiddingstone Castle, [Penshurst Place](http://www.penshurstplace.com/), and Haysden Country Park. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chiddingstone, or Penshurst. Chiddingstone lunch stop: the [Castle Inn](http://www.thecastleinnchiddingstone.com/). Penshurst lunch stop: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](http://www.leicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk/). A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 21 km.
TON.publicWalksS2S: **[Tonbridge to Penshurst via Harden Vineyard](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-to-penshurst/)**: Lakes, water meadows, riverside paths, low hills, orchards, vineyards, farmland, and picturesque villages. Sights: [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, Harden Vineyard, Larkin's Brewery, St Mary, Chiddingstone, Chiding Stone, and Penshurst Platform Gallery. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst, or Hoath Corner. Penshurst lunch stops: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). Hoath Corner lunch stop: the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com). 18 km. 4 hours.
TON.publicWalksCircular: **[Tonbridge Circular via Tudeley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-circular-via-tudeley/)**: Parkland, farm fields, paddocks, orchards, country park, riverside paths, residential streets, and fishing and sailing lakes. Sights: [All Saints Church, Tudeley](https://www.tudeley.org/Groups/363846/Chagall_Windows.aspx), [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), Tonbridge Castle, Somerhill House, Hadlow Tower, Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, Stone Lock, Heusenstamm Wood, and Tonbridge Memorial Garden. Lunch in Tudeley Hale: the [Poacher & Partridge](https://elitepubs.com/venue/the-poacher-and-partridge). 19 km. 4.5 hours.
TON.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Hever to Tonbridge via Tonbridge Castle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-tonbridge/)**: Gentle farmland, orchards, river valleys, parkland, country park lakes, and riverside paths into a market town. Sights: [Hever Castle](https://www.hevercastle.co.uk), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Tonbridge Castle](https://www.tonbridgecastle.org), St Peter's Church Hever, St John the Baptist Church Penshurst, Leicester Square Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Chippens Bank House, Withers, Straight Mile, and Powder Mills. Can be muddy. Lunch in Penshurst: the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk), or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry). 16 km. 3.5 hours.

TRI.name: Tring
TRI.adminWalksAll:
**Tring Circular via the Ashridge Estate**: An essential walk! The Ashridge Estate is very beautiful if you appreciate woodland, and covers more then enough ground to cover a day of hiking in itself. There are paths everywhere and you can wander freely. Ancient trees, rolling chalk downlands, and lush meadows. Sights: [Ashridge Estate](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/essex-bedfordshire-hertfordshire/ashridge-estate). Can be muddy. Main paths can be very busy on weekends so take the minor paths to avoid the crowds. Lunch in Ashridge House: the Bakehouse (very nice). *Last reviewed 2 November 2025.* 13 km. 190 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2665054524)**

**Tring Circular via Ivinghoe Beacon**: Ridgeway escarpment, gravel track through beechwoods, and optional bluebell woodland diversion. Sights: Ivinghoe Beacon, Flat Isley, Bridgewater Monument, and Ashridge Estate. Very hilly and very windy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Ashridge Estate: the Monument Cafe. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 14 km. 270 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925756026)**

**Tring to Wendover via Aston Clinton**: Flat canal towpath, reservoir banks, open arable fields, wooded Chilterns hills, and steep descents. Sights: Tring Reservoirs, All Saints Church, Buckland, St Mary the Virgin, Drayton Beauchamp, Halton House, Wendover Arm Canal, Boddington Hill Fort, Coombe Hill, Go Ape, and Rumsey's Chocolaterie. Steep chalky descent. Lunch in Aston Clinton: the Oak. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 22 km. 230 m uphill. 6 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920846007)**

**Tring Circular via Ridgeway**: Chiltern escarpment ridge, open downland, ancient beechwoods, National Trust estate, gentle hills, and a classic village. Sights: [Ashridge Estate](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/essex-bedfordshire-hertfordshire/ashridge-estate), Ivinghoe Beacon, Ridgeway, Dockey Wood, Flat Isley, Bridgewater Monument, and Little Gaddesden Church. Best in Oct–Nov. Lunch in Little Gaddesden, or Ashridge Estate. Little Gaddesden lunch stop: the Bridgewater Arms. Ashridge Estate lunch stop: the Monument Cafe. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km. 5 hours.

**[Tring to Berkhamsted via Tom's Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tring-to-berkhamsted/)**: Canal towpath, ancient woodland, chalk commons, open grassy hillsides, beech and oak estate woodland, and ridge paths with town views. Sights: Grand Union Canal, Ashridge Estate, Bridgewater Monument, Ashridge Hult College, Berkhamsted WW1 Troop Training Trenches, Berkhamsted Castle, Little Gaddesden Parish Church (St Peter and St Paul), Little Gaddesden Village Green War Memorial, Frithsden Beeches, Thunderdell Lodge, and Old Park Lodge. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Little Gaddesden, Ashridge House, or Frithsden. Little Gaddesden lunch stop: the Bridgewater Arms. Ashridge House lunch stop: the Bakehouse. Frithsden lunch stop: the Alford Arms. 15 km.

**Tring to Berkhamsted via Tom's Hill & Tom's Hill House**: An alternative variant of [Tring to Berkhamsted via Tom's Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tring-to-berkhamsted/). Hertfordshire Way field paths, hedged lanes, light woodland, and ridge paths; avoids canal towpath. Sights: Ashridge Estate, Bridgewater Monument, Ashridge Hult College, Berkhamsted WW1 Troop Training Trenches, Berkhamsted Castle, Little Gaddesden Parish Church (St Peter and St Paul), Little Gaddesden Village Green War Memorial, Frithsden Beeches, and Tom's Hill House. Lunch in Little Gaddesden, or Ashridge House. Little Gaddesden lunch stop: the Bridgewater Arms. Ashridge House lunch stop: the Bakehouse. 13 km.

**Tring to Berkhamsted via Aldbury village stocks**: Direct route through Ashridge beechwoods, passing Aldbury village and Bridgewater Monument, and then open hills to Berkhamsted. Sights: Aldbury village stocks, Bridgewater Monument, Ashridge Estate, and Berkhamsted Castle. Lunch in Aldbury, or Ashridge Estate. Aldbury lunch stops: the Greyhound Inn, or the Trooper. Ashridge Estate lunch stop: the Monument Cafe. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 9 km.

**Tring to Berkhamsted via Tom's Hill & Ivinghoe Beacon**: A longer variant of [Tring to Berkhamsted via Tom's Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tring-to-berkhamsted/). Open downland, Ivinghoe Beacon ridge, farmland, ancient woodland, and chalk commons. Sights: Ivinghoe Beacon, Ashridge Estate, Bridgewater Monument, Berkhamsted WW1 Troop Training Trenches, and Berkhamsted Castle. Lunch in Little Gaddesden: the Bridgewater Arms. 18 km.

**Tring to Berkhamsted via Ivinghoe Beacon**: Ridgeway escarpment, open downland, ancient beechwoods, Ashridge Estate, open hills, and canal-side town. Sights: Ivinghoe Beacon, Ashridge Estate, Bridgewater Monument, and Berkhamsted Castle. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Little Gaddesden, or Ashridge Estate. Little Gaddesden lunch stop: the Bridgewater Arms. Ashridge Estate lunch stop: the Monument Cafe. A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 20 km.

**Tring to Berkhamsted via Hockeridge Woods**: A longer variant of [Berkhamsted Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berkhamsted-circular/). Canal towpath, wide bridleways, fields, woodland paths, and town centre. Sights: Berkhamsted Castle, Grand Union Canal, Hockeridge Woods, and River Bulbourne. Can be muddy. Lunch in Berkhamsted: the [Crystal Palace](https://www.crystalpalace-pub.co.uk/), the [Boat](https://www.boatberkhamsted.co.uk/), or the Rising Sun. 24 km.
TRI.publicWalksS2S: **Tring to Wendover via Aston Clinton**: Flat canal towpath, reservoir banks, open arable fields, wooded Chilterns hills, and steep descents. Sights: Tring Reservoirs, All Saints Church, Buckland, St Mary the Virgin, Drayton Beauchamp, Halton House, Wendover Arm Canal, Boddington Hill Fort, Coombe Hill, Go Ape, and Rumsey's Chocolaterie. Steep chalky descent. Lunch in Aston Clinton: the Oak. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 22 km. 230 m uphill. 6 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920846007)**
