There are paths everywhere and you can wander freely. Ancient trees, rolling chalk downlands, and lush meadows. Sights: [Ashridge Estate](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/essex-bedfordshire-hertfordshire/ashridge-estate). Can be muddy. Main paths can be very busy on weekends so take the minor paths to avoid the crowds. Lunch in Ashridge House: the Bakehouse (very nice). 13 km. 190 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2665054524)**

**Tring Circular via Ivinghoe Beacon**: Ridgeway escarpment, gravel track through beechwoods, and optional bluebell woodland diversion. Sights: Ivinghoe Beacon, Flat Isley, Bridgewater Monument, and Ashridge Estate. Very hilly and very windy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Ashridge Estate: the Monument Cafe. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 14 km. 270 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2925756026)**

TWI.name: Twickenham
TWI.adminWalksAll:
**[Twickenham Circular via 3 rivers](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/twickehham-circular/)**: Flat urban riverside paths, small river corridors, local parks, Thames towpath, luxury riverside houses, and Palladian parkland. Sights: [River Crane](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Crane,_London), [Duke of Northumberland's River](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Duke_of_Northumberland%27s_River), [Twickenham Stadium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Twickenham_Stadium), [Marble Hill House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/marble-hill-house/), Marble Hill Park, Ham House, Eel Pie Island, York House, and Isleworth Ait. Lunch in Isleworth: the London Apprentice. 11 km.

**[Twickenham Circular via Crane Island Nature Reserve](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/river-crane/)**: Flat river path, urban parks, woods, nature reserves, and paved all-weather surfaces. Sights: Crane Island Nature Reserve, Brick (Shot) Tower, Kneller Gardens, Hounslow Heath, [River Crane](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Crane,_London), and [Duke of Northumberland's River](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Duke_of_Northumberland%27s_River). Can be muddy. Lunch in Kneller Gardens: the Park Cafe. 9 km.

**Wimbledon to Twickenham**: An alternative variant of [Wimbledon to Kingston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-kingston/). Common heathland, woodland, royal deer park, Thames riverside path, Palladian riverside park, and Italianate gardens. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](http://www.cannizaropark.org.uk/index.htm), [Richmond Park](http://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond_park/), Isabella Plantation, Pembroke Lodge, [Marble Hill House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/marble-hill-house/), [Orleans House Art Gallery](https://www.orleanshousegallery.org/), [York House Riverside Garden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Naked_Ladies), and [Eel Pie Island](http://www.eelpieislandartists.co.uk). Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Wimbledon Common, Richmond Park, or Twickenham riverside. Wimbledon Common lunch stop: the Fox and Grapes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Pembroke Lodge. Twickenham riverside lunch stop: the White Swan.
TWI.publicWalksCircular:
**[Twickenham Circular via 3 rivers](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/twickehham-circular/)**: Flat urban riverside paths, small river corridors, local parks, Thames towpath, luxury riverside houses, and Palladian parkland. Sights: [River Crane](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Crane,_London), [Duke of Northumberland's River](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Duke_of_Northumberland%27s_River), [Twickenham Stadium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Twickenham_Stadium), [Marble Hill House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/marble-hill-house/), Marble Hill Park, Ham House, Eel Pie Island, York House, and Isleworth Ait. Lunch in Isleworth: the London Apprentice. 11 km.

**[Twickenham Circular via Crane Island Nature Reserve](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/river-crane/)**: Flat river path, urban parks, woods, nature reserves, and paved all-weather surfaces. Sights: Crane Island Nature Reserve, Brick (Shot) Tower, Kneller Gardens, Hounslow Heath, [River Crane](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Crane,_London), and [Duke of Northumberland's River](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Duke_of_Northumberland%27s_River). Can be muddy. Lunch in Kneller Gardens: the Park Cafe. 9 km.

UBHI.name: Buckhurst Hill
UBHI.adminWalksAll:
**Buckhurst Hill to Epping**: A shorter variant of [Manor Park to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/manor-park-to-epping/). Ancient woodland, forest rides, gravel tracks, and meadows. Sights: Connaught Water, Ambresbury Banks Iron Age Hillfort, and [Queen Elizabeth's Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre). Can be muddy. Lunch in High Beach, Ivy Chimneys, or Epping. High Beach lunch stops: the Original Tea Hut, or the [King's Oak Hotel](https://www.kingsoakhotel.com/home). Ivy Chimneys lunch stop: the [Forest Gate Inn](http://www.forestgateinnepping.co.uk/). Epping lunch stops: the [Duke of Wellington](https://dowepping.co.uk//), or the [George & Dragon](http://www.georgeanddragonepping.co.uk/). 15 km.

**Manor Park to Buckhurst Hill**: A shorter variant of [Manor Park to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/manor-park-to-epping/). Open flats, commons, woodland, forest rides, and gravel tracks. Sights: [Epping Forest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Epping_Forest), Wanstead Flats, Hollow Pond, Boating Lake in Highams Park, and Connaught Water. Can be muddy. Lunch in Manor Park, Whipps Cross, Highams Park, or Buckhurst Hill. Manor Park lunch stop: the Blakesley Arms. Whipps Cross lunch stop: the Lakeside Diner. Highams Park lunch stop: the [Humphry's Café](https://highamspark.london/cafes/humphrys-cafe/). Buckhurst Hill lunch stops: the [Three Colts](http://thethreecolts.com/), or the Railway Bar & Grill. 14 km.
UBHI.publicWalksS2S: **Buckhurst Hill to Epping**: A shorter variant of [Manor Park to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/manor-park-to-epping/). Ancient woodland, forest rides, gravel tracks, and meadows. Sights: Connaught Water, Ambresbury Banks Iron Age Hillfort, and [Queen Elizabeth's Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre). Can be muddy. Lunch in High Beach, Ivy Chimneys, or Epping. High Beach lunch stops: the Original Tea Hut, or the [King's Oak Hotel](https://www.kingsoakhotel.com/home). Ivy Chimneys lunch stop: the [Forest Gate Inn](http://www.forestgateinnepping.co.uk/). Epping lunch stops: the [Duke of Wellington](https://dowepping.co.uk//), or the [George & Dragon](http://www.georgeanddragonepping.co.uk/). 15 km.
UBHI.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Manor Park to Buckhurst Hill**: A shorter variant of [Manor Park to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/manor-park-to-epping/). Open flats, commons, woodland, forest rides, and gravel tracks. Sights: [Epping Forest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Epping_Forest), Wanstead Flats, Hollow Pond, Boating Lake in Highams Park, and Connaught Water. Can be muddy. Lunch in Manor Park, Whipps Cross, Highams Park, or Buckhurst Hill. Manor Park lunch stop: the Blakesley Arms. Whipps Cross lunch stop: the Lakeside Diner. Highams Park lunch stop: the [Humphry's Café](https://highamspark.london/cafes/humphrys-cafe/). Buckhurst Hill lunch stops: the [Three Colts](http://thethreecolts.com/), or the Railway Bar & Grill. 14 km.

UBMA.name: Boston Manor
UBMA.adminWalksAll: **[Boston Manor to Northfields](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/boston-manor-to-northfields/)**: Urban parks, canal towpath with locks, golf course, riverside woodland, nature reserve, and suburban streets. Sights: Boston Manor House, [Three Bridges](http://www.disused-stations.org.uk/features/windmill_lane_bridge/), [Brent Lodge Park](https://www.ealing.gov.uk/info/201136/parks_in_the_borough/664/hanwell_parks/2), [Millennium Maze](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Millenium_Maze_in_Brent_Lodge_Park_in_Hanwell_-_panoramio.jpg), [Hanwell Zoo](https://hanwellzoo.co.uk/), [Wharncliffe Viaduct](https://victorianweb.org/technology/bridges/43.html), Hanwell flight of locks, Elthorne Park Nature Reserve, and Blondin Park. Lunch in Boston Manor, Boston Manor Park, Brent Lodge Park, Hanwell, or Northfields. Boston Manor lunch stop: the [Boston Manor House Cafe](https://www.cooksandpartners.co.uk/boston-manor-house-cafe). Boston Manor Park lunch stop: the [Potting Shed cafe](https://thepottingshed.london/menu). Brent Lodge Park lunch stop: the [Brent Lodge Park Cafe (San Remo)](http://brentlodgeparkcafe.co.uk). Hanwell lunch stop: the [Fox Inn](https://www.thefoxpub.co.uk/). Northfields lunch stops: the [Plough](https://www.ploughnorthfields.co.uk/), or the Owl and the Pussy Cat. 14 km.
UBMA.publicWalksS2S: **[Boston Manor to Northfields](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/boston-manor-to-northfields/)**: Urban parks, canal towpath with locks, golf course, riverside woodland, nature reserve, and suburban streets. Sights: Boston Manor House, [Three Bridges](http://www.disused-stations.org.uk/features/windmill_lane_bridge/), [Brent Lodge Park](https://www.ealing.gov.uk/info/201136/parks_in_the_borough/664/hanwell_parks/2), [Millennium Maze](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Millenium_Maze_in_Brent_Lodge_Park_in_Hanwell_-_panoramio.jpg), [Hanwell Zoo](https://hanwellzoo.co.uk/), [Wharncliffe Viaduct](https://victorianweb.org/technology/bridges/43.html), Hanwell flight of locks, Elthorne Park Nature Reserve, and Blondin Park. Lunch in Boston Manor, Boston Manor Park, Brent Lodge Park, Hanwell, or Northfields. Boston Manor lunch stop: the [Boston Manor House Cafe](https://www.cooksandpartners.co.uk/boston-manor-house-cafe). Boston Manor Park lunch stop: the [Potting Shed cafe](https://thepottingshed.london/menu). Brent Lodge Park lunch stop: the [Brent Lodge Park Cafe (San Remo)](http://brentlodgeparkcafe.co.uk). Hanwell lunch stop: the [Fox Inn](https://www.thefoxpub.co.uk/). Northfields lunch stops: the [Plough](https://www.ploughnorthfields.co.uk/), or the Owl and the Pussy Cat. 14 km.

UCHE.name: Chesham
UCHE.adminWalksAll:
**Chesham Circular Walk**: Chilterns fields, woods, and hamlets on a circular route from Chesham. Sights: St Mary Church. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 11 km.

**Chesham to Great Missenden via St John the Baptist Church**: Undulating Chilterns, sloping fields, woods, hamlets, village greens, and beech woodland on Frith Hill. Sights: St Mary Church, St John the Baptist Church, Great Missenden Abbey, and [Roald Dahl Museum](http://www.roalddahlmuseum.org/cafe/). Steep descent into Great Missenden. Lunch in The Lee: the [Cock and Rabbit Inn](http://www.graziemille.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 15 km. 5 hours.

**Chesham to Great Missenden via St Mary Church**: Undulating Chilterns, sloping fields, woods, and hamlets. Sights: St Mary Church and Great Missenden Abbey. A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 9 km. 3 hours.

**[Chorleywood to Chesham via Bedford Chapel](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chorleywood-to-chesham/)**: Chess Valley, Chiltern Hills, hill climbs, panoramic views, woods, celandine and bluebell woods, valley rim permissive path, and suburban finish. Sights: Chenies Manor, Church of St Michaels, Bedford Chapel, and Latimer House. Can flood after rain. Best in Mar–Apr. Lunch in Chorleywood, Sarratt, or Chenies. Chorleywood lunch stop: the Cafe on the Common. Sarratt lunch stop: the Cock Inn. Chenies lunch stops: the Red Lion, or the Bedford Arms. 15 km.
UCHE.publicWalksS2S: **Chesham to Great Missenden via St John the Baptist Church**: Undulating Chilterns, sloping fields, woods, hamlets, village greens, and beech woodland on Frith Hill. Sights: St Mary Church, St John the Baptist Church, Great Missenden Abbey, and [Roald Dahl Museum](http://www.roalddahlmuseum.org/cafe/). Steep descent into Great Missenden. Lunch in The Lee: the [Cock and Rabbit Inn](http://www.graziemille.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 15 km. 5 hours.

Sights: Chenies Manor, Church of St Michaels, Bedford Chapel, and Latimer House. Can flood after rain. Best in Mar–Apr. Lunch in Chorleywood, Sarratt, or Chenies. Chorleywood lunch stop: the Cafe on the Common. Sarratt lunch stop: the Cock Inn. Chenies lunch stops: the Red Lion, or the Bedford Arms. 15 km.

UCK.name: Uckfield
UCK.adminWalksAll:
**[Uckfield Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-via-van-hoogstraatens-path-circular/)**: Undulating low Weald countryside, fields, green lanes, woods, golf course, and suburban estates. Sights: [Hempstead Nature Reserve](http://www.localauthoritypublishing.co.uk/councils/uckfield/enviro.html), [Sussex Horse Rescue Centre](http://sussexhorserescuetrust.org.uk/), [Framfield](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Framfield), [East Sussex National Golf Resort and Spa](http://www.eastsussexnational.co.uk/dine-drink/hotel-lounge-bar/), [Boothland Wood](http://www.localauthoritypublishing.co.uk/councils/uckfield/enviro.html), [Uckfield](http://www.localauthoritypublishing.co.uk/councils/uckfield/index.html), and Framfield Church. Quite a few stiles. Lunch in Framfield, or East Sussex National Golf Resort. Framfield lunch stop: the Hare and Hounds. East Sussex National Golf Resort lunch stop: the [Half Way House Tea Room](http://www.eastsussexnational.co.uk/dine-drink/hotel-lounge-bar/). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Uckfield to Lewes via Glyndebourne opera house](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-lewes/)**: River valleys, fields, woods, golf course, open chalk downland, South Downs escarpment, and historic town. Sights: Horsted Place, Lavender Line (Dingley Dell terminal), Glyndebourne opera house, Glyndebourne Wind Turbine, Southeram Nature Reserve, Lewes Castle, and Caburn Hillfort. Can be muddy. Lunch in Barcombe Mills: the Anchor Inn. 20 km.

**[Uckfield to Buxted via East Sussex National Golf Course](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-buxted/)**: Undulating Low Weald countryside, Uck Valley, golf course, stream valleys, old deer park, parkland, and bluebell woods. Sights: East Sussex National Golf Course, High Cross Estate, Tickerage Mill, Buxted Park, and Buxted House. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Blackboys: the [Blackboys Inn](http://www.theblackboys.co.uk/). 21 km. 5 hours.

**Uckfield to Buxted via Tickerage Mill**: A shorter variant of [Uckfield to Buxted](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-buxted/). Undulating Low Weald countryside, stream valleys, parkland, bluebell woods, and routed through Framfield. Sights: Tickerage Mill, Buxted Park, and Buxted House. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Framfield: the [Hare & Hounds](https://hareandhounds.net/). 18 km.

**Uckfield to Lewes via Barcombe Mills sluices and river pools**: An alternative variant of [Uckfield to Lewes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-lewes/). River valleys, fields, mill streams, riverside path, small villages, and historic town centre. Sights: Barcombe Mills sluices and river pools, St Mary the Virgin church, St Peter church, Hamsey, Lewes Castle, Lewes Barbican, Horsted Place, and Lavender Line (Dingley Dell terminal). Lunch in Barcombe Mills: the Anchor Inn. 18 km.

**Uckfield to Balcombe**: A shorter variant of [Uckfield to Pease Pottage](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-pease-pottage/). Linear walk through East Sussex countryside, villages, and farmland ending at Balcombe station. Lunch in Fletching, or Horsted Keynes. Fletching lunch stop: the [Griffin Inn](https://thegriffininn.co.uk/). Horsted Keynes lunch stops: the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrownhorstedkeynes.co.uk/), or the [Green Man](http://greenmanhorstedkeynes.co.uk/). 29 km.

**[Robertsbridge to Uckfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/robertsbridge-to-uckfield/)**: Linear walk across East Sussex, farmland, fields, villages, and countryside between Robertsbridge and Uckfield. Can be overgrown. Lunch in Wood's Corner, Heathfield, Cade Street, Heathfield, Old Heathfield, Heathfield, Heathfield, Waldron, Heathfield, or Blackboys, Uckfield. Wood's Corner, Heathfield lunch stop: the [Swan Inn](https://swaninndallington.co.uk/). Cade Street, Heathfield lunch stop: the [Half Moon Inn](https://www.halfmoon-inn.co.uk/). Old Heathfield, Heathfield lunch stop: the [Star Inn](https://www.starinnoldheathfield.co.uk/). Heathfield lunch stop: the [Runt In Tun](https://www.runtintun.co.uk/). Waldron, Heathfield lunch stop: the [Star Inn](https://www.thestarinnwaldron.co.uk/). Blackboys, Uckfield lunch stop: the [Blackboys Inn](https://www.theblackboys.co.uk/). 38 km.
UCK.publicWalksS2S:
**[Uckfield to Lewes via Glyndebourne opera house](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-lewes/)**: River valleys, fields, woods, golf course, open chalk downland, South Downs escarpment, and historic town. Sights: Horsted Place, Lavender Line (Dingley Dell terminal), Glyndebourne opera house, Glyndebourne Wind Turbine, Southeram Nature Reserve, Lewes Castle, and Caburn Hillfort. Can be muddy. UCOC.name: Cockfosters
UCOC.adminWalksAll:
**Totteridge & Whetstone to Cockfosters via Hadley Green**: A shorter variant of [Totteridge Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/totteridge-circular/). Greenbelt meadows, Hadley Green, Monken Hadley Common, and wooded parkland. Sights: Hadley Green, Monken Hadley Common, Cockfosters tube station, and Trent Park. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hadley Green: the Monk Pub. 8 km.

**Totteridge & Whetstone to Cockfosters via Darlands Lake Nature Reserve**: A longer variant of [Totteridge Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/totteridge-circular/). Greenbelt meadows, woodland, Hadley Green, Monken Hadley Common, and wooded parkland finishing at a Modernist underground station. Sights: Darlands Lake Nature Reserve, Hadley Green, Battle of Barnet site, Monken Hadley Common, Cockfosters tube station, and Trent Park. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Mill Hill: the Three Hammers, the Summer House, or the [Rising Sun](http://www.therisingsunmillhill.com). 20 km.
UCOC.publicWalksS2SEnding:

UCWO.name: Colliers Wood
UCWO.adminWalksAll:
**[Mitcham Junction to Colliers Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mitcham-junction-to-colliers-wood/)**: Flat urban riverside parks, nature reserves, chalk stream, industrial heritage, and south London commons and green corridors. Sights: [Wandle Valley Regional Park](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk), [Wandle Trail](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk/projects/wandle-trail), [Beddington Farmlands](https://bfnr.org.uk), [Sutton Ecology Centre](https://www.sutton.gov.uk/w/biodiversity-and-wildlife-sutton-ecology-centre), [Honeywood Museum](https://friendsofhoneywood.co.uk/index.html), [Carshalton Water Tower](https://carshaltonwatertower.co.uk/index.html), [Morden Hall Park](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/morden-hall-park), [Deen City Farm](https://www.deencityfarm.co.uk), [Merton Abbey Mills](https://www.mertonabbeymills.org.uk), Carew Manor, Watermeads Nature Reserve, Ravensbury Park, Wilderness Island, Morden Hall, Snuff Mill, and Merton Priory. Lunch in Carshalton: the [Greyhound Hotel](https://www.thegreyhoundhotel.com), the [Hope](https://hopecarshalton.co.uk), or the [Sun](https://www.thesuncarshalton.com/food). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**Waddon to Colliers Wood**: An alternative variant of [Mitcham Junction to Colliers Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mitcham-junction-to-colliers-wood/). Flat urban riverside parks, Wandle Trail, small ornamental ponds, residential streets, and south London green corridors. Sights: [Wandle Trail](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk/projects/wandle-trail), [Sutton Ecology Centre](https://www.sutton.gov.uk/w/biodiversity-and-wildlife-sutton-ecology-centre), [Honeywood Museum](https://friendsofhoneywood.co.uk/index.html), [Carshalton Water Tower](https://carshaltonwatertower.co.uk/index.html), [Morden Hall Park](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/morden-hall-park), [Deen City Farm](https://www.deencityfarm.co.uk), [Merton Abbey Mills](https://www.mertonabbeymills.org.uk), Waddon Ponds, Carew Manor, Beddington Park, Watermeads Nature Reserve, Ravensbury Park, Wilderness Island, Morden Hall, and Merton Priory. Lunch in Carshalton: the [Greyhound Hotel](https://www.thegreyhoundhotel.com), the [Hope](https://hopecarshalton.co.uk), or the [Sun](https://www.thesuncarshalton.com/food). 14 km. 3 hours.

**Carshalton to Colliers Wood**: A shorter variant of [Mitcham Junction to Colliers Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mitcham-junction-to-colliers-wood/). Flat urban riverside parks, nature reserves, chalk stream, and south London green corridors. Sights: [Wandle Trail](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk/projects/wandle-trail), [Sutton Ecology Centre](https://www.sutton.gov.uk/w/biodiversity-and-wildlife-sutton-ecology-centre), [Honeywood Museum](https://friendsofhoneywood.co.uk/index.html), [Carshalton Water Tower](https://carshaltonwatertower.co.uk/index.html), [Morden Hall Park](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/morden-hall-park), [Deen City Farm](https://www.deencityfarm.co.uk), [Merton Abbey Mills](https://www.mertonabbeymills.org.uk), Carshalton Ponds, Watermeads Nature Reserve, Ravensbury Park, Wilderness Island, Morden Hall, and Merton Priory. Lunch in Carshalton: the [Greyhound Hotel](https://www.thegreyhoundhotel.com), the [Hope](https://hopecarshalton.co.uk), or the [Sun](https://www.thesuncarshalton.com/food). 9 km. 2 hours.

Sights: [Wandle Valley Regional Park](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk), [Wandle Trail](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk/projects/wandle-trail), [Beddington Farmlands](https://bfnr.org.uk), [Sutton Ecology Centre](https://www.sutton.gov.uk/w/biodiversity-and-wildlife-sutton-ecology-centre), [Honeywood Museum](https://friendsofhoneywood.co.uk/index.html), [Carshalton Water Tower](https://carshaltonwatertower.co.uk/index.html), [Morden Hall Park](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/morden-hall-park), [Deen City Farm](https://www.deencityfarm.co.uk), [Merton Abbey Mills](https://www.mertonabbeymills.org.uk), Carew Manor, Watermeads Nature Reserve, Ravensbury Park, Wilderness Island, Morden Hall, Snuff Mill, and Merton Priory. Lunch in Carshalton: the [Greyhound Hotel](https://www.thegreyhoundhotel.com), the [Hope](https://hopecarshalton.co.uk), or the [Sun](https://www.thesuncarshalton.com/food). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

UEFI.name: East Finchley
UEFI.adminWalksAll: **East Finchley to Hampstead**: A shorter variant of [Highgate to Hampstead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/highgate-to-hampstead/). Urban green spaces, woods, heathland, parkland, ponds, garden suburb streets, and panoramic hilltops. Sights: [Hampstead Garden Suburb](http://www.hgs.org.uk/history/index.html), [Free Church](https://www.hgsfreechurch.org.uk), [St Jude-on-the-Hill](https://www.stjudeonthehill.com), [Hampstead Heath](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hampstead_Heath), [Golders Hill Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Golders_Hill_Park), [The Pergola](https://www.historic-uk.com/HistoryMagazine/DestinationsUK/Hampstead-Pergola-Hill-Gardens), [Kenwood House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/kenwood), [Burgh House](https://www.burghhouse.org.uk), [Fenton House and Garden](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/fenton-house-and-garden), [St John-at-Hampstead](https://hampsteadparishchurch.org.uk), The Archer, Hill Garden, and Parliament Hill. Can be muddy. Lunch in North End Road, or Golders Hill Park. North End Road lunch stop: the [Old Bull & Bush](https://www.thebullandbush.co.uk). Golders Hill Park lunch stop: the Refreshment House. 13 km. 3.5 hours.
UEFI.publicWalksS2S: **East Finchley to Hampstead**: A shorter variant of [Highgate to Hampstead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/highgate-to-hampstead/). Urban green spaces, woods, heathland, parkland, ponds, garden suburb streets, and panoramic hilltops. Sights: [Hampstead Garden Suburb](http://www.hgs.org.uk/history/index.html), [Free Church](https://www.hgsfreechurch.org.uk), [St Jude-on-the-Hill](https://www.stjudeonthehill.com), [Hampstead Heath](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hampstead_Heath), [Golders Hill Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Golders_Hill_Park), [The Pergola](https://www.historic-uk.com/HistoryMagazine/DestinationsUK/Hampstead-Pergola-Hill-Gardens), [Kenwood House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/kenwood), [Burgh House](https://www.burghhouse.org.uk), [Fenton House and Garden](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/fenton-house-and-garden), [St John-at-Hampstead](https://hampsteadparishchurch.org.uk), The Archer, Hill Garden, and Parliament Hill. Can be muddy. Lunch in North End Road, or Golders Hill Park. North End Road lunch stop: the [Old Bull & Bush](https://www.thebullandbush.co.uk). Golders Hill Park lunch stop: the Refreshment House. 13 km. 3.5 hours.

UEPP.name: Epping
UEPP.adminWalksAll:
**[Epping to Ingatestone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/epping-to-ingatestone-via-chipping-ongar/)**: Mildly undulating Essex countryside, ancient woods, arable fields, historic villages, old churches, manor houses, and London Clay subsoil. Sights: Chipping Ongar Motte-and-Double-Bailey, St. Andrew's Church, Greensted-juxta-Ongar, St. Laurence Church, Blackmore, and Gernon Bushes SSSI. Can be muddy. Lunch in Coopersale: the Theydon Oak. 26 km. 6 hours.

**[Loughton to Epping via Loughton Camp](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/loughton-to-epping/)**: Ancient woodland, pollarded oaks, beeches and hornbeams, open countryside, farmland, parkland near a ruined country house, and bustling market town. Sights: Epping Forest, Loughton Camp, [High Beach Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/high-beach-visitor-centre), and [Copped Hall](http://www.coppedhalltrust.org.uk/). Can be muddy. Best in Dec–May. Lunch in High Beach, or Upshire. High Beach lunch stops: the [High Beach Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/high-beach-visitor-centre), or the [Kings Oak](http://www.kingsoakhotel.com/). Upshire lunch stop: the [Horseshoes](https://www.instagram.com/thehorseshoesupshire/?hl=en). 13 km. 4 hours.

**[Theydon Bois to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/theydon-bois-to-epping/)**: Marshland, woods, gently rolling meadow-land, scenic hamlets, quiet tarmac roads with grassy verges, and motorway crossings. Sights: GreenAcres and [Theydon Oak pub](https://theydonoak.co.uk/). Boardwalks rotten in places. Lunch in Woodhatch, North Weald, or Theydon Garnon. Woodhatch lunch stop: the [Dog House](https://www.facebook.com/themoletrappubepping/). North Weald lunch stop: the [Kings Head](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thekingsheadnorthweald#/). Theydon Garnon lunch stop: the [Garnon Bushes](https://www.garnonbushespub.com/). 15 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Manor Park to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/manor-park-to-epping/)**: Ancient woodland, open flats, commons, forest rides, gravel tracks, residential roads, and meadows. Sights: [Epping Forest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Epping_Forest), [Queen Elizabeth's Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre), [The View Epping Forest Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre), Ambresbury Banks Iron Age Hillfort, Wanstead Flats, Hollow Pond, Connaught Water, and Boating Lake in Highams Park. Can be muddy. Lunch in Manor Park, Whipps Cross, Highams Park, Rangers Road, High Beach, Ivy Chimneys, or Epping. Manor Park lunch stop: the Blakesley Arms. Whipps Cross lunch stop: the Lakeside Diner. Highams Park lunch stop: the [Humphry's Café](https://highamspark.london/cafes/humphrys-cafe/). Rangers Road lunch stop: the [Larder at Butler's Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/). High Beach lunch stops: the Original Tea Hut, the Oyster Shack & Seafood Bar, the King's Oak Café, or the [King's Oak Hotel](https://www.kingsoakhotel.com/home). Ivy Chimneys lunch stops: the [Forest Gate Inn](http://www.forestgateinnepping.co.uk/), or the [Perky Blenders](https://perkyblenders.com/). Epping lunch stops: the [Duke of Wellington](https://dowepping.co.uk//), the Dada Café, the [George & Dragon](http://www.georgeanddragonepping.co.uk/), the Black Lion, or the [Funky Monk](https://funkymonk.co.uk/). 24 km. 5.5 hours.

**Chingford to Epping**: A shorter variant of [Manor Park to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/manor-park-to-epping/). Ancient woodland, forest rides, gravel tracks, and meadows. Sights: [Queen Elizabeth's Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre), [The View Epping Forest Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre), Connaught Water, and Ambresbury Banks Iron Age Hillfort. Can be muddy. Lunch in Rangers Road, High Beach, Ivy Chimneys, or Epping. Rangers Road lunch stop: the [Larder at Butler's Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/). High Beach lunch stops: the Original Tea Hut, or the [King's Oak Hotel](https://www.kingsoakhotel.com/home). Ivy Chimneys lunch stop: the [Forest Gate Inn](http://www.forestgateinnepping.co.uk/). Epping lunch stops: the [Duke of Wellington](https://dowepping.co.uk//), or the [George & Dragon](http://www.georgeanddragonepping.co.uk/). 13 km.

**Buckhurst Hill to Epping**: A shorter variant of [Manor Park to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/manor-park-to-epping/). Ancient woodland, forest rides, gravel tracks, and meadows. Sights: Connaught Water, Ambresbury Banks Iron Age Hillfort, and [Queen Elizabeth's Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre). Can be muddy. Lunch in High Beach, Ivy Chimneys, or Epping. High Beach lunch stops: the Original Tea Hut, or the [King's Oak Hotel](https://www.kingsoakhotel.com/home). Ivy Chimneys lunch stop: the [Forest Gate Inn](http://www.forestgateinnepping.co.uk/). Epping lunch stops: the [Duke of Wellington](https://dowepping.co.uk//), or the [George & Dragon](http://www.georgeanddragonepping.co.uk/). 15 km.

**Loughton to Epping via Epping Forest**: A shorter variant of [Loughton to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/loughton-to-epping/). Almost entirely woodland on the Three Forest Way, crossing roads, and with a shorter start through Loughton. Sights: Epping Forest. Good midsummer evening. 9 km.

**Leytonstone to Epping**: A longer variant of [Leytonstone to Loughton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leytonstone-to-loughton/). Open woodland, earth tracks, forest trails, ponds, Iron Age earthworks, and gentle slopes. Sights: [Queen Elizabeth Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/queen-elizabeths-hunting-lodge), Epping Forest, Loughton Camp, and Ambresbury Banks. Can be muddy. Best in Oct–Nov. Lunch in Chingford: the [Butlers Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/). 20 km.
UEPP.publicWalksS2S: **[Epping to Ingatestone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/epping-to-ingatestone-via-chipping-ongar/)**: Mildly undulating Essex countryside, ancient woods, arable fields, historic villages, old churches, manor houses, and London Clay subsoil. Sights: Chipping Ongar Motte-and-Double-Bailey, St. Andrew's Church, Greensted-juxta-Ongar, St. Laurence Church, Blackmore, and Gernon Bushes SSSI. Can be muddy. Lunch in Coopersale: the Theydon Oak. 26 km. 6 hours.
UEPP.publicWalksS2SEnding:
**[Loughton to Epping via Loughton Camp](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/loughton-to-epping/)**: Ancient woodland, pollarded oaks, beeches and hornbeams, open countryside, farmland, parkland near a ruined country house, and bustling market town. Sights: Epping Forest, Loughton Camp, [High Beach Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/high-beach-visitor-centre), and [Copped Hall](http://www.coppedhalltrust.org.uk/). Can be muddy. Best in Dec–May. Lunch in High Beach, or Upshire. High Beach lunch stops: the [High Beach Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/high-beach-visitor-centre), or the [Kings Oak](http://www.kingsoakhotel.com/). Upshire lunch stop: the [Horseshoes](https://www.instagram.com/thehorseshoesupshire/?hl=en). 13 km. 4 hours.

**[Theydon Bois to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/theydon-bois-to-epping/)**: Marshland, woods, gently rolling meadow-land, scenic hamlets, quiet tarmac roads with grassy verges, and motorway crossings. Sights: GreenAcres and [Theydon Oak pub](https://theydonoak.co.uk/). Boardwalks rotten in places. Lunch in Woodhatch, North Weald, or Theydon Garnon. Woodhatch lunch stop: the [Dog House](https://www.facebook.com/themoletrappubepping/). North Weald lunch stop: the [Kings Head](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thekingsheadnorthweald#/). Theydon Garnon lunch stop: the [Garnon Bushes](https://www.garnonbushespub.com/). 15 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Manor Park to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/manor-park-to-epping/)**: Ancient woodland, open flats, commons, forest rides, gravel tracks, residential roads, and meadows. Sights: [Epping Forest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Epping_Forest), [Queen Elizabeth's Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre), [The View Epping Forest Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre), Ambresbury Banks Iron Age Hillfort, Wanstead Flats, Hollow Pond, Connaught Water, and Boating Lake in Highams Park. Can be muddy. Lunch in Manor Park, Whipps Cross, Highams Park, Rangers Road, High Beach, Ivy Chimneys, or Epping. Manor Park lunch stop: the Blakesley Arms. Whipps Cross lunch stop: the Lakeside Diner. Highams Park lunch stop: the [Humphry's Café](https://highamspark.london/cafes/humphrys-cafe/). Rangers Road lunch stop: the [Larder at Butler's Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/). High Beach lunch stops: the Original Tea Hut, the Oyster Shack & Seafood Bar, the King's Oak Café, or the [King's Oak Hotel](https://www.kingsoakhotel.com/home). Ivy Chimneys lunch stops: the [Forest Gate Inn](http://www.forestgateinnepping.co.uk/), or the [Perky Blenders](https://perkyblenders.com/). Epping lunch stops: the [Duke of Wellington](https://dowepping.co.uk//), the Dada Café, the [George & Dragon](http://www.georgeanddragonepping.co.uk/), the Black Lion, or the [Funky Monk](https://funkymonk.co.uk/). 24 km. 5.5 hours.

UHAE.name: Hammersmith (District and Piccadilly lines)

Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Barnes: the [Sun Inn](https://www.thesuninnbarnes.co.uk), the [Coach & Horses](https://www.coachandhorsesbarnes.co.uk), the [Bulls Head](https://www.thebullsheadbarnes.com), or the [Watermans Arms](https://www.watermansarms.co.uk). 16 km. 3.5 hours.

UHBA.name: High Barnet

Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Mill Hill: the Three Hammers, the Summer House, or the [Rising Sun](http://www.therisingsunmillhill.com). 18 km.

**Loughton to Epping via Epping Forest**: A shorter variant of [Loughton to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/loughton-to-epping/). Almost entirely woodland on the Three Forest Way, crossing roads, and with a shorter start through Loughton. Sights: Epping Forest. Good midsummer evening. 9 km.

**[Leytonstone to Loughton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leytonstone-to-loughton/)**: Open woodland, densely forested areas, grassy paths, earth tracks, ponds, and gentle slopes. Sights: [Queen Elizabeth Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/queen-elizabeths-hunting-lodge), Epping Forest, and High Stone. Can be muddy. Best in Oct, Nov, Apr and May. Lunch in Chingford: the [Butlers Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/). 13 km.
ULOU.publicWalksS2S: **[Loughton to Epping via Loughton Camp](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/loughton-to-epping/)**: Ancient woodland, pollarded oaks, beeches and hornbeams, open countryside, farmland, parkland near a ruined country house, and bustling market town. Sights: Epping Forest, Loughton Camp, [High Beach Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/high-beach-visitor-centre), and [Copped Hall](http://www.coppedhalltrust.org.uk/). Can be muddy. Best in Dec–May. Lunch in High Beach, or Upshire. High Beach lunch stops: the [High Beach Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/high-beach-visitor-centre), or the [Kings Oak](http://www.kingsoakhotel.com/). Upshire lunch stop: the [Horseshoes](https://www.instagram.com/thehorseshoesupshire/?hl=en). 13 km. 4 hours.
ULOU.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Leytonstone to Loughton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leytonstone-to-loughton/)**: Open woodland, densely forested areas, grassy paths, earth tracks, ponds, and gentle slopes. Sights: [Queen Elizabeth Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/queen-elizabeths-hunting-lodge), Epping Forest, and High Stone. Can be muddy. Best in Oct, Nov, Apr and May. Lunch in Chingford: the [Butlers Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/). 13 km.

ULSQ.name: Leicester Square
ULSQ.adminWalksAll: **[Leicester Square Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/central-london-circular/)**: Urban streets, royal parks, riverside Thames path, historic city squares, and financial district. Sights: Leicester Square, Trafalgar Square, St James's Royal Park, Westminster Abbey, Houses of Parliament, London Eye, National Theatre, Globe Theatre, HMS Belfast, Tower Bridge, St Catherine's Dock, Tower of London, Bank of England, St Paul's Cathedral, Lincoln's Inn Fields, and Covent Garden. 15 km.
ULSQ.publicWalksCircular: **[Leicester Square Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/central-london-circular/)**: Urban streets, royal parks, riverside Thames path, historic city squares, and financial district. Sights: Leicester Square, Trafalgar Square, St James's Royal Park, Westminster Abbey, Houses of Parliament, London Eye, National Theatre, Globe Theatre, HMS Belfast, Tower Bridge, St Catherine's Dock, Tower of London, Bank of England, St Paul's Cathedral, Lincoln's Inn Fields, and Covent Garden. 15 km.

UMHU.name: Manor House
UMHU.adminWalksAll: **Alexandra Palace to Manor House**: A shorter variant of [Alexandra Palace to Tottenham Hale](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/alexandra-palace-to-tottenham-hale/). Disused railway trackbed nature reserve, ancient woodland, reservoir circuit, and residential streets. Sights: [Alexandra Palace](https://www.alexandrapalace.com), [Parkland Walk](https://www.parkland-walk.org.uk), Highgate Wood, Finsbury Park, and [Woodberry Wetlands](https://www.wildlondon.org.uk/woodberry-wetlands-nature-reserve). Lunch in Finsbury Park, or Woodberry Wetlands. Finsbury Park lunch stop: the Finsbury Park Café. Woodberry Wetlands lunch stop: the Coal House Café. 11 km. 2.5 hours.
UMHU.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Alexandra Palace to Manor House**: A shorter variant of [Alexandra Palace to Tottenham Hale](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/alexandra-palace-to-tottenham-hale/). Sights: [Alexandra Palace](https://www.alexandrapalace.com), [Parkland Walk](https://www.parkland-walk.org.uk), Highgate Wood, Finsbury Park, and [Woodberry Wetlands](https://www.wildlondon.org.uk/woodberry-wetlands-nature-reserve). Lunch in Finsbury Park, or Woodberry Wetlands. Finsbury Park lunch stop: the Finsbury Park Café. Woodberry Wetlands lunch stop: the Coal House Café. 11 km. 2.5 hours.

UMOR.name: Morden
UMOR.adminWalksAll:
**Mitcham Junction to Morden**: A shorter variant of [Mitcham Junction to Colliers Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mitcham-junction-to-colliers-wood/). Flat urban riverside parks, nature reserves, chalk stream, industrial heritage, and south London commons and green corridors. Sights: [Wandle Valley Regional Park](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk), [Wandle Trail](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk/projects/wandle-trail), [Beddington Farmlands](https://bfnr.org.uk), [Sutton Ecology Centre](https://www.sutton.gov.uk/w/biodiversity-and-wildlife-sutton-ecology-centre), [Honeywood Museum](https://friendsofhoneywood.co.uk/index.html), [Carshalton Water Tower](https://carshaltonwatertower.co.uk/index.html), [Morden Hall Park](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/morden-hall-park), Carew Manor, Watermeads Nature Reserve, Ravensbury Park, and Wilderness Island. Lunch in Carshalton: the [Greyhound Hotel](https://www.thegreyhoundhotel.com), the [Hope](https://hopecarshalton.co.uk), or the [Sun](https://www.thesuncarshalton.com/food). 13 km.

**Waddon to Morden**: An alternative variant of [Mitcham Junction to Colliers Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mitcham-junction-to-colliers-wood/). Flat urban riverside parks, Wandle Trail, small ornamental ponds, residential streets, and south London green corridors. Sights: [Wandle Trail](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk/projects/wandle-trail), [Sutton Ecology Centre](https://www.sutton.gov.uk/w/biodiversity-and-wildlife-sutton-ecology-centre), [Honeywood Museum](https://friendsofhoneywood.co.uk/index.html), [Carshalton Water Tower](https://carshaltonwatertower.co.uk/index.html), [Morden Hall Park](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/morden-hall-park), Waddon Ponds, Carew Manor, Beddington Park, Watermeads Nature Reserve, Ravensbury Park, and Wilderness Island. Lunch in Carshalton: the [Greyhound Hotel](https://www.thegreyhoundhotel.com), the [Hope](https://hopecarshalton.co.uk), or the [Sun](https://www.thesuncarshalton.com/food). 12 km.

**Carshalton to Morden**: A shorter variant of [Mitcham Junction to Colliers Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mitcham-junction-to-colliers-wood/). UNOR.name: Northfields
UNOR.adminWalksAll: **[Boston Manor to Northfields](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/boston-manor-to-northfields/)**: Urban parks, canal towpath with locks, golf course, riverside woodland, nature reserve, and suburban streets. Sights: Boston Manor House, [Three Bridges](http://www.disused-stations.org.uk/features/windmill_lane_bridge/), [Brent Lodge Park](https://www.ealing.gov.uk/info/201136/parks_in_the_borough/664/hanwell_parks/2), [Millennium Maze](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Millenium_Maze_in_Brent_Lodge_Park_in_Hanwell_-_panoramio.jpg), [Hanwell Zoo](https://hanwellzoo.co.uk/), [Wharncliffe Viaduct](https://victorianweb.org/technology/bridges/43.html), Hanwell flight of locks, Elthorne Park Nature Reserve, and Blondin Park. Lunch in Boston Manor, Boston Manor Park, Brent Lodge Park, Hanwell, or Northfields. Boston Manor lunch stop: the [Boston Manor House Cafe](https://www.cooksandpartners.co.uk/boston-manor-house-cafe). Boston Manor Park lunch stop: the [Potting Shed cafe](https://thepottingshed.london/menu). Brent Lodge Park lunch stop: the [Brent Lodge Park Cafe (San Remo)](http://brentlodgeparkcafe.co.uk). Hanwell lunch stop: the [Fox Inn](https://www.thefoxpub.co.uk/). Northfields lunch stops: the [Plough](https://www.ploughnorthfields.co.uk/), or the Owl and the Pussy Cat. 14 km.
UNOR.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Boston Manor to Northfields](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/boston-manor-to-northfields/)**: Urban parks, canal towpath with locks, golf course, riverside woodland, nature reserve, and suburban streets. Sights: Boston Manor House, [Three Bridges](http://www.disused-stations.org.uk/features/windmill_lane_bridge/), [Brent Lodge Park](https://www.ealing.gov.uk/info/201136/parks_in_the_borough/664/hanwell_parks/2), [Millennium Maze](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Millenium_Maze_in_Brent_Lodge_Park_in_Hanwell_-_panoramio.jpg), [Hanwell Zoo](https://hanwellzoo.co.uk/), [Wharncliffe Viaduct](https://victorianweb.org/technology/bridges/43.html), Hanwell flight of locks, Elthorne Park Nature Reserve, and Blondin Park. Lunch in Boston Manor, Boston Manor Park, Brent Lodge Park, Hanwell, or Northfields. Boston Manor lunch stop: the [Boston Manor House Cafe](https://www.cooksandpartners.co.uk/boston-manor-house-cafe). Boston Manor Park lunch stop: the [Potting Shed cafe](https://thepottingshed.london/menu). Brent Lodge Park lunch stop: the [Brent Lodge Park Cafe (San Remo)](http://brentlodgeparkcafe.co.uk). Hanwell lunch stop: the [Fox Inn](https://www.thefoxpub.co.uk/). Northfields lunch stops: the [Plough](https://www.ploughnorthfields.co.uk/), or the Owl and the Pussy Cat. 14 km.

UOST.name: Osterley
UOST.adminWalksAll: **[Greenford to Osterley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/greenford-to-osterley/)**: Urban parks, open meadows, river towpath, Grand Union Canal, flat West London countryside, and stately home grounds. Sights: Osterley Park, Osterley House, Hanwell Locks, and Brent River Park. Lunch in Tentelow Lane: the Plough. 12 km. 3 hours.
UOST.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Greenford to Osterley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/greenford-to-osterley/)**: Urban parks, open meadows, river towpath, Grand Union Canal, flat West London countryside, and stately home grounds. Sights: Osterley Park, Osterley House, Hanwell Locks, and Brent River Park. Lunch in Tentelow Lane: the Plough. 12 km. 3 hours.

UPBR.name: Putney Bridge
UPBR.adminWalksAll:
**[Tower Hill to Putney Bridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tower-hill-to-putney-bridge-via-thames-path/)**: Entirely tarmac and hard surfaces, riverside Thames Path, urban parks, historic bridges, embankments, and modern riverside developments. Sights: Tower of London, All Hallows by the Tower, Tower Bridge, HMS Belfast, Globe Theatre, Tate Modern, [Millennium Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Millennium_Bridge,_London), St Paul's Cathedral, National Theatre, South Bank Centre, Royal Festival Hall, Cleopatra's Needle, London Eye, County Hall, London Dungeon, London Sealife Aquarium, Palace of Westminster, Tate Britain, Battersea Power Station, Battersea Park, London Peace Pagoda, Fulham Palace, and Tattershall Castle. Lunch in Millbank, Wandsworth, or Putney. Millbank lunch stop: the Morpeth Arms. Wandsworth lunch stop: the Ship. Putney lunch stop: the Rocket. 18 km.

**[Stoneleigh to Putney Bridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/beverley-brook/)**: Suburban streets, riverside brookside paths, commons, parkland, re-naturalised meanders, wet woodland, and Thames-side esplanade. Sights: Wimbledon Common, Richmond Park, Barnes Common, Beverley Meads LNR, Craven Cottage, and Barn Elms. Can be muddy. Lunch in Worcester Park, Motspur Park, New Malden, West Wimbledon, London SW15, East Sheen, or Barnes. Worcester Park lunch stop: the [Brook Pub & Dining](https://thebrook worcesterpark.co.uk/). Motspur Park lunch stops: the Blossom & Brew, the Café Rich Point, or the [Earl Beatty](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/surrey/earl-beatty). New Malden lunch stop: the [Glasshouse](https://www.glasshouse.pub/). West Wimbledon lunch stop: the [Da Mimmo](https://www.damimmowimbledon.com/). London SW15 lunch stop: the [Roehampton Gate Café](https://www.colicci.co.uk/roehampton-gate-cafe). East Sheen lunch stop: the [Palewell Café on the Common](https://www.colicci.co.uk/roehampton-gate-cafe). Barnes lunch stops: the Café Loris, or the [Stag's Head](https://www.stagsheadbarnes.co.uk/). 21 km. 4.5 hours.

**New Malden to Putney Bridge**: A shorter variant of [Beverley Brook](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/beverley-brook/). Parkland, commons, brookside paths, wet woodland, and Thames-side esplanade. Sights: Wimbledon Common, Richmond Park, Barnes Common, and Beverley Meads LNR. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Wimbledon, London SW15, East Sheen, or Barnes. West Wimbledon lunch stop: the [Da Mimmo](https://www.damimmowimbledon.com/). London SW15 lunch stop: the [Roehampton Gate Café](https://www.colicci.co.uk/roehampton-gate-cafe). East Sheen lunch stop: the [Palewell Café on the Common](https://www.colicci.co.uk/roehampton-gate-cafe). Barnes lunch stops: the Café Loris, or the [Stag's Head](https://www.stagsheadbarnes.co.uk/). 13 km.

**Barnes to Putney Bridge via Chiswick House & Gardens**: A variant of [Norbiton to Hammersmith or Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/norbiton-to-hammersmith-or-putney/). Village green, Thames riverfront, historic riverside houses, landscape garden, modern riverside developments, riverside park, and palace grounds. Sights: [Chiswick House & Gardens](https://chiswickhouseandgardens.org.uk), Hogarth Tomb-Monument, Craven Cottage, [Fulham Palace](https://www.fulhampalace.org), and [London Wetland Centre](https://www.wwt.org.uk/wetland-centres/london). Lunch in Barnes: the [Sun Inn](https://www.thesuninnbarnes.co.uk), the [Coach & Horses](https://www.coachandhorsesbarnes.co.uk), the [Bulls Head](https://www.thebullsheadbarnes.com), or the [Watermans Arms](https://www.watermansarms.co.uk). 10 km.

**Westminster to Putney Bridge**: A shorter variant of [Tower Hill to Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tower-hill-to-putney-bridge-via-thames-path/). Entirely tarmac and hard surfaces, Thames riverside embankments, urban parks, bridges, and modern riverside developments. Sights: Palace of Westminster, Tate Britain, Battersea Power Station, Battersea Park, London Peace Pagoda, and Fulham Palace. Lunch in Millbank, Wandsworth, or Putney. Millbank lunch stop: the Morpeth Arms. Wandsworth lunch stop: the Ship. Putney lunch stop: the Rocket. 7 km.

**Norbiton to Putney Bridge**: A longer variant of [Norbiton to Hammersmith or Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/norbiton-to-hammersmith-or-putney/). Deer park, woodland garden, open grassland, riverside common, village green, Thames riverfront, modern riverside developments, riverside park, and palace grounds. Sights: [Richmond Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond-park), Isabella Plantation, Pen Ponds, [Chiswick House & Gardens](https://chiswickhouseandgardens.org.uk), Hogarth Tomb-Monument, [Hogarth House](https://williamhogarthtrust.org.uk), Craven Cottage, [Fulham Palace](https://www.fulhampalace.org), Lancelot Capability Brown statue, and [London Wetland Centre](https://www.wwt.org.uk/wetland-centres/london). Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Barnes: the [Sun Inn](https://www.thesuninnbarnes.co.uk), the [Coach & Horses](https://www.coachandhorsesbarnes.co.uk), the [Bulls Head](https://www.thebullsheadbarnes.com), or the [Watermans Arms](https://www.watermansarms.co.uk). 19 km. 4.5 hours.

**Barnes to Putney Bridge via Barnes Common**: A shorter variant of [Beverley Brook](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/beverley-brook/). Commons, brookside paths, and Thames-side esplanade. Sights: Barnes Common and Barn Elms. Lunch in Barnes: the Café Loris, or the [Stag's Head](https://www.stagsheadbarnes.co.uk/). 4 km.

UTBO.name: Theydon Bois
UTBO.adminWalksAll: **[Theydon Bois to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/theydon-bois-to-epping/)**: Marshland, woods, gently rolling meadow-land, scenic hamlets, quiet tarmac roads with grassy verges, and motorway crossings. Sights: GreenAcres and [Theydon Oak pub](https://theydonoak.co.uk/). Boardwalks rotten in places. Lunch in Woodhatch, North Weald, or Theydon Garnon. Woodhatch lunch stop: the [Dog House](https://www.facebook.com/themoletrappubepping/). North Weald lunch stop: the [Kings Head](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thekingsheadnorthweald#/). Theydon Garnon lunch stop: the [Garnon Bushes](https://www.garnonbushespub.com/). 15 km. 4.5 hours.

UTWH.name: Totteridge & Whetstone
UTWH.adminWalksAll:
**[Totteridge & Whetstone Circular via Alexandra Palace](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/totteridge-circular/)**: Greenbelt meadows, former hayfields, young oak woodland, wooded wetland, village greens, and suburban streets on London's Northern Heights. Sights: Darlands Lake Nature Reserve, Darlands Lake, and Alexandra Palace. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Mill Hill: the Three Hammers, the Summer House, or the [Rising Sun](http://www.therisingsunmillhill.com). 14 km.

**Totteridge & Whetstone Circular via Darlands Lake Nature Reserve**: A shorter variant of [Totteridge Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/totteridge-circular/). Greenbelt meadows, former hayfields, young oak woodland, wooded wetland, village greens, and suburban streets on London's Northern Heights. Sights: Darlands Lake Nature Reserve and Darlands Lake. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Mill Hill: the Three Hammers, the Summer House, or the [Rising Sun](http://www.therisingsunmillhill.com). 13 km.

**Totteridge & Whetstone Circular via Totteridge Green**: A variant of [Totteridge Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/totteridge-circular/). Greenbelt meadows, woodland, Totteridge village, Totteridge Green, and suburban roads. Sights: Darlands Lake Nature Reserve and Totteridge Green. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Mill Hill: the Three Hammers, the Summer House, or the [Rising Sun](http://www.therisingsunmillhill.com). 14 km.

**Totteridge & Whetstone to Cockfosters via Hadley Green**: A shorter variant of [Totteridge Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/totteridge-circular/). Greenbelt meadows, Hadley Green, Monken Hadley Common, and wooded parkland. Sights: Hadley Green, Monken Hadley Common, Cockfosters tube station, and Trent Park. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hadley Green: the Monk Pub. 8 km.

**Totteridge & Whetstone to High Barnet**: A longer variant of [Totteridge Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/totteridge-circular/). Greenbelt meadows, woodland, suburban streets, Hadley Green, and fine houses around a historic battlefield site. Sights: Darlands Lake Nature Reserve, Hadley Green, and Battle of Barnet site. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Mill Hill: the Three Hammers, the Summer House, or the [Rising Sun](http://www.therisingsunmillhill.com). 18 km.

**Totteridge & Whetstone to Cockfosters via Darlands Lake Nature Reserve**: A longer variant of [Totteridge Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/totteridge-circular/). Greenbelt meadows, woodland, Hadley Green, Monken Hadley Common, and wooded parkland finishing at a Modernist underground station. Sights: Darlands Lake Nature Reserve, Hadley Green, Battle of Barnet site, Monken Hadley Common, Cockfosters tube station, and Trent Park. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Mill Hill: the Three Hammers, the Summer House, or the [Rising Sun](http://www.therisingsunmillhill.com). 20 km.
UTWH.publicWalksCircular: **[Totteridge & Whetstone Circular via Alexandra Palace](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/totteridge-circular/)**: Greenbelt meadows, former hayfields, young oak woodland, wooded wetland, village greens, and suburban streets on London's Northern Heights. Sights: Darlands Lake Nature Reserve, Darlands Lake, and Alexandra Palace. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Mill Hill: the Three Hammers, the Summer House, or the [Rising Sun](http://www.therisingsunmillhill.com). 14 km.

UWES.name: Westminster
UWES.adminWalksAll:
**[Westminster to Cutty Sark for Maritime Greenwich](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-bridge-to-greenwich-via-the-south-bank/)**: Flat riverside pavement, historic South Bank, converted Victorian wharves, residential riverside developments, industrial heritage, and pocket parks. Sights: London Eye, Southbank Centre, National Theatre, Tate Modern, Millennium Bridge, Globe Theatre, Southwark Cathedral, Golden Hinde, Hays Galleria, [HMS Belfast](http://www.iwm.org.uk/visits/hms-belfast), City Hall, [Tower Bridge](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tower_Bridge), Design Museum, [Brunel Museum](http://www.brunel-museum.org.uk/), Surrey Docks Farm, Statue of Peter the Great, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, [Cutty Sark](http://www.rmg.co.uk/cuttysark), [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), Royal Observatory, Winchester Palace, and Tower of London. Lunch in Bermondsey, or Rotherhithe. Bermondsey lunch stop: the Angel Pub. Rotherhithe lunch stops: the [Mayflower pub](http://www.mayflowerpub.co.uk/), or the Salt Quay Pub.

**Westminster to Putney Bridge**: A shorter variant of [Tower Hill to Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tower-hill-to-putney-bridge-via-thames-path/). Entirely tarmac and hard surfaces, Thames riverside embankments, urban parks, bridges, and modern riverside developments. Sights: Palace of Westminster, Tate Britain, Battersea Power Station, Battersea Park, London Peace Pagoda, and Fulham Palace. Lunch in Millbank, Wandsworth, or Putney. Millbank lunch stop: the Morpeth Arms. Wandsworth lunch stop: the Ship. Putney lunch stop: the Rocket. 7 km.
UWES.publicWalksS2S: **[Westminster to Cutty Sark for Maritime Greenwich](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/london-bridge-to-greenwich-via-the-south-bank/)**: Flat riverside pavement, historic South Bank, converted Victorian wharves, residential riverside developments, industrial heritage, and pocket parks. Sights: London Eye, Southbank Centre, National Theatre, Tate Modern, Millennium Bridge, Globe Theatre, Southwark Cathedral, Golden Hinde, Hays Galleria, [HMS Belfast](http://www.iwm.org.uk/visits/hms-belfast), City Hall, [Tower Bridge](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tower_Bridge), Design Museum, [Brunel Museum](http://www.brunel-museum.org.uk/), Surrey Docks Farm, Statue of Peter the Great, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, [Cutty Sark](http://www.rmg.co.uk/cuttysark), [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), Royal Observatory, Winchester Palace, and Tower of London. Lunch in Bermondsey, or Rotherhithe. Bermondsey lunch stop: the Angel Pub. Rotherhithe lunch stops: the [Mayflower pub](http://www.mayflowerpub.co.uk/), or the Salt Quay Pub.

UWL.name: Upper Warlingham
UWL.adminWalksAll:
**[Upper Warlingham Circular via Selsdon Wood Nature Reserve](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/warlingham-circular/)**: Chalk downland, bluebell woods, open meadows, enclosed bridleways, isolated valleys, farmland, and nature reserves on the London/Surrey border. Sights: [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), [Selsdon Wood Nature Reserve](http://www.friendsofselsdonwood.co.uk), [Ledgers Wood](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/ledgers-wood), King's Wood, St Mary the Virgin, Farleigh, Sanderstead to Whyteleafe Countryside Area, South London Downs National Nature Reserve, and Hamsey Green Pond. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Great Farleigh Green: the [Harrow](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/theharrowwarlingham). 17 km. 4 hours.

**Upper Warlingham Circular via Riddlesdown**: A shorter variant of [Warlingham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/warlingham-circular/). Chalk downland, bluebell woods, open meadows, enclosed bridleways, and nature reserves on the London/Surrey border. Sights: [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), King's Wood, St Mary the Virgin, Farleigh, Sanderstead to Whyteleafe Countryside Area, South London Downs National Nature Reserve, and Hamsey Green Pond. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Great Farleigh Green: the [Harrow](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/theharrowwarlingham). 13 km. 3.5 hours.

**Upper Warlingham to Woldingham via Riddlesdown**: An alternative variant of [Warlingham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/warlingham-circular/). Chalk downland, bluebell woods, enclosed bridleways, woodland nature reserves, and Halliloo valley views. Sights: [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), [Ledgers Wood](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/ledgers-wood), King's Wood, St Mary the Virgin, Farleigh, Sanderstead to Whyteleafe Countryside Area, South London Downs National Nature Reserve, Hamsey Green Pond, Clock Tower, and Coal Tax post. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Great Farleigh Green, or Chelsham Common. Great Farleigh Green lunch stop: the [Harrow](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/theharrowwarlingham). Chelsham Common lunch stop: the [Bull Inn](https://www.thebullinnchelsham.co.uk). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**Upper Warlingham to Woldingham via Selsdon Wood Nature Reserve**: An alternative variant of [Warlingham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/warlingham-circular/). Chalk downland, bluebell woods, enclosed bridleways, isolated valleys, golf course descent, woodland nature reserves, and Halliloo valley views. Sights: [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), [Selsdon Wood Nature Reserve](http://www.friendsofselsdonwood.co.uk), [Ledgers Wood](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/ledgers-wood), King's Wood, St Mary the Virgin, Farleigh, Sanderstead to Whyteleafe Countryside Area, South London Downs National Nature Reserve, Hamsey Green Pond, Clock Tower, and Coal Tax post. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Great Farleigh Green, or Chelsham Common. Great Farleigh Green lunch stop: the [Harrow](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/theharrowwarlingham). Chelsham Common lunch stop: the [Bull Inn](https://www.thebullinnchelsham.co.uk). 17 km. 4 hours.

**Hurst Green to Upper Warlingham**: A shorter variant of [Hurst Green to Riddlesdown](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hurst-green-to-riddlesdown/). Heathland common, golf courses, farmland, North Downs escarpment, open downland, woodland strips, and views across the Caterham Valley. Sights: Limpsfield Common, River Eden, Titsey church, Tatsfield church, and Henley Wood Enclosure. Steep descent. Lunch in Tatsfield: the Old Ship. 19 km.
UWL.publicWalksCircular: **[Upper Warlingham Circular via Selsdon Wood Nature Reserve](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/warlingham-circular/)**: Chalk downland, bluebell woods, open meadows, enclosed bridleways, isolated valleys, farmland, and nature reserves on the London/Surrey border. Sights: [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), [Selsdon Wood Nature Reserve](http://www.friendsofselsdonwood.co.uk), [Ledgers Wood](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/ledgers-wood), King's Wood, St Mary the Virgin, Farleigh, Sanderstead to Whyteleafe Countryside Area, South London Downs National Nature Reserve, and Hamsey Green Pond. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Great Farleigh Green: the [Harrow](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/theharrowwarlingham). 17 km. 4 hours.

WAD.name: Wadhurst
WAD.adminWalksAll:
**[Wadhurst Circular via Bewl Water Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-via-bewl-water-circular/)**: Rambler favourite! Hidden valleys, ancient woods, quaint old farms, reservoir shoreline, hilltop village, and gentle but rolling Wealden landscape. Sights: Bewl Water Reservoir. Can be muddy. Lunch in Cousley Wood: the Old Vine. 17 km.

**Wadhurst Circular via Bewl Water**: High Weald AONB, rolling hills, woods, hedgerows, quiet farmland, and historic villages. Sights: Church of St Peter and St Paul, [Bewl Water](http://www.bewl.co.uk), and Church of Saint Dunstans. Can be muddy. Lunch in Mayfield, or Wadhurst. Mayfield lunch stops: the [Rose & Crown Inn](http://www.roseandcrownmayfield.co.uk/), or the [Middle House](http://www.themiddlehousemayfield.co.uk/). Wadhurst lunch stop: the [Greyhound](http://www.thegreyhoundwadhurst.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 19 km. 6 hours.

**Wadhurst Circular via Church of St Peter and St Paul**: High Weald AONB, rolling hills, woods, and quiet farmland. Sights: Church of St Peter and St Paul. Can be muddy. Lunch in Wadhurst: the [White Hart](http://www.whitehartwadhurst.co.uk/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 10 km. 3 hours.

**[Wadhurst to Tunbridge Wells via Tunbridge Wells](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-to-tunbridge-wells/)**: High Weald ridges, wooded valleys, open fields, landscaped parkland, and back streets and alleyways of a spa town. Sights: [Sussex Border Path](https://www.sussexborderpath.co.uk), [Tunbridge Wells](https://www.visittunbridgewells.com/explore), Eridge Old Park, Saxonbury Hill Tower, The Mount, St Alban, Frant, Nevill Crest, High Weald Landscape Trail, Tunbridge Wells Circular Walk, [Juliets](https://www.julietsandmore.com), and [Opera House](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/all-pubs/england/kent/opera-house-royal-tunbridge-wells). Can be boggy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Frant: the George Inn, or the [Abergavenny Arms](https://www.abergavennyarms.co.uk). 13 km. 3.5 hours.

**Wadhurst to Frant**: A shorter variant of [Wadhurst to Tunbridge Wells](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-to-tunbridge-wells/). High Weald ridges, wooded valleys, open fields, descent through woodland, and brief roadside stretch. Sights: [Sussex Border Path](https://www.sussexborderpath.co.uk), Eridge Old Park, Saxonbury Hill Tower, The Mount, St Alban, Frant, High Weald Landscape Trail, Tunbridge Wells Circular Walk, and [Brecknock Arms](https://www.thebrecknockarmspub.com). No pavement on final road. Lunch in Frant: the George Inn, or the [Abergavenny Arms](https://www.abergavennyarms.co.uk). 11 km. 3 hours.

**Wadhurst to Stonegate**: High Weald AONB, rolling hills, woods, quiet farmland, and historic villages. Sights: Church of St Peter and St Paul and Church of Saint Dunstans. Can be muddy. Lunch in Mayfield: the [Rose & Crown Inn](http://www.roseandcrownmayfield.co.uk/). An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km.

**Wadhurst to Tunbridge Wells via Dunorlan Park**: A longer variant of [Wadhurst to Tunbridge Wells](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-to-tunbridge-wells/). High Weald ridges, wooded valleys, parkland, recreation grounds, suburban roads, and Victorian park gardens. Sights: [Sussex Border Path](https://www.sussexborderpath.co.uk), Eridge Old Park, Saxonbury Hill Tower, The Mount, St Alban, Frant, Nevill Crest, High Weald Landscape Trail, Tunbridge Wells Circular Walk, [Dunorlan Park](https://tunbridgewells.gov.uk/community-and-leisure/parks-and-play-areas/dunorlan-park), [Calverley Grounds](https://tunbridgewells.gov.uk/community-and-leisure/parks-and-play-areas/calverley-grounds), and Calverley Park. Can be boggy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Frant: the George Inn, or the [Abergavenny Arms](https://www.abergavennyarms.co.uk). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Wadhurst to Robertsbridge**: High Weald AONB, rolling hills, woods, Bewl Water lakeside, Sussex Border Path, and quiet farmland. Sights: [Bewl Water](http://www.bewl.co.uk) and Church of St Peter and St Paul. Can be muddy. Lunch in Cousley Wood, or Bewl Water. Cousley Wood lunch stop: the [Old Vine](http://www.theoldvineinn.co.uk/). Bewl Water lunch stop: the [Look Out restaurant](http://www.bewl.co.uk). A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 34 km. 7 hours.

**[Etchingham to Wadhurst via Ticehurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/etchingham-to-wadhurst-via-ticehurst/)**: Hilly Wealden pasture and woodland, panoramic valley views, converted oast houses, cobnut orchard, back lanes with wildflower verges, and reservoir shoreline. Sights: Bewl Water, Ticehurst village, and Wadhurst village. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Ticehurst, or Three Leg Cross. Ticehurst lunch stop: the Bell. Three Leg Cross lunch stop: the Bull Inn. 17 km.

**Robertsbridge to Wadhurst**: A variant of [Etchingham to Wadhurst via Ticehurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/etchingham-to-wadhurst-via-ticehurst/). Hilly Wealden pasture and woodland, panoramic valley views, converted oast houses, cobnut orchard, back lanes with wildflower verges, and reservoir shoreline. Sights: Bewl Water, Ticehurst village, and Wadhurst village. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Ticehurst, or Three Leg Cross. Ticehurst lunch stop: the Bell. Three Leg Cross lunch stop: the Bull Inn. 21 km.

**Stonegate to Wadhurst**: Rolling hills, tranquil valleys, woodland, pastureland, and remote wilderness sections. Sights: [Bateman's](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk) and Church of St. Bartholomew. Nettles and brambles. Lunch in Burwash Weald: the [Wheel Inn](http://www.thewheelburwashweald.co.uk/). An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 32 km.
WAD.publicWalksS2S: **[Wadhurst to Tunbridge Wells via Tunbridge Wells](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-to-tunbridge-wells/)**: High Weald ridges, wooded valleys, open fields, landscaped parkland, and back streets and alleyways of a spa town. Sights: [Sussex Border Path](https://www.sussexborderpath.co.uk), [Tunbridge Wells](https://www.visittunbridgewells.com/explore), Eridge Old Park, Saxonbury Hill Tower, The Mount, St Alban, Frant, Nevill Crest, High Weald Landscape Trail, Tunbridge Wells Circular Walk, [Juliets](https://www.julietsandmore.com), and [Opera House](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/all-pubs/england/kent/opera-house-royal-tunbridge-wells). Can be boggy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Frant: the George Inn, or the [Abergavenny Arms](https://www.abergavennyarms.co.uk). 13 km. 3.5 hours.

Hidden valleys, ancient woods, quaint old farms, reservoir shoreline, hilltop village, and gentle but rolling Wealden landscape. Sights: Bewl Water Reservoir. Can be muddy. **Wadhurst Circular via Bewl Water**: High Weald AONB, rolling hills, woods, hedgerows, quiet farmland, and historic villages. Mayfield lunch stops: the [Rose & Crown Inn](http://www.roseandcrownmayfield.co.uk/), or the [Middle House](http://www.themiddlehousemayfield.co.uk/). Wadhurst lunch stop: the [Greyhound](http://www.thegreyhoundwadhurst.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 19 km. 6 hours.

Three Leg Cross lunch stop: the Bull Inn. 17 km.

WAN.name: Wanborough
WAN.adminWalksAll:
**Wanborough to Godalming via Compton**: Open fields, North Downs ridge, vineyard, golf course, mixed woodland, riverside path along the River Wey, and historic market town. Sights: Wanborough Church, Wanborough Manor House, Greyfriars Vineyard, [Watts Gallery](http://www.wattsgallery.org.uk/en-gb/visit/), Watts Chapel, Phillips Memorial Cloister, and Church of St Peter and St Paul. Can be muddy. Lunch in Compton: the [Withies Inn](http://www.thewithiesinn.com/), or the [Watts Gallery Tea Shop](http://www.wattsgallery.org.uk/en-gb/visit/tea-shop/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 12 km. 150 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920224124)**

**Wanborough to Ash Vale**: A shorter variant of [Ash Vale Circular via North Downs Way](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-circular-via-north-downs-way/). North Downs Way, heathland commons, open fields, mixed woodland, and the Ash Ranges. Sights: Puttenham Common, St John the Baptist, Broadstreet Common, and Ash/Pirbright Ranges. MOD firing range closures. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in Puttenham: the Good Intent. 16 km.

**Wanborough to Guildford**: Open fields, North Downs ridge, vineyard, golf course, mixed woodland, and River Wey towpath all the way to Guildford. Sights: Wanborough Church, Wanborough Manor House, Greyfriars Vineyard, [Watts Gallery](http://www.wattsgallery.org.uk/en-gb/visit/), and Watts Chapel. Can be muddy. Lunch in Compton: the [Withies Inn](http://www.thewithiesinn.com/), or the [Watts Gallery Tea Shop](http://www.wattsgallery.org.uk/en-gb/visit/tea-shop/). A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 19 km.

**Ash Vale to Wanborough via Ash Ranges**: An alternative variant of [Ash Vale to Worplesdon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-to-worplesdon/). Basingstoke Canal towpath, MOD firing range heathland, pine woodland, and farmland. Sights: Basingstoke Canal and Ash Ranges. MOD closures apply. 11 km.

**Ash Vale to Wanborough via North Downs Way & Broadstreet Common**: A shorter variant of [Ash Vale Circular via North Downs Way](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-circular-via-north-downs-way/). Canal towpath, disused railway line, heathland, North Downs Way, open fields, and mixed woodland. Sights: Basingstoke Canal, Puttenham Common, St John the Baptist, and Broadstreet Common. Can be muddy. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in Puttenham: the Good Intent. 20 km.
WAN.publicWalksS2S: **Wanborough to Godalming via Compton**: Open fields, North Downs ridge, vineyard, golf course, mixed woodland, riverside path along the River Wey, and historic market town. Sights: Wanborough Church, Wanborough Manor House, Greyfriars Vineyard, [Watts Gallery](http://www.wattsgallery.org.uk/en-gb/visit/), Watts Chapel, Phillips Memorial Cloister, and Church of St Peter and St Paul. Can be muddy. Lunch in Compton: the [Withies Inn](http://www.thewithiesinn.com/), or the [Watts Gallery Tea Shop](http://www.wattsgallery.org.uk/en-gb/visit/tea-shop/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 12 km. 150 m uphill. 3.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920224124)**

WAS.name: Watton-at-Stone
WAS.adminWalksAll:
**[Watton-at-Stone Circular via Hertfordshire puddingstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/watton-at-stone-circular/)**: Rolling Hertfordshire hills, farmland, woods, hedgerows, and riverside path along the River Beane. Sights: [Hertfordshire puddingstone](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hertfordshire_puddingstone). Lunch in Benington, Dane End, or Tonwell. Benington lunch stop: the [Lordship Arms](http://www.lordshiparms.com/). Dane End lunch stop: the [Boot](http://theboot.eu.org/). Tonwell lunch stop: the [Robin Hood](https://www.facebook.com/RobinHoodTonwell/). 20 km.

**Watton-at-Stone Circular**: A shorter variant of [Watton-at-Stone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/watton-at-stone-circular/). Rolling Hertfordshire hills, farmland, woods, hedgerows, and with a short section along a twisting road. Road section included. Lunch in Benington, Dane End, or Tonwell. Benington lunch stop: the [Lordship Arms](http://www.lordshiparms.com/). Dane End lunch stop: the [Boot](http://theboot.eu.org/). Tonwell lunch stop: the [Robin Hood](https://www.facebook.com/RobinHoodTonwell/). 16 km.
WAS.publicWalksCircular: **[Watton-at-Stone Circular via Hertfordshire puddingstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/watton-at-stone-circular/)**: Rolling Hertfordshire hills, farmland, woods, hedgerows, and riverside path along the River Beane. Sights: [Hertfordshire puddingstone](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hertfordshire_puddingstone). Lunch in Benington, Dane End, or Tonwell. Benington lunch stop: the [Lordship Arms](http://www.lordshiparms.com/). Dane End lunch stop: the [Boot](http://theboot.eu.org/). Tonwell lunch stop: the [Robin Hood](https://www.facebook.com/RobinHoodTonwell/). 20 km.

WBC.name: Waterbeach
WBC.adminWalksAll: **Waterbeach to Ely**: Rambler favourite! Quaint village. From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 3 August 2024.* 19 km. 20 m uphill. 5 hours.
