WBY.name: West Byfleet
WBY.adminWalksAll:
**[West Byfleet to Hampton Court](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/west-byfleet-to-hampton-court/)**: Flat canal towpath, riverside Thames Path, tarmaced and paved surfaces, waterside scenery, locks, islands, and houseboats. Sights: [Coxes Lock](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coxes_Lock), [D'Oyly Carte Island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/D%27Oyly_Carte_Island), [Hampton Court Palace](https://www.hrp.org.uk/hampton-court-palace), New Haw Lock, and Desborough Island. Lunch in West Byfleet, New Haw Lock, Weybridge, D'Oyly Carte Island, Walton, Between Walton and West Molesey, West Molesey, or Hampton Court. West Byfleet lunch stop: the Station. New Haw Lock lunch stop: the White Hart. Weybridge lunch stop: the Old Crown. D'Oyly Carte Island lunch stop: the [D'Oyle's cafe](https://www.doylycarteisland.com/doylys). Walton lunch stops: the Anglers, or the Swan. Between Walton and West Molesey lunch stop: the Weir. West Molesey lunch stops: the [Miss Polly cafe](https://www.misspollycafe.co.uk/miss-polly-riverside), or the [Eight on the River](https://www.eightontheriver.com/). Hampton Court lunch stops: the Mitre, or the Cardinal Wolsey. 17 km.

**West Byfleet to Kingston**: A longer variant of [West Byfleet to Hampton Court](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/west-byfleet-to-hampton-court/). Flat canal towpath, Thames riverside path, deer park, and town centre. Sights: [Coxes Lock](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coxes_Lock), [D'Oyly Carte Island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/D%27Oyly_Carte_Island), [Hampton Court Palace](https://www.hrp.org.uk/hampton-court-palace), Hampton Court Park, and Desborough Island. Lunch in West Byfleet, New Haw Lock, Weybridge, D'Oyly Carte Island, Walton, Between Walton and West Molesey, or West Molesey. West Byfleet lunch stop: the Station. New Haw Lock lunch stop: the White Hart. Weybridge lunch stop: the Old Crown. D'Oyly Carte Island lunch stop: the [D'Oyle's cafe](https://www.doylycarteisland.com/doylys). Walton lunch stops: the Anglers, or the Swan. Between Walton and West Molesey lunch stop: the Weir. West Molesey lunch stops: the [Miss Polly cafe](https://www.misspollycafe.co.uk/miss-polly-riverside), or the [Eight on the River](https://www.eightontheriver.com/). 21 km.

**[Horsley to West Byfleet](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/horsley-to-west-byfleet/)**: Woods, fields, open sandy heathland, commons, canal towpath, and suburban road. Sights: Chatley Semaphore Tower, [RHS Garden Wisley](http://www.rhs.org.uk/Gardens/Wisley), Ockham Mill, River Wey and Wey Navigation, and Hatchford Park. Can be muddy. Lunch in Martyr's Green, Ockham Common car park, or Pyrford Lock. Martyr's Green lunch stop: the [Black Swan](http://www.blackswanockham.com/). Ockham Common car park lunch stop: the Ockham Bites. Pyrford Lock lunch stop: the [Anchor](http://anchorpyrford.co.uk/). 15 km.

WCH.name: Whitchurch
WCH.adminWalksAll:
**Whitchurch Circular via St Nicholas, Freefolk**: An alternative variant of [Overton to Whitchurch](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/overton-to-whitchurch/). Upper Test valley, water meadows, mill-streams, parkland, riverside paths, and quiet lanes. Sights: [River Test](https://www.fishingbreaks.co.uk/chalkstream/test.htm), [Whitchurch Silk Mill](https://whitchurchsilkmill.org.uk), [All Hallows Church](https://www.hantswhit.church/all-hallows), [St Nicholas, Freefolk](https://www.visitchurches.org.uk/visit/church-listing/st-nicholas-freefolk.html), Bere Mill, Laverstoke Mill, Laverstoke House, Laverstoke Park, St Mary, Tufton, Fulling Mill, and Whitchurch Town Hall. Can be muddy. Lunch in Freefolk, or Whitchurch. Freefolk lunch stop: the [Watership Down Inn](https://www.watershipdowninn.com). Whitchurch lunch stops: the [White Hart Hotel](https://www.whiteharthotelwhitchurch.co.uk), or the [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarmswhitchurch.com). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**Whitchurch Circular via River Test**: A shorter variant of [Overton to Whitchurch](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/overton-to-whitchurch/). Upper Test valley, water meadows, mill-streams, riverside paths, and quiet lanes. Sights: [River Test](https://www.fishingbreaks.co.uk/chalkstream/test.htm), [Whitchurch Silk Mill](https://whitchurchsilkmill.org.uk), [All Hallows Church](https://www.hantswhit.church/all-hallows), Bere Mill, Fulling Mill, and Whitchurch Town Hall. Can be muddy. Lunch in Whitchurch: the [White Hart Hotel](https://www.whiteharthotelwhitchurch.co.uk), or the [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarmswhitchurch.com). 11 km. 2.5 hours.

**Whitchurch to Andover**: Open Hampshire countryside, dismantled railway embankment, riverside paths along River Test, thatched-cottage village, concreted forest tracks through Harewood Forest, farmland, and suburban approach into Andover. Sights: All Hallows Church, Whitchurch Silk Mill, St Mary's Church Tufton, Longparish Upper Mill, Church of St Nicholas, Britwell Priors, Harewood Forest, St Mary's Church Andover, Lower Guildhall, and Andover Museum. Can be muddy. Lunch in Longparish: the Cricketers Inn, or the Plough Inn. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 19 km. 5 hours.

**[Overton to Whitchurch via Laverstoke House](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/overton-to-whitchurch/)**: Upper Test valley, water meadows, mill-streams, ancient drove road, parkland, riverside paths, and quiet lanes. Sights: [River Test](https://www.fishingbreaks.co.uk/chalkstream/test.htm), [Whitchurch Silk Mill](https://whitchurchsilkmill.org.uk), [All Hallows Church](https://www.hantswhit.church/all-hallows), [St Nicholas, Freefolk](https://www.visitchurches.org.uk/visit/church-listing/st-nicholas-freefolk.html), Bere Mill, Laverstoke Mill, Laverstoke House, Laverstoke Park, Overton Mill, Southington Mill, St Mary, Overton, Harroway, Town Mill, Fulling Mill, St Mary, Tufton, Whitchurch Town Hall, Voters Cottage, Berehill House, and Manor Cottages. Can be muddy. Lunch in Freefolk, or Whitchurch. Freefolk lunch stop: the [Watership Down Inn](https://www.watershipdowninn.com). Whitchurch lunch stops: the [White Hart Hotel](https://www.whiteharthotelwhitchurch.co.uk), or the [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarmswhitchurch.com). 17 km. 4 hours.

**Overton to Whitchurch via River Test**: A shorter variant of [Overton to Whitchurch](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/overton-to-whitchurch/). Upper Test valley, water meadows, mill-streams, ancient drove road, parkland, and riverside paths. Sights: [River Test](https://www.fishingbreaks.co.uk/chalkstream/test.htm), [Whitchurch Silk Mill](https://whitchurchsilkmill.org.uk), [All Hallows Church](https://www.hantswhit.church/all-hallows), [St Nicholas, Freefolk](https://www.visitchurches.org.uk/visit/church-listing/st-nicholas-freefolk.html), Bere Mill, Laverstoke Mill, Fulling Mill, and Whitchurch Town Hall. Can be muddy. Lunch in Freefolk, or Whitchurch. Freefolk lunch stop: the [Watership Down Inn](https://www.watershipdowninn.com). Whitchurch lunch stops: the [White Hart Hotel](https://www.whiteharthotelwhitchurch.co.uk), or the [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarmswhitchurch.com). 14 km.

WCM.name: Wickham Market
WCM.adminWalksAll: **[Wickham Market to Felixstowe](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wickham-market-to-felixstowe/)**: Farmland, Suffolk heathland (Sandlings), river bank, grassy sea wall, shingle beach, isolated coastline, and urban Felixstowe approaches. Sights: Sandlings Walk, Suffolk Coast Path, Havergate Island RSPB Reserve, Shingle Street, Bawdsey Manor, Orford Ness, Martello Towers, Hollesley Marshes Nature Reserve, and Butley Ferry. Long shingle sections. Lunch in Tunstall, or Chillesford. Tunstall lunch stop: the [Green Man](http://www.greenmantunstall.co.uk/). Chillesford lunch stop: the [Froize Freehouse](http://froize.co.uk/). 35 km.
WCM.publicWalksS2S: **[Wickham Market to Felixstowe](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wickham-market-to-felixstowe/)**: Farmland, Suffolk heathland (Sandlings), river bank, grassy sea wall, shingle beach, isolated coastline, and urban Felixstowe approaches. Sights: Sandlings Walk, Suffolk Coast Path, Havergate Island RSPB Reserve, Shingle Street, Bawdsey Manor, Orford Ness, Martello Towers, Hollesley Marshes Nature Reserve, and Butley Ferry. Long shingle sections. Lunch in Tunstall, or Chillesford. Tunstall lunch stop: the [Green Man](http://www.greenmantunstall.co.uk/). Chillesford lunch stop: the [Froize Freehouse](http://froize.co.uk/). 35 km.

WDB.name: Woodbridge
WDB.adminWalksAll: **[Melton to Woodbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/melton-to-woodbridge-via-sutton-hoo/)**: Lowland heathland, river marshes, estuary walls, reedbeds, poplar rows, quiet lanes, heathland golf course, and compact riverside town. Sights: [Sutton Hoo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sutton_Hoo), [Woodbridge Tide Mill](https://www.riverdeben.org/), Bromeswell Green Nature Reserve, and Tranmer House. Café in ticketed area. Lunch in Bromeswell, or Lower Ufford. Bromeswell lunch stop: the [Unruly Pig Pub & Kitchen](https://theunrulypig.co.uk/). Lower Ufford lunch stop: the [Ufford White Lion](https://uffordwhitelion.co.uk/). 20 km. 4.5 hours.
WDB.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Melton to Woodbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/melton-to-woodbridge-via-sutton-hoo/)**: Lowland heathland, river marshes, estuary walls, reedbeds, poplar rows, quiet lanes, heathland golf course, and compact riverside town. Sights: [Sutton Hoo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sutton_Hoo), [Woodbridge Tide Mill](https://www.riverdeben.org/), Bromeswell Green Nature Reserve, and Tranmer House. Café in ticketed area. Lunch in Bromeswell, or Lower Ufford. Bromeswell lunch stop: the [Unruly Pig Pub & Kitchen](https://theunrulypig.co.uk/). Lower Ufford lunch stop: the [Ufford White Lion](https://uffordwhitelion.co.uk/). 20 km. 4.5 hours.

WDE.name: Wood End
WDE.adminWalksAll: **[Wood End Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-887)**: Warwickshire countryside, river crossing, fields, kissing gates, village roads, and railway crossing. Sights: [St. Mary Magdalene](https://www.tanworthchurch.co.uk), [Umberslade Estate](https://www.umberslade-estate.com), and River Alne. No shops en route. Lunch in Wood End, Tanworth-in-Arden, Umberslade, or Carpenters Hill Farm. Wood End lunch stop: the [Warwickshire Lad](https://www.thewarwickshirelad.co.uk). Tanworth-in-Arden lunch stop: the [Bell Inn](https://www.thebellattanworthinarden.co.uk). Umberslade lunch stops: the [Umberslade's Pizzeria](https://www.umberslade-estate.com), or the [Espresso Farm](https://www.umberslade-estate.com). Carpenters Hill Farm lunch stop: the [Straight From the Cow](https://www.straightfromthecow.co.uk). 4 km. 1 hours.
WDE.publicWalksCircular: **[Wood End Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-887)**: Warwickshire countryside, river crossing, fields, kissing gates, village roads, and railway crossing. Sights: [St. Mary Magdalene](https://www.tanworthchurch.co.uk), [Umberslade Estate](https://www.umberslade-estate.com), and River Alne. No shops en route. Lunch in Wood End, Tanworth-in-Arden, Umberslade, or Carpenters Hill Farm. Wood End lunch stop: the [Warwickshire Lad](https://www.thewarwickshirelad.co.uk). Tanworth-in-Arden lunch stop: the [Bell Inn](https://www.thebellattanworthinarden.co.uk). Umberslade lunch stops: the [Umberslade's Pizzeria](https://www.umberslade-estate.com), or the [Espresso Farm](https://www.umberslade-estate.com). Carpenters Hill Farm lunch stop: the [Straight From the Cow](https://www.straightfromthecow.co.uk). 4 km. 1 hours.

WDO.name: Waddon
WDO.adminWalksAll:
**Waddon to Morden**: An alternative variant of [Mitcham Junction to Colliers Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mitcham-junction-to-colliers-wood/). Flat urban riverside parks, Wandle Trail, small ornamental ponds, residential streets, and south London green corridors. Sights: [Wandle Trail](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk/projects/wandle-trail), [Sutton Ecology Centre](https://www.sutton.gov.uk/w/biodiversity-and-wildlife-sutton-ecology-centre), [Honeywood Museum](https://friendsofhoneywood.co.uk/index.html), [Carshalton Water Tower](https://carshaltonwatertower.co.uk/index.html), [Morden Hall Park](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/morden-hall-park), Waddon Ponds, Carew Manor, Beddington Park, Watermeads Nature Reserve, Ravensbury Park, and Wilderness Island. Lunch in Carshalton: the [Greyhound Hotel](https://www.thegreyhoundhotel.com), the [Hope](https://hopecarshalton.co.uk), or the [Sun](https://www.thesuncarshalton.com/food). 12 km.

**Waddon to Colliers Wood**: An alternative variant of [Mitcham Junction to Colliers Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mitcham-junction-to-colliers-wood/). Flat urban riverside parks, Wandle Trail, small ornamental ponds, residential streets, and south London green corridors. Sights: [Wandle Trail](https://wandlevalleypark.co.uk/projects/wandle-trail), [Sutton Ecology Centre](https://www.sutton.gov.uk/w/biodiversity-and-wildlife-sutton-ecology-centre), [Honeywood Museum](https://friendsofhoneywood.co.uk/index.html), [Carshalton Water Tower](https://carshaltonwatertower.co.uk/index.html), [Morden Hall Park](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/morden-hall-park), [Deen City Farm](https://www.deencityfarm.co.uk), [Merton Abbey Mills](https://www.mertonabbeymills.org.uk), Waddon Ponds, Carew Manor, Beddington Park, Watermeads Nature Reserve, Ravensbury Park, Wilderness Island, Morden Hall, and Merton Priory. Lunch in Carshalton: the [Greyhound Hotel](https://www.thegreyhoundhotel.com), the [Hope](https://hopecarshalton.co.uk), or the [Sun](https://www.thesuncarshalton.com/food). 14 km. 3 hours.

WDT.name: West Drayton
WDT.adminWalksAll: **[West Drayton to Cookham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/west-drayton-to-cookham/)**: Lowland heaths, mature woodland, canal towpath, farmland, and historic riverside village. Sights: [Langley Park County Park](http://www.buckscc.gov.uk/leisure-and-culture/country-parks/langley-park/), Black Park County Park, [Burnham Beeches](http://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/burnham-beeches-and-stoke-common/Pages/default.aspx), Lord Boston's Victorian Folly, Grand Union Canal, and River Thames. Can be muddy. Lunch in Fulmer, Farnham Common, Burnham Beeches, Littleworth Common, or Cookham. Fulmer lunch stop: the [Black Horse](http://www.theblackhorsefulmer.co.uk/). Farnham Common lunch stop: the [Forresters](http://www.theforesterspub.com/). Burnham Beeches lunch stop: the Beeches Cafe. Littleworth Common lunch stop: the Blackwood Arms. Cookham lunch stops: the Infusions, the Kings Arms, or the Bel and the Dragon. 27 km.
WDT.publicWalksS2S: **[West Drayton to Cookham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/west-drayton-to-cookham/)**: Lowland heaths, mature woodland, canal towpath, farmland, and historic riverside village. Sights: [Langley Park County Park](http://www.buckscc.gov.uk/leisure-and-culture/country-parks/langley-park/), Black Park County Park, [Burnham Beeches](http://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/burnham-beeches-and-stoke-common/Pages/default.aspx), Lord Boston's Victorian Folly, Grand Union Canal, and River Thames. Can be muddy. Lunch in Fulmer, Farnham Common, Burnham Beeches, Littleworth Common, or Cookham. Fulmer lunch stop: the [Black Horse](http://www.theblackhorsefulmer.co.uk/). Farnham Common lunch stop: the [Forresters](http://www.theforesterspub.com/). Burnham Beeches lunch stop: the Beeches Cafe. Littleworth Common lunch stop: the Blackwood Arms. Cookham lunch stops: the Infusions, the Kings Arms, or the Bel and the Dragon. 27 km.

WEL.name: Wellingborough
WEL.adminWalksAll:
**[Wellingborough Circular via Summer Leys Nature Reserve](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wellingborough-circular/)**: River Nene towpath, gravel quarry-edge paths, fishing lakes, grassy fields, nature reserve, four villages, and Swanspool Brook parkland. Sights: Summer Leys Nature Reserve, All Saints' Church Earls Barton, [White Mills Marina](http://www.whitemillsmarina.co.uk/), Wellingborough Museum, Dulley's Baths, and St Nicholas Church Great Doddington. Can be muddy. Lunch in Grendon Lakes, White Mills Marina, or Earls Barton. Grendon Lakes lunch stop: the Grendon Lakes Clubhouse. White Mills Marina lunch stop: the [Boathouse](http://www.whitemillsmarina.co.uk/). Earls Barton lunch stops: the Whites of Earls Barton, the Jeyes of Earls Barton, the [Old Swan](https://www.theoldswan.pub/), or the Stag's Head. 28 km.

**Wellingborough Circular via St Mary Magdalene Church Ecton**: A longer variant of [Wellingborough Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wellingborough-circular/). River Nene towpath, gravel quarry-edge paths, fishing lakes, grassy fields, nature reserve, four villages including Ecton, and Swanspool Brook parkland. Sights: Summer Leys Nature Reserve, St Mary Magdalene Church Ecton, All Saints' Church Earls Barton, [White Mills Marina](http://www.whitemillsmarina.co.uk/), Wellingborough Museum, Dulley's Baths, St Nicholas Church Great Doddington, and Ecton Hall. Can be muddy. Lunch in Grendon Lakes, White Mills Marina, Ecton, or Earls Barton. Grendon Lakes lunch stop: the Grendon Lakes Clubhouse. White Mills Marina lunch stop: the [Boathouse](http://www.whitemillsmarina.co.uk/). Ecton lunch stop: the [World's End](http://www.theworldsend.org/). Earls Barton lunch stops: the Jeyes of Earls Barton, the [Old Swan](https://www.theoldswan.pub/), or the Stag's Head. 31 km.

**[Wellingborough to Northampton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wellingborough-to-northampton/)**: Riverside paths along the Nene, meadows, quarry lakes, arable fields, villages, urban riverside, nature reserve, and raised flood embankments. Sights: River Nene, Cogenhoe Mill, St Peter's Church, Cogenhoe, St Mary's, Little Houghton, Northampton Washlands, Nene Whitewater Centre, Becket's Park, St Peter's Church, Marefair, National Lift Tower, Northampton Castle, Hazelrigg House, Grand Union Canal Northampton Arm, and Carlsberg Brewery. Prone to flooding. Lunch in Cogenhoe, or Little Houghton. Cogenhoe lunch stop: the Royal Oak. Little Houghton lunch stop: the Four Pears Inn. 24 km.
WEL.publicWalksS2S: **[Wellingborough to Northampton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wellingborough-to-northampton/)**: Riverside paths along the Nene, meadows, quarry lakes, arable fields, villages, urban riverside, nature reserve, and raised flood embankments. Sights: River Nene, Cogenhoe Mill, St Peter's Church, Cogenhoe, St Mary's, Little Houghton, Northampton Washlands, Nene Whitewater Centre, Becket's Park, St Peter's Church, Marefair, National Lift Tower, Northampton Castle, Hazelrigg House, Grand Union Canal Northampton Arm, and Carlsberg Brewery. Prone to flooding. Lunch in Cogenhoe, or Little Houghton. Cogenhoe lunch stop: the Royal Oak. Little Houghton lunch stop: the Four Pears Inn. 24 km.
WEL.publicWalksCircular: **[Wellingborough Circular via Summer Leys Nature Reserve](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wellingborough-circular/)**: River Nene towpath, gravel quarry-edge paths, fishing lakes, grassy fields, nature reserve, four villages, and Swanspool Brook parkland. Sights: Summer Leys Nature Reserve, All Saints' Church Earls Barton, [White Mills Marina](http://www.whitemillsmarina.co.uk/), Wellingborough Museum, Dulley's Baths, and St Nicholas Church Great Doddington. Can be muddy. Lunch in Grendon Lakes, White Mills Marina, or Earls Barton. Grendon Lakes lunch stop: the Grendon Lakes Clubhouse. White Mills Marina lunch stop: the [Boathouse](http://www.whitemillsmarina.co.uk/). Earls Barton lunch stops: the Whites of Earls Barton, the Jeyes of Earls Barton, the [Old Swan](https://www.theoldswan.pub/), or the Stag's Head. 28 km.

WEY.name: Weymouth
WEY.adminWalksAll: **Weymouth Circular**: A variant of [Weymouth via Isle of Portland Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/weymouth-via-isle-of-portland-circular/). Disused railway path, pebble causeway, steep hill climb, clifftop ridge with panoramic views, and South West Coast Path back to harbour. Sights: [Rodwell Trail](http://www.weymouth.gov.uk/home.asp?sv=169), Chesil Beach, The Verne, Verne Citadel, and Weymouth Harbour. Lunch in The Verne, Portland, or Portland. The Verne, Portland lunch stop: the [Jailhouse Cafe](http://jailhousecafe.co.uk/). Portland lunch stop: the Heights Hotel. 15 km.
WEY.publicWalksCircular: **Weymouth Circular**: A variant of [Weymouth via Isle of Portland Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/weymouth-via-isle-of-portland-circular/). Disused railway path, pebble causeway, steep hill climb, clifftop ridge with panoramic views, and South West Coast Path back to harbour. Sights: [Rodwell Trail](http://www.weymouth.gov.uk/home.asp?sv=169), Chesil Beach, The Verne, Verne Citadel, and Weymouth Harbour. Lunch in The Verne, Portland, or Portland. The Verne, Portland lunch stop: the [Jailhouse Cafe](http://jailhousecafe.co.uk/). Portland lunch stop: the Heights Hotel. 15 km.

WFJ.name: Watford Junction
WFJ.adminWalksAll:
**[Watford Junction to St Albans Abbey](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/)**: Way-marked trail through the Ver and Colne Valleys and linking all seven Abbey Line stations — can be done end-to-end or as individual sections with a train hop back. Sights: Sopwell Nunnery and St Albans Abbey. Sections can become waterlogged after heavy rain, sometimes without obvious alternative routes. 14 km.

**Watford North to Watford Junction**: An alternative variant of [Abbey Trail](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/). 2 km.
WFJ.publicWalksS2S: **[Watford Junction to St Albans Abbey](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/)**: Way-marked trail through the Ver and Colne Valleys and linking all seven Abbey Line stations — can be done end-to-end or as individual sections with a train hop back. Sights: Sopwell Nunnery and St Albans Abbey. Sections can become waterlogged after heavy rain, sometimes without obvious alternative routes. 14 km.

WFN.name: Watford North
WFN.adminWalksAll:
**Watford North to Watford Junction**: An alternative variant of [Abbey Trail](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/). 2 km.

**Garston to Watford North**: An alternative variant of [Abbey Trail](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/). 4 km.
WFN.publicWalksS2S: **Watford North to Watford Junction**: An alternative variant of [Abbey Trail](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/). 2 km.
WFN.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Garston to Watford North**: An alternative variant of [Abbey Trail](https://abbeylinecommunityrail.org.uk/days-out/abbey-trail/). 4 km.

WGC.name: Welwyn Garden City
WGC.adminWalksAll:
**[Welwyn Garden City Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/welwyn-circular/)**: Rolling Hertfordshire countryside, landscaped parkland, arable fields, picturesque village, and Gothic church ruin. Sights: [Shaw's Corner](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shaw%27s_Corner), Old St. Lawrence's Church, Brocket Hall, and Lamer House. Lunch in Ayot St. Lawrence, or Wheathampstead. Ayot St. Lawrence lunch stop: the [Brocket Arms](http://www.brocketarms.com/). Wheathampstead lunch stops: the Bull, or the [Swan](http://theswanwheathampstead.co.uk/). 18 km.

**[Knebworth to Welwyn Garden City via Brocket Hall](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knebworth-to-welwyn-garden-city/)**: Gently undulating woodland, open fields, deer park, golf course, nature reserve, and formal garden city streets. Sights: Knebworth House, St Mary and Thomas of Canterbury Church, Ayot St Lawrence Palladian Church, Old St Lawrence Church, Brocket Hall, Shaw's Corner, and Sherrardspark Wood. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Ayot St Lawrence: the Brocket Arms. 21 km.

**Knebworth to Welwyn Garden City via Ayot Greenway**: A shorter variant of [Knebworth to Welwyn Garden City](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knebworth-to-welwyn-garden-city/). Gently undulating woodland, open fields, deer park, and disused railway trackbed through tree-lined cutting. Sights: Knebworth House, St Mary and Thomas of Canterbury Church, Ayot St Lawrence Palladian Church, Old St Lawrence Church, Shaw's Corner, and Ayot Greenway. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Ayot St Lawrence: the Brocket Arms. 19 km.
WGC.publicWalksCircular: **[Welwyn Garden City Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/welwyn-circular/)**: Rolling Hertfordshire countryside, landscaped parkland, arable fields, picturesque village, and Gothic church ruin. Sights: [Shaw's Corner](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shaw%27s_Corner), Old St. Lawrence's Church, Brocket Hall, and Lamer House. Lunch in Ayot St. Lawrence, or Wheathampstead. Ayot St. Lawrence lunch stop: the [Brocket Arms](http://www.brocketarms.com/). Wheathampstead lunch stops: the Bull, or the [Swan](http://theswanwheathampstead.co.uk/). 18 km.
WGC.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Knebworth to Welwyn Garden City via Brocket Hall](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knebworth-to-welwyn-garden-city/)**: Gently undulating woodland, open fields, deer park, golf course, nature reserve, and formal garden city streets. Sights: Knebworth House, St Mary and Thomas of Canterbury Church, Ayot St Lawrence Palladian Church, Old St Lawrence Church, Brocket Hall, Shaw's Corner, and Sherrardspark Wood. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Ayot St Lawrence: the Brocket Arms. 21 km.

WHE.name: Whalley
WHE.adminWalksAll: **[Whalley to Clitheroe](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pendle-hill/)**: Abbey precinct, town centre, riverside path, golf course, open pasture, old drove road, moorland ridge, plateau summit, steep scarp, lush valley, meandering river, and nature reserve. Sights: Whalley Abbey Precinct, Pendle Hill, Nick O' Pendle, Ogden Clough, Downham village, River Ribble, and St. Leonard's Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Sabden, or Downham. Sabden lunch stop: the [Wellsprings](https://thewellsprings.co.uk/). Downham lunch stops: the Downham Ice Cream Shop, or the [Assheton Arms](https://www.jamesplaces.com/assheton-arms/). 23 km. 5.5 hours.
WHE.publicWalksS2S: **[Whalley to Clitheroe](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pendle-hill/)**: Abbey precinct, town centre, riverside path, golf course, open pasture, old drove road, moorland ridge, plateau summit, steep scarp, lush valley, meandering river, and nature reserve. Sights: Whalley Abbey Precinct, Pendle Hill, Nick O' Pendle, Ogden Clough, Downham village, River Ribble, and St. Leonard's Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Sabden, or Downham. Sabden lunch stop: the [Wellsprings](https://thewellsprings.co.uk/). Downham lunch stops: the Downham Ice Cream Shop, or the [Assheton Arms](https://www.jamesplaces.com/assheton-arms/). 23 km. 5.5 hours.

WHI.name: Whitstable
WHI.adminWalksAll:
**[Faversham to Whitstable](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/faversham-to-whitstable/)**: Easy coastal walk, flat terrain, seaside villages, and open shoreline. Lunch in Seasalter: the [Sportsman](http://www.thesportsmanseasalter.co.uk/), or the [Poppet's Café & Tea Room](http://www.poppetscafeseasalter.com/). 15 km. 4.5 hours.

**Birchington-on-Sea to Whitstable**: A longer variant of [Birchington to Herne Bay](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/birchington-to-herne-bay/). Flat coastal promenade and sea wall; shingle beach, beach huts, caravan-park front, residential clifftops, and seaside towns. Sights: [Reculver Towers and Roman Fort](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reculver), Long Rock nature reserve, and Herne Bay Pier. Lunch in Reculver: the [King Ethelbert Inn](https://www.kingethelbertinn.co.uk/), or the [HatHats Coffee Company](https://hathats.co.uk/pages/reculver). 21 km.
WHI.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Faversham to Whitstable](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/faversham-to-whitstable/)**: Easy coastal walk, flat terrain, seaside villages, and open shoreline. Lunch in Seasalter: the [Sportsman](http://www.thesportsmanseasalter.co.uk/), or the [Poppet's Café & Tea Room](http://www.poppetscafeseasalter.com/). 15 km. 4.5 hours.

WHY.name: Whyteleafe
WHY.adminWalksAll:
**[Whyteleafe Circular via Ss Peter and Paul, Chaldon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whyteleafe-circular/)**: Chalk downland, commons, airfield perimeter, wooded valleys, meadows, and restored grassland on the London/Surrey border. Sights: [Kenley Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/kenley-common), [Kenley Airfield](https://www.kenleyrevival.org/content/category/visiting), [Coulsdon Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/coulsdon-common), [Ss Peter and Paul, Chaldon](https://www.exploringsurreyspast.org.uk/themes/places/surrey/tandridge/chaldon/chaldon_church_mural), [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), RAF Kenley Tribute, Happy Valley, Burial Ground, and Riddlesdown Quarry. Can be muddy. Lunch in Coulsdon Common, or Caterham Barracks. Coulsdon Common lunch stop: the [Fox](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thefoxcoulsdoncommon#). Caterham Barracks lunch stop: the [Boundary on the Green](https://www.boundaryonthegreenpub.co.uk). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Whyteleafe Circular via Kenley Common**: A shorter variant of [Whyteleafe Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whyteleafe-circular/). Chalk downland, commons, airfield perimeter, wooded valleys, and restored grassland; omits Happy Valley loop. Sights: [Kenley Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/kenley-common), [Kenley Airfield](https://www.kenleyrevival.org/content/category/visiting), [Coulsdon Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/coulsdon-common), [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), RAF Kenley Tribute, and Riddlesdown Quarry. Can be muddy. Lunch in Coulsdon Common, or Caterham Barracks. Coulsdon Common lunch stop: the [Fox](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thefoxcoulsdoncommon#). Caterham Barracks lunch stop: the [Boundary on the Green](https://www.boundaryonthegreenpub.co.uk). 12 km. 3 hours.

**[Whyteleafe to Hayes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whyteleafe-to-hayes/)**: Gently rolling countryside, shady footpaths and bridleways, open valleys, woods, chalk downland, and quiet rural hamlets near the London/Surrey border. Sights: [Biggin Hill Airport](https://www.military-airshows.co.uk/ven10.htm), Hutchinson's Bank Local Nature Reserve, Greatpark Clock Tower, Coal Tax posts, Hayes Common, London Outer Orbital Path, Leaves Green Circular Walk, and Nash Circular Walk. Can be muddy. Lunch in Fickleshole, or Great Farleigh Green. Fickleshole lunch stop: the [White Bear](https://www.whitebearwarlingham.co.uk). Great Farleigh Green lunch stop: the [Harrow](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/theharrowwarlingham). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Coulsdon South to Whyteleafe**: An alternative variant of [Whyteleafe Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whyteleafe-circular/). Chalk downland, commons, wooded valleys, Happy Valley, airfield perimeter, and restored grassland. Sights: [Farthing Downs](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/farthing-downs), [Kenley Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/kenley-common), [Kenley Airfield](https://www.kenleyrevival.org/content/category/visiting), [Coulsdon Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/coulsdon-common), [Ss Peter and Paul, Chaldon](https://www.exploringsurreyspast.org.uk/themes/places/surrey/tandridge/chaldon/chaldon_church_mural), [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), Happy Valley, Burial Ground, and Riddlesdown Quarry. Can be muddy. Lunch in Coulsdon Common, or Caterham Barracks. Coulsdon Common lunch stop: the [Fox](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thefoxcoulsdoncommon#). Caterham Barracks lunch stop: the [Boundary on the Green](https://www.boundaryonthegreenpub.co.uk). 14 km. 3.5 hours.
WHY.publicWalksS2S: **[Whyteleafe to Hayes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whyteleafe-to-hayes/)**: Gently rolling countryside, shady footpaths and bridleways, open valleys, woods, chalk downland, and quiet rural hamlets near the London/Surrey border. Sights: [Biggin Hill Airport](https://www.military-airshows.co.uk/ven10.htm), Hutchinson's Bank Local Nature Reserve, Greatpark Clock Tower, Coal Tax posts, Hayes Common, London Outer Orbital Path, Leaves Green Circular Walk, and Nash Circular Walk. Can be muddy. Lunch in Fickleshole, or Great Farleigh Green. Fickleshole lunch stop: the [White Bear](https://www.whitebearwarlingham.co.uk). Great Farleigh Green lunch stop: the [Harrow](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/theharrowwarlingham). 16 km. 4 hours.
WHY.publicWalksCircular: **[Whyteleafe Circular via Ss Peter and Paul, Chaldon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whyteleafe-circular/)**: Chalk downland, commons, airfield perimeter, wooded valleys, meadows, and restored grassland on the London/Surrey border. Sights: [Kenley Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/kenley-common), [Kenley Airfield](https://www.kenleyrevival.org/content/category/visiting), [Coulsdon Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/coulsdon-common), [Ss Peter and Paul, Chaldon](https://www.exploringsurreyspast.org.uk/themes/places/surrey/tandridge/chaldon/chaldon_church_mural), [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), RAF Kenley Tribute, Happy Valley, Burial Ground, and Riddlesdown Quarry. Can be muddy. Lunch in Coulsdon Common, or Caterham Barracks. Coulsdon Common lunch stop: the [Fox](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thefoxcoulsdoncommon#). Caterham Barracks lunch stop: the [Boundary on the Green](https://www.boundaryonthegreenpub.co.uk). 16 km. 4 hours.

WIC.name: Wickford
WIC.adminWalksAll:
**[Wickford to Battlesbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wickford-to-battlesbridge/)**: Rolling grassy fields, quiet lanes, scattered woods, reservoir nature reserve, river valley, and tidal mudflats. Sights: Hanningfield Reservoir Nature Reserve and Battlesbridge Antiques Centre. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hanningfield Reservoir, or South Hanningfield. Hanningfield Reservoir lunch stop: the [Café on the Water (& The Tackle Shop)](https://www.watersideparksuk.com/park/hanningfield/cafe-on-the-water/). South Hanningfield lunch stop: the [Old Windmill](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/oldwindmill/). 21 km. 4.5 hours.

**Wickford to South Woodham Ferrers**: An alternative variant of [Wickford to Battlesbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wickford-to-battlesbridge/). Flat brook-side path, fields, fenland nature reserve, saltmarshes, and tidal River Crouch with mudflats and creeks. Sights: Woodham Fenn Nature Reserve and Marsh Farm Country Park. Best at low tide. Lunch in Hanningfield Reservoir, or South Hanningfield. Hanningfield Reservoir lunch stop: the [Café on the Water (& The Tackle Shop)](https://www.watersideparksuk.com/park/hanningfield/cafe-on-the-water/). South Hanningfield lunch stop: the [Old Windmill](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/oldwindmill/). 29 km.

**Ingatestone to Wickford**: An alternative variant of [Ingatestone to Battlesbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ingatestone-to-battlesbridge/). Wid Valley fields, riverside paths, cattle farm estate, bluebell woods, tree-lined tracks, and Crowsheath Community Woodland. Sights: Ingatestone Hall, St. Mary's Church Buttsbury, Stock Tower Mill, and Hanningfield Reservoir Nature Discovery Centre. Can be muddy. Lunch in Stock, or South Hanningfield. Stock lunch stops: the [Bakers Arms](http://www.bakersarms.co.uk/), the [Hoop](http://www.thehoop.co.uk/), the [Dandelion & Burdock Kitchen & Lounge](https://www.dandelion-burdock.co.uk/), the [Bear Inn](http://www.thebearstock.com/), or the [Harvard Inn](https://theharvardinn.co.uk/). South Hanningfield lunch stop: the [Old Windmill](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/oldwindmill/). 18 km.
WIC.publicWalksS2S: **[Wickford to Battlesbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wickford-to-battlesbridge/)**: Rolling grassy fields, quiet lanes, scattered woods, reservoir nature reserve, river valley, and tidal mudflats. Sights: Hanningfield Reservoir Nature Reserve and Battlesbridge Antiques Centre. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hanningfield Reservoir, or South Hanningfield. Hanningfield Reservoir lunch stop: the [Café on the Water (& The Tackle Shop)](https://www.watersideparksuk.com/park/hanningfield/cafe-on-the-water/). South Hanningfield lunch stop: the [Old Windmill](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/oldwindmill/). 21 km. 4.5 hours.

WIM.name: Wimbledon
WIM.adminWalksAll:
**[Wimbledon Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-circular/)**: Urban streets, open common, wooded paths, golf fairways, parkland, and quiet roads. Sights: Wimbledon Common, Cannizaro Park, Wimbledon Park, All England Lawn Tennis Club, and Queen's Mere. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Wimbledon Common: the Windmill Cafe. 10 km. 2.5 hours.

**[Wimbledon to Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-putney/)**: Suburban streets, open common, landscaped park, woodland riverside path, Royal Park grassland, suburban roads, and riverside Thames path. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](https://cannizaropark.com/about-cannizaro-park), [Wimbledon Common](https://www.wpcc.org.uk), [Richmond Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/visit/parks/richmond-park), [Barnes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barnes,_London), [Barnes Common](https://barnescommon.org.uk), [London Wetland Centre](https://www.wwt.org.uk/wetland-centres/london), [Oxford and Cambridge Boat Race](https://www.theboatrace.org/the-course), [Beverley Brook Walk](https://www.merton.gov.uk/system/files?file=beverley_brook_walk_web.pdf), Millennium Fountain, Cannizaro Park Aviary, Sunken Garden, Mediterranean Garden, Italian Garden, Rose Garden, Diana and the Fawn statue, Caesar's Camp Iron Age hill fort, Barnes Common Old Cemetery, Craven Cottage, St Mary's Church Putney, All Saints Church Putney, and Gustav Holst blue plaque. Can be muddy. Lunch in Richmond Park, or Barnes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Roehampton Gate café. Barnes lunch stops: the [Crossing](https://www.thecrossing-barnes.co.uk), the [Ye White Hart](https://www.whitehartbarnes.co.uk), the [Watermans Arms](https://www.watermansarms.co.uk), the [Bull's Head](https://www.thebullsheadbarnes.com), the [Coach & Horses](https://www.coachandhorsesbarnes.co.uk), or the [Sun Inn](https://www.thesuninnbarnes.co.uk). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Wimbledon to Kingston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-kingston/)**: Common heathland, woodland, royal deer park, formal gardens, Thames riverside path, and open grassland. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](http://www.cannizaropark.org.uk/index.htm), [Cannizaro House](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cannizaro_Park), [Richmond Park](http://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond_park/), [Southside House](http://www.southsidehouse.com/), [Caesar's Camp](http://www.themodernantiquarian.com/site/2393/caesars_camp_wimbledon.html), Isabella Plantation, Pembroke Lodge, King Henry VIII Mound, Ham House, Teddington Lock, and Hampton Court Palace. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Wimbledon Common, Richmond Park, or Richmond Hill. Wimbledon Common lunch stop: the Fox and Grapes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Pembroke Lodge. Richmond Hill lunch stop: the Roebuck. 18 km.

**Wimbledon to Barnes Bridge**: A shorter variant of [Wimbledon to Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-putney/). Suburban streets, open common, landscaped park, woodland riverside path, Royal Park grassland, suburban roads, and Thames riverbank. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](https://cannizaropark.com/about-cannizaro-park), [Wimbledon Common](https://www.wpcc.org.uk), [Richmond Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/visit/parks/richmond-park), Millennium Fountain, Caesar's Camp Iron Age hill fort, Barnes Railway Bridge, and Ye White Hart. Can be muddy. Lunch in Richmond Park, or Barnes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Roehampton Gate café. Barnes lunch stops: the [Crossing](https://www.thecrossing-barnes.co.uk), the [Ye White Hart](https://www.whitehartbarnes.co.uk), the [Watermans Arms](https://www.watermansarms.co.uk), the [Bull's Head](https://www.thebullsheadbarnes.com), the [Coach & Horses](https://www.coachandhorsesbarnes.co.uk), or the [Sun Inn](https://www.thesuninnbarnes.co.uk). 11 km. 2.5 hours.

**Wimbledon to Twickenham**: An alternative variant of [Wimbledon to Kingston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-kingston/). Common heathland, woodland, royal deer park, Thames riverside path, Palladian riverside park, and Italianate gardens. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](http://www.cannizaropark.org.uk/index.htm), [Richmond Park](http://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond_park/), Isabella Plantation, Pembroke Lodge, [Marble Hill House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/marble-hill-house/), [Orleans House Art Gallery](https://www.orleanshousegallery.org/), [York House Riverside Garden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Naked_Ladies), and [Eel Pie Island](http://www.eelpieislandartists.co.uk). Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Wimbledon Common, Richmond Park, or Twickenham riverside. Wimbledon Common lunch stop: the Fox and Grapes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Pembroke Lodge. Twickenham riverside lunch stop: the White Swan.

**Wimbledon to Kew Bridge**: An alternative variant of [Wimbledon to Kingston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-kingston/). Common heathland, woodland, royal deer park, and Thames riverside path northward to Kew. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](http://www.cannizaropark.org.uk/index.htm), [Richmond Park](http://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond_park/), Isabella Plantation, and Pembroke Lodge. Lunch in Wimbledon Common, or Richmond Park. Wimbledon Common lunch stop: the Fox and Grapes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Pembroke Lodge.
WIM.publicWalksS2S:
**[Wimbledon to Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-putney/)**: Suburban streets, open common, landscaped park, woodland riverside path, Royal Park grassland, suburban roads, and riverside Thames path. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](https://cannizaropark.com/about-cannizaro-park), [Wimbledon Common](https://www.wpcc.org.uk), [Richmond Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/visit/parks/richmond-park), [Barnes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barnes,_London), [Barnes Common](https://barnescommon.org.uk), [London Wetland Centre](https://www.wwt.org.uk/wetland-centres/london), [Oxford and Cambridge Boat Race](https://www.theboatrace.org/the-course), [Beverley Brook Walk](https://www.merton.gov.uk/system/files?file=beverley_brook_walk_web.pdf), Millennium Fountain, Cannizaro Park Aviary, Sunken Garden, Mediterranean Garden, Italian Garden, Rose Garden, Diana and the Fawn statue, Caesar's Camp Iron Age hill fort, Barnes Common Old Cemetery, Craven Cottage, St Mary's Church Putney, All Saints Church Putney, and Gustav Holst blue plaque. Can be muddy. Lunch in Richmond Park, or Barnes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Roehampton Gate café. Barnes lunch stops: the [Crossing](https://www.thecrossing-barnes.co.uk), the [Ye White Hart](https://www.whitehartbarnes.co.uk), the [Watermans Arms](https://www.watermansarms.co.uk), the [Bull's Head](https://www.thebullsheadbarnes.com), the [Coach & Horses](https://www.coachandhorsesbarnes.co.uk), or the [Sun Inn](https://www.thesuninnbarnes.co.uk). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Wimbledon to Kingston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-kingston/)**: Common heathland, woodland, royal deer park, formal gardens, Thames riverside path, and open grassland. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](http://www.cannizaropark.org.uk/index.htm), [Cannizaro House](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cannizaro_Park), [Richmond Park](http://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond_park/), [Southside House](http://www.southsidehouse.com/), [Caesar's Camp](http://www.themodernantiquarian.com/site/2393/caesars_camp_wimbledon.html), Isabella Plantation, Pembroke Lodge, King Henry VIII Mound, Ham House, Teddington Lock, and Hampton Court Palace. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Wimbledon Common, Richmond Park, or Richmond Hill. Wimbledon Common lunch stop: the Fox and Grapes. Richmond Park lunch stop: the Pembroke Lodge. Richmond Hill lunch stop: the Roebuck. 18 km.
WIM.publicWalksCircular: **[Wimbledon Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-circular/)**: Urban streets, open common, wooded paths, golf fairways, parkland, and quiet roads. Sights: Wimbledon Common, Cannizaro Park, Wimbledon Park, All England Lawn Tennis Club, and Queen's Mere. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Wimbledon Common: the Windmill Cafe. 10 km. 2.5 hours.

WIN.name: Winchester
WIN.adminWalksAll:
**[Winchester Circular via Wolvesey Palace](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchester-circular/)**: Historic cathedral city streets, watermeadows, iron age hillfort, downland ridges, river valley, and ancient meadows. Sights: Winchester Cathedral, Great Hall, King Arthur's Round Table, Westgate, Kingsgate, St Catherine's Hill, Wolvesey Castle, Wolvesey Palace, St Cross Hospital, St Cross Church, Winchester College, and City Mill. M3 motorway noise. Lunch in Shawford: the Bridge. 16 km.

**Winchester Circular via Winchester Cathedral & Wolvesey Castle**: A shorter variant of [Winchester Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchester-circular/). Historic cathedral city streets, watermeadows, iron age hillfort, downland ridges, river valley, and ancient meadows. Sights: Winchester Cathedral, Great Hall, King Arthur's Round Table, Westgate, Kingsgate, St Catherine's Hill, Wolvesey Castle, St Cross Hospital, St Cross Church, Winchester College, and City Mill. M3 motorway noise. Lunch in Shawford: the Bridge. 15 km.

**Winchester to Shawford**: A shorter variant of [Winchester Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchester-circular/). Historic cathedral city streets, watermeadows, iron age hillfort, and river valley. Sights: Winchester Cathedral, Great Hall, King Arthur's Round Table, Westgate, and St Catherine's Hill. M3 motorway noise. Lunch in Shawford: the Bridge. 8 km.
WIN.publicWalksCircular: **[Winchester Circular via Wolvesey Palace](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchester-circular/)**: Historic cathedral city streets, watermeadows, iron age hillfort, downland ridges, river valley, and ancient meadows. Sights: Winchester Cathedral, Great Hall, King Arthur's Round Table, Westgate, Kingsgate, St Catherine's Hill, Wolvesey Castle, Wolvesey Palace, St Cross Hospital, St Cross Church, Winchester College, and City Mill. M3 motorway noise. Lunch in Shawford: the Bridge. 16 km.

WIV.name: Wivenhoe
WIV.adminWalksAll:
**Wivenhoe Circular**: River estuary mudflats, sea wall paths, woodland, water meadows, saltmarshes, farmland, and quaint riverside villages. Sights: Nottage Maritime Institute, St Mary's Church, River Colne Flood Surge Barrier, Church of St Lawrence, Church of St Andrew, Fingringhoe Mill, and John Brunning Walk. Check ferry timetable. Lunch in Wivenhoe, or Rowhedge. Wivenhoe lunch stops: the Rose & Crown, or the Greyhound. Rowhedge lunch stops: the Anchor, or the Ye Olde Albion. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 4.5 hours.

**Wivenhoe to Clacton-on-Sea (via ferry)**: A longer variant of [Wivenhoe to West Mersea or Clacton-on-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wivenhoe-to-west-mersea-or-clacton-on-sea/). Tidal river estuary, saltings, mud flats, disused railway trackbed, harbourfront, ferry crossing, coastal sea wall, caravan park frontages, sandy beach, and seaside town promenade. Sights: Jaywick Martello Tower and [Colne Point National Nature Reserve](http://www.essexwt.org.uk/reserves/colne-point). Check ferry times. Lunch in Brightlingsea, or Point Clear. Brightlingsea lunch stops: the No. 1 Harbour Square, or the [Yachtsman's Arms](https://www.facebook.com/theyachtsmansarms). Point Clear lunch stop: the Ferry Boat Inn. 24 km.
WIV.publicWalksCircular: **Wivenhoe Circular**: River estuary mudflats, sea wall paths, woodland, water meadows, saltmarshes, farmland, and quaint riverside villages. Sights: Nottage Maritime Institute, St Mary's Church, River Colne Flood Surge Barrier, Church of St Lawrence, Church of St Andrew, Fingringhoe Mill, and John Brunning Walk. Check ferry timetable. Lunch in Wivenhoe, or Rowhedge. Wivenhoe lunch stops: the Rose & Crown, or the Greyhound. Rowhedge lunch stops: the Anchor, or the Ye Olde Albion. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 4.5 hours.

WKM.name: Wokingham
WKM.adminWalksAll:
**[Blackwater to Wokingham via Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackwater-to-wokingham/)**: River meadows, gravel-pit lakes, nature reserves, country lanes, mixed and pine woodland, heathland, golf course, soft fruit farms, and historic market town. Sights: Shepherd Meadows Nature Reserve, [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), Ambarrow Court Local Nature Reserve, Wellington College, Heathlake Nature Reserve, [Wokingham](https://www.wokinghamsociety.org.uk/history.html), St James's Church, Wellingtonia Avenue, Devil's Highway, Wokingham Town Hall, Tudor House, Rose Street, Wokingham railway footbridge, and Ludgrove School. Flood risk in places. Lunch in Finchampstead: the [Queen's Oak](https://www.thequeensoak.com), or the [Greyhound](https://greyhoundfinchampstead.co.uk). 25 km. 6 hours.

**Sandhurst to Wokingham via Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre**: A variant of [Blackwater to Wokingham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackwater-to-wokingham/). Roads from station, Horseshoe Lake circuit, fields, pine woodland, heathland, golf course, soft fruit farms, and market town. Sights: [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), Ambarrow Court Local Nature Reserve, Heathlake Nature Reserve, and [Wokingham](https://www.wokinghamsociety.org.uk/history.html). Lunch in Horseshoe Lake: the [Lake View Café](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk/lakeviewcafe). 14 km.

**Blackwater to Wokingham via Shepherd Meadows Nature Reserve**: An alternative variant of [Blackwater to Wokingham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackwater-to-wokingham/). River meadows, Horseshoe Lake circuit, fields, pine woodland, heathland, golf course, soft fruit farms, and market town. Sights: Shepherd Meadows Nature Reserve, [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), Ambarrow Court Local Nature Reserve, Wellington College, Heathlake Nature Reserve, [Wokingham](https://www.wokinghamsociety.org.uk/history.html), Wokingham Town Hall, Tudor House, Rose Street, and Wokingham railway footbridge. Flood risk in places. Lunch in Horseshoe Lake: the [Lake View Café](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk/lakeviewcafe). 17 km. 4 hours.

**Sandhurst to Wokingham via Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve**: A variant of [Blackwater to Wokingham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackwater-to-wokingham/). Roads from station, gravel-pit lakes, nature reserves, country lanes, mixed and pine woodland, heathland, golf course, soft fruit farms, and historic market town. Sights: [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), Ambarrow Court Local Nature Reserve, Wellington College, Heathlake Nature Reserve, [Wokingham](https://www.wokinghamsociety.org.uk/history.html), St James's Church, Wellingtonia Avenue, Wokingham Town Hall, Tudor House, and Rose Street. Lunch in Finchampstead: the [Queen's Oak](https://www.thequeensoak.com), or the [Greyhound](https://greyhoundfinchampstead.co.uk). 22 km.
WKM.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Blackwater to Wokingham via Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackwater-to-wokingham/)**: River meadows, gravel-pit lakes, nature reserves, country lanes, mixed and pine woodland, heathland, golf course, soft fruit farms, and historic market town. Sights: Shepherd Meadows Nature Reserve, [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), Ambarrow Court Local Nature Reserve, Wellington College, Heathlake Nature Reserve, [Wokingham](https://www.wokinghamsociety.org.uk/history.html), St James's Church, Wellingtonia Avenue, Devil's Highway, Wokingham Town Hall, Tudor House, Rose Street, Wokingham railway footbridge, and Ludgrove School. Flood risk in places. Lunch in Finchampstead: the [Queen's Oak](https://www.thequeensoak.com), or the [Greyhound](https://greyhoundfinchampstead.co.uk). 25 km. 6 hours.

WMA.name: West Malling
WMA.adminWalksAll:
**[West Malling Circular via River Medway](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/west-malling-circular/)**: Orchards, coppiced woodland, water meadows, low hills, river valley, parkland, and market town. Sights: [Horticultural Research Institute](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Malling_Research_Station), [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), [Manor Park Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/find-a-kent-country-park/manor-park-country-park), [St Leonard's Tower](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/st-leonards-tower), [West Malling](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/West_Malling), Teston Bridge, Tutsham Hall, Smiths Hall, Barham Court, Teston Bridge Country Park, Warren Woods Nature Park, Douce's Manor, St Mary's Abbey, St Mary the Virgin, and St James the Great. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh: the [Tickled Trout](https://thetickledtrout.com). 20 km. 5 hours.

**West Malling Circular via Horticultural Research Institute**: A shorter variant of [West Malling Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/west-malling-circular/). Orchards, coppiced woodland, parkland, and market town fringes. Sights: [Horticultural Research Institute](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Malling_Research_Station), Warren Woods Nature Park, [Manor Park Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/find-a-kent-country-park/manor-park-country-park), [St Leonard's Tower](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/st-leonards-tower), Douce's Manor, St Mary's Abbey, and St Mary the Virgin. Can be muddy. Lunch in Red Hill: the [North Pole](https://northpolepub.co.uk). 11 km. 2.5 hours.

**West Malling to East Farleigh**: A variant of [East Malling to Maidstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-malling-to-maidstone/). Orchards, country park, coppiced woodland, water meadows, riverside paths, and low hills. Sights: St Mary's Abbey, Manor Park Country Park, St Leonard's Tower, Douce's Manor, Warren Woods Nature Park, St Mary the Virgin, Nettlestead Place, Medway Valley Walk, Tutsham Hall, Smiths Hall, Teston Bridge, East Farleigh Bridge, and East Farleigh Lock. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh, or Wateringbury. West Farleigh lunch stop: the [Tickled Trout](https://thetickledtrout.com). Wateringbury lunch stops: the [River Kitchen](https://www.theriverkitchen.co.uk), or the Ramblers Rest Café. 17 km.

**West Malling to Maidstone East**: A longer variant of [East Malling to Maidstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-malling-to-maidstone/). Orchards, country park, coppiced woodland, water meadows, riverside paths, low hills, and pedestrianised town centre. Sights: [East Malling Research Station (Horticultural Research Institute)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Malling_Research_Station), [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), [Maidstone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maidstone), St Mary's Abbey, Manor Park Country Park, St Leonard's Tower, Douce's Manor, Warren Woods Nature Park, St Mary the Virgin, Nettlestead Place, Medway Valley Walk, Tutsham Hall, Smiths Hall, Barham Court, Teston Bridge, Maidstone Millennium River Park, Barming Bridge, East Farleigh Bridge, East Farleigh Lock, Lockmeadow Millennium Bridge, The Stag, All Saints Church, Archbishop's Palace, Russian Cannon, Maidstone Town Hall, Maidstone Museum, Battle of Maidstone memorial, Brenchley Gardens, and Iguanodon. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh, or Wateringbury. West Farleigh lunch stop: the [Tickled Trout](https://thetickledtrout.com). Wateringbury lunch stops: the [River Kitchen](https://www.theriverkitchen.co.uk), or the Ramblers Rest Café. 21 km. 5 hours.

**East Malling to West Malling**: A shorter variant of [West Malling Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/west-malling-circular/). Orchards, coppiced woodland, water meadows, low hills, river valley, parkland, and market town. Sights: [Horticultural Research Institute](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Malling_Research_Station), [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), Teston Bridge, Tutsham Hall, Smiths Hall, Barham Court, Teston Bridge Country Park, Warren Woods Nature Park, [Manor Park Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/find-a-kent-country-park/manor-park-country-park), [St Leonard's Tower](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/st-leonards-tower), Douce's Manor, St Mary's Abbey, and St Mary the Virgin. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh: the [Tickled Trout](https://thetickledtrout.com). 18 km. 4.5 hours.
WMA.publicWalksCircular: **[West Malling Circular via River Medway](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/west-malling-circular/)**: Orchards, coppiced woodland, water meadows, low hills, river valley, parkland, and market town. Sights: [Horticultural Research Institute](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Malling_Research_Station), [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), [Manor Park Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/find-a-kent-country-park/manor-park-country-park), [St Leonard's Tower](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/st-leonards-tower), [West Malling](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/West_Malling), Teston Bridge, Tutsham Hall, Smiths Hall, Barham Court, Teston Bridge Country Park, Warren Woods Nature Park, Douce's Manor, St Mary's Abbey, St Mary the Virgin, and St James the Great. Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh: the [Tickled Trout](https://thetickledtrout.com). 20 km. 5 hours.

WMC.name: Wilmcote
WMC.adminWalksAll: **[Wilmcote Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-828)**: River Alne banks, canal village, old railway track, open fields, ridge and furrow, flood bank, and historic villages. Sights: [Mary Arden's Farm](https://www.shakespeare.org.uk), [St John the Baptist Church](https://www.warwickshirechurches.weebly.com), [Yew Tree Farm](https://www.yew-tree-farm.co.uk), Edstone Aqueduct, Palmer's Farm, and Stratford-upon-Avon Canal. Can be muddy. Lunch in Wilmcote, Aston Cantlow, near Edstone Aqueduct, or Wootton Wawen. Wilmcote lunch stops: the [Mary Arden Inn](https://www.mary-arden.co.uk), or the Wilmcote Village Stores. Aston Cantlow lunch stops: the [Kings Head](https://www.thekingshead.co), or the Island Meadow Caravan Park Shop. near Edstone Aqueduct lunch stop: the [View](https://www.theview-eat.co.uk). Wootton Wawen lunch stop: the [Yew Tree Farm coffee shop and chocolaterie](https://www.yew-tree-farm.co.uk). 8 km. 3 hours.
WMC.publicWalksCircular: **[Wilmcote Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-828)**: River Alne banks, canal village, old railway track, open fields, ridge and furrow, flood bank, and historic villages. Sights: [Mary Arden's Farm](https://www.shakespeare.org.uk), [St John the Baptist Church](https://www.warwickshirechurches.weebly.com), [Yew Tree Farm](https://www.yew-tree-farm.co.uk), Edstone Aqueduct, Palmer's Farm, and Stratford-upon-Avon Canal. Can be muddy. Lunch in Wilmcote, Aston Cantlow, near Edstone Aqueduct, or Wootton Wawen. Wilmcote lunch stops: the [Mary Arden Inn](https://www.mary-arden.co.uk), or the Wilmcote Village Stores. Aston Cantlow lunch stops: the [Kings Head](https://www.thekingshead.co), or the Island Meadow Caravan Park Shop. near Edstone Aqueduct lunch stop: the [View](https://www.theview-eat.co.uk). Wootton Wawen lunch stop: the [Yew Tree Farm coffee shop and chocolaterie](https://www.yew-tree-farm.co.uk). 8 km. 3 hours.

WMN.name: Warminster
WMN.adminWalksAll: **[Westbury to Warminster](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/westbury-to-warminster-via-imber-range/)**: Chalk downland, Iron Age hillforts, five hills, Salisbury Plain military range, abandoned village, and sweeping views over Wiltshire and Somerset. Sights: Westbury White Horse, Bratton Camp, Scratchbury Camp, Battlesbury Camp, Imber Village, and St Giles, Imber. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Imber: the St Giles, Imber (Tea and Biscuits). 30 km. 7 hours.
WMN.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Westbury to Warminster](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/westbury-to-warminster-via-imber-range/)**: Chalk downland, Iron Age hillforts, five hills, Salisbury Plain military range, abandoned village, and sweeping views over Wiltshire and Somerset. Sights: Westbury White Horse, Bratton Camp, Scratchbury Camp, Battlesbury Camp, Imber Village, and St Giles, Imber. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Imber: the St Giles, Imber (Tea and Biscuits). 30 km. 7 hours.

WMR.name: Widney Manor
WMR.adminWalksAll: **[Widney Manor Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-888)**: Suburban streets, open fields, M42 motorway bridge crossing, River Blythe, and parkland. Sights: Widney Manor Cemetery, [River Blythe](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Blythe), Blythe Valley Nature Reserve, and [Malvern & Brueton Park](https://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/). Lunch in Widney Manor, on route, or Dorridge. Widney Manor lunch stop: the [Fieldhouse](https://www.emberinns.co.uk). on route lunch stops: the [Dark Horse Espresso](https://www.dhespresso.com), or the [Drum and Monkey](https://www.chefandbrewer.com). Dorridge lunch stops: the [Butchers Social](https://www.thebutchers.social), or the Skogen. 4 km. 1.5 hours.
WMR.publicWalksCircular: **[Widney Manor Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-888)**: Suburban streets, open fields, M42 motorway bridge crossing, River Blythe, and parkland. Sights: Widney Manor Cemetery, [River Blythe](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Blythe), Blythe Valley Nature Reserve, and [Malvern & Brueton Park](https://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/). Lunch in Widney Manor, on route, or Dorridge. Widney Manor lunch stop: the [Fieldhouse](https://www.emberinns.co.uk). on route lunch stops: the [Dark Horse Espresso](https://www.dhespresso.com), or the [Drum and Monkey](https://www.chefandbrewer.com). Dorridge lunch stops: the [Butchers Social](https://www.thebutchers.social), or the Skogen. 4 km. 1.5 hours.

WNC.name: Windsor and Eton Central
WNC.privateNote:
LON Trainline says 28m — Paddington via Slough
CLJ 1h
RMD Goog says 1h14 (using bus). Trainline says 1h18 (RMD > Feltham > Windsor & Eton Riverside > Windsor & Eton Central.
WNC.adminWalksAll: **Sunningdale to Windsor and Eton Central**: Parkland, lake shores, ornamental gardens, light woodland, deer park, tree-lined avenue, and historic town streets. Sights: Holy Trinity Sunningdale Church, Coworth Park, Virginia Water Lake, Valley Gardens, Totem Pole, Obelisk, [Savill Gardens](http://www.windsorgreatpark.co.uk/), Cow Pond, Royal Lodge, Copper Horse, Long Walk, Windsor Castle, and Guildhall. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Savill Gardens: the Savill Garden Restaurant. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 4 hours.
WNC.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Sunningdale to Windsor and Eton Central**: Parkland, lake shores, ornamental gardens, light woodland, deer park, tree-lined avenue, and historic town streets. Sights: Holy Trinity Sunningdale Church, Coworth Park, Virginia Water Lake, Valley Gardens, Totem Pole, Obelisk, [Savill Gardens](http://www.windsorgreatpark.co.uk/), Cow Pond, Royal Lodge, Copper Horse, Long Walk, Windsor Castle, and Guildhall. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Savill Gardens: the Savill Garden Restaurant. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 4 hours.

WND.name: Wendover
WND.adminWalksAll:
**Wendover Circular via Great Kimble (RG)**: Rambler favourite! From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 24 July 2022.* 12 km. 190 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/849746255)**

**Wendover Circular via Upper Bacombe**: *Last reviewed 6 February 2022.* 9 km. 250 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921485105)**

**Tring to Wendover via Aston Clinton**: Flat canal towpath, reservoir banks, open arable fields, wooded Chilterns hills, and steep descents. Sights: Tring Reservoirs, All Saints Church, Buckland, St Mary the Virgin, Drayton Beauchamp, Halton House, Wendover Arm Canal, Boddington Hill Fort, Coombe Hill, Go Ape, and Rumsey's Chocolaterie. Steep chalky descent. Lunch in Aston Clinton: the Oak. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 22 km. 230 m uphill. 6 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920846007)**

**Wendover Circular via Pulpit Hill fort**: Wooded hills, hidden vales, fields, Chiltern escarpment, panoramic ridge views, and wooded valleys. Sights: Whiteleaf Cross, Pulpit Hill fort, Anne Boleyn's Cottages, and Ellesborough Church. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Whiteleaf, or Lower Cadsden. Whiteleaf lunch stop: the [Red Lion](http://www.theredlionwhiteleaf.co.uk/). Lower Cadsden lunch stop: the [Plough](http://plough-at-cadsden.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 18 km. 6 hours.

**[Wendover Circular via The Lee and Swan Bottom](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wendover-circular-via-the-lee-and-swan-bottom/)**: Chiltern Hills, woodland, bluebell woods in spring, open fields, conservation village green, escarpment holloway, forest tracks with panoramic views, and ancient hillfort site. Sights: Boddington Hill Iron Age Hillfort, St. Mary's Church, and Wendover Woods. Steep descents. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in The Lee: the Cock and Rabbit Inn. 20 km. 5 hours.

**Wendover Circular via Whiteleaf Cross**: Wooded hills, fields, Chiltern escarpment, and shortened via Cross Coppice. Sights: Whiteleaf Cross and Anne Boleyn's Cottages. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Butler's Cross: the [Russell Arms](http://therussellarms.co.uk/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 13 km. 4 hours.

**Wendover Circular via Coombe Hill**: Wooded hills, escarpment, and with alternative ending via Coombe Hill from Ellesborough. Sights: Coombe Hill and Ellesborough Church. Best in Sep–Nov. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse).

**Wendover to Monks Risborough**: Wooded hills, fields, Chiltern escarpment, and descending to Monk's Risborough. Sights: Whiteleaf Cross and Anne Boleyn's Cottages. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Whiteleaf: the [Red Lion](http://www.theredlionwhiteleaf.co.uk/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 11 km. 3.5 hours.

**Wendover to Great Missenden**: An alternative variant of [Wendover Circular via The Lee and Swan Bottom](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wendover-circular-via-the-lee-and-swan-bottom/). Chiltern Hills, grassy fields, varied woodland, beautiful valleys, and scenic descent into Great Missenden. Sights: Boddington Hill Iron Age Hillfort and St. Mary's Church. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in The Lee: the Cock and Rabbit Inn. 21 km.

**Princes Risborough to Wendover via Whiteleaf Cross**: Rambler favourite! Beech woods, chalk downlands, high ridgeline, nature reserves, escarpments with wide views, and a pleasant old market town. Sights: Whiteleaf Cross, Chequers, Coombe Hill, and Boer War Monument. Can be muddy. Lunch in Smokey Row (Great Kimble), or Lower Cadsden. Smokey Row (Great Kimble) lunch stop: the Swan. Lower Cadsden lunch stop: the Plough. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**Monks Risborough to Wendover**: Wooded hills, valleys, Chiltern escarpment, open ridges, and returning to Wendover. Sights: Pulpit Hill fort and Ellesborough Church. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Lower Cadsden, or Butler's Cross. Lower Cadsden lunch stop: the [Plough](http://plough-at-cadsden.co.uk/). Butler's Cross lunch stop: the [Russell Arms](http://therussellarms.co.uk/). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 9 km. 3 hours.
WND.publicWalksCircular:
**Wendover Circular via Great Kimble (RG)**: Rambler favourite! From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 12 km. 190 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/849746255)**

**Wendover Circular via Upper Bacombe**: 9 km. 250 m uphill. 3 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921485105)**
WND.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Tring to Wendover via Aston Clinton**: Flat canal towpath, reservoir banks, open arable fields, wooded Chilterns hills, and steep descents. Sights: Tring Reservoirs, All Saints Church, Buckland, St Mary the Virgin, Drayton Beauchamp, Halton House, Wendover Arm Canal, Boddington Hill Fort, Coombe Hill, Go Ape, and Rumsey's Chocolaterie. Steep chalky descent. Lunch in Aston Clinton: the Oak. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 22 km. 230 m uphill. 6 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2920846007)**

WNF.name: Winchfield
WNF.adminWalksAll:
**Winchfield Circular**: A variant of [Hook to Winchfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hook-to-winchfield/). Canal towpath, parkland estate, rural fields, village lanes, and woodland. Sights: [Basingstoke Canal](https://www.canalguide.co.uk/canals/basingstokcanal.html), [Dogmersfield Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dogmersfield_Park), Tundry Pond, All Saints Church Dogmersfield, Old Thatch Cottage, and St Mary the Virgin Winchfield. Can be muddy. Lunch in Crookham Wharf, or Dogmersfield. Crookham Wharf lunch stop: the [Exchequer](https://www.exchequercrookham.co.uk). Dogmersfield lunch stop: the [Queens Head](https://www.queensheadpub.co.uk). 13 km. 3 hours.

**[Hook to Winchfield via Odiham High Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hook-to-winchfield/)**: Heath, river meadows, canal towpath, parkland estate, fields, and country lanes. Sights: [Bartley Heath](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/hook-common-and-bartley-heath), [Basingstoke Canal](https://www.canalguide.co.uk/canals/basingstokcanal.html), [Odiham Castle](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/odihamcastle), [Odiham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Odiham), [Dogmersfield Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dogmersfield_Park), Greywell Tunnel, St Mary the Virgin, Greywell Mill, Pest House, All Saints Church, Odiham High Street, Tundry Pond, Hunting Lodge, Old Thatch Cottage, and St Mary the Virgin Winchfield. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lodge Farm, North Warnborough, Greywell, or Odiham. Lodge Farm lunch stop: the [Newlyns Farm Shop & Café](https://www.newlyns-farmshop.co.uk/visit-us). North Warnborough lunch stop: the [Mill House](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/millhouse). Greywell lunch stop: the Fox & Goose. Odiham lunch stops: the [Bel & the Dragon](https://belandthedragon-odiham.co.uk), the [Red Lion](https://www.redlionodiham.co.uk), or the [Waterwitch](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/hampshire/waterwitch). 20 km. 4.5 hours.

**Hook to Winchfield via Bartley Heath**: A shorter variant of [Hook to Winchfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hook-to-winchfield/). Heath, river meadows, canal towpath, Odiham Common, and country lanes. Sights: [Bartley Heath](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/hook-common-and-bartley-heath), [Basingstoke Canal](https://www.canalguide.co.uk/canals/basingstokcanal.html), [Odiham Castle](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/odihamcastle), [Odiham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Odiham), Greywell Tunnel, St Mary the Virgin, Greywell Mill, Pest House, All Saints Church, Hunting Lodge, Old Thatch Cottage, and St Mary the Virgin Winchfield. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lodge Farm, North Warnborough, Greywell, or Odiham. Lodge Farm lunch stop: the [Newlyns Farm Shop & Café](https://www.newlyns-farmshop.co.uk/visit-us). North Warnborough lunch stop: the [Mill House](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/millhouse). Greywell lunch stop: the Fox & Goose. Odiham lunch stops: the [Bel & the Dragon](https://belandthedragon-odiham.co.uk), the [Red Lion](https://www.redlionodiham.co.uk), or the [Waterwitch](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/hampshire/waterwitch). 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**Hook to Winchfield via All Saints Church Odiham**: A longer variant of [Hook to Winchfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hook-to-winchfield/). Heath, river meadows, canal towpath, parkland estate, rural fields, village lanes, and woodland. Sights: [Bartley Heath](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/hook-common-and-bartley-heath), [Basingstoke Canal](https://www.canalguide.co.uk/canals/basingstokcanal.html), [Odiham Castle](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/odihamcastle), [Odiham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Odiham), [Dogmersfield Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dogmersfield_Park), Greywell Tunnel, St Mary the Virgin, Greywell Mill, Pest House, All Saints Church Odiham, All Saints Church Dogmersfield, Tundry Pond, Hunting Lodge, Old Thatch Cottage, and St Mary the Virgin Winchfield. Can be muddy. Lunch in Lodge Farm, North Warnborough, Greywell, Odiham, Crookham Wharf, or Dogmersfield. Lodge Farm lunch stop: the [Newlyns Farm Shop & Café](https://www.newlyns-farmshop.co.uk/visit-us). North Warnborough lunch stop: the [Mill House](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/millhouse). Greywell lunch stop: the Fox & Goose. Odiham lunch stops: the [Bel & the Dragon](https://belandthedragon-odiham.co.uk), the [Red Lion](https://www.redlionodiham.co.uk), or the [Waterwitch](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/hampshire/waterwitch). Crookham Wharf lunch stop: the [Exchequer](https://www.exchequercrookham.co.uk). Dogmersfield lunch stop: the [Queens Head](https://www.queensheadpub.co.uk). 24 km. 5.5 hours.
