Odiham lunch stops: the [Bel & the Dragon](https://belandthedragon-odiham.co.uk), the [Red Lion](https://www.redlionodiham.co.uk), or the [Waterwitch](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/hampshire/waterwitch). 20 km. 4.5 hours.

WNH.name: Warnham
WNH.adminWalksAll: **Ockley to Warnham**: Fields, oak woods, gentle hills, farmland, quiet lanes, and sleepy Surrey-Sussex border countryside. Sights: [Parish Church of St John the Baptist](https://friendsofokewoodchurch.org/#history), [Hannah Peschar Sculpture Garden](https://www.hannahpescharsculpture.com/), Leith Hill, and Warnham Lodge Estate water tower. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Walliswood, or Okewood Hill. Walliswood lunch stop: the Scarlett Arms. Okewood Hill lunch stop: the Punchbowl. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 5 hours.
WNH.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Ockley to Warnham**: Fields, oak woods, gentle hills, farmland, quiet lanes, and sleepy Surrey-Sussex border countryside. Sights: [Parish Church of St John the Baptist](https://friendsofokewoodchurch.org/#history), [Hannah Peschar Sculpture Garden](https://www.hannahpescharsculpture.com/), Leith Hill, and Warnham Lodge Estate water tower. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Walliswood, or Okewood Hill. Walliswood lunch stop: the Scarlett Arms. Okewood Hill lunch stop: the Punchbowl. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 5 hours.

WNR.name: Windsor and Eton Riverside
WNR.adminWalksAll:
**Windsor Circular via Windsor Great Park**: *Last reviewed 2 January 2023.* 18 km. 130 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921499158)**

**Sunningdale to Windsor**: Parkland, lake shores, ornamental gardens, light woodland, deer park, tree-lined avenue, and historic town streets. Sights: Holy Trinity Sunningdale Church, Coworth Park, Virginia Water Lake, Valley Gardens, Totem Pole, Obelisk, [Savill Gardens](http://www.windsorgreatpark.co.uk/), Cow Pond, Royal Lodge, Copper Horse, Long Walk, Windsor Castle, and Guildhall. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Savill Gardens: the Savill Garden Restaurant. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 90 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921106856)**

**Windsor and Eton Riverside to Egham**: An alternative variant of [Slough to Egham via Windsor Great Park](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/slough-to-egham-via-windsor-great-park/). Flat parkland, deer park, historic royal landscape, ornamental gardens, and lake. Sights: Windsor Castle, Savill Gardens, Valley Gardens, Virginia Water lake, and [The Royal Landscape](http://www.theroyallandscape.co.uk/visiting/).
WNR.publicWalksCircular: **Windsor Circular via Windsor Great Park**: 18 km. 130 m uphill. 4.5 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921499158)**
WNR.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Sunningdale to Windsor**: Parkland, lake shores, ornamental gardens, light woodland, deer park, tree-lined avenue, and historic town streets. Sights: Holy Trinity Sunningdale Church, Coworth Park, Virginia Water Lake, Valley Gardens, Totem Pole, Obelisk, [Savill Gardens](http://www.windsorgreatpark.co.uk/), Cow Pond, Royal Lodge, Copper Horse, Long Walk, Windsor Castle, and Guildhall. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Savill Gardens: the Savill Garden Restaurant. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 14 km. 90 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921106856)**

WOB.name: Woburn Sands
WOB.adminWalksAll:
**Bow Brickhill to Woburn Sands**: Woods, golf course, gentle hills, landscaped deer park, village streets, playing fields, and golf links. Sights: Woburn Abbey, Woburn Park, [Woburn Safari Park](https://www.woburnsafari.co.uk/), St Mary Old Church, St Mary parish church, Parish Church of St Botolph, and Sierra redwood (Wellingtonia) trees. Can be muddy. Lunch in Woburn: the [Bell Inn](https://www.greeneking-pubs.co.uk/pubs/bedfordshire/bell-hotel-inn/), the [Longs Inn](https://www.longsinn.co.uk/), the [Woburn Brasserie](http://www.woburnbrasserie.co.uk/), or the [Black Horse](http://www.blackhorsewoburn.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 19 km. 230 m uphill. 5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921098649)**

**[Woburn Sands to Ridgmont](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/woburn-sands-to-ridgmont/)**: Farmland, conifer plantation, semi-ancient woodland, deer park, open grassland, safari park perimeter, and muddy field paths. Sights: Aspley Woods, Danesborough Hill Fort, Woburn Abbey, Church of St John the Baptist, Woburn Safari Park, Ridgmont Station Heritage Centre, and St James' Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Church End, Eversholt, or Woburn. Church End, Eversholt lunch stop: the [Green Man](https://thegreenmaneversholt.co.uk/). Woburn lunch stops: the Fratellis, or the Stop 'n' Stuff. 20 km.
WOB.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Bow Brickhill to Woburn Sands**: Woods, golf course, gentle hills, landscaped deer park, village streets, playing fields, and golf links. Sights: Woburn Abbey, Woburn Park, [Woburn Safari Park](https://www.woburnsafari.co.uk/), St Mary Old Church, St Mary parish church, Parish Church of St Botolph, and Sierra redwood (Wellingtonia) trees. Can be muddy. Lunch in Woburn: the [Bell Inn](https://www.greeneking-pubs.co.uk/pubs/bedfordshire/bell-hotel-inn/), the [Longs Inn](https://www.longsinn.co.uk/), the [Woburn Brasserie](http://www.woburnbrasserie.co.uk/), or the [Black Horse](http://www.blackhorsewoburn.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 19 km. 230 m uphill. 5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921098649)**

WOH.name: Woldingham
WOH.adminWalksAll:
**[Woldingham Circular via Titsey Place](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/woldingham-circular-via-titsey-place/)**: Rural valleys, North Downs escarpments, beech woods, plantation paths, open downland, hilltop village, and historic garden village. Sights: [Marden Park Woods](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/marden-park), [Titsey Place](https://www.titsey.org), Woldingham School, North Downs Way, Oxted Downs, Titsey Plantation, Greenwich Meridian, Woldingham Garden Village, Peace Centre, St Paul's Church, and Madeira Walk. Can be muddy. Lunch in Botley Hill, or Titsey Place. Botley Hill lunch stop: the [Botley Hill Farmhouse](http://www.botleyhill-farmhouse.co.uk). Titsey Place lunch stop: the [Titsey Place Tea Room](https://www.titsey.org/tea-rooms-and-facilities). 17 km. 5 hours.

**[Woldingham to Oxted via Godstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/woldingham-to-oxted/)**: Rambler favourite! North Downs woodland ridge, open chalk downland, M25 crossing, village green, Greensand Way farmland, small settlements, and ancient churchyards. Sights: [Marden Park Woods](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/marden-park), [Godstone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Godstone), [Tandridge Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/tandridge-churchyard-765), [Godstone Vineyard](https://www.godstonevineyards.com), St Mary's Homes, and St Nicholas Godstone. Chalk paths can be slippery. Lunch in Godstone: the [Hare & Hounds](https://www.hareandhoundsgodstone.co.uk), the [White Hart](https://www.thewhitehartongodstonegreen.com), or the [Bell Inn](https://www.thebellgodstone.co.uk). 16 km. 4 hours.

**Woldingham to Oxted via Great Church Wood**: A shorter variant of [Woldingham to Oxted](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/woldingham-to-oxted/). Rambler favourite! North Downs woodland ridge, South Hawke bridleway, M25 footbridge, open farmland, Greensand Way, ancient churchyard, and golf course. Sights: [Marden Park Woods](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/marden-park), [Tandridge Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/tandridge-churchyard-765), Great Church Wood, and St Peter Tandridge. Can be muddy. Lunch in Old Oxted, Broadham Green, or Tandridge. Old Oxted lunch stop: the [Old Bell](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/surrey/old-bell). Broadham Green lunch stop: the [Haycutter](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/haycutter). Tandridge lunch stop: the [Barley Mow](https://www.barleymowtandridge.co.uk). 11 km. 3 hours.

**Woldingham to Oxted via Titsey Place**: A shorter variant of [Woldingham Circular via Titsey Place](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/woldingham-circular-via-titsey-place/). Rural valleys, North Downs escarpments, plantation paths, open downland, River Eden meadows, and market town streets. Sights: [Marden Park Woods](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/marden-park), [Titsey Place](https://www.titsey.org), North Downs Way, Oxted Downs, Titsey Plantation, Greenwich Meridian, and St Peter's Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Botley Hill, Titsey Place, or Limpsfield. Botley Hill lunch stop: the [Botley Hill Farmhouse](http://www.botleyhill-farmhouse.co.uk). Titsey Place lunch stop: the [Titsey Place Tea Room](https://www.titsey.org/tea-rooms-and-facilities). Limpsfield lunch stop: the [Bull Inn](https://thebullinnlimpsfield.com). 13 km. 3.5 hours.

**Upper Warlingham to Woldingham via Riddlesdown**: An alternative variant of [Warlingham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/warlingham-circular/). Chalk downland, bluebell woods, enclosed bridleways, woodland nature reserves, and Halliloo valley views. Sights: [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), [Ledgers Wood](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/ledgers-wood), King's Wood, St Mary the Virgin, Farleigh, Sanderstead to Whyteleafe Countryside Area, South London Downs National Nature Reserve, Hamsey Green Pond, Clock Tower, and Coal Tax post. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Great Farleigh Green, or Chelsham Common. Great Farleigh Green lunch stop: the [Harrow](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/theharrowwarlingham). Chelsham Common lunch stop: the [Bull Inn](https://www.thebullinnchelsham.co.uk). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**Upper Warlingham to Woldingham via Selsdon Wood Nature Reserve**: An alternative variant of [Warlingham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/warlingham-circular/). Chalk downland, bluebell woods, enclosed bridleways, isolated valleys, golf course descent, woodland nature reserves, and Halliloo valley views. Sights: [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), [Selsdon Wood Nature Reserve](http://www.friendsofselsdonwood.co.uk), [Ledgers Wood](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/ledgers-wood), King's Wood, St Mary the Virgin, Farleigh, Sanderstead to Whyteleafe Countryside Area, South London Downs National Nature Reserve, Hamsey Green Pond, Clock Tower, and Coal Tax post. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in Great Farleigh Green, or Chelsham Common. Great Farleigh Green lunch stop: the [Harrow](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/theharrowwarlingham). Chelsham Common lunch stop: the [Bull Inn](https://www.thebullinnchelsham.co.uk). 17 km. 4 hours.

Chalk paths can be slippery. Lunch in Godstone: the [Hare & Hounds](https://www.hareandhoundsgodstone.co.uk), the [White Hart](https://www.thewhitehartongodstonegreen.com), or the [Bell Inn](https://www.thebellgodstone.co.uk). 16 km. 4 hours.

Sights: [Marden Park Woods](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/marden-park), [Titsey Place](https://www.titsey.org), Woldingham School, North Downs Way, Oxted Downs, Titsey Plantation, Greenwich Meridian, Woldingham Garden Village, Peace Centre, St Paul's Church, and Madeira Walk. Can be muddy. Lunch in Botley Hill, or Titsey Place. Botley Hill lunch stop: the [Botley Hill Farmhouse](http://www.botleyhill-farmhouse.co.uk). Titsey Place lunch stop: the [Titsey Place Tea Room](https://www.titsey.org/tea-rooms-and-facilities). 17 km. 5 hours.

WOL.name: Wolverton
WOL.adminWalksAll: **[Wolverton to Northampton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wolverton-to-northampton/)**: Fields, meadows, riverside cycle paths, medieval hunting forest, parkland, university campus, and riverside town approach. Sights: Wolverton Viaduct, Salcey Forest, Church of St Peter and St Paul, Delapre Abbey, Queen Eleanor Cross, Waterside Campus University of Northampton, Carlsberg Brewery, St Peter's Church, and Northampton Castle. Can be muddy. Lunch in Salcey Forest, or Piddington. Salcey Forest lunch stop: the [Salcey Forest Café](http://www.salceyforestcafe.com/index.html). Piddington lunch stop: the Spread Eagle. 30 km.
WOL.publicWalksS2S: **[Wolverton to Northampton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wolverton-to-northampton/)**: Fields, meadows, riverside cycle paths, medieval hunting forest, parkland, university campus, and riverside town approach. Sights: Wolverton Viaduct, Salcey Forest, Church of St Peter and St Paul, Delapre Abbey, Queen Eleanor Cross, Waterside Campus University of Northampton, Carlsberg Brewery, St Peter's Church, and Northampton Castle. Can be muddy. Lunch in Salcey Forest, or Piddington. Salcey Forest lunch stop: the [Salcey Forest Café](http://www.salceyforestcafe.com/index.html). Piddington lunch stop: the Spread Eagle. 30 km.

WON.name: Walton-on-the-Naze
WON.adminWalksAll:
**[Walton-on-the-Naze Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/walton-on-the-naze-circular/)**: Flat coastal walk, Victorian seaside town, red London Clay eroding cliffs, fossil-rich headland, sandy spit, salt marsh sea wall, and backwater views. Sights: [Walton Pier](https://waltonpier.co.uk/), [Naze Tower](http://www.nazetower.co.uk/), Stone Point, Hamford Water National Nature Reserve, and [John Weston Reserve](http://www.essexwt.org.uk/reserves/john-weston). Check tide timings. Stone Point spit section may not be reliable — hasn't been walk-checked recently. Lunch in The Naze: the [Naze Tower Tea Room](http://www.nazetower.co.uk/). 12 km.

**Walton-on-the-Naze to Frinton-on-Sea**: A longer variant of [Walton-on-the-Naze Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/walton-on-the-naze-circular/). Flat coastal seafront, beach huts, and sandy shore southward to Frinton. Check tide timings. Stone Point spit section may not be reliable — hasn't been walk-checked recently.

**[Clacton-on-Sea to Walton-on-the-Naze](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/clacton-on-sea-to-walton-on-the-naze/)**: Flat North Sea coastal path, sea wall, beaches, and seaside resort promenades. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in Holland-on-Sea: the Oakwood Inn. 11 km.
WON.publicWalksCircular: **[Walton-on-the-Naze Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/walton-on-the-naze-circular/)**: Flat coastal walk, Victorian seaside town, red London Clay eroding cliffs, fossil-rich headland, sandy spit, salt marsh sea wall, and backwater views. Sights: [Walton Pier](https://waltonpier.co.uk/), [Naze Tower](http://www.nazetower.co.uk/), Stone Point, Hamford Water National Nature Reserve, and [John Weston Reserve](http://www.essexwt.org.uk/reserves/john-weston). Check tide timings. Stone Point spit section may not be reliable — hasn't been walk-checked recently. Lunch in The Naze: the [Naze Tower Tea Room](http://www.nazetower.co.uk/). 12 km.
WON.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Clacton-on-Sea to Walton-on-the-Naze](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/clacton-on-sea-to-walton-on-the-naze/)**: Flat North Sea coastal path, sea wall, beaches, and seaside resort promenades. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch in Holland-on-Sea: the Oakwood Inn. 11 km.

WOO.name: Wool
WOO.adminWalksAll: **[Wool Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wool-circular/)**: Rolling Thomas Hardy Country pastures, large woods, dairy farms, remote scenic coombes, steep chalk clifftops, rock stacks, limestone arches, caves, beautiful coves, parkland, heath, and bluebell woods. Sights: Durdle Door, Stair Hole, Lulworth Cove, Lulworth Castle, Coombe Heath Nature Reserve, and [Lulworth Castle Tearoom](https://lulworth.com/tearoom/). Can be muddy. Lunch in East Knighton, Winfrith Newburgh, or Chaldon Herring. East Knighton lunch stop: the [Countryman Inn](https://ourlocal.pub/pubs/countryman-dorchester/). Winfrith Newburgh lunch stop: the [Red Lion Inn](http://winfrithredlion.co.uk/). Chaldon Herring lunch stop: the [Sailor's Return](http://www.sailorsreturnpub.com/m/index.php). 30 km. 7 hours.
WOO.publicWalksCircular: **[Wool Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wool-circular/)**: Rolling Thomas Hardy Country pastures, large woods, dairy farms, remote scenic coombes, steep chalk clifftops, rock stacks, limestone arches, caves, beautiful coves, parkland, heath, and bluebell woods. Sights: Durdle Door, Stair Hole, Lulworth Cove, Lulworth Castle, Coombe Heath Nature Reserve, and [Lulworth Castle Tearoom](https://lulworth.com/tearoom/). Can be muddy. Lunch in East Knighton, Winfrith Newburgh, or Chaldon Herring. East Knighton lunch stop: the [Countryman Inn](https://ourlocal.pub/pubs/countryman-dorchester/). Winfrith Newburgh lunch stop: the [Red Lion Inn](http://winfrithredlion.co.uk/). Chaldon Herring lunch stop: the [Sailor's Return](http://www.sailorsreturnpub.com/m/index.php). 30 km. 7 hours.

WPL.name: Worplesdon
WPL.adminWalksAll:
**[Ash Vale to Worplesdon via Basingstoke Canal Visitors Centre](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-to-worplesdon/)**: Basingstoke Canal towpath, hilly MOD heathland (Ash Ranges), pine-covered heath, farmland tracks, mixed woodland, open common, and mature woodland on Whitmoor Common. Sights: Basingstoke Canal, [Basingstoke Canal Visitors Centre](http://www3.hants.gov.uk/basingstoke-canal/canal-centre.htm), Ash Ranges, Merrist Wood College, [St Mary the Virgin Church](http://www.exploringsurreyspast.org.uk/GetRecord/SHHER_8732), Whitmoor Common, Fox Way, Cobbett Hill Earth Station, and Brookwood Cemetery. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Stanford Common: the [Royal Oak](http://www.royaloak-pub-pirbright.co.uk/). 19 km.

**Ash Vale to Worplesdon via Basingstoke Canal**: A shorter variant of [Ash Vale to Worplesdon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-to-worplesdon/). Basingstoke Canal towpath, restricted MOD firing range, pine-covered heathland, farmland tracks, mixed woodland, and Whitmoor Common. Sights: Basingstoke Canal, Ash Ranges, [St Mary the Virgin Church](http://www.exploringsurreyspast.org.uk/GetRecord/SHHER_8732), and Whitmoor Common. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Stanford Common: the [Royal Oak](http://www.royaloak-pub-pirbright.co.uk/). 17 km.
WPL.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Ash Vale to Worplesdon via Basingstoke Canal Visitors Centre](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ash-vale-to-worplesdon/)**: Basingstoke Canal towpath, hilly MOD heathland (Ash Ranges), pine-covered heath, farmland tracks, mixed woodland, open common, and mature woodland on Whitmoor Common. Sights: Basingstoke Canal, [Basingstoke Canal Visitors Centre](http://www3.hants.gov.uk/basingstoke-canal/canal-centre.htm), Ash Ranges, Merrist Wood College, [St Mary the Virgin Church](http://www.exploringsurreyspast.org.uk/GetRecord/SHHER_8732), Whitmoor Common, Fox Way, Cobbett Hill Earth Station, and Brookwood Cemetery. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Stanford Common: the [Royal Oak](http://www.royaloak-pub-pirbright.co.uk/). 19 km.

WRH.name: Worthing
WRH.adminWalksAll:
**Goring-by-Sea to Worthing**: From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 18 June 2022.* 19 km. 310 m uphill. 5.5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921495389)**

**Worthing to Littlehampton**: A shorter variant of [Shoreham By Sea to Littlehampton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shoreham-by-sea-to-littlehampton/). Flat coastal promenade, grassy paths, shingle and sandy beach, and seaside resort. Sights: [Sea Lane Cafe](http://www.sealanecafe.co.uk/), [Blue Bird Cafe](https://thebluebirdcafeferring.co.uk/), and [East Beach Cafe](http://www.eastbeachcafe.co.uk/). Check tide timings. Lunch in Goring, Ferring, or Littlehampton. Goring lunch stop: the [Sea Lane Cafe](http://www.sealanecafe.co.uk/). Ferring lunch stop: the [Blue Bird Cafe](https://thebluebirdcafeferring.co.uk/). Littlehampton lunch stop: the [East Beach Cafe](http://www.eastbeachcafe.co.uk/). 13 km.

**Angmering to Worthing**: A longer variant of [Arundel to Goring-by-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/arundel-to-goring-by-sea/). Residential streets, village lanes, bridleways, open downland, chalk hills, coastal plain, seafront promenade, and town streets. Sights: [Highdown Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Highdown_Hill), [Highdown Gardens](https://highdowngardens.co.uk), Iron Age hill fort, Ecclesden Mill, St Margaret, Miller's Tomb, Canadian War Memorial, Worthing Lido, Worthing Pier, Pavilion Theatre, and Christ Church. Lunch in Highdown, or Angmering. Highdown lunch stop: the [Highdown](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/highdown). Angmering lunch stops: the [Lamb at Angmering](https://www.thelamb-angmering.com), or the [Spotted Cow](https://www.spottedcowangmering.co.uk/index). 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**Shoreham-by-Sea to Worthing**: A shorter variant of [Shoreham By Sea to Littlehampton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shoreham-by-sea-to-littlehampton/). Flat coastal path, shingle beach, seafront promenade, and seaside resort. Sights: Shoreham Fort, Widewater Lagoon Nature Reserve, Worthing Pier, and [Perch on the Pier](https://perchonthepier.com/). Check tide timings. Lunch in Lancing, or Worthing. Lancing lunch stop: the Perch at Lancing. Worthing lunch stops: the Coast Cafe, or the [Perch on the Pier](https://perchonthepier.com/). 10 km.

**Arundel to Worthing**: A longer variant of [Arundel to Goring-by-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/arundel-to-goring-by-sea/). River path, wooded estate tracks, open downland, chalk hills, coastal plain, seafront promenade, and town streets. Sights: [Arundel Castle](https://www.arundelcastle.org), [Angmering Park Estate](https://www.angmeringwalks.com/angmering-park-estate), [Highdown Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Highdown_Hill), [Highdown Gardens](https://highdowngardens.co.uk), Iron Age hill fort, St John the Divine, Miller's Tomb, Canadian War Memorial, Worthing Lido, Worthing Pier, Pavilion Theatre, and Christ Church. Can be muddy. Lunch in Patching, or Highdown. Patching lunch stop: the [World's End](https://www.worldsendpatching.co.uk). Highdown lunch stop: the [Highdown](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/highdown). 22 km. 5 hours.
WRH.publicWalksS2SEnding: **Goring-by-Sea to Worthing**: From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 19 km. 310 m uphill. 5.5 hours. Hard. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2921495389)**

WRP.name: Warwick Parkway
WRP.adminWalksAll: **[Warwick Parkway Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-826)**: Rolling farmland, Grand Union Canal towpath, woodland, and farm tracks. Sights: [Hatton Locks](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Hatton Country World](https://www.hattonworld.com), [St Michael's, Budbrooke](https://www.stmichaels-budbrooke.org.uk), and [Church Farm Brewery](https://www.churchfarmbrewery.co.uk). Busy A road crossing. Lunch in Hatton: the [Hatton Arms](https://www.hattonarms.com), or the [Hatton Locks Cafe](https://www.facebook.com/Hatton-Locks-287533011322479). 8 km. 2.5 hours.
WRP.publicWalksCircular: **[Warwick Parkway Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-826)**: Rolling farmland, Grand Union Canal towpath, woodland, and farm tracks. Sights: [Hatton Locks](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Hatton Country World](https://www.hattonworld.com), [St Michael's, Budbrooke](https://www.stmichaels-budbrooke.org.uk), and [Church Farm Brewery](https://www.churchfarmbrewery.co.uk). Busy A road crossing. Lunch in Hatton: the [Hatton Arms](https://www.hattonarms.com), or the [Hatton Locks Cafe](https://www.facebook.com/Hatton-Locks-287533011322479). 8 km. 2.5 hours.

WRW.name: Warwick
WRW.adminWalksAll: **[Warwick Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-683)**: Historic market town, River Avon, Grand Union Canal towpath, open fields, racecourse, golf course, and woodland. Sights: [Warwick Castle](https://www.warwick-castle.com), [Collegiate Church of St Mary](https://www.stmaryswarwick.org.uk), Lord Leycester Hospital, [Market Hall Museum](https://www.visitwarwick.co.uk/market-hall-museum/), [Guy's Cliffe Estate](https://www.guyscliffewalledgarden.org.uk), and [Mill Garden](https://www.visitwarwick.co.uk/the-mill-garden/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Warwick, Guy's Cliffe, The Cape, or Smith Street, Warwick. Warwick lunch stop: the [Pasteleria Portuguesa](https://www.pastelariaportugesa.co.uk). Guy's Cliffe lunch stop: the [Saxon Mill](https://www.saxonmill.co.uk). The Cape lunch stop: the Cape of Good Hope. Smith Street, Warwick lunch stops: the Smithery, or the Golden Tea Monkey. 12 km. 4 hours.
WRW.publicWalksCircular: **[Warwick Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-683)**: Historic market town, River Avon, Grand Union Canal towpath, open fields, racecourse, golf course, and woodland. Sights: [Warwick Castle](https://www.warwick-castle.com), [Collegiate Church of St Mary](https://www.stmaryswarwick.org.uk), Lord Leycester Hospital, [Market Hall Museum](https://www.visitwarwick.co.uk/market-hall-museum/), [Guy's Cliffe Estate](https://www.guyscliffewalledgarden.org.uk), and [Mill Garden](https://www.visitwarwick.co.uk/the-mill-garden/). Can be muddy. Lunch in Warwick, Guy's Cliffe, The Cape, or Smith Street, Warwick. Warwick lunch stop: the [Pasteleria Portuguesa](https://www.pastelariaportugesa.co.uk). Guy's Cliffe lunch stop: the [Saxon Mill](https://www.saxonmill.co.uk). The Cape lunch stop: the Cape of Good Hope. Smith Street, Warwick lunch stops: the Smithery, or the Golden Tea Monkey. 12 km. 4 hours.

WSB.name: Westbury
WSB.adminWalksAll: **[Westbury to Warminster](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/westbury-to-warminster-via-imber-range/)**: Chalk downland, Iron Age hillforts, five hills, Salisbury Plain military range, abandoned village, and sweeping views over Wiltshire and Somerset. Sights: Westbury White Horse, Bratton Camp, Scratchbury Camp, Battlesbury Camp, Imber Village, and St Giles, Imber. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Imber: the St Giles, Imber (Tea and Biscuits). 30 km. 7 hours.
WSB.publicWalksS2S: **[Westbury to Warminster](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/westbury-to-warminster-via-imber-range/)**: Chalk downland, Iron Age hillforts, five hills, Salisbury Plain military range, abandoned village, and sweeping views over Wiltshire and Somerset. Sights: Westbury White Horse, Bratton Camp, Scratchbury Camp, Battlesbury Camp, Imber Village, and St Giles, Imber. MOD closures apply. Lunch in Imber: the St Giles, Imber (Tea and Biscuits). 30 km. 7 hours.

WSE.name: Winchelsea
WSE.adminWalksAll:
**Winchelsea to Hastings**: Rambler favourite! From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). *Last reviewed 29 June 2024.* 18 km. 320 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/693693647)**

**Winchelsea Circular**: Rambler favourite! Flat river levels, farmland, coastal path to Pett Level, and return via connecting walk. Sights: All Saints Church and Hog Hill windmill. Lunch in Icklesham, or Pett. Icklesham lunch stop: the Queens Head. Pett lunch stop: the Royal Oak Inn. A variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km.

**Winchelsea to Hastings via All Saints Church**: Rambler favourite! Flat river levels, farmland, hilly coastal cliffs, wooded glens, naturist beach, and historic old town. Sights: All Saints Church, Hog Hill windmill, [Hastings Country Park](https://www.hastingscountryparkhistory.com/index.php), Fairlight Glen nudist beach, and Hastings Castle. Steep cliff climbs. Best in Mar–Aug. Lunch in Icklesham, Pett, Pett Level, or Fairlight Cove. Icklesham lunch stop: the Queens Head. Pett lunch stop: the Royal Oak Inn. Pett Level lunch stops: the [C-Side cafe](https://www.csidecafe.co.uk/), or the [Cockles and Dreams](https://www.facebook.com/cocklesanddreams/). Fairlight Cove lunch stop: the [Cove Inn](https://www.thecovefairlight.co.uk/). From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 20 km. 6 hours.

**[Winchelsea to Hastings via Three Oaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings-via-three-oaks/)**: Undulating ridge, pastureland, farms, woods, hilly territory with fine views, coastal cliff path with steep ascents and descents, and historic walled town. Sights: Winchelsea, New Gate, Icklesham Church, Doleham station, Fairlight Hall, Fairlight Glen, Ecclesbourne Glen, East Hill Lift, and Hastings Old Town. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch in Three Oaks, or Fairlight. Three Oaks lunch stop: the Three Oaks pub. Fairlight lunch stops: the Coastguard Cafe, or the Grumpy Cook Cafe. 26 km.

**Winchelsea to Three Oaks**: A shorter variant of [Winchelsea to Hastings via Three Oaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings-via-three-oaks/). Undulating ridge, pastureland, farms, woods, and hilly territory with fine views over the Brede valley. Sights: Winchelsea, New Gate, and Icklesham Church. Lunch in Icklesham, or Three Oaks. Icklesham lunch stop: the Queen's Head. Three Oaks lunch stop: the Three Oaks pub. 12 km.

**Hastings to Winchelsea**: Rambler favourite! Clifftop coastal path, steep climbs, naturist beach, flat marshland, and ancient hilltop town. Sights: Hastings Castle, St Clements Caves, New Gate, and St. Thomas' Church. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch in Fairlight: the [Cove](https://www.thecovefairlight.co.uk). A shorter variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 2](https://archive.org/details/timeoutcountrywa0000unse). 16 km.
WSE.publicWalksS2S: **Winchelsea to Hastings**: Rambler favourite! From [The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East](https://shop.roughguides.com/book/the-rough-guide-to-walks-in-around-london/9781839058493). 18 km. 320 m uphill. 4 hours. Moderate. **[Komoot route →](https://www.komoot.com/tour/693693647)**

WTE.name: Whitlocks End
WTE.adminWalksAll: **[Whitlocks End Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-827)**: Farmland, Christmas tree plantation, canal crossing, and suburban pavements and footpaths. Sights: [Akamba Heritage Centre](https://www.akamba.co.uk), [Shirley Park](https://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/Shirley-Park), and Stratford-upon-Avon Canal. Lunch in Whitlocks End, Dickens Heath, or Shirley. Whitlocks End lunch stop: the [Akamba Heritage Centre](https://www.akamba.co.uk). Dickens Heath lunch stops: the [Moreton's Kitchen](https://www.mortonskitchen.co.uk), or the [Coffee Cloud](https://www.coffeecloud.co.uk). Shirley lunch stops: the [Frankie's Café](https://www.frankiescafeshirley.com), or the [Fiesta del Asado](https://www.fiestadelasado.co.uk). 2 km. 1 hours.
WTE.publicWalksCircular: **[Whitlocks End Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-827)**: Farmland, Christmas tree plantation, canal crossing, and suburban pavements and footpaths. Sights: [Akamba Heritage Centre](https://www.akamba.co.uk), [Shirley Park](https://www.solihull.gov.uk/Parks-and-open-spaces/Shirley-Park), and Stratford-upon-Avon Canal. Lunch in Whitlocks End, Dickens Heath, or Shirley. Whitlocks End lunch stop: the [Akamba Heritage Centre](https://www.akamba.co.uk). Dickens Heath lunch stops: the [Moreton's Kitchen](https://www.mortonskitchen.co.uk), or the [Coffee Cloud](https://www.coffeecloud.co.uk). Shirley lunch stops: the [Frankie's Café](https://www.frankiescafeshirley.com), or the [Fiesta del Asado](https://www.fiestadelasado.co.uk). 2 km. 1 hours.

WTR.name: Wateringbury
WTR.adminWalksAll:
**[Wateringbury Circular via Teston Lock](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wateringbury-circular/)**: River Medway valley, riverside paths, low hills, orchard country, country lanes, water meadows, and woodland. Sights: Teston Bridge, Teston Bridge Country Park, Teston Lock, Tutsham Oil Mill, Barham Court, East Farleigh Bridge, East Farleigh Lock, Barming Bridge, Tutsham Hall, Maidstone Millennium River Park, St Mary, East Farleigh, All Saints, West Farleigh, and Good Intent pub. Can be muddy. Lunch in Farleigh Green, or East Farleigh. Farleigh Green lunch stop: the Good Intent. East Farleigh lunch stops: the Bull Inn, or the Victory. 14 km. 3 hours.

**Wateringbury Circular via Horseshoes Inn**: A variant of [Wateringbury Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wateringbury-circular/). River Medway valley, riverside paths, low hills, orchard country, country lanes, water meadows, and woodland. Sights: Teston Bridge, Teston Bridge Country Park, East Farleigh Bridge, East Farleigh Lock, Barming Bridge, Tutsham Hall, [Horseshoes Inn](https://www.thehorseshoeseastfarleigh.co.uk), and St Mary, East Farleigh. Can be muddy. Lunch in East Farleigh: the [Horseshoes Inn](https://www.thehorseshoeseastfarleigh.co.uk), the Bull Inn, or the Victory. 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**Wateringbury Circular via St Mary the Virgin, Nettlestead**: A variant of [Wateringbury Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wateringbury-circular/). River Medway valley, riverside paths, low hills, orchard country, country lanes, churchyard, water meadows, and woodland. Sights: St Mary the Virgin, Nettlestead, Nettlestead Place, Teston Bridge, Teston Bridge Country Park, East Farleigh Bridge, East Farleigh Lock, Barming Bridge, Tutsham Hall, Brooms House, and St Mary, East Farleigh. Can be muddy. Lunch in Farleigh Green, or East Farleigh. Farleigh Green lunch stop: the Good Intent. East Farleigh lunch stops: the Bull Inn, or the Victory. 17 km. 4 hours.

**Wateringbury to Yalding**: A shorter variant of [Yalding Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-circular/). Flat riverside paths, farmland fields, orchards, quiet lanes, and a floodplain. Sights: [Teston Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teston_Bridge), [Teston Bridge Country Park](http://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/teston-bridge-country-park), [Tea Pot Island](http://www.teapotisland.co.uk/), [Yalding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yalding), and [Twyford Bridge](http://yaldinghistory.webplus.net/page17.html). Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh: the [Tickled Trout](http://www.thetickledtrout.com/). 9 km.

**East Farleigh to Wateringbury**: An alternative variant of [Wateringbury Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wateringbury-circular/). River Medway valley, riverside paths, low hills, orchard country, woodland, and country lanes. Sights: East Farleigh Bridge, [North Pole](https://northpolepub.co.uk), Teston Bridge, Teston Bridge Country Park, Tutsham Hall, Ss Peter and Paul, Teston, Alfred Reader factory, and Wateringbury Place. Can be muddy. Lunch in Red Hill: the [North Pole](https://northpolepub.co.uk). 13 km. 3 hours.

**Paddock Wood to Wateringbury via River Medway**: A longer variant of [Paddock Wood to Yalding](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/paddock-wood-to-yalding/). Flat flood plain, riverside meadows, tree-lined field edges, mixed woods, open countryside, village streets, and extended riverside path to Wateringbury. Sights: [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), [Yalding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yalding), Twyford Bridge, Yalding Lees, Ss Peter and Paul, Yalding, Yalding Town Bridge, Warde's Moat, Medway Valley Walk, Cheveney Mill, Yalding Fen, Kenward House, Nettlestead Place, and St Mary the Virgin, Nettlestead. Lunch in Twyford Bridge: the [Boathouse](https://www.boathouseyalding.co.uk). 15 km.

**Maidstone West to Wateringbury via Teston Lock**: A shorter variant of [Wateringbury Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wateringbury-circular/). River Medway towpath, riverside paths, flat valley walking, and water meadows. Sights: Archbishop's Palace, All Saints, Maidstone, The Stag, Lockmeadow Millennium Bridge, Tovil Railway Bridge, East Farleigh Lock, East Farleigh Bridge, Barming Bridge, Teston Lock, Teston Bridge, and Maidstone Millennium River Park. 9 km. 2 hours.

**Maidstone West to Wateringbury via Archbishop's Palace**: A longer variant of [Wateringbury Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wateringbury-circular/). River Medway towpath, riverside paths, low hills, orchard country, country lanes, and water meadows. Sights: Archbishop's Palace, All Saints, Maidstone, The Stag, Lockmeadow Millennium Bridge, Tovil Railway Bridge, East Farleigh Lock, East Farleigh Bridge, Barming Bridge, [Horseshoes Inn](https://www.thehorseshoeseastfarleigh.co.uk), Teston Bridge, Tutsham Hall, and Maidstone Millennium River Park. Can be muddy. Lunch in East Farleigh: the [Horseshoes Inn](https://www.thehorseshoeseastfarleigh.co.uk), the Bull Inn, or the Victory. 17 km. 4 hours.

**Paddock Wood to Wateringbury via St Mary, Hunton**: A longer variant of [Paddock Wood to Yalding](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/paddock-wood-to-yalding/). Flat flood plain, riverside meadows, tree-lined field edges, mixed woods, open countryside, village streets, Greensand Ridge slopes, and extended riverside path to Wateringbury. Sights: [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), [Yalding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yalding), Twyford Bridge, Yalding Lees, Ss Peter and Paul, Yalding, Yalding Town Bridge, Warde's Moat, St Mary, Hunton, Greensand Way, Medway Valley Walk, Cheveney Mill, Yalding Fen, Kenward House, Nettlestead Place, and St Mary the Virgin, Nettlestead. Lunch in Twyford Bridge: the [Boathouse](https://www.boathouseyalding.co.uk). 20 km.

**Maidstone West to Wateringbury via North Pole**: A longer variant of [Wateringbury Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wateringbury-circular/). River Medway towpath and valley, riverside paths, low hills, orchard country, woodland, water meadows, and country lanes. Sights: Archbishop's Palace, All Saints, Maidstone, The Stag, Lockmeadow Millennium Bridge, East Farleigh Bridge, East Farleigh Lock, [North Pole](https://northpolepub.co.uk), Teston Bridge, Teston Bridge Country Park, Tutsham Hall, Ss Peter and Paul, Teston, Barham Court, and [Horseshoes Inn](https://www.thehorseshoeseastfarleigh.co.uk). Can be muddy. Lunch in Red Hill, or East Farleigh. Red Hill lunch stop: the [North Pole](https://northpolepub.co.uk). East Farleigh lunch stop: the [Horseshoes Inn](https://www.thehorseshoeseastfarleigh.co.uk). 25 km. 6 hours.
WTR.publicWalksCircular: **[Wateringbury Circular via Teston Lock](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wateringbury-circular/)**: River Medway valley, riverside paths, low hills, orchard country, country lanes, water meadows, and woodland. Sights: Teston Bridge, Teston Bridge Country Park, Teston Lock, Tutsham Oil Mill, Barham Court, East Farleigh Bridge, East Farleigh Lock, Barming Bridge, Tutsham Hall, Maidstone Millennium River Park, St Mary, East Farleigh, All Saints, West Farleigh, and Good Intent pub. Can be muddy. Lunch in Farleigh Green, or East Farleigh. Farleigh Green lunch stop: the Good Intent. East Farleigh lunch stops: the Bull Inn, or the Victory. 14 km. 3 hours.

WTS.name: Whatstandwell
WTS.adminWalksAll: **[Whatstandwell to Cromford](https://www.visitambervalley.com/media/1121/florence-nightingale-trail.pdf)**: The route of 6 miles starts from the A6 at Whatstandwell and goes via Holloway, Lea, Dethick, and Lea Bridge to rejoin the A6 at Cromford. 9 km.
WTS.publicWalksS2S: **[Whatstandwell to Cromford](https://www.visitambervalley.com/media/1121/florence-nightingale-trail.pdf)**: The route of 6 miles starts from the A6 at Whatstandwell and goes via Holloway, Lea, Dethick, and Lea Bridge to rejoin the A6 at Cromford. 9 km.

WTY.name: Witley
WTY.adminWalksAll:
**Witley to Haslemere via Crown Inn**: Gentle farmland, bridleways, mixed woodland, open fields, National Trust copses, and a medieval village green. Sights: Crown Inn, St Mary's Church, Sydenhurst Estate, Combe Court, Frillinghurst Wood, Swan Barn Farm, Witley Copse, Haslemere Museum, and RSPCA Lockwood Equine Centre. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chiddingfold: the Crown Inn, the Swan, or the Elliott's Coffee Shop. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 13 km. 4 hours.

**[Witley to Haslemere via Thursley, Surrey Hills](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/witley-to-haslemere-via-thursley-surrey-hills/)**: Wooded countryside, heathland, sunken byway, prolonged hill climb, moorland rim, greensand hills, and quaint village. Sights: [Thursley](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thursley), [Gibbet Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gibbet_Hill,_Hindhead), Devil's Punch Bowl, [Sailor's Stone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unknown_Sailor), St Michael and All Angels Church, and [Fly Agaric](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amanita_muscaria). Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Thursley: the Three Horseshoes. 17 km.

**Witley to Gomshall**: A longer variant of [Witley to Ewhurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/witley-to-ewhurst/). Rural Surrey Hills, Wey Valley, small villages, forested ridge, and long extension north to Gomshall. Sights: [Hurtwood Inn](http://www.hurtwoodinn.com/) and [Gomshall Mill](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/gomshallmill/). Very long day. Lunch in Hascombe, or Shamley Green. Hascombe lunch stop: the [White Horse](https://www.whitehorsehascombe.co.uk). Shamley Green lunch stops: the [Bricklayers Arms](http://www.bricklayersarmspub.co.uk), or the [Red Lion](https://www.redlionshamleygreen.com). 25 km.

**Witley to Godalming**: Sandy bridleways, sunken footpaths, woodland, open fields, arboretum lakes, canal towpath, and historic town streets. Sights: [St Peter's Church, Hascombe](http://www.openchurchestrust.org.uk/Churches/Hascombe.htm), Winkworth Arboretum, [River Wey and Godalming Navigation](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-riverwey_godalmingnavigations_dapdunewharf), and Church of St Peter and St Paul. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Hascombe: the White Horse. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).
WTY.publicWalksS2S:
**Witley to Haslemere via Crown Inn**: Gentle farmland, bridleways, mixed woodland, open fields, National Trust copses, and a medieval village green. Sights: Crown Inn, St Mary's Church, Sydenhurst Estate, Combe Court, Frillinghurst Wood, Swan Barn Farm, Witley Copse, Haslemere Museum, and RSPCA Lockwood Equine Centre. Can be muddy. Lunch in Chiddingfold: the Crown Inn, the Swan, or the Elliott's Coffee Shop. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 13 km. 4 hours.

**[Witley to Haslemere via Thursley, Surrey Hills](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/witley-to-haslemere-via-thursley-surrey-hills/)**: Wooded countryside, heathland, sunken byway, prolonged hill climb, moorland rim, greensand hills, and quaint village. Sights: [Thursley](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thursley), [Gibbet Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gibbet_Hill,_Hindhead), Devil's Punch Bowl, [Sailor's Stone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unknown_Sailor), St Michael and All Angels Church, and [Fly Agaric](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amanita_muscaria). Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch in Thursley: the Three Horseshoes. 17 km.

WVF.name: Wivelsfield
WVF.adminWalksAll:
**[Wivelsfield to Haywards Heath via Cuckfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wivelsfield-to-haywards-heath/)**: Woods, wildflower meadows, farmland, High Weald ridge, golf course, and suburban road sections. Sights: [Bedelands Farm Local Nature Reserve](https://bh-green-circle.org.uk/sites/bedelands-local-nature-reserve), [Holy Trinity Church](https://www.holytrinitycuckfield.org/welcome/church-history-2), [Cuckfield](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cuckfield), [Cuckfield Museum](https://cuckfieldmuseum.org), [High Weald Landscape Trail](https://highweald.org/things-to-do/high-weald-landscape-trail), [Borde Hill Garden](https://www.bordehill.co.uk), Ockenden Manor, Cuckfield Park, New England Wood, and Ouse Valley Viaduct. Can be muddy. Lunch in Cuckfield: the [Talbot](https://www.thetalbotcuckfield.co.uk), the Cuckfield Pantry & Tearoom, or the [Tom's Food](https://tomsfood.co.uk/home). 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Wivelsfield to Haywards Heath via Bedelands Farm Local Nature Reserve**: A shorter variant of [Wivelsfield to Haywards Heath](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wivelsfield-to-haywards-heath/). Woods, wildflower meadows, farmland, High Weald, parkland, and suburban road sections. Sights: [Bedelands Farm Local Nature Reserve](https://bh-green-circle.org.uk/sites/bedelands-local-nature-reserve), [Holy Trinity Church](https://www.holytrinitycuckfield.org/welcome/church-history-2), [Cuckfield Museum](https://cuckfieldmuseum.org), [Borde Hill Garden](https://www.bordehill.co.uk), Ockenden Manor, and Ouse Valley Viaduct. Can be muddy. Lunch in Cuckfield: the [Talbot](https://www.thetalbotcuckfield.co.uk), the Cuckfield Pantry & Tearoom, or the [Tom's Food](https://tomsfood.co.uk/home). 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**[East Grinstead to Wivelsfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-grinstead-to-wivelsfield/)**: Hills, streams, ponds, heaths, mixed woodland rich in bluebells and wood anemones, pastures, and heathery commons with South Downs views. Sights: Weir Wood Reservoir, Broadhurst Manor, Chailey Common, Chailey Windmill, and Bluebell Railway. Mud and flood prone. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Horsted Keynes, or Scaynes Hill. Horsted Keynes lunch stops: the [Green Man](http://greenmanhorstedkeynes.co.uk), or the [Crown Inn](http://www.thecrownhorstedkeynes.co.uk). Scaynes Hill lunch stop: the [Sloop Inn](https://thesloopinn.com/). 31 km. 7.5 hours.
WVF.publicWalksS2S: **[Wivelsfield to Haywards Heath via Cuckfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wivelsfield-to-haywards-heath/)**: Woods, wildflower meadows, farmland, High Weald ridge, golf course, and suburban road sections. Sights: [Bedelands Farm Local Nature Reserve](https://bh-green-circle.org.uk/sites/bedelands-local-nature-reserve), [Holy Trinity Church](https://www.holytrinitycuckfield.org/welcome/church-history-2), [Cuckfield](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cuckfield), [Cuckfield Museum](https://cuckfieldmuseum.org), [High Weald Landscape Trail](https://highweald.org/things-to-do/high-weald-landscape-trail), [Borde Hill Garden](https://www.bordehill.co.uk), Ockenden Manor, Cuckfield Park, New England Wood, and Ouse Valley Viaduct. Can be muddy. Lunch in Cuckfield: the [Talbot](https://www.thetalbotcuckfield.co.uk), the Cuckfield Pantry & Tearoom, or the [Tom's Food](https://tomsfood.co.uk/home). 18 km. 4.5 hours.
WVF.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[East Grinstead to Wivelsfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-grinstead-to-wivelsfield/)**: Hills, streams, ponds, heaths, mixed woodland rich in bluebells and wood anemones, pastures, and heathery commons with South Downs views. Sights: Weir Wood Reservoir, Broadhurst Manor, Chailey Common, Chailey Windmill, and Bluebell Railway. Mud and flood prone. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Horsted Keynes, or Scaynes Hill. Horsted Keynes lunch stops: the [Green Man](http://greenmanhorstedkeynes.co.uk), or the [Crown Inn](http://www.thecrownhorstedkeynes.co.uk). Scaynes Hill lunch stop: the [Sloop Inn](https://thesloopinn.com/). 31 km. 7.5 hours.

WWW.name: Wootton Wawen
WWW.adminWalksAll: **[Wootton Wawen Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-871)**: Canal towpath, aqueduct, open fields, hedgerows, and working farm. Sights: [St Peter's Church](www.saxonsanctuary.org.uk), [Edstone Aqueduct](www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Mary Arden's Farm](www.shakespeare.org.uk), and Yew Tree Farm. Can be boggy/muddy. Lunch in Wootton Wawen, nr Edstone Aqueduct, or Wilmcote. Wootton Wawen lunch stops: the [Bulls Head](www.thebullshead.pub), the [Navigation Inn](www.v4navigation.co.uk), or the General Stores. nr Edstone Aqueduct lunch stop: the [View, The](www.theview-eat.co.uk). Wilmcote lunch stop: the Inn. 7 km. 2.5 hours.
WWW.publicWalksCircular: **[Wootton Wawen Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-871)**: Canal towpath, aqueduct, open fields, hedgerows, and working farm. Sights: [St Peter's Church](www.saxonsanctuary.org.uk), [Edstone Aqueduct](www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Mary Arden's Farm](www.shakespeare.org.uk), and Yew Tree Farm. Can be boggy/muddy. Lunch in Wootton Wawen, nr Edstone Aqueduct, or Wilmcote. Wootton Wawen lunch stops: the [Bulls Head](www.thebullshead.pub), the [Navigation Inn](www.v4navigation.co.uk), or the General Stores. nr Edstone Aqueduct lunch stop: the [View, The](www.theview-eat.co.uk). Wilmcote lunch stop: the Inn. 7 km. 2.5 hours.

WYE.name: Wye
WYE.adminWalksAll:
**Wye Circular via Crown Memorial**: North Downs escarpments, chalk downland, beech and chestnut woods, open fields, riverbank along Great Stour, churchyard, and farmland. Sights: Crown Memorial, Crown Millennium Stone, Church of St Mary, Crundale, Crundale House, Chapel and Manor of Olantigh, Church of St Gregory and St Martin, and Wye College. Can be muddy. Lunch in Sole Street, Crundale: the Timber Batts at the Compasses. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 17 km. 5 hours.

**Wye Circular via Wye Nature Reserve**: North Downs escarpments, chalk downland, light woodland, open arable fields, farmland, villages, and riverbank along Great Stour. Sights: Crown Memorial, Crown Millennium Stone, Wye Nature Reserve, Devils Kneading Trough, Church of St Mary, Crundale, Crundale House, Chapel and Manor of Olantigh, Church of St Gregory and St Martin, and Wye College. Can be muddy. Lunch in Hastingleigh, or Sole Street, Crundale. Hastingleigh lunch stop: the [Bowl Inn](http://thebowlonline.co.uk/). Sole Street, Crundale lunch stop: the Timber Batts at the Compasses. A longer variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 22 km.

**Wye to Chilham**: A variant of [Chilham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chilham-circular/). Downland escarpment, upland fields, woods, country estate, and a hilltop village. Sights: Godmersham Park and [Chilham Castle](http://www.chilham-castle.co.uk). Lunch in Sole Street: the Timber Batts @ The Compasses. 17 km.

**Wye to Sandling**: An alternative variant of [Sandling to Wye](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-wye/). North Downs escarpment ridge, open downland, woods, sloping fields, and fine views over Kent coast. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Stowting, or Brabourne. Stowting lunch stop: the Tiger Inn. Brabourne lunch stop: the Five Bells Inn. 20 km.

**Wye to Folkestone West via NDW**: A longer variant of [Sandling to Wye](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-wye/). North Downs ridge, valley, disused railway, spectacular Channel views, and road walking at end. Sights: Channel Tunnel Terminal. Road walking at end.

**[Sandling to Wye](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-wye/)**: North Downs escarpment ridge, open downland, woods, sloping fields, and fine views over Kent coast. Sights: Tickled Trout. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Stowting, or Brabourne. Stowting lunch stop: the Tiger Inn. Brabourne lunch stop: the Five Bells Inn. 20 km.

**Chilham to Wye**: A variant of [Chilham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chilham-circular/). Downland escarpment, upland fields, woods, country estate, and a hilltop village. Sights: Godmersham Park and [Chilham Castle](http://www.chilham-castle.co.uk). Lunch in Sole Street: the Timber Batts @ The Compasses. 16 km.
WYE.publicWalksCircular: **Wye Circular via Crown Memorial**: North Downs escarpments, chalk downland, beech and chestnut woods, open fields, riverbank along Great Stour, churchyard, and farmland. Sights: Crown Memorial, Crown Millennium Stone, Church of St Mary, Crundale, Crundale House, Chapel and Manor of Olantigh, Church of St Gregory and St Martin, and Wye College. Can be muddy. Lunch in Sole Street, Crundale: the Timber Batts at the Compasses. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 17 km. 5 hours.
WYE.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Sandling to Wye](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-wye/)**: North Downs escarpment ridge, open downland, woods, sloping fields, and fine views over Kent coast. Sights: Tickled Trout. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Stowting, or Brabourne. Stowting lunch stop: the Tiger Inn. Brabourne lunch stop: the Five Bells Inn. 20 km.

WYT.name: Wythall
WYT.adminWalksAll: **[Wythall Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-830)**: Pasture, meadows, fields, riding school, canal aqueduct, River Cole, and suburban streets. Sights: [Transport Museum](http://www.wythall.org.uk), [Wythall Park](http://www.wythall-park.org.uk), [Berry Mound Iron Age Hill Fort](http://www.historicengland.org.uk), and Berry Mound Pastures. Broken stile; horses present. Lunch in Wythall, Major's Green, or Shirley. Wythall lunch stop: the [Beckett's Farm](http://www.beckettsfarm.co.uk). Major's Green lunch stop: the [Drawbridge Inn](http://www.greeneking-pubs.co.uk). Shirley lunch stop: the [Colebrook](http://www.emberinns.co.uk). 4 km. 1.5 hours.
WYT.publicWalksCircular: **[Wythall Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-830)**: Pasture, meadows, fields, riding school, canal aqueduct, River Cole, and suburban streets. Sights: [Transport Museum](http://www.wythall.org.uk), [Wythall Park](http://www.wythall-park.org.uk), [Berry Mound Iron Age Hill Fort](http://www.historicengland.org.uk), and Berry Mound Pastures. Broken stile; horses present. Lunch in Wythall, Major's Green, or Shirley. Wythall lunch stop: the [Beckett's Farm](http://www.beckettsfarm.co.uk). Major's Green lunch stop: the [Drawbridge Inn](http://www.greeneking-pubs.co.uk). Shirley lunch stop: the [Colebrook](http://www.emberinns.co.uk). 4 km. 1.5 hours.

YAL.name: Yalding
YAL.privateNote:
LON Trainline says 57m — London Bridge via Paddock Wood
Issue CLJ>YAL. App says 1 hour and 50 minutes from Clapham Junction. Two changes: London Bridge and Paddock Wood. But Trainline says 1h31m CLJ>Waerloo>{walk to...]WaterlooEast>PaddockWood>CLJ
YAL.adminWalksAll:
**[Yalding Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-circular/)**: Flat riverside paths, farmland fields, orchards, quiet lanes, and a floodplain. Sights: [Teston Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teston_Bridge), [Nettlestead Church](http://nettlesteadchurch.co.uk/), [Teston Bridge Country Park](http://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/teston-bridge-country-park), [Tea Pot Island](http://www.teapotisland.co.uk/), [Yalding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yalding), [Almshouses](http://yaldinghistory.webplus.net/page272.html), and [Twyford Bridge](http://yaldinghistory.webplus.net/page17.html). Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh: the [Tickled Trout](http://www.thetickledtrout.com/). 12 km.

**[Yalding to Sevenoaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-to-sevenoaks/)**: Rambler favourite! Undulating Kent farmland, orchards, bluebell woods, greensand escarpment, and deer park. Sights: Ightham Mote, Knole House, and One Tree Hill. Waymarking can disappear. Best in Apr–May. Lunch in West Peckham, Dunks Green, or Shipbourne. West Peckham lunch stop: the Swan. Dunks Green lunch stop: the Kentish Rifleman. Shipbourne lunch stop: the Chaser Inn. 21 km.

**Yalding to Borough Green & Wrotham via Church of St Lawrence**: River Medway start, open fields, deer-fenced woodland, Kentish Weald countryside, vast managed woods, bluebell woodland valleys, orchard edges, and small villages. Sights: Roydon Hall, St Michaels Church, Church of St Lawrence, and Mereworth Wines. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Mereworth: the Mereworth Wines. From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 5 hours.

**Yalding to Borough Green & Wrotham via Roydon Hall**: Open fields, country lanes, managed Mereworth Woods, bluebell woodland valleys, and small villages. Sights: Roydon Hall, St Michaels Church, and Mereworth Wines. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Mereworth: the Moody Mare Country Pub. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 5 hours.

**Yalding to Beltring**: A longer variant of [Yalding Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-circular/). Flat riverside paths, farmland, woods, and a pretty stretch of river Medway. Sights: [Teston Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teston_Bridge), [Nettlestead Church](http://nettlesteadchurch.co.uk/), [Teston Bridge Country Park](http://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/teston-bridge-country-park), and [Tea Pot Island](http://www.teapotisland.co.uk/). Busy A-road crossing. Lunch in West Farleigh, or East Peckham. West Farleigh lunch stop: the [Tickled Trout](http://www.thetickledtrout.com/). East Peckham lunch stop: the [Man of Kent](http://themanofkentpub.com/). 20 km.

**Yalding to Harrietsham**: A longer variant of [Yalding to Sutton Valence](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-to-sutton-vallance/). Flat Medway Valley start, gentle Greensand Ridge climb, arable fields, apple orchards, scenic ridge walking, coppiced woodland with wood anemone, and then descent to Harrietsham. Sights: Boughton Monchelsea church, Buttercup Goat Sanctuary, and Pepper Box pub. Best in Mar–Apr. Lunch in Linton: the Bull. 24 km.

**Yalding to Paddock Wood**: A longer variant of [Yalding Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-circular/). Flat riverside paths, farmland, meadows, woods, and quiet lanes. Sights: [Teston Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teston_Bridge), [Nettlestead Church](http://nettlesteadchurch.co.uk/), [Teston Bridge Country Park](http://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/teston-bridge-country-park), and [Tea Pot Island](http://www.teapotisland.co.uk/). Lunch in West Farleigh, or East Peckham. West Farleigh lunch stop: the [Tickled Trout](http://www.thetickledtrout.com/). East Peckham lunch stop: the [Man of Kent](http://themanofkentpub.com/).

**Yalding to Borough Green & Wrotham via St Dunstan's Church**: Sunken bridleways, open fields, Greensand Way, Gower Hill woodland, Mereworth Woods, bluebell valley, and small villages. Sights: Roydon Hall, St Michaels Church, and St Dunstan's Church. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in West Peckham: the Swan on the Green. An alternative variant of [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse).

**[Paddock Wood to Yalding via St Mary, Hunton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/paddock-wood-to-yalding/)**: Flat flood plain, riverside meadows, tree-lined field edges, mixed woods, open countryside, village streets, and gentle Greensand Ridge slopes. Sights: [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), [Yalding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yalding), Twyford Bridge, Yalding Lees, Ss Peter and Paul, Yalding, Yalding Town Bridge, Warde's Moat, St Mary, Hunton, Greensand Way, Medway Valley Walk, Cheveney Mill, and Yalding Fen. Lunch in Twyford Bridge: the [Boathouse](https://www.boathouseyalding.co.uk). 18 km. 4 hours.

**Paddock Wood to Yalding via River Medway**: A shorter variant of [Paddock Wood to Yalding](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/paddock-wood-to-yalding/). Flat flood plain, riverside meadows, tree-lined field edges, mixed woods, open countryside, and village streets. Sights: [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), [Yalding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yalding), Twyford Bridge, Yalding Lees, Ss Peter and Paul, Yalding, Yalding Town Bridge, Warde's Moat, Medway Valley Walk, Cheveney Mill, and Yalding Fen. Lunch in Twyford Bridge: the [Boathouse](https://www.boathouseyalding.co.uk). 12 km. 2.5 hours.

**Wateringbury to Yalding**: A shorter variant of [Yalding Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-circular/). Flat riverside paths, farmland fields, orchards, quiet lanes, and a floodplain. Sights: [Teston Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teston_Bridge), [Teston Bridge Country Park](http://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/teston-bridge-country-park), [Tea Pot Island](http://www.teapotisland.co.uk/), [Yalding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yalding), and [Twyford Bridge](http://yaldinghistory.webplus.net/page17.html). Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh: the [Tickled Trout](http://www.thetickledtrout.com/). 9 km.

From [Time Out Country Walks Volume 1](https://archive.org/details/timeoutbookofcou0001unse). 16 km. 5 hours.
YAL.publicWalksCircular: **[Yalding Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-circular/)**: Flat riverside paths, farmland fields, orchards, quiet lanes, and a floodplain. Sights: [Teston Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teston_Bridge), [Nettlestead Church](http://nettlesteadchurch.co.uk/), [Teston Bridge Country Park](http://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/teston-bridge-country-park), [Tea Pot Island](http://www.teapotisland.co.uk/), [Yalding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yalding), [Almshouses](http://yaldinghistory.webplus.net/page272.html), and [Twyford Bridge](http://yaldinghistory.webplus.net/page17.html). Can be muddy. Lunch in West Farleigh: the [Tickled Trout](http://www.thetickledtrout.com/). 12 km.
YAL.publicWalksS2SEnding: **[Paddock Wood to Yalding via St Mary, Hunton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/paddock-wood-to-yalding/)**: Flat flood plain, riverside meadows, tree-lined field edges, mixed woods, open countryside, village streets, and gentle Greensand Ridge slopes. Sights: [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), [Yalding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yalding), Twyford Bridge, Yalding Lees, Ss Peter and Paul, Yalding, Yalding Town Bridge, Warde's Moat, St Mary, Hunton, Greensand Way, Medway Valley Walk, Cheveney Mill, and Yalding Fen. Lunch in Twyford Bridge: the [Boathouse](https://www.boathouseyalding.co.uk). 18 km. 4 hours.

ZTU.name: Turnham Green
ZTU.adminWalksAll: **Norbiton to Turnham Green**: A shorter variant of [Norbiton to Hammersmith or Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/norbiton-to-hammersmith-or-putney/). Deer park, woodland garden, open grassland, riverside common, village green, Thames riverfront, suburban streets, and common land. Sights: [Richmond Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond-park), Isabella Plantation, Pen Ponds, [Chiswick House & Gardens](https://chiswickhouseandgardens.org.uk), [Hogarth House](https://williamhogarthtrust.org.uk), and Chiswick Back Common. Best in Mar–May. Lunch in Barnes: the [Sun Inn](https://www.thesuninnbarnes.co.uk), the [Coach & Horses](https://www.coachandhorsesbarnes.co.uk), the [Bulls Head](https://www.thebullsheadbarnes.com), or the [Watermans Arms](https://www.watermansarms.co.uk). 15 km.
